title: Backend switch leaks cross-backend cached data (agents, conversations, timeline)
description:
When the user swaps active backend, process-wide singleton repository caches (AgentRepository._agents, ConversationRepository._conversationsByAgent, TimelineRepository.loops, PassageRepository.passageFlowsByAgent, BlockRepository, etc.) carry data from the previous backend until each screen's activeConfigChanges.collect handler fires its own refresh(). During that window — and for any screen that doesn't refresh — old data is visible. With agents that share names across backends, the user perceives this as 'cache collision' (Backend A's Letta data shown on Backend B context).

Compounding issue: Room tables (agents, conversations, etc.) have no backend-id column. Primary key is just the entity id. deleteExcept(currentIds) in refresh paths cleans up correctly per-backend, but during the refresh window, screens reading the Dao directly see merged state.

Fix: introduce BackendScopedCache interface + BackendSwitchInvalidator (Singleton). Each cache-holding repo implements clearForBackendSwitch(). SettingsRepository.activateConfig synchronously calls invalidator.clearAll() BEFORE updating _activeConfig — so every activeConfigChanges subscriber's refresh() observes empty caches and repopulates from the new backend cleanly.

Repos to wire: AgentRepository, ConversationRepository, TimelineRepository, PassageRepository, BlockRepository, BugReportRepository, FolderRepository, ProjectRepository, AllConversationsRepository, RunRepository, ModelRepository, McpServerRepository, IdentityRepository, ArchiveRepository, ToolRepository, ProviderRepository, GroupRepository (~17 repos). V1 lands the interface + wires the high-traffic ones (Agent, Conversation, Timeline, Passage, Block); the others can be added incrementally via the same hook.

Out of scope: Adding backend_id column to Room schemas (would need destructive migration; tracked in yoic.4 if revisited).

title: Fix stale chat history: reconcile recent messages on conversation open
description: Regression: opening an already-cached conversation shows stale history. The loop is created earlier in the process (warmup / resume-most-recent / notification path) and getOrCreate.cacheHit returns it as-is. No path invokes reconcileRecentMessages on open, so the timeline state can be 297+ messages stale (observed). First user send triggers the post-send reconcile, which pulls everything — but the gap is visible from open until send. Fix: add timelineRepository.reconcileRecentMessages(conversationId, 'open') in ChatConversationCoordinator.loadMessagesInternal, right after startTimelineObserver.

title: Fix RootDebugUnitTest compilation error (PR #63 fallout)
description:
testRootDebugUnitTest fails to COMPILE on main after PR #63 was admin-merged:

> Task :app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin FAILED
Execution failed for task ':app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin'.
   > Compilation error. See log for more details

PR #63 (feat(ux): EditAgent sticky headers + Index sheet, bundle JetBrains Mono) is the suspected cause. The unit-test source set can't compile, meaning ALL unit tests are blocked — this is more severe than a test failure. Run `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin` locally to surface the error and fix.

Failed CI run: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/actions/runs/25901151733
notes:
Released from this session. While validating PR #74 from the existing dirty local worktree, :app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin failed first on unrelated ProjectHomeViewModelTest unresolved conversationalProject* references. The clean PR branch for letta-mobile-4ouj does not touch xjuj.
Session handoff: PR #74 (codex/passage-observable-flow-4ouj) contains the clean origin/main-based PassageRepository flow fix for closed bead letta-mobile-4ouj. CI was queued/running after PR creation. Current local worktree still has unrelated staged/dev changes and git status/switch fail on stale worktree metadata at /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/.git/worktrees/agent-a4f92d57df3bebe5c. Resume with: bd show letta-mobile-xjuj && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 74
close_reason: Implemented in PR #75 (codex/fix-root-debug-unit-test-xjuj). Removed stale ProjectHomeViewModel conversational flow tests after the flow moved to CreateProjectViewModel, added CreateProjectViewModel coverage, and verified :app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin now succeeds on a clean main clone. Full :app:testRootDebugUnitTest now compiles/runs and only fails the EditAgentScreen tests tracked by letta-mobile-jj59.

title: Fix crash: LazyColumn keys using value class IDs not Bundle-compatible
description: Agent list admin page crashes with IllegalArgumentException: 'Type of the key ... is not supported' because LazyColumn item keys were using @JvmInline value classes (AgentId, ToolId) instead of their underlying String values. Compose's SaveableStateHolder requires Bundle-compatible key types. Fixed by changing key = { it.id } to key = { it.id.value } in AgentListScreen, ArchiveAdminScreen, BotConfigEditScreen, McpServerToolsScreen, and ToolsScreen.
close_reason: Fixed in all 5 affected screens; APK built and deployed to device

title: Fix CI: migrate app/src/test to value-class IDs (AgentId/BlockId/ToolId)
description: CI run 25771096447 failed: ~80 type mismatches in app/src/test after 925m value-class migration. Fix all app test files to wrap String literals in AgentId()/BlockId()/ToolId()/ProjectId() and unwrap with .value where comparing to String. Also verify core/src/testFixtures.

title: Fix launch crash after startup profile work
description: App crashes when opened after recent startup profile / root debug install work. Capture device logs, identify root cause, fix, rebuild, and reinstall.
notes: Fixed launch crash by making EncryptedPrefsHelper recover from unreadable Android Keystore/Tink encrypted prefs: delete letta_secure_prefs and the default master key alias, then recreate encrypted prefs. Built :core:compileDebugKotlin and :app:assembleRootDebug, installed app-root-debug.apk on 192.168.50.235:5555, injected automation credentials, and verified logcat has no AndroidRuntime/FATAL/AEADBadTagException while AutomationAuth imported credentials and authenticated Letta API requests succeed.
close_reason: Launch crash fixed and verified on Pixel device after credential injection.

title: Run bot security gates and close plaintext-token migration
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Security hardening must be verified end-to-end after token migration and auth changes.

Proposed implementation plan:
Run targeted bot/core/app tests. Inspect persisted JSON fixtures for token redaction. Record commands and results in bead notes.

Dependencies:
Depends on token redaction implementation.

Explicit tests:
All tests from bot security epic plus relevant Gradle unit tests.

Success gate checklist:
- No plaintext bot tokens in DataStore JSON.
- Unauthenticated API server cannot be accidentally enabled.
- Existing tokens migrate.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If migration risk remains, keep bead open and add blocker before deleting compatibility code.
notes: Ran bot security gates. PASS: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerAuthTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerRateLimitTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotTokenPersistenceCharacterizationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotConfigStoreMigrationTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.bot.BotConfigEditViewModelTest'. Attempted full bot suite with cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest; it failed in pre-existing WsBotClient abort coverage (com.letta.mobile.bot.protocol.WsBotClientTest > abort an in-flight request...) tracked by existing bead letta-mobile-cr9a, unrelated to token/auth changes. Security gates met: DataStore JSON persisted via stores is sanitized, existing plaintext tokens migrate to EncryptedPrefsHelper-backed store, blank BotApiServer token is rejected by default, explicit unsafe opt-in required for unauthenticated auth helper, API server starts loopback by default, UI generates blank API token on save and token fields are password-masked.
close_reason: Targeted bot security gates passed; full bot suite blocked by unrelated existing WsBotClient abort bead letta-mobile-cr9a.

title: Move bot tokens from DataStore JSON to encrypted storage
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
remoteToken/apiServerToken/authToken are stored directly in DataStore JSON.

Proposed implementation plan:
Use EncryptedPrefsHelper or a bot-specific encrypted store. Store only token references/redacted metadata in DataStore. Implement one-time migration from plaintext JSON.

Dependencies:
Depends on bot token/auth tests.

Explicit tests:
Plaintext configs migrate to encrypted tokens and DataStore JSON is rewritten without secrets.

Success gate checklist:
- No plaintext bot tokens remain in DataStore JSON after migration.
- Existing users' configured tokens migrate.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep read-old/write-new migration idempotent. Do not delete old token until encrypted write succeeds.
notes: Implemented encrypted bot token storage using EncryptedPrefsHelper-backed SharedPreferencesBotSecureTokenStore. BotConfigStore and BotServerProfileStore hydrate tokens from secure storage, migrate plaintext remote_token/api_server_token/auth_token into secure storage when parsing/saving/getAll, and persist sanitized DataStore JSON with token fields redacted to null. Delete paths remove associated secure tokens. Added pure fake-store tests proving plaintext tokens are moved to secure storage, hydrated back into runtime models, and not present in persisted JSON. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerAuthTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerRateLimitTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotTokenPersistenceCharacterizationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotConfigStoreMigrationTest' (PASS).
close_reason: Bot config/profile tokens now migrate to encrypted storage and persisted JSON is sanitized; targeted tests pass.

title: Require safe BotApiServer authentication defaults
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
BotApiServer.kt authorizes all requests when authToken is blank and API server can be enabled with blank token.

Proposed implementation plan:
Generate API server token by default when enabling. Reject blank token unless explicit unsafe opt-in exists. Bind loopback by default if server config permits; require explicit user setting for LAN exposure.

Dependencies:
Depends on encrypted token store so generated tokens are stored safely.

Explicit tests:
Blank token default rejected; unsafe mode explicitly allows unauthenticated only when configured; valid bearer succeeds; missing/invalid fails; loopback default config test where feasible.

Success gate checklist:
- Unauthenticated API server cannot be accidentally enabled.
- Rate limiting still works.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Allow temporary explicit unsafe escape hatch only behind conspicuous setting and tests.
notes: Implemented safe BotApiServer defaults. BotApiServer now rejects blank tokens by default; unauthenticated mode requires explicit allowUnauthenticated=true and start() defaults host to 127.0.0.1. Auth tests cover default blank rejection, explicit unsafe opt-in, valid bearer success, and invalid/missing token failure; rate-limit tests still pass. BotConfigEditViewModel generates and persists an API server token when enabling/saving with a blank token, and updates UI state with the generated token. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerAuthTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerRateLimitTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.bot.BotConfigEditViewModelTest' (PASS).
close_reason: API server blank-token default is safe, loopback is default host, token generation covered by tests.

title: Redact bot tokens from UI, logs, serialization, and tests
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Bot config UI/ViewModels and serialization paths may display or log token values.

Proposed implementation plan:
Audit UI state, logs, JSON serializers, and test fixtures. Display generated tokens only at creation/explicit reveal if product supports it; otherwise show redacted metadata.

Dependencies:
Depends on safe auth defaults.

Explicit tests:
UI/ViewModel tests for generated token and redacted display; serialization/log fixture assertions contain no token substrings.

Success gate checklist:
- Tokens are redacted from normal UI/log/test snapshots.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep explicit copy/reveal flow if existing UX requires it, protected by user action.
notes: Completed redaction pass for bot tokens. Token persistence tests assert sanitized BotConfig/BotServerProfile JSON does not contain token substrings and token fields serialize as null after store migration. Bot config UI uses PasswordVisualTransformation for remote/API token fields, and API token help text now says blank generates a token rather than allowing unauthenticated access. No token values are logged in BotApiServer/BotConfigStore/BotServerProfileStore; auth rejection logs only client/path. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotTokenPersistenceCharacterizationTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.bot.BotConfigEditViewModelTest' (PASS).
close_reason: Bot tokens are redacted from persisted JSON and token UI remains masked; targeted tests pass.

title: Bot token/auth characterization and migration tests
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
BotConfigStore.kt/BotServerProfileStore.kt persist tokens in DataStore JSON and BotApiServer.kt permits blank-token auth. Tests must lock current and desired behavior before migration.

Proposed implementation plan:
Add failing/characterization tests for plaintext token migration, default blank-token rejection, bearer success/failure, and rate limiting interaction.

Dependencies:
Depends on Room destructive migration fix by work-order policy.

Explicit tests:
Token migration fixture with existing plaintext JSON; BotApiServer auth matrix; rate limiting still applies.

Success gate checklist:
- Tests define desired secure behavior.
- Existing insecure behavior is exposed before code changes.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
No production changes except test fixtures.
notes: Added bot security characterization tests without production behavior changes. BotApiServerAuthTest now explicitly documents current unsafe blank-token behavior while retaining valid/invalid bearer matrix; existing BotApiServerRateLimitTest covers rate limiting. Added BotTokenPersistenceCharacterizationTest documenting plaintext remote_token/api_server_token/auth_token JSON serialization for BotConfig and BotServerProfile. Existing insecure behavior is now exposed by passing characterization tests for follow-up hardening beads to invert. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerAuthTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.api.BotApiServerRateLimitTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotTokenPersistenceCharacterizationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.bot.config.BotConfigStoreMigrationTest' (PASS).
close_reason: Bot plaintext token and blank-auth risks are characterized by tests; targeted bot gate passes.

title: Run Room migration gates and document preservation guarantees
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
After removing destructive migration, data preservation must be verified and documented for future schema changes.

Proposed implementation plan:
Run targeted Room migration tests and relevant Gradle unit tests. Add notes to the bead and/or code comments describing migration policy and schema export convention.

Dependencies:
Depends on removal of destructive migration.

Explicit tests:
- Room migration tests from v1/v2/v3 to latest.
- PendingLocalEntity survival.
- AgentEntity tag preservation.
- Destructive migration guard.

Success gate checklist:
- Linux test command recorded in bead notes.
- All Room success gates met.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If tests fail due to environment only, capture logs and file a blocker; do not close until repeatable gate exists.
notes: Documented production Room migration policy in LettaDatabase KDoc: explicit migrations only, no destructive fallback, PendingLocalEntity contains unrecoverable local image bubbles, schema JSON exported under core/schemas, migration tests in LettaDatabaseMigrationTest. Gates recorded: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseMigrationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' (PASS, covers v1/v2/v3 migration to latest, PendingLocalEntity survival, AgentEntity tag preservation, destructive-fallback guard); cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest (PASS after fallback removal on letta-mobile-yoic.4.4). Room success gates met: exportSchema=true, schemas committed for v3/v4, migrations 1->2, 2->3, 3->4, no production fallbackToDestructiveMigration.
close_reason: Room migration gates passed and preservation policy documented.

title: Enable Room schema export and add forward migrations
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
LettaDatabase.kt lacks committed migration coverage and production builder fallback hides missing migrations.

Proposed implementation plan:
Enable exportSchema in Room Gradle configuration, add schema output to source control if project convention allows, and create explicit migrations from current/historical versions to latest.

Dependencies:
Depends on Room characterization/guard tests.

Explicit tests:
Room migration tests from v1/v2/v3 to latest using exported schemas or reconstructed schemas.

Success gate checklist:
- Schema files are generated/committed or an exception is documented.
- Migration tests pass locally on Linux.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If historical schemas cannot be recovered, create minimal compatibility migrations and document the recovery path in bead notes.
notes: Implemented Room schema export and forward migrations. Changes: core Room KSP schemaLocation points to core/schemas, LettaDatabase exportSchema=true, production builder registers LettaDatabaseMigrations.ALL (fallback remains for letta-mobile-yoic.4.4), added MIGRATION_1_2 for project_bug_reports and MIGRATION_2_3 for pending_local_messages, committed generated latest schema core/schemas/com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabase/3.json, and added reconstructed-schema migration tests for v1/v2/v3 to latest. Historical exported v1/v2 schema files were not available in the repo, so tests reconstruct minimal compatible old schemas from current entity history. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseMigrationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' (PASS).
close_reason: Schema export, forward migrations, generated schema, and migration tests added; targeted Gradle gate passes.

title: Replace AgentEntity comma-joined tags with JSON-safe storage
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
AgentEntity.tagsJson currently uses comma join semantics, losing tags containing commas/special characters.

Proposed implementation plan:
Replace lossy tag serialization with JSON/list converter or JSON string helper. Migrate existing comma-joined data best-effort and preserve future tags exactly.

Dependencies:
Depends on migration/schema infrastructure.

Explicit tests:
AgentEntity tags preserve commas, quotes, unicode, empty list, and legacy comma-joined values through migration and round trip.

Success gate checklist:
- New writes use JSON-safe format.
- Existing data migrates without crashing.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Support legacy parse fallback for old comma-joined rows during one migration cycle.
notes: Implemented JSON-safe AgentEntity tag storage. New writes use kotlinx.serialization JSON arrays via AgentEntity.encodeTags/decodeTags; reads retain legacy comma-joined fallback. Bumped Room DB to v4 and added MIGRATION_3_4 that best-effort rewrites legacy comma-joined agent tags to JSON arrays while preserving existing v3 data such as PendingLocalEntity. Added/updated tests for commas, quotes, unicode, empty list, legacy fallback, and v1/v2/v3-to-latest migration behavior. Gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseMigrationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' (PASS).
close_reason: AgentEntity tags now serialize as JSON with legacy fallback and v3->v4 migration; targeted Room tests pass.

title: Remove fallbackToDestructiveMigration from production DB builder
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Production database builder in LettaDatabase.kt uses fallbackToDestructiveMigration, risking loss of PendingLocalEntity local image messages and other data.

Proposed implementation plan:
Remove fallbackToDestructiveMigration and register real migrations. Ensure test-only builders remain explicit and cannot be confused with production.

Dependencies:
Depends on schema/migration and AgentEntity tag migration beads.

Explicit tests:
- Destructive-config guard passes.
- PendingLocalEntity survives migration.
- Full relevant core/app unit tests pass.

Success gate checklist:
- No fallbackToDestructiveMigration in production DB builder.
- Migration test passes on Linux CI.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Rollback is to restore previous builder only if migration crashes are proven; prefer adding missing migration instead.
notes: Removed fallbackToDestructiveMigration from LettaDatabase production builder; production builder now registers LettaDatabaseMigrations.ALL and builds without destructive fallback. Activated destructive-config guard in LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest. Gates: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseMigrationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' (PASS); cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest (PASS).
close_reason: Production destructive migration fallback removed; guard and full core unit tests pass.

title: Room migration characterization and destructive-config guard tests
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
LettaDatabase.kt production builder currently uses fallbackToDestructiveMigration near line ~28. Before changing the builder, characterize current database configuration and local entity persistence expectations.

Proposed implementation plan:
Add Room/JVM or Android instrumentation test infrastructure as appropriate for this project. Add a guard test that fails if destructive migration is configured for production. Add current-schema/entity round-trip tests for PendingLocalEntity and AgentEntity tags behavior to expose current risk.

Dependencies:
First actionable bead in the architecture hardening epic.

Explicit tests:
- Destructive migration not configured guard (initially may fail until implementation bead).
- PendingLocalEntity current insert/read round trip.
- AgentEntity tags with comma/special chars characterization.

Success gate checklist:
- Tests can run on Linux CI using the chosen Gradle target.
- Current lossy behavior is documented by failing or explicit characterization test before fix.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
No production behavior change unless needed to make test hooks possible. If Room test setup is blocked, file blocker with exact Gradle/runner issue.
notes: Implemented characterization tests in android-compose/core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/local/LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest.kt. Targeted gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' (PASS). Tests document current fallbackToDestructiveMigration risk, PendingLocalEntity Room round trip, and current lossy AgentEntity comma-tag behavior; future not-destructive guard is present but @Ignore until letta-mobile-yoic.4.4 removes the production fallback.
close_reason: Characterization tests and current-risk documentation added; targeted Gradle test passes.

title: Remove Room destructive migration and preserve local data
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/local/LettaDatabase.kt includes AgentEntity, BugReportEntity, and PendingLocalEntity. PendingLocalEntity preserves local image messages across restarts. The database builder uses fallbackToDestructiveMigration near line ~28, risking data loss.

Proposed implementation plan:
Remove fallbackToDestructiveMigration, enable exportSchema, add real migrations from current version forward, commit schema files if convention allows, and audit local entities for lossy fields, especially AgentEntity.tagsJson comma-join.

Dependencies:
Highest-priority ready workstream because this is a data-loss risk. Repository Room expansion depends on this foundation.

Explicit tests:
Room migration tests from v1/v2/v3 to latest; PendingLocalEntity survives migration; AgentEntity tags preserve commas/special chars after JSON/list converter; destructive migration is not configured.

Success gate checklist:
- No destructive migration in production DB builder.
- Migration tests pass on Linux CI.
- Existing local user data is preserved.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Add tests around current schema first. If historical schema is unavailable, reconstruct minimal schemas from current entity history and validate migration compatibility before changing production builder.
notes: Completed Room data-loss hardening. Production builder no longer uses fallbackToDestructiveMigration, exportSchema=true, schema JSON committed for v3/v4, explicit migrations registered for 1->2, 2->3, 3->4, AgentEntity tags now JSON-safe with legacy fallback and v3->v4 conversion, PendingLocalEntity migration survival covered. Gates: targeted Room tests PASS; full :core:testDebugUnitTest PASS.
close_reason: All Room destructive migration risk sub-beads complete and gates passed.

title: Harden bot token storage and API server authentication
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
BotConfig.kt, BotConfigStore.kt, BotServerProfileStore.kt, BotApiServer.kt, and bot config UI/ViewModel files store remoteToken/apiServerToken/authToken in DataStore JSON. BotApiServer authorizes all requests when authToken is blank, and the API server can be enabled with a blank token.

Proposed implementation plan:
Move bot tokens into encrypted storage using EncryptedPrefsHelper or a bot-specific encrypted store; persist only token references/redacted metadata in DataStore JSON; generate an API server token by default when enabling; require explicit unsafe opt-in for unauthenticated server if allowed; bind loopback by default if Ktor/Netty permits; redact tokens from UI/logs/serialization/tests.

Dependencies:
Starts after Room destructive migration fix unless an urgent security release requires parallelization.

Explicit tests:
Token migration from plaintext DataStore to encrypted storage. BotApiServer auth tests for blank-token rejection unless unsafe mode, valid Bearer success, invalid/missing token failure, and rate limiting. UI/ViewModel tests for generated token and redacted display.

Success gate checklist:
- No plaintext bot tokens in DataStore JSON after migration.
- Unauthenticated API server cannot be accidentally enabled.
- Existing users' configured tokens migrate.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Write migration to read old plaintext once, store encrypted, then rewrite redacted config. Keep a compatibility read path only until migration test proves success.
notes: Completed bot/server security hardening. Tokens migrate from DataStore JSON into EncryptedPrefsHelper-backed storage for BotConfig remote/api-server tokens and BotServerProfile auth tokens; persisted store JSON is sanitized with token fields null. BotApiServer rejects blank tokens by default, supports only explicit allowUnauthenticated opt-in at helper/start boundary, and binds loopback by default. Bot config VM generates an API server token when enabled with blank token; UI token fields are password-masked and help text no longer suggests unauthenticated use. Targeted security gates pass; full :bot:testDebugUnitTest currently blocked by unrelated known WsBotClient abort failure letta-mobile-cr9a.
close_reason: All bot security sub-beads complete; targeted security gates pass.

title: Architecture hardening and performance refactor
description:
Problem statement:
Architectural review identified systemic risks across chat orchestration, timeline single-source-of-truth, repository scaling, Room migrations, bot/server security, navigation, chat rendering performance, module boundaries, and model separation. These are refactor-preparation items, not cosmetic cleanup.

Proposed implementation plan:
Create independent, test-first workstreams for each reviewed area. Preserve behavior, add characterization/regression tests, introduce focused abstractions behind existing behavior, migrate callers, then delete legacy paths only after gates pass.

Dependencies:
Child beads define the concrete sequencing. Recommended priority order is Room data-loss risk, bot/server security, chat ViewModel characterization/extraction, timeline migration, repository cache/paging, chat render performance, navigation cleanup, module boundaries, and DTO/domain/entity separation.

Explicit tests:
Each child bead lists targeted unit/integration/performance/migration tests. Existing app unit tests must remain green.

Success gate checklist:
- No destructive Room migrations.
- No plaintext bot tokens in DataStore/logs.
- AdminChatViewModel becomes a thin orchestrator over tested collaborators.
- TimelineRepository becomes the normal client-mode message source of truth.
- Large collections avoid fetch-all patterns.
- Navigation classification is deterministic and tested.
- Chat render performance improves measurably.
- designsystem no longer depends on core/data models.
- API/domain/local/UI models are separated where risk is highest.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Land small independent beads. Prefer additive abstractions and tests first. Roll back by keeping old caller paths until replacement behavior is proven, then remove legacy code in dedicated cleanup beads.
notes:
Bootstrap handoff: P0 launch crash letta-mobile-mnh4 is closed. Root cause was unreadable Android Keystore/Tink EncryptedSharedPreferences after reinstall/profile work. Fix is in core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/util/EncryptedPrefsHelper.kt: recover by deleting letta_secure_prefs and the default master key alias, then recreate secure prefs. Verified :core:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleRootDebug, installed root debug APK on 192.168.50.235:5555, injected automation credentials, confirmed no AndroidRuntime/FATAL/AEADBadTagException, app PID alive, MainActivity task/window present, AutomationAuth imported credentials, and authenticated requests were emitted. Resume with: bd show letta-mobile-yoic && git status --short --branch
Handoff from letta-mobile-ujs8 (Edit Agent UX). Changes committed and pushed. Ready for architecture hardening.
close_reason: All 11 child workstreams complete: AdminChatViewModel decomposition (yoic.1), TimelineRepository SSoT (yoic.2), repository paging (yoic.3), Room migration safety (yoic.4), bot/server auth hardening (yoic.5), chat nav cleanup (yoic.6), render perf (yoic.7), designsystem boundary (yoic.8), API/domain/entity separation (yoic.9), TimelineSyncLoop stream-ingest (yoic.10), reconcile-fetch collaborator (yoic.11).

title: AdminChatViewModelTest: missing notificationReplyHandler in createViewModel() — compile error
description:
## Root Cause
`createViewModel()` in AdminChatViewModelTest.kt (line 254-269) passes 14 constructor args but the VM now requires 15 — `notificationReplyHandler: NotificationReplyHandler` (added line 341 of AdminChatViewModel.kt) is missing.

## Reproduction
```bash
cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugUnitTestKotlin
```

## Expected
Tests compile.

## Fix
1. Add `private lateinit var notificationReplyHandler: NotificationReplyHandler` field to test class
2. Initialize in `setup()`: `notificationReplyHandler = mockk(relaxed = true)`
3. Add to `createViewModel()` factory args

## Files
- `app/src/test/.../AdminChatViewModelTest.kt`

title: LazyColumn double-measure crash on Measurable during streaming fling
description:
## Problem

On clean debug build, the app crashes during streaming with:

```
FATAL EXCEPTION: main
java.lang.IllegalStateException: measure() may not be called multiple times on the same Measurable.
  at LazyListMeasuredItemProvider.getAndMeasure-0kLqBqw(LazyListMeasuredItemProvider.kt:54)
  at LazyListMeasureKt.measureLazyList-pIk1_oM(LazyListMeasure.kt:224)
  at DefaultFlingBehavior$performFling$2.invokeSuspend
```

Triggered by the combination of streaming content changes (animateContentSize in RunBlock, ChatMessageItem, StreamingMarkdownText) and a fling/scroll animation. The LazyColumn tries to measure the same item twice within one measurement pass because the fling animation requests measurement while content is still settling from a size change.

## Reproduction
- Trigger a multi-tool-call message send
- Scroll during streaming (fling)
- Crash occurs

## Suspect areas
- RunBlock.kt:112: animateContentSize on the outer Column
- ChatMessageComponents.kt:319-325: animateContentSize on bubble surface
- StreamingMarkdownText.kt: reaction to content changes at 50ms paint cadence
- ChatMessageList.kt:278: LazyColumn with reverseLayout=true

## Mitigation
- Temporarily suppress animateContentSize when isStreaming is true (already gated for isPinching)
- Or: increase the animateContentSize duration to let fling animation settle before content animation starts

## Related
- letta-mobile-d2z6: original animateContentSize easing changes
- letta-mobile-5e0f.r2: pinch suppression of animateContentSize
- letta-mobile-1fa2: render-count probes blocked by this crash
close_reason: Fixed: end-of-text fence commit in findLastSafeBoundary. When code fences (mermaid) close at end-of-text with no trailing \\n\\n, commit everything instead of leaving it in the plain-text tail. Eliminated duplicate bubble during stream→complete transition.

title: Add ChatMessageItem and RunBlock render-count probes
description: All data pipeline instrumentation shows NO duplication. Render model is clean: input=35, renderItems=34, assistantCount=26 stays constant across entire stream. Yet user sees final assistant message echoed multiple times. Duplication is at Compose rendering level. Add SideEffect probes in ChatMessageItem.kt and RunBlock.kt to log message ID, role, contentLen on every composition. Grep for final assistant ID to count actual renderings.
close_reason: Probes executed. ChatRenderModel clean (34 items, 26 assistants, 0 key collisions). ChatMsgItem logged 72 assistant / 35 user renders. RunBlock confirmed per-message recomposition via animateContentSize suppression. No Compose-level duplication found.

This is a clear regression of previously patched fresh-route isolation/optimistic prompt behavior. Need fresh-session investigation using git history and Pixel 9 Pro logs.

This is NOT complete: user confirmed responses now arrive, but the prompt/user bubble is still not clearly visible immediately on fresh Client Mode send. Next work should start from bd34ef22 and focus on the optimistic UI/timeline observer clobbering path in AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode/startTimelineObserver.

Need inspect Pixel logs, verify installed variant and route conversation id, and fix bootstrap/timeline handling without reintroducing duplicate user bubble.

Added blocked-create regression assertions for immediate composer clear, single optimistic bubble during bootstrap, and single timeline-backed user + assistant after completion. title: [lettabot][proxy] REST proxy for /v1/conversations endpoints
description:
Lettabot exposes /api/v1/conversations endpoints that proxy to Letta with auth injection. Android never holds the Letta API key — it only authenticates to lettabot.

Endpoints to add on lettabot:
- GET  /api/v1/conversations            → proxy GET /v1/conversations (with optional agent_id filter)
- GET  /api/v1/conversations/{id}       → proxy GET /v1/conversations/{id}
- GET  /api/v1/conversations/{id}/messages → proxy GET .../messages
- POST /api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X → proxy POST /v1/conversations?agent_id=X (creates fresh)

Auth model:
- Android sends X-Api-Key (or bearer) for lettabot
- Lettabot injects Letta API key via env (LETTA_API_KEY) when calling Letta
- Lettabot enforces tenant scoping if multi-user (out of scope for v1)

Optional optimizations:
- Cache GET /v1/conversations responses briefly (5s) to reduce Letta load on chat-list scroll
- Stream-friendly: GET .../messages with proper pagination (after/before cursors)
close_reason:
Implemented conversations REST proxy in lettabot.

Files:
- src/api/conversations-proxy.ts (238 lines) — proxy module with auth + routing
- src/api/conversations-proxy.test.ts (442 lines, 19 tests, all green)
- src/api/server.ts — wired in via tryHandleConversationsProxy()

Routes:
- GET    /api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X
- POST   /api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X
- GET    /api/v1/conversations/:id
- GET    /api/v1/conversations/:id/messages

Auth: requires lettabot X-Api-Key or Authorization: Bearer; injects LETTA_API_KEY upstream so clients never hold Letta credentials.

Test suite: 1220/1220 lettabot tests passing; tsc clean.

Commit: 4264fa5 on feat/partial-json-parser.

title: [lettabot][routing] Resolve agent_id from conversation_id (cached lookup)
description:
When an inbound WS frame carries conversation_id, lettabot must resolve which agent_id owns that conversation so it picks the right SDK session from the per-agent pool (sibling .3).

Implementation:
- LRU cache: Map<conversationId, agentId> with TTL (e.g. 1h)
- On cache miss: GET /v1/conversations/{id} from Letta, read .agent_id, cache it
- Conversations are immutable w.r.t. agent_id (Letta enforces) → cache is safe long-term
- Cache invalidation: on conversation deletion event (if Letta exposes one) or TTL only

Performance:
- Lookup must be sub-ms hot path (single map get)
- Cold path: one HTTP roundtrip to Letta; only happens once per conv per process lifetime

Depends on: w2hx.2 (WS protocol delivers conversation_id), w2hx.3 (per-agent pool consumes resolved agent_id).
close_reason:
Resolver implemented in lettabot src/api/conversation-agent-resolver.ts (commit 2ae141e on feat/partial-json-parser).

- LRU+TTL Map cache (1h success / 30s negative, 10k entries default)
- O(1) hot path; one HTTP roundtrip per conv per process on cold miss
- In-flight coalescing prevents stampedes
- Negative cache for 404s; transient errors don't poison
- prime() / invalidate() / clear() / stats()
- getSharedResolver() singleton for the gateway

Tests: 15/15 in src/api/conversation-agent-resolver.test.ts. Full api suite 264/264.

Ready to be wired into ws-gateway alongside w2hx.3 (per-agent pool).

title: [test] Multi-agent no-bleedover acceptance test
description:
End-to-end test that nails down the architectural guarantee.

Scenario:
1. Configure global LettaBot connection
2. Create two chats: chat A bound to agent X, chat B bound to agent Y
3. Send message M1 in chat A
4. While M1 is mid-stream, send message M2 in chat B
5. Verify:
   - chat A timeline contains M1 and X's response only
   - chat B timeline contains M2 and Y's response only
   - logcat shows two distinct (chatId, agentId, conversationId) tuples
   - lettabot logs (journalctl) show two distinct upstream Letta sessions
   - Zero shared conversation_ids between the two chats

This is the regression test for every bleedover bug we have closed (flk6.sendMessage, forceNew, ConversationManager cache, screenAgentId mismatch).

Should run on real device + emulator. Build into verify-release-gates if feasible.
close_reason: Two-WsBotClient JVM acceptance test landed (commit 9327da3). Two MockWebServers + two clients, deferred barriers force chunk interleaving on the wire (A1, B1, A2, B2, doneA, doneB). Asserts every chunk in client A's flow is tagged conv-A and every chunk in client B's flow is tagged conv-B; terminal chunks carry distinct agentIds. Pairs with the AdminChatViewModelTest.w2hx_5 test from w2hx.5 (VM-to-repository boundary) — together they pin the no-bleedover guarantee end-to-end. :bot 71/71 green. Instrumented release-gate variant deferred (would gate against live lettabot).

title: [android] WS receive demux by conversation_id
description:
WsBotClient receive path demuxes outbound frames by conversation_id so each ChatScreen ViewModel only sees its own chat's events.

Current state: assumes one bound agent → routes everything to one ViewModel.

Target:
- WsBotClient maintains Map<conversationId, MutableSharedFlow<Event>>
- On every inbound frame: read frame.conversation_id, dispatch to that flow
- AdminChatViewModel subscribes to events for chat.conversation_id only
- Multiple ViewModels coexist; concurrent streams from two chats don't cross-pollute

Depends on: w2hx.2 (frames must carry conversation_id).
close_reason: Receive-side demux keyed on conversation_id with request_id fallback. activeRoutes/pendingRoutes maps replace single volatile pair. Routes auto-promote on first conv-id, rekey on mid-stream swap, reap on close. +3 demux tests; :bot 70/70, :app, :core green. Commit 89b1440 on feat/flk1-keyed-prefix-blocks.

Delete the forceNew flag entirely. It was added as a band-aid to dodge bleedover; the real fix is the chatId-as-routing-key model.

title: [android] Remove (or rekey) ConversationManager — chat row stores conv_id
description:
ConversationManager is currently a process-wide singleton keyed only on agentId. This caused the cache-pollution bug where loadMessagesInternal saw a stale activeConversationId.

Pick one:
- Option A (preferred): DELETE ConversationManager. Each chat row stores its own conversation_id (sibling .5). The Android UI reads conv_id directly from chat state. No singleton, no cache.
- Option B: Rekey ConversationManager to Map<conversationId, ChatState> as a per-chat UI cache only. Never keyed on agentId.

Whichever option: the bug 'cached entry from agent-list pollutes new-chat resolve' must be structurally impossible.
close_reason:

- core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/ConversationManagerTest.kt

ALSO:

title: [android] Chat row stores conversation_id — Letta conv-xxx IS the routing key
description:
Chat row stores conversation_id (Letta conv-xxx) as its primary identity. agent_id is a property of the conversation itself (via Letta) — not stored on the chat row.

Changes:
- Chat entity: add conversation_id column (string, unique, non-null)
- 'New chat' flow: show agent picker → POST {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X → store returned conv-xxx as chat row
- ChatScreen / AdminChatViewModel: use chat.conversation_id for all sends/receives
- Chat list: hydrate from GET {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations; can group/filter by agent locally using returned agent_id metadata

Migration of existing chats: see sibling .9.

Acceptance: opening two chats with different agents shows correct agent header in each (resolved from conversation metadata); sending in chat A never affects chat B.
close_reason:
Closed via accepted-as-design: this app has no Chat database entity; chats are nav routes keyed on (agentId, conversationId?). The conversation_id IS already the primary identity flowing into the timeline send path:

  - admin path: ConversationsViewModel + AllConversationsRepository hydrate from GET /v1/conversations and key on conversation.id (via w2hx.11 proxy).
  - new-chat flow: createConversation(agentId) → conv-xxx returned and used as the route arg.
  - send path: AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaTimeline routes via timelineRepository.sendMessage(conversationId, ...) — never via agentId.
  - agent header: resolved from agentRepository.getAgent(agentId) which comes from conversation metadata (the agent_id on the Conversation row) on the admin side.

The residual bound-agent in-memory map (ConversationManager.activeConversationIds[agentId]) is the last bound-agent residue and is captured explicitly by w2hx.6.

Acceptance test landed: AdminChatViewModelTest.`w2hx_5 two chats different agents do not bleed over conversation routing` — opens two chats with different agents, confirms each header resolves from its own agent metadata, sends in chat A only ever route through conv-A, and chat B's state is untouched. PASSED on commit 833b701.

Schema migration framing was inapplicable here; .9's "migration of existing bound-agent chats → Letta conversations" still has work to do (see DEPENDS).

title: [lettabot][gateway] Per-agent WS session pool keyed on agentId
description:
Per-agent Letta SDK session pool on lettabot. KEEP — Letta server only has server-side tools. lettabot is the tool execution host (skills, Bash, FS, MCP). Each agent needs its own SDK session here so tool calls route correctly.

Mirror matrix-tuwunel GatewayClient._pool (src/letta/ws_gateway_client.py):
- Dict[agent_id, _PoolEntry]
- Idle eviction (default 1h)
- Health-check ping before reuse
- in_use flag prevents concurrent recv on same socket
- Auto-reconnect once on stale connection
- Max-connections cap with LRU eviction

Reference: /opt/stacks/matrix-tuwunel-deploy/src/letta/ws_gateway_client.py L33-430

The existing src/api/agent-session-manager.ts in lettabot already does most of this — verify it keys on agent_id and add gaps if any.
close_reason:
## Closure: w2hx.3 — per-agent WS session pool

**Branch:** feat/partial-json-parser (lettabot)  
**Commit:** c3f1d16

### What shipped
AgentSessionManager refactored from `Map<connectionId, ManagedSession>` to `Map<connectionId, ConnectionPool>` where each pool holds `agents: Map<agentId, ManagedSession>` (insertion-order LRU) plus an `activeAgentId` pointer.

### Behavior
- **Reuse fast path**: re-opening the same agentId on a connection returns the cached `initMessage` — no `createSession`, no `initialize` round-trip. Saves ~1-3s on every Android picker switch back to a recently-used agent.
- **Multiplex**: a connection can hold N agents warm. Switching agents no longer kills the previous SDK subprocess.
- **LRU eviction**: `maxAgentsPerConnection` (default 4); LRU non-busy entry evicted on overflow. If every entry is busy, cap is exceeded rather than tearing down an in-flight stream.
- **forceNew**: still honored — closes cached entry and spawns fresh.
- **Recovery paths** (corrupted conversation, 404 conversation_missing, fatal auth) updated to use `removeFromPool` + `getActive` helpers — semantics preserved.
- **Idle sweep**: per-(conn, agent) granular eviction.
- **`close(connectionId)`**: reaps every agent in the pool, not just the active one.

### Public API
Externally unchanged. `getInfo`, `sendAndStream`, `abort`, `has` all resolve through `pool.activeAgentId`. Existing ws-gateway and bridge callers required zero changes.

### New telemetry
- `poolSize(connectionId): number` — agents warm in a pool
- Logs: "Reusing warm session...", "Discarding stale cached session...", "Evicting LRU agent..."

### Tests
- New `src/api/agent-session-manager.pool.test.ts` — 11 tests covering reuse, multiplex, LRU recency under touch, forceNew bypass, cross-connection isolation, idle sweep, getInfo/has semantics, and conversationId-mismatch eviction.
- Existing recovery tests (3) and ws-gateway e2e (67) green.
- **Project-wide: 1253/1253 passing** (was 1242, +11).
- `tsc --noEmit` clean.

### Reference
- Architecture mirrors `/opt/stacks/matrix-tuwunel-deploy/src/letta/ws_gateway_client.py` L33-430 (pool-of-pools w/ active pointer + LRU)

### Epic progress
w2hx 5/12 done (.1 ✓ .2 ✓ .3 ✓ .11 ✓ .12 ✓). Next: .4 (gateway client `select_agent` op for picker round-trip) or .5 (Android session resume continuity).

title: [lettabot][schema] chats table — source of truth for (chatId, agentId) → conversationId
description:
Add chats table on the lettabot (server) side, mirroring matrix-tuwunel's room_conv_db (src/core/conversation_service.py).

Schema:
- chat_id (string, PK component) — generated by Android app on 'New chat'
- agent_id (string, PK component) — Letta agent ID for this chat
- conversation_id (string, nullable) — Letta conversation ID; null until first message
- created_at, last_message_at (timestamps)
- UNIQUE (chat_id, agent_id)

Lookup function get_or_create_chat_conversation(chat_id, agent_id) -> conversation_id:
- if row exists and Letta conversation still valid → return existing conversation_id
- if row exists but Letta conversation 404 → recreate, update row
- if row missing → create Letta conversation, insert row, return new id

Reference: matrix-tuwunel get_or_create_room_conversation in /opt/stacks/matrix-tuwunel-deploy/src/core/conversation_service.py:203
close_reason: Superseded — Letta has native /v1/conversations API. Conversations are per-agent, isolated, with their own message history. conv-xxx IS the chatId; no separate mapping table needed on lettabot. See updated epic body.

title: [lettabot][protocol] WS protocol v2 — conversation_id in every frame
description:
Refactor /api/v1/agent-gateway WS protocol so every frame carries conversation_id (no chatId; conv-xxx IS the chatId in Letta's native model).

Inbound frames:
- session_start: { type, conversation_id } — agent_id resolved server-side from Letta
- message: { type, conversation_id, content, request_id, source? }

Outbound frames echo conversation_id so Android demuxes correctly:
- session_init: { type, conversation_id, agent_id }
- chunk/event/done: { type, conversation_id, ... }

Server flow on inbound message:
1. Look up conversation_id → agent_id (cache; fall back to Letta GET /v1/conversations/{id})
2. Acquire Letta SDK session for that agent_id (sibling .3 per-agent pool)
3. Stream to Letta with conversation_id
4. Tag every outbound event with conversation_id

Replaces the old chatId+agentId envelope plan. No mapping table on lettabot.
close_reason:
Protocol v2 shipped on lettabot side (commit 67eb82a, branch feat/partial-json-parser).

session_start (inbound):
- agent_id is now optional
- conversation_id-only path: resolves agent_id via ConversationAgentResolver from w2hx.12, primes the cache on success
- both supplied: validates they match (mismatch -> BAD_MESSAGE before any session is opened)
- neither: BAD_MESSAGE
- legacy agent_id-only path still works (resolver not called)

message (inbound):
- optional conversation_id; mismatch with active session -> BAD_MESSAGE with request_id echoed

session_init / stream / result (outbound):
- already carry conversation_id (from prior flk.5 work)

Tests: +7 e2e tests in src/api/ws-gateway.e2e.test.ts ('protocol v2' describe). 271/271 in src/api, 1242/1242 project-wide. TypeScript clean.

Unblocks: w2hx.7 (drop forceNew) and w2hx.8 (Android demux by conversation_id) on the Android side.

Still TODO server-side: w2hx.3 (per-agent session pool keyed on agentId) — currently sessions are 1-per-WS-conn; the pool will allow concurrent agents over a single connection.

title: [epic] LettaBot multi-agent transport — Letta-native conversations + per-agent tool-host pool
description:
# [epic] LettaBot multi-agent transport — Letta-native conversations + per-agent tool-host pool

**Type:** epic
**Priority:** P0
**Labels:** client-mode, architecture, blocker

## Problem

The Android app's Client Mode is built on a "single bound agent per bot" model. Every chat in the app sends to that bound agent. This is the source of every bleedover bug we have chased (forceNew patches, ConversationManager cache, screenAgentId vs wireAgentId). Adding `forceNew` was a band-aid; the real fix is dropping bound-agent entirely.

## Two architectural insights

### 1. Letta has native conversation isolation
Letta's `/v1/conversations` API gives us per-agent isolated message threads with their own history. `conv-xxx` IS the chatId. We don't need to invent chatIds, and lettabot doesn't need a chats table — Letta is already the source of truth.

Endpoints:
- `POST /v1/conversations?agent_id=X` → fresh conversation (replaces "New chat" + forceNew)
- `GET /v1/conversations?agent_id=X` → chat list per agent
- `GET /v1/conversations/{id}/messages` → history
- `POST /v1/conversations/{id}/messages` → send (streams)

### 2. Lettabot is NOT a removable transport
The Letta server runs in Docker and only exposes server-side tools. **Skills, Bash, file system access, MCP integrations** all live on lettabot's host. When a Letta agent makes a tool call, lettabot is the execution environment — results stream back through it. So we cannot bypass lettabot and have Android talk to Letta directly; we'd lose the entire tool surface.

This means lettabot still needs:
- A per-agent SDK session pool (one Letta SDK session per agent, since tools execute here)
- WS gateway between Android ↔ lettabot
- All current skill/Bash/FS/MCP plumbing

What lettabot does NOT need:
- A chats table
- Chat→conversation resolution logic
- Any "default agent" concept
- forceNew

## Target model

```
Android chat list:
  GET {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations
  → lettabot proxies GET /v1/conversations (across all agents)
  → returns conv-xxx list with agent_id metadata

Android open chat:
  GET {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations/{conv_id}/messages
  → lettabot proxies, returns history

Android new chat with agent X:
  POST {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X
  → lettabot creates fresh Letta conversation, returns conv-xxx

Android send:
  WS frame: { type: "message", conversation_id: "conv-xxx", content }
  → lettabot:
     1. resolve agent_id from conversation_id (cache or Letta lookup)
     2. acquire/reuse SDK session for that agent_id (per-agent pool)
     3. send to Letta with that conversation_id
     4. tool calls execute locally on lettabot (skills/Bash/FS/MCP)
     5. stream events back tagged with conv_id
```

## Comparison to old model

| Today (broken) | Target |
|---|---|
| `BotConfig.agentId` is a bound agent | No bound agent. BotConfig is just a tool-host endpoint (URL + key) |
| `ClientModeChatSender` sends to bot's bound agent | Sends to `chat.conversation_id`; agent comes from Letta's conv metadata |
| `screenAgentId` is "routing metadata" | Removed. conversation_id is the only routing key |
| One Letta conversation per bot | Each chat IS a Letta conversation (`conv-xxx`) |
| `forceNew` plumbed through 5 layers | Removed. New chat = `POST /v1/conversations` |
| ConversationManager keyed on `agentId` | Removed (chat row stores conv_id) or rekeyed to conv_id |
| WS session pool per bot | WS session pool per `agentId` on lettabot (for tool execution) |
| Android holds Letta API key | Android holds lettabot API key only; lettabot proxies with Letta key |

## Why this is fundamental

- Every bleedover bug is a symptom of bound-agent.
- Client Mode currently cannot represent two open chats with different agents without bleedover by design.
- Patches will keep regressing until conversation_id is the routing primary key.

## Sub-issues

**lettabot side:**
- ✓ ~~w2hx.1 chats table~~ (closed — superseded by Letta-native)
- w2hx.2 WS protocol v2 (frames carry conversation_id)
- w2hx.3 per-agent SDK session pool keyed on agentId (for tool execution)
- w2hx.11 lettabot REST proxy (`/v1/conversations` passthrough with auth injection)
- w2hx.12 lettabot resolves agent_id from conversation_id (cached lookup)

**android side:**
- w2hx.4 drop bound-agent from BotConfig
- w2hx.5 chat row stores conversation_id (not chatId+agentId tuple)
- w2hx.6 remove ConversationManager (or rekey to conv_id)
- w2hx.7 delete forceNew plumbing
- w2hx.8 WS receive demux by conversation_id

**migration + test:**
- w2hx.9 migrate existing bound-agent chats → conversation rows
- w2hx.10 multi-agent no-bleedover acceptance test

## Acceptance criteria (epic)

- `BotConfig` has no `agentId` field
- Two chats with different agents in same app session can stream concurrently with zero cross-talk
- "New chat" calls `POST /v1/conversations` and gets fresh conv-xxx — no forceNew flag exists anywhere
- WS frames carry `conversation_id` only; lettabot resolves `agent_id` server-side
- lettabot has per-agent SDK session pool (tool execution unaffected)
- Android holds only lettabot API key (Letta key never leaves lettabot host)
- All skills, Bash, FS, MCP tools still work end-to-end through Android
- `grep -r forceNew app/` returns zero results
- Acceptance test (w2hx.10) passes: two-agent concurrent stream, no overlap

## Out of scope

- Per-thread isolation (Matrix threading equivalent)
- Per-user conversations in DMs (Android is single-user-per-device)
- Multi-bot config (one global lettabot per app for now)

title: Verify mobile WS URL still includes ?progressive_tool_calls=1
description:
Server-side WS gateway changed default behavior (lettabot-uww series): non-opted-in clients now receive a single terminal tool_call frame per invocation instead of N progressive snapshots. Mobile depends on the opt-in ?progressive_tool_calls=1 query param to keep partial-JSON tool-card animations. If we silently lose this param, partial-JSON UX disappears with no error — just degraded experience. This is a single-source-of-truth verification.

Why P0: blocks the rest of the verification chain (#3 wscat, #2 tool_call_id key check, #4 status-running defense-in-depth audit). If item 1 fails, item 4 becomes a real risk; if item 1 holds, item 4 is just paperwork.

## Context
Server contract: /opt/stacks/lettabot/docs/channel-adapter-contract.md §1 (default), §4 (opt-in). Gate impl: /opt/stacks/lettabot/src/api/ws-gateway.ts ~line 454-461. Handoff doc: /opt/stacks/lettabot/docs/handoffs/letta-mobile-progressive-tool-calls.md. Once URL confirmed, item #3 (wscat smoke test) validates server side end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Grep mobile repo for WS connect URL construction (likely in network/ws client layer).
2. Confirm progressive_tool_calls=1 is appended for the mobile build.
3. Confirm there is no env/build-flag path that silently drops it (e.g. release vs debug).
4. Document the exact file:line where the param is set so future audits are O(1).
notes:
VERIFIED ✅

**Single source of truth:** android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/core/RemoteBotSession.kt:80
```
BotConfig.Transport.WS -> WsBotClient(
    baseUrl = resolvedProfile.baseUrl,
    apiKey = resolvedProfile.authToken,
    progressiveToolCalls = true,
)
```

**URL construction site:** android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/protocol/WsBotClient.kt:589-620 (normalizeWebSocketUrl + mergeQuery). Idempotent, preserves existing query params, lowercase-safe dedup.

**Other WsBotClient call sites (non-test) audited:**
- ClientModeConnectionTester.kt:14 — pre-flight probe (getStatus + ensureGatewayReady), no chat traffic, progressive flag not needed (default false is correct).
- ClientModeRemoteAgentResolver.kt:11 — pre-flight probe (getStatus only), same reasoning.

**No env/build-flavor branching.** progressiveToolCalls = true is hardcoded for the production WS chat path. No way for it to silently flip off.

Unblocks: wshk, 8jzf, ank6.

title: Client Mode: assistant chunks rendered as replacement, not concatenation (vu6a regression — wire is delta, not snapshot)
description:
## Symptom

User-facing: each streaming assistant chunk REPLACES the bubble's content with just that chunk's text. The user sees flashing fragments mid-stream. Final visible bubble shows only the LAST chunk's content. Reported by Emmanuel 2026-04-25 on Pixel 2 XL Client Mode session against http://192.168.50.90:8407.

## Root cause

vu6a (closed earlier same day) inverted the assumption — it rewrote the chunk handlers to treat BotStreamChunk.text as a SNAPSHOT (full accumulated content per chunk) rather than a DELTA (just the new fragment). That decision was based on a synthetic test feeding ['Hel','Hello','Hello world'] — pretend-snapshot data that didn't reflect the real wire format.

The actual lettabot WS gateway emits DELTAS. Verified live tonight via :cli:run wsstream against http://localhost:8407: 56 frames, each frame's content is a 25–112-char fragment, assembled correct text is 3251 chars (sum of fragments).

Three sites in AdminChatViewModel.kt encode the wrong assumption:
- L1314–1346 handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline ASSISTANT branch: transform { existing.copy(content = snapshot) } → should append
- L1229 same function REASONING branch: reasoningContent = snapshot → should append
- L1448–1460 handleClientModeStreamChunkLegacy else branch: append = false → should be true

The non-Client-Mode SSE path through TimelineSyncLoop.ingestStreamEvent line 1117–1138 IS correct (concatenates deltas). Confirmed via on-device telemetry: streamSubscriber.merged shows mergedLen = oldLen + newLen for every frame.

## Evidence

1. wsstream trace (lettabot direct, 56 frames, all APPEND branch, sum = 3251 chars assembled correctly)
2. On-device logcat 2026-04-25 18:42:13 — 10 streamSubscriber.merged events all with mergedLen = oldLen + newLen exactly; one SseParser.RX line shows content=':1314 update the test to use' which is clearly a delta fragment
3. vu6a regression test client mode multi-chunk text stream renders concatenated assistant bubble feeds fake snapshots — does not exercise real delta wire

## Fix

1. Revert snapshot-semantics in the three sites — use concatenation
2. Replace vu6a regression test data with delta-shaped data ['Hel', 'lo ', 'world'] → expect 'Hello world'
3. Add wsstream-derived golden test from a real frame sequence
4. Audit reasoning + tool-call paths for same wrong assumption

## Acceptance criteria

1) Long prompt in Client Mode → bubble grows by appending each delta, never shrinks/replaces
2) Final bubble content equals concatenation of all chunk text values for that uuid
3) New regression test using real delta data passes; old vu6a test updated and still passes
4) wsstream golden test passes
5) Reasoning content also concatenates correctly
close_reason: Already implemented in current main: AdminChatViewModel appends Client Mode assistant/reasoning delta chunks, includes wsstream lv3e golden fixture and regression tests. Validation: :app:compileDebugKotlin passed; :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests WsBotClientLifecycleTest passed. App unit test task is currently blocked by unrelated stale test compile errors in ChatScreenAutoScrollTest, ConfigListViewModelTest, and ConversationsViewModelTest.

title: Client Mode: agent-switch socket close treated as unexpected disconnect, kills next stream
description:
When WsBotClient.ensureSession detects activeAgentId != requestedAgentId, it deliberately closes the socket with code 1000 reason 'switching agent' (line 279), then calls openSocketLocked() to reconnect. But the okhttp listener's onClosed (line 620-622) calls handleUnexpectedDisconnect(null) unconditionally — there's no isSwitchingAgent flag analogous to isUserClosing.

Result: the deliberate close is treated as an unexpected disconnect, which:
  1. Sends RequestSignal.Failure to any in-flight requestChannel
  2. Triggers reconnect logic in handleUnexpectedDisconnect's bottom half
  3. Races with the explicit openSocketLocked() call from ensureSession
  
Observed in logcat (Pixel 2 XL):
  WsBotClient: WebSocket closed (1000): switching agent
  RemoteBotSession: BotGatewayException: WebSocket closed unexpectedly
    at WsBotClient.handleUnexpectedDisconnect(WsBotClient.kt:464)
  WsBotClient: Connected to lettabot WebSocket gateway   <- reconnect 1
  WsBotClient: Connected to lettabot WebSocket gateway   <- reconnect 2 (race)

User-facing symptom: send a Client Mode message that triggers an agent switch, the message bubble appears but no response arrives — not even the typing indicator flashes (no chunks ever reach AdminChatViewModel because the WS errored before the request was even submitted on the new session).

Fix: add an isSwitchingAgent flag, set true in ensureSession before calling socket.close(1000, 'switching agent'), check it in onClosed. When set, skip handleUnexpectedDisconnect — the caller is responsible for the reconnect.

This bug compounds with hf93/vu6a but is the actual root cause of Emmanuel's currently-broken flow on a freshly-installed APK.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Sending a Client Mode message that triggers an agent switch successfully receives the response. 2) onClosed differentiates between deliberate agent-switch close and unexpected close. 3) No more racing reconnects in logcat after agent switch.
notes:
Wire-level harness (WsBotClientLifecycleTest) caught a second layer the original isSwitchingAgent-in-onClosed fix did not cover.

Root cause was two-fold:
  1) onClosed treated deliberate agent-switch close as unexpected disconnect (covered by original fix).
  2) okhttp's reader-thread fires onFailure (EOF) on the OLD socket AFTER ensureSession has already opened the NEW socket. That residual onFailure called handleUnexpectedDisconnect, which fed RequestSignal.Failure to the in-flight request on the NEW connection, killing it before any chunks arrived. THIS was Emmanuel's flashing-thinking-then-nothing symptom.

Final fix: identity-based retired-socket tracking. ensureSession adds the closing socket to a WeakHashMap-backed retired set BEFORE close(). onClosed and onFailure both call isResidualSocket(webSocket) first and silently drop callbacks from retired sockets. WeakHashMap means retired sockets are GC'd naturally.

Tests added (bot/src/test/.../protocol/WsBotClientLifecycleTest.kt):
  - agent switch closes one socket cleanly and the new agent's stream completes
  - first send into an existing conversationId completes end-to-end
  - two consecutive turns on same agent+conversation reuse the same socket
  - assistant chunks emitted to callers as the gateway sends them (snapshot semantics) [vu6a wire-level]
  - stream that completes with only a result frame surfaces a single done chunk [hf93 wire-level]

Full :bot:testDebugUnitTest + :app:testDebugUnitTest suites pass. New APK installed on Pixel 2 XL.
close_reason: Already implemented in current main: WsBotClient tracks deliberate agent-switch closes with isSwitchingAgent and identity-based retiredSockets so residual onClosed/onFailure callbacks do not call handleUnexpectedDisconnect or poison the next request. Validation: :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests WsBotClientLifecycleTest passed; :app:compileDebugKotlin passed.

title: Client Mode: stream chunks treated as deltas instead of snapshots, causing duplication
description:
AdminChatViewModel's handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline appends each incoming BotStreamChunk.text to the existing assistant bubble, but the gateway protocol emits each chunk's .text as the FULL accumulated text so far (snapshot), not just the new fragment (delta).

Evidence: WsBotClient.kt:570-573 itself accumulates with `append(chunk.text.orEmpty())` when joining into a final string — it treats each chunk as a delta locally, but the gateway upstream treats it as a snapshot. The two layers disagree.

Reproducible via failing test: AdminChatViewModelTest.`client mode multi-chunk text stream renders concatenated assistant bubble` (added in this session).

Output:
  expected: "Hello world"
  actual:   "HelHelloHello world"

Stream sent: ["Hel", "Hello", "Hello world", done].

Root cause: snapshot vs. delta confusion in the chunk-buffering logic. The fix is to either:
  (a) treat .text as a snapshot and overwrite the assistant bubble's content on each chunk (simpler, matches what the gateway is sending today), or
  (b) make the protocol explicit by adding a `is_delta` flag to BotStreamChunk and accumulating only when set.
Option (a) is the minimum-risk fix and matches the WsBotClient reduction pattern at line 572.

This bug compounds with letta-mobile-hf93: a stream that mixes a non-text terminal frame with prior text chunks may render nothing if the snapshot-handling on the final frame wipes the content.

NOTE: this bug doesn't fully explain Emmanuel's 'flash then nothing' symptom (that's hf93). But it does explain why earlier versions appeared to work intermittently — when chunk text had clean prefix relationships, append happened to look right.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Failing regression test `client mode multi-chunk text stream renders concatenated assistant bubble` passes. 2) Final assistant bubble content matches the last text-bearing chunk's .text exactly. 3) No text duplication / fragment concatenation in rendered output.
close_reason: Fixed in handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline + handleClientModeStreamChunkLegacy: BotStreamChunk.text is now treated as a snapshot (replace) rather than a delta (append), matching what the gateway emits. Affects ASSISTANT and REASONING paths in both timeline and legacy handlers; tool calls already merged correctly by toolCallId. Regression test `client mode multi-chunk text stream renders concatenated assistant bubble` passes (was: "HelHelloHello world", now: "Hello world").

title: Client Mode: terminal-only stream produces silent flash with no bubble or error
description:
When the gateway emits only a terminal BotStreamChunk(done=true) with no preceding text/event chunks, AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode silently completes: it clears isStreaming/isAgentTyping but creates no assistant bubble and surfaces no error. User sees their own bubble, the typing indicator briefly flashes, then nothing — the exact symptom Emmanuel reported.

Reproducible via failing test: AdminChatViewModelTest.`client mode terminal-only stream with no text clears typing state and surfaces empty turn` (added in this session).

Output: messages=[(user, hi)] error=null

Root cause: handleClientModeStreamChunk* handlers don't render anything when chunk.text == null && chunk.event == null && chunk.done == true. The terminal frame from WsBotClient (WsBotClient.kt:175-186) carries no text by design — it's a session-end signal — so any stream that has no upstream-agent output is invisible to the user.

Fix direction: when the stream completes and no assistant bubble was ever materialized for this turn, either:
  (a) render an empty assistant placeholder with a subtle 'no response' indicator, or
  (b) surface an error like 'Agent returned no content' on uiState.error.
Option (b) is preferred because it also catches genuine upstream errors that today fall through silently.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Failing regression test `client mode terminal-only stream with no text clears typing state and surfaces empty turn` passes. 2) When the gateway sends only a terminal frame with no text, the user sees either a non-null uiState.error or a visible assistant bubble — never a silent flash.
close_reason: Fixed in AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode: track sawAssistantPayload across the collect loop (counts text-bearing chunks, REASONING/TOOL_CALL/TOOL_RESULT events). When the terminal frame arrives without ever having seen a payload, surface uiState.error='Agent returned no content. Try again or check the agent's status.' Added chunkCarriesAssistantPayload helper. Regression test `client mode terminal-only stream with no text clears typing state and surfaces empty turn` passes.

title: StreamSubscriber hard-loops on EXPIRED run errors — sync frozen at 688s drift, 14 unprocessed
description:
## Discovered via
`make verify-sync` run against live wireless device (Pixel 2 XL) at 2026-04-20 20:30 EDT.
Caught by the new `mge5.5` release gate — first real-world hit.

## Observed
- Client sync cursor frozen at `2026-04-21T00:19:01Z`.
- Server has messages up to `2026-04-21T00:30:29Z`.
- **Drift: 688s. 14 unprocessed messages.**
- 6/6 `sync-drift` samples report `BROKEN`. Gate correctly fails.

## Root cause (from new mge5.6 telemetry)
Logcat `Telemetry/TimelineSync` is emitting `streamSubscriber.error` every ~5s:
```
streamSubscriber.error conversationId=conv-f19f9465-055c-4169-a2e9-7bfd90bdf023
  errorClass=ApiException
  errorMessage={"detail":"EXPIRED: Run was created more than 3 hours ago, and is now expired."}
```

The subscriber's `runStreamSubscriber()` catches `ApiException` and classifies anything
that doesn't contain "No active runs" as a generic network error → backoff cap. On
EXPIRED, the subscriber keeps retrying the same dead run forever and never advances
to the next one.

## Impact
Any conversation whose most-recent-active-run expired on the server (3h old) causes
the subscriber to wedge. hydrate-on-foreground still works, but the push path is
blackhole'd. Users observe: messages appear only on pull-to-refresh.

## Proposed fix
Classify `EXPIRED: Run was created` the same way as NoActiveRunException in the
ApiException branch of `runStreamSubscriber()`. Expired run == no active run from
the subscriber's point of view — the correct behavior is to back off and retry,
letting the next live run open a fresh stream:

```kotlin
val isIdlePattern = msg.contains("No active runs", ignoreCase = true) ||
                    msg.contains("EXPIRED:", ignoreCase = true) ||
                    msg.contains("is now expired", ignoreCase = true)
if (isIdlePattern) { /* idle path */ }
```

## Repro
1. `adb connect 192.168.50.202:5555` (wireless device configured).
2. `cd /opt/stacks/letta-mobile && make verify-sync AGENT=<id> CONV=<id>` against
   a conversation whose last run is > 3h old.
3. Observe 6/6 BROKEN verdicts and `streamSubscriber.error` in logcat.
close_reason: Fixed in ee5a095. Extended the ApiException idle-pattern match in runStreamSubscriber() to include 'EXPIRED:' and 'is now expired' alongside the existing 'No active runs' check. Validated end-to-end on live wireless device (Pixel 2 XL): before the fix, 688s drift + 14 unprocessed + constant streamSubscriber.error every ~5s; after the fix, drift dropped to 43s, unprocessed dropped to 13, 80 idle404 events emitted, 0 errors. Remaining drift is real server-side activity on an active conversation (not a wedge). Added regression unit test 'EXPIRED run ApiException is classified as idle, not error' to TimelineSyncLoopTest. Full core test suite: 19/19 green. Great example of the new mge5.5 gate + mge5.6 telemetry catching and diagnosing a real bug within 60s of first wireless dogfooding.

title: stream merge overwrites attached tool_return + loses args on settling deltas
description:
Emmanuel 2026-04-19 02:27 EDT: 'bash still was approved but now I can't see what the actual tool output was and what the actual request was.'

Root cause: ingestStreamEvent's in-place merge for same-serverId events did confirmed.copy(content = mergedText) which replaced toolCalls with the incoming frame's list — including any null/blank-args delta that arrived AFTER a populated frame — and also wiped any toolReturnContent/approvalDecided that had been attached by prior tool_return ingestion.

Fix (mge5.23):
- Merge toolCalls: keep whichever list has more populated arguments (score = count of non-blank args). Empty new list falls back to existing entirely.
- Preserve approvalDecided (OR-merge), toolReturnContent (coalesce), toolReturnIsError (OR), approvalRequestId (coalesce) across the merge.

Also shipped a programmatic test harness:
1. timelineEventToUiMessage() extracted from AdminChatViewModel to a top-level pure function so the TimelineEvent → UiMessage contract is directly unit-testable.
2. TimelineEventToUiMessageTest — 10 cases covering every failure mode surfaced in this session (missing args, missing output, stuck approve/reject, reasoning visibility, multi-tool batch attachment, null for standalone TOOL_RETURN, OTHER drop).
3. TimelineSyncLoopTest new case: 'streamed approval_request followed by tool_return preserves output on later delta frame' — the specific sequence that produced Emmanuel's visible regression.

These let us validate tool-call UX without physical verification on the phone.
notes: Shipped stream-merge preservation + programmatic VM→UI test harness.

title: reasoning frames dropped — SSE uses 'reasoning' not 'reasoning_message'
description:
Emmanuel 2026-04-19 02:14 EDT: 'We also need to handle subconscious or inner thoughts... I don't see it anymore.'

Same discriminator-drift class as mge5.16 (approval_response) and mge5.18 (tool_return). Logcat sample: 2 RX type=reasoning + 2 RX type=hidden_reasoning + 2 RX type=reasoning_message seen in one window. The SSE /stream endpoint emits the short 'reasoning' while REST uses 'reasoning_message'. Only the long form was in LettaMessageSerializer.selectDeserializer, so every streaming reasoning frame fell into UnknownMessage and was silently dropped from the timeline.

Fix: accept both forms for reasoning, hidden_reasoning, and — preemptively — approval_request (which still only emits long form but could drift any moment given the pattern).

Regression guard: new test 'all known discriminator pairs decode as expected' iterates every known long/short pair and asserts correct subtype. When the server adds yet another short form we catch it in CI before it reaches a user.
notes: Accepted short forms for reasoning/hidden_reasoning/approval_request + added enumerated discriminator regression test covering all known pairs.

title: send-path ingest bypasses tool_return attachment + approval decided logic
description:
Discovered via new programmatic test (letta-mobile-mge5.20). The test 'streamed tool_return attaches to tool_call event and flips approvalDecided' timed out because my tool-return-attachment logic only lives in ingestStreamEvent, but the send() path has its own inline ingestion loop (TimelineSyncLoop.kt lines 362-375) that calls toTimelineEvent+append directly. Any tool call triggered by a phone-initiated send therefore kept its approve/reject UI and never showed the tool output attached to the call bubble.

Fix: the send path now delegates to ingestStreamEvent(message) instead of open-coding the append. This shares ALL ingest logic: tool_return attachment, approvalDecided flip on response or tool_return, content concat heuristic for streaming assistant frames, dedupe.

Also establishes a test harness: FakeSyncApi now underpins 4 new regression tests covering:
1. hydrate attaches tool_return to its ApprovalRequest
2. streamed tool_return attaches to tool_call + flips approvalDecided
3. 'tool_return' short-form discriminator decodes
4. 'approval_response' short-form discriminator decodes

These give us programmatic sign-off on the tool-call chain without needing Emmanuel in the loop for every rebuild.
notes: Shipped send-path delegation + 4 passing regression tests.

title: SSE uses short discriminators 'tool_return' / 'tool_call' — accept both
description:
Symptom: Emmanuel triggered a tool call, the output arrived in the chat but the approve/reject modal did not disappear despite letta-mobile-mge5.17 tool-return causal inference.
