title: [epic] Review round — visual consistency + string cleanup
description: 14 issues found: 1 critical bug, visual inconsistencies, 25 unused strings, 7 hardcoded strings still in screens.

title: Fix agent list pagination — API uses cursor-based, not offset-based
description:
AgentPagingSource sends limit+offset but the Letta API uses before/after cursor pagination. The offset parameter is silently ignored, so only the first page loads.

Fix options:
1. Simple: Use refreshAgents() with limit=1000 for all agents (most users have <100)
2. Proper: Switch AgentPagingSource to cursor-based using after parameter

Going with option 1 for reliability + simplicity. Keep paging infrastructure for future large-scale use.

Success metric: all agents visible in the list regardless of count

title: Add LLM Config section to EditAgent — temperature, tokens, parallel tools
description:
From the Letta ADE screenshot, these fields are configurable:
- Temperature (slider 0-2)
- Context window (read-only display from model)
- Parallel tool calls (switch)
- Enable max output tokens (switch)
- Max output tokens (number field, shown when enabled)

These map to AgentUpdateParams.modelSettings (ModelSettings).

Implementation:
1. Add these fields to EditAgentUiState
2. Load from agent.modelSettings on init
3. Show in a 'LLM Config' section with Accordions component
4. Save via AgentUpdateParams(modelSettings = ModelSettings(...))

Success metric: temperature, parallel tool calls, max tokens all editable

title: Add Agent ID display (read-only, copyable) to EditAgent
description:
Show agent ID at top of EditAgent screen with copy button. Important for debugging and API usage.

Implementation: Read-only Text + IconButton(ContentCopy) that copies to clipboard with snackbar confirmation.

Success metric: agent ID visible and copyable

title: Fix model dropdown not populating — add error logging + fallback
description:
loadModels() has catch (_: Exception) {} which silently swallows all errors. Likely causes:
1. API endpoint /v1/models returns different JSON shape than LlmModel expects
2. Auth failure on the models endpoint
3. Network error silently ignored

Fix:
1. Add Log.w to the catch block so errors are visible
2. Add a fallback: if models fail to load, let user type model handle manually (keep dropdown but also allow free text)
3. Verify LlmModel fields match actual API response — may need to add @SerialName or make fields optional

Success metric: dropdown shows available models OR logs a clear error

title: [epic] EditAgent full config — match Letta ADE feature parity
description:
EditAgent screen currently shows: Name, Description, Model, Persona/Human blocks, System Prompt, Tags. Missing nearly all agent configuration from the Letta ADE.

Missing sections:
1. Agent ID (read-only, copyable)
2. Embedding model dropdown
3. LLM Config: temperature, context window, parallel tool calls, max output tokens, reasoning
4. Agent type display
5. Sleep-time frequency
6. Embedding config display
7. Model dropdown fix (not populating)

The model dropdown silently catches all exceptions so errors are invisible.

title: Add SettingsRepository.clearAllData() method
description:
No method to clear all persisted settings. Needed for logout and data reset.

Implementation: Clear encrypted prefs, reset configs StateFlow, reset activeConfig StateFlow, clear DataStore preferences.

Success metric: after clearAllData(), configs.value is empty and activeConfig.value is null

title: Implement actual data clearing in About screen logout
description:
About screen's logout callback just navigates to config — doesn't clear any data. Need to:
1. Add clearAllData() to SettingsRepository that clears configs, active config, and encrypted prefs
2. Call clearAllData() in the onLogout callback before navigating
3. Also clear any cached agents/conversations in memory

Success metric: after logout, app restarts at config screen with no saved data

title: Wire ModelDropdown into EditAgentScreen model field
description:
ModelDropdown composable exists but EditAgentScreen still uses a free-text OutlinedTextField for model. Need to:
1. Inject ModelRepository into EditAgentViewModel
2. Replace model OutlinedTextField with ModelDropdown
3. Wire onLoadModels to modelRepository.refreshLlmModels()
4. Wire onModelSelected to viewModel.updateModel()

Success metric: model field in EditAgent is a dropdown with available models

title: Implement conversation picker ModalBottomSheet in AgentScaffold
description:
showConversationPicker state exists but no UI renders when it's true. Need a ModalBottomSheet that:
1. Lists conversations for current agent from ConversationRepository
2. Shows conversation summary + relative time
3. 'New Conversation' button at top
4. Tapping a conversation navigates via onSwitchConversation
5. Current conversation highlighted

Success metric: tapping conversation subtitle in TopAppBar shows picker with agent's conversations

title: [epic] Wave 4 — Complete half-wired features
description: 6 features were started but never finished. This wave completes them.

title: Add @Stable to all UiState data classes for Compose performance
description:
14 UiState data classes (ChatUiState, AgentListUiState, etc.) plus UiMessage, UiToolCall missing stability annotations. Causes unnecessary recompositions during streaming.

Fix: Add @Immutable to all UiState data classes and UI model classes. Add @Stable to UiState sealed interface.

Success metric: every data class used in Compose state has @Stable or @Immutable

title: Migrate all ViewModels to safeLaunch — consistent error handling
description:
34 viewModelScope.launch calls across 12 ViewModels, 0 use safeLaunch. Silent coroutine crashes leave UI in broken state.

Fix: Replace all viewModelScope.launch { try { ... } catch { ... } } with safeLaunch(onError = { _uiState.value = UiState.Error(it.message) }) across all 12 ViewModels.

Success metric: grep 'viewModelScope.launch' returns zero in ViewModels

title: SECURITY: Remove LogLevel.ALL from LettaApiClient — leaks auth tokens to logcat
description:
LettaApiClient.kt L56-62: Logging at LogLevel.ALL exposes ALL HTTP headers including Bearer tokens to logcat. Any app with logcat access can extract user credentials.

Fix: Set LogLevel.NONE for release builds, LogLevel.HEADERS for debug. Never log BODY in production.

Success metric: grep LogLevel.ALL returns zero in release config

title: Display ConnectionStatusBanner in AgentScaffold
description:
ConnectionStatusBanner and ConnectivityMonitor were built but never wired into any screen. Users get blank errors when offline with no indication why.

Fix: Add ConnectionStatusBanner below TopAppBar in AgentScaffold, fed by ConnectivityMonitor state. Also show on ConversationsScreen.

Success metric: banner visible when device goes offline

title: [epic] Production hardening — Wave 3
description: 33 issues found across error handling, offline UX, security, performance, and missing production features. Consolidated into 15 actionable beads.

title: Add Archival Memory link from AgentScaffold drawer
description:
Archival route exists but is unreachable from the agent chat UI.

Fix: Add 'Archival Memory' item in DrawerContent that navigates to agent/{agentId}/archival. Also consider adding as 4th bottom nav tab.

Success metric: archival memory accessible from agent chat

title: Fix bottom nav dead-ends — wire ToolsScreen and AgentSettings navigation
description:
ToolsScreen in bottom nav has no navigation callbacks. AgentSettingsScreen has onNavigateBack = {} (empty).

Fix: Pass proper navigation callbacks from AgentScaffold to both screens. ToolsScreen needs onNavigateToMcp, onNavigateToModels. AgentSettings needs real onNavigateBack.

Success metric: no dead-end screens in bottom nav

title: Show agent name in chat TopAppBar
description:
AgentScaffold TopAppBar shows generic 'screen_chat_title'. Should show agent's actual name.

Fix: Read agentName from ChatViewModel.uiState and pass to TopAppBar title.

Success metric: TopAppBar displays the agent's name

title: Fix DrawerContent — connect to agent state, show real data
description:
AgentScaffold DrawerContent is a non-functional stub: empty model field, hardcoded 0.5 progress bar. Should show:
- Agent name + model
- Memory usage (context window with real progress)
- Tool count
- Links to: Archival Memory, Edit Agent, Model Browser
- Sleeptime status

Success metric: drawer shows real agent data from ChatViewModel

title: [epic] UX polish sprint — 15 issues from comprehensive audit
description:
Comprehensive UX audit found 87 issues, consolidated to 15 high-impact beads.
5 critical, 6 high, 4 medium priority.

title: Add first-run config check — redirect to ConfigScreen if no server
description:
AppNavGraph startDestination is always 'conversations' with a TODO comment. New users get API errors immediately.

Fix: Check SettingsRepository.activeConfig on app start. If null, startDestination = 'config'. Show a welcome banner on config screen for first-time setup.

Success metric: first launch goes to config screen, not conversations

title: Fix conversation names — auto-generate summary from first message
description:
All conversations show 'New Conversation' because Letta API doesn't auto-set summary.

Fix:
1. Better fallback label when summary is null: show 'Agent Name · relative time' instead of 'New Conversation'
2. After first user message, auto-set summary by calling updateConversation() with first 50 chars of user message
3. ChatViewModel.sendMessage() should check if conversation has no summary, and if so, update it after the first send

Success metric: conversations show meaningful names, not 'New Conversation'

title: Remove delete buttons from conversation/agent cards — use long press instead
description:
Delete buttons add visual clutter. Replace with long-press + context menu on both ConversationCard and AgentCard.

Files: ConversationsScreen.kt, AgentListScreen.kt

Success metric: no visible delete icon buttons on cards, long press shows delete option

title: Fix duplicate conversations in pagination
description:
AllConversationsRepository.loadNextPage() appends with + operator. If refresh() races or cursor pagination overlaps, duplicates appear.

Fix:
1. Use distinctBy { it.id } when appending new conversations
2. In refresh(), clear list atomically with .update{}
3. Add duplicate check: if conversation ID already in list, skip

Success metric: no duplicate conversation IDs in the list

title: Pass conversationId through navigation to chat screen
description:
Clicking a conversation navigates to agent/{agentId}/chat but doesn't pass the conversationId. Every conversation opens the same default chat.

Fix:
1. Add conversationId as optional nav argument: agent/{agentId}/chat?conversationId={conversationId}
2. Update ConversationsScreen onConversationClick to pass both agentId and conversationId
3. ChatViewModel already reads conversationId from SavedStateHandle — just needs the nav param
4. Update AppNavGraph route to accept optional conversationId

Success metric: clicking a specific conversation opens that conversation's messages

title: Implement agent picker dialog for new conversations
description:
AgentPickerDialog is a stub showing 'not implemented'. Users can't create new conversations.

Fix:
1. Inject AgentRepository into ConversationsViewModel
2. Load agents list on dialog open
3. Show scrollable list of agents with name + model
4. On agent selection, create conversation and navigate to chat
5. Show loading state while agents load

Success metric: can create a new conversation by picking an agent from the dialog

title: [epic] Conversation UX overhaul — labels, agent names, dedup, navigation
description:
Conversations screen has multiple UX issues:
1. No agent name shown — users can't tell which agent a conversation belongs to
2. Duplicate conversations appear due to missing deduplication in pagination
3. Clicking a conversation doesn't open that specific conversation (no conversationId in nav)
4. Agent picker dialog is a stub showing 'not implemented'
5. Raw ISO timestamps instead of relative time ('2h ago')
6. No conversation preview (first/last message snippet)

title: Show agent name on conversation cards
description:
ConversationCard only shows summary and raw timestamp. Users need to see which agent the conversation belongs to.

Fix:
1. ViewModel needs to resolve agentId → agent name via AgentRepository
2. Create a map of agentId → agentName in ConversationsUiState
3. Pass agent name to ConversationCard
4. Show agent name as subtitle below summary with SmartToy icon
5. If agent not found, show truncated agentId as fallback

Success metric: every conversation card shows the agent name

title: Fix StateFlow race conditions with MutableStateFlow.update{}
description:
All repositories do read-modify-write on MutableStateFlow maps:
  _messagesByAgent.value = _messagesByAgent.value.toMutableMap().apply { put(k, v) }

This is NOT atomic — two concurrent coroutines can lose writes.

Fix: Replace all .value = .value.toMutableMap().apply patterns with MutableStateFlow.update{}:
  _messagesByAgent.update { current -> current.toMutableMap().apply { put(k, v) } }

.update{} uses atomic compare-and-set loop internally — no lost writes.

Files to fix:
- MessageRepository.kt (6 instances)
- ConversationRepository.kt (6 instances)
- ToolRepository.kt (4 instances)
- McpServerRepository.kt (3 instances)
- AllConversationsRepository.kt (3 instances)
- AgentRepository.kt (1 instance)

Success metric: grep shows zero '.value = .*toMutableMap' patterns remaining

title: Add logging to all silent catch blocks — no more invisible failures
description:
Multiple catch blocks silently swallow exceptions:
- MessageRepository L184: JSON parse errors drop messages silently
- SseParser L53: malformed events vanish
- SettingsRepository L57: config load failures silently default to empty
- ChatScreen L182: date parsing errors silently skipped

Fix: Add android.util.Log.w() to every catch block that currently swallows:
  catch (e: Exception) { Log.w(TAG, 'Failed to parse message', e) }

This doesn't change behavior but makes debugging 10x easier.

Success metric: grep 'catch.*Exception.*\{' finds zero empty/comment-only catch bodies

title: [epic] Bug hardening — reduce subtle bugs via defensive patterns
description:
35+ bug-prone patterns found across the codebase. Fix the most dangerous ones to reduce the likelihood of subtle bugs.

Categories:
1. StateFlow race conditions in cache updates (read-modify-write not atomic)
2. Silent error swallowing in JSON parsing and coroutines
3. LaunchedEffect key instability in ChatScreen
4. Missing keys in LazyColumn items
5. ConnectivityMonitor thread safety
6. Kotlin lint and static analysis setup

title: Test: LettaMessageSerializer (10+ tests) — polymorphic deserialization
description:
LettaMessageSerializer dispatches JSON to correct LettaMessage subclass based on message_type field. This is the most critical serialization code.

Test cases:
- Deserialize user_message to UserMessage
- Deserialize assistant_message to AssistantMessage  
- Deserialize reasoning_message to ReasoningMessage
- Deserialize tool_call_message to ToolCallMessage
- Deserialize tool_return_message to ToolReturnMessage
- Deserialize approval_request_message to ApprovalRequestMessage
- Deserialize hidden_reasoning_message to HiddenReasoningMessage
- Deserialize event_message to EventMessage
- Deserialize unknown message_type to UnknownMessage (fallback)
- Handle missing message_type field gracefully
- Handle null content fields
- UserMessage.content extracts from string, array, and null contentRaw
- ToolReturnMessage.toolReturn handles string and object variants

Use real JSON fixtures from API responses

title: Test: SseParser (8+ tests) — SSE stream parsing to LettaMessage
description:
SseParser converts raw SSE byte streams into typed LettaMessage objects. Critical for streaming chat.

Test cases:
- Parse single data: line into LettaMessage
- Parse multiple messages from stream
- Handle [DONE] sentinel correctly (stream terminates)
- Handle data: prefix with whitespace
- Handle empty lines between messages
- Handle malformed JSON gracefully (skip, don't crash)
- Handle multi-line data: continuation
- Parse all LettaMessage subtypes (user, assistant, reasoning, tool_call, tool_return)

Dependencies: None (operates on raw strings/channels)
Create test strings that simulate real SSE responses

title: Test: AgentSearch (8+ tests) — fuzzy search with weighted scoring
description:
AgentSearch has complex scoring logic with multiple strategies that must be verified.

Test cases:
- Empty query returns all agents unfiltered
- Exact name match gets highest score
- Partial name match works
- Tag matching boosts score
- Model matching boosts score  
- Description matching works with lower weight
- Score threshold filters out low matches
- Case insensitive matching
- Special characters in query don't crash
- Empty agent list returns empty

Dependencies: None (pure function)
Simplest test to write — good warm-up

title: Test: ConversationRepository (8+ tests) — CRUD with optimistic updates
description:
ConversationRepository has optimistic update/rollback patterns that need thorough testing.

Test cases:
- refreshConversations() fetches from API and updates StateFlow
- refreshConversations() handles API error (keeps existing cache)
- createConversation() adds to cache optimistically
- deleteConversation() removes from cache optimistically
- deleteConversation() rolls back on API failure
- updateConversation() updates in cache optimistically
- updateConversation() rolls back on API failure
- forkConversation() creates new conversation from existing

Dependencies: FakeConversationApi
Key pattern: verify rollback by checking StateFlow value after simulated API failure

title: Test: ChatViewModel (12+ tests) — send flow, streaming states, typing
description:
ChatViewModel drives the core chat experience. Most user-facing logic.

Test cases:
- loadMessages() sets UiState.Loading then Success with messages
- loadMessages() maps AppMessage to UI Message correctly
- loadMessages() sets agentName from repository
- loadMessages() sets UiState.Error on repository failure
- sendMessage() clears input text immediately
- sendMessage() sets isStreaming=true and isAgentTyping=true

- sendMessage() clears isAgentTyping on first Streaming state
- sendMessage() sets isStreaming=false on Complete
- sendMessage() sets UiState.Error on StreamState.Error
- updateInputText() updates only inputText in state
- Message.toUiMessage() maps all MessageType variants correctly

Dependencies: FakeMessageRepository, FakeAgentRepository, FakeConversationRepository
Use Turbine for flow assertions, UnconfinedTestDispatcher

description:
MessageRepository is the most complex repository (241 lines). Critical for chat UX.

Test cases:
- getMessages() returns cached messages as Flow
- fetchMessages() calls API and updates cache
- fetchMessages() returns cached data on API failure (offline resilience)
- sendMessage() emits StreamState.Sending → Streaming → Complete
- sendMessage() emits StreamState.Error on API failure
- sendMessage() handles tool execution states
- resetMessages() clears cached messages

- Paging source returns correct pages
- Empty conversation returns empty list

Dependencies: FakeMessageApi, FakeMessageProcessor

title: Create test infrastructure: FakeApis, MainCoroutineRule, test fixtures
description:
Foundation for all other tests. Must be done first.

Create:
1. test/.../testutil/MainCoroutineRule.kt:
   - @get:Rule for setting Dispatchers.Main = UnconfinedTestDispatcher
   - @Before setMain, @After resetMain
2. test/.../testutil/FakeAgentApi.kt:
   - Implements same interface/methods as AgentApi
   - Returns configurable responses (success/error)
   - Tracks method calls for verification
3. test/.../testutil/FakeMessageApi.kt
4. test/.../testutil/FakeConversationApi.kt
5. test/.../testutil/FakeToolApi.kt
6. test/.../testutil/FakeBlockApi.kt
7. test/.../testutil/FakeMcpServerApi.kt
8. test/.../testutil/TestData.kt:
   - sampleAgent, sampleConversation, sampleMessage, sampleTool, sampleBlock
   - Factory functions: createAgent(name=...), createMessage(role=...)
   - Reusable across ALL test files

Pattern for each FakeApi:
- Constructor takes optional responses/errors
- Has var shouldFail: Boolean = false
- Tracks calls: val calls = mutableListOf<String>()
- Returns configured data or throws ApiException

title: API error handling: wrap all API calls with ApiResult sealed class

All 8 API classes (AgentApi, MessageApi, ConversationApi, BlockApi, ToolApi, McpServerApi, ModelApi) call .body() directly with zero error handling. Any network error crashes the app.

Replace 2 TODO('Wire to repository') in ChatViewModel.kt:
- loadMessages() L54: Get agent name from agentRepository.getAgent(agentId), get messages from messageRepository.getMessages(agentId) → ChatUiState
- sendMessage() L69: Call messageRepository.sendMessage(agentId, text), collect StreamState flow, update messages/isStreaming state

This is the core chat experience. Follow Gallery's pattern for streaming state management.

title: Conversation state: single source of truth across REST + WS + SSE
description:
## Problem

Five concurrent channels currently touch the same conversation state, none aware of each other:

1. POST /v1/conversations/{id}/stream (SSE-shaped, ~30–48 min lifetimes) — agent run output streamed to client.
2. /mobile/ws WebSocket — A2UI frames, message deltas, tool-approval routing.
3. GET /v1/conversations/{id}/messages (REST) — full history; observed with inconsistent shapes (limit=200/250, order=asc/desc) from multiple consumers.
4. GET /v1/runs/{id}/steps (REST polling) — live-run pane.
5. Agent-pool stdin — the actual write path into the Letta messages table.

Storage is fine (single Letta messages table). Server-to-client delivery is the chaos zone. Client-side reconciliation has no single ConversationStateHolder — multiple writers, no monotonic ordering, no shared cursor.

Symptoms observed in /tmp/admin-shim.log (2026-05-19 ~early morning):
- Same conv simultaneously has open SSE stream + WS session + REST /messages polling.
- Hydration on reopen misses latest user prompts (server has them, hydrator returns stale snapshot).
- Surface history flashes/replays entire timeline on conversation load.
- Actions silently dropped when WS is stale.
- a2ui turn_metrics consistently shows total_frames=0 even when A2UI is rendering — A2UI is going through WS-only while metrics expect splitter path; the two layers don't know about each other.

## Goal

A single canonical transport for conversation state mutations, observed by exactly one client-side state holder. REST as cold-start replay only. SSE either retired or downgraded to observability.

## Strategy: WS as canonical state mutator

Pick WS as the single source-of-truth transport. Reasoning:
- Already carries A2UI, tool-approval, and message frames — SSE-stream and REST-poll are subsets.
- WS has session-level state (sess-* IDs) — natural attach point for 'live view of conv X'.
- Recent durability work makes runs server-side; pairs naturally with WS-as-live-tail.
- May 16 architecture decision already steers Android stack toward WS-as-primary; this lands that decision in the message-state layer.

## Phases / child issues

P1. Define the WS-canonical contract.
   Update admin-shim/docs/MOBILE_WS_PROTOCOL.md to spell out:
   - WS frame shapes for message_appended, message_updated, run_step, A2UI surface ops.
   - REST cold-start contract: exactly one /messages call on cold-open, no polling thereafter.
   - SSE /stream status: retired (preferred) or observability-only (transition).
   - Per-conversation monotonic seq carried on every WS frame for client-side ordering.

P2. Single ConversationStateHolder on Android.
   One writer interface (WS), one reader interface for UI subscribers, REST only on cold-open. All existing ad-hoc paths refactored to converge here.

P3. Retire (or quarantine) POST /v1/conversations/{id}/stream.
   Once WS demonstrably carries the same payload, remove the SSE long-lived stream. If full retirement is too risky in one shot, gate it behind a feature flag and disable for mobile clients first.

P4. Stop REST /messages polling.
   /messages becomes cold-start-only. Live updates flow exclusively through WS. Pull-to-refresh is the only manual trigger.

P5. Unify A2UI metrics across paths.
   Wire a2ui turn_metrics to fire regardless of which transport delivered the frame. Right now total_frames=0 hides the WS-only A2UI flow from observability.

## Open child bugs subsumed by this epic

- letta-mobile-mvon (P1) — A2UI action send fails silently when WS is stale; must queue + reconnect. Naturally part of WS-as-canonical (action emitter buffers on non-OPEN state).
- letta-mobile-c3h4 (P1) — Three-way transport race (SSE + WS + REST). Directly fixed by phases P1, P3, P4.
- letta-mobile-16li (P1) — Hydration misses latest user prompts on reopen. Directly fixed by phases P1, P2, P4.
- letta-mobile-g2qg (P2) — A2UI surface history flashes/replays on conversation load. Fixed by P2 (single state holder with proper fold-on-load semantics).

Orthogonal but co-tracked under A2UI initiative:
- letta-mobile-92wq (P1) — ChoicePicker doesn't render. Renderer-side; not addressed here but stops being confounded by race noise once this epic lands.

## Acceptance

- Exactly one transport mutates conversation state on the wire. All others are read-only or retired.
- On Android, all UI subscribes to one ConversationStateHolder; multi-writer paths removed.
- Hydration on reopen always reflects latest server state, no missing messages.
- No duplicate or out-of-order frames visible to user across WS reconnects.
- a2ui turn_metrics reflects A2UI frames actually delivered.
- The four child P1/P2 bugs above close as fixed-by-this-epic.

## Related context

- Epic letta-mobile-51xm (A2UI renderer initiative) — sibling epic; this one is about state-plumbing, that one is about widget rendering.
- letta-mobile-a7ij — earlier instance of SSE vs WS race in Client Mode; same disease family, narrower scope.
- May 16 2026 architecture decision: Android as primary rich UI surface, Matrix downgraded to audit/notification only, tool-approval flows over WS.

Phase table inside the section maps to this epic's P1–P5. Next concrete step: land the per-conv seq field on the wire (lib/types/wire.ts + lib/store.ts persistence).

title: Conversation hydration misses latest user prompts on reopen
description:
Repro (2026-05-19 ~06:25–06:29 EDT, /tmp/admin-shim.log on letta-code-parallel host):
Android client reopens a conversation that already has fresh user messages on the server. The hydration result shown in the UI is stale — does not include the most recent user prompts that the server clearly has.

Log evidence (conv-617e458d-5a3c-4fb5-b295-6531344a3298):
- 06:26:32 GET /messages?limit=250&order=desc → res=143003B (initial bulk hydrate after reopen)
- 06:26:33 GET /messages?limit=200&order=desc → res=143003B (immediate refresh)
- 06:27:39 GET /messages?limit=200&order=asc  → res=147261B  (+4258 bytes, newer content exists server-side)
- 06:29:22 GET /messages?limit=200&order=desc → res=149394B  (+2133 bytes more)

Server has the new messages. The hydrator either:
(a) caches the first response and does not re-render when subsequent fetches return more, OR
(b) parses 'order=desc' results assuming oldest-newest semantics (or vice versa) so the newest user prompt is being cut by the limit window.

Also evident: at least two or three distinct consumers are hitting /messages with inconsistent shapes:
- limit=250 order=desc
- limit=200 order=desc
- limit=200 order=asc
That suggests multiple code paths each doing their own pagination, with no shared cursor or invalidation. The 'newest' user prompts are landing in the gap between them.

This is closely related to letta-mobile-c3h4 (SSE+WS+REST race) — same underlying disease: multiple sources of truth, no monotonic ordering, no single hydration owner. May be subsumed by that fix; treat this as a forcing function for it.

Acceptance:
- Reopen conversation that has 1+ new user messages on server → UI shows them on first render, no manual scroll/pull-to-refresh needed.
- /messages is fetched by exactly one consumer with a consistent order+limit shape.
- WS frames arriving after hydration append in order; no message is lost in the seam between REST hydrate and WS live stream.

Related: letta-mobile-c3h4 (transport race), letta-mobile-g2qg (history flash on load), letta-mobile-a7ij (SSE vs WS race root cause). Epic: letta-mobile-51xm.
notes: PR #133 opened for Android-side hydration fix: recent/open reconcile now converts server messages before dedupe so fresh user prompts without client otids are appended by synthetic otid/server-id identity; tool_return standalone bubbles remain suppressed; reconcile uses same 250/order=desc window as hydrate. Verified TimelineSyncLoopTest, compileRootDebugKotlin, testRootDebugUnitTest, assembleRootDebug, and Pixel install. Handoff retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-16li && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 133

title: Wire WS A2UI frames into turn_metrics
description: Why this issue exists: c3h4 found that A2UI surfaces can render on Android through /mobile/ws while server turn_metrics.total_frames remains 0 because the metrics splitter only sees non-A2UI SSE/REST representations. What needs to be done: update the admin-shim metrics path so WebSocket A2UI frame delivery increments the same turn_metrics frame counter used by other transports, then verify visible A2UI frames and turn_metrics agree.
close_reason: WS A2UI turn metrics fix landed in admin-shim PR #2

title: Transport race: SSE /stream + WS /mobile/ws + REST polling all rehydrate same conversation concurrently
description:
Repro (2026-05-19 ~early morning, observed in /tmp/admin-shim.log on letta-code-parallel host):
Android client (192.168.50.235) opens a chat conversation and simultaneously:
1. POSTs /v1/conversations/{conv-id}/stream (SSE long-lived; observed durations ~48 minutes per stream).
2. Establishes a /mobile/ws WebSocket session ([mobile:ws] hello accepted, a2ui negotiated).
3. Polls /v1/conversations/{conv-id}/messages?limit=200&order=desc every few seconds.

All three carry overlapping (and at times conflicting) view of the same conversation state. There is no monotonic ordering or single source of truth, so:
- A2UI surfaces emitted via WS can be clobbered by an SSE-delivered stale message snapshot.
- REST poll responses can race in between WS frames and rebuild a UI state from older data.
- Symptoms: full timeline flash on conversation load (already filed as letta-mobile-g2qg, but root-cause overlap with this), occasional surface/state inconsistencies during active chat.

Also observed: the shim's a2ui turn_metrics consistently shows total_frames=0 even when A2UI is rendering on device. That suggests A2UI is flowing via the WS-only path and bypassing the splitter that emits the metrics, while SSE/REST are pulling a non-A2UI representation. The two streams are not aware of each other.

This is the same family as letta-mobile-a7ij (SSE vs WS race in Client Mode).

Proposed direction:
- Pick a single canonical transport for state-mutating frames (WS), make SSE+REST observer-only or fully fold them into one sequenced log with a monotonic counter.
- Stop polling /messages while a stream is open for the same conv.
- Wire A2UI emission into the same metrics path regardless of which transport delivers it (right now total_frames=0 hides the bug).

Acceptance:
- Open conversation on mobile → exactly one rehydration source produces the visible state.
- WS, SSE, REST log lines never describe contradictory state for the same conv within the same minute.
- turn_metrics reflects A2UI frames actually delivered.

Related: letta-mobile-a7ij (root case in Client Mode), letta-mobile-mvon (action queueing on stale WS), letta-mobile-92wq (ChoicePicker doesn't render), letta-mobile-g2qg (history flash on load — likely subsumed by this once race is fixed). Epic: letta-mobile-51xm (A2UI renderer initiative).
notes: PR #131 opened for mobile-side mitigation: TimelineSyncLoop tracks active ambient SSE streams and skips ad-hoc recent REST /messages reconciles while the stream owns visible timeline state. Verified focused TimelineSyncLoopTest, compileRootDebugKotlin, testRootDebugUnitTest, assembleRootDebug, and Pixel install. Remaining server-side A2UI turn_metrics work is letta-mobile-n48r. Handoff retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-c3h4 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 131

title: A2UI ChoicePicker widget does not render in Android Compose renderer
description:
Repro (2026-05-18 evening, surface 'log-watch-form-7' and earlier rounds 3/4/5/6):
1. Agent emits an A2UI v0.9 updateComponents message containing a ChoicePicker component:
   {
     "id": "feel-picker",
     "component": "ChoicePicker",
     "label": "Render feel?",
     "options": [
       { "label": "Clean", "value": "clean" },
       { "label": "Glitchy", "value": "glitchy" },
       { "label": "Slow", "value": "slow" }
     ]
   }
2. Surface renders with Card → Column children = [title, note-field, feel-picker, submit-btn].
3. On the Pixel 9 Pro (192.168.50.235:5555), the TextField, Text, and Button render correctly. The ChoicePicker does NOT render — its slot in the Column is blank/skipped.

Confirmed across rounds 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 — picker is consistently absent. Submitting always yields 'feel': null because the user has no way to set it.

Expected: ChoicePicker should render as a selectable control (radio group / dropdown / segmented row per Compose renderer's chosen affordance) wired to the data model so 'feel-picker.value' resolves to the selected option's 'value' string.

Acceptance:
- ChoicePicker component from A2UI v0.9 Basic Catalog renders as an interactive control.
- Selecting an option writes the option's 'value' into the data model.
- Action context '$feel-picker.value' resolves to the selected value (or null when nothing selected).
- Visual test surface ('log-watch-form-X' equivalent) shows picker between TextField and Button.

Spec reference: A2UI v0.9 Basic Catalog — ChoicePicker is one of the required widgets in the basic set (Text, Image, Icon, Video, AudioPlayer, Row, Column, List, Card, Tabs, Modal, Divider, Button, TextField, CheckBox, ChoicePicker, Slider, DateTimeInput).

Related: letta-mobile-mvon (action queueing on stale WS). Filed under the same A2UI renderer initiative (epic letta-mobile-51xm).
close_reason: Implemented ChoicePicker rendering, data-model writes, synthetic input binding, and regression tests.

title: A2UI action send fails silently when WS is stale — must queue + reconnect
description:
Repro (2026-05-18 evening, surface input-widget-test):
1. Render A2UI surface in Android Compose renderer.
2. Put device down; let app go to background long enough for WS to drop.
3. Return, fill TextField/ChoicePicker/Slider, tap submit Button.

Observed: send fails with an error toast/log; action payload is lost — nothing reaches the server. The agent never sees the user's submission.

Expected: tapping an A2UI Button when the WS is disconnected must:
- queue the action locally (with surfaceId, action name, resolved context, timestamp),
- trigger a WS reconnect if not already reconnecting,
- flush the queued action on the next successful hello/negotiate,
- surface a transient 'reconnecting…' state in the UI, not a terminal error.

Notes:
- This is the same class of failure as any HTTP retry-on-resume — A2UI actions are user intent and must not be discarded on transport state.
- Related to the dispatcher gate work on the shim side (lcp-uo5.8) but this fix lives in android-compose WS client + A2UI action emitter.
- Trace context from session: surface 'input-widget-test', action name 'submit-test'.

Acceptance:
- Background app long enough for WS drop, return, submit A2UI action → action arrives at server after reconnect.
- Unit test: action emitter buffers when socket state is not OPEN, flushes on reconnect, drops on explicit user cancel only.
notes: Bootstrap after n48r: bd show letta-mobile-mvon && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 136 && gh pr view 2 --repo oculairmedia/letta-code-admin-shim. Completed letta-mobile-n48r via admin-shim PR #2 and mobile bookkeeping PR #136. Validation: admin-shim npm test; Android :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555.
close_reason: Queued A2UI actions now trigger WS reconnect and flush after welcome; explicit cancel/disconnect drops queued intent. Added regression coverage for stale socket approval submit.

title: Renderer: local-only UI state convention
description:
# Why

Tab open, accordion expanded, "dismissed this card", picker visibility — local UX states that shouldn't round-trip to the agent. Today the renderer scatters per-widget remember calls without a convention; domain widgets keep reinventing this.

# Scope

- Define a recognized raw["localState"] block on container components: { tabIndex: 0, isExpanded: false, ... } with initial values for non-bound state.
- Renderer reads localState at the container boundary; exposes a typed scope to descendants via a Compose CompositionLocal (LocalA2uiLocalState).
- Reset semantics: keyed on (surfaceId, componentId); survives recomposition; resets on surface deletion or component-id change.
- A small helper API for widgets that want a local state slot — registers under a key, gets a MutableState back.
- Document the convention in renderer module README or KDoc.

# Out of scope

- Persistence across surface deletion (deferred — would need a per-conversation cache).
- Optional sync-to-server hooks (deferred until a domain widget needs it).

# Acceptance

- Renderer exposes a Compose CompositionLocal scope for local state under a container.
- A demo widget (or the Tabs widget in qr6r.11) consumes the scope and persists tab selection across recomposition without a data-model patch.
- Tests: state survives composition recomposition; resets when surface is deleted.
- Convention documented for catalog authors.

title: Renderer: ListView widget with per-item binding scope
description:
# Why

Renderer-side counterpart to qr6r.1. Once the protocol can express templated lists, the renderer needs to walk the items array, instantiate the template per item with a scoped binding root, and render in a LazyColumn.

# Scope

- Add a "ListView" branch to A2uiComponentNode (designsystem/.../A2uiRenderer.kt).
- New Compose binding scope: an "item path" overrides path resolution. resolveBindingText({ path: "title" }) inside a ListView item resolves against /items/<idx>/title rather than the root.
- LazyColumn-backed; reuse the existing visited-set cycle guard; cap MaxItems (e.g. 256) and warn-log past that.
- Per-item itemKey path drives Compose key() for stable IDs across data-model updates.
- Tap actions inside an item attach { context: { itemId } } automatically.
- Renderer tests in feature-chat/src/test: 50-item render, key-stable updates, nested binding lookup.

# Out of scope

- Grid / horizontal lists — separate widgets.
- Sticky headers / footers.
- Drag-to-reorder.
- Virtualization beyond LazyColumn defaults.

# Acceptance

- A2UI surface emits one CreateSurface + UpdateComponents containing a ListView referencing an item-template + items-path; renderer displays N rows from the template.
- Updating /items via updateDataModel re-renders only the changed rows (keyed).
- Tapping a button inside a row emits a user_action with the item's id in context.
- Tests cover 50+ items and skeleton-on-missing-template fallback.

# Dependencies

- Blocked on qr6r.1 (protocol spec).

title: Protocol: list templating spec + Kotlin types
description:
# Why

The A2UI v0.9 Basic Catalog has no template/repeat primitive. To render N items today (issues, schedules, agents, completed-work entries), the agent has to emit N separate components in updateComponents. That bloats the wire and makes per-item interaction awkward. List templating is the single biggest enabling work to "drastically bulk up" A2UI.

# Scope

- Define the wire-level extension: e.g. ListView { itemTemplate: <componentId>, items: { path: "/x/items" }, itemKey: "id" } where the item-template is a regular component in the surface registry referencing bindings that resolve against each item's path.
- Specify per-item binding scope: a Text component inside the template referencing { path: "title" } resolves against the current item (/x/items/<idx>/title) rather than the surface root.
- Specify list-mutation actions: emit { type: "user_action", name, context: { itemId } } so tap on an item carries item identity.
- Coordinate with shim/server side (likely an lcp-side sister bead).
- Land Kotlin types in core/.../data/a2ui/A2uiProtocol.kt (new component variant + payload).
- Decode/encode round-trip tests.

# Out of scope

- Renderer implementation — covered in qr6r.2.
- Virtualization / lazy paging beyond LazyColumn defaults.
- Multi-column / grid layouts — separate widget.

# Acceptance

- A2UI v0.9-compatible wire extension proposed (back-compat: unknown widget falls through to skeleton on older clients).
- A2uiProtocolTest round-trips a frame with an itemTemplate + items array.
- Kotlin model adds a typed payload for the template metadata (item-key path, items-path, template-component-id).
- Sister bead filed for the shim/server side OR coordination ticket linked.

title: Epic: A2UI v2 — list templating, catalog expansion, schedule/v1 reference
description:
# Summary

Follow-on to the now-complete letta-mobile-51xm A2UI epic. With the v0.9 Basic Catalog and com.letta.mobile:tool-approval/v1 shipped, the renderer can express simple cards, forms, and the tool-approval flow — but it cannot scale to surfaces with N items (project issues, schedules, agents, completed-work timelines) without bloating the wire, and it cannot host UX with local-only state (tabs, accordions) without ad-hoc workarounds. This epic invests in the two enabling protocol/renderer extensions, then bulks up the Basic Catalog with the everyday primitives agents will reach for, and ships one reference domain catalog (schedule/v1) to template future ones.

# Why now

User direction 2026-05-18 (project memory architecture-legacy-vs-future): the bot module / Client Mode is sunset; the phone is a remote viewer; everything visible-to-user lives behind shim WS + REST + agent-emitted A2UI surfaces. Product depth shifts from "more Compose code" to "richer A2UI catalogs + smarter agents." This epic is that depth investment.

# Goals

1. Land list templating. Without it, lists/timelines/charts-of-N can't scale on the wire — each item is a separate component today.
2. Establish a local-UI-state convention. Tab open, accordion expanded, "I dismissed this card" should not round-trip to the agent.
3. Add the cheap basic widgets in batches (form inputs, numeric, dropdown, chips/badge, progress, icon, markdown/code).
4. Ship one domain catalog end-to-end — schedule/v1 — as the reference for subsequent domain catalogs (project-issue, agent-dispatch, ...).
5. Delete GeneratedUiRegistry (the bot-skills-era agent UI system) once bot module cleanup lands.

# Non-goals

- Native ScheduleListScreen replacement decision — defer; this epic provides the catalog; replacement vs augmentation is a separate product call.
- Charts as widgets (Vico bindings) — defer; needs list templating first.
- AgentAvatar cross-layer identity resolution — defer.
- Map / LocationPicker — defer; out-of-scope dependency.
- Domain catalogs beyond schedule/v1 — defer; pattern established here.

# Dependencies / coordination

- letta-mobile-51xm.8 (round-trip test) closes out v0.9 Basic — assume baseline.
- letta-mobile-d52f (Schedules tab via WS cron protocol) is in-flight as a native ScheduleListScreen; this epic's schedule/v1 catalog is the agent-driven alternative surface. They can coexist (native list + agent-pushed cards into chat) or the catalog can eventually subsume the native screen — product decision deferred.
- Bot module cleanup: the GeneratedUiRegistry removal child should land alongside whatever issue retires the bundled bot-skills.
- Likely requires sister beads on the shim/letta side for the list templating spec and the schedule/v1 catalog id reservation.

# Phases

Ordered for unblocking:

1. Protocol: list templating spec + Kotlin types
2. Renderer: ListView widget with per-item scope
3. Renderer: local-UI-state convention
4. Renderer: form widgets — Checkbox / Switch / Radio
5. Renderer: numeric inputs — Slider / Stepper
6. Renderer: Dropdown / Select
7. Renderer: Chip / FilterChip / Badge
8. Renderer: Progress (Linear, Circular)
9. Renderer: Icon (named, from LettaIcons)
10. Renderer: MarkdownBlock + CodeBlock
11. Renderer: Tabs / Accordion
12. Catalog: com.letta.mobile:schedule/v1 reference implementation
13. Cleanup: remove GeneratedUiRegistry
14. Docs: A2UI catalog authoring guide

# Success criteria (epic-level)

Agent on the server can emit a single A2UI frame describing a list of N schedules (using the templating pattern), the renderer displays N native ScheduleCards from one template, the user can tap into an editor that uses local state for picker visibility, edit it via two-way-bound inputs, submit a fixed-shape schedule.save user_action, and see the result update via updateDataModel — all without the client knowing anything domain-specific other than the schedule/v1 catalog widgets.

title: Harden A2UI actions when run_id is missing
description: A2UI ToolApproval routing works when run_id is present, but missing run_id on outgoing user_action frames causes shim/agent-side glitches. Add client-side fallback/deferral so mobile does not emit unroutable A2UI actions.
close_reason: Implemented client-side A2UI run_id fallback/deferral with active turn routing and missing-run tests; Gradle gates and APK install passed.

title: Fix A2UI queued action when chat WS unavailable
description: A2UI button tap showed 'Action queued until the chat connection returns' instead of being delivered or giving actionable recovery. Investigate why sendA2uiAction returns Queued during normal chat use and adjust WS lifecycle/UX.
close_reason: Preserved A2UI surface run/turn metadata, emitted routing ids on ToolApproval user_action frames, and kept queued action frame IDs stable so replayed outcomes can correlate after reconnect.

title: Gradient agent-thinking indicator: mandatory visual signal while awaiting agent reply
description:
The mobile chat surface lacks a visible "agent thinking" indicator while waiting for an A2UI-triggered (or any other) agent turn to begin streaming. Emmanuel has called this out as MANDATORY and specifically wants a gradient-based design - this is intentional product UX, not just a generic spinner.

This is the THIRD of three deliverables for a usable A2UI tap experience. It is also broadly useful for the chat surface beyond A2UI: any time the agent is processing and has not yet started streaming a response (warm-up latency, tool calls, gated approvals), the thinking indicator should be visible.

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:

A gradient-based "thinking" indicator that activates whenever the chat is awaiting agent output. Triggers include:

1. After a user sends a regular text message (existing case - the indicator should now show here too if it does not already).
2. After a user_action with outcome=injected_as_input arrives (the action will produce an agent turn).
3. After a user_action with outcome=matched_approval (the agent will resume its blocked turn and the next output is incoming).
4. After the user sends any input that will trigger an agent turn.

It deactivates when:

- The first frame of streaming agent output (reasoning_message, assistant_message, or tool_call_message) arrives in the chat surface.
- The turn ends without output (stop_reason arrives with no assistant content).
- A timeout fires (default 60s).
- The user cancels the in-flight turn via existing cancel UI.

VISUAL DESIGN:

Gradient-based. Open suggestions for the implementer (but Emmanuel has design input):

- A horizontally-flowing gradient bar (animated linear sweep, similar to indeterminate progress but with brand colors as gradient stops) positioned just below the last chat message or just above the composer.
- Alternative: a soft pulsing gradient ring around the agent avatar / status chip.
- Alternative: a gradient-rendered text token "Meridian is thinking..." with the gradient sweeping across the characters (like the iMessage typing animation but gradient-shifted).

Use the existing app primary color as one gradient stop. The secondary stop should derive from the material3 dynamic-color secondary (if available) or a complementary tint of primary. Animation duration ~1.2s per cycle.

The indicator should respect the user's reduced-motion accessibility preference - in reduced-motion mode, fall back to a static gradient bar with no animation.

WHY THIS MATTERS:

Quoting Emmanuel directly (2026-05-17 20:08 EDT): "I need a confirmation from the toast and then I also need an indication that the message has been received. AKAD thinking indicator that we design with the gradients this is mandatory. as of right now this is not functioning."

The thinking indicator is what bridges "the server got my input" (snackbar from sibling bead) and "the agent is replying" (streaming output). Without it, the user sees their action confirmed, then a long silence, then a chat bubble appears with no warning. The thinking indicator is the "the gears are turning" beat that makes the latency tolerable.

NON-GOALS:

- The actual gradient art direction (Emmanuel and design own the final palette and motion specs). This bead lands the technical implementation with a workable default that can be themed later.
- Replacing or refactoring existing streaming indicators if they already exist for non-A2UI cases (separate concern; this bead either adds a new indicator or extends an existing one).
- Persisting thinking-indicator state across navigation (transient, lives on the chat screen).

DEPENDS ON:

- This bead can land independently of A2UI work for the regular text-message case.
- For the A2UI cases (triggers 2 and 3), this depends on the sibling outcome-toast bead resolving the action so the thinking state can begin. Without that, the thinking indicator has no trigger event.

ROLLBACK SIGNAL:

If the gradient animation introduces frame jank on lower-end devices, swap to a static gradient bar by default and gate the animation behind a device-tier check or a build flag.

LIKELY SURFACES:

- Chat screen Compose tree: add a ThinkingIndicator composable, place it in the chat layout, hoist its visible state.
- Chat ViewModel: add a thinking state (StateFlow<ThinkingState>) with transitions driven by: user-message sent, user_action_outcome injected_as_input, user_action_outcome matched_approval, streaming first frame received, stop_reason received with no content, timeout, user cancel.
- Theme / design tokens: define the two gradient color stops as ThemeColors.thinkingGradientStart / End so they can be retuned without code changes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. After sending a regular text message, the gradient thinking indicator appears within 100ms and remains until the first agent output frame arrives.
2. After receiving a user_action_outcome with outcome=injected_as_input, the indicator appears.
3. After receiving a user_action_outcome with outcome=matched_approval where the agent will produce more output, the indicator appears.
4. The indicator deactivates the moment the first reasoning_message / assistant_message / tool_call_message frame is ingested by streamSubscriber.
5. The indicator times out at 60s if no output arrives, replaced by a small "Response delayed - check your connection" subtitle.
6. Cancel button (existing UI) clears the indicator immediately.
7. Reduced-motion accessibility preference renders a static gradient bar with no animation.
8. The indicator uses a gradient between two themable color stops (defined in ThemeColors, not hard-coded).
9. Visual QA pass on Pixel 9 Pro and at least one lower-tier device (or emulator) confirms no frame jank.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After sending a regular text message, the gradient thinking indicator appears within 100ms and remains until the first agent output frame arrives.
2. After user_action_outcome with outcome=injected_as_input, the indicator appears.
3. After user_action_outcome with outcome=matched_approval where the agent will produce more output, the indicator appears.
4. The indicator deactivates on first reasoning_message / assistant_message / tool_call_message frame.
5. Times out at 60s with a 'Response delayed - check your connection' subtitle.
6. Cancel button clears the indicator immediately.
7. Reduced-motion accessibility renders a static gradient bar with no animation.
8. Uses a gradient between two themable color stops defined in ThemeColors.
9. Visual QA on Pixel 9 Pro plus one lower-tier device confirms no frame jank.

title: A2UI server-ack toast: Snackbar driven by user_action_outcome (matched/injected/recorded/rejected/error)
description:
After a user taps an A2UI Button, the action travels to the shim via WS but no visible confirmation surfaces back to the user. The mobile client needs to consume the server's user_action_outcome frame (spec defined in lcp-uo5.14) and render a toast/snackbar confirming the server received the action.

This is the SECOND of three deliverables for a usable A2UI tap experience. It depends on lcp-uo5.14 landing server-side (the outcome ack frame), so this bead is blocked-by that one in spirit even though it cannot be enforced via beads since the dep crosses rigs.

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:

When a user_action_outcome WS frame arrives correlated to a previously-sent user_action (by frameId), the client renders a Material3 Snackbar at the bottom of the chat surface that conveys the outcome plainly.

Snackbar behaviors per outcome enum (from lcp-uo5.14 spec):

- matched_approval: "Approved" or "Denied" snackbar with subtle color (green or grey). Auto-dismisses after 2s.
- injected_as_input: "Sent" snackbar with subtle blue/neutral color. Auto-dismisses after 1.5s. The agent thinking indicator (sibling bead) takes over immediately after.
- recorded_only: "Saved" snackbar, low-key, 2s. This is the "agent is not currently listening but we recorded your input" case.
- rejected: "Could not send" snackbar with error color and the server's user-readable reason inline. Sticky (stays until user dismisses or taps Retry). Includes a Retry button if the action is idempotent.
- error: "Something went wrong" snackbar with error color. Sticky. Includes a Retry button.

Visual style: Material3 Snackbar with the app's accent color (light variant) for success states, error red for failure. Use the same Material3 SnackbarHost the rest of the app uses if one exists; otherwise add one scoped to the chat surface.

Correlation: the client tracks pending user_actions by frameId in a small in-memory map (keyed by frameId, valued by the surfaceId + componentId + tap timestamp). When user_action_outcome arrives with matching frameId, the map entry resolves and the corresponding Button's in-flight state clears (this hands off to the sibling optimistic-feedback bead's state machine).

WHY THIS MATTERS:

Without this, the user gets a Button that animates into a loading state and stays there for 10 seconds before timing out, even when the server happily received the action. The toast is the bridge between "I tapped a button" and "the agent is doing something" - and in failure cases, it is the ONLY signal that something went wrong.

NON-GOALS:

- Persistent action history / log view (separate concern).
- Customizing the Snackbar appearance per surface (use one consistent style).
- Internationalization of toast strings (use English defaults for now, ship in en first).
- Rich error UI beyond a Snackbar (errors that need a modal or full-screen treatment are separate UX work).

DEPENDS ON:

lcp-uo5.14 (server-side) - the outcome ack frame must exist for this client behavior to have anything to react to. Before that lands, this bead can be implemented against a mocked outcome stream in tests but produces no real-world effect.

ROLLBACK SIGNAL:

If the Snackbar competes with existing chat-surface snackbars or stacks badly, revert and rethink the surface. The toast must not block the user from continuing to interact with chat input or other UI.

LIKELY SURFACES:

- A2UI dispatcher data layer: add a user_action_outcome handler that resolves the pending-action map and emits outcome events.
- Chat screen Compose tree: add or reuse a SnackbarHost; subscribe to the outcome Flow and call snackbarHostState.showSnackbar() per outcome.
- Pending-action map: probably a simple StateFlow<Map<String, PendingAction>> in the chat ViewModel, keyed by frameId.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. When a user_action_outcome frame arrives, a Material3 Snackbar appears within 100ms with the correct message per outcome enum.
2. Successful outcomes (matched_approval, injected_as_input, recorded_only) auto-dismiss within 2s.
3. Failure outcomes (rejected, error) are sticky and include a Retry button (when idempotent) and the server's reason text.
4. The corresponding Button's in-flight state clears the moment the outcome arrives (handoff to sibling bead's state machine).
5. The pending-action frameId map drops the entry when the outcome arrives, so memory doesn't grow unboundedly.
6. Stale outcomes (frameId not in the pending map) are logged and silently dropped, not crashed on.
7. A unit/instrumentation test covers each of the five outcome types against a mocked WS frame.
acceptance_criteria:
1. When a user_action_outcome frame arrives, a Material3 Snackbar appears within 100ms with the correct message per outcome enum.
2. Successful outcomes auto-dismiss within 2s.
3. Failure outcomes are sticky and include a Retry button when idempotent and the server's reason text.
4. The corresponding Button's in-flight state clears the moment the outcome arrives.
5. The pending-action frameId map drops entries on outcome receipt; no unbounded growth.
6. Stale outcomes (frameId not in map) are logged and silently dropped, not crashed on.
7. A test covers each of the five outcome types against a mocked WS frame.
notes:
Bootstrap from completed local-feedback work: PR #116 is open at https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/116 on branch fix/a2ui-envelope-contract. Closed letta-mobile-ndtc.1 after implementing local A2UI Button submitting feedback in A2uiRenderer.kt with renderer tests in A2uiRendererTest.kt. Validation passed: :feature-chat:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.feature.chat.A2uiRendererTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555. Retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-ndtc.2 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 116
Device repro evidence from Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235 on 2026-05-17 20:35 EDT: local A2UI Button loading indicator appeared, and logcat shows outbound path only: 20:35:11.069 Button onClick dispatching surfaceId=post-stroke-test-btn componentId=root event=a2ui.test.send; 20:35:11.078 ChannelTransport user_action sent frameId=1fa0e1ab-e993-4230-ab18-9eafbd69adc3; 20:35:11.078 submitA2uiAction result=Sent. Full log window grep found no user_action_outcome, no matching frameId return, and no additional A2UI/WsChatBridge/MobileWsFrames receipt logs. This confirms client local-send succeeds and current missing UX is receipt/outcome acknowledgement, matching this bead.

Added user_action_outcome parsing/routing, pending frameId correlation, chat snackbar receipts with retryable indefinite failures, and server-receipt submit-state clearing. title: A2UI Button: optimistic local feedback on tap (spinner, disable, haptic, sibling read-only)
description:
A2UI Button tap currently produces zero visible feedback on the rendered surface between tap-down and (eventually-never-arriving) server response. The user is left wondering whether the tap registered at all.

Verified 2026-05-17 20:07 EDT on Pixel 9 Pro PID 21561: tap fires Compose onClick, A2UI dispatcher constructs the action, ChannelTransport sends the user_action WS frame and returns result=Sent within ~7ms. The Button visual state does not change. The surface does not change. There is no spinner, no disable, no overlay, no haptic.

This bead is the FIRST of three deliverables needed for a usable A2UI tap experience. It is the purely-local piece: it does not depend on any server work and can ship immediately.

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:

Within ~100ms of any A2UI Button tap, the surface must visibly transition into an "in flight" state. The user must not stare at unchanged pixels wondering if the tap registered.

Required behaviors:

1. Tapped Button enters a pressed-then-loading visual state. The simplest correct version: button shows a small inline indeterminate spinner replacing or beside its label, with the label dimmed. The Button is disabled (no repeated taps) until the action resolves or times out.

2. Any sibling input components in the same logical group (e.g. TextField in the same surface or paired surface) become read-only (not greyed out, just non-editable) so the user does not feel like they can keep editing while submission is in progress.

3. A subtle haptic on the tap (Android HapticFeedbackConstants.CONTEXT_CLICK or equivalent). Optional but recommended.

4. This state persists until EITHER the server outcome arrives (covered by sibling bead for outcome ack) OR a local timeout fires (default 10s) and the Button reverts to its enabled state with no error chip - because at that point we know the WS sent the frame, we just have no server feedback yet. The timeout exists only so the user is not locked out if the server happens to be broken.

5. While in the in-flight state, the action handler MUST coalesce repeated taps. Today: tapping 5 times in 2 seconds sends 5 frames. After this lands: 5 rapid taps produce one frame, and the Button stays in-flight until the first response resolves.

WHY THIS MATTERS:

Even if all server-side feedback work (uo5.14 outcome ack, uo5.13 agent injection) ships perfectly, the gap between tap and server reply will always be at least one network round-trip plus agent thinking time. Without local feedback, that gap looks like "broken." Local feedback is the bedrock; server feedback is the additional confirmation.

NON-GOALS:

- Server-side outcome handling (separate sibling bead, ack is its own UX surface).
- Full agent-thinking gradient indicator (separate sibling bead).
- Custom per-button styles (use existing Material3 button state semantics).
- Animation polish beyond Compose's default ripple + indeterminate progress.

ROLLBACK SIGNAL:

If the disable + spinner introduces a "stuck" state where the Button never re-enables because the local timeout misfires, revert. The fix must keep the Button reactive within at most 10s of any tap regardless of server behavior.

LIKELY SURFACES:

- A2UI Button composable (the one that emits Button onClick: dispatching ... in logcat). Wrap the existing onClick with state hoisting: capture "inflight" state, dispatch action, set state, schedule timeout, render variant based on state.
- A2UI dispatcher / submitA2uiAction in the data layer: expose a result Flow or callback so the Button can subscribe to "outcome received" (until uo5.14 ships server-side, this Flow will just emit a timeout event at 10s).
- Surface state container: any TextFields in the same surface should observe the in-flight state and switch to read-only.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. Tapping any A2UI Button produces a visible state change on the Button within 100ms (measured by a screen recording or instrumentation timing).
2. The Button is disabled and shows an inline indeterminate spinner from tap-down until outcome-or-timeout.
3. Rapid taps (5 in 2 seconds) produce exactly 1 user_action frame in adb logcat - confirmed by `adb logcat | grep "user_action sent" | wc -l`.
4. If no outcome arrives within 10s, the Button re-enables and the spinner clears. No error chip is shown - this is silent recovery for the case where the server is still missing the outcome feature.
5. Sibling TextField components become read-only during in-flight; tapping into one does not bring up the keyboard, and a brief subtitle/caption indicates "submitting...".
6. Optional haptic confirmation fires on tap-down (one short pulse).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping any A2UI Button produces a visible state change on the Button within 100ms.
2. The Button is disabled and shows an inline indeterminate spinner from tap-down until outcome-or-timeout.
3. Rapid taps (5 in 2 seconds) produce exactly 1 user_action frame in adb logcat.
4. If no outcome arrives within 10s, the Button re-enables and the spinner clears silently.
5. Sibling TextField components become read-only during in-flight with a 'submitting...' caption.
6. Optional haptic confirmation fires on tap-down.
close_reason: Implemented local A2UI Button submitting feedback with spinner, disabled state, sibling TextField read-only caption, haptic feedback, timeout recovery, and regression tests.

title: A2UI tap-to-reply UX: full lifecycle (epic for 3-piece feedback contract)
description:
Umbrella epic for the three-part user feedback contract on A2UI Button taps. Filed to give the dev agent a coherent narrative across the three sibling implementation beads and to capture the full tap-to-reply lifecycle in one place.

THE PROBLEM (verified 2026-05-17 20:07 EDT, Pixel 9 Pro PID 21561):

User taps an A2UI Button. ChannelTransport sends a user_action WS frame in ~7ms with result=Sent. Then for the next several seconds, NOTHING visible happens. No Button state change, no toast, no thinking indicator, no agent reply. The user has no way to tell whether:

- The tap registered at all
- The frame was sent
- The server received it
- The agent is processing
- The agent will eventually reply
- An error occurred silently

This makes the entire A2UI feature feel broken even when the underlying transport is working. Emmanuel has flagged this as mandatory to fix and called out specifically wanting a gradient-based thinking indicator.

THE FULL TAP-TO-REPLY LIFECYCLE WE WANT:

T+0: User taps Button.
T+50ms: Ripple animates (existing Compose default).
T+100ms: Button shows inline spinner, disables; sibling inputs become read-only; subtle haptic. [BEAD 1: optimistic local feedback]
T+~10-200ms: WS frame arrives at server. ChannelTransport already logged "user_action sent".
T+~50-300ms: Server emits user_action_outcome WS frame back. [Depends on lcp-uo5.14 server-side, not in this rig]
T+~200-500ms: Client receives outcome. Snackbar shows ("Sent" / "Approved" / etc.). Button's in-flight state clears. [BEAD 2: toast confirmation]
T+~500-1000ms: If outcome was injected_as_input or matched_approval, the gradient thinking indicator activates. [BEAD 3: thinking indicator]
T+variable (1-15s typically): First frame of agent output arrives. Thinking indicator deactivates. Agent reply begins streaming into chat.
T+later: Agent reply completes.

EVERY SINGLE BEAT in that timeline currently lacks a visible signal. Three sibling beads cover the three local-UI pieces; one server-side bead in the lcp- rig (lcp-uo5.14) covers the outcome ack.

THE THREE SIBLING BEADS UNDER THIS EPIC:

(Will link via dependencies after creation - bead IDs assigned at file time.)

1. Optimistic local feedback (T+100ms button state, haptic, sibling input read-only).
2. Toast confirmation (Snackbar driven by user_action_outcome, plus failure handling).
3. Gradient thinking indicator (mandatory per Emmanuel, gradient design, activates after outcome=injected_as_input or matched_approval and on regular text messages too).

CROSS-RIG DEPENDENCY:

lcp-uo5.14 (server-side outcome ack frame) is a hard prerequisite for bead 2 to do anything real-world; bead 2 can be developed against mocked outcomes in unit tests but won't fire in production until uo5.14 lands.

WHY ALL THREE NEED TO LAND TOGETHER:

Any one in isolation leaves the user worse off than nothing:

- Just bead 1 (local feedback) without bead 2 (toast): user sees button enter loading state, never sees it resolve, just times out silently after 10s. Looks broken.
- Just bead 2 (toast) without bead 1: button looks unchanged, then suddenly a toast appears. Disconnected.
- Just bead 3 (thinking indicator) without 1+2: button looks unchanged after tap, then thinking indicator appears below. Disconnected and unclear why.

The three must ship as a unit. If they cannot all ship at once, ship bead 1 first (it has no dependencies), then bead 2 (after uo5.14), then bead 3 last (because it depends on outcome events).

NON-GOALS FOR THIS EPIC:

- The actual A2UI Button click sending the frame (already works; verified end-to-end).
- The server-side dispatcher routing (covered by lcp-uo5.8, lcp-uo5.12, lcp-uo5.13, lcp-uo5.14).
- Agent response content quality (out of scope; the agent is whoever it is).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (epic-level):

1. Tapping an A2UI Button produces visible state change within 100ms (bead 1).
2. Server outcome (when uo5.14 ships) produces a visible Snackbar within 100ms of arrival (bead 2).
3. While the agent is processing post-action, a gradient thinking indicator is visible (bead 3).
4. End-to-end: user taps Send, sees button enter loading, sees "Sent" toast within ~1s, sees thinking indicator until agent reply streams in, sees reply land in chat. No silent gaps.
5. Failure cases (no ack within 10s, error outcome, etc.) produce visible feedback - no silent dead-ends.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping an A2UI Button produces visible state change within 100ms.
2. Server outcome (when uo5.14 ships) produces a Snackbar within 100ms of arrival.
3. While the agent is processing post-action, a gradient thinking indicator is visible.
4. End-to-end: user taps Send, sees button loading, sees 'Sent' toast within ~1s, sees thinking indicator until agent reply streams in. No silent gaps.
5. Failure cases produce visible feedback - no silent dead-ends.

title: A2UI widgets render but are non-interactive (no focus, no input, no taps) - entire interaction layer absent
description:
A2UI widgets render visually but are non-interactive: no focus, no input, no taps respond.

Observed 2026-05-17 17:00 EDT on Pixel 9 Pro, post-Button-fix-attempt debug build. Test surface render-test-7 (Card + Column + Heading + Divider + TextField + DateTimeInput + Divider + Row + 2 Buttons + Dividers) renders correctly visually but is entirely display-only:

- TextField "Your name" - tapping the field does not focus it, the keyboard does not appear, even if the keyboard is already up no characters land in the field. The field renders as a static labeled outlined input.
- DateTimeInput "Pick a date" - tapping does not open a date picker dialog. No visible response.
- Submit Button (primary variant) - tap does not produce any visible response. No ripple, no state change, no user_action over the WS.
- Cancel Button (default variant) - same as submit. Static pill.

The Card, Column, Row, Divider, and Text components are not expected to be interactive, so they are not part of this report.

Likely root cause:

The A2UI widget composables landed as visual stubs only. The interactive bindings (onClick for Button, focusable + onValueChange for TextField, onClick + DatePicker dialog for DateTimeInput) are not wired up. There are several plausible variants:

1. The composables were written with static layout but the click/focus modifiers were never added.
2. The composables have onClick lambdas but the lambdas are no-ops (TODO placeholders).
3. The composables call into an action dispatcher that is null or stubbed out for the A2UI surface context.
4. Pointer/touch input is being intercepted by a parent overlay (the chat container or a Card surface that absorbs the gesture before children see it).

Variant 4 is worth checking first because it is the cheapest to verify - inspect the Card composable for any pointerInteropFilter, clickable, or Modifier.testTag that swallows clicks. Card in the catalog is described as having no action of its own, so the spec expects clicks to pass through to children.

Diagnostic checklist:

1. Logcat for tap events on the form region - is GestureDetector firing at all when the TextField is tapped? If yes, the composable received the tap but ignored it. If no, a parent absorbed it.
2. Compose UI Inspector (if available) - check the Modifier chain on the rendered TextField. clickable, focusable, focusable(true) should be present.
3. Sourcedive: search for the Button composable in the A2UI renderer package. Confirm the onClick body actually invokes a dispatcher that knows how to construct a user_action frame from the Action.event in the props.

Why this is critical:

A2UI's entire value is interactive surfaces. ToolApprovalCard with 4 non-tappable affordances is no better than a Markdown blockquote. The vertical slice in lcp-uo5.7 explicitly requires the user_action round-trip, which is gated on the renderer producing the WS frame.

Non-goals:

- Adding new widgets (the catalog set declared is already present visually).
- Theming or animation polish.
- Auto-focus on first field (separate UX bead).
- Keyboard IME logic (just the focus + accepts-input baseline).

Rollback signal:

If wiring up interactivity introduces a crash on any rendered surface, revert and re-spec. Display-only is bad but a crash is worse.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping a TextField focuses it, brings up the keyboard, and accepts characters which appear in the field.
2. Tapping a DateTimeInput opens a date picker dialog; selecting a date persists into the field value.
3. Tapping a Button shows a touch ripple, runs its action handler, and emits a user_action WS frame with the correct event.name and context.
4. Card and Column do not absorb tap gestures intended for child widgets.
5. The user_action frame format matches the schema declared by Action.event in the catalog (name + context fields preserved).
6. A renderer-side unit or instrumented test covers tap + focus + input flow for each interactive widget type (Button, TextField, DateTimeInput, CheckBox, ChoicePicker, Slider).
7. Once landed, the form on render-test-7 supports actually filling Your name, picking a date, and tapping Submit to emit a user_action visible in adb logcat.
notes:
TAP CONFIRMATION 2026-05-17 17:12 EDT: Emmanuel reports tapping the rendered primary Button (submit-test-1 surface, single Button root with Text label "Tap me", Action.event name "a2ui.test.tap"). adb logcat on Pixel 9 Pro (PID 16455) shows ZERO evidence of:

- user_action frame sent over the WS
- a2ui or Action handler activity
- Any ws.send / writeFrame / WebSocket outbound activity correlated with the tap
- The "a2ui.test.tap" event name or "submit-test-1" surface id anywhere in the trace

The only "click" or "tap" related lines in the log are Android system Autofill chatter, unrelated to the A2UI surface.

What this narrows for the implementer:

Within the broader "widgets non-interactive" failure described in this bead, the specific failure for Button is:
- The visual rendering is fine (Button frame + Text label both visible).
- The tap gesture is accepted by Compose (Emmanuel saw it tap-respond).
- But the onClick lambda is either a no-op TODO or it's calling into a dispatcher that does not translate the A2UI Action.event into a user_action WS frame.

So the missing piece is the bridge between "Compose onClick fired" and "WS adapter sends user_action with the right name + context fields." This is likely a single function call in the Button composable that needs to invoke the A2UI dispatcher with the action's event payload. If a dispatcher exists but is unwired, the fix may be even smaller.

Diagnostic snippet for the dev agent to use as a verification gate:

After the fix, taping the same submit-test-1 surface's Button should produce a logcat line like:
  I/A2UI: user_action sent surfaceId=submit-test-1 event=a2ui.test.tap

And adb -s 192.168.50.235:5555 logcat | grep -i "user_action\|a2ui.test.tap" must return at least one matching line per tap.

Until this bridge lands, the entire user_action round-trip is dead, lcp-uo5.7 (vertical slice) cannot pass, and lcp-uo5.8 (server-side dispatcher gate) has nothing to gate against.
RESOLUTION 2026-05-17 17:19 EDT: Button → user_action emission is now working end-to-end. The fix landed sometime between 17:12 (initial tap-confirmation showing zero activity) and 17:19 (this verification).

Test surface: tap-test-final, single primary Button root with Text child labeled "Tap me", Action.event name "a2ui.test.confirm-tap".

Logcat evidence on Pixel 9 Pro PID 18361 - every tap produces three sequential log lines:

  I/A2UI:             Button onClick: dispatching surfaceId=tap-test-final componentId=root event=a2ui.test.confirm-tap
  I/ChannelTransport: user_action sent  surfaceId=tap-test-final event=a2ui.test.confirm-tap frameId=<uuid>
  I/A2UI:             submitA2uiAction surfaceId=tap-test-final event=a2ui.test.confirm-tap result=Sent

Emmanuel tapped the button 8 times in 4 seconds; all 8 emissions made it through cleanly. Each user_action got a unique frameId (good for future dedup/ack correlation in lcp-uo5.8).

What this resolves:
- AC #3: "Tapping a Button shows a touch ripple, runs its action handler, and emits a user_action WS frame with the correct event.name and context." VERIFIED.
- AC #5: "The user_action frame format matches the schema declared by Action.event in the catalog (name + context fields preserved)." VERIFIED via the logcat output showing surfaceId + event passed through.

Still open within this bead's scope:
- AC #1 (TextField focus + input): VERIFIED earlier in the day - Emmanuel typed into a Your name field successfully.
- AC #2 (DateTimeInput date picker): untested this session.
- AC #4 (Card/Column not absorbing taps): implicitly verified - Button inside surfaces taps register.
- AC #6 (regression test): unknown, ask the implementing agent.
- AC #7 (form on render-test-7): partially - the Button half works; date picker half untested.

Recommendation:
- Close this bead with the Button+TextField paths verified.
- File a follow-up if DateTimeInput / CheckBox / ChoicePicker / Slider need explicit verification (the underlying gesture dispatcher is likely shared so these probably work for free, but worth a confirmation pass).
- Unblock lcp-uo5.7 vertical slice and lcp-uo5.8 server-side dispatcher gate - both have real client behavior to gate against now.

Side observation: the user-tap → no agent response behavior is correct under current state. Phase 5b (lcp-uo5.8) is the server-side handler that consumes these recorded actions and unblocks tool approval. Until that lands, user_action arrives at the shim, gets recorded by lcp-uo5.6 sidecar, and that is the terminus. Not a bug at this layer.
close_reason: A2UI interaction paths are wired: TextField updates data model, DateTimeInput opens picker, Button resolves Action.event and emits user_action; Pixel logcat notes confirm tap emission and A2uiRendererTest passes.

title: REGRESSION: image upload preview in composer is empty thumbnail again (Coil vs native render bug returned)
description:
