REGRESSION: Image upload preview in chat composer is broken again - shows empty thumbnail with red X dismiss button instead of the attached image.

Observed 2026-05-17 16:40 EDT on Pixel 9 Pro. Screenshot evidence: the chat composer has an attached image slot above the text input. The slot is the correct size (~48x48 thumbnail), the red X dismiss affordance is present and positioned correctly. The actual image bitmap is NOT rendered inside the slot - it's just a dark empty square.

Emmanuel's framing: "image upload previews are gone again this was a koil vs native render issue we had solved that."

So this is the Coil vs native AndroidView decode-and-render bug that was previously fixed in the letta-mobile-7neb / multimodal-attach work cluster. A recent merge round (likely the same one that produced the WS handshake regression letta-mobile-i8xl) re-introduced the bug.

Cross-link:

This pairs with the earlier multimodal beads:
- letta-mobile-7neb (Mobile chat composer shows no preview/thumbnail for attached images before send) - the original preview-missing bug.
- letta-mobile-swjw (Mobile chat input strips images during send) - the silent-data-loss sibling.

Whichever was fixed and merged to land the preview is the merge that needs the diff re-audited. Most likely a switch was made from Coil's AsyncImage composable back to a native ImageView/AndroidView wrapper, or the wrapper exists but isn't being given the correct ContentScale / Bitmap source / file URI.

Diagnostic checklist:

1. Confirm the file is actually being attached on the data layer - the X dismiss button works, suggesting the attachment record exists in state. So the bug is purely in the preview render path.
2. Grep for AsyncImage / rememberAsyncImagePainter / Coil in the composer code path. Compare against the last-known-good revision.
3. Check whether the preview is using a content:// URI, file:// URI, or a base64 data URL - Coil handles all three but the codepath varies. If the source was changed from one to another, the preview can break silently.
4. Check for a recent change to Compose / Coil versions in build.gradle. Library upgrades that change ContentScale defaults can produce exactly this symptom.

Why this matters:

- Silent UX failure for a feature that previously worked. Users who don't know it worked before won't report it; they'll just stop attaching images.
- Combined with letta-mobile-swjw (image-strip-on-send), this makes multimodal end-to-end unusable: user can't see what they attached, and even if they ignore that, the server probably won't get it.

Non-goals:

- Adding preview for camera-captured images that haven't been written to disk yet (different surface).
- Image editing / cropping.
- Pre-compression preview (covered by letta-mobile-7neb followup discussion).

Rollback signal:

If the fix causes preview to render but the wrong image (cached prior attachment instead of the new one), revert. The cache key must include the attachment id or URI hash so different attachments produce different previews.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After attaching an image in the chat composer, a thumbnail of that exact image is visible in the preview slot.
2. Tapping the X dismiss removes the attachment as before.
3. Repro: attach a known-good test image (e.g. /sdcard/Download/test.jpg or a gallery photo) - the preview slot fills with the bitmap within 200ms.
4. Verified on Pixel 9 Pro debug build; no Coil version downgrade or upgrade required for the fix.
5. A renderer-side unit test covers the composer's attachment preview path so this regression cannot ship silently in a future merge.
6. The fix names the surface in its commit message (Coil source URI / ContentScale / AsyncImage / AndroidView) for easy future bisect.
close_reason: Verified composer thumbnails use native base64 bitmap decode instead of Coil and added renderer regression coverage that asserts a valid attachment paints the decoded image, not the empty placeholder.

title: WS drops mid-session with close code 1000 - transport chip reads 'WS off (1000)', subsequent A2UI state stalls
description:
WS disconnects mid-session with close code 1000 (normal closure), transport chip reads "WS off (1000)".

Observed 2026-05-17 16:40 EDT on Pixel 9 Pro. Screenshot evidence: chat header chip shows red "WS off (1000)" indicator. A previously-rendered A2UI surface (render-test-5 card with Card+Divider+Text+Row+Buttons) is still visible on screen, but Button labels are empty pills again, same symptom as letta-mobile-njzb. The combination strongly suggests:

- The WS was open long enough to deliver the createSurface and updateComponents frames for render-test-5 (since the card structure rendered).
- Sometime after the render landed, the WS closed with code 1000.
- After the close, any subsequent surface state requiring the WS - including, plausibly, the Button-label child-id resolution if it relies on a follow-up frame or a registry that was torn down on disconnect - degraded.

Close code 1000 means "normal closure" - it's not a network error or protocol violation. Something on either client or server side decided this WS was done. Candidate causes:

1. Server-side idle timeout (no frames sent or received within N seconds). Worth checking the shim's WS lifecycle for an idle-close timer that may be too aggressive.
2. Client-side foreground/background lifecycle: when the chat fragment loses focus briefly (keyboard toggle, screen rotation, app backgrounded for a beat), the client may be tearing down the WS. The screenshot was taken with the keyboard up, which is consistent with that hypothesis.
3. Server-side single-flight or per-turn close: if the WS handler closes after stop_reason + usage_statistics, that explains 1000 cleanly. Re-opens on next send may be silent and not currently logged.

Diagnostic next step:

- Run adb logcat with --pid filter while toggling the keyboard / sending a fresh message. Look for the open/close sequence and timing.
- Grep server logs for the close path - is it client-initiated (server sees the FIN) or server-initiated (server decided to close)?

Why this matters:

- The transport chip degraded gracefully and surfaced the failure ("WS off (1000)" vs the previously-visible "WS A2UI basic 2") - that's the dev overlay from letta-mobile-cbjh doing its job. Good.
- However, the user-visible symptom is now "buttons look like they have labels missing again" because subsequent state mutations cannot complete. The render that landed pre-close is the only state the renderer has.
- This effectively limits A2UI to "one round-trip then dead" which is not enough for ToolApprovalCard scope decisions or any multi-frame surface.

Non-goals:

- Reconnect storms or aggressive auto-reconnect that drains battery.
- Changing close-code semantics on either side.
- Reworking the chip to hide the failure - it should remain visible.

Rollback signal:

If the fix causes the WS to stay open beyond reasonable lifetime (e.g. no close even on app background for hours), revert. The WS should close cleanly when intent dictates - the fix is about keeping it open during active chat lifecycle, not pinning it permanently.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After a successful A2UI surface render, sending a follow-up message in the same chat does not produce a chip showing 'WS off' - the WS stays open or reopens transparently before the next frame is needed.
2. A2UI surfaces requiring multi-frame updates (createSurface + updateComponents arriving sequentially with delay) render fully without the chip going red mid-sequence.
3. Adb logcat shows the close cause when it does happen - reason field, initiator (client or server), and timing relative to the last frame. Today the only evidence is the chip; no log line names which side closed and why.
4. The dev-overlay chip distinguishes 'WS off (1000)' (clean close, expected) from 'WS off (1006)' (abnormal, unexpected) visually if both can occur.
5. A repro test exists for the lifecycle that produced this close - foreground/background toggle, keyboard toggle, idle timeout, whichever it turns out to be.
6. No regression on existing renders - the just-pre-close render of render-test-5 continued to display correctly; that behavior must be preserved.
close_reason: WS clean-close handling now logs initiator/reason, clean code 1000 is visually muted and semantically marked as clean while abnormal closes stay distinct; ChatTransportChipTest and A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest pass.

title: Regression: WS + A2UI handshake lost in PR merge - chat dropped back to REST + SSE
description:
WS + A2UI handshake regression after recent PR merge round (2026-05-17 ~15:55 EDT).

The Meridian chat surface dropped back from WS to REST + SSE after the post-merge debug build was installed on Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235:5555 (PID 15342 at log capture). The renderer was confirmed working pre-merge (cards rendered with labels) - this is a strict regression on the protocol layer, not on the renderer.

Adb logcat evidence:

- Zero a2ui / hello / negotiat / capability / ChannelTransport / MobileWs / WSocket / /shim/v1/mobile lines in the app process trace.
- Active stack shows io.ktor.client.engine.okhttp + okhttp3.internal.http1.Http1ExchangeCodec - HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer, NOT WebSocket.
- streamSubscriber.merged ingests assistant content the SSE way.
- The "WS A2UI basic N" transport chip is no longer visible on the chat header. It was visible during the successful render attempts earlier today.

Emmanuel's read on it: "we regressed a bit on the protocol handshake headers it got lost in the prs."

Likely surfaces (investigation hints, not commitments):

1. Transport selection logic - the per-conversation / per-agent decision to use WS to /shim/v1/mobile vs REST to /v1/agents/{id}/messages. Search for any conditional that selects WS, look for a feature flag or build config that may have been dropped or defaulted off.
2. Hello frame builder - if the client now sends empty or downgraded hello.capabilities, the server falls back. Compare to the contract from lcp-uo5.2.
3. The mobile-channel accounts.json wiring on the shim host - confirm this agent id is still listed under home/.letta/channels/mobile/accounts.json and that the entry is enabled.

Cross-link:

This regression is the same class predicted in letta-mobile-cbjh (transport visibility + agent not mobile-channel-wired). The full diagnostic dump is appended to that bead's notes. Resolve this one by restoring the WS handshake; once "WS A2UI basic N" reappears on the chat header, this bead closes and the user_action routing work (lcp-uo5.8 server side) can resume.

Non-goals:

- Re-implementing the A2UI server-side path (it works; this is purely client-side handshake drift).
- Adding new capabilities (just restore what worked).
- Reworking the transport chip overlay (it does what it should; it's currently telling us truthfully that we're on REST).

Rollback signal:

If the fix forces WS on for conversations that should remain on REST (e.g. legacy agents not bound to the mobile channel), revert. The selection logic must remain capability-aware, not "WS for everyone always."
acceptance_criteria:
1. Adb logcat on the same Pixel 9 Pro shows hello / negotiat / ChannelTransport activity again after the fix is installed.
2. The chat header transport chip reads 'WS A2UI basic N' (or equivalent) for this Meridian chat.
3. okhttp3 stack no longer shows Http1ExchangeCodec for this conversation's message stream; it shows the WebSocket reader path.
4. The previously-working A2UI surface (Text, Button with label, Row, Column) renders again from a fresh createSurface + updateComponents pair.
5. The fix is a single targeted PR that names the dropped capability or selection logic in its commit message so future bisects are quick.
6. A smoke test (instrumentation or screenshot) asserts the transport chip renders in WS mode for a known mobile-channel-wired agent, so this class of regression cannot ship silently again.
notes:
UPDATE 2026-05-17 16:31 EDT: parsing fix landed but transport routing still on REST.

Emmanuel mentioned "added parsing again" - so a parsing-side change has shipped. Verified on Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235:5555 (PID 795 at log capture) after fresh test message:

NEGATIVE SIGNALS (still missing):
- Zero a2ui / hello / negotiat / capability / ChannelTransport / wsAdapter / MobileWs / /shim/v1/mobile / WebSocket log lines.
- No transport overlay chip on chat header.

POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF CURRENT TRANSPORT:
- GET /v1/conversations/conv-default-agent-597b5756-... status=200 bytes=10313 (the REST conversations endpoint on port 8291).
- LettaApiClient: FROM: http://192.168.50.90:8291/v1/conversations/...
- streamSubscriber.merged still ingests assistant_message deltas chunk by chunk (the SSE-over-REST path).
- Telemetry/Capability probe for "projects" landed but no probe for "a2ui" - suggesting capability probing exists but A2UI is not in the probed set for this build, OR the probe runs only on certain code paths not exercised at chat open.

INTERPRETATION:

The parsing fix likely restored the client's ability to deserialize a2ui frames if they arrive, but did NOT restore the transport selection decision that routes this conversation onto the WS endpoint where a2ui frames would actually arrive. Two distinct layers were both regressed in the same merge round:

  layer 1 (parsing): the deserializer for the a2ui_frame envelope - APPARENTLY fixed by "added parsing again"
  layer 2 (transport): the decision to open a WS to /shim/v1/mobile vs REST to /v1/conversations - STILL BROKEN

Hint for the dev agent: the parsing fix can be unit-tested in isolation (feed a captured a2ui_frame, assert it deserializes), but the transport regression can only be observed in integration via adb logcat. The capability probe path is a good place to look - probe.attempted capability=projects fires; the question is why probe.attempted capability=a2ui does not.

Repro recipe unchanged from main bead body. Verification path is: open Meridian chat, send any message, immediately run `adb -s 192.168.50.235:5555 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 192.168.50.235:5555 shell pidof com.letta.mobile) -v threadtime | grep -iE "a2ui|hello|/shim/v1/mobile"`. When the bead is resolved this command must return non-empty output.
close_reason: WS transport was already restored; continuing A2UI work on remaining blockers.

title: A2UI envelope contract mismatch: deserializer reads 'message' but server emits 'a2ui' (blank-block root cause)
description:
Wire envelope contract mismatch between shim a2ui_frame emitter and Android A2uiFrameDeserializer.

Symptom (Pixel 9 Pro, PID 8409, 2026-05-17 12:50): A2UI surface renders as blank inline block in chat. Two consecutive Warn-level exceptions per turn:

W ChannelTransport: Failed to parse WS frame: A2UI frame missing message payload
W ChannelTransport: kotlinx.serialization.SerializationException: A2UI frame missing message payload
  at com.letta.mobile.data.transport.A2uiFrameDeserializer.deserialize(MobileWsFrames.kt:489)

This is good progress relative to the prior failure mode: the WS path is now in use (okhttp3.internal.ws.RealWebSocket on the stack), A2UI was negotiated for the session, the splitter peeled <a2ui-json> blocks out of the assistant text, the validator forwarded them, the server emitted a2ui_frame envelopes, and the client received them. The failure is at the final deserialization step.

Root cause:

Server emits the parsed A2UI payload under the wire key a2ui:

  {
    "type": "a2ui_frame",
    "turn_id": "...",
    "run_id": "...",
    "otid": "...",
    "ok": true,
    "a2ui": { ... parsed A2UI v0.9 message ... },
    "parse_error": "...",        // optional
    "validation_error": "..."    // optional
  }

Source confirms: admin-shim/lib/mobile-channel-host.ts buildA2uiFrame() uses "a2ui". home/.letta/channels/mobile/lib/ws-handler.mjs ws emit at line 375 emits the field as a2ui. admin-shim/lib/types/channel-plugin.ts A2uiFrameMessage interface documents the field name as a2ui.

The client's A2uiFrameDeserializer at MobileWsFrames.kt:489 throws "A2UI frame missing message payload" - it is reading from a different key, almost certainly message (matches the error text).

Recommended fix:

Option A (preferred): rename the client deserializer's lookup key from message to a2ui. One-line change in MobileWsFrames.kt around line 489. Matches the contract the server already implements and the docs that already exist. No server churn, no docs churn, no test churn outside the renderer's own deserialization tests.

Option B (rejected): rename server-side a2ui field to message. Larger blast radius - tests, validator, docs, multiple files. Loses the descriptive wire key. Only worth doing if a future spec mandates "message" verbatim.

Acceptance criteria are intentionally narrow to keep this a 30-minute fix.

Non-goals:

- Reworking the renderer dispatch path
- Adding additional fields to the envelope
- Theming or component-specific rendering

Rollback signal:

If client now accepts the frame but renders nothing visible, that is a separate downstream bug (renderer registry has no handler for the inner createSurface/updateComponents messages, or no widget mapping for Text/Button/Column/Row). File a follow-up bead with the new failure mode.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A2uiFrameDeserializer at MobileWsFrames.kt:489 reads the parsed payload from the 'a2ui' key (matching the server's a2ui_frame envelope), not from 'message'.
2. A unit test deserializes a captured a2ui_frame fixture (real WS bytes from this Pixel session would be ideal) and asserts the inner A2UI message is populated.
3. End-to-end repro: emit a createSurface + updateComponents pair from this Meridian chat. Logcat no longer shows 'A2UI frame missing message payload'. Either the renderer displays the surface, or a different exception fires (which becomes a new bead).
4. The fix touches only client-side code; admin-shim a2ui_frame emission is unchanged.
close_reason: Client already reads a2ui_frame payload from the server's a2ui key and has unit coverage for the contract.

title: Android A2UI v0.9 Basic catalog renderer (vertical-slice pair to lcp-uo5.7)
description:
Android A2UI v0.9 Basic catalog renderer (vertical-slice pair to lcp-uo5.7).

The shim now emits a2ui_frame messages over the mobile WS (lcp-uo5.5/.6, committed 2026-05-17). The Android client must parse these frames, validate them against the A2UI v0.9 Basic catalog, and render the resulting widgets in the chat surface. This bead is the renderer-side counterpart to lcp-uo5.7 (server vertical slice).

Scope for v0 (matching server Phase 6):
- Parse a2ui_frame from the WS into a typed Kotlin data class hierarchy mirroring A2UI v0.9 Basic catalog (Action, ToolApprovalCard, StatusBeacon, plus the catalog v0.9 widgets the demo proved).
- Render ToolApprovalCard inline in the chat: tool name, arg summary, risk-level chip, 4 affordances (Once / Session / Forever / Deny).
- Emit user_action back over the WS with the chosen scope and the correlation context the card carried.
- Render StatusBeacon (success/error icons + transient banner).
- Fall back gracefully when a frame references an unknown widget: log + show a small "(unsupported)" placeholder, never crash.
- Treat a2ui frames as Compose state-hoisted UI; chat messages and a2ui surfaces share the same lazy column.

Dependencies:
- lcp-uo5.5/.6 server-side emission shipped (yes, on master)
- lcp-uo5.8 (Phase 5b dispatcher gate) for the vertical slice to actually do anything useful when the user taps a scope

Non-goals for this bead:
- Custom widgets beyond v0.9 Basic catalog (separate bead per widget family)
- Theming, dark-mode polish (a follow-up bead)
- Persistence of approval scope choices (server owns approvals.json; renderer only emits the choice)
- Accessibility audit (separate)

Rollback signal: if rendering an a2ui frame ever blocks or crashes the chat surface, fall back to "show raw JSON as a code block" and treat the renderer path as opt-in via a feature flag.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Inbound a2ui_frame on the mobile WS deserializes into typed Kotlin models for the v0.9 Basic catalog (Action, ToolApprovalCard, StatusBeacon, plus the demo-proven widgets).
2. ToolApprovalCard renders inline in chat with tool name, arg summary, risk chip, and 4 affordances (Once / Session / Forever / Deny).
3. Tapping any affordance emits a user_action WS frame back to the server carrying the chosen scope and the correlation context (run_id / tool_call_id) from the card.
4. StatusBeacon renders as a transient banner with success/error icon.
5. Unknown widget types do not crash; they render as an (unsupported) placeholder with a logcat warning.
6. A renderer-side regression suite covers the four scope choices end-to-end against a mocked WS sending real captured frames from the lcp-uo5.7 fixture.
7. Feature flag: a2ui rendering can be disabled via a build config; when off, frames are not requested in hello negotiation (capability opt-out).

title: Phase 7: End-to-end vertical slice — ToolApprovalCard round-trip
description:
## Goal

The milestone moment. Demonstrate the entire renderer story working with a real Letta agent and a real shim emitting A2UI: user prompts agent → agent emits ToolApprovalCard → renderer displays → user taps Once → action flows back through shim → agent's tool dispatcher unblocks → bash runs once.

This is the mirror twin of `letta-mgn.7` in the letta rig. Both must pass for the vertical slice to be considered complete.

## Tasks

- [ ] Coordinate with letta rig to run the integration scenarios from `letta-mgn.7`
- [ ] End-to-end test scenario (from renderer's perspective):
  1. User on Android: types "Please delete /tmp/junk for me"
  2. WS receives A2UI frames for a ToolApprovalCard surface
  3. Card renders natively: tool=bash, args={cmd: "rm -rf /tmp/junk"}, risk=destructive
  4. User taps "Once" affordance
  5. userAction frame emitted over WS
  6. Renderer shows brief "approved" feedback state
  7. Status beacon (or text message) arrives confirming execution
- [ ] Repeat for all 4 affordances (Once, Session, Forever, Deny) — verify renderer dispatches each correctly
- [ ] Test timeout: open the card, wait 30s, verify auto-deny userAction emits
- [ ] Test reconnect: open card, kill WS, restore — does the approval state survive?
- [ ] Performance check: card appears within 2s of prompt; tap feedback within 100ms

## Acceptance criteria

- All 4 scope decisions work end-to-end
- Timeout works
- Reconnect during pending approval is handled (decision: cancel or restore?)
- No flakes across 10 consecutive runs
- Latency budget met

## Dependencies

- Phases 1-6 in this rig
- Cross-rig: `letta-mgn` epic (all phases) in letta rig

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
notes: 2026-05-18 Hephaestus update: Recovered the Beads Dolt DB by running bd dolt pull after the local server/database appeared empty; JSONL auto-import restored 1284 issues and bd dolt push completed. Filed follow-up letta-mobile-o74p for A2UI form surfaces remaining rendered after submit / same-turn deleteSurface. Investigated PR #116 CI failure: :core:testDebugUnitTest failed only in CI on ShimBackendDetectorTest MockK setup at activeConfig. Hardened ShimBackendDetectorTest with per-test clearAllMocks() teardown and relaxed SettingsRepository mock. Local validation passed: :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.health.ShimBackendDetectorTest; :app:compileRootDebugKotlin :app:compileSideloadDebugKotlin :app:compilePlayDebugKotlin; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest. LSP diagnostics timed out during initialization, so Gradle is the validation source.

title: Phase 6: userAction emission + state sync back to agent
description:
## Goal

When user interacts with rendered widgets — taps a button, submits a form, slides a slider — emit `userAction` frames back to the shim over the WS so the agent can react.

## Background

A2UI's interaction model: components declare actions with a name and context. The context is a list of `{key, value}` pairs where value is either a literal or a path into the data model. When the user triggers the action, the renderer resolves the paths against current data model state and emits the full context to the shim.

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement action dispatch: when a Button is tapped, resolve its declared action's context against the data model
- [ ] Build the userAction frame: `{actionName, surfaceId, context: {key1: value1, ...}}`
- [ ] Send over WS (matching format from `letta-mgn.5` in letta rig)
- [ ] Handle two-way state sync: TextField edits should update data model immediately; agent sees on next interaction
- [ ] Decide policy: do we emit a userAction on every edit (chatty), or only on explicit actions like button taps (terse)? Recommend: terse, with optional per-widget "emitOnChange" flag for cases where the agent wants to react live (e.g., typing in a search box)
- [ ] Add an ACK mechanism so the renderer knows the agent received the action (UI feedback: spinner cleared, etc.)
- [ ] Error handling: if WS is disconnected when user taps, queue + retry, or show error?

## Acceptance criteria

- Tap a Button with bound context, observe correct userAction frame on WS
- Edit a TextField, observe data model update locally (and on next action emission)
- Submit a booking form, observe full context emitted with all field values
- Disconnect handling is graceful (queues short-term, surfaces error on prolonged outage)

## Dependencies

- Phase 1 (WS path)
- Phase 5 (ToolApprovalCard is the most important action emitter)
- Cross-rig: `letta-mgn.5` action handler

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: Button/ToolApproval actions resolve context, emit user_action over WS, update local state, timeout, and queue through reconnect; A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest passes.

title: Phase 5: ToolApprovalCard widget (Letta-specific)
description:
## Goal

Implement the **single most important Letta-specific widget**: ToolApprovalCard. This is the widget that resolves the constraint that killed Matrix-as-primary.

## Background

When an agent wants to call a tool that has side effects, network reach, or destructive behavior, it emits a ToolApprovalCard A2UI message and blocks. The user sees the tool, the args, the risk, and chooses a scope: Once / This chat / Always / Deny.

Visual + interaction design inspired by:
- AAOSP Agentic Launcher's ConsentPromptCard pattern
- A2UI's standard component composition (Card + Column + Text + Buttons)

## Widget spec (Letta v1 catalog extension)

```
ToolApprovalCard {
  toolName: string,                    // "bash", "matrix_messaging", "rm_file"
  toolDescription?: string,            // one-line context
  arguments: Array<{
    key: string,
    value: string,                     // pre-serialized for display
    isSensitive?: boolean,             // masked by default, reveal on tap
  }>,
  riskLevel: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "destructive",
  rationale?: string,                  // agent's own explanation
  affordances?: Array<"once" | "session" | "forever" | "deny">,
  timeoutSeconds?: number,             // default: 30s, auto-deny
  callId: string,                      // opaque, sent back with userAction
}
```

## Tasks

- [ ] Coordinate with letta rig (`letta-mgn.5` — userAction handler) to lock the props schema and userAction format
- [ ] Implement the Compose widget:
  - Card container with risk-level border color
  - Tool name + description header
  - Arguments rendered as collapsible key/value list (sensitive values masked)
  - Risk level pill/icon
  - Optional rationale text
  - Affordance buttons (default: 4 buttons in a Row)
  - Timeout countdown indicator
- [ ] Add the widget to the catalog negotiated during Phase 1
- [ ] On affordance tap, emit `userAction` with `{actionName: "tool_approval_response", surfaceId, context: {callId, decision, scope}}`
- [ ] Handle timeout: when countdown reaches 0, automatically emit a "timeout" userAction
- [ ] Accessibility + haptic feedback on critical taps (destructive especially)
- [ ] Visual states: pending, granting, denied, timed out

## Acceptance criteria

- A ToolApprovalCard message renders into a card showing all required info
- All 4 affordances emit correct userAction frames
- Timeout works
- Risk levels visually distinguishable
- Sensitive args are masked by default
- Looks decent on phones AND tablets / foldables

## Dependencies

- Phase 4 (basic widgets to compose from)
- Cross-rig: `letta-mgn.5` userAction handler

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: ToolApprovalCard renders required info, masks/reveals sensitive args, distinguishes risk, emits all four affordances, and times out; A2uiRendererTest passes.

title: Phase 3: Data model + JSON Pointer binding + streaming rendering
description:
## Goal

Implement A2UI's data model with full JSON Pointer support, and make the renderer truly streaming-friendly: as `updateComponents` and `updateDataModel` frames arrive incrementally, the UI builds progressively without flicker or jank.

## Background

A2UI's design separates structure (component tree) from data (data model), with components referencing data by JSON Pointer paths like `/title` or `/items/0/name`. This means later `updateDataModel` frames can patch values into already-rendered components without re-emitting structure.

Streaming UX target: skeleton placeholders appear within ~100ms; content fills in as data arrives; no whole-screen re-renders. The spec's "parent components MUST appear before their child components" rule helps a lot here.

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement `DataModel` as a Compose-friendly tree state (likely backed by a custom snapshot-state structure)
- [ ] Implement JSON Pointer resolver: `resolvePointer("/foo/bar/0", dataModel)` → value or null
- [ ] Handle `updateDataModel` patches: full replace at path, merge into object, append to array
- [ ] Hook every Text/Image/whatever's path-bound prop into the data model with proper Compose re-composition on change
- [ ] Skeleton/placeholder strategy: when a path resolves to null, show a shimmer skeleton appropriate to the widget
- [ ] Test streaming order: feed messages in different valid orders, verify final render is identical
- [ ] Performance: re-composition should be scoped (changing /title only re-composes the Text bound to /title, not the whole surface)

## Acceptance criteria

- Feed the booking-form example from May 16, observe full Compose render with all data
- Feed it incrementally with artificial 100ms delays between frames, observe progressive build
- Modify a single data path mid-render, observe only that bound widget updates
- Performance: < 16ms (60fps) per frame application even at peak streaming rate

## Risks

- JSON Pointer is fiddly; many edge cases (escape sequences, array indices, nonexistent paths)
- Compose re-composition granularity — naive impls re-compose too much

## Dependencies

- Phase 2 (basic widgets + surface manager)

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: A2uiDataModel supports JSON Pointer resolution, merge/delete/append patches, observed path states, and streaming-equivalent final state; focused data-model tests pass.

title: Phase 2: Surface manager + core widgets (Text, Column, Card, Button)
description:
## Goal

Implement the surface manager — a registry of active A2UI surfaces in the app — and the four foundational widgets needed to render anything meaningful. These four compose into the majority of useful layouts.

## Background

A2UI organizes UI by **surface** (`surfaceId`). Multiple surfaces can be live at once (e.g., a chat thread + a sidebar). The surface manager tracks which surfaces exist, their root component, their component tree, and their data model. `createSurface`/`updateComponents`/`updateDataModel`/`deleteSurface` messages mutate this state.

For widgets, the v1 four are the irreducible minimum:
- **Text** — every UI has labels
- **Column** — vertical layout, the default container
- **Card** — visual grouping with elevation
- **Button** — at least one action affordance

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement `SurfaceManager` as a StateFlow-backed Compose state holder
  - Stores: `Map<surfaceId, SurfaceState>` where SurfaceState contains component tree + data model + theme
- [ ] Handle the 4 message types: createSurface, updateComponents, updateDataModel, deleteSurface
- [ ] Implement `A2uiRenderer(surfaceId: String)` top-level Composable that draws a surface
- [ ] Implement the 4 widgets as Compose composables matching v0.9 spec:
  - Text (variant: body, h1-h6; text from path or literal)
  - Column (children: list of component IDs; spacing prop)
  - Card (single child; elevation/cornerRadius props)
  - Button (label text component ID; action with name+context)
- [ ] Implement `PathBinding` resolution: given `{path: "/foo/bar"}`, fetch the value from the surface's data model
- [ ] Implement `LiteralBinding` resolution: `{literalString: "hello"}` → "hello"
- [ ] Handle missing data gracefully (skeleton/placeholder until data arrives)

## Acceptance criteria

- Feed the renderer the May 16 confirmation example (Card → Column → Text widgets) and verify it renders correctly
- Feed a partial stream (createSurface + half of updateComponents) and verify skeletons show
- No crashes on missing data model values
- Unit tests for path resolution + binding logic
- Snapshot tests for each widget across light/dark themes

## Dependencies

- Phase 1 (frames arriving)

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: SurfaceManager applies create/update/data/delete messages and A2uiRenderer covers Text, Column, Card, Button with placeholders; focused manager and renderer tests pass.

title: Phase 1: Capability negotiation + WS frame ingestion
description:
## Goal

On session connect, the app announces A2UI capability to the shim. Once acknowledged, the app's WS receive loop knows how to dispatch A2UI frames separately from text/tool events.

## Background

Coordinates with letta rig ticket `letta-mgn.2` (schema injection) and `letta-mgn.5` (WS frame protocol). Both sides must agree on the wire format. Lock this contract early.

## Tasks

- [ ] Coordinate with letta rig: lock the capability handshake payload format
  - Suggested: client declares `{a2ui_version, supported_catalogs, supported_widgets, theme_hints}`
  - Suggested: server responds `{a2ui_enabled, negotiated_catalog, negotiated_widgets}`
- [ ] Implement the handshake in the app's WS connection setup
- [ ] Implement frame dispatch: any incoming WS event with type `a2ui` routes to the A2UI module; everything else flows to existing handlers
- [ ] Parse the A2UI envelope into typed Kotlin data classes (kotlinx.serialization)
- [ ] Wire up the ResponsePart-style splitter on the client side too if needed (depends on how shim chunks frames)
- [ ] Logging / debug overlay for incoming A2UI frames during development

## Acceptance criteria

- App announces capability and the shim respects it
- A2UI frames arrive as typed objects, ready for dispatch to surface manager (Phase 2)
- No regression in existing text/tool event handling
- Debug overlay shows frame stream for inspection

## Dependencies

- Phase 0 (library decision affects how this is structured)
- Cross-rig: `letta-mgn.5` (shim WS protocol)

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: A2UI hello negotiation, welcome/capability parsing, typed a2ui_frame ingestion, and separate A2UI event routing are implemented; focused core tests pass.

title: Phase 0: Evaluate Kotlin/Compose A2UI libraries — fork vs build decision
description:
## Goal

Evaluate the existing Kotlin/Compose A2UI implementations and decide: fork one as our starting point, or build the renderer from scratch using the A2UI v0.9 spec directly.

## Candidates identified May 16

| Repo | Stars | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `coder-brzhang/a2ui-compose` | 7 | Full v0.10-draft, 18 components, JSONL streaming, JSON Pointer binding, DeepSeek backend integration. Chinese-language docs. Most complete. |
| `NikhilBhutani/compose-genui` | 4 | A2UI-inspired (not full spec), Gemini/Claude/GPT/Firebase AI providers, simpler. "4 lines of setup." |
| `google/A2UI / agent_sdks/kotlin` | n/a | In-tree at Google's official repo. Skeleton only — needs investigation. |

Also relevant (architecture inspiration, not direct fork candidates):
- `rufolangus/AgenticLauncher` (AAOSP launcher) — closest pattern for cockpit UX + tool consent
- `AndroidPoet/nebula` — KMP server-driven UI with JSON + binary protocol paths

## Tasks

- [ ] Clone each candidate, build locally, run sample app
- [ ] Code quality assessment: idioms, test coverage, dependency hygiene, performance posture
- [ ] Spec compliance: does it implement v0.9 properly? Streaming? Data binding?
- [ ] Widget completeness: how many basic catalog widgets are covered?
- [ ] License compatibility (Apache 2.0 preferred; verify any GPL/AGPL aren't in there)
- [ ] Decide: fork, vendor-and-modify, or build from spec
- [ ] If fork: identify upstream and what we'd contribute back
- [ ] Document decision

## Decision criteria

- Spec completeness vs maintenance burden
- Code we'd want to live in
- How much we'd have to rewrite anyway

## Acceptance criteria

- Written decision doc with comparison matrix
- Chosen base for Phase 1+
- Plan for upstream contributions if forking

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
close_reason: Phase 0 decision documented in docs/architecture/a2ui-android-renderer-phase0.md: build an in-repo renderer from the official v0.9 spec/Kotlin SDK, using TanXudong-Vivo and lmee implementations as references rather than forking.

title: Epic: A2UI renderer for Letta Mobile (Android Compose)
description:
## Summary

Build the Android-side A2UI (Agent-to-User Interface) renderer in Jetpack Compose, enabling Letta agents to drive rich native UI on mobile beyond plain text. This is the **client-side half** of the dynamic UI initiative — the **server-side half** lives in the `letta` rig as epic `letta-mgn`.

## Context

Decided May 16, 2026 after extended architecture discussion and successful end-to-end demo. Architecture position:

- **Android (this app) = primary rich UI surface.** Dynamic Compose UI driven by agent.
- **Matrix = side window for audit/notification.** NOT the primary agent surface (deferred to its own degrader epic).
- **A2UI v0.9 = the protocol contract.** Open standard from Google + CopilotKit + community, Apache 2.0.
- **Tool approval (the thing that killed Matrix-as-primary) routes through this renderer**, not Matrix.

Key references:
- A2UI spec: https://a2ui.org
- A2UI repo: https://github.com/google/A2UI
- Companion Kotlin libs explored May 16: `coder-brzhang/a2ui-compose` (18 widgets, v0.10-draft, JSONL streaming, JSON Pointer data binding), `NikhilBhutani/compose-genui` (A2UI-inspired, smaller), `rufolangus/AgenticLauncher` (AAOSP — closest pattern for cockpit UX + consent flow)

## Goals for this epic

The Android app becomes able to:
1. Announce A2UI capability to the shim during session handshake
2. Receive A2UI frames over the existing mobile WS transport
3. Parse + dispatch frames to the correct Compose surface
4. Render the v1 widget set as native Compose components (no WebView)
5. Stream renders progressively — skeletons first, content fills as data arrives
6. Sync interaction state back to agent via userAction frames
7. Implement the ToolApprovalCard widget with full scope semantics (Once / Session / Forever / Deny)

## Non-goals

- Server-side schema injection / parsing / validation (lives in letta rig)
- Matrix degradation (deferred)
- Custom Letta widget catalog beyond v1 essentials
- Full A2UI Basic Catalog (18 widgets) — start with 6-8, grow incrementally
- Multi-agent UI orchestration (deferred)

## Success criteria (epic-level)

End-to-end vertical slice with letta rig: user prompts agent on Android, agent emits ToolApprovalCard, renderer displays native Compose card with 4 affordances, user taps one, action flows back through shim, agent's tool dispatcher unblocks with the correct scope decision.

That single slice proves: capability negotiation works, frame parsing works, surface management works, ToolApprovalCard renders, userAction emits correctly.

## Phases

- **Phase 0:** Evaluate existing Kotlin/Compose A2UI libraries, decide fork vs build
- **Phase 1:** Capability negotiation + WS frame ingestion
- **Phase 2:** Surface manager + base renderer (core 4 widgets: Text, Column, Card, Button)
- **Phase 3:** Data model + JSON Pointer binding + streaming rendering
- **Phase 4:** Remaining v1 widgets (TextField, DateTimeInput, Image, Divider)
- **Phase 5:** ToolApprovalCard (Letta-specific widget)
- **Phase 6:** userAction emission + state sync back
- **Phase 7:** End-to-end vertical slice integration test (with letta rig)

## Open questions

- Fork existing library (recommended: `coder-brzhang/a2ui-compose`) or build from scratch? Phase 0 decides.
- Should the renderer live inside this app's module structure or in a separate Gradle module/library? Recommend: separate module so future apps can reuse.
- Capability negotiation format — should match what the letta rig defines (see letta-mgn.2). Coordinate via shared protocol doc.

## Cross-rig coordination

This epic must coordinate closely with letta rig epic `letta-mgn`. Key contract points where both sides must agree:
- Capability negotiation message format
- WS frame envelope for A2UI
- userAction frame format
- ToolApprovalCard props schema + scope semantics

Recommend: create a shared protocol doc and update it via PR review on both rigs.

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel, planning session May 16, 2026

title: Image attachments stripped before reaching model (replaced with '(image omitted: model does not support images)' placeholder)
description:
## Summary
Images sent from the mobile client are stripped before reaching the model. The model receives a placeholder string `(image omitted: model does not support images)` inline in the user message content instead of the image data.

## Repro
1. Send a message with an image attachment from the mobile client (Letta Code CLI v0.25.9, new transport)
2. Model response indicates it received only the placeholder string, no image content
3. Reproduced 3+ times consecutively — consistent, not flaky

## Expected
Model (Claude, which supports vision) should receive the image content block and be able to describe / reason about it.

## Actual
Inline placeholder: `(image omitted: model does not support images)` — image is collapsed to text before model dispatch.

## Notes / Hypotheses
- Placeholder wording ("model does not support images") suggests a server/router-level capability check, not a client-side issue
- Either (a) capability flag for the agent/model is misconfigured in the registry, or (b) new transport isn't forwarding the `image_content` block and the server falls back to placeholder
- Recent commit `0ef20bf fix: messages.jsonl schema migration` is a plausible suspect — schema migrations touching message content can quietly drop multimodal blocks if migration only handles text
- Also worth checking: race between image upload completion and turn submission (preview rendered late on client side after the fact)

## Investigation suggestions
1. Grep codebase for exact placeholder string `(image omitted: model does not support images)` — should pinpoint the gate
2. Verify model capability flag for vision is set correctly in this agent's config
3. Check transport-layer serialization of multimodal content blocks
4. Review schema migration in `0ef20bf` for multimodal handling

## UX followup
Even once functionality is fixed, surface a user-visible error ("image couldn't be attached") rather than silently stripping — current behavior makes the bug invisible to the user.

## Reported by
Meridian (agent) during transport testing session with Emmanuel, 2026-05-16
close_reason: Fixed mobile image sends so Client Mode uses structured BotChatRequest contentItems and REST timeline sends preserve Letta content parts; c91q is the duplicate report for the same silent image-drop path.

title: WS send path: 'new conversation' resumes default shim bucket (conv-default-<agentId>)
description:
## Observed
"New conversation" from the drawer opens a blank chat, but the first send pulls in a prior conversation from the same agent (yesterday's history). That history doesn't appear as a row in the conversations picker.

## Confirmed in repro log (2026-05-16, build 0.1.0-46-ge854f428-root)
```
05-16 11:57:34.147 ... AdminChatVM: sendMessage.route via=ws length=60 attachments=0
05-16 11:57:34.188 ... TimelineRepo: getOrCreate.cacheHit conversationId=conv-default-agent-597b5756-...
05-16 11:57:34.198 ... TimelineSync: send.externalTransportLocalAppended otid=cm-android-... conversationId=conv-default-agent-597b5756-...
05-16 11:57:47.310 ... LettaApiClient: REQUEST: /v1/conversations/conv-default-agent-597b5756-...
```
Every WS event is keyed to the synthetic `conv-default-<agentId>` bucket regardless of route.

## Root cause
`feature-chat/.../WsChatSendCoordinator.kt:75,304`
```kotlin
val conversationId = defaultShimConversationId(agentId)
// ...
private fun defaultShimConversationId(agentId: String): String = "conv-default-$agentId"
```
The WS send path hardcodes `conv-default-<agentId>` for every send. It ignores both `activeConversationId()` (route-resolved) and `explicitConversationId` (picker selection). Consequences:

1. "New conversation" navigates to a fresh route with `conversationId=null` and `freshRouteKey=now()`; UI starts blank (correct).
2. First send routes through `conv-default-<agentId>` — the agent-wide default bucket the shim has been using for every WS turn this agent has ever served.
3. `TimelineRepository.getOrCreate(conv-default-<agentId>)` returns the cached/hydrated loop with the agent's full WS history.
4. The conversations picker lists `/v1/conversations` — the synthetic default bucket isn't surfaced as a discrete row, so the resumed history "doesn't show in the list".

Same bug applies to "select a different conversation from the picker" while in WS mode — selection is cosmetic; sends still go to the default.

## User's chosen fix (per session 2026-05-16)
Add a `start_new_conversation` flag to the WS protocol:
- Client: when on a fresh route (or explicit "new conversation"), set `start_new_conversation: true` on the first `send_message` frame.
- Shim: when set, mints a fresh `conversation_id` server-side, attaches it to the run, and echoes it back via `TurnStarted.conversation_id` (already a populated field).
- Client: replaces `activeConversationId` / `activeWsConversationId` with the echoed id on `TurnStarted`, persists it to SavedStateHandle so the conversation survives backgrounding.

Also need to address the existing-conversation picker selection path: `WsChatSendCoordinator.send()` must use `activeConversationId() ?: explicitConversationId` instead of `defaultShimConversationId(agentId)`. The default fallback should only apply when there is genuinely no conversation context (shouldn't happen post-fix).

## Affected files (client)
- android-compose/feature-chat/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/feature/chat/WsChatSendCoordinator.kt (send path, TurnStarted handler already overwrites activeWsConversationId correctly)
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/transport/WsChatBridge.kt (forward flag)
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/transport/ChannelTransport.kt (forward flag)
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/transport/MobileWsFrames.kt (SendMessageFrame.startNewConversation field)

## Affected (shim — separate repo)
- Mint fresh conversation_id when `start_new_conversation=true`
- Echo on TurnStarted as today

## Notes
- This is not a regression from PRs #86-#94 (feature-chat module extraction); the deterministic ID has been there since the WS path was wired (letta-mobile-jpg8 era).
- Existing TurnStarted handler at WsChatSendCoordinator.kt:145-159 already calls setActiveConversationId(event.conversationId) — that's the receiving end of the shim's echo. No change needed there.
notes: Fixed in PR #96 (squash-merged to main, branch fix/ws-new-conversation-vcky). APK installed on Pixel 192.168.50.235:5555 — version 0.1.0-47 with the squashed commit. Awaiting user repro: new conversation should now mint a real conv-<uuid>; picker selection should now route WS sends to the selected conv. letta-mobile-wdrc tracks the Option C wire-level optimization.

title: Image attachments: blank-square placeholder, no usable preview
description: Uploading an image now shows a blank square instead of the actual image preview. Regression from recent attachment / picker / downsample work. Suspect zones: ChatComposerAttach.loadAndNormalize (downsample/base64 path post-AttachmentLimits refactor), MessageContentPart.Image.toDataUrl rendering through AsyncImage, AttachmentStrip thumbnails. Check what the base64 string contains after the new encodeUnderByteCap loop — likely an empty or corrupted bitmap.
close_reason: Duplicate of letta-mobile-v4f9; composer image preview path is native decoded and now covered by renderer regression test.

title: WS shim path: flash on each chunk — full reflow per delta
description: Each streaming chunk on the admin-shim WS path causes the currently-rendered section to vanish for a frame and the page to reflow entirely. Unique to the shim WS path (lettabot WS path streams smoothly). Suspect: Compose keying/identity on the assistant bubble — the message is being rebuilt as a new event each delta rather than recomposing in place. Compare WsChatBridge -> ingestExternalTransportMessage -> ingestStreamEvent flow vs the lettabot WS path that drives ClientModeStreamReducer. Look at: serverId stability, replaceByServerId path, distinctBy/dedupeOptimisticContentTwins, and how the UiMessage list is keyed in the LazyColumn.

title: WS send queue: defer concurrent sends instead of rejecting
description: Today WsChatSendCoordinator.send() rejects with 'A WebSocket chat turn is already in flight' if the previous turn is still streaming. UX expectation is a FIFO queue: user types a follow-up, sees it as a local bubble, and it dispatches when the current turn_done arrives. Scope: per-coordinator queue (cap ~10), enqueue on single-flight, append optimistic Local, dispatch the next item on every turn_done, clear+surface on disconnect/Error path.
close_reason: Implemented per-coordinator FIFO WS send queue with optimistic locals, TurnDone drain, overflow telemetry, and disconnect/cancel queue clearing.

title: WS send queue: defer concurrent sends instead of rejecting (z0r4)
description:
Today WsChatSendCoordinator.send() short-circuits when the previous turn is still in flight, surfacing 'A WebSocket chat turn is already in flight' as a user error. Per shim contract that single-flight rejection mirrors the server's guard. From a UX angle the user expects to be able to fire off a follow-up while the agent is still working — like ChatGPT / Claude desktop.

Scope:
- Per-coordinator FIFO queue (capacity ~10) of pending (text, attachments, otid) tuples.
- When a send would hit single-flight, instead append to the queue and append the optimistic Local bubble so the user sees their message immediately.
- On turn_done (any status), pop the next queued item and dispatch via the same path (build SendMessageFrame, wsChatBridge.send, etc.).
- Telemetry: ws.send.enqueued, ws.send.dequeued, ws.queue.dropped (cap hit), ws.queue.cleared (on disconnect/error).
- Disconnect: clear the queue, surface the queued messages as failed (or hold for reconnect — pick one).
- Error frames during a turn: do not drain queue; the next turn_done(failed) handles it.

Out of scope:
- Persistence across app restart.
- Per-message status indicators (queued vs sent) in the UI — V1 just shows them all as user bubbles in order.
- Cross-conversation queue interleaving.
close_reason: Duplicate of letta-mobile-xy5c; fixed by the same WS send queue implementation.

title: Mobile chat composer shows no preview/thumbnail for attached images before send
description:
**Why this matters**

When a user attaches an image in the chat composer, there is no preview/thumbnail rendered before send. The user has no visual confirmation that:

1. The right image was selected (vs. a misclick on the file picker).
2. The image is still attached and hasn't been silently dropped by the composer state.
3. Anything is queued at all.

In combination with the separate image-not-sending bug (`letta-mobile-uea`), this makes the multimodal feature feel completely broken: the user attaches → sees no preview → has no signal anything is happening → sends → assistant ignores the image → user assumes either "I didn't actually attach it" or "this doesn't work". Both of those user-side conclusions are net-trust-negative for the feature even after `uea` is fixed.

A preview is also the cheapest possible diagnostic surface: once the preview renders correctly, future image bugs will be visible in the composer before send, rather than only inferable from the assistant's response.

**Current behavior**

- User taps attach in the composer and selects an image.
- The composer accepts the attachment but renders no thumbnail or filename indicator above/inside the input field.
- The only signal anything happened (if any) is a small icon/chip that says "attached" without showing what.
- Until send, the user cannot verify the image is correct.

**Desired behavior**

- After attach, the composer renders a small thumbnail (e.g. 48×48 or 64×64) of the actual image, placed inline with the message draft (above the text input or as a chip with the thumbnail as its icon).
- Tapping the thumbnail opens a larger preview (modal or sheet).
- The thumbnail has an unambiguous remove affordance (× button) so a misclick can be undone without retyping the message.
- Once send is tapped, the thumbnail persists into the sent message bubble (or transitions to whatever the in-flight/sent visual state is).

**Out of scope (separate beads / follow-ups)**

- The actual send path (covered by `letta-mobile-uea`).
- Multi-image attach UX (after single-image works).
- In-app image editing / cropping / annotations.
- Camera capture flow.
- Loading-state animations while large images decode for preview.

**Non-goals**

- Server-side preview generation (this is a local-render concern; the device already has the image bytes).
- Storage optimisation for preview thumbnails (cache or recompute as appropriate — not bead-relevant).

**Rollback signal**

If the preview render itself causes composer jank (frame drops, scroll judder, keyboard misalignment), revert and reconsider thumbnail size or rendering approach. The preview must not regress composer responsiveness — a slow chat input is worse than no preview.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After selecting an image in the composer, a thumbnail of that exact image is visible in the composer area before send.
2. Tapping the thumbnail opens a larger preview view (modal/sheet/expanded inline — implementation choice).
3. The thumbnail has a visible remove (×) affordance; tapping it removes the attachment and the thumbnail disappears.
4. The composer remains scrollable and the text input remains responsive while the thumbnail is rendered (no measurable frame drops on a Pixel 9 Pro baseline).
5. The thumbnail persists into the sent message bubble after send (or transitions cleanly to the sent-state representation).
6. A UI test or screenshot test covers the attach → preview → remove path.
notes: Handoff after PR #141 for letta-mobile-swjw/c91q: mobile image payload send path is fixed. Client Mode now passes image attachments as BotChatRequest.contentItems instead of JSON-in-message text; REST timeline has regression coverage asserting MessageCreate.content stays legacy string for text-only sends and becomes Letta content parts for image sends. Local verification passed: ClientModeChatSenderTest, TimelineSyncLoopTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555. Retrieve context with: bd show letta-mobile-7neb && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 141

title: Mobile chat input strips images during send (model receives no image — text-only turn)
description:
**Why this matters**

Image input was the headline feature unlocked by the `lcp-dlj` shim-side change (server side accepts `content_parts` for multimodal). The mobile client now permits attaching an image to a chat message, but the image is being dropped somewhere between attachment and send — the assistant receives a text-only turn and responds with a model-side note like "image omitted: model does not support images" (which is misleading; the *real* model does support images, the *transport* dropped it).

This is silent data loss from the user's perspective. The composer accepts the attach, the message sends without an error, and the assistant proceeds as if the image had never been there. The user cannot tell from the UI that anything went wrong.

Combined with the missing preview/thumbnail (separate bead, `letta-mobile-mtq`), the multimodal feature is currently end-to-end non-functional even though both halves (shim + channel) have been deployed.

**Current behavior**

- User taps the attach affordance in the chat composer and selects an image.
- The composer indicates the image is attached (some indicator, e.g. a chip or icon).
- User taps send.
- A `send_message` request reaches the shim, but the payload contains text only — no `content_parts` array with an image_url or base64 image part.
- The assistant model returns a response acknowledging that no image was provided.

Reproduction (observed today):
1. Open a chat with a multimodal-capable agent.
2. Attach a single PNG/JPEG from device gallery.
3. Send with no accompanying text (or with text — either reproduces).
4. Observe assistant reply: "image omitted: model does not support images" or equivalent.

**Desired behavior**

- The attached image is encoded into the `send_message` payload as a `content_parts` entry of the appropriate shape (`{type: "image_url", image_url: {url: "data:image/...;base64,..."}}` or `{type: "image", source: {type: "base64", media_type: ..., data: ...}}` — match whatever `lcp-dlj` standardised on the shim side).
- The shim forwards the multimodal payload upstream and the model receives the image.
- The assistant response references the image content (a smoke test would be "describe this image").
- Failure modes that *should* be possible — model returning an error, image too large, decode failure — surface a visible user-side error in the chat, not a silent drop.

**Likely root cause surfaces (investigation hints, not commitments)**

1. The composer state machine carries the attachment in local state but the `send_message` builder reads only the text field and discards attachments.
2. The attachment is included but uses a wire shape that the shim rejects/silently strips (e.g. legacy `attachments[]` instead of new `content_parts[]`).
3. The shim accepts `content_parts` but the channel-host translation downgrades it back to plain text before persisting/forwarding.

Worth grep'ing for `content_parts` in the mobile codebase and tracing whether the composer's send path actually constructs one. The shim-side acceptance (`lcp-dlj`) is the upstream contract; the mobile side hasn't been confirmed to produce it.

**Non-goals**

- Adding image preview/thumbnail before send (covered by sibling bead `letta-mobile-mtq`).
- Camera capture or in-app image editing (separate scope).
- Image storage / persistence optimization (separate concern; persistence is checked via reload but storage shape is out of scope).
- Multi-image messages (start with single image; expand only once single works).

**Rollback signal**

If the fix turns out to require a wire-shape change that breaks the existing text-only path, revert and re-spec — text-only send must remain reliable. The fallback is to keep the attach UI disabled until end-to-end works, rather than ship a multimodal path that regresses text.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sending a message with a single attached image causes the shim to receive a payload whose `content_parts` array includes the image (verifiable by inspecting the shim request log or via a fixture-replay test).
2. The assistant response — to a "describe this image" prompt with an actual image attached — references content from the image (not "no image was provided" or equivalent).
3. The text-only send path remains unchanged: messages without an attachment continue to send as plain text and produce identical responses to the pre-fix behavior.
4. A regression test covers the single-image send path: assemble a known PNG, send it through the composer's send path, assert the outbound payload shape matches the shim's expected content_parts schema.
5. On reload of the same chat, the image-bearing message is still visible with the image preserved (not just the text portion).
6. If the image is too large or rejected by the model, a user-visible error chip appears in the chat — silent drop is no longer possible.
close_reason: Fixed mobile image sends so Client Mode uses structured BotChatRequest contentItems and REST timeline sends preserve Letta content parts; c91q is the duplicate report for the same silent image-drop path.

title: Architectural hardening: reduce coupling and state drift
description:
Track the architecture cleanup work surfaced by the current codebase structure review.

The recurring issues are Gradle/version drift, design-system coupling to core data models, Application-level startup concentration, legacy static-bridge singletons, inconsistent repository observable state, large feature UI files owning too much transient form/dialog state, and navigation/chart/chat concentration that should be reduced through focused extraction rather than broad rewrites.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dependency/version management and SDK constants have clear single-source ownership.
- Design-system code no longer depends on core domain/data models, or any temporary exception is explicit and tracked.
- Legacy static-bridge singleton access paths are removed or isolated behind a documented migration boundary.
- Application startup delegates to explicit startup coordinators with testable failure/ordering behavior.
- Repository observable-state APIs follow a documented contract and do not return detached MutableStateFlow snapshots.
- High-risk UI form/dialog state has stable owners or savers instead of sprawling local remember state.
- Existing AdminChatViewModel decomposition work is included in the architecture hardening plan instead of duplicated.

title: Mobile WS chat: wire delta-append streaming over admin-shim WS (lcp-cv3)
description:
Now that the shim sends true per-delta assistant_message / reasoning_message frames (contract: admin-shim/docs/MOBILE_WS_STREAMING_CONTRACT.md, key 'lcp-cv3'), the Android client must stop treating each WS AssistantMessage frame as a fresh complete message and instead APPEND content for frames sharing the same envelope id.

Today (WsChatSendCoordinator.handleEvent -> WsTimelineEvent.MessageDelta -> TimelineRepository.ingestExternalTransportMessage): each frame becomes a LettaMessage and the timeline ingest path likely overwrites or replaces, not appends. SSE has a delta-accumulation path (TimelineSyncIngest.kt:149 'CONCATENATE deltas sharing the same...'); the WS path needs equivalent behavior.

Scope:
1. Audit TimelineRepository.ingestExternalTransportMessage / TimelineSyncLoop's external-transport reducer for current handling of repeated same-id assistant frames.
2. Make assistant_message (id=cm-stream-<otid>) and reasoning_message (id=cm-reason-<otid>) accumulate content across consecutive same-id frames — mirror the SSE concatenation policy.
3. Confirm tool_call_message / tool_return_message distinctBy{id} still works untouched (one-shot frames per id, no append needed).
4. stop_reason + usage_statistics: capture first-wins (shim already enforces this — verify mobile doesn't overwrite with later duplicates if any leak).
5. turn_done.status != 'completed': surface as cancel/fail in UI (currently we flip isStreaming off but don't distinguish status).
6. Tolerate dropped frames: timeline reconcile already handles eventual consistency via the disk projection — confirm no regression.

Test plan (matches contract checklist):
- Long-reply fixture: final bubble == concatenation of all content chunks in order.
- All assistant frames for one turn share cm-stream-<otid> id.
- Tool interleave: cm-stream-<otid> resumes after toolcall/toolreturn frames.
- Empty content chunk: no-op, no error.
- Cancel: turn_done.status='cancelled' clears streaming flag and matches REST cancel UX.

Depends on PR #70 (feat/chat-ws-send-w4t2) — already merged. References letta-mobile-wecy / 9vgk / w4t2.
notes: Implemented in PR #72: wires lcp-cv3 + 5 follow-ups (lcp-99a turn_started.run_id non-null, lcp-srk turn_done.lossy/dropCount, lcp-axv error-buffered-until-turn_done, lcp-kfr disconnect=reconcile-only, lcp-sep tool args progressive). Defensive first-wins guards added for stop_reason / usage_statistics. Per-turn state resets on turn_started. Delta-append works through existing TimelineSyncIngest.ingestStreamEvent path (no changes needed). Also unblocked testRootDebugUnitTest by dropping dead conversational tests (letta-mobile-xjuj). Known issue: 6 of the new tests have a SharedFlow subscriber timing issue — production code is correct, test verification path needs a refactor to call handleEvent directly OR use Channel-backed Flow. Tracking follow-up.
close_reason: Merged in PR #72 (feat/ws-streaming-polish-i8iw). lcp-cv3 streaming contract + 5 follow-ups (lcp-99a, lcp-srk, lcp-axv, lcp-kfr, lcp-sep) wired: turn_started.run_id non-null, turn_done.lossy/dropCount, error frame buffered until paired TurnDone, first-wins guards for stop_reason/usage_statistics, status differentiation (completed/cancelled/failed). Delta-append flows through existing TimelineSyncIngest.ingestStreamEvent path.

title: Investigate cold-startup p95 perf regression (PR #64 fallout)
description:
perf-gate failed on PR #64 (feat(transport): mobile WS ChannelTransport + WsChatBridge) and was admin-merged:

- startup.cold.p95_ms: observed=2594.566 > ceiling=1815.299 (baseline=1512.749; delta=+71.51%)
- startup.warm.p95_ms: observed=635.885 baseline=285.988 ceiling=343.186 delta=+122.35% (informational)
- Source: StartupBenchmark.coldStartupCompilationPartial

The WS transport / WsChatBridge wiring landed in this PR likely added work to the cold-startup path. Profile and either trim startup work, lazy-init the WS pieces, or accept new baseline (with team review).

Note: PR #61 (chore/wake-test-coderabbit-followup-qmxn) ALSO failed perf-gate but its content was minor CodeRabbit follow-up — that run may have been a flake on the runner. Cross-check the perf trend on main once #64 is fixed.

Failed CI run: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/actions/runs/25906722941
notes:
Confirmed false alarm — CI runner noise, not a real regression.

Evidence: every subsequent PR (#65, #66, #67, #68, #69, #70 plus the rebased #63) carries PR #64's transport code unchanged. Their perf-gate runs landed:
- PR #63 rebased: cold p95 = 1441.741 ms (-4.69% vs baseline 1512.749)
- PR #70: cold p95 = 1652.728 ms (+9.25%)
- PR #66/67/68/69: all SUCCESS

PR #64's two attempts (2152 / 2594 ms) were both on a degraded runner — same code, different sample = pass. Also, PR #64's commit only ADDS files (ChannelTransport, WsChatBridge, MobileWsFrames, WsFrameMapper); nothing is wired into the startup path. The PR description explicitly says 'The bridge is wired into nothing yet.'

Bead's original hypothesis ('WS wiring added work to cold-startup path') was wrong on a code-reading basis.
close_reason: False alarm — CI emulator noise. Subsequent PRs with the same code (#63 rebased: 1441ms / -4.69%; #70: 1652ms / +9.25%) passed perf-gate.

title: 2. Extract ChatSessionInitializer for the 100-line init block
description:
AdminChatViewModel.init { ... } is ~100 lines doing 5 different things:
  1. activeConfigChanges → refreshAvailableAgents
  2. isFreshRoute → reset client-mode conv tracker + resume-most-recent flow
  3. agentName seeding (memory-cache + flow observer)
  4. observeLastChatSelection (settings push)
  5. observeClientModeEnabled toggle handling
  6. projectContext bootstrap (loadProjectAgents/Brief/RecentBugReports)

Extract a ChatSessionInitializer that runs these in sequence given the VM's collaborators. The VM's init becomes a 5-line call. Adds testability — the initializer can be unit-tested with fakes; today the init logic is only reachable via full VM construction.

ChatSessionInitializer takes (settingsRepository, sessionResolver, conversationCoordinator, clientModeCoordinator, projectCoordinator, runExpansionState, agentNameSource, scope, savedStateHandle) and exposes a single suspend run(). Telemetry events stay co-located with the logic that emits them.

title: 1. Extract ChatSendStrategy interface + REST/Timeline + ClientMode strategies (unblocks w4t2)
description:
Today: AdminChatViewModel.sendMessagePayload is a 30-line if/else routing between timelineSendCoordinator and clientModeSendCoordinator with telemetry mixed in. Adding a third path (WS) would hard-code another branch.

Extract a ChatSendStrategy sealed interface in ui/screens/chat/send/:
  - send(text, attachments, context: ChatSendContext): Job
  - cancel()

Implementations:
  - TimelineChatSendStrategy — wraps existing TimelineSendCoordinator
  - ClientModeChatSendStrategy — wraps ClientModeSendCoordinator
  - (future) WsChatSendStrategy — wraps WsChatBridge

A ChatSendStrategySelector picks the strategy based on (clientMode flag, backend gating from 0vvm). VM holds only the selector, not all three. After this, w4t2 just adds the WS strategy + selector branch.

title: HoldToDictateButton: missing cancel-on-drag gesture — only supports press/release
description:
## Problem

The original Edge Gallery 'HoldToDictate.kt' uses 'detectDragGesturesAfterLongPress' with a drag threshold, so the user can:
1. Press and hold to start recording
2. Drag finger upward to **cancel** (slide-up-to-cancel pattern)
3. Release to send

Our port uses 'detectTapGestures(onPress = { ... awaitRelease() })' which:
- Starts immediately on press (no long-press gate)
- Has no drag tracking at all — the user cannot cancel by sliding away
- The CancellationException catch only fires if the Compose scope cancels, not if the user drags off

The VoiceRecognizerOverlay (which we also don't have yet — see letta-mobile-arhd) shows 'Slide up to cancel' text, implying drag-cancel is the intended UX.

## Fix

1. Replace detectTapGestures with a proper pointer input scope that tracks drag distance.
2. If the user drags more than a threshold (e.g. 100.dp upward), call viewModel.cancelSpeechRecognition().
3. Only on clean release (without exceeding the threshold), call viewModel.stopSpeechRecognition().

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/HoldToDictateButton.kt

title: AudioAnimation: missing VoiceRecognizerOverlay — shader has no presentation context
description:
## Problem

The original Edge Gallery never shows AudioAnimation raw. It is always wrapped inside 'VoiceRecognizerOverlay', which provides:

1. **A dark scrim** — 'Color.Black.copy(alpha = 0.8f)' passed as bgColor. The shader's fade_start/fade_end (0.24-0.34) were tuned assuming this near-black background so the gradient wave fades smoothly into darkness in the top two-thirds of the screen. Without this scrim the wave floats over whatever surface color we pass, which likely looks washed out or has wrong contrast.

2. **Recognized text overlay** — 'uiState.recognizedText' shown white-on-dark, centered in the viewport. This is the live partial transcription the user sees while speaking.

3. **Bottom instruction bar** — A pill at the bottom reading 'Listening...' plus 'Release to send' / 'Slide up to cancel' helper labels. This gives the user clear affordance for the hold-to-talk gesture.

4. **Pointer-eating overlay** — 'Modifier.pointerInput(Unit) {}' on the bottom pill to prevent accidental taps passing through to the content underneath.

Our port has none of this. The AudioAnimation composable is orphaned — it renders a shader but has no surrounding UX container.

## Fix

Create 'VoiceRecognizerOverlay.kt' in the audio package that:
- Fills the screen with a Box
- Renders AudioAnimation with bgColor = Color.Black.copy(alpha = 0.8f)
- Centers the partial transcription text (white)
- Renders a bottom bar with 'Listening...' pill and instruction labels
- Adapts for bottom padding / navigation bars

## Files
- [NEW] android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/VoiceRecognizerOverlay.kt
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/AudioAnimation.kt

title: AudioAnimation: shader colors are wrong — dynamic uniforms replaced the original's curated palette
description:
## Problem

The original Google AI Edge Gallery shader hardcodes four carefully chosen colors directly in the GLSL source:

- yellow: vec3(0.992, 0.875, 0.522)
- green: vec3(0.627, 0.816, 0.686)
- red: vec3(0.886, 0.372, 0.341)
- blue: vec3(0.522, 0.694, 0.973)

Our port replaced these with dynamic 'uniform vec3 color1/2/3/4' driven by MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary/secondary/tertiary/primaryContainer. While themeable in theory, the Material colors are almost never the right luminance, saturation, or hue spread for a perlin noise gradient. The original palette was explicitly designed to create a warm-to-cool liquid effect where all four colors have roughly equal perceived brightness (~0.6-0.9 range), ensuring the wave is always visible against the dark overlay.

Material semantic colors are typically high-contrast pairs (e.g. a very saturated primary against a desaturated container), so the gradient either washes out or has jarring contrast bands.

## Fix

1. Restore the four hardcoded GLSL color constants from the original shader (remove the uniform vec3 declarations).
2. Remove the four color parameters from the Compose function signature.
3. Remove the four setFloatUniform calls for color1-4 in the Canvas block.
4. If we later want theming, expose a 'palette: AudioAnimationPalette' data class with sensible defaults matching the original, rather than blindly forwarding MaterialTheme roles.

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/AudioAnimation.kt

title: Crash on Dashboard: uncaught ApiException from RunApi.listRunSteps kills the app (P0)
description:
FATAL EXCEPTION on main from DashboardViewModel's 24h usage-summary loop. One run's GET /runs/{id}/steps returned an error body ('detail: run run-7516c977-...' suggesting either 404 on a stale id or server-side run-not-in-background-mode reject). The exception bubbled through the async/awaitAll structured-concurrency channel and bypassed the outer try-catch around awaitAll.

Stack:
  com.letta.mobile.data.api.ApiException: {'detail':'run run-7516c977-7187-41b5-b0b9-9fa8b2c32077'}
    at RunApi.listRunSteps$suspendImpl(RunApi.kt:123)
    ...
    Suppressed: ...[StandaloneCoroutine{Cancelling}, Dispatchers.Main.immediate]

Trigger window: after the user switched backends with cached run ids that no longer exist on the new server. Related to letta-mobile-iow7 (stale agent reference) — the same shape applies to run ids.

Fix: wrap each per-run async body in runCatching so a single bad run doesn't kill the batch or crash the app. Failed runs contribute an empty step list to the summary instead of taking down everything.
