title: Channel transport for chat — WebSocket alongside REST
description:
## Summary

Add a `ChannelTransport` to letta-mobile alongside the existing
`RestTransport`. Chat sends route through a WebSocket connection to
letta-code's mobile channel plugin (on the letta-code admin shim); admin
endpoints (agents list, blocks, models, etc.) keep using REST. Toggleable
via a setting so we can A/B against the REST chat path.

This is Phase 1 of the larger "mobile-as-channel" epic — a minimum viable
WS round-trip with a hard-coded token. Pairing flow, sync cursor,
multi-conv routing, server-pushed lifecycle events, and push
notifications are later phases.

## Why

With Client Mode disabled, mobile's chat goes through the standard Letta
REST API. That works but locks us out of:

- **Server-initiated events** reaching mobile (agent replies to a Matrix
  message, cron-triggered messages, multi-channel awareness)
- **Multi-agent in one session** without per-agent stream juggling
- **Shared infrastructure** with other channels (Matrix, Slack, etc.) —
  typing indicators, mention detection, dedupe, pairing all live in
  letta-code's channel pipeline today

Mobile-as-channel collapses all of those under one transport. Admin stays
on REST.

## Server-side context (out of scope here)

The server side is tracked in the `letta-code-parallel` project's beads
under epic **`lcp-gj2`** — see
`/opt/stacks/letta-code-parallel/docs/MOBILE_CHANNEL_DESIGN.md` for the
full Phase 0 design doc. Server pieces:

- `lcp-gj2.1` — channel adapter scaffold (~/.letta/channels/mobile/)
- `lcp-gj2.2` — wire WS upgrade into admin-shim
- `lcp-gj2.3` — send_message → worker pool integration
- `lcp-gj2.4` — protocol test harness

This bead (the mobile side) is `lcp-gj2.5` from the server's perspective.

## Wire protocol (Phase 1)

WebSocket. JSON frames, one per WS message. Base envelope on every frame:

```jsonc
{
  "v": 1,
  "type": "<frame-type>",
  "id": "<uuid>",
  "ts": "2026-05-14T16:00:00.000Z"
  // + type-specific fields
}
```

### Phase 1 frame vocabulary

**Client → server:**

| `type` | Required fields |
|---|---|
| `hello` | `token`, `device_id`, `client_version` |
| `send_message` | `agent_id`, `conversation_id`, `text` |
| `ack` | `target_id` |
| `bye` | (none) |
| `pong` | (in reply to `ping`) |

**Server → client:**

| `type` | Required fields |
|---|---|
| `welcome` | `server_id`, `session_id`, `device_id` |
| `error` | `code`, `message` |
| `ping` | (none) |
| `turn_started` | `agent_id`, `conversation_id`, `turn_id` |
| `assistant_message` | `agent_id`, `conversation_id`, `turn_id`, `content` |
| `stop_reason` | `turn_id`, `reason` |
| `usage_statistics` | `turn_id`, `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, ... |

### Frame ordering invariant per turn

```
turn_started
  → assistant_message (one, coalesced, matching vanilla shape)
  → stop_reason
  → usage_statistics (optional)
```

Identical to the vanilla REST/SSE order so the existing UI rendering
applies.

## Module layout

```
core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/channel/
├── ChannelTransport.kt        # WS connect, frame send/recv loop
├── ChannelProtocol.kt         # Frame data classes (kotlinx.serialization)
├── ChannelConfig.kt           # Settings keys for url, token, device_id
└── ChannelChatSender.kt       # send_message + collect frames as Flow<UiMessage>
```

## Wiring

- `AdminChatViewModel` (or `TimelineSendCoordinator`) consults a settings
  flag `chat.transport: "rest" | "channel"`. Default `"rest"`.
- When `"channel"`: route sends through `ChannelChatSender`. Everything
  else (conv list, blocks, models, agent CRUD) stays on REST.
- `ChannelTransport` is foreground-only in Phase 1: connect on chat
  screen open, disconnect on close. No persistent background connection.
- `device_id` generated client-side as a random UUID on first launch,
  persisted in settings. Resists fingerprinting; survives reinstall via
  Android backup.

## Settings

- `chat.transport`: `rest` (default) | `channel` — toggle which transport
  the chat path uses. Dev-visible in Phase 1.
- `chat.channel.url`: `wss://<host>:8291/shim/v1/mobile`
- `chat.channel.token`: hard-coded shared secret. Phase 2 replaces with
  per-device token from pairing flow.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Setting toggle `chat.transport = channel` → `ChannelTransport`
   connects to the configured WS URL with the configured token.
2. `welcome` frame arrives within 1s of connect.
3. User types + sends → mobile receives `turn_started`,
   `assistant_message` (one coalesced frame), `stop_reason` within
   ~5s under warm-pool conditions on the server.
4. Mobile UI renders the reply identically to the REST/SSE path —
   no visible behavior change for the user.
5. Toggle off → mobile falls back to REST transport with no chat
   regressions.
6. Pointing the build at the existing Python Letta server (which
   *doesn't* have the channel) and toggling `chat.transport = channel`
   gracefully errors (the WS connect fails); mobile auto-falls-back to
   REST or surfaces a clear setting-mismatch banner. Decide which in
   review.

## Test plan

### Unit (`ChannelTransportTest`, `ChannelChatSenderTest`)

- Frame serialization round-trips through `kotlinx.serialization`.
- Hello → welcome happy path with a mock WS.
- Hello → error (bad token) → transport reports auth failure.
- send_message → ordered receipt of turn_started, assistant_message,
  stop_reason.
- WS disconnects mid-turn → caller sees a `TransportFailure` and can
  retry on REST.

### Instrumented

- Toggle setting → connect → smoke chat → assert UI matches.
- Toggle off mid-session → next send falls back to REST.

### Manual

- Drive against `wss://<dev-host>:8291/shim/v1/mobile` with the dev
  token. Verify the acceptance criteria above.

## Out of scope (Phase 1)

- Pairing flow. Phase 2 — `chat.channel.token` is hard-coded.
- Multi-conv routing in one WS — Phase 1 may open a new WS per chat
  screen open; multiplexing comes in Phase 2.
- Reconnect-with-cursor — Phase 1 gives up on disconnect; user reopens
  app.
- Typing indicators, reactions, edits, deletes via the channel — Phase 3.
- Push notifications — Phase 4. Foreground-only WS only.
- Multi-device fan-out — Phase 5.

## Coordination

- File the PR with title `feat(chat): channel transport (Phase 1)`.
- Reference letta-code-parallel epic `lcp-gj2` and child `lcp-gj2.5` in
  the PR description.
- Server-side work (`lcp-gj2.1` → `.4`) should land before this is
  merge-ready, or at least be on a branch we can test against.
- Update bead to `in_progress` when starting, `completed` when merged.

## Context
Mirror of lcp-gj2.5 in the letta-code-parallel project's bead db. Server-side design lives at /opt/stacks/letta-code-parallel/docs/MOBILE_CHANNEL_DESIGN.md (Phase 1 spec). Parent epic on the server side is lcp-gj2. This bead tracks the Android-side ChannelTransport implementation only.

title: Resume most-recent conversation on chat-open, with explicit + New Chat affordance
description:
## Problem

Mobile currently creates a brand-new conversation every time the chat screen
opens on a "fresh route" (see `TimelineSendCoordinator` and the
`isFreshRoute` branch in `AdminChatViewModel`'s init). That was the right
default while Client Mode was the primary chat transport: the LettaBot
gateway pinned a single persistent conversation per agent server-side, so
the per-VM-fresh-conv model was invisible to users.

With Client Mode disabled and chat going straight through the Letta REST API
(via the admin shim, but the same would be true against a vanilla Letta
server), users see one chat per session and lose continuity:

- Open agent → type one message → response → navigate back → open agent
  again → empty new chat. Previous conversation isn't resumed.
- The "recent" conversation appears in the list but the user has to
  actively browse the conversation list to find it.

Users expect chat apps to remember where they were. This issue restores that
behavior with an explicit "+ New Chat" affordance to escape it.

## User-facing behavior

Default: **opening an agent's chat resumes their most-recent conversation
with that agent.** Sending a message continues that thread. History is
visible immediately on open.

Escape hatch: **"+ New Chat"** button (in the chat toolbar or kebab) starts
a fresh conversation. The current chat goes into the conversation list,
accessible whenever the user wants to return.

First-time interaction with a new agent: no prior conversation exists →
fall through to the existing create-on-send path. No behavior change for
this case.

## Why server-side won't fix this

The admin shim could intercept `POST /v1/conversations` and return the
agent's most-recent conv idempotently. That was tried briefly and rejected
because:

1. It deviates from the Letta REST API contract — mobile would behave
   differently against the shim vs the vanilla Letta server.
2. The server doesn't know whether the user intends "new" vs "resume" —
   that signal exists only on the client.
3. Recency windows, "default vs non-default" classification, and edge
   cases pile up in shim heuristics — entropy creeps.

Putting the resume logic on the client is lower-entropy: zero server
changes, single mobile-side code path, identical behavior against vanilla
Letta and the shim.

## Design

### Resume-on-open

In `AdminChatViewModel` (or wherever `isFreshRoute` is currently
interpreted in chat-init), replace the unconditional
`setActiveConversationId(null)` on fresh routes with a check:

```kotlin
private suspend fun resolveInitialConversationId(
    agentId: AgentId,
    explicitNewChat: Boolean
): String? {
    if (explicitNewChat) return null
    val recent = conversationRepository
        .conversationsForAgent(agentId)
        .filterNot { it.archived }
        .maxByOrNull { it.lastMessageAt ?: it.createdAt }
    return recent?.id  // null falls through to create-on-send
}
```

Call this in the chat-screen init before `setActiveConversationId(...)`.
If a recent conv exists and `explicitNewChat` is false, use it. Otherwise
preserve the current create-on-send fallback.

### Explicit "+ New Chat"

Add a UI affordance in the chat screen toolbar / overflow that:

1. Sets a transient `explicitNewChat = true` flag on the next chat-open.
2. Navigates back to the chat route for the same agent.
3. The fresh route reads the flag, skips resume logic, lands on an empty
   composer. Next send will trigger `createConversation(...)`.

The flag should be consumed on read — one fresh-route open per tap.

### Feature flag

Wrap the resume logic behind `feature.resume_recent_conversation`
(default: enabled for internal testing, disabled in release until soaked).
This lets us A/B against the current always-create behavior and roll
back instantly if regressions emerge.

## Acceptance criteria

1. **Resume**: open agent A's chat (was previously chatted with) → see
   prior conversation's history, not an empty screen. Composer is empty;
   typing + send continues the existing conv.
2. **New chat**: tap "+ New Chat" in the toolbar → empty composer, no
   history. Send creates a fresh conv. After send, the chat list shows
   the previous conv AND the new conv.
3. **First chat with agent**: open a never-before-chatted agent → empty
   composer (no resume target). Send creates the conv. Behavior matches
   today.
4. **Vanilla compatibility**: pointing the build at the Python Letta
   server (192.168.50.90:8289) instead of the shim shows identical
   behavior. No server-side dependency.
5. **Feature flag off**: with `resume_recent_conversation` disabled, the
   app behaves exactly as it does today (fresh conv per session).
6. **Telemetry**: log `chat.resume_attempted`, `chat.resume_succeeded`,
   `chat.resume_no_recent`, `chat.explicit_new_chat`. Fields:
   `agent_id`, `resumed_conv_id` (when succeeded), `last_message_age_sec`.

## Test plan

- Unit: `resolveInitialConversationId` returns most-recent non-archived
  conv; returns null when list is empty; respects `explicitNewChat`.
- Instrumented: chat-open with seeded recent conv → assert
  `activeConversationId` matches; chat-open with empty list → assert null
  → send → create endpoint hit.
- E2E (against shim): send 3 messages across two app-foreground cycles →
  verify all 3 land in the same conv on disk.

## Out of scope

- Cross-device "active conversation" sync. Mobile decides per-install
  based on local view of `/v1/conversations`. Multi-device sync via the
  Letta server's notion of "active" is a follow-up.
- "Resume to message position" (scroll restoration). This issue is
  scoped to resuming the correct conv; scroll position is independent.
- Changing the create-on-send path for archived/cleared convs.

## Context
Discovered while standing up the letta-code admin shim as a vanilla-Letta-REST replacement. With Client Mode disabled, mobile reverts to TimelineSendCoordinator's fresh-route-creates-conv default — making every chat-screen-open a new conv. Users lose continuity. Server-side fix would force the shim to deviate from vanilla; client-side fix works against both shim and vanilla identically.

title: Cold-start first prompt renders below assistant response (10s fuzzy window too tight for USER)
description:
**Reproduction (cold start required):**
1. Force-stop com.letta.mobile, cold-launch from launcher.
2. Open an existing conversation with cached history.
3. Send a short prompt (e.g., 'pong').
4. Wait for the response. Observe: the user bubble appears BELOW the assistant response in the chat list. Visually broken.

**Root cause:**
- ClientModeSendCoordinator.appendClientModeLocal writes user_local with sentAt = Instant.now() at type-and-send time.
- Lettabot WS gateway wraps the user message with several <system-reminder> envelopes, then forwards to Letta server. Server timestamps the user_message when the RUN STARTS (not when the WS frame arrives), which can be many seconds after the local sentAt.
- For runs that take > 10 seconds, server's user_message.date ends up >10s after local.sentAt.
- Timeline.collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch uses CLIENT_MODE_FUZZY_WINDOW_MS = 10_000 for both USER and ASSISTANT. 
- ASSISTANT fuzzy works because the assistant Local's sentAt = first-chunk-arrival ≈ server's run-start date (small delta).
- USER fuzzy fails the time window. Reconcile appends server's user_confirmed at the end of the events list (after the just-collapsed assistant_confirmed). 
- LazyColumn(reverseLayout=true) renders user_confirmed below assistant_confirmed.

**Evidence (cold start trace 21:44:24-21:44:48):**
- 21:44:24.723 send.clientModeLocalAppended localId=cm-436acb90-... contentLength=4
- 21:44:48.508 recentReconcile.fuzzyCollapsed localOtid=cm-assist-... contentPrefix=🏓 (assistant only)
- 21:44:48.512 recentReconcile (1857ms) reason=client_mode_stream_done serverCount=192 appended=1 (the 1 = user_confirmed)
- curl /messages confirms server's user_message.date is 01:44:40 UTC, 16s after local sentAt at 01:44:24 UTC.
- After stripEnvelopeReminders, content equality holds ('pong' == 'pong'); only the time-window check fails.

**Fix:**
Use a per-type fuzzy window in Timeline.collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch (and its inverse, the Local-to-Confirmed search at line ~531). USER messages need a window that accommodates run-processing delay. Proposal:
- CLIENT_MODE_USER_FUZZY_WINDOW_MS = 300_000 (5 min)
- Keep CLIENT_MODE_FUZZY_WINDOW_MS = 10_000 for ASSISTANT/REASONING/TOOL_CALL (existing behavior is correct for these)

**Files:**
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt (collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch lines 362-418, plus the symmetric search at lines 525-540)

**Test plan:**
- Cold start app, open conv, send prompt, run > 10s.
- Verify user bubble lands ABOVE the assistant response (correct chronological order).
- Verify exactly ONE user bubble per send (no duplicate Local + Confirmed).
- Logcat should show recentReconcile.fuzzyCollapsed for BOTH the assistant AND the user.
notes: Fix shipped on PR #45 https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/45 (branch fix/cold-start-user-fuzzy-window-cdm5, commit a8302922). Per-type fuzzy window: USER gets 5 min, all others keep 10s. New helper effectiveFuzzyWindow(type, explicit) returns max(explicit, base) so test-injection of explicit windows still works. User confirmed cold-start repro fixed on device ('bug squashed') 2026-05-13. Combined APK (d9zy.5 + cdm5) installed locally for d9zy.5 design verification — feat/d9zy5-retry branch reset to origin so the test-only merge commit isn't accidentally pushed.

title: Live tool feedback regresses during streaming; duplicate final response (shared root cause suspected)
description:
Two symptoms observed after the recent chat fine-tuning round, suspected shared root cause:

## Symptom 1 — Live tool-feedback regression

In-flight tool calls inside a `RunBlock` no longer feel like they update in place. Each update (status `running` → `succeeded`, streaming arguments / output growth) reads as the entire tool-call group sliding/fading in fresh. The streaming UX used to be "the card sits still and its inner text/status changes"; now it's "the card re-enters every frame".

## Symptom 2 — Duplicate final response

The assistant's final response appears duplicated in the timeline. Two visible bubbles for what should be one final message after streaming completes.

## Suspected root cause (shared)

The grouped run-step path in `RunBlock.kt:422` was changed to wrap `CompactToolCallGroupCard` in `ToolCallEntrance`:

```kotlin
ToolCallEntrance {
    CompactToolCallGroupCard(
        toolCalls = step.toolCalls,
        // …
        animateRows = animateRows,
        rowAnimationKeyPrefix = \"run|\${step.key}\",
    )
}
```

`ToolCallEntrance` (in `ChatMessageComponents.kt:991`) is:

```kotlin
@Composable
internal fun ToolCallEntrance(content: @Composable () -> Unit) {
    var visible by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    LaunchedEffect(Unit) { visible = true }
    AnimatedVisibility(
        visible = visible,
        enter = ChatMotion.verticalEnter(slideDivisor = 10),
        exit = ChatMotion.expandExit(),
    ) { content() }
}
```

That has no key parameter. The `remember` + `LaunchedEffect(Unit)` are scoped to the current composition slot. During streaming, `visibleSteps` reshapes (steps are merged/compacted as `compactRunToolCallSteps(visibleMessages)` re-runs on every message tick), so the `Column { visibleSteps.forEachIndexed { idx, step -> key(step.key) { … } } }` can shift step rows in/out of the composition. Combined with the new `Column(modifier = …animateContentSize(streamingSizeSpec))` wrapper on that column, every streaming update can trigger:
1. `ToolCallEntrance.visible` resetting to `false → true` for any newly-positioned step (looks like a fresh entrance instead of an update),
2. The outer column animating its content size around that re-entrance,
3. `RunStepRow` line geometry recalculating against the in-flight enter animation.

That explains Symptom 1. Hypothesis for Symptom 2: the same step-reshape behavior (when `compactRunToolCallSteps` decides the final assistant message becomes either its own step or gets merged differently between the last streamed frame and the post-stream final frame) could cause the final response to be rendered twice — once via the old composition slot's tail and once via the new one — especially if `AnimatedVisibility`'s exit animation hasn't completed yet when the new bubble enters. Worth verifying.

## Files touched in the offending change

- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlock.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt` (`ToolCallEntrance` made `internal`, `AnimatedVisibility` added around expanded sections of `CompactToolCallRow` and `ToolCallCard`)
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageList.kt` (new `LocalChatIsManuallyAnimating` plumbing + `signalManualAnimation()` on run collapse toggles)
- `android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/theme/ChatTheme.kt` (`LocalChatIsManuallyAnimating` composition local)

All currently uncommitted in the worktree as of 2026-05-12.

## Repro

1. Open any agent that runs tool calls (e.g. an agent that performs a multi-step shell or fetch action).
2. Send a prompt that triggers ≥ 1 tool call.
3. Observe the run block during streaming — the grouped tool-call card visibly re-enters with slide/fade on each update instead of updating in place.
4. After streaming completes, observe the final assistant text — appears duplicated.

## Fix options

- **A.** Drop the `ToolCallEntrance` wrapper from `RunBlock.RunToolCallGroupStepRow`. The grouped card already has per-row entrance via `animateRows = animateRows`. The outer wrapper is redundant and harmful here. Likely fixes Symptom 1 immediately and very likely Symptom 2 if the duplicate-final-response is being caused by entrance/exit overlap.
- **B.** Give `ToolCallEntrance` a stable key (`step.key` / message id) and use `remember(key)` so the visibility flag survives recomposition. Preserves animation for genuinely-new steps only.
- **C.** Remove the streaming-path `animateContentSize(streamingSizeSpec)` from the grouped run-step `Column` to stop the height-amplification effect on top of the entrance animations.
- **D.** Confirm `compactRunToolCallSteps` produces stable step boundaries across the last-streaming-frame → final-frame transition. If it doesn't, that's the duplicate-final-response source independently of the entrance wrapper.

Recommended sequence: A first (revert the regression cheaply), then verify Symptom 2 in isolation; if it persists, apply D-style audit of `compactRunToolCallSteps`.

## Verification

- Manual: prompt with tool calls. Tool-call card stays geometrically stable while its inner content updates. Final assistant response renders once.
- Add a screenshot test or recording-based regression check for the streaming run block if practical.
- `:app:testPlayDebugUnitTest` for `com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.*` stays green.

## Severity

Both symptoms degrade the perceived quality of streaming, which is the headline experience. Filing P1.
notes:
Reported by user on 2026-05-12 ~22:40 EDT during a fine-tuning round. Bug landed in the uncommitted worktree changes — not yet in main. Worth fixing before commit so the regression never ships.

---
## 2026-05-12 23:02 EDT — live repro update

Tested with a tool-calling response. Observed symptom is sharper than originally framed:

- The assistant text streams in normally (token-by-token / chunk-by-chunk visible).
- The tool chain does NOT stream in. Nothing visible while the tool call is in flight.
- When the tool chain completes, the entire chain (call + arguments + output) **pops in as a finished unit** all at once.

This is not "the card re-enters on every update" — it's "the card is not rendered at all until completion, then enters once with the entrance animation." Practically that means in-flight `running` status, partial argument streams, and streaming output are invisible to the user. The fine motion problem is downstream of a worse problem: the tool call never appears in the live-streaming state.

Likely the `ToolCallEntrance` wrapper's `visible` flag is being kept `false` while the parent `RunBlock` is still in its streaming/compacting path — possibly because the step gets emitted into `visibleSteps` only after it transitions out of `running`, or because the wrapper's `LaunchedEffect(Unit) { visible = true }` doesn't get a chance to run for the in-flight step due to recomposition churn.

Either way: Fix A (drop the wrapper from `RunBlock.RunToolCallGroupStepRow`) is no longer just a polish revert — it's the live-streaming visibility fix. Bumping urgency. Also: investigate whether `compactRunToolCallSteps` is even emitting in-flight steps with `running` status, or only emitting them after the call resolves. That may be the deeper root cause and would also explain the duplicate-final-response (final assistant content gets emitted twice when the step finally resolves and the prior streamed text was already rendered).

Recommended action: implement Fix A immediately, then independently verify that `compactRunToolCallSteps` returns in-flight rows during the streaming window.
close_reason: Dropped ToolCallEntrance wrapper from RunToolCallGroupStepRow (Fix A). CompactToolCallGroupCard handles its own per-row entrance via animateRows; the outer wrapper suppressed in-flight steps entirely because visible=false during recomposition churn.

title: externalRunReconcile appended events bypass fuzzy collapse, yielding duplicate finals in Client Mode
description:
Client Mode turns are surfacing duplicate final messages in the chat UI. Live device tracing (adb logcat, pid 26687, 2026-05-11 15:03) shows the per-event fuzzy collapse path (collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch) IS running and successfully stabilizing the user-turn Local against the server Confirmed:

```
15:03:07.194  Telemetry/TimelineSync: externalRunReconcile.fuzzyCollapsed
  runId=run-b9f06124-2139-4e9d-b97d-001074e1a4fc
  localOtid=cm-4ea5d97c-4088-4937-a732-6efd303a379e
  serverId=message-c52884ff-eac2-4c2d-8b9e-fd4b2a605e95
  deltaMs=7613
  contentPrefix='you would have access to raw adb just us'
  source=CLIENT_MODE_HARNESS
```

But the very next log line shows externalRunReconcile appending two additional events on top of the stabilized turn, without those appended events passing through the fuzzy/serverId reconciliation gate:

```
15:03:07.201  externalRunReconcile (1121ms) runId=run-b9f06124-…
  serverCount=233 appended=2
```

Stream frames then arrive ~ms later carrying the *same* server messages, classified as approval_request_message deltas merging onto an existingType=TOOL_CALL by serverId:

```
15:03:07.201  streamSubscriber.merged serverId=message-9d65d3ed-…
  messageType=approval_request_message existingType=TOOL_CALL
  oldLen=234 newLen=4 mergedLen=238 ...
```

The tool-call merge works because serverId matches existing. The danger is the OTHER appended event in the appended=2 batch — if it lands as the final assistant prose and a subsequent stream frame (or a later externalRunReconcile pass) re-emits the same server final with a different messageType than what externalRunReconcile chose at append time, Timeline.insertOrdered's otid-only dedupe lets it through as a second bubble, and replaceByServerId's messageType-strict match misses it.

Notably: no streamSubscriber.eventDeduped events fired in the duplicate window — confirming the two finals carry different otids (or insufficient overlap) and miss otid dedupe, AND have a messageType mismatch from any existing event with the same serverId so replaceByServerId is a no-op.

This is NOT a regression in collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch — that matcher fired and worked. The hole is between externalRunReconcile.appended path and the stream subscriber merge path.

## Context
Live evidence:
- Device: 192.168.50.235:5555
- App pid: 26687
- Conversation: conv-279c6392-f2dc-4a2c-abb4-bed061f044e8
- Run that exhibited the trace: run-b9f06124-2139-4e9d-b97d-001074e1a4fc
- Local otid that fuzzy-collapsed correctly: cm-4ea5d97c-4088-4937-a732-6efd303a379e
- Server final id: message-c52884ff-eac2-4c2d-8b9e-fd4b2a605e95
- deltaMs at collapse: 7613 (well within CLIENT_MODE_FUZZY_WINDOW_MS = 10000)
- externalRunReconcile reported serverCount=233 appended=2 for that run.

Recent commits in this lane (likely interaction surface, not necessarily the regression source):
- 645d5fab 'Collapse client-mode external reconcile replays'
- 8d90dd8f 'fix(chat): tighten Client Mode duplicate reconciliation'
- 4498f585 'fix(chat): robustify Client Mode deduplication against duplicates'
- 26248b72 'Fix fresh client-mode bootstrap streaming'
- a74fc217 'Fix duplicated assistant stream frames'
- 6e098579 'Reconcile client mode after websocket drops'

Related files:
- core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt (collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch, replaceByServerId, insertOrdered)
- core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt (externalRunReconcile, streamSubscriber dispatch)
design:
Two viable repair shapes — pick one or combine:

1. Funnel externalRunReconcile.appended through the per-event reconciliation gate.
   - Before insertOrdered on each appended event, run collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch (already idempotent — returns FuzzyCollapseResult with null collapsed when no match).
   - If a same-serverId event already exists (regardless of messageType for ASSISTANT/REASONING — note that TOOL_CALL vs approval_request_message merge already works), upgrade replaceByServerId to be messageType-tolerant for the assistant/reasoning text path.

2. Strengthen replaceByServerId at the Timeline layer.
   - Currently requires it.messageType == confirmed.messageType. Relax for ASSISTANT family (ASSISTANT, REASONING) so a same-serverId arrival of either type collapses onto the existing one. Add explicit unit tests that TOOL_CALL ↔ approval_request_message merging still works (it currently relies on serverId+messageType match, so the relaxation must not break it).

Add new telemetry hook: externalRunReconcile.appendedSkippedFuzzy with serverId, messageType, windowMs, localOtid candidates considered, deltaMs, contentPrefix. Mirrors the existing fuzzyCollapsed event shape so we can diff successes vs misses.
acceptance_criteria:
- A characterization test in TimelineReducerCharacterizationTest (or TimelineSyncLoopTest) reproduces the race: externalRunReconcile appends N server events (including a final assistant) AFTER collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch stabilizes the user Local, and a stream subscriber dispatch then arrives with the same serverId but with messageType=ASSISTANT (or a delta payload) that today produces a duplicate final assistant bubble.
- The fix routes externalRunReconcile.appended events through the same fuzzy/serverId reconciliation gate the per-event stream path uses (or equivalently strengthens replaceByServerId / insertOrdered so a same-serverId arrival never produces a second event regardless of messageType for final assistant prose).
- Telemetry: add a streamSubscriber.fuzzyCollapseSkipped (or externalRunReconcile.appendedSkippedFuzzy) INFO event capturing messageType, windowMs, localContentPrefix, confirmedContentPrefix when the reconcile path inserts without a fuzzy match — so future regressions are visible in Grafana.
- No duplicate final assistant bubble visible in the UI for a Client Mode round-trip where both externalRunReconcile and stream subscriber deliver the same run.
notes:
Fixed by calling postHandlerCollapse from ClientModeSendCoordinator.reconcileClientModeConversation, mirroring NotificationReplyHandler's letta-mobile-iuh6 pattern. Root cause wasn't 'externalRunReconcile bypasses fuzzy' (fuzzy already runs at applyRecentMessagesSnapshotLocked line 905) but a race: when SSE wins, reconcile appends Confirmed before harness has written its Local; harness then writes a Local that no later pass absorbs. Two regression tests added in TimelineSyncLoopTest: 'late harness local is absorbed by postHandlerCollapse after reconcile appends confirmed' (positive case) and 'mismatched harness local content is NOT collapsed by postHandlerCollapse' (boundary: only prefix/equal content collapses, no silent false-positive merges). All 37 TimelineSyncLoopTest cases + all core tests + ClientModeSendCoordinatorTest + NotificationReplyHandlerTest pass. APK built and installed to Pixel 192.168.50.235:5555.
Field-confirmed not fully resolved by 97aee5a0. Reproduces when a multi-tool sequence completes with a final assistant response — the final still doubles in the UI. Initial fix (postHandlerCollapse after reconcileRecentMessages in ClientModeSendCoordinator) addressed the single-turn race but does not cover the multi-tool case.

Hypothesis: the multi-tool sequence completes via a different terminal path than reconcileClientModeConversation, OR the harness writes the final-assistant Local at a moment when postHandlerCollapse has already run (so the new Local arrives orphaned and no further pass absorbs it).

Need to:
1. Capture fresh adb logcat from a confirmed multi-tool reproduction (pid changed to 5146 after restart — earlier 26687 buffer no longer relevant).
2. Identify whether ClientModeSendCoordinator.reconcileClientModeConversation actually runs at end-of-run for multi-tool sequences, or if completion is signalled elsewhere (e.g., StreamCoordinator end-of-stream, tool-return terminal handler).
3. If reconcile fires before the harness writes the final-assistant Local, postHandlerCollapse needs to be re-invoked AFTER the harness writes its final Local (or upsertClientModeLocal's hasCompatibleConfirmedForClientModeLocal guard needs to be the primary defense, with reconcile order swapped).

Reopening.
Field-confirmed FIXED. Emmanuel confirmed multi-tool sequence with final assistant response no longer duplicates. Closing again — my earlier reopen was premature; the duplicate I was worried about did not actually reproduce. 97aee5a0 (Absorb orphan harness Locals after Client Mode reconcile) holds.

title: Resolve OpenCode TodoWrite versus Beads instruction contradictions
description: Why this issue exists: OhMyOpenAgent injects TodoWrite discipline while letta-mobile requires bd/beads for task tracking. What needs to be done: scan local OpenCode/OhMyOpenAgent/project prompt sources, patch effective local configuration/instructions to prefer bd in this repo, and verify no remaining high-priority contradictions in runtime-readable files.
notes: Closed after resolving OpenCode/OhMyOpenAgent task-tracking contradictions: disabled todo-description-override and tasks-todowrite-disabler in /root/.config/opencode/oh-my-openagent.json; patched active oh-my-openagent@4.0.0 runtime/declaration artifacts so TodoWrite/TaskCreate discipline defers to project tracker and letta-mobile uses bd/beads. Verification: node --check on dist/index.js passed; grep found no remaining generic TodoWrite/TaskCreate discipline phrases in active dist JS/d.ts; repo scan found letta-mobile instructions already consistent. Retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-lxqp && git status --short --branch
close_reason: Patched active OhMyOpenAgent/OpenCode local prompt sources to defer to project task tracking, disabled TodoWrite/TaskCreate-related hooks, and verified letta-mobile repo instructions have no contradictions.

title: Progressively load project issues
description: Project issue list loading gets exponentially slower when a project has many Beads. Replace eager large-list loading with progressive/lazy pagination and keep analytics independent from the full issue list.

title: Fix empty project issue analytics
description: Project issue creation volume and completed timeline render empty on device. Diagnose whether issue date/completion data is missing from API/model mapping or filtered on the display side, then fix and verify.
notes: Root cause was app-side JSON contract mismatch for issue analytics: IssueAnalyticsResponse expected camelCase fields, while backend-style project APIs use snake_case. Snake_case analytics payloads deserialized to default empty createdBuckets/completedTimeline, so UI showed empty states despite data existing. Fixed ProjectWork.kt analytics models with @SerialName snake_case plus @JsonNames camelCase aliases, added ProjectWorkApiTest coverage for snake_case analytics payload, verified :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.ProjectWorkApiTest and :app:assembleRootDebug, installed APK on 192.168.50.235:5555, injected credentials, app PID alive.
close_reason: Fixed JSON contract mismatch for analytics deserialization. Added @SerialName/@JsonNames dual-format support to all analytics models. Verified with ProjectWorkApiTest covering numeric priority coercion and snake_case format. Device install successful.

title: Fix Android app not receiving agent responses
description: Android chat sends or connects, but agent responses are not appearing in the app while Matrix receives responses. Diagnose the Android-only response pipeline including transport, stream handling, persistence, and UI rendering; fix the root cause and verify with Android build/tests.
notes: Bootstrap: Android Client Mode response failure was caused by unreachable saved URL on Pixel. Device DataStore had ws://100.93.254.12:8407/api/v1/agent-gateway; TCP from Pixel timed out. Host gateway responds on http://192.168.50.90:8407/api/v1/status and Pixel TCP to 192.168.50.90:8407 succeeds. Updated device DataStore to ws://192.168.50.90:8407/api/v1/agent-gateway. Retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-sq23 && git status --short --branch
close_reason: Resolved: Android Client Mode was configured with unreachable URL ws://100.93.254.12:8407/api/v1/agent-gateway; updated device setting to reachable ws://192.168.50.90:8407/api/v1/agent-gateway.

Move project-specific actions such as active agents, project brief, bug reporting, and issues into the project hamburger/action menu; make project cards readable again and rebuild/install the dev APK.

Route plumbing adds ProjectChatStartAction/projectStartAction so Report bug opens the existing structured bug sheet in AgentScaffold. Retrieve with: bd show letta-mobile-o8uj && git status --short --branch

title: Fix Android Beads issue screen load error
description: Opening the new Project issue tracker for a project with issues reports an error. Diagnose the real API/runtime failure, patch the Android issue tracker list/detail flow, rebuild the dev APK, and install it on the Pixel dev device.
notes: Fix details: ProjectWork models now use snake_case SerialName fields for project_id, ready_work, status_label, updated_at, etc.; ProjectWorkApi omits null query params; ProjectIssuesViewModel no longer fails the whole list screen when ready-work fails separately. Verification: ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.ProjectWorkApiTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.ProjectWorkRepositoryTest; ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin; ./gradlew :app:assembleRootDebug; adb install -r app-root-debug.apk succeeded on 192.168.50.235:5555.

title: Finish AdminChatViewModel thin-orchestrator pass under 800 lines
description:
Problem statement:
The first pass met the <1,500 line gate, and current AdminChatViewModel.kt is 1,164 lines. The epic follow-up target is <800 lines if feasible. After route/load and timeline send/paging are extracted, remaining VM code should be mostly public UI delegates, composer facade, settings delegates, and lifecycle hooks.

Proposed implementation plan:
Review residual responsibilities after yoic.1.7/1.8. Move only low-risk leftover clusters that have clear boundaries: agent-name cache seeding/observation into the route coordinator, interruptRun into a ChatRunControlCoordinator if it still carries repository/bot client dependencies, and lifecycle currentConversationTracker pause/resume into a small helper if useful. Prefer deleting stale comments/imports and tightening collaborator factories over introducing over-fragmented classes.

Explicit tests:
Run full AdminChatViewModelTest plus focused collaborator tests from the second pass. At minimum include stop/interrupt, composer while streaming, onScreenPaused/onScreenResumed, client-mode toggle, reset, and send route tests.

Success gate checklist:
- AdminChatViewModel.kt is under 800 lines, or a note explains why stopping above 800 is safer.
- VM public API remains stable for ChatScreen.
- Constructor dependencies are reduced where collaborator factories make that safe, or documented as deferred.
- Full chat unit gate passes.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
This is the cleanup/gate bead, not a place for semantic changes. If tests reveal hidden coupling, file a follow-up rather than forcing the line target.
acceptance_criteria: AdminChatViewModel is a stable thin orchestrator with documented line-count outcome and chat gates passing
close_reason: AdminChatViewModel is under 800 lines (715) after second-pass coordinator extraction; chat gates passed.

title: Extract timeline send and history paging coordinators
description:
Problem statement:
AdminChatViewModel.kt still owns non-client-mode sendMessageViaTimeline, sendMessagePayload route selection/logging, loadOlderMessages paging/backfill merge, and part of resetMessages. These paths are conceptually separate from screen orchestration and keep repositories in the VM constructor.

Proposed implementation plan:
Extract TimelineSendCoordinator for timeline-sync send: validation inputs, create/update conversation summary, optimistic timelineRepository.sendMessage with attachments, observer start, and stream flags. Extract ChatHistoryPager if it stays cleaner separately: loadOlderMessages guard, repository fetchOlderMessages, ChatTimelineObserver.mergeOlderPage, and stale-conversation guard. AdminChatViewModel should retain sendMessage/rerunMessage public API and delegate route choice.

Explicit tests:
TimelineSendCoordinator tests for new conversation creation on fresh non-client route, existing conversation summary update once, attachment send path, repository failure clearing streaming flags, and observer start. ChatHistoryPager tests for guard conditions, no pending messages as before cursor, successful page merge, stale conversation ignored, and failure clears older-loading only for current conversation. Existing AdminChatViewModel send/loadOlder tests remain green.

Success gate checklist:
- Timeline send and older-page behavior moved out of VM.
- No change in composer clearing, stream flags, active conversation assignment, or timeline observer binding.
- VM line count drops further toward <800.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep exact telemetry event names and log strings during extraction. If summary behavior is unclear, add a characterization test before moving that branch.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline send and older paging are delegated to tested collaborators
close_reason: Extracted TimelineSendCoordinator and ChatHistoryPager with focused tests; AdminChatViewModel is 715 lines.

title: Extract ChatConversationCoordinator from AdminChatViewModel
description:
Problem statement:
AdminChatViewModel.kt still owns route/fresh-client-mode resolution, activeConversationId and saved-state conversation pointers, initial route message consumption, loadMessages/loadMessagesInternal, retryConversationLoad, and resetMessages client-mode branch. This is the most fragile remaining state machine and is the main blocker to getting the VM below 800 lines.

Proposed implementation plan:
After second-pass seam tests land, introduce ChatConversationCoordinator (or ChatRouteCoordinator) that owns: active conversation state, client-mode bootstrap state, explicit/fresh route policy, resolveConversationAndLoad, loadMessagesInternal, consumeInitialMessageIfPresent, reset conversation state, current conversation tracker updates during resolution, and timeline observer start/stop callbacks. Keep AdminChatViewModel public methods as thin delegates. Preserve one state output path through MutableStateFlow<ChatUiState>.

Explicit tests:
Coordinator tests for fresh route no fallback, existing explicit conversation hydration, client-mode non-fresh fallback to most recent, blank conversation id fresh behavior, initial route message consumed once across re-resolves, duplicate fresh route local prompt mirror, and resolver failure error state. Keep corresponding AdminChatViewModel characterization tests green.

Success gate checklist:
- AdminChatViewModel loses the route/load/reset state machine and remains behavior-compatible.
- Fresh route, explicit route, client-mode toggle, and initial-message regressions stay green.
- Line count should drop by roughly 250-350 lines.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Do not change navigation args or UI state model. Land as internal package collaborator with callbacks first; if behavior diverges, revert only the coordinator wiring while keeping tests.
acceptance_criteria: Route/load/reset behavior moved behind tested coordinator; VM tests stay green
close_reason: Extracted ChatConversationCoordinator with focused tests and kept AdminChatViewModel regressions green.

title: Second-pass seam tests for chat coordinators
description:
Problem statement:
AdminChatViewModel.kt is down to 1,164 lines, but the riskiest remaining behavior is covered mostly through the giant AdminChatViewModelTest. ClientModeSendCoordinator.kt (536 lines) and ChatTimelineObserver.kt (194 lines) are extracted but do not have focused unit tests, so further movement could regress fresh-route bootstrap, migration, notification, and timeline observer behavior.

Proposed implementation plan:
Add collaborator-level tests before moving more code: ClientModeSendCoordinator fresh bootstrap optimistic user echo, pre-conversation chunk buffer/replay when conversationId arrives on a later chunk, recoverable stream exception after conversationId, failure before conversationId, conversation substitution banner/route update, and final notification when terminal frame omits conversation id. Add ChatTimelineObserver tests for same-conversation idempotent bind, conversation switch rebind, older-page prefix merge, and active reply stream preserving typing flags. Keep existing AdminChatViewModel tests as characterization, not as the only safety net.

Explicit tests:
New ClientModeSendCoordinatorTest and ChatTimelineObserverTest plus AdminChatViewModelTest targeted cases that already exist around these seams.

Success gate checklist:
- Focused coordinator tests pass.
- Existing VM tests covering client-mode/timeline observer still pass.
- No production behavior change.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Test-only bead. If fakes are too broad, keep tests at package level with minimal fake repositories/flows and do not change production code.
acceptance_criteria: Focused coordinator tests exist and pass without production behavior changes
close_reason: Added focused ClientModeSendCoordinatorTest and ChatTimelineObserverTest seam coverage; gates passed.

title: Matrix heartbeat creates fresh conversations instead of reusing existing thread
description:
Matrix heartbeat functionality appears to create/populate fresh conversations repeatedly instead of reusing the intended existing conversation/thread.

Observed insight:
- The native/admin project/conversation surfaces make multiple conversations visible side-by-side.
- From that administrative view, heartbeat activity appears to be landing in fresh conversations each time.
- This regression would be hard to notice from Matrix alone because Matrix presents a single stream and can hide potential conversation disconnects/routing drift.

Expected:
- Heartbeat messages for the same Matrix/channel/agent context should route to a stable conversation identity unless an explicit new-conversation action occurs.
- Conversation identity should be durable across heartbeat ticks, process restarts, reconnects, and Matrix transport retries.
- Admin/conversation views should not show heartbeat-created conversation fan-out for one logical Matrix stream.

Likely investigation areas:
- Matrix channel/conversation identity mapping.
- Heartbeat sender/routing code that chooses conversationId/chatId/channelId.
- Any fallback path where missing conversationId defaults to force-new or creates a fresh Letta conversation.
- Persistence of Matrix thread/channel -> Letta conversation mapping.
- Difference between Matrix single-stream presentation and native conversation list/admin feed.

Acceptance:
- Repeated heartbeat events for the same Matrix context reuse the same conversation.
- Existing accidental duplicate heartbeat conversations are understood and can be ignored/migrated/cleaned up if needed.
- Add a regression test that simulates multiple heartbeat ticks and asserts one stable conversation id.
notes: Bootstrap after letta-mobile-dw69: TimelineSyncLoop first decomposition landed in commit 7282f9b3, but this P1 product bug is the recommended next priority if heartbeat fan-out is user-visible. Start by locating Matrix heartbeat sender/routing code and the persistence/mapping for Matrix channel/thread -> Letta conversation. Add a regression test that simulates multiple heartbeat ticks for the same Matrix context and asserts one stable conversation id. Retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-bunm && git status --short --branch
close_reason: Moved out of letta-mobile ownership. Filed LettaBot owner bead lettabot-g58 for heartbeat conversation reuse/routing, and Matrix/Synapse deployment owner bead matrix-tuwunel-cje for Matrix thread/identity preservation. Mobile can observe/admin-display the symptom, but the routing identity source of truth belongs to LettaBot + Matrix/Synapse.

title: Define Android Beads data contract for project workspaces
description:
Define the data/API contract for exposing Beads issues to the Android project workspace UI.

Scope:
- Decide how Android obtains Beads data for a project: local embedded service endpoint, project-management backend endpoint, or controlled tool bridge.
- Define DTOs for issue list and issue detail: id, title, type, priority, status, labels, assignee, parent/dependencies, description, notes/comments, updated time.
- Define query/filter parameters matching common `bd list` usage: status, ready, priority, labels, type, limit.
- Define error states for missing project folder, unavailable Beads database, unavailable Dolt SQL server, and unsupported repo.
- Decide whether first implementation is read-only and what write operations are explicitly out of scope.

Acceptance:
- A small documented contract exists before UI implementation.
- Contract supports the native issue browser without requiring chat transcript parsing.
- Read-only MVP is clearly separated from future edit/update/close actions.

Added /api/projects catalog support, ProjectWork DTOs, ProjectWorkApi, ProjectWorkRepository, focused API/repository tests, and docs/architecture/project-work-api-android-contract.md. Validation: ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.ProjectApiTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.ProjectWorkApiTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.ProjectRepositoryTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.ProjectWorkRepositoryTest; ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin. Next bead: letta-mobile-m1sq for native read-only issue browser UI.

title: Native Beads issue browser in project workspace
description:
Add a native Beads issue browser to the Android project workspace so users can inspect issues/tasks created by agents without relying on chat summaries.

Motivation:
- The project already uses Beads (`bd`) as source of truth for issue tracking.
- Agents frequently file/update/close beads during refactor and QA passes.
- The user currently has no native way in the app to verify what was created, updated, or scheduled; they must take the agent's word for it.
- The main chat UI is not the right place to dump issue lists, but the project interface is a natural home.

Proposed UX:
- Add a Beads/Issues tab or section under each Project workspace.
- Show open/in-progress/ready/closed beads scoped to the project/repo.
- Support basic filters: status, priority, type, labels, assignee.
- Show issue detail: title, id, status, priority, description, notes/comments, labels, dependencies/parent.
- Link issue rows back to relevant project/chat context when available.
- Start read-only; editing can be a later bead.

Architecture notes:
- Use `bd`/Beads as the source of truth, not a parallel issue model.
- Prefer a small repository/API boundary around Beads queries so UI does not shell out directly from Compose.
- Since Beads lives in project folders, route requests through the project workspace/backend/tooling layer with explicit project directory context.
- Handle unavailable Beads/Dolt server gracefully with diagnostics and retry, e.g. suggest/trigger `bd dolt start` in developer contexts.

Acceptance:
- From a project detail/workspace screen, user can inspect Beads created for that project.
- User can verify at least id, title, status, priority, labels, and notes for a bead.
- UI is read-only initially and does not clutter main chat.
- Empty/error states explain when Beads is unavailable or no issues exist.
notes: Bootstrap from completed data contract: bd show letta-mobile-qmbg && git status --short --branch. Android now has ProjectWork DTOs/API/repository and /api/projects catalog support. Start UI with read-only project issue browser backed by ProjectWorkRepository.refreshReadyWork(), refreshIssues(), and getIssue(). Use ProjectHomeScreen/RunMonitorViewModel patterns; mutation APIs exist but m1sq acceptance says read-only initially.

title: Clear ChatPushService ingested listener on service destroy
description: Finding P1: ChatPushService.kt around line 187 assigns a new anonymous TimelineRepository.ingestedListener on each install, while onDestroy around line 104 only cancels the service scope. TimelineRepository.kt around line 55 is singleton-owned and can retain the destroyed service through the listener, calling into stale dependencies. Store the installed listener in a field and clear it on destroy if it is still active. Add a service teardown regression test or focused unit seam to prove destroyed services are not retained/called.
notes:
Session handoff / bootstrap for next agent (2026-05-10):

Repo is on main. Recent completed work was pushed after finishing dirty TimelineSyncLoop telemetry and TimelineRepository cache guard fixes:

Current next bead is letta-mobile-8vj5: Clear ChatPushService ingested listener on service destroy. The suspected leak is that ChatPushService installs TimelineRepository.ingestedListener with an anonymous object and onDestroy only cancels serviceScope, so the singleton TimelineRepository can retain a destroyed service/dependencies.

Likely implementation: add a ChatPushService field holding the installed IngestedMessageListener; assign that field when installing; in onDestroy clear timelineRepository.ingestedListener only if it still refers to the field (identity check) then null the field and cancel serviceScope. Add a focused unit/seam test if feasible; otherwise targeted service/logic test around listener install/clear.

Expected quality gates after fix: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin plus targeted channel/service tests.

title: Keep TimelineSyncLoop write mutex off slow work
description: Finding P1: TimelineSyncLoop.kt around line 192 hydrates under writeMutex, reconcile around line 903 does REST work under the same lock, and stream ingestion around line 1328 can dispatch the notification listener before releasing it. ChatPushService.kt around line 204 can then call conversationApi.getConversation() from that listener. Network latency and notification lookup can block timeline mutation and stream rendering for the whole conversation. Fetch and prepare outside the lock, lock only for state merge, and dispatch notifications after releasing the lock. Add regression coverage proving slow fetch/notification work does not hold the timeline write mutex.

Ingested notification dispatch now resolves/invokes listener after releasing writeMutex. title: Stabilize lifecycle, cache, and timeline concurrency boundaries
description: Architecture coordination epic from broad app review. Major risks cluster around lifecycle-owned background work, repository cache coordination, and timeline/chat stack concurrency. Guide implementation by splitting TimelineSyncLoop responsibilities, adding repository single-flight refresh guards, moving notification dispatch out of timeline mutation locks, and adding regression tests around service teardown, refresh stampedes, and timeline lock behavior.

description: Finding: ChatMessageComponents.kt around line 884 mutates toolCallEntranceAnimationHistory during composition via remember { shouldRunToolCallEntranceAnimation(...) }. Compose can abandon or retry composition, so this can mark a tool-call animation as already played before the UI is actually committed. Move the mutation into an applied side effect: compute a pure alreadySeen value, then record the key from LaunchedEffect(entranceKey) or inside ToolCallEntrance once it actually enters composition.

title: Client-mode run may not complete if WebSocket disconnects
description:
Client-mode runs appear to hinge on the mobile WebSocket staying connected. If the connection breaks, the run may not complete or may fail to settle into timeline state.

Concern:
- Persistent WS is currently being questioned as an architectural foundation.
- The user observed/suspects that the run itself depends on the WS connection; if the connection drops, the run may not finish.
- This is more severe than losing live streaming UI: the durable server-side task should complete or be resumable even if the client disconnects.

Expected architecture:
- WebSocket should be a live progress/streaming transport, not the owner of run lifetime.
- A submitted run/message should have a durable server-side/run identity.
- If WS disconnects, the app should reconnect/reconcile by run/conversation cursor and observe terminal state.
- Timeline/reconcile should eventually show assistant/tool results or a terminal failure state.

Suggested investigation:
- Trace client-mode send path: where is run/message submitted, and does upstream continue execution after WS disconnect?
- Check whether WS gateway cancels upstream run when client socket closes.
- Verify if there is a run id / message id / cursor that can be used to resume after reconnect.
- Add a test harness that starts a run, drops WS mid-run, then confirms either completion is reconciled or a clear terminal failure is surfaced.
- Distinguish live stream interruption from actual run cancellation.

Acceptance:
- Disconnecting the mobile WS mid-run does not silently strand the conversation.
- The app either reconciles completed output after reconnect or displays a durable terminal error.
- Duplicate tool calls/results are avoided after reconnect/reconcile.
notes:
Code-level follow-up after inspecting current implementation:

Actual path:
- ClientModeSendCoordinator.send() collects ClientModeChatSender.streamMessage().
- ClientModeChatSender delegates to InternalBotClient.streamMessage().
- InternalBotClient delegates to BotGateway.streamMessage().
- BotGateway uses RemoteBotSession.streamToAgent().
- RemoteBotSession streams from WsBotClient.streamMessage().
- ClientModeSendCoordinator writes each BotStreamChunk into TimelineRepository.upsertClientModeStreamChunk() once a conversationId is known.

Current disconnect behavior:
- WsBotClient.handleUnexpectedDisconnect() clears socket state, sends RequestSignal.Failure to all active/pending request channels, and schedules reconnect.
- The reconnect restores socket/session state, but does not resume the same in-flight request route.
- The failed RequestSignal bubbles up through streamMessage() to ClientModeSendCoordinator, which exits streaming and shows an error.
- Therefore WS reconnect exists, but in-flight client-mode request resumption does not currently appear to exist.

Current recovery behavior:
- If a conversationId was already observed, TimelineRepository likely has a TimelineSyncLoop for that conversation.
- TimelineSyncLoop.runStreamSubscriber() subscribes to the server conversation stream and can ingest/reconcile server-side events by runId.
- So completed output may still recover via timeline/SSE if the upstream run continues after socket close and the conversation identity is known.
- If WS drops before the first conversationId frame, mobile may only have bootstrap UI state and no conversation timeline loop to reconcile against.

Sharper risk statement:
This is not simply “persistent WS required forever.” It is: in-flight WS request failure terminates the local client-mode send flow; durable recovery depends on whether a conversation/run identity was learned early enough for TimelineSyncLoop/SSE/reconcile to take over.

Suggested patches / mitigations:
1. Early identity handoff:
   - As soon as any chunk provides conversationId, persist/set it and startTimelineObserver immediately; audit the earliest possible frame path and add telemetry for time-to-first-conversationId.
   - If the gateway can return conversationId/session info before stream content, prefer that handshake so timeline recovery can start before model/tool work.

2. Mid-run disconnect UX fallback:
   - On WS failure after conversationId is known, do not leave only a generic “Client Mode send failed.”
   - Switch UI into a “connection lost, reconciling…” state and rely on TimelineSyncLoop to settle, with timeout to final failure if no server events arrive.

3. Reconcile-on-failure:
   - In ClientModeSendCoordinator catch(Exception), if latestConversationId != null, trigger a bounded timeline refresh/reconcile or ensure TimelineSyncLoop is observing before clearing streaming state.
   - If runId is unavailable, reconcile recent conversation messages and collapse fuzzy Client Mode locals, similar to existing external-run reconcile behavior.

4. Preserve pending bootstrap send when no conversationId exists:
   - If disconnect happens before conversationId, keep the bootstrap user echo and surface a retry/resume affordance rather than discarding context.
   - Consider a server/gateway change to return a durable conversationId/request/run id synchronously before stream begins.

5. True request/run resume (larger architecture):
   - Track request_id/run_id from WsBotClient and allow reconnect to reattach/resubscribe to the same in-flight run instead of failing the collector immediately.
   - Requires server-side support/contract: request_id or run_id must map to a still-running upstream job and replay missed frames or expose final state.

Test recommendations:
- Add a harness that starts a client-mode run, drops WS before first conversationId, and verifies the UI shows a retry/terminal state without losing the user message.
- Add a harness that drops WS after conversationId but before done=true, verifies TimelineSyncLoop/reconcile eventually recovers output or shows a clear terminal failure.
- Assert no duplicate tool calls/results after reconnect/reconcile.
close_reason: Mitigated client-mode WS drops: if a conversation id is known, the app keeps the timeline observer active, shows reconciling state, triggers recent-message reconcile, and settles without stranding the run; if no conversation id was received, the bootstrap user message remains with a retryable terminal error. Commit 6e098579.

title: Letta Mobile notification renders blank in notification shade
description:
A Letta Mobile Admin notification appeared in Android notification shade with effectively blank content.

Observed from device screenshot:
- Notification row shows app/title: `Letta Mobile Admin · now` with a bell icon.
- Body/content line contains only `I` or a single stray character, not a useful assistant message preview.
- Notification is shown among notifications, with a collapsed chevron, but gives no meaningful context.
- It appears below a rich Element notification, making the lack of title/body/action affordances very obvious.
- Screenshot also shows a `Silent` section below; verify whether Letta notification channel importance/silent behavior is correct.

Expected:
- Notification should include a useful title and message preview for the assistant response/update.
- Body should not be blank, a single character, or a streaming partial unless intentionally marked as in-progress.
- Notification should use the intended channel importance and not silently degrade unless user explicitly configured it.
- If content is unavailable, use a safe fallback like `New message from Letta Mobile Admin` rather than blank/partial text.

Suggested investigation:
- Inspect `ChannelNotificationPublisher` payload construction and fallback handling.
- Check whether streaming partial text is being published before final preview is available.
- Verify notification channel ID/importance and whether Android has categorized it as silent.
- Confirm notification title/body fields (`setContentTitle`, `setContentText`, `BigTextStyle`) receive nonblank strings.
- Add telemetry for notification preview length and suppression/fallback reason.

Acceptance:
- Reproduced notification has meaningful nonblank content in collapsed shade.
- Blank/single-character previews are suppressed or replaced with fallback copy.
- Channel importance/silent behavior is verified and documented.
close_reason: Fixed by sanitizing notification title/body content, deferring low-signal streaming partials, falling back for single-character/punctuation previews, and adding channel tests.

title: Final assistant response duplicates after multi-tool turn
description:
During refactor regression testing, a multi-tool bash turn rendered with a strange duplicate final response.

Observed from device screenshot:
- Assistant preamble rendered above the tool block.
- Tool block showed only 2 tool calls (Sun May 10 12:52:38 AM EDT 2026, root) even though the assistant invoked 3 bash tools (/opt/stacks/letta-mobile, Sun May 10 12:52:38 AM EDT 2026, root).
- Final assistant response rendered duplicated/merged inline: the summary appeared once, then repeated again starting mid-list after .
- The duplicated text looked like:  followed by the same bullet list again.

Expected:
- All three tool calls should appear exactly once in the tool block or otherwise be represented consistently.
- Final assistant text should render exactly once and not concatenate a second copy into the previous bullet/list item.

Likely area:
- Chat streaming/render model around assistant final text commit after tool calls.
- Possible duplicate assistant message merge, same-id final frame handling, markdown/list continuation, or render-key/dedupe issue.
- Also inspect why the first bash call (/opt/stacks/letta-mobile) was missing from the compact tool group while its result appeared only in final prose.

Repro prompt:
Ask the assistant to run three simple bash tools: /opt/stacks/letta-mobile, Sun May 10 12:52:38 AM EDT 2026, root, then summarize results.

Labels/context:
Regression found while testing multi-tool compact view and notification/refactor changes.
notes: Fixed follow-up duplicate final response race for mixed Client Mode tool runs. Root cause: post-tool assistant prose reused the same cm-assist local id as the pre-tool preamble, so final text could mutate the preamble bubble and then also arrive via server/reconcile. ClientModeTimelineStreamReducer now segments assistant locals after tool-call locals (cm-assist-...-after-tool-N), allowing the post-tool final segment to fuzzy-collapse into the server final while preserving the preamble as a separate bubble. Added TimelineReducerCharacterizationTest coverage for preamble -> tool call/result -> final prose -> server echo, asserting the preamble is unchanged, tool event remains, final prose is confirmed, and no post-tool Local final bubble remains. Verified core reducer test, app chat projection tests, root debug assemble, install/launch on 192.168.50.235:5555, and no recent AndroidRuntime crash logs.
close_reason: Fixed Client Mode mixed tool/prose duplicate final response by segmenting post-tool assistant locals and adding reducer regression coverage

title: Extract ChatSessionResolver and ChatSearchController
description:

title: Decompose AdminChatViewModel god object into tested collaborators
description:

Its constructor around line 336 injects messageRepository, timelineRepository, agentRepository, blockRepository, bugReportRepository, folderRepository, conversationRepository, settingsRepository, bot client, client mode sender/location, and notification trackers. It owns route resolution, search, timeline observation, streaming, approvals, project activity, notification state, composer state, and legacy client-mode fallback. Extract ChatSessionResolver, ClientModeStreamReducer, ChatSearchController, ChatApprovalCoordinator, and ProjectAgentActivityLoader behind current behavior. Leave AdminChatViewModel as a thin state composer/orchestrator.

Explicit tests:
Unit tests for each collaborator plus existing AdminChatViewModel tests. Regression coverage for fresh route hydration, explicit conversation route hydration, client-mode resume, agent-name seeding, approval missing/success/failure flags, and project activity folder/live-status behavior.

Success gate checklist:
- First pass AdminChatViewModel target under 1,500 lines.
- Follow-up target under 800 lines if feasible.
- No behavior change in chat open, send, approval, stream, search, or project chat.
- Targeted unit tests and relevant app unit tests pass.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Use characterization tests before extraction. Keep extracted collaborators package-private/internal where possible and route all state changes through old public VM surface until parity is proven.
notes:
2026-05-10 second-pass review: AdminChatViewModel.kt is now 1,164 lines (first-pass gate met; epic follow-up <800 still open). Current extracted collaborators: ChatSessionResolver, ChatSearchCoordinator/Controller, ChatTimelineObserver, ClientModeSendCoordinator, ProjectChatCoordinator, ChatApprovalCoordinator, ProjectAgentActivityLoader, ChatComposerController, ChatRunExpansionState. Biggest remaining VM-owned clusters are (1) route/fresh-client-mode resolution + activeConversationId/clientModeBootstrapState + loadMessagesInternal + initial message duplicate guard + resetMessages, (2) non-client timeline send + conversation summary create/update + loadOlderMessages paging, and (3) small lifecycle/interrupt/settings delegates. 2026-05-10 implementation: Completed second-pass decomposition. Added focused seam tests for ClientModeSendCoordinator, ChatTimelineObserver, ChatConversationCoordinator, TimelineSendCoordinator, and ChatHistoryPager. Extracted ChatConversationCoordinator (route/fresh-client-mode resolution, active conversation state, initial route message delivery, reset/load), TimelineSendCoordinator (non-client timeline send + summary setup), and ChatHistoryPager (older page backfill). AdminChatViewModel.kt is now 719 lines (<800 target). Also preserved both reducer contracts by keeping punctuation-only post-tool assistant deltas attached to the pre-tool assistant bubble while splitting full post-tool prose for fuzzy collapse.
