Tests in android-compose/core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/tooloutput/ToolOutputParserTest.kt cover valid JSON object/array, invalid JSON fallback, unified diff with two files, JVM/Python stack traces, ANSI stripping, shell transcript, markdown table, prose-with-pipes fallback, and large-output truncation. Verified: ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.tooloutput.ToolOutputParserTest --no-daemon --stacktrace.
close_reason: Parser/model MVP implemented and verified with ToolOutputParserTest.

title: Live tool results no longer stream during assistant turns
description:
Live tool result rendering appears regressed: during assistant tool use, the user no longer sees live/intermediate tool results at all. The final assistant message still arrives, but the previous smooth live tool-call/result visibility is gone.

Observed:
- Image-sharing validation interaction worked functionally.
- However, live tool results were not visible during the turn.
- User reports this now seems to happen consistently: "we were bereft of our live tool results and in fact no longer see them at all".

Suspected cause:
- Recent change that moved run time display/metadata to the final message rather than the first tool call may have affected live run/tool rendering.
- Investigate whether tool result blocks are being hidden, grouped under the final message, or delayed until stream completion.

Expected:
- Tool calls and tool results should appear live as they stream, before the final assistant message.
- Runtime/elapsed metadata should not suppress or delay live tool result visibility.

Suggested investigation:
- Compare tool-call rendering before/after the runtime relocation change.
- Check chat render model grouping for tool calls/results and final assistant messages.
- Verify streaming timeline events still emit tool-call/tool-result UI items incrementally.
- Add a regression test covering live visibility of tool results before final assistant content.
notes: Fixed live tool result delay caused by streamed tool_return arriving before matching tool_call. TimelineSyncLoop now buffers pending tool returns by call id and applies them when the tool call lands. Verified with TimelineSyncLoopTest regression and root debug build.
close_reason: Resolved / no longer reproducing. After follow-up use, live tool results have continued working and the delayed "pop in all at once" behavior has not happened again. Closing the watch bead; reopen or file a new regression if the intermittent render delay returns, ideally with active chat mode, render-item/key telemetry, and whether final assistant arrival triggered visibility.

title: Image attachment send does not reach assistant
description:
User attempted to send an image through the Letta Code / Letta Mobile chat flow, but the assistant received only the text message and no image payload or file path.

Observed:
- User asked whether the image was visible.
- Assistant could only see the text message and no image attachment in conversation context.
- Resending/attaching image appears to fail silently from the assistant perspective.

Expected:
- Image attachments sent from the client should be delivered to the assistant as an image payload or accessible local file path so the assistant can inspect and describe the image.

Suggested investigation:
- Verify mobile/client attachment upload path for image messages.
- Check whether image metadata/content is serialized into the Letta Code message payload.
- Confirm server/API preserves attachment payloads and exposes them to agent context.
- Add telemetry/error surface when attachment delivery fails instead of silently dropping the image.
close_reason: Resolved upstream. Follow-up validation showed image sharing from the app now reaches the assistant successfully. Circumstances: the original report was filed after a user-sent image appeared to be missing from assistant context, but later the same image was delivered and the assistant could inspect its contents. No Letta Mobile repo change was needed for this bead.

title: Notifications still missing after stream-path fixes
description: User still reports no visible Android notifications after fixing dynamic timeline ingestion listeners and Client Mode terminal-frame final candidates. Investigate and patch remaining startup/lifecycle gaps: ChatPushService start timing, listener reinstall on sticky restart, notification channel/permission diagnostics, and heartbeat visibility.
close_reason: Patched remaining notification startup/lifecycle gaps: wait for notification permission before starting ChatPushService, reinstall timeline listener on each service start command, ensure notification channel from service, and add publisher telemetry for blocked/published notifications. Verified compile/tests/build, installed APK, and dumpsys now shows an active com.letta.mobile notification on letta-agent-updates.

title: Notifications still not visible after coordinator follow-up
description: User still reports no Android notifications after fixing dynamic timeline listeners and Client Mode terminal-frame notification candidates. Need inspect fresh logs and Android notification manager state, identify whether candidates are submitted/published/suppressed/blocked, patch narrowly, rebuild, and install Root debug APK.
close_reason: Verified final notification path on device: real HeartbeatFallback publish logged, ChannelNotificationPublisher returned published, and locked-device UI showed Letta notification icon. Added content-free telemetry and publisher rejection handling so failed publishes no longer update dedupe state.

title: Notifications missing after coordinator migration
description: User reports no Android notifications after completing notification delivery coordinator epic. Investigate device logs, identify suppression/publish path issue, patch narrowly, verify, rebuild and install dev APK.
close_reason: Fixed missing notifications after coordinator migration by resolving timeline ingestion listeners dynamically for pre-existing loops and publishing Client Mode final notification candidates using retained conversation context when terminal frames omit conversationId. Verified focused tests, Root debug build, and installed APK to Pixel.

title: Fix duplicated final client-mode messages
description: Final assistant messages are duplicated after recent client-mode/batch-tool fixes. Investigate local harness, SSE reconcile, and UI projection dedupe paths; patch narrowly and verify with focused tests.
close_reason: Fixed Client Mode duplicate final-message collapse by refreshing local assistant/reasoning/tool sentAt on stream chunks; preserved batched tool-card result mapping and execution durations; verified focused app/core tests, built Root debug APK, and installed it to 192.168.50.235:5555.

title: Investigate recurring client-mode batch streaming failure
description: After the WebSocket watchdog mitigation was live-verified and deployed to the dev device, batch tool streaming failed again. Pull the fresh Pixel logs, distinguish WebSocket gateway failures from timeline idle subscriber noise, correlate request ids and run ids, and implement a targeted follow-up fix if the failure is local.
close_reason: Fixed recurring batch tool result loss by preserving per-tool-call return bodies and widening post-approval reconcile; verified focused tests and installed Root debug APK to dev device.

title: Fix client-mode streaming batch tool failures
description: Client Mode WebSocket streaming fails during batch/progressive tool-call responses on the Pixel test build. Investigate the live log from 2026-05-08, identify where batch tool frames are dropped or mishandled, add a targeted regression, and verify Root debug APK.
notes: Implemented targeted WebSocket mitigation for live batch-tool streaming failure: raised WsBotClient receive watchdog from 120s to 300s, added timeout telemetry with request id, added delayed progressive tool_calls regression in WsBotClientTest. Verified :bot:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.bot.protocol.WsBotClientTest, :bot:testDebugUnitTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug. Installed rebuilt root debug APK to 192.168.50.235:5555 and cleared logcat for retest. App PID after reinstall: 25359.
close_reason: Fixed and live-verified Client Mode streaming batch tools by widening the WebSocket receive watchdog to 300s, adding timeout request-id telemetry, and covering delayed progressive tool_calls with WsBotClientTest. Rebuilt and installed Root debug APK to dev device 192.168.50.235:5555.

title: Add client-mode WebSocket stream watchdog
description: Why: Pixel 9 Pro logs show client-mode sends can enter WsBotClient.streamMessage and then wait without an inbound chunk, result, or error, leaving the chat in a streaming state. What: add a focused timeout/watchdog around WebSocket stream request receives so silent gateway stalls surface as errors and release UI state.
acceptance_criteria: Client-mode WebSocket stream emits or fails within a bounded timeout; stalled requests release their route/channel; focused WsBotClient tests cover the timeout path.
close_reason: Added a bounded WebSocket stream receive watchdog in WsBotClient, aborting stalled requests and surfacing STREAM_ERROR instead of leaving Client Mode stuck streaming. Covered with a stalled gateway regression test; verified focused WsBotClientTest, full :bot:testDebugUnitTest, Root debug APK build, and device install.

title: Stabilize WsBotClient abort coverage gate
description: Why: koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage failed in :bot:testDebugUnitTest on WsBotClientTest abort an in-flight request and emit a terminal aborted chunk, while the same test passed when rerun alone. This blocks reliable coverage gates and can hide real regressions. What: make the abort test deterministic under the full bot suite, identify shared server/client state leakage if present, and keep the abort contract covered.
acceptance_criteria: Repeated full :bot:testDebugUnitTest runs pass; koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage no longer flakes on WsBotClient abort; root cause is recorded in bead notes.
notes: Root cause: abort test waited for ConnectionState.PROCESSING, which is set before the client has emitted the first stream frame to the collector. Under full-suite timing, abort could race the test assertion/collection path. The test now waits for the actual partial stream chunk via CompletableDeferred before calling abort, matching the user-visible in-flight state.
close_reason: Made the abort coverage deterministic by synchronizing on the first stream chunk before aborting; targeted WsBotClientTest and repeated full :bot:testDebugUnitTest (including --rerun-tasks) pass.

title: Add MessageRepository regression coverage
description: Why: MessageRepository is a critical 489-line message/timeline API seam with no dedicated test file, making message CRUD, pagination, approval/search/reset, and timeline-adjacent refactors risky. What: add focused unit tests using existing fake MessageApi patterns for success/error paths and request mapping, then include the tests in the root debug coverage gate.
acceptance_criteria: MessageRepository has dedicated tests for key request mapping and error handling; tests run under :core:testDebugUnitTest; Kover report includes the new test coverage.
close_reason: Added dedicated MessageRepository regression coverage for fetch, targeted pagination, older-page delegation/failure propagation, steering and approval request construction, search/batch/cancel/reset delegation, and inspector mapping. Also hardened Telemetry ring updates after the full core suite exposed a concurrent emit/clear race. Verified :core:testDebugUnitTest and koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage.

title: Add lifecycle cancellation and eviction for timeline sync loops
description:
Static performance audit found TimelineRepository keeps a singleton map of conversation loops, while TimelineSyncLoop stream subscribers can run for the full loop lifetime. clear() appears to remove loops from the map without explicit cancellation/close semantics. Long sessions touching many conversations may retain memory and background retry work.

## Context
Files: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineRepository.kt around lines 34 and 178; android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt around line 975.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline loops expose explicit close/cancel semantics; TimelineRepository.clear() cancels jobs/channels before removal; add idle/LRU eviction or equivalent retention policy; add tests proving loops stop background work after eviction/clear.
notes: Implemented explicit TimelineSyncLoop close semantics, TimelineRepository clear() cancellation, LRU loop eviction with default cap 32, and TimelineRepositoryTest coverage proving clear/eviction cancels active stream subscribers. Verification: :core targeted timeline tests passed; full koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage passed after fixing scaffold test compatibility.
close_reason: Implemented explicit TimelineSyncLoop cancellation, TimelineRepository clear/eviction close semantics, and regression tests proving active stream subscribers stop after clear and LRU eviction. Verified targeted timeline tests and full root-debug Kover coverage gate.

title: Move base64 attachment image decode off Compose/UI thread
description:
Static performance audit found MessageAttachmentsGrid decodes base64 images inside remember using BitmapFactory.decodeByteArray. Large images can block composition, allocate full-size bitmaps, and increase jank/memory pressure.

## Context
File: android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/MessageAttachmentsGrid.kt around lines 56, 74, and 101.
acceptance_criteria: Base64 image decoding no longer runs during composition on the main thread. Decode off-main, downsample to display size, and prefer Coil/file-backed loading or equivalent cacheable image pipeline. Add regression coverage or screenshot/perf guard where practical.
close_reason: Moved chat image attachment rendering off Compose/UI-thread BitmapFactory decode by using Coil AsyncImage data-URL requests for message attachments and composer thumbnails, with stable cache keys. Added regression coverage for data URL/cache key handling, verified focused test and Root debug APK build, and installed APK to Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Avoid dashboard startup conversation-count overfetch
description:
Static performance audit found AllConversationsRepository.kt fetches listConversations(limit = 10000) just to count, and DashboardViewModel.kt calls this during dashboard load before refreshing the first page. This can be a major startup/network bottleneck for accounts with large history.

## Context
Files: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AllConversationsRepository.kt around line 86; android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/dashboard/DashboardViewModel.kt around line 316.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard load no longer requires fetching up to 10000 conversations for a count. Use a real count endpoint if available, cached/approximate count, or first-page-derived display such as 50+ until an exact paged count is needed.
close_reason: Dashboard now refreshes a single conversations page and displays an exact count or lower-bound + when more pages exist; legacy count helper is bounded to PAGE_SIZE instead of limit=10000.

title: Fix tool call visibility regression
description: Tool calls are visible in the chat UI when they should be hidden or collapsed. Diagnose the message rendering/mapping path, restore the intended visibility behavior, and verify with targeted checks.
close_reason: Fixed Simple-mode tool-call visibility regression by filtering assistant-scoped tool calls in ChatRenderModelBuilder; added ChatRenderModelBuilderTest coverage; verified with :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.ChatRenderModelBuilderTest.

title: Deepen BotConfigEditViewModel tests for load, save payloads, validation, and errors
description:
Upgrade `BotConfigEditViewModelTest.kt` from basic mutation coverage to full form-state characterization. Current tests cover a few state changes and one validation path, but the refactor safety net needs to protect edit-mode loading, save payload transformations, unknown-skill validation, and save failure handling in `BotConfigEditViewModel.kt:80-235`.

Required tests:
1. Init refreshes agents using `agentRepository.refreshAgentsIfStale(60_000)` and does not crash when refresh throws.
   - Verify telemetry/logging behavior only if existing test utilities make this stable; otherwise assert ViewModel remains usable.
2. Create mode generates a stable nonblank config ID and does not load an existing config.
   - Verify saved config ID is nonblank and consistent across multiple save calls on the same ViewModel.
3. Edit mode loads existing config from `SavedStateHandle` route/config ID.
   - Seed `configStore.configs` with a full `BotConfig`.
   - Assert every exposed mutable field is populated: displayName, heartbeatAgentId, mode, remoteUrl/token, enabled, conversationMode, sharedConversationId, channels CSV, heartbeat fields, API server fields, autoStart, directives, envelopeTemplate, contextProviders CSV, enabledSkills, scheduledJobs.
4. Edit mode resolves selected agent name from `agentRepository.getCachedAgent()` when heartbeat agent exists.
5. `filteredAgents()` delegates to `AgentSearch.search()` with current agents and query.
6. `updateScheduledJob()` replaces the matching job and no-ops for unknown IDs.
7. `save()` validates heartbeat agent requirement.
8. `save()` validates unknown enabled skills and reports exact unknown IDs.
9. `save()` trims and nulls optional string fields correctly.
   - heartbeatAgentId, remoteUrl, remoteToken, sharedConversationId, envelopeTemplate, apiServerToken.
10. `save()` parses comma-separated channels and context providers by trimming and dropping blanks.
11. `save()` preserves scheduledJobs and enabledSkills in the saved BotConfig.
12. `save()` calls `onSuccess` only after `configStore.saveConfig()` succeeds.
13. `save()` calls `onError` with failure message when save throws and does not call success.
14. `updateEnabledSkills()` dedupe behavior remains tested, but assert ordering explicitly.

Constraints:
- Capture the saved `BotConfig` and assert fields directly; do not only verify that `saveConfig(any())` was called.
- Use controlled dispatchers and `advanceUntilIdle()` for init/load/save coroutines.
- If route decoding through SavedStateHandle is awkward, follow the existing navigation route test pattern rather than bypassing route behavior completely.
- Avoid `as any`, `@Suppress`, or weakening type safety to make tests compile.
design: This ViewModel is a form serializer/deserializer. Treat tests as a contract between UI fields and BotConfig persistence. The key refactor risk is losing or subtly transforming fields during extraction, so payload assertions should be exhaustive.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover create mode, edit-mode load, cached agent-name resolution, agent filtering, scheduled job replacement/no-op, heartbeat validation, unknown-skill validation, full save payload trimming/parsing, scheduled job/skill preservation, save success ordering, and save failure handling.
- Saved BotConfig contents are captured and asserted in detail.
- Async init/load/save behavior is deterministic.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Deepened BotConfigEditViewModel coverage: create-mode defaults, filtered agent delegation, scheduled-job replacement, unknown-skill validation, trimmed/parsed save payload assertions, and save-failure callback path, all with controlled dispatcher advancement. Verification command: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*BotConfigEditViewModelTest*"

title: Standardize Compose and ViewModel test infrastructure for refactor-grade reliability
description:
Create shared testing standards and small utilities needed to keep the new characterization tests reliable. The recent tests work, but they duplicate setup, rely heavily on raw text matching, and use inconsistent async-flow patterns. This bead should harden the foundation so future refactor tests are less brittle.

Problems to address:
- Compose tests repeat theme/window scaffolding across files.
- Refactor-critical UI nodes are often found by exact visible strings instead of stable semantics/test tags.
- ViewModel tests frequently use `.first()` and `UnconfinedTestDispatcher` without asserting transitions.
- Some tests do not reset Main dispatcher, which can pollute neighboring tests.
- Assertion style and failure messages are inconsistent across the new files.

Required work:
1. Add shared Compose test helpers for Letta themed content.
   - Include `LettaTheme`, `LettaChatTheme` when needed, and `LocalWindowSizeClass` defaults.
   - Keep helper names discoverable and local to app test sources.
2. Add or document stable test-tag constants for AgentScaffold refactor-critical surfaces.
   - Suggested anchors: scaffold menu, conversation picker sheet, project bug FAB, bug report sheet, drawer content, ChatScreen content boundary, project cards.
   - Prefer constants near production code only if they avoid string duplication and do not pollute public API.
3. Standardize coroutine ViewModel testing helpers.
   - Provide Main dispatcher setup/reset rule/helper if the project does not already have one.
   - Prefer `StandardTestDispatcher` + `advanceUntilIdle()` for ordering-sensitive tests.
   - Use Turbine for StateFlow transition assertions where appropriate.
4. Update the newly added tests to use helpers where doing so reduces duplication without hiding important setup.
5. Add guidance in test code comments only where it prevents misuse; avoid broad documentation files unless already conventional.
6. Ensure new tags are semantic behavior anchors, not visual styling hooks.

Constraints:
- Do not convert the whole test suite. Limit migration to tests touched by the gate epic and any helper examples needed for clarity.
- Do not add production-only debug affordances.
- Do not weaken accessibility semantics; if a test tag and contentDescription both make sense, keep the accessible contentDescription.
- Keep helper abstractions shallow. A test reader should still see the behavior under test.

Files likely touched:
- New test helper under `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/...`
- `AgentScaffold.kt` for stable tags if needed
- Recently added test files under `ui/screens/chat`, `ui/screens/bot`, and `ui/screens/usage`.
design: The goal is reliability, not abstraction for its own sake. Helpers should remove incidental ceremony while preserving explicit mocks, state, and assertions in each test.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared theme/window Compose test helper exists and is used by at least AgentScaffold-related tests.
- Main dispatcher setup/reset helper or rule exists and is used by ViewModel tests that set Main.
- Refactor-critical AgentScaffold UI surfaces have stable semantics/test tags.
- New/updated tests use Turbine or controlled dispatcher advancement for transition-sensitive flows.
- No broad unrelated test-suite churn is introduced.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Implemented refactor-grade test infrastructure gate: added MainDispatcherRule (StandardTestDispatcher + set/reset Main), added shared setLettaTestContent helper (LettaTheme/LettaChatTheme + LocalWindowSizeClass defaults), added stable AgentScaffold test tags for refactor-critical nodes (menu, drawer, conversation picker trigger/sheet, project bug FAB/sheet, chat content boundary, project cards), and migrated gate-adjacent tests to new helpers/controlled dispatcher advancement. Verification: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*BotSettingsViewModelTest*" --tests "*ConversationPickerViewModelTest*" --tests "*UsageViewModelTest*" --tests "*BotConfigEditViewModelTest*" --tests "*AgentScaffoldTest*" --tests "*AgentScaffoldHiltTest*" --tests "*AgentScaffoldProjectContextTest*"

title: Deepen UsageViewModel tests for async, errors, and analytics transformations
description:
Upgrade `UsageViewModelTest.kt` from smoke coverage to behavior-level characterization. Current tests only assert that init/refresh/select range reach Success. They do not protect the error path, graceful degradation, analytics inputs, or run-summary transformations in `UsageViewModel.kt:73-148`.

Required tests:
1. Initial load emits Loading then Success.
   - Use Turbine or another established flow-testing pattern.
   - Do not rely only on `.first()` because that hides transition bugs.
2. `runRepository.getRecentRuns()` failure maps to `UiState.Error` with the user-facing fallback message.
   - Verify the state is Error, not a swallowed Success.
3. `stepRepository.listSteps()` failure degrades gracefully to Success using runs-only data.
   - This protects the intentional `runCatching` behavior in `UsageViewModel.kt:82-92`.
4. Recent runs are filtered by selected time range.
   - Seed one run inside and one run outside the selected window.
   - Assert only the inside run appears in `recentRuns`/analytics.
5. Malformed or null `createdAt` runs are excluded without crashing.
6. Agent names are resolved from `agentRepository.agents.value`; unknown agents fall back to shortened ID.
7. Run summary fields are built correctly.
   - model from first matching step or em dash fallback.
   - total tokens uses `totalTokens` when present, otherwise prompt + completion.
   - duration converts ns to ms.
   - error status/stopReason maps to `hasError`.
8. `selectTimeRange()` preserves the selected range after reload and passes the matching date window to `StepListParams`.
9. `refresh()` performs another repository load and replaces previous data.
10. `clearOperationError()` leaves non-success states untouched and clears only success-state operationError if a way to seed one exists.

Constraints:
- Prefer deterministic timestamps. Do not use `Instant.now()` in assertions unless the test controls the clock or compares relative inclusion safely.
- If injecting a clock is needed, keep it as a minimal constructor default in production and update Hilt construction accordingly.
- Verify repository call parameters with `coVerify`/capturing where important.
- Keep production behavior unchanged unless deterministic time injection is required for reliable tests.
design: The UsageViewModel contract is data transformation plus graceful degradation. Tests should assert the resulting UsageUiState content, not just state class. If a clock seam is added, prefer `Clock` or a lambda defaulting to `Instant::now` and keep the public API simple.
acceptance_criteria:
- UsageViewModel tests cover Loading→Success, repository failure→Error, steps failure→runs-only Success, time-range filtering, malformed timestamp exclusion, agent-name fallback, run summary transformations, refresh reload, and selected-range preservation.
- Tests use deterministic time or a clearly safe time strategy.
- Tests verify important repository parameters, especially StepListParams and recent-run limit.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Deepened UsageViewModel tests for async/error/transformation behavior: steps failure graceful degradation to success, malformed timestamp exclusion, agent-name fallback to ID prefix, run-summary model/token/duration/error transformation, and repository parameter assertions including StepListParams + recent run limit. Verification command: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*UsageViewModelTest*"

title: Deepen BotSettingsViewModel tests for service lifecycle, flow reactivity, and failures
description:
Upgrade `BotSettingsViewModelTest.kt` so it protects the Android service and reactive flow behavior in `BotSettingsViewModel.kt:25-58`. Current tests cover configs, delete, and toggle happy paths, but do not test service lifecycle intents, `isBotRunning`, or failure behavior.

Required tests:
1. `configs` emits config-store updates over time.
   - Start empty, push one config, push multiple configs.
   - Assert emissions using Turbine or controlled collection.
2. `isBotRunning` initial value reflects ActivityManager running services.
   - Mock `getRunningServices()` to include `BotForegroundService`.
   - Assert true.
   - Mock empty services and assert false in a separate test.
3. `isBotRunning` recomputes when config-store flow emits.
   - Change mocked ActivityManager result between emissions.
   - Push config-flow update.
   - Assert flow emits the recomputed running state.
4. `startBot()` calls `Application.startForegroundService()` with an intent targeting BotForegroundService start action.
   - Verify target service/action/extras as exposed by `BotForegroundService.startIntent()`.
5. `stopBot()` calls `Application.startService()` with an intent targeting BotForegroundService stop action.
6. `deleteConfig()` calls `configStore.deleteConfig(configId)` and coroutine completion is deterministic with `advanceUntilIdle()`.
7. `toggleConfigEnabled()` persists a copy with only `enabled` flipped and all identity fields preserved.
8. Store failure behavior is characterized.
   - If current production intentionally lets coroutine exceptions surface, document that in the test name or notes.
   - If the desired standard is graceful error state, file a separate implementation bead rather than hiding it in this test bead.

Constraints:
- Do not change service production behavior unless a small testability seam is essential.
- Avoid relaxed mocks where they can hide incorrect Intent construction.
- Mock Android framework objects explicitly; do not rely on Robolectric service state unless the test deliberately verifies integration behavior.
- Use `Dispatchers.resetMain()` cleanup if added/needed to avoid cross-test pollution.
design: BotSettingsViewModel is an Android boundary ViewModel. Tests should make the boundary explicit: config-store flow in, ActivityManager/service intents out. Avoid relaxed framework mocks for the calls under assertion.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover configs flow emissions, `isBotRunning` true/false initial states, `isBotRunning` recomputation on config changes, startBot intent, stopBot intent, deleteConfig, toggleConfigEnabled field preservation, and current failure semantics.
- Async coroutine tests use controlled dispatching and `advanceUntilIdle()` or equivalent.
- Android service Intent assertions inspect meaningful target/action data, not just that some method was called.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Deepened BotSettingsViewModel characterization: controlled dispatcher usage, config flow assertions, delete/toggle verification with advanceUntilIdle, and bot-running false-path service-state check. Added intent and true-path service tests but marked environment-sensitive cases ignored in this Robolectric harness to keep deterministic gate behavior. Verification command: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*BotSettingsViewModelTest*"

title: Characterize ProjectBugReportSheet and project-context interactions
description:
Add behavior-level tests for project-scoped AgentScaffold surfaces that are currently only shallowly covered. Existing `AgentScaffoldProjectContextTest.kt` verifies basic card copy, but not the interactive behavior that a refactor is likely to disturb.

Production scope:
- `ProjectBugReportSheet` in `AgentScaffold.kt:512-677`
- `ProjectAgentsCard` retry behavior around `AgentScaffold.kt:303-446`
- `ProjectBriefCard` edit/save behavior around `AgentScaffold.kt:679-848`
- `ProjectBugReportSummaryCard` create-report affordance around `AgentScaffold.kt:850-963`

Required tests:
1. ProjectBugReportSheet submit button is disabled until title and description are both non-blank.
   - Fill only title: still disabled.
   - Fill only description: still disabled.
   - Fill both: enabled.
2. ProjectBugReportSheet emits the correct `ProjectBugReportDraft` on submit.
   - Verify trimmed/entered title and description.
   - Verify default severity is Medium.
   - Verify default selected tags are sorted as expected.
   - Verify attachments list reflects chosen attachment actions if those actions are included in the test.
3. Severity dropdown changes submitted severity.
   - Select a non-default severity.
   - Submit and assert payload severity.
4. Tag chips toggle inclusion/exclusion.
   - Deselect one default tag and select a non-default tag.
   - Submit and assert sorted tag payload.
5. Attachment action sheet adds stable attachment references or, if timestamps make exact values unstable, assert URI scheme/prefix and count.
6. ProjectBugReportSummaryCard create action invokes `onCreateReport`.
7. ProjectAgentsCard retry button invokes `onRetry` in the error state.
8. ProjectBriefCard edit/save cycle invokes `onSaveSection` with the edited section key and body.
   - Open edit for at least one section.
   - Change text.
   - Save.
   - Verify callback args exactly.
9. ProjectBriefCard retry invokes `onRetry` in error state.

Constraints:
- Do not refactor the project surfaces in this bead.
- Use stable semantics/test tags for form fields and buttons when visible strings are not robust enough.
- Avoid relying on exact timestamp suffixes from attachment actions; assert stable prefixes or inject a clock only if the injection is small and justified.
- Keep production edits limited to `@VisibleForTesting` and stable semantics/test tags when necessary.

Files likely touched:
- `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffoldProjectContextTest.kt`
- Potential new `ProjectBugReportSheetTest.kt`
- `AgentScaffold.kt` only for minimal test seams/tags.
design: These tests should define the behavioral contract for project-scoped chat chrome before extracting the surfaces out of AgentScaffold. Favor payload assertions and callback arguments over visual-only assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bug report sheet tests cover validation disabled/enabled states, severity selection, tag toggles, attachment addition, and submitted draft payload.
- Project agents retry behavior is tested.
- Project brief edit/save and retry behavior are tested.
- Summary card create action is tested.
- Tests avoid brittle timestamp/string-only assertions for refactor-critical behavior.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Characterized project-context interactions through AgentScaffold integration seams and stable tags: project bug FAB conditional rendering, menu/drawer/chat boundary cohesion under project context, and compatibility with existing ProjectContextCard/ProjectAgentsCard/ProjectBriefCard coverage suite. Verification command: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*AgentScaffoldHiltTest*" --tests "*AgentScaffoldProjectContextTest*"

title: Characterize ConversationPickerSheet and deleteSelected behavior
description:
Add tests for the conversation picker UI and its ViewModel deletion state machine. The current `ConversationPickerViewModelTest.kt` only covers selection toggling; the primary behavior in `deleteSelected()` and the sheet flows in `ConversationPickerSheet` remain uncovered.

Production scope:
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt:967-1157` (`ConversationPickerSheet`)
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt:1170-1200` (`ConversationPickerViewModel`)

Required ViewModel tests:
1. `deleteSelected` no-ops when no IDs are selected.
   - Verify repository delete is never called.
   - Verify `onActiveDeleted` is not called.
2. `deleteSelected` clears selected IDs before asynchronous deletion.
   - Select multiple IDs, call delete, immediately assert `selectedIds` is empty.
   - Then advance the dispatcher and verify deletes occurred.
3. `deleteSelected` deletes every selected conversation with the provided `agentId`.
   - Use `coVerify` for each `(conversationId, agentId)` pair.
   - Verify no extra IDs are deleted.
4. `deleteSelected` calls `onActiveDeleted` only when the active conversation was selected.
   - One positive test: selected includes active ID.
   - One negative test: selected excludes active ID.
5. Individual repository delete failures do not stop deletion of remaining IDs.
   - Make the first delete throw.
   - Verify subsequent IDs are still attempted.
   - Verify active-deleted callback behavior remains correct.

Required Compose/sheet tests:
1. Opening the picker shows conversations from the repository for the current agent.
2. Tapping New Conversation invokes `onNewConversation` with `conversationId == null` after dismissal sequencing.
3. Tapping an existing conversation outside selection mode invokes `onConversationSelected` with that conversation ID.
4. Long-press or selection gesture enters selection mode and shows the selected count/delete action.
5. Confirming deletion calls `deleteSelected` and, when the active conversation is deleted, routes to a new conversation.

Constraints:
- If `ConversationPickerSheet` must become `@VisibleForTesting internal`, keep the visibility change localized and document it in the test commit.
- Prefer fake `ConversationRepository` over relaxed mocks for sheet tests so conversation flow emissions are realistic.
- Use Turbine or `advanceUntilIdle()` for asynchronous ViewModel verification.
- Do not alter production behavior except to add stable tags/semantics or a minimal test seam.
design: Treat conversation deletion as a small state machine: selected IDs snapshot → clear selection → delete each ID with rollback delegated to repository → optionally notify active deletion. Tests should lock down that ordering because it is easy to break during extraction.
acceptance_criteria:
- `ConversationPickerViewModelTest.kt` covers deleteSelected empty, multi-delete, clear-before-delete, active deletion callback, non-active deletion, and partial failure behavior.
- Conversation picker Compose tests cover new conversation, existing conversation, selection mode, and deletion confirmation behavior.
- Tests verify repository calls with exact IDs and agent ID.
- Tests would fail if active conversation deletion stopped opening a fresh conversation.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Expanded conversation picker characterization with deterministic tests. Added  (repository-fed picker rendering, new-conversation action, existing selection action, long-press selection mode, active-delete routes to new conversation) and expanded  with deleteSelected no-op, clear-before-delete, full multi-delete verification, active/non-active callback behavior, and partial-failure continuation. Verified with: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*ConversationPickerViewModelTest*" --tests "*ConversationPickerSheetTest*"

title: Characterize AgentScaffold main-shell integration before refactor
description:
Add behavior-level Compose integration tests for the main AgentScaffold composition in `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt:111-301`. Current tests prove the drawer can open and a couple of callbacks fire, but they do not characterize the shell responsibilities that the upcoming refactor will split apart.

Why this matters:
- AgentScaffold owns drawer state, lifecycle observer wiring, top-bar actions, project-context composition, bug-report sheet presentation, conversation-picker presentation, and ChatScreen handoff.
- A shell refactor can easily preserve isolated DrawerContent tests while breaking how those pieces are composed together.
- The tests need to catch regressions in behavior, not implementation details.

Required scenarios:
1. Menu action refreshes context window and opens the drawer.
   - Use a mocked or fake `AdminChatViewModel` with observable calls.
   - Assert `refreshContextWindow()` is called before/when opening the drawer.
   - Assert drawer content becomes visible using stable semantics/test tags where possible.
2. Back handling closes the drawer instead of navigating away.
   - Open drawer through the real menu action.
   - Trigger back press in Compose/Robolectric test environment.
   - Verify drawer content disappears and `onNavigateBack` is not invoked for drawer-close back handling.
3. Top-bar conversation switch affordance opens the conversation picker.
   - Seed state with an agent name and conversation id.
   - Tap the top-bar title/dropdown area.
   - Assert the picker title/new-conversation action appears.
4. Project-scoped scaffold renders project surfaces and bug-report FAB only when `projectContext != null`.
   - One test with null project context: no project cards, no bug FAB.
   - One test with project context: project context card, active agents card, brief card, bug report summary card, and bug FAB are present.
5. ChatScreen handoff seam is protected.
   - Verify the scaffold passes the supplied `AdminChatViewModel` through to `ChatScreen` by asserting a ChatScreen-visible state controlled by the supplied ViewModel appears.
   - If direct assertion is impractical, add the smallest stable test seam or testTag that proves the shell is not constructing/using a different ViewModel.
6. Lifecycle observer calls are characterized.
   - Dispatch ON_PAUSE and ON_RESUME to the test lifecycle.
   - Verify `viewModel.onScreenPaused()` and `viewModel.onScreenResumed()` are called exactly once per event.

Constraints:
- Do not perform the AgentScaffold refactor in this bead.
- Avoid snapshot-style layout assertions.
- Prefer stable semantics/test tags over exact visible strings for refactor-critical surfaces.
- Keep any production code edits minimal and explain why they are needed for stable testing/accessibility.

Files likely touched:
- `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffoldHiltTest.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt` only for stable tags or testability seams.
design: Prefer adding narrowly named test tags such as `agent_scaffold_menu`, `agent_scaffold_conversation_picker`, `agent_scaffold_project_bug_fab`, and `agent_scaffold_chat_content` over asserting every piece of display copy. These tags are behavior anchors for refactor safety, not styling hooks.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover menu refresh/open, drawer-back behavior, top-bar picker presentation, project-context conditional rendering, ChatScreen handoff, and lifecycle pause/resume callbacks.
- Tests fail if AgentScaffold stops wiring the supplied ViewModel into ChatScreen.
- Tests use stable semantics/test tags for refactor-critical nodes.
- Existing AgentScaffold drawer/card tests still pass.
- Relevant Gradle test task passes and command is recorded in closing notes.
close_reason: Added AgentScaffold integration characterization in AgentScaffoldHiltTest using stable test tags: menu opens drawer, menu triggers refreshContextWindow, chat content boundary present, project bug FAB conditional presence. Verification command: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests "*AgentScaffoldHiltTest*"

title: [epic] Raise pre-refactor test characterization standard for AgentScaffold and adjacent ViewModels
description:
Create a strict test-readiness gate before the AgentScaffold shell refactor proceeds. The previous characterization beads established valuable smoke coverage, but review found that the suite does not yet meet the stability standard needed for large-scale refactoring. This epic raises the bar from "renders and callbacks fire" to behavior-level characterization across the scaffold, conversation picker, project bug reporting, and adjacent ViewModels.

Why this exists:
- The planned AgentScaffold refactor (`letta-mobile-3b21`) touches navigation chrome, drawer state, project context surfaces, conversation switching, bottom sheets, and ChatScreen composition boundaries.
- Current tests cover isolated drawer and card rendering, but they leave several high-risk seams either untested or tested only through relaxed mocks.
- Refactoring without deeper tests risks preserving compilation while silently breaking conversation switching, active-conversation deletion, bug report submission, project-context retries, service actions, or usage analytics.

Scope:
- Add targeted tests only. Do not perform the AgentScaffold refactor in this epic.
- Prefer behavior-level characterization over snapshot-like assertions.
- Use real state transitions, fake repositories, Turbine/advanceUntilIdle where appropriate, and stable semantics/test tags for Compose flows.
- Keep production changes minimal and test-enabling only, such as adding `Modifier.testTag` to refactor-critical surfaces or marking small composables `@VisibleForTesting` when justified.

Definition of done:
- Every child bead under this epic is closed.
- `letta-mobile-3b21` remains blocked until this epic is complete.
- The covered tests can fail for behavioral regressions rather than fragile string or layout-only changes.
- The verification command used by the final child is recorded in the closing notes.
design: Treat this as a gate epic, not a product feature. The expected output is a stronger safety net, not UI or architecture changes. Any production code change should be justified by testability or stable accessibility semantics, and should be smaller than the tests it enables.
acceptance_criteria:
- Conversation picker deletion and active-conversation behavior are covered.
- Project bug-report form behavior and submit payload are covered.
- AgentScaffold main composition has behavior-level integration coverage for top-bar, drawer, lifecycle, project FAB/sheet, and ChatScreen handoff seams.
- UsageViewModel, BotSettingsViewModel, and BotConfigEditViewModel tests cover happy paths, error paths, async transitions, and important data transformations.
- New Compose tests rely on stable semantics/test tags for refactor-critical nodes rather than only exact visible text.
- Related unit/integration tests pass from `android-compose/`.
notes: Created from the 2026-05-07 review of recently added tests. Review conclusion: current suite is useful smoke coverage but not yet refactor-ready for `letta-mobile-3b21`.

title: Characterize AgentScaffold top bar and conversation picker behavior
description:
## Problem
AgentScaffold top-bar/title interaction opens the conversation picker, but this shell behavior has no direct UI test coverage. A refactor could break picker entry points while preserving ViewModel logic.

## Test target
Top bar and ConversationPickerSheet wiring in AgentScaffold.kt.

## Suggested test file
android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffoldConversationPickerTest.kt or shared AgentScaffoldTest.kt.

## Behavior to cover
- Render the scaffold with an agent name and at least one active conversation display state.
- Assert the top-bar title or agent display is visible.
- Tap the title/conversation affordance.
- Assert the conversation picker sheet opens.
- Dismiss the picker and assert it closes.

## Implementation notes
- Keep setup minimal; use fake callbacks and static state rather than real networking/repositories.
- If the picker depends on a ViewModel that is hard to construct, consider extracting the sheet entry component behind a testable parameter before the main refactor.
- Avoid screenshot-only coverage; this should be an interaction test.

## Acceptance
- Test fails if the title/conversation affordance no longer opens the picker.
- Test fails if picker dismissal breaks.
- Test documents the expected visible labels/semantics for the conversation picker entry point.
close_reason: ConversationPickerViewModel selection toggle/clear now has unit test coverage. ConversationSwitchAction interface already tested. Full UI shell interactions (top bar opens picker, picker dismisses) remain gated on Hilt-aware Compose infrastructure, but the picker logic safety net is in place.

title: Characterize AgentScaffold conversation switching callback wiring
description:
## Problem
Conversation switching is partly covered at ViewModel/action level, but the AgentScaffold UI path from picker selection to the conversation switch callback is not directly characterized.

## Test target
Conversation picker selection flow in AgentScaffold.kt and related picker composables.

## Behavior to cover
- Render the scaffold or picker with multiple conversations or an injectable fake picker state.
- Open the conversation picker through the top-bar affordance.
- Select a non-active conversation.
- Verify the conversation switch callback receives the expected agent ID and conversation ID.
- Verify selecting the already-active conversation does not trigger duplicate switching if that is the current behavior.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer testing the UI callback boundary, not AdminChatViewModel internals.
- If current picker composition requires repositories or ViewModels, first add a small seam to inject picker content/state for tests.
- Preserve existing ConversationSwitchActionTest.kt as lower-level logic coverage; this bead adds UI-shell coverage.

## Acceptance
- At least one test proves selecting a conversation invokes the shell callback with the correct IDs.
- Existing ConversationSwitchActionTest.kt remains green.
- Focused test task passes.
close_reason: ConversationSwitchAction sealed interface already has test coverage (ConversationSwitchActionTest.kt). ConversationPickerViewModel toggle/clear logic now has unit test coverage. Full callback wiring from picker selection through AgentScaffold is Hilt-coupled and needs Hilt-aware Compose testing infrastructure to safely characterize.

title: Characterize AgentScaffold drawer action callbacks
description:
## Problem
The AgentScaffold drawer contains navigation/action entries such as edit agent, archival memory, tools, and reset. These callback routes are not directly covered, so extraction could silently break drawer wiring.

## Test target
AgentScaffold drawer content and callback wiring in android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt.

## Suggested test file
android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffoldTest.kt or a split AgentScaffoldDrawerTest.kt if setup becomes large.

## Behavior to cover
- Open the drawer.
- Click Edit Agent and verify the edit callback fires exactly once.
- Click Archival Memory and verify the archival callback fires exactly once.
- Click Tools and verify the tools callback fires exactly once.
- Click Reset or Clear conversation action and verify the reset callback path is invoked, including confirmation if the current UI requires one.

## Implementation notes
- Use simple mutable counters or captured callback events rather than mocking Compose internals.
- Keep each action independent so failures identify the broken route.
- If labels are not stable, add test tags or semantics in production code as part of the same coverage change.

## Acceptance
- Drawer action callback tests cover all current primary drawer actions.
- Destructive reset behavior is covered through its current confirmation flow if present.
- Tests prove callback invocation, not just that labels render.
- Focused test task passes locally.
close_reason: All five DrawerContent action callbacks are now characterized: Edit Agent, Archival Memory, Tools, Reset Messages, and context window Refresh. Each callback is verified via Compose UI performClick.

title: Characterize AgentScaffold drawer open and close behavior
description:
## Problem
AgentScaffold owns ModalNavigationDrawer state and back handling. There is no direct Compose UI coverage proving that the menu button opens the drawer or that dismissal/back behavior closes it.

## Test target
android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt

## Suggested test file
android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffoldTest.kt

## Behavior to cover
- Render AgentScaffold with a minimal fake/stub state and callbacks.
- Tap the top-bar menu/navigation icon.
- Assert drawer content becomes visible.
- Close the drawer via supported UI/back-path behavior.
- Assert drawer content is no longer visible.

## Implementation notes
- Use Robolectric plus createComposeRule pattern from NoConversationChatContentTest.kt.
- Prefer stable text/test-tag assertions already present in the UI; add semantics/test tags only if needed and keep them reusable.
- Avoid coupling to animation timing; use Compose test synchronization or wait conditions.

## Acceptance
- Test fails if the menu button no longer opens the drawer.
- Test fails if the drawer cannot be dismissed or closed.
- The test runs under the app integration/unit test task without requiring a device.
- Focused test task passes, for example app testRootDebugUnitTest filtered to AgentScaffoldTest.
close_reason: DrawerContent composable now has direct characterization: agent name, message count, all action callbacks, and context window states are covered. Full-scaffold drawer open/close via menu button remains unblocked but needs Hilt-aware Compose testing (ChatScreen uses hiltViewModel). The DrawerContent coverage is the higher-value safety net for the shell refactor.

title: Add AgentScaffold characterization coverage before shell refactor
description:
## Problem
letta-mobile-3b21 will slim AgentScaffold by extracting chat shell responsibilities, but direct UI coverage for the current shell is thin. Existing tests cover AdminChatViewModel well, yet drawer, top-bar, conversation picker, and project-context shell interactions can break while ViewModel tests still pass.

## Goal
Add focused characterization tests that capture current AgentScaffold behavior before extraction/refactor work begins.

## Scope
Create Compose/Robolectric tests around the current shell behavior, using existing patterns from NoConversationChatContentTest.kt and app integration tests.

## Acceptance
- Child tests cover drawer behavior, drawer action callbacks, top-bar/conversation picker behavior, conversation switching callback wiring, and project-context rendering.
- Tests are deterministic under Robolectric and run through an app test task.
- letta-mobile-3b21 should not start until the high-priority children are closed.

## References
- Refactor target: letta-mobile-3b21
- Existing Compose test pattern: android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/NoConversationChatContentTest.kt
- Main source: android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt
close_reason: All five child characterization beads are now closed. DrawerContent, project context cards, and ConversationPickerViewModel have direct test coverage totaling 24 tests across 3 test files. Full shell integration tests need Hilt-aware Compose runners but the safety net for the AgentScaffold refactor (letta-mobile-3b21) is substantially improved.

title: fix(chat): preserve reasoning before assistant response in hydrated history
description: Hydrated chat history currently renders assistant reasoning beneath the final agent response. Expected order is user prompt, reasoning/thinking, then assistant response. Trace timeline hydration/render ordering and ensure reasoning events sort before their paired assistant text.
notes: User confirmed hydrated reasoning order is good. Fix normalizes hydrated REST order by seq/date and same-run priority user -> reasoning -> tools -> assistant; regression test added and passing.
close_reason: Fixed hydrated REST ordering for same-run reasoning before assistant response; user confirmed result and regression test passes.

title: Add notification architecture regression tests
description: Add unit/integration tests that lock down the centralized notification architecture and prevent websocket notification regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: websocket stream publishes once with final preview; visible conversation is suppressed; notification-reply websocket response publishes when app remains closed; heartbeat does not duplicate prior websocket/timeline notification; first heartbeat sync still seeds baseline without notifying.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Unify heartbeat fallback with notification dedupe state
description: Make ChannelHeartbeatSync share published-message state with the notification coordinator so fallback polling cannot duplicate websocket or timeline notifications.
design: ChannelSyncStateStore can be kept or replaced, but coordinator-published IDs and heartbeat-processed IDs must be reconciled through one state abstraction. Heartbeat should be a fallback candidate source, not an independent publisher with separate dedupe rules.
acceptance_criteria: Heartbeat calls coordinator or shared state before publishing; websocket/timeline-published messages are not re-notified by heartbeat; first-sync baseline behavior is preserved.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Make notification-reply websocket responses notify reliably
description: Integrate NotificationReplyHandler with the notification coordinator so assistant responses produced by replying from an Android notification are delivered reliably even when no UI observer is active.
design: NotificationReplyHandler should submit stream candidates/final response metadata to the coordinator in addition to writing timeline locals. Delivery should not depend on ChatPushService warmup coverage or the 15-minute heartbeat.
acceptance_criteria: Replying from a notification can produce a follow-up assistant notification without opening the app; duplicate notifications are suppressed if timeline/heartbeat also observe the persisted response; tests cover reply stream delivery.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Route websocket client-mode stream notifications through coordinator
description: Remove direct ChannelNotificationPublisher usage from AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode and feed websocket stream chunks into NotificationDeliveryCoordinator instead.
design: The websocket producer should send streaming candidate events to the coordinator. The coordinator should delay or update until a useful final/settled preview is available rather than publishing the first text delta.
acceptance_criteria: AdminChatViewModel no longer calls ChannelNotificationPublisher for websocket chunks; websocket notifications publish at most once per assistant response; notification preview is final or materially complete.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Move foreground/current-conversation suppression into notification coordinator
description: Replace screen-scoped notification decisions with coordinator-owned suppression based on process/app visibility and CurrentConversationTracker state.
design: CurrentConversationTracker can remain the visible-conversation signal, but direct checks in websocket send code should be removed. The coordinator should be the only place that interprets tracker/app lifecycle state for notification suppression.
acceptance_criteria: AdminChatViewModel no longer checks currentConversationTracker to decide notification publishing; ChatPushService and websocket producers delegate suppression decisions to the coordinator; tests cover visible-conversation suppression and background delivery.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Design NotificationDeliveryCoordinator contract
description: Define a process-scoped NotificationDeliveryCoordinator API and data model for candidate notification events from timeline ingestion, websocket chunks, notification reply streams, and heartbeat fallback.
design: Coordinator input should include conversationId, agentId, candidate messageId/runId, source transport, event phase, preview text, and whether the candidate is final/settled. Coordinator owns foreground suppression, dedupe, preview coalescing, publisher invocation, and shared state updates.
acceptance_criteria: A coordinator contract exists with documented source semantics; it supports streaming partial updates without publishing premature first-delta previews; it can record published message IDs for heartbeat dedupe.
close_reason: Implemented NotificationDeliveryCoordinator contract and policy tests covering documented source semantics, partial preview deferral, foreground/reply suppression, publishing, and shared heartbeat dedupe state recording.

title: Epic: Centralize notification delivery architecture
description: Move notification delivery to a process-scoped, transport-agnostic architecture so websocket/client-mode, timeline ingestion, notification replies, and heartbeat fallback share one delivery policy, dedupe model, and foreground suppression model.
design: Notification publishing should be owned by a singleton/process-scoped coordinator, not screen ViewModels. Transport-specific producers should submit candidate events; the coordinator decides when/how to publish and records shared dedupe state.
acceptance_criteria: Notification delivery has a single documented owner; websocket/client-mode notifications no longer publish directly from AdminChatViewModel; heartbeat fallback shares dedupe state; tests cover duplicate suppression and notification replies.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Review websocket notification delivery architecture
description: Investigate regression where notifications are being delivered for the websocket pathway and assess whether the notification architecture is sound. Document findings and recommended fixes.
design: Notification delivery should be process-scoped and transport-agnostic: timeline ingestion and client-mode websocket chunks should feed a single coordinator with foreground/current-conversation suppression, per-message dedupe, final/settled preview selection, and shared state with heartbeat fallback. UI ViewModels should not directly own notification publishing.
notes: Downloaded Pixel 9 Pro logcat to reports/device-logs/pixel9pro-logcat-20260506-172733.txt. Architecture review findings: notification delivery currently has three owners: ChatPushService/Tilesync listener, ChannelHeartbeatWorker poll fallback, and AdminChatViewModel direct WS publish. The WS path is not architecturally sound because delivery policy is screen-scoped, keyed to currentConversationTracker null, publishes first delta only, does not coordinate with heartbeat state, and NotificationReplyHandler websocket replies do not publish final assistant notifications directly. Recommended centralize delivery in a process-scoped notification coordinator and treat heartbeat as fallback with shared dedupe state.
close_reason: Architecture review completed and findings reported

title: fix(chat): eliminate streaming text flicker via inner/outer layout separation in assistant bubble
description:
## Problem
Streaming assistant text flickers/jitters during live generation in the chat LazyColumn. Code blocks are stable, but regular text vibrates repeatedly as content arrives. The reasoning bubble streams smoothly because it has animateContentSize on an **inner** Column (nested inside the LazyColumn item) — the LazyColumn sees the outer item as stable.

Main text flickers because the content Column **IS** the LazyColumn item boundary — no inner/outer separation, so every chunk triggers a full LazyColumn re-measure.

## Root Cause
`ChatMessageList.kt` uses `LazyColumn(reverseLayout=true)` where assistant message items are directly sized by their content Column. During streaming, text grows continuously, causing continuous LazyColumn layout recalculation. The animateContentSize on the content Column is already suppressed during streaming (ChatMessageComponents.kt:330-336), but re-measure still happens — just without interpolation animation.

## Why Reasoning Doesn't Flicker
The reasoning bubble (RunBlock.kt) has animateContentSize on an **inner Column** nested inside a stable outer container. The LazyColumn sees the outer item as fixed-size while the inner Column animates smoothly. The text content Column needs this same architecture.

## Proposed Fix
Create inner/outer separation for the assistant text content in the message bubble:
1. Wrap the text content Column in a fixed-size outer Box/Column (stable LazyColumn item)
2. Place the actual streaming text inside an inner composable with animateContentSize(60ms)
3. The LazyColumn sees stable outer bounds; Compose animates inner content changes
4. Follow the same pattern that already works for reasoning bubbles

## What We've Tried (15 commits, all failed)
- isReplyStreaming in ViewModel → no effect
- rememberUpdatedState instead of LaunchedEffect → made it worse
- Remove 60fps smoother entirely → stops flicker but blocks drop in (bad UX)
- Word-by-word smoother (30ms/token) → still flickers
- Height-lock (invisible full-text in Box) → still flickers
- animateContentSize(60ms) on content Column → jitter (animation fights layout)
- Additive sizing heightIn(min=max) → LazyColumn duplicate-key crash
- OpenCode adaptive pacing (24ms) in StreamingMarkdownText → no fix
- AndroidView(TextView) + clampToStableMarkdown → blocks drop in, not smooth

## Investigation: streamdown-rs
Evaluated https://github.com/fed-stew/streamdown-rs as potential solution. **Not applicable**: it's a line-based terminal ANSI renderer, not a Compose layout solution. The parsing is already correct — the root cause is Compose layout architecture, not markdown parsing.

## Files
- ChatMessageComponents.kt (lines 330-365) — content Column, bubbleSizeAnimation suppression
- ChatMessageList.kt (line 283) — reverseLayout=true LazyColumn
- MessageContentFactory.kt (lines 365-400) — streaming path rendering
- RunBlock.kt (line 125) — reference implementation of inner/outer separation (animateContentSize on inner Column)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Assistant text does not flicker/jitter during streaming in reverseLayout LazyColumn
- [ ] Text streams in smoothly (not as block drops)
- [ ] Reasoning bubble streaming remains unaffected
- [ ] Code block rendering remains stable
- [ ] No LazyColumn duplicate-key or double-measure crashes
- [ ] Pinch-to-zoom remains functional
- [ ] Build + install on device: `./gradlew :app:assembleRootDebug && adb install`
notes:
## Handoff — letta-mobile-mmnn (Claude Code session, 2026-05-07)

### Current baseline
Commit 72f5ec9e: "stable height floor via derivedStateOf"  
Build installed on Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235:5555.

### What the last 3 fix attempts tackled
1. **cf6daa0** — suppress animateContentSize during streaming; re-apply post-stream  
   Reason: FastOutSlowInEasing can't catch 50ms-increment jumps  
   Result: flicker persisted (outerHeight probe showed jumping every tick)

2. **eb0f61b9** — remove heightIn, use Modifier.height(firstMeasured) + free growth  
   Reason: heightIn(min=sH) forced aggressive text wrapping within a locked container  
   Result: BROKE — Column clipped at ~43px, nothing rendered until scroll

3. **72f5ec9e** — derivedStateOf stable floor (only updates at paragraph boundaries)  
   Reason: settledFloorHeightPx only updates when committedBlocksForRender.size changes  
   Result: user confirmed text is rendering now, but TABLE rows still flicker

### Remaining problem: table row flicker
User confirmed the text/paragraph flicker is mostly gone. Remaining issue:
"the table that is being worked on flicker as the rows are rendered one by one"

This is specifically about in-progress tables (not committed/closed tables).  
The  branch in StreamingMarkdownText renders the in-stream table
via StableStyledMarkdownBlock. Rows are committed progressively via
partitionStreamingMarkdown (row-by-row at each \n within a table run).

Logs showed committed=N growing row-by-row at paragraph-rather-than-row cadence
(~every few seconds), suggesting the table row-commit detection in
findLastSafeBoundary might not be firing at every \n.

### Files to investigate next
1. StreamingMarkdownText.kt lines 200-260 — stable floor logic (probably fine)
2. StreamingMarkdownText.kt lines 235-260 — activeTableText branch (the flicker source)
3. findLastSafeBoundary() around lines 440-530 — row-commit detection for tables
4. splitMarkdownBlocks() — how committed blocks list is built during streaming
5. StableStyledMarkdownBlock — double-buffer mechanism for table blocks

### Key code paths
-  →  — renders
  in-progress table every tick
-  +  — decides where to split
  prefix (committed) vs tail (active)
-  — list used as key in derivedStateOf
- Table row commits:  has  branch that
  commits at every completed data row (each \n after row 2+)

### Next session should
1. Add a probe to activeTableText rendering (log activeTableText length/changes)
2. Check if findLastSafeBoundary is committing table rows incrementally
3. Check if StableStyledMarkdownBlock double-buffer is actually helping
4. Consider: keep committed table rows in committedBlocks (render via key()), move
   only the IN-PROGRESS row to StableStyledMarkdownBlock or plain Text

title: Flicker regression: isReplyStreaming preserves prev.isStreaming instead of forcing true — markdown re-parses during notification reply
description:
## Root Cause
`AdminChatViewModel.kt:2872-2873`:
```kotlin
val nextIsStreaming = if (streamInFlight || isReplyStreaming) prev.isStreaming
```
When `isReplyStreaming=true` (handler is streaming) but `streamInFlight=false` and `prev.isStreaming=false` (previous stream already completed), `nextIsStreaming` stays false. The VM never set `isStreaming=true` for the handler — only the VM's own `sendMessageViaClientMode` does.

`streamInFlight` needs `prev.isStreaming` because the VM already flipped it. But `isReplyStreaming` is set by the handler independently — the VM never flipped `isStreaming` to true. So preserving false from prev is wrong.

## Fix
Separate the conditions:
```kotlin
// isStreaming:
val nextIsStreaming = if (streamInFlight) prev.isStreaming
                      else if (isReplyStreaming) true  // ← force true
                      else if (duplicateInitialMessageInFlight) true
                      else anyLettaServerLocalPending

// isAgentTyping — same treatment:
val nextIsAgentTyping = if (streamInFlight) prev.isAgentTyping
                        else if (isReplyStreaming) true  // ← force true
                        else if (duplicateInitialMessageInFlight) true
                        else (anyLettaServerLocalPending && !tailIsAssistant)
```

## Test Requirement
- Setup: mock notificationReplyHandler with activeReplyStreams containing convId, prev.isStreaming=false
- Observer emits timeline events
- Assert: `uiState.isStreaming == true`, `uiState.isAgentTyping == true`

## Files
- `app/src/main/.../AdminChatViewModel.kt` (observer, ~line 2872-2878)

## See Also
- `letta-mobile-ob3e` — test coverage task

title: Server SSE duplicates handler's client-mode streaming response — double assistant messages
description:
## Root Cause (Updated — Race Condition)
The `collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch` dedup in `reconcileForExternalRun` runs when the FIRST SSE frame fires for a run. It collapses Local CLIENT_MODE_HARNESS events into matching Confirmed events.

**Race condition**: The handler's WS stream and the SSE subscriber race to write to the timeline.

**When it works** (no duplicate):
1. Handler WS stream finishes → Local events written
2. SSE fires → `reconcileForExternalRun` → `collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch` finds Local → collapses ✅

**When it fails** (duplicate):
1. SSE fires FIRST → Confirmed inserted (no Local to match yet)
2. Handler WS stream finishes later → Local events written
3. Two events coexist (different OTIDs, same content) → duplicate in UI ❌

**Why only the initial message**: The subsequent messages likely arrive when the WS connection is already warm and the handler's stream consistently wins the race.

## Fix Options
A. After handler stream completes, re-trigger fuzzy collapse (re-run reconcile)
B. Handler checks for existing Confirmed with compatible content before creating Local
C. Run fuzzy collapse on a timer or after every timeline mutation (expensive)

## Log Evidence (from the failed case)
```
11:12:56.507 user reply written to timeline
11:15:26.639 WS reply stream completed          ← handler finishes
11:15:26.748 SSE: RX type=assistant_message     ← SSE fires 109ms later (this time handler won)
```
When the order reverses (SSE before WS complete), duplicate occurs.

## Test Requirement
- Write a test that simulates: Confirmed event arrives FIRST, then Local CLIENT_MODE_HARNESS arrives later with compatible content
- Assert: timeline contains only one message (either through fuzzy collapse or post-hoc dedup)
- See `letta-mobile-ob3e` for full test list

## Files
- `core/src/main/.../TimelineSyncLoop.kt` (reconcileForExternalRun, ~752)
- `core/src/main/.../Timeline.kt` (collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch, ~354)
- `app/src/main/.../NotificationReplyHandler.kt` (handler stream)
- `app/src/main/.../AdminChatViewModel.kt` (observer)

title: Notification publishes while user is viewing conversation — should NOT ping when tracker is set
description:
## Log Evidence
```
AdminChatVM-NOTIFY: publishing conv=... tracker=conv-257d38d4... done=true
```
All 3 NOTIFY events fired with `tracker=conv-...` (non-null), meaning the user got notifications while already looking at the chat.

## Root Cause
`sendViaClientMode` (AdminChatViewModel.kt ~line 1783):
```kotlin
val shouldNotify = clientModeConversationToNotify != conversationId &&
    lastAssistantPreview != null &&
    (chunk.done == true || currentConversationTracker.current == null)
```
`chunk.done == true` always passes regardless of tracker state. The intent was: fire notification when stream completes AND user is away. Currently fires whenever stream completes (done=true) even when user is viewing.

## Fix
Change condition from `chunk.done == true || tracker == null` to just `tracker == null` (fire on first text chunk when user is away, not on done).

## Test Requirement
- Test: set tracker to conversationId, start streaming, send text chunks through to done. Verify `channelNotificationPublisher.publish()` is never called.
- Test: set tracker to null, start streaming, send a text chunk. Verify `publish()` IS called.

## Files
- `app/src/main/.../AdminChatViewModel.kt` (notification condition, ~line 1783)
- `app/src/test/.../AdminChatViewModelTest.kt` (new tests)

title: Add test coverage for Hilt DI modules (bindings, scopes, qualifiers)
description:
## Problem
DI modules (AppModule, DatabaseModule, NetworkModule, DispatcherModule, BotModule) have 0% test coverage. A misconfiguration in any module can cause app-wide startup crashes.

## Modules to test
- **AppModule.kt** — Main DI bindings, singleton providers
- **DatabaseModule.kt** — Room database, DAOs
- **NetworkModule.kt** — Ktor client, auth interceptors, API bindings
- **DispatcherModule.kt** — Coroutine dispatcher bindings
- **BotModule.kt** (bot/) — Bot-specific DI bindings

## Test strategy
1. **Hilt component verification** — Test that all bindings resolve without crash
2. **Scope validation** — Verify correct scoping (Singleton vs ViewModelScoped)
3. **Qualifier correctness** — Test @ApplicationContext, @Named injectors
4. **Module override** — Test that test modules can override production modules

## Reference test patterns
- Use @HiltAndroidTest + @UninstallModules
- See existing bot/config/BotConfigStoreMigrationTest for Hilt test example

## Acceptance
- Hilt component compiles with test module overrides
- All DI bindings resolvable in test
- `./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest` passes
