title: Migrate 24 screens from raw AlertDialog to ConfirmDialog/ActionSheet/TextInputDialog
description:
## Background
The component migration policy is defined (letta-mobile-ns9n closed) and the reusable wrappers exist (ConfirmDialog, ActionSheet, TextInputDialog). But 24 screens still use raw `AlertDialog`.

## Screens with raw AlertDialog (migration targets)

### Admin Screens (highest usage, highest impact)
| Screen | Raw AlertDialogs | Migrate to |
|--------|-----------------|------------|
| GroupAdminScreen | 5 | ConfirmDialog + TextInputDialog |
| ProviderAdminScreen | 5 | ConfirmDialog + TextInputDialog |
| ArchiveAdminScreen | 5 | ConfirmDialog + TextInputDialog |
| McpServerToolsScreen | 4 | ConfirmDialog |
| JobMonitorScreen | 3 | ConfirmDialog |
| IdentityListScreen | 3 | ConfirmDialog + ActionSheet |

### List/Detail Screens
| Screen | Raw AlertDialogs | Migrate to |
|--------|-----------------|------------|
| AgentListScreen | 3 | ConfirmDialog |
| ConversationsScreen | 3 | ConfirmDialog |
| RunMonitorScreen | 2 | ConfirmDialog + ActionSheet |
| MessageBatchMonitorScreen | 2 | ConfirmDialog |
| McpScreen | 2 | ConfirmDialog |
| ScheduleListScreen | 2 | ConfirmDialog |
| AgentSettingsScreen | 2 | ConfirmDialog |
| ArchivalScreen | 1 | ConfirmDialog |

### Picker/Create Dialogs
| Screen | Raw AlertDialogs | Migrate to |
|--------|-----------------|------------|
| ToolPickerDialog | 1 | ActionSheet or redesign |
| CreateToolDialog | 1 | Redesign with FormItem |
| BlockPickerDialog | 1 | ActionSheet or redesign |
| ToolsScreen | 1 | ConfirmDialog |
| ModelBrowserScreen | 1 | ConfirmDialog |
| BotConfigEditScreen | 1 | ConfirmDialog |
| EditAgentScreen | 1 | ConfirmDialog |

## Migration rules
1. **Destructive actions** (delete, reset, disconnect) → ConfirmDialog
2. **Contextual menus** (edit, rename, copy) → ActionSheet
3. **Single-field input** (rename, create) → TextInputDialog
4. **Error/info dialogs** with acknowledge button → ConfirmDialog
5. **Multi-field forms** → Keep as custom dialog but use FormItem layout

## Reference implementations
- ConfirmDialog pattern: ConfigListScreen, AboutScreen
- ActionSheet pattern: AgentListScreen, ProjectHomeScreen, ConversationsScreen
- TextInputDialog pattern: ArchivalScreen, GroupAdminScreen, ConversationsScreen

## Acceptance
- 0 raw `androidx.compose.material3.AlertDialog` imports in screens listed above
- Each migration preserves existing functionality
- `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin` passes
close_reason: Migrated 24+ screens from raw AlertDialog → ConfirmDialog/ActionSheet/TextInputDialog/MultiFieldInputDialog. 0 AlertDialog imports remain across all screen files. Build passes.

title: Replace !! non-null assertions with safe alternatives
description:
## Problem
Non-null assertions (!!) in production code cause NPE crashes when assumptions are violated. 12 ViewModels use `savedStateHandle.get<String>("...")!!` pattern.

### Production !! instances (high risk):
- AdminChatViewModel.kt:350 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("agentId")!!`
- EditAgentViewModel.kt:124 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("agentId")!!`
- AgentSettingsViewModel.kt:68 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("agentId")!!`
- ArchivalViewModel.kt:35 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("agentId")!!`
- ToolsViewModel.kt:31 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("agentId")!!`
- ToolDetailViewModel.kt:36 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("toolId")!!`
- McpServerToolsViewModel.kt:50 — `savedStateHandle.get<String>("serverId")!!`
- BotHeartbeatSync.kt:142,160 — `heartbeatAgentId!!`
- MathBlock.kt:68 — `renderError!!`
- MermaidDiagram.kt:127 — `renderError!!`
- RemoteBotSession.kt:85 — `client!!.getStatus()`
- BotSettingsScreen.kt:133 — `config.heartbeatAgentId!!.take(8)`

## Fix
- ViewModels: replace !! with `?: run { _state.value = UiState.Error(IllegalStateException(...)); return@launch }`
- BotHeartbeatSync: add null-check with early return and Telemetry.warn
- MathBlock/MermaidDiagram: `renderError?.let { }` or provide default error message
- RemoteBotSession: `requireNotNull(client)` or lateinit + check

## Acceptance
- 0 !! assertions in production source (not tests)
- `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin` passes
- `./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest` passes

title: Fix empty catch blocks swallowing errors in production code
description:

title: [epic] Code quality cleanup: empty catches, unsafe casts, debug logging, large files
description:
Umbrella epic for code quality improvements identified in the 2026-05-05 Sisyphus project audit.

## Audit findings (541 Kotlin files, ~97K LOC)

### Empty catch blocks (CRITICAL - 5 instances)

title: Notification not dismissed after reply
description:
Bug 2: When replying from notification, the original notification is not dismissed.

## Analysis (2026-05-05)
NotificationReplyReceiver.cancel(notificationId) is called after sendReply() launches async. Cancel should work — uses correct notificationId from intent extras.

Fix applied: removed NotificationReplyHandler's notification publish block (was lines 101-111) to prevent re-publishing. AdminChatViewModel notification path is in sendMessageViaClientMode only (line 1790-1803), not in observer — so no new notification should appear from reply flow.

User confirms: notification still not dismissed after fix.

## Files
- channel/NotificationReplyReceiver.kt (cancel at line 38)
- channel/ChannelNotificationPublisher.kt (publish at lines 50-111)
- ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt (notify at lines 1790-1803)
close_reason: Dismiss replies robustly by cancelling tagged and legacy notification IDs before dispatching reply, publishing conversation notifications with tags, and suppressing ChatPushService notifications while notification-reply streams are active.

title: Notification reply thinking indicator not shown
description:
Bug 3: When replying from notification, the thinking indicator (REASONING bubble) does not appear when user returns to chat.

## Analysis (2026-05-05)
Handler writes REASONING Locals via upsertClientModeLocalAssistantChunk with TimelineMessageType.REASONING + reasoningContent directly into timeline. This mirrors AdminChatViewModel's handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline exactly.

Pipeline verified:
- hydrate uses writeMutex (TimelineSyncLoop:174), serializing with handler writes
- TimelineEventToUiMessage handles REASONING correctly (lines 169-180, isReasoning=true)
- filterMessagesForMode drops REASONING in Simple mode only (ChatRenderModelBuilder:162-163)
- Default is Interactive mode which includes REASONING

Possible root cause: observer may not be running when handler writes (user pressed back). When user returns, observer starts and should pick up cached Local events from StateFlow.

## Files
- channel/NotificationReplyHandler.kt (REASONING write at lines 46-71)
- ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt (observer at lines 2705-2897)
- ui/screens/chat/TimelineEventToUiMessage.kt (mapping at lines 169-180)
- ui/screens/chat/ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt (filters at lines 39-53)
- core/.../timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt (hydrate at lines 174-269)
close_reason: Preserve notification-reply reasoning bubbles by giving server REASONING messages unique UI IDs when they share server IDs with assistant messages, and align handler local chunk timestamps with the normal Client Mode path.

title: Add render model dedup and message count comparison probe
description:
## Problem

The render model pipeline in ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt has two dedup stages:
1. dedupeReasoningAssistantEchoes (line 92) — removes assistant messages that exactly duplicate preceding reasoning
2. dedupeGroupedMessagesForLazyKeys (line 122) — HashSet dedup by message ID before LazyColumn

After these, the final ChatRenderItem list goes to the LazyColumn. If any stage silently drops or duplicates messages, the counts won't match.

The handoff identifies TimelineEventToUiMessage.kt as suspect #1: does the 1:1 mapping from timeline events hold up at scale? With client-mode harness suffix generation (line 292-296), duplicate serverIds could produce distinct render IDs that bypass dedup.

## What

Add a one-shot comparison probe in buildChatRenderModel:

```kotlin
fun buildChatRenderModel(
    messages: List<UiMessage>,
    mode: ChatDisplayMode,
): ChatRenderModel {
    val inputCount = messages.size
    val afterDedup = dedupeReasoningAssistantEchoes(messages)
    val visibleMessages = attachLatencyMetadata(filterMessagesForMode(...))
    val groupedMessages = groupMessages(...)
    val afterKeyDedup = dedupeGroupedMessagesForLazyKeys(groupedMessages)
    val reversed = afterKeyDedup.asReversed()
    val renderItems = groupMessagesForRender(reversed)
    
    if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
        val renderItemMessageCount = renderItems.sumOf { 
            when (it) {
                is ChatRenderItem.Single -> 1
                is ChatRenderItem.RunBlock -> it.messages.size
            }
        }
        Telemetry.event(ChatRenderModel, pipeline,
            inputCount to inputCount,
            afterReasoningDedup to afterDedup.size,
            visibleCount to visibleMessages.size,
            groupedCount to groupedMessages.size,
            afterKeyDedup to afterKeyDedup.size,
            renderItemsCount to renderItems.size,
            renderItemMessageCount to renderItemMessageCount,
            assistantCount to messages.count { it.role == assistant && !it.isReasoning },
        )
    }
    
    return ChatRenderModel(...)
}
```

This gives a single-line log showing each pipeline stage's message count, so we can immediately see where the count diverges from input.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Single log line per recomposition shows counts at every pipeline stage
- [ ] renderItemMessageCount == visibleCount (no message lost or duplicated by grouping)
- [ ] assistantCount matches the expected number from timeline liveCount
- [ ] No divergence between stages observed during multi-tool-call streams
- [ ] All code gated behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG`

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt` (lines ~39-61)

title: Add timeline observer message dedup telemetry
description:
## Problem

The timeline observer in startTimelineObserver (AdminChatViewModel.kt:2727-2741) combines olderMessagesPrefix with live timeline events, deduplicating by message ID. When a Local event is updated in-place (same ID, new content streaming in), olderMessagesPrefix could carry a stale version of the same message. Since dedup is by ID (not content), the stale version from prefix could be added while the live version is skipped.

During multi-tool-call streams, more emissions occur, increasing the window where stale prefix entries can cause issues.

## What

Add DEBUG-gated logging at the timeline observer collection point:

```kotlin
val (prefixConv, prefixList) = olderMessagesPrefix
val prefix = if (prefixConv == conversationId) prefixList else emptyList()
val seenIds = HashSet<String>(live.size + prefix.size)
val combined = ArrayList<UiMessage>(live.size + prefix.size)

if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
    val prefixIds = prefix.map { it.id }.toSet()
    val liveIds = live.map { it.id }.toSet()
    val overlap = prefixIds intersect liveIds
    if (overlap.isNotEmpty()) {
        Telemetry.event(TimelineObs, prefixLiveOverlap,
            overlapCount to overlap.size,
            prefixCount to prefix.size,
            liveCount to live.size,
            staleAssistantCount to overlap.count { id ->
                val prefixMsg = prefix.first { it.id == id }
                val liveMsg = live.first { it.id == id }
                prefixMsg.role == assistant && prefixMsg.content != liveMsg.content
            },
        )
    }
}

for (m in prefix) if (seenIds.add(m.id)) combined.add(m)
for (m in live) if (seenIds.add(m.id)) combined.add(m)
```

Also log per-emission: emission index within the stream, combined size, prefix size, live size, dedup count.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Logs fire on every timeline observer emission during streaming
- [ ] Overlap between prefix and live is captured when IDs collide
- [ ] Stale content detection: when same ID has different content in prefix vs live
- [ ] Emission index reset on new stream, increments monotonically within a stream
- [ ] All code gated behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG`

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt` (lines ~2727-2741)

title: Add StreamingMarkdownText partition boundary telemetry
description:
## Problem

StreamingMarkdownText.kt splits live markdown at safe block boundaries into committedBlocks (rendered via MarkdownText with stable keys) and activeTail (plain Text). The KDoc at lines 30-38 documents a known historical regression where content briefly appeared to duplicate as the tail moved into a new prefix block.

With multiple tool calls, the streaming cadence is irregular (chunks interleaved with tool events), which could destabilize the boundary calculation and cause committedBlocks to double-include content.

## What

Add a DEBUG-gated telemetry probe at line 156 of StreamingMarkdownText.kt:
```kotlin
val partition = remember(displayed) { 
    val result = partitionStreamingMarkdown(displayed)
    if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
        Telemetry.event("StreamingMd", "partition",
            "committedBlockCount" to result.committedBlocks.size,
            "activeTailLen" to result.activeTail.length,
            "textLen" to displayed.length,
            "committedTotalLen" to result.committedBlocks.sumOf { it.text.length },
        )
    }
    result
}
```

Also add a validation that committedTotal + tailLen == textLen. Log a WARN if mismatch detected.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Partition telemetry fires on every paint tick (50ms cadence) during streaming
- [ ] committedTotalLen + activeTailLen == textLen on every tick (no boundary over-count)
- [ ] committedBlockCount changes monotonically (never decreases)
- [ ] Logs include a per-block hash/prefix so duplicates are identifiable across ticks
- [ ] All code gated behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG`

## Files
- `designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/StreamingMarkdownText.kt` (line ~156)

description:

title: fix: Client Mode multi-tool-call duplication — fuzzy collapse absorbs only one Local
description:
## Bug

Tool call cards and/or assistant text appears duplicated in the chat UI when the agent makes multiple tool calls. Single tool calls render correctly.

## Actual Symptom (updated from logs)

User reports: during the final assistant response rendering, at each markdown breakpoint (code block close, etc.), the ENTIRE output duplicates. One extra copy per tool call made.

## Log Evidence (run at 15:50:35–15:51:56, Pixel 9 Pro)

- Timeline liveCount stays **flat at 102** during all ASSISTANT streaming (14 TOOL_CALL chunks → 93→101, then ASSISTANT chunks → 101→102, STABLE)
- This proves NO data-layer duplication — only ONE assistant event exists in the timeline
- SSE stream returned 400 for this run — data came purely through Client Mode WS
- 14 TOOL_CALL chunks across ~6 agent steps, each creating separate Locals
- ASSISTANT chunks: textLen=349, 303, 331, 252 (large ~300-char coalesced deltas)

## Root Cause: Rendering/Projection Layer, NOT Timeline

The duplication is in how timeline events are converted to UiMessages and rendered. Candidates:
1. `TimelineEventToUiMessage.kt` — converting events to UI model
2. `MessageMapper.kt` — tool call batching/pairing logic
3. `ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt` — dedup/reasoning-echo filtering
4. Markdown renderer recomposition — content replacement at block boundaries

## Key Files

- `app/.../chat/TimelineEventToUiMessage.kt` — event → UiMessage projection
- `core/.../mapper/MessageMapper.kt` — tool call grouping, return attachment
- `app/.../chat/ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt` — dedupe logic
- `app/.../chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt` — markdown rendering
- `app/.../chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt` lines 2157-2228 (WS tool-call handler)
- `core/.../timeline/Timeline.kt` lines 354-410 (fuzzy collapse — secondary issue)

## Original (possibly still valid) Fuzzy Collapse Issue

`collapseClientModeFuzzyMatch()` picks ONE Local via maxByOrNull. When WS creates N Locals (one per tool call) and SSE later delivers ONE batched Confirmed, N-1 Locals are orphaned. This is a REAL bug but may not be the PRIMARY duplication cause since SSE was offline for this run.

## Next Steps

1. Instrument `TimelineEventToUiMessage` and `MessageMapper` to log the UiMessage list on each observer emit
2. Compare UiMessage count with timeline event count — if UiMessages > events, the projection is multiplying
3. Check if multiple REASONING Locals from different steps are being projected as separate assistant messages
4. Check if the markdown renderer handles content replacement (not appending) correctly
5. Reproduce with SSE stream active to test fuzzy collapse theory separately
design: Scope multi-match to TOOL_CALL only. USER/ASSISTANT/REASONING retain maxByOrNull single-match. Position Confirmed at earliest matched Local position to prevent visual jumps.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Multi-tool-call: all matching Locals absorbed by single Confirmed, no orphaned bubbles
2. Single-tool-call: existing 1:1 collapse preserved
3. USER messages: existing single-match (most-recent) behavior preserved
4. FuzzyCollapseTrace includes matchCount for telemetry
5. Existing tests pass, new test for multi-tool-case added
notes: Device: Pixel 9 Pro at 192.168.50.235:5555. Logcat cleared and ready for reproduction. SseParser tag logs every received frame. Working tree has unrelated changes (mermaid, chat push).
close_reason: Root cause: animateContentSize() height interpolations in RunBlock and MessageBubble during streaming created visual echoes that looked like text duplication. Data pipeline was always clean (confirmed by ChatRenderModel, TimelineObs, StreamingMd, StreamingDisplay probes). Suppressing animateContentSize during streaming (letta-mobile-lbur) eliminated the visual artifact. No actual message duplication existed at any layer.

title: Client Mode: missing final assistant text after run survives screen-off
description:
Follow-up to letta-mobile-etc1.

After the etc1 fix the WS gateway stays alive across screen-off and Client Mode runs continue (verified by ClientModeController.appBackgrounded gatewayKeptAlive=true). On return from sleep, the user reports two remaining issues:

1. Duplication of in-flight content in the chat \u2014 partially addressed in commit 972db625 (handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline ASSISTANT branch ignored its computed merged value and unconditionally appended delta). Re-test required after this fix to confirm whether duplication is fully resolved.
2. The run kept executing more tool calls after returning to the chat, but no final ASSISTANT text bubble was rendered for the run. Tool calls completed successfully; only the terminal assistant message did not appear.

Suspects for #2:
- WS gateway terminal frame (done=true) arrived while the chat ViewModel was paused/destroyed and was dropped, leaving the bubble open without a final write.
- Final ASSISTANT delta vs done=true frame race: the assistant bubble Local was created via upsertClientModeLocalAssistantChunk but never reconciled against the eventual SSE-confirmed echo from Letta, and the Local was somehow cleared on resume.
- Server-side: letta marked the run done but the gateway never forwarded the final ASSISTANT delta because the per-agent WS session reconnected mid-stream.

Investigation steps:
- Repeat the test on Pixel 9 Pro with the fresh build (commit 972db625) and a 32 MiB logcat buffer.
- Filter for: AdminChatVM-DEBUG, Telemetry/AdminChatVM, Telemetry/TimelineSync, Telemetry/WsBotClient, Telemetry/RemoteBotSession, Telemetry/ClientModeController.
- Look for: streamSubscriber.eventReceived for ASSISTANT after wake, sendViaClientMode chunk received done=true with sawPayload accounting, any silenceTimeout / watchdogReconnect, and whether the WS session (rid=...) is the same before and after wake.
- Check whether the legacy duplication is gone (re-test #1 closes when no doubled bubble).

Acceptance:
- The terminal ASSISTANT message renders in the chat after a run completes across a screen-off cycle.
- No duplicated bubbles after returning to the chat.
- Add regression test or telemetry to detect the missing-terminal-frame condition.
close_reason: Fixed and field-confirmed after retest. Review found the first duplication patch over-applied prefix collapse to all ASSISTANT deltas; commit 4b1cd13a constrains collapse to text-bearing done=true terminal snapshot frames, preserving pure-delta byte-perfect streaming. Added AdminChatViewModel regression test for terminal snapshot dedup and re-ran AdminChatViewModelTest + ClientModeControllerTest successfully. Built and installed rootDebug on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Share-to-agent still sends shared message twice
description:
User reports Android share-to-agent still sends a shared link/message twice after the AtomicBoolean initialMessage guard fix. Need deeper investigation of duplicate route creation, duplicate ACTION_SEND delivery, duplicate ViewModel instances, or send path duplication.

Context:
- Share flow was added in commit 5dac540c (Add Android share-to-agent flow).
- Behavior changed to send to most recent conversation in commit 21bc5637 by removing freshRouteKey from ShareToAgentRoute -> AgentChatRoute navigation.
- Attempted fix in commit 401b405a changed AdminChatViewModel.initialMessageConsumed to AtomicBoolean.compareAndSet, but user confirmed duplication persists on device.

Likely areas to inspect:
1. MainActivity/AppLaunchTarget/onNewIntent may parse the same ACTION_SEND more than once.
2. AppNavGraph LaunchedEffect(notificationTarget) may navigate an initial share target plus a subsequent same target. ShareLaunchTarget has requestId but equality likely still allows unexpected navigation/route creation behavior.
3. AgentListScreen share mode may call onNavigateToAgent more than once due to double tap or recomposition? Add click debouncing/consumed state if needed.
4. NavHost typed route may create multiple AdminChatViewModel instances for same initialMessage route. Check AgentChatRoute.toViewModelKey excludes initialMessage, so existing VM reuse may interact with new route delivery.
5. sendMessageViaTimeline/clientMode could be invoked twice via resolveConversationAndLoad collector races despite AtomicBoolean if there are two VM instances. Instrument VM instance id + initialMessage hash + send path.

Suggested debug plan:
- Add temporary Log.d markers in ShareLaunchTarget.fromIntent, MainActivity.onCreate/onNewIntent, AppNavGraph share route navigation, AgentListScreen agent click, AdminChatViewModel init/consumeInitialMessageIfPresent/sendMessageViaTimeline/sendMessageViaClientMode. Include route hash, VM identityHashCode, agentId, conversationId, and message hash.
- Reproduce by sharing a link from Chrome/another app on Pixel 9 Pro.
- Use adb logcat filtered for these markers and confirm whether duplicate originates before navigation, in navigation, from multiple VMs, or inside send.

Acceptance criteria:
- Sharing a link to an agent sends exactly one user message to the most recent conversation.
- Add a regression test that simulates the actual duplicate trigger once identified.
- Build rootDebug and install on Pixel 9 Pro after fix.
notes: HANDOFF: The previous fix (401b405a AtomicBoolean) did not solve duplicate sends according to user. Treat as unresolved. Start by adding targeted instrumentation to determine whether duplication is from duplicate ACTION_SEND intents, duplicate share-route navigation, duplicate agent click, multiple AdminChatViewModel instances, or duplicate send path. Pixel 9 Pro is at 192.168.50.235:5555. Last installed build was rootDebug version 1.2.6-root at 2026-05-02 19:12:50. Previous related closed issues: letta-mobile-t2go, letta-mobile-dmfv, letta-mobile-we59.

Added AdminChatViewModel regression test for duplicate share route ViewModels. Verified :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests duplicate-share-route test, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests AdminChatViewModelTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and installed app-root-debug.apk successfully on 192.168.50.235:5555.

title: Make chat auto-scroll follow streaming content only near bottom
description:
Why this exists: Google Gallery keeps the chat pinned to the newest streaming response while the user is already near the bottom, but does not yank the viewport when the user scrolls away. Letta Mobile currently gates auto-scroll primarily on message count, so an existing assistant bubble can grow during streaming without the viewport following.

What to do: Update android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageList.kt to observe the newest rendered message identity/content growth (or equivalent streaming marker) in addition to message count. Keep the existing reverseLayout semantics and only animateScrollToItem(0) when the user is near the bottom and scrollToMessageId is null. Consider IME bottom changes as a separate, guarded scroll trigger if it improves composer visibility.

Reference: /tmp/google-gallery/Android/src/app/src/main/java/com/google/ai/edge/gallery/ui/common/chat/ChatPanel.kt streaming LaunchedEffect keyed by lastMessage, content, latency.
acceptance_criteria: During a live streaming assistant response, the list follows content growth when already near the bottom. If the user scrolls away, streaming updates do not auto-scroll. Search-result scrollToMessageId behavior is preserved. Older-message pagination is not triggered incorrectly. Tests or focused manual verification cover reverseLayout behavior.

title: Design Android host tool approval and audit engine
description: Build the reusable approval/audit layer needed before exposing high-risk Android host tools. Context: ifd7.1 mapped Letta Code's permission checker/analyzer/headless approval loop to Letta Mobile. Current LocalBotSession executes supported tools immediately; future SAF writes, contacts writes, notification forwarding, accessibility, local shell, Shizuku/Sui, and root tools need app-side approval decisions before submitToolResult(). Scope: define approval request model, UI/notification/deferred transport behavior, remember-per-session/per-scope rules, capability registry integration, structured denied/unavailable tool results, and audit event schema with redaction. This should not block the safe user-mediated intent batch unless a direct-state action cannot be made safe without it.
notes: Implemented foundation: HostToolApprovalRequest/Decision/Risk/Policy/Audit models, DefaultHostToolApprovalEngine with ask/remember policies, structured denied payloads, host-tool audit redaction/truncation, BotToolRegistry approval gate, LocalBotSession denied result submission, and SystemAccessCapability -> HostToolApprovalMetadata mapper. Existing exposed tools remain policy=None; no root/local shell tools were exposed.

title: Implement Letta Code-inspired Android OS action host tools
description:
Implement the first batch of Letta Code-inspired Android-native host tools in the existing LocalBotSession tool path.

Use Google AI Edge Gallery's Mobile Actions pattern and Letta Code's host-tool pattern:
- Tool call is model/Letta-facing.
- AndroidExecutionBridge executes typed Android actions locally.
- Prefer Android user-mediated intents/APIs for first version rather than raw privileged operations.

Candidate tools:
- android_open_wifi_settings
- android_show_location_on_map(location)
- android_send_email_draft(to, subject, body)
- android_send_sms_draft(phone_number, body)
- android_create_calendar_event_draft(title, datetime)
- android_create_contact_draft(first_name, last_name, phone_number?, email?)

Implementation areas:
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/tools/AndroidExecutionBridge.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/tools/BotToolRegistry.kt
- SystemAccessCapabilityRegistry integration where a capability/tool id already exists or should be added.

Safety model:
- Intents that open Android draft/insert/settings UI are acceptable as first pass.
- Mutations that bypass Android confirmation should require explicit app-side approval/audit before implementation.
- Root/shell/filesystem tools remain separate issues and must not be bundled into this safe intent batch.
acceptance_criteria: Tools are registered/synced through BotToolRegistry; AndroidExecutionBridge has typed handlers; missing permissions/capabilities return structured unavailable results; user-mediated intents launch with FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK where needed; unit tests cover argument parsing and success/unavailable paths.
notes: Input from ifd7.1 design note: implement this as the first safe user-mediated Android OS action batch. Include model-facing parameter schemas in local tool definitions if current Letta tool sync supports them or create a small extension/follow-up if not. Keep draft/settings/insert intents user-mediated; gate/audit direct device state such as flashlight through a capability/tool id or file a follow-up if the registry lacks one. Do not include root/shell/filesystem tools in this batch.
close_reason: Implemented Android OS host tools through BotToolRegistry and AndroidExecutionBridge; added JSON schemas, argument parsing, structured results, tests; verified :bot:testDebugUnitTest and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Spike Letta Code-style Android host tool architecture
description:
Spike the Letta Code host-tool architecture as the blueprint for Android-local device/system access in Letta Mobile.

Context:
- User clarified the reference is https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code.git, not lettabot.
- Letta Code's relevant source lives under src/tools/, src/permissions/, and src/headless.ts.
- Letta Code proves the desired split: the Letta agent may be server-side, but filesystem/shell/device tools execute on the local host.
- For Android, the local host should be the app process via LocalBotSession/BotToolRegistry/AndroidExecutionBridge rather than bundling/running the whole Bun/Node Letta Code runtime inside the APK.

Questions to answer:
1. Which Letta Code abstractions should be mirrored directly in Kotlin: schema, markdown description, implementation, permission policy, toolset alias mapping, approval modes.
2. Which parts should not be ported directly: Node process lifecycle, child_process shell execution, desktop filesystem assumptions, TUI/headless CLI internals.
3. Whether a future Termux/root Letta Code sidecar is worthwhile, and where it would sit relative to Android-native tools.
4. How to expose Android-native OS actions as Letta Code-style host tools while preserving Android permission and approval boundaries.
5. How this aligns with existing LocalBotSession tool sync/result continuation and SystemAccessCapabilityRegistry.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a short design note or bead notes mapping Letta Code source concepts to Letta Mobile Kotlin classes; document native-vs-sidecar recommendation; identify first tool families to implement; list risks/approval tiers; create or update follow-up implementation beads.
notes: Design note created at docs/architecture/letta-code-android-host-tools.md. It maps Letta Code schema/description/implementation/toolset/alias/permission/approval concepts to BotToolRegistry, AndroidExecutionBridge, LocalBotSession, and SystemAccessCapabilityRegistry; recommends native Kotlin host tools over bundling Bun/Node in the APK; identifies safe Android intents, storage/file tools, and shell/privileged tool families; and defines approval/risk tiers.
close_reason: Completed design spike in docs/architecture/letta-code-android-host-tools.md; updated follow-up beads ifd7.2/ifd7.3 and created related approval/audit engine follow-up letta-mobile-1g4k.

title: Fix duplicate initial user bubble after client-mode bootstrap

Need reconcile/suppress duplicate bootstrap user bubble when createConversation/gateway/server has already persisted the initial message.
notes: Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235 logs 2026-05-02 14:54:05: clientMode.bootstrapFreshConversation conversationId=conv-7b579fb7..., streamMessage conv=conv-7b579fb7 (vynx fix active), hydrate rawCount=1 eventCount=1 before send.clientModeLocalAppended contentLength=3. This points to duplicate local append against an already-present server/bootstrap user message, not old-history leakage.
close_reason: Fixed fresh Client Mode bootstrap duplicate by detecting an already-hydrated matching bootstrap user message before appending the optimistic local bubble; added AdminChatViewModel regression coverage.

title: Fix client-mode new conversation bootstrap isolation

Pixel 9 Pro logs at 14:51 showed old pre-vynx code running: sendViaClientMode logged forceFresh=true and streamMessage convId=null; no clientMode.bootstrapFreshConversation/createConversation log. Installed current playDebug build on 192.168.50.235:5555 and cleared logs for retest.

title: Investigate persistent client-mode first-message duplicate on device
description: User reports duplicate/echo still occurs on the new message after prior initial-route fix. Reproduce on Pixel 9 via adb, inspect app/logcat/client-mode timeline behavior, and identify remaining cause for duplicate prompt/response echo or missing optimistic state.
notes: ADB Pixel 9 Pro logs showed remaining duplicate was not initial-message routing. After first client-mode send, TimelineSync streamSubscriber merged a reasoning_message and assistant_message with the same serverId (message-dbd32e5e...) into one Confirmed event: existingType=ASSISTANT oldLen=214 newLen=200 mergedLen=414, then existingType=REASONING oldLen=414 newLen=214 mergedLen=628. Root cause: Timeline.findByServerId/replaceByServerId and fallback server otid keyed only by server id, but Letta can reuse id across reasoning and assistant frames for a step. Fix scopes server-id merge/replace/dedupe by TimelineMessageType and includes message type in fallback server otid.

title: Persistent streams: add screen-off delivery diagnostics
description:
Why:
Persistent stream failures are hard to distinguish: service killed, foreground promotion failed, no heartbeats, stale socket, reconnect backoff, Doze delay, or battery optimization. We need structured diagnostics before and during rollout.

Scope:
- Add telemetry/logs for ChatPushService lifecycle, active stream count, warmed conversations, foreground promotion status, and last stream liveness.
- Add stream state fields: opened at, last event, last heartbeat, reconnect count, last reconnect reason, current backoff.
- Provide a debug/log dump path if an existing diagnostics screen or logger is available.
- Ensure metadata is safe and not overly noisy.
design: This issue can be implemented before or alongside watchdog/heartbeat work. Avoid logging message content; conversation ids and timing metadata are sufficient.
acceptance_criteria:
- Service lifecycle logs show created/foregrounded/destroyed/warmup outcomes.
- Stream telemetry can answer whether each persistent stream is open and recently alive.
- Reconnect reason and last liveness timestamps are available in logs or debug diagnostics.
- Telemetry is rate-limited or bounded to avoid noisy logs during healthy heartbeats.
- Documentation notes how to debug screen-off delivery using these events.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/channel/ChatPushService.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineRepository.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/telemetry/TelemetryScreen.kt` if exposing/debugging via existing telemetry UI
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/util/Telemetry.kt` or actual Telemetry path if needed

Existing telemetry:
- ChatPushService: `created`, `destroyed`, `startForeground.failed`, `suppressedForegroundConv`, `warmup.complete`, `warmup.failed`.
- TimelineSync: `streamSubscriber.opened`, `eventReceived`, `closed`, `idle404`, `networkError`, plus ingest/dedupe events.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add bounded, low-noise diagnostics, not a heartbeat log storm.
2. In `ChatPushService` add/ensure telemetry attrs:
   - `created`: `foregrounded`
   - `onStartCommand`: event `startCommand` with `startId` if useful
   - `warmup.start`: `budget`
   - `warmup.complete`: `conversationCount`, `budget`, maybe `durationMs`
   - per-conversation warmup failures can stay Log.w or aggregate into count to avoid noise.
3. In `TimelineSyncLoop` add stream state telemetry:
   - `streamSubscriber.opened`: include `conversationId`
   - `streamSubscriber.closed`: include `durationMs`, `eventsReceived`
   - `streamSubscriber.heartbeat`: only if sampled/rate-limited, include `conversationId` and maybe `sinceOpenMs`, not every 30s forever in production logs unless Telemetry ring can handle it.
   - `streamSubscriber.silenceTimeout` and `watchdogReconnect` when `jmzq.2` is implemented.
4. Consider an internal data class for stream diagnostics if needed, e.g. last opened, last event, last heartbeat, reconnect count/reason. Expose only if there is an existing debug surface; do not build a new complex UI unless scoped.
5. If using `TelemetryScreen`, confirm it can filter by tag `TimelineSync` and `ChatPushService` already.

Acceptance-specific verification:
- Run app/service and check TelemetryScreen/logcat for service start and warmup events.
- Trigger/open a stream and verify open/close/event metadata.
- Verify no message content, API tokens, or raw server URLs are logged.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin`
- If tests added: `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest :app:testDebugUnitTest`

Do not:
- Do not log raw message content or authorization tokens.
- Do not emit unbounded heartbeat telemetry every 30s per stream unless sampled/rate-limited.
- Do not rename existing telemetry events used by docs unless you update docs/tests.

title: Persistent streams: add silence watchdog and stale-socket reconnect
description:
Why:
Even with process lifetime preserved, idle streams can become stale without producing EOF/RST. The client needs an explicit silence watchdog that closes and reconnects stale streams rather than waiting indefinitely or relying only on generic socket timeouts.

Scope:
- Track last liveness frame time in TimelineSyncLoop.runStreamSubscriber().
- Treat message events and heartbeat/comment frames as liveness.
- Cancel/reopen the stream when no liveness frame arrives within a configured threshold.
- Use fast jittered reconnect for stale-socket timeouts, distinct from no-active-run and generic network-error backoff.
- Add telemetry for silence timeout and watchdog reconnects.
design: Suggested default: server heartbeat 25-30s, client silence threshold 75-90s. Keep thresholds centralized/configurable enough for test overrides.
acceptance_criteria:
- A stream that receives no event/heartbeat for the configured threshold is cancelled and reconnected.
- Silence timeout uses a separate reconnect reason and does not escalate immediately to long idle/network backoff.
- Existing NoActiveRunException and ApiException 400 idle behavior is preserved.
- Telemetry records last frame age, reconnect reason, and conversation id.
- Unit tests or targeted integration tests cover stale-socket timeout behavior.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoopTest.kt`
- Depends on parser frame API from `letta-mobile-jmzq.1`.

Current state:
- `runStreamSubscriber()` loops forever while coroutine is active.
- It opens `messageApi.streamConversation(conversationId)` then collects `SseParser.parse(channel)`.
- It tracks `runOpenedAtMs` and `runEventsCount` but not last liveness time.
- Idle/no-active-run cases intentionally use `streamSubscriber.idle404` and the longer `STREAM_IDLE_BACKOFF_MAX_MS` cap.
- Generic failures use `streamSubscriber.networkError` and delay `STREAM_BACKOFF_MAX_MS`.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add constants near existing stream constants:
   - `STREAM_HEARTBEAT_EXPECTED_MS = 30_000L` if needed for docs.
   - `STREAM_SILENCE_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000L` for production.
   - Provide a test override if the class already supports injected timings; otherwise add a small internal constructor parameter with default production value. Keep public API stable if possible.
2. Use the parser frame API from `SseParser.parseFrames(channel)` instead of `parse(channel)`.
3. The collector must handle frames:
   - `Heartbeat`: update last liveness timestamp and optionally telemetry `streamSubscriber.heartbeat`.
   - `Message`: update last liveness timestamp, increment `runEventsCount`, existing `eventReceived` telemetry, then existing reconcile/ingest logic.
4. Implement timeout without leaking coroutines. Safer patterns:
   - Wrap frame receive/collection in `withTimeout(STREAM_SILENCE_TIMEOUT_MS)` repeatedly if using a channel/flow loop, OR
   - Convert frames to a channel and use `withTimeoutOrNull` around each `receive`, cancelling the child collection in `finally`.
5. On timeout, close/cancel the stream collection, emit telemetry, and reconnect quickly with jitter.
   - Suggested event: `Telemetry.event("TimelineSync", "streamSubscriber.silenceTimeout", "conversationId" to conversationId, "silenceMs" to age, "timeoutMs" to timeout)`
   - Suggested reconnect event: `streamSubscriber.watchdogReconnect`
6. Do not classify silence timeout as `idle404` or `networkError` unless an actual exception follows.
7. Do not let stale-socket reconnect increase the idle 404 backoff ladder. It is a stale connection, not a server idle response.

Tests to add/update:
- Add fake API that returns a `ByteReadChannel` which emits one heartbeat/message then remains open without more bytes. With a small injected silence timeout, verify telemetry `streamSubscriber.silenceTimeout` appears and `streamConversation` is called again.
- Verify a heartbeat arriving before timeout prevents reconnect until silence exceeds threshold.
- Verify normal message events still ingest into timeline.
- Verify `NoActiveRunException` / ApiException idle tests still pass and use `idle404` telemetry.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.timeline.TimelineSyncLoopTest'`
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:compileDebugKotlin`

Do not:
- Do not reintroduce `subscriptionCount` or UI observer gating.
- Do not cancel the parent `TimelineSyncLoop` scope on a single stale stream.
- Do not use wall-clock `System.currentTimeMillis()` for timeout correctness if virtual-time tests need monotonic/test-controlled timing; prefer coroutine timeout primitives for tests.
- Do not create an unbounded reconnect loop; add small jitter/delay for repeated silence timeouts.
close_reason: Added 90s default stream silence watchdog with per-frame liveness reset, silenceTimeout/watchdogReconnect telemetry, fast jittered reconnect, and regression coverage for silent stale streams.

title: Persistent streams: configure OkHttp/Ktor transport for long-lived streams
description:
Why:
The API client currently lacks OkHttp pingInterval and uses generic HTTP timeouts for all calls. Long-lived streams need transport-level keepalive where available and timeout behavior that does not accidentally own stream lifetime.

Scope:
- Add OkHttp pingInterval, initially ~30s.
- Review Ktor HttpTimeout settings for streaming calls.
- Introduce streaming-specific timeout overrides or a streaming-specific client if needed.
- Confirm behavior for HTTP/2 vs HTTP/1.1 SSE.
- Document decisions in or near LettaApiClient/stream API code.
design: HTTP/2 PING helps transport liveness; HTTP/1.1 SSE still requires server heartbeats. Prefer explicit watchdog ownership of stale stream detection over relying on generic socket timeout.
acceptance_criteria:
- OkHttp engine has a deliberate pingInterval configuration.
- SSE requests do not rely solely on a blunt global socketTimeoutMillis for liveness.
- Normal REST request timeouts remain bounded.
- Streaming timeout choices are documented.
- Existing API tests/build still pass.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/LettaApiClient.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/MessageApi.kt` for stream request timeout overrides if needed
- Existing tests under `android-compose/core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/` if client behavior is testable

Current state:
- `LettaApiClient.createClient()` builds one Ktor `HttpClient(OkHttp)`.
- OkHttp dispatcher is already customized: `maxRequests = 64`, `maxRequestsPerHost = 16`.
- There is no `pingInterval` set in the OkHttp builder config.
- Global Ktor timeout is `requestTimeoutMillis = 60_000`, `connectTimeoutMillis = 30_000`, `socketTimeoutMillis = 60_000`.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add `import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit` if needed.
2. In the OkHttp engine `config { ... }` block, add:
   - `pingInterval(30, TimeUnit.SECONDS)`
3. Review the SSE request in `MessageApi.streamConversation(...)`:
   - If the stream currently inherits the global `requestTimeoutMillis = 60_000`, add a request-level timeout override for streaming calls.
   - For SSE, prefer `requestTimeoutMillis = HttpTimeout.INFINITE_TIMEOUT_MS` or a very long timeout, while leaving `connectTimeoutMillis` bounded.
   - Let the explicit watchdog from `jmzq.2` own stale-stream detection.
4. Keep normal REST calls on bounded timeouts.
5. Add a short code comment explaining HTTP/2 vs HTTP/1.1 behavior: OkHttp PING helps HTTP/2; HTTP/1.1 SSE still needs server heartbeats.

Potential Ktor shape for stream request timeout override:
- In the request builder for SSE only, use `timeout { requestTimeoutMillis = HttpTimeout.INFINITE_TIMEOUT_MS }` if Ktor version supports it.
- Do not change global timeouts to infinite.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:compileDebugKotlin`
- If tests exist/changed: `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest`

Do not:
- Do not remove the existing custom dispatcher settings; they were added to avoid stream starvation.
- Do not set all global REST timeouts to infinite.
- Do not change base URL, auth, cache, retry, logging, or TelemetryInterceptor behavior unless directly required.
- Do not assume pingInterval alone solves HTTP/1.1 SSE idle drops.
close_reason: Configured OkHttp HTTP/2 ping interval and stream-specific infinite request/socket timeouts for long-lived stream requests while keeping REST timeouts bounded.

title: [epic] Persistent screen-off chat streams
description:
Why:
Screen-off chat delivery currently depends on foreground-service process lifetime plus long-lived SSE subscribers. That keeps the app alive, but idle sockets can silently die and Android Doze can delay CPU/network scheduling. We need an incremental plan to make persistent streams explicit, observable, and configurable.

Scope:
Coordinate the client-side work for persistent screen-off streams: server heartbeat contract, client liveness watchdog, OkHttp/Ktor transport settings, stream budget, service diagnostics, battery optimization flow, service revival, and optional high-priority wake-lock mode.

Reference:
docs/architecture/screen-off-streaming-findings.md

Acceptance:
- Child issues exist for each incremental implementation slice.
- Dependencies reflect a safe rollout order: cheap/low-risk stream liveness first, battery-heavy wake-lock work last.
- The final architecture supports a bounded number of persistent streams, detects stale sockets, recovers service death, and exposes enough telemetry to debug screen-off delivery latency.

title: Persistent streams: verify SSE heartbeat contract and add parser support
description:
Why:
Persistent idle SSE sockets need regular liveness frames. The findings doc says the mobile client currently has no app-level heartbeat contract, so idle sockets can be silently dropped by NATs, proxies, or server reapers.

Scope:
- Verify whether the Letta server emits SSE comment heartbeats for conversation streams.
- If it does, document the cadence and payload shape.
- If it does not, file/record an upstream server follow-up requesting comment heartbeats every ~25-30s.
- Ensure the mobile SSE parser/client can surface heartbeat/comment frames as liveness signals without treating them as user-visible messages.
- Add telemetry for heartbeat frames.
design: Prefer SSE comment heartbeats such as `: ping\n\n`. Treat any valid frame/comment as stream liveness. Do not invent a client-only same-connection ping for HTTP/1.1 SSE; the server must send heartbeats for true idle liveness.
acceptance_criteria:
- Server heartbeat behavior is verified and recorded in the issue notes or docs.
- If absent, a follow-up server issue/reference is recorded.
- SseParser/stream consumer can observe heartbeat/comment frames as liveness.
- Heartbeat frames do not create timeline messages or break existing SSE event parsing.
- Telemetry includes heartbeat receipt with conversation id and cadence-safe metadata.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/stream/SseParser.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/stream/SseParserTest.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt` around `runStreamSubscriber()`
- Server behavior reference: `docs/architecture/screen-off-streaming-findings.md`

Current state:
- `SseParser.parse(ByteReadChannel): Flow<LettaMessage>` only emits `LettaMessage` values.
- `processEvent()` ignores non-`data:` lines, so SSE comment heartbeats like `: ping` are currently dropped and cannot reset a client watchdog.
- JSON messages with `message_type == "ping"` are also filtered out.
- `TimelineSyncLoop.runStreamSubscriber()` currently calls `SseParser.parse(channel).collect { message -> ... }`, so it has no way to observe heartbeat/comment liveness.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Introduce an internal parsed-frame representation in `SseParser.kt`, for example:
   - `sealed interface SseFrame`
   - `data class Message(val message: LettaMessage) : SseFrame`
   - `data object Heartbeat : SseFrame`
   - `data object Done : SseFrame` if useful internally
2. Add a new API such as `fun parseFrames(channel: ByteReadChannel): Flow<SseFrame>`.
3. Keep the existing `fun parse(channel): Flow<LettaMessage>` as a compatibility wrapper:
   - `parseFrames(channel).filterIsInstance<SseFrame.Message>().map { it.message }`
4. Treat an SSE event block containing only comment lines beginning with `:` as `Heartbeat`.
5. Treat JSON data frames with `message_type == "ping"` as `Heartbeat`, not as a timeline message.
6. Do not emit anything user-visible for heartbeats.
7. Add telemetry only at the consumer layer (`TimelineSyncLoop`) or rate-limit it heavily; do not log every parser heartbeat noisily from `SseParser`.

Test cases to add/update in `SseParserTest`:
- `parseFrames` emits heartbeat for `": ping\n\n"`.
- `parseFrames` emits heartbeat for `"data: {\"id\":\"1\",\"message_type\":\"ping\"}\n\n"`.
- Existing `parse()` still returns no messages for comment heartbeat and ping message.
- Mixed stream preserves order: heartbeat, user_message, heartbeat, assistant_message.
- `[DONE]` still terminates and later data does not emit.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.stream.SseParserTest'`
- If shared API changed: `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:compileDebugKotlin`

Do not:
- Do not convert heartbeat/comment frames into `LettaMessage`.
- Do not remove the existing `parse()` API unless all call sites are updated.
- Do not log raw message content beyond existing behavior while adding heartbeat support.
- Do not implement the silence watchdog in this bead; only make liveness observable and recorded.
close_reason: Added SseFrame parser API preserving parse(), surfaced comment/data ping heartbeats as liveness frames, added rate-limited heartbeat telemetry in TimelineSyncLoop, and added parser tests. Existing docs/architecture/poc-validation-results.md records server POST streams emit JSON ping frames; live comment-heartbeat cadence still needs server/runtime verification.

Track the branch/commit/PR packaging for this work.

title: ModelDropdown sends wrong field (name vs handle) breaking agent model updates
description:
## Bug
When editing an agent and selecting a different model from ModelDropdown, the save operation fails because ModelDropdown sends model.name instead of model.handle to the selection callback.

## Root Cause
ModelDropdown.kt line 108:
```kotlin
onClick = {
    onModelSelected(model.name)  // ❌ Wrong field
    filterText = ""
    expanded = false
}
```

Should send: `model.handle ?: model.name`

## Evidence
- Test data shows Agent.model stores handle-style identifiers: "letta/gpt-4o", "openai/gpt-4o"
- EditAgentViewModel embedding resolution uses handle as canonical field (lines 136-139)
- LlmModel.displayName computed property prioritizes handle over name
- When user selects model with name="GPT-4o" and handle="letta/gpt-4o", API receives "GPT-4o" and rejects it

## Impact
- Primary: LLM model dropdown in EditAgentScreen
- Secondary: Embedding model dropdown (currently masked but fragile)

## Fix Required
1. ModelDropdown.kt line 108: `onModelSelected(model.handle ?: model.name)`
2. EditAgentScreen.kt lines 419-424: Map embedding handle explicitly in LlmModel construction

## Test Gap
No tests exist for model selection → save flow or ModelDropdown identifier handling

title: Checkpoint and restore agent configuration before/after compaction
description:
When a conversation undergoes compaction (memory compression), the agent's configuration (model, modelSettings, contextWindowLimit, etc.) must be preserved. Currently there is no explicit checkpoint/restore mechanism.

Scope:
1. Before triggering compaction, snapshot the agent's current configuration
2. After compaction completes, verify the agent configuration matches the snapshot
3. If configuration was reset, restore it from the checkpoint
4. Add telemetry to track when configuration drift occurs during compaction

Implementation approach:
- Add checkpoint logic in ConversationRepository or AdminChatViewModel before compaction
- Store snapshot in memory or local state (not persistent storage initially)
- After compaction, compare current agent config with snapshot
- If drift detected, call agentRepository.updateAgent() to restore
- Log telemetry event for configuration restoration

Related files:
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AgentRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/ConversationRepository.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Agent.kt (AgentUpdateParams)

This ensures agent configuration remains stable across compaction operations.

title: Fix agent config model save flow and UX
description: The agent configuration screen has UX issues and currently fails when saving model selection changes from the edit-agent flow. We need to verify the correct Letta API model-config payload shape, align the mobile save request with that contract, and improve the agent config interaction so changing models is understandable and reliable.

title: Add cross-boundary regression harness for chat app-bot-timeline seam
description:
Add focused regression coverage that spans the exact seam where recent chat regressions occurred: route/session selection in app, streaming behavior in bot, and timeline ingestion in core. The aim is to keep future chat changes from reintroducing bugs that only appear when multiple layers interact.

Coverage targets:
- wrong-agent existing-conversation Client Mode send
- terminal done=true duplicate-final-message regression
- delta assistant chunk concatenation
- deliberate session restart / agent switch behavior

Suggested test surfaces:
- app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ClientModeChatSenderTest.kt
- app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModelTest.kt
- bot/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/core/RemoteBotSessionTest.kt
- bot/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/protocol/WsBotClientTest.kt
- core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoopTest.kt (only if needed for reconcile assertions)

Deliverables:
1. A small matrix of contract regressions with named tests tied to the failure modes above.
2. Shared fixtures or helper builders where appropriate so future workers do not need to rediscover setup.
3. Notes in the parent issue about which tests are the canonical tripwires for this architecture seam.
acceptance_criteria:
1) The recent wrong-agent and duplicate-final regressions each have dedicated automated regression coverage.
2) Tests are named after the behavior contract, not just the implementation method.
3) At least one test fixture is reusable enough that a future worker can extend it without rebuilding the entire setup.
4) Parent issue records which tests should be run when touching chat boundary code.
close_reason: Added docs/architecture/chat-boundary-regression-harness.md with the app↔bot↔timeline contract test matrix, reusable fixtures, and run commands; added a reusable ClientModeSenderFixture plus route-agent/conversation preservation test; verified app/core/bot targeted tripwires.

title: Define timeline ingestion and reconcile rules for Client Mode events
description:
Document and harden how Client Mode events enter the timeline and how they are later confirmed, replaced, or preserved. The goal is to stop USER/ASSISTANT/REASONING/TOOL events from each inventing their own ad hoc lifecycle rules.

Problem background:
- Timeline is now the source of truth, but Client Mode still performs optimistic local appends and assistant upserts before confirmed server ingestion.
- Reconcile behavior differs by message type and still relies on implicit knowledge spread across TimelineRepository, TimelineSyncLoop, Timeline.kt, and AdminChatViewModel.
- The earlier migration doc focused on otid and single-timeline semantics; this task should update the current reality for the gateway-backed path.

Files to inspect first:
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt

Required contract points:
1. Which layer may append optimistic USER locals
2. Which layer may upsert local assistant/reasoning/tool events
3. Which message types may fuzzy-collapse, replace-in-place, or append-only
4. What distinguishes confirmed server events from client-mode harness locals

Deliverables:
- explicit rules in docs or code comments
- tests covering user, assistant, reasoning, and tool event ingestion
- identified follow-up debt if strict otid-based reconcile is still blocked upstream
acceptance_criteria:
1) The allowed timeline lifecycle for USER, ASSISTANT, REASONING, TOOL_CALL, and TOOL_RESULT is written down in one place.
2) Timeline tests cover at least one Client Mode local→confirmed path and one assistant streaming path.
3) No message type relies on undocumented special-case behavior across multiple files.
4) Any remaining fuzzy-match or heuristic paths are explicitly documented as temporary.
close_reason: Added the Client Mode timeline lifecycle contract covering allowed writers, local/confirmed identity, per-type reconcile behavior, tool result folding, and fuzzy-match debt. Added reducer characterization tests for USER and REASONING Client Mode local-to-confirmed collapse; existing tests cover assistant streaming and tool folding.

title: Codify Client Mode conversation ownership and route-agent binding
description:
Define the authoritative rules for how an existing conversation maps to an agent and how Client Mode chooses or restarts a gateway session for that route. This work should eliminate hidden assumptions between AdminChatViewModel, ClientModeController, ClientModeChatSender, BotGateway, and WsBotClient.

Implementation context:
- Current route agent comes from AdminChatViewModel savedStateHandle agentId
- Existing conversation reuse flows through ChatRouteSessionResolver and ClientModeChatSender
- ClientModeController currently owns session startup and route-agent binding
- Recent regression: existing conversation belonged to one agent while Client Mode picked another session agent

Files to inspect first:
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ClientModeChatSender.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/clientmode/ClientModeController.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatRouteSessionResolver.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/core/BotGateway.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/protocol/WsBotClient.kt

Deliverables:
1. A documented contract comment/doc section or adjacent architecture note describing route agent, conversation owner, and gateway session authority.
2. Production code aligned to that contract with no fallback to arbitrary preferred agent selection when route agent is known.
3. Regression tests proving existing-conversation send, fresh-route send, and session restart all bind to the route agent consistently.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Existing-conversation Client Mode send never opens a session for a different agent than the route agent.
2) Fresh-route Client Mode send restarts/ensures a session for the route agent explicitly.
3) Tests cover route agent + conversation reuse + session restart scenarios with concrete assertions on requested agent IDs.
4) Issue documents which layer is authoritative for conversation ownership.

title: Define bot stream chunk contract for Client Mode
description:
Make the stream contract explicit between WsBotClient, RemoteBotSession, and AdminChatViewModel so assistant/reasoning/tool events and terminal done chunks cannot drift semantically again.

Problem background:
- Recent regression: terminal done=true chunk replayed the full assistant text, duplicating the final message.
- Earlier regressions: delta chunks were treated as snapshots instead of append fragments.
- AdminChatViewModel currently assumes non-terminal chunks may carry user-visible deltas while terminal chunks primarily signal completion.

Files to inspect first:
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/protocol/WsBotClient.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/core/RemoteBotSession.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/core/RemoteBotSessionTest.kt
- android-compose/bot/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/protocol/WsBotClientTest.kt

Required contract points:
1. Whether ASSISTANT and REASONING chunks are deltas or snapshots
2. Whether TOOL_CALL / TOOL_RESULT are snapshot-shaped or append-shaped
3. What fields a terminal done=true chunk may contain
4. Where directive parsing occurs and what data is preserved on completion

Deliverables:
- code and tests that encode the contract
- fixture-backed tests for progressive assistant streaming and terminal completion
- a short architecture note or inline contract reference in the owning layer
acceptance_criteria:
1) Terminal done=true chunks do not replay already-rendered assistant text.
2) Delta vs snapshot semantics are documented and enforced in tests for assistant, reasoning, and tool paths.
3) RemoteBotSession/WsBotClient tests fail if terminal chunks become content-bearing again without intentional contract change.
4) AdminChatViewModel assumptions align with the bot-layer contract.

title: Define explicit chat contracts across app, bot, and timeline
description:
Post-migration chat regressions are concentrated at the seam between app route state, client-mode gateway session selection, bot streaming semantics, and timeline reconciliation. This feature hardens that seam by making the contract explicit in code, docs, and tests so future chat work no longer depends on hidden state coupling.

Scope:
1. Define conversation ownership and route-agent/session binding rules for Client Mode.
2. Define bot stream contract for delta chunks, terminal done chunks, and directive metadata.
3. Define timeline ingestion/reconcile rules for USER, ASSISTANT, REASONING, TOOL_CALL, and TOOL_RESULT events.
4. Add regression tests and wire-trace-backed fixtures at the app↔bot↔timeline boundary.

Out of scope:
- agent admin model/save consistency issues
- new chat UI features
- embedded runtime expansion beyond what is needed to stabilize current chat behavior.
design:
Execution order for this section:
A. Conversation ownership + gateway session binding
B. Streaming chunk semantics
C. Timeline ingestion and reconcile contract
D. Cross-boundary regression harness

Rationale: the recent wrong-agent and duplicate-final regressions both came from implicit boundary rules. The next worker should treat this as contract hardening, not feature expansion.
notes: Canonical chat boundary regression matrix lives in docs/architecture/chat-boundary-regression-harness.md. Tripwires: ClientModeChatSenderTest for request shape, AdminChatViewModelTest for app route/timeline observation, WsBotClientLifecycleTest and RemoteBotSessionTest for bot stream/session contracts, TimelineReducerCharacterizationTest for core local/confirmed folding.
close_reason: All contract-hardening dependencies are complete: route/session ownership, bot stream semantics, timeline lifecycle rules, and the cross-boundary regression harness are documented and covered by canonical tests.

Move explicitConversationId, fresh-route handling, client-mode conversation restoration, active conversation selection, and most-recent fallback into a small testable unit so conversation selection bugs stop coupling to send/stream logic.

title: Compare Letta chat architecture to RikkaHub and add IME auto-scroll hook
description: Analyze Letta Mobile chat architecture against RikkaHub's chat stack to identify exact refactor targets, then implement a small IME-driven LazyList auto-scroll hook in Letta Mobile chat if it fits safely with current behavior.
notes:
Refactor plan derived from RikkaHub comparison:
1) letta-mobile-azmn — extract conversation resolution from AdminChatViewModel
2) letta-mobile-egas — extract composer state/controller
3) letta-mobile-s3zq — extract pure render-model pipeline from ChatScreen
4) letta-mobile-g23t — extract ChatMessageList and scroll behavior
5) letta-mobile-x8xg — isolate IME and viewport hooks
6) letta-mobile-3b21 — slim AgentScaffold into shell only

Priority rationale: separate route/session selection first, then isolate composer and message shaping, then split list/scroll behavior, then finish with IME hooks and scaffold slimming. Session-style architecture was intentionally deferred; the first pass should shrink coupling before adding new infrastructure.

title: Restore default-to-most-recent conversation behavior in chat view
description: Chat view should open the most recent conversation by default when no explicit conversation is selected. That behavior regressed and now the wrong conversation (or none) is shown by default. Diagnose the routing/viewmodel regression and restore the prior default selection behavior.

title: Fix Mermaid WebView lifecycle regression

Root cause: DisposableEffect(webViewRef) disposed when webViewRef changed from null to the newly-created WebView, destroying the WebView immediately after creation. Fixed MermaidDiagram and MathBlock disposal keys to follow source/display lifecycle instead of ref assignment. Verified :app:assembleDebug and installed app-debug.apk on Pixel 2 XL.

title: Fix chat history load flash

Root cause: AdminChatViewModel timeline observer cleared isLoadingMessages on first non-empty partial timeline emission instead of waiting for TimelineSyncEvent.Hydrated. Fix implemented so Hydrated/HydrateFailed own loading-state completion while observer only updates message/streaming state. Verified with :app:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleDebug, AdminChatViewModelTest run reached :app:testDebugUnitTest without source failures, and app-debug.apk installed on Pixel 2 XL.

title: Investigate Pixel 2 XL crash after nav fix install
description: The updated debug build with the chat-to-admin routing fix installs and launches on the remote Pixel 2 XL, but the app crashes on-device. Capture logcat, isolate the exception/stack trace, and identify the failing code path.
notes:
Fresh Pixel 2 XL logcat confirmed repeated Android 11 native RenderThread SIGSEGV at libhwui GLFunctorDrawable::onDraw, with WebView sandbox startup immediately before crash. Implemented mitigation in MermaidDiagram.kt and MathBlock.kt: Android 11 and older now use Compose source/fallback rendering instead of instantiating WebView-backed Mermaid/KaTeX blocks. Verified with :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin, :app:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleDebug, and installed app-debug.apk successfully on Pixel 2 XL 192.168.50.202:5555.
Correction after user feedback: the Android 11 WebView fallback workaround was unacceptable and has been removed. Pixel 2 XL WebView rendering is restored in MermaidDiagram.kt and MathBlock.kt while retaining lifecycle hardening. Current installed build instead addresses the pinned-agent/Admin ownership path by making Admin-from-chat pop back to an existing AdminRoute when present, avoiding saved-state restoration of AgentChatRoute. Verified :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin, :app:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleDebug, and adb install -r -t succeeded on Pixel 2 XL 192.168.50.202:5555.
close_reason: Revised fix installed: Pixel 2 WebView rendering restored; pinned-agent AdminRoute pop-back path fixed

title: Fix chat-to-admin navigation routing

Implemented chat-to-admin navigation fix in AdaptiveScaffold.kt: the Admin-from-chat special case now uses the same popUpTo(graph.startDestinationId) state-saving behavior as other top-level destinations. Verified with :app:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleDebug, and installed app-debug.apk successfully on Pixel 2 XL 192.168.50.202:5555.
Revised fix after pinned-agent root-cause clarification: Admin-from-chat now first calls popBackStack<AdminRoute>(inclusive = false). This handles the AdminRoute -> AgentChatRoute pinned-agent stack by returning to the existing Admin entry instead of navigating with restoreState and potentially restoring/reloading chat. Fallback navigation remains for chats opened without Admin below them. Verified :app:compileDebugKotlin, :app:assembleDebug, and installed on Pixel 2 XL.

title: Deepen project-scoped active agents and runs dashboard

Expand that into a more operational project-scoped dashboard that makes active agents, run state, project progress, and related runtime activity much more visible and actionable from the existing project shell.

title: Deepen Huly/VibeSync project workspace surface on Android
description: The Android client already has a real Huly/VibeSync-backed project home, project CRUD, and project-scoped chat routing. This work should deepen that existing project shell into a denser operational surface rather than rebuilding projects from scratch. Focus on session continuity, conversation recovery, explicit lifecycle ownership, reconnect semantics, and eliminating hidden state transitions between mobile and gateway.
