title: Android client: hydrator fetches messages but doesn't render history
description: The hydrator correctly fetches and renders message history. However, the model's reasoning/chain-of-thought traces are not being fetched or displayed. The reasoning steps that appear during streaming are not available when viewing history.

title: Android client: runs are incorrectly interrupted when screen sleeps
description: When the Android app's screen goes to sleep, ongoing agent runs are being interrupted/cancelled. This appears to be unintended behavior - runs should continue in the background when the phone is locked, similar to how other apps handle background work. This is likely related to lifecycle handling or coroutine scope management that cancels work on process pause.
notes:
Field test 2026-05-02 on Pixel 9 Pro confirmed phase 1 fix works: gateway stays alive across screen-off (Telemetry/ClientModeController appBackgrounded gatewayKeptAlive=true) and the Client Mode run continued executing tool calls through the sleep cycle. Two follow-ups remain:

1. Doubled assistant content on return \u2014 fixed in commit 972db625. handleClientModeStreamChunkViaTimeline ASSISTANT branch was ignoring its computed dedup value and unconditionally appending delta. Re-tested via build+install on Pixel 9 Pro.
2. Missing final ASSISTANT text bubble \u2014 tracked in letta-mobile-mewb.

title: Fix stream-end flash and doubled assistant content
description: Investigate and fix a frontend bug where assistant streaming looks correct while active, then flashes and becomes doubled when the stream ends on the dev device.
notes: Fixed the intermittent device duplicate-final-response race. Device logcat had no TimelineSync/AdminChat tags for the running build, but code inspection found ClientModeSendCoordinator launched each timeline upsert asynchronously and then ran stream-end reconcile immediately on the terminal frame. That allowed reconcile to fetch/insert the final server response before the terminal local chunk had folded, intermittently leaving both local and confirmed assistant bubbles. Changed handleStreamChunk to suspend and await timelineRepository.upsertClientModeStreamChunk inline before stream_done reconcile. Added AdminChatViewModelTest 'client mode terminal chunk is folded before stream-end reconcile' with a delayed terminal upsert to pin ordering. Verified :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.AdminChatViewModelTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, installed root debug APK on 192.168.50.235:5555, launched com.letta.mobile, checked fresh AndroidRuntime logs (none), and git diff --check.
close_reason: Fixed stream-end reconcile race causing intermittent duplicate final response

title: Debug-only POST /api/v1/test/evict-session endpoint on lettabot ws-gateway
description:
# Debug-only `POST /api/v1/test/evict-session` endpoint on lettabot ws-gateway

## Why

The NO_SESSION class of bug only reproduces *after* the gateway evicts an idle session — currently after a 5-min timeout. Waiting 5 min per scenario is a non-starter for a CI-grade E2E harness. We need a **debug-only** way to force an eviction on demand from the test driver.

## What

Add a Node.js HTTP endpoint to lettabot (in `src/api/`, alongside `ws-gateway.ts`):

```
POST /api/v1/test/evict-session
Body: { "conn_id": "<id>" }  // or { "agent_id": "...", "conversation_id": "..." }
```

Behavior: looks up the matching session in the per-(conn, agent) SDK session pool (introduced in c3f1d16) and force-evicts it the same way the idle eviction path does — including emitting the same close code (4003 IDLE_TIMEOUT or a new 4004 TEST_EVICTION code) so client-side handling exercises the real code path.

## Gating (security)

Two-layer guard, both required (single `NODE_ENV` check is insufficient — see OWASP MASVS-RESILIENCE-1):

1. **Build-time**: only register the route when `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` AND `process.env.LETTABOT_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS === '1'`.
2. **Runtime**: requires header `X-Test-Hook-Token: <value>` matching `process.env.LETTABOT_TEST_HOOK_TOKEN`. If unset → 404 (not 401, to avoid leaking existence).
3. Bind only on `127.0.0.1` interface for the test-hooks subrouter (or refuse non-loopback origins via middleware).

In `release` builds (the ones we publish), the route file isn't even registered.

## Acceptance criteria

- Endpoint exists, gated as above; route returns 404 in production builds even if the token header is correct.
- Endpoint evicts the named session synchronously and the next client message on that connection observes NO_SESSION (or whichever close code we standardize on for forced eviction).
- E2E test in `src/api/ws-gateway.e2e.test.ts` exercises the endpoint and asserts the eviction is observable on the wire.
- Documented in `docs/architecture/test-hooks.md` (new) with security caveats.
- A negative test asserts the route returns 404 when `LETTABOT_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS` is unset.

## Research notes

- OWASP MASVS-RESILIENCE-1: never ship test hooks in production — applies to backends too. <https://mas.owasp.org/MASVS/>
- The per-(conn, agent) SDK session pool with LRU + idle eviction is in commit c3f1d16; eviction logic should reuse the existing path, not duplicate it.
- Consider adding `evict_at_ms_from_now` query param later if we want soft-evictions (don't expand scope here).
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint exists and is double-gated (NODE_ENV + LETTABOT_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS + token header + 127.0.0.1 bind); 404 in production even with token; evicts target session synchronously; e2e test covers happy path + negative gating; docs/architecture/test-hooks.md exists

title: make verify-no-session-recovery DEVICE=… smoke harness (first cut)
description:
# `make verify-no-session-recovery DEVICE=…` smoke harness (first cut)

## Why

Tier-1 deliverable for the parent epic — wires the structured log markers (sibling bead) and the test-evict endpoint (sibling bead) into a single `make` target that proves the NO_SESSION recovery path actually fires end-to-end on the Pixel 2XL.

## What

A shell script (`scripts/e2e/verify-no-session-recovery.sh`) and Makefile target that:

1. **Setup** — confirms `adb devices` shows `$DEVICE`, runs `make verify-device-ready`, then `pm clear com.letta.mobile`, grants required runtime permissions, disables animation scales (`settings put global window_animation_scale 0` etc.).
2. **Cold-start** — launches the app via deep link (`am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d "lettamobile://chat?…"` or equivalent) at a known conversation.
3. **First message** — uses `input text` + `input keyevent KEYCODE_ENTER` (or a known testTag-resolved tap coord from `uiautomator dump`) to send `"e2e-probe-1"`. Tails `adb logcat -s WsBotClient:*` and asserts a `WsBotClient.session state=started` marker within 10 s, then a result chunk within 30 s.
4. **Force eviction** — calls `POST /api/v1/test/evict-session` against the lettabot dev backend with the conn id observed from logcat (or from a debug logcat marker that prints conn_id at session start).
5. **Second message** — sends `"e2e-probe-2"`. Asserts:
   - `WsBotClient.recovery state=attempted reason=NO_SESSION` marker appears,
   - followed by `WsBotClient.recovery state=succeeded`,
   - and a streamed result lands within 30 s.
6. **Failure artifacts** — on any assertion miss: `screencap` PNG + last 500 lines of logcat + Makefile target name → `build/e2e-artifacts/<timestamp>/`. Exit non-zero.

## Acceptance criteria

- `make verify-no-session-recovery DEVICE=711KPAE0914240` exits 0 on a healthy build.
- Reverting the gateway-side recovery fix (or the client-side handler) → target exits non-zero with a clear "did not see WsBotClient.recovery state=succeeded within budget" message.
- Total runtime < 90 s (no 5-min idle waits).
- Artifacts on failure are useful: screenshot is current, logcat tail covers the failure window.
- Target is opt-in (not yet in `verify-all`) until the broader `verify-e2e` lands.
- `.skills/android-deploy/SKILL.md` updated with a one-paragraph "verify-no-session-recovery" section.

## Dependencies

- Sibling: structured WsBotClient recovery log markers.
- Sibling: debug-only evict-session endpoint on lettabot.

## Research notes

- `adb shell input` is race-prone vs Compose recomposition; for tier-1 we accept this and tap by coord (acceptable since this is a probe, not a regression-grade harness yet). Tier-3 swaps to UIAutomator2 with `testTagsAsResourceId`.
- Disable animations via `settings put global window_animation_scale 0.0` (and `transition`, `animator`) — Espresso/Android docs flag this as the #1 flake mitigation. <https://developer.android.com/training/testing/espresso/setup>
- `adb exec-out screencap -p > shot.png` is the preferred no-temp-file capture pattern.
acceptance_criteria: make verify-no-session-recovery DEVICE=711KPAE0914240 exits 0 on healthy build; reverting recovery fix breaks it with clear error; runtime < 90s; failure artifacts (screencap + logcat tail) saved; opt-in target; android-deploy SKILL.md updated

title: Add structured WsBotClient recovery log markers (greppable post-deploy)
description:
# Add structured WsBotClient recovery log markers (greppable post-deploy)

## Why

Tier-1 prerequisite for the adb-driven E2E harness (parent epic). Today the recovery path in `WsBotClient.kt` emits free-form log lines that are hard to assert on with `adb logcat | grep`. A shell harness needs **stable, machine-greppable** markers so a scenario can prove "yes, the NO_SESSION recovery code path actually fired, with this request_id, in this state, for this reason."

## What

Add structured logcat markers to the recovery state machine in `WsBotClient`. Format (one line per state transition, fixed prefix, key=value pairs):

```
WsBotClient.recovery state=<attempted|succeeded|failed|skipped> reason=<NO_SESSION|EVICTED|TIMEOUT|MANUAL> request_id=<id> conversation_id=<id> attempt=<n> elapsed_ms=<n>
```

Plus paired markers for the broader connection lifecycle:

```
WsBotClient.lifecycle state=<connecting|open|closing|closed> close_code=<n?> reason=<text?>
WsBotClient.session   state=<starting|started|evicted|invalid> conversation_id=<id> session_id=<id?>
```

## Acceptance criteria

- All recovery branches in `WsBotClient.kt` (and any helper classes) emit a `WsBotClient.recovery` line at every state transition.
- Tag is `WsBotClient` so `adb logcat -s WsBotClient:*` filters cleanly.
- Lines pass a regex parse: `^WsBotClient\.(recovery|lifecycle|session) (\w+=\S+ )+$`.
- A unit test (`WsBotClientLifecycleTest` or sibling) asserts the exact marker text for each transition (snapshot-style).
- Existing free-form log calls in the same paths are removed or downgraded to DEBUG to avoid duplication.
- `docs/testing/e2e-harness.md` (created in parent epic) gets a "log marker contract" section listing the keys.

## Out of scope

- Recovery *behavior* changes — markers only.
- Adding to non-recovery code paths.

## Research notes

- Structured log markers are the cheapest, most stable hook for shell-driven assertions; they survive Compose recompositions and don't depend on view IDs.
- Keep markers ASCII-only and on a single line — `adb logcat` line-buffers and breaks long lines.
acceptance_criteria: All recovery branches emit WsBotClient.recovery markers with stable key=value format; tag is WsBotClient; passes regex parse; unit test snapshots each transition; existing duplicate logs removed/downgraded; log marker contract documented

title: [epic] adb-driven E2E test harness for letta-mobile (Pixel 2XL)
description:
# [epic] adb-driven E2E test harness for letta-mobile (Pixel 2XL)

## Context

Recent NO_SESSION class of bugs (e.g. session eviction → next message fails with no recovery) shipped because we have no automated end-to-end coverage of the running app on a real device. `make verify-stream` exercises the gateway in isolation but never asserts that the **actual app** on a **physical device** can:
- Cold-start, send a message, receive a streamed result.
- Recover from gateway-side session eviction (the NO_SESSION case).
- Survive network drops mid-stream.
- Switch conversations without dropping the next stream.
- Stream tool-call deltas correctly.

Pixel 9 Pro has dropped off the test bench. **Pixel 2XL (serial 711KPAE0914240, codename `taimen`)** is the target device going forward.

## Goal

A reproducible, scriptable, real-device E2E harness that gates `make verify-release` and prevents NO_SESSION-class regressions from reaching production builds.

## Layered approach (each layer = a child epic / set of beads)

1. **Tier 1 — tonight (≈30 min, immediate payoff):**
   - Structured WsBotClient recovery log markers (greppable from logcat).
   - Debug-only `POST /api/v1/test/evict-session` endpoint on lettabot (skip the 5-min idle wait).
   - First-cut `make verify-no-session-recovery DEVICE=…` shell script.

2. **Tier 2 — this week:**
   - `scripts/e2e/driver.sh` + 5 scenarios (cold-start, NO_SESSION recovery, network drop, tool-call streaming, multi-conversation switch).
   - Wire into `make verify-e2e DEVICE=…`, gate `verify-release`.

3. **Tier 3 — soon:**
   - `Modifier.testTag` + `Modifier.semantics { testTagsAsResourceId = true }` codemod on critical Compose surfaces.
   - UIAutomator2 (`am instrument`) instrumented tests over the shell harness for the flows that need recomposition-aware idling.

4. **Tier 4 — eventually:**
   - mitmproxy reverse-proxy WS frame transcript capture in CI (full wire-level assertions).

## Acceptance criteria (epic)

- `make verify-e2e DEVICE=711KPAE0914240` runs all scenarios green on the Pixel 2XL.
- A NO_SESSION regression introduced into lettabot or letta-mobile fails `verify-e2e` *before* a release publish.
- Failure artifacts (screenshots + screen recording + logcat tail + WS frame dump) are captured automatically and attached to the run.
- Documented in `docs/testing/e2e-harness.md` and referenced from `RELEASE.md`.

## Non-goals

- Multi-device matrix (just Pixel 2XL for now).
- Emulator coverage (real-device-only quirks like Doze are the whole point).
- Replacing unit / instrumented tests — this complements them.

## Research notes (cited)

- Compose `testTag` is invisible to `uiautomator dump` unless `Modifier.semantics { testTagsAsResourceId = true }` is set on the root. <https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose/testing#uiautomator-interop>
- `screenrecord` caps at 180 s, no audio — chunk + concat or use `scrcpy --record`. <https://developer.android.com/tools/adb#screenrecord>
- `dumpsys deviceidle force-idle` requires `dumpsys battery unplug` first on Android 14+. <https://developer.android.com/training/monitoring-device-state/doze-standby#testing_doze>
- mitmproxy 11.x has stable WebSocket interception via `flow.websocket.messages` and supports `--mode reverse:` for `adb reverse` setups. <https://docs.mitmproxy.org/stable/overview-features/#websocket>
- Debug-only endpoints: gate by `BuildConfig.DEBUG` *and* a runtime token / localhost-only bind; single `NODE_ENV` checks routinely leak. <https://mas.owasp.org/MASVS/>

## Links

- Pixel 2XL: serial `711KPAE0914240`, codename `taimen`.
- Existing skill: `.skills/android-deploy/SKILL.md`.
- Existing gates: `gates.yaml`, `scripts/release/verify-release.sh`.
acceptance_criteria: make verify-e2e DEVICE=711KPAE0914240 runs green on Pixel 2XL; NO_SESSION regressions fail verify-release before publish; failure artifacts (screenshots, screen recording, logcat, WS frames) auto-captured; docs/testing/e2e-harness.md exists and is referenced from RELEASE.md

title: [epic] Client Mode chat view: streaming-first state refactor
description:
Umbrella for the cluster of client-mode chat-rendering bugs Emmanuel reported on 2026-04-28. All three appear to share a single root cause: the chat list is rebuilt from `GET /api/v1/conversations/:id/messages` after the agent's terminal `result` frame, instead of being maintained as streaming-first local state.

## Children

- `letta-mobile-e75s` (P1) — new conv leaks `system_message` into chat
- `letta-mobile-9pfm` (P1) — no optimistic user-message echo on send
- `letta-mobile-b2u1` (P2) — no thinking/typing indicator
- `letta-mobile-pv4y` (P2) — user message renders late, alongside agent reply

## Why one epic

Independently filed in case any can be fixed in isolation, but the cleanest implementation is a single refactor of `AdminChatViewModel`:

1. **Local list is the source of truth for the active turn.**
   - Optimistic user bubble appended at send-tap (fixes 9pfm).
   - Thinking indicator state machine driven by frame types (fixes b2u1).
   - Assistant chunks accumulate locally per OTID/conversation (fixes pv4y).

2. **Server reconciliation only on cold load.**
   - Don't refetch messages on every terminal frame.
   - When refetching, filter by `message_type` to keep only `user_message` + `assistant_message` (fixes e75s, plus future-proofs against any other server quirk).

3. **New-conversation special case.**
   - Either defer `POST /conversations` until first send, or treat a freshly-created conv as having an empty local list and skip the initial `GET /messages` (also fixes e75s).

## Server-side context (lettabot)

The companion lettabot bug `lettabot-y4j` (system-reminder envelope leaking into chat) is fixed in the proxy. That fix is correct because envelopes are lettabot's own wrapping — not Letta state. The four bugs in this epic are NOT server bugs and should NOT be patched in the proxy; the proxy returns Letta-stored history faithfully and other clients (Debug mode) want to see all message types.
notes:
Status update 2026-04-28: e75s fix landed in commit 3ec1122 (TimelineEventToUiMessage drops SYSTEM events; tests added).

Hypothesis: the other three children (9pfm optimistic user echo, b2u1 thinking indicator, pv4y delayed user message) were partly or wholly observation artifacts caused by e75s — the SYSTEM bubble at the top of the new conversation made all the optimistic UI state look broken. Architecture review confirms the underlying machinery for those three is already wired correctly:

- 9pfm: appendClientModeLocal + in-memory clientModeMessages both set the user bubble immediately on send.
- b2u1: ChatScreen.kt:539 gates TypingIndicator on isStreaming; AdminChatViewModel sets isStreaming=true before the launch.
- pv4y: optimistic user bubble exists; pre-fix it was being overwritten by SYSTEM emission from the first hydrate. Post-fix the optimistic bubble survives.

Recommended: Emmanuel re-tests on a device after e75s ships. If 9pfm/b2u1/pv4y still reproduce, file fresh repro details on each child and we'll re-investigate. Detailed architectural notes already added to each child bead.

title: Client Mode: send has no optimistic user-message echo
description:
After tapping send in client mode, the user's message does NOT appear in the chat view immediately. It only pops in later — sometimes alongside the agent's response. This makes the app feel broken/laggy and creates uncertainty about whether the send actually went through.

Expected behavior (industry-standard chat UX): user's message bubble appears in the chat instantly on tap-send, before any network round-trip completes.

Symptom (Emmanuel, 2026-04-28):
> 'when i send a message ... there is no visual feedback. i don't see my user message echoed back ... after a while my message pops in.'

Likely root cause: `AdminChatViewModel` / `ClientModeChatSender` is waiting for a server frame (probably the WS `assistant` chunk or terminal `result`) before adding the user bubble to the local list. Should add an optimistic local entry the instant send is dispatched, keyed by an OTID, and reconcile it when the server echoes back.
acceptance_criteria:
1. On send, a user-message bubble appears in the chat list within one frame (≤16ms).
2. The optimistic bubble persists until either:
   (a) the server-echoed message replaces it (matched by OTID), OR
   (b) send fails and the bubble shows an error/retry state.
3. If the user sends multiple messages in quick succession, each appears immediately in the order tapped.
4. Network failure shows an inline error on the optimistic bubble; the bubble is NOT silently dropped.
5. Test: instrumented send flow asserts message-list size increments synchronously at tap-send.
notes:
Architecture review (2026-04-28, while fixing e75s):

The optimistic user-bubble path is already wired:
- Known-conv fresh send (sendMessageViaClientMode line 1071 of AdminChatViewModel): isStreaming=true, isAgentTyping=true set BEFORE the launch; appendClientModeLocal puts the user bubble in the timeline; observer renders it as a Local with role='user'.
- Fresh-route send (line 1097+): clientModeMessages gets a UiMessage with role='user' immediately, _uiState.messages = nextMessages.toImmutableList() — also instant.

Suspect e75s was masking 9pfm: the SYSTEM bubble at top of the new conv made the user think their bubble didn't appear, when really it did but was visually displaced/buried by the leaked system_message. Now that e75s lands, please re-test 9pfm on a real device — bug may already be invisible.

If 9pfm still reproduces post-e75s, the next places to look are:
1. The TimelineGrouper (ui/common/TimelineGrouper.kt) — does it group user bubbles into something invisible?
2. ChatScreen's reverseLayout LazyColumn ordering — is the user message getting positioned off-screen?
3. handleClientModeStreamChunk replacing _uiState.messages out from under the optimistic bubble.
REGRESSION CONFIRMED 2026-04-28 ~3:57pm EDT: Emmanuel installed v0.1-snapshot with the e75s fix on Pixel 9 (commit 3ec1122) and reports 'no optimistic user bubble' AND doubled messages still occur. My earlier hypothesis that 9pfm/b2u1/pv4y were observation artifacts of e75s was WRONG. Reopening for genuine investigation.

Code review post-confirmation:
- sendMessageViaClientMode (AdminChatViewModel:1009-1120) does write the optimistic bubble in BOTH branches:
  - known-conv (priorConversationId != null): timelineRepository.appendClientModeLocal(convId, text) at line 1071
  - fresh-route (priorConversationId == null): clientModeMessages = nextMessages + _uiState.messages = nextMessages.toImmutableList() at lines 1107-1109
- For both, isStreaming/isAgentTyping are set true BEFORE the bubble write.

So the bubble IS being written. Hypotheses for why it doesn't appear:
1. Observer race: startTimelineObserver fires immediately after appendClientModeLocal (line 1095), and something in the observer flow (lines 2095-2166) is *replacing* messages with an old/stale snapshot that doesn't contain the local.
2. Migration block (lines 1217-1340) clobbers _uiState.messages on the first chunk before the user can see the optimistic state.
3. ChatScreen reverseLayout LazyColumn never scrolls to expose the new bubble (less likely — would still appear after scroll).

Next session must: (a) tail logcat during a real repro and capture the appendClientModeLocal call site, the resulting timeline state, and the observer's response to confirm where the bubble disappears.
Programmatic regression test added in commit dc6c060: 'fresh route client mode shows optimistic user bubble before any chunk arrives'. Test PASSES — the VM logic correctly writes the user bubble to _uiState.messages BEFORE the first stream chunk arrives, with isStreaming/isAgentTyping=true. So the in-the-wild missing-bubble symptom is NOT in sendMessageViaClientMode itself.

E2E UI repro was attempted but BLOCKED: device 4B091FDAP002XG screen is locked behind PIN (AlternateBouncerView), no automation creds available. Need either:
1. Device PIN documented in the repo so adb can unlock and drive the UI, OR
2. A Compose UI test (ComposeRule) that mounts ChatScreen + AdminChatViewModel and asserts the user bubble appears, OR
3. Emmanuel running the repro manually and capturing logcat with AdminChatViewModel/TimelineSync/SseParser tags.

Next investigation must shift from VM logic → render path. Suspects:
- ChatScreen reverseLayout LazyColumn: messages emitted but not scrolled into view.
- Compose recomposition skip: collectAsStateWithLifecycle dropping intermediate emissions because the list is structurally equal in some edge.
- Route-recreation: 'New Conversation' nav recreates the VM mid-send, the new VM instance has empty messages and the old VM's writes are orphaned.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Client Mode: 'New Conversation' renders agent system_message before first send
description:
When the user taps the agent name → opens the conversation menu → selects 'New Conversation' and sends a message, the resulting chat view shows leaked history alongside the user's prompt and the agent reply. Investigation against the Letta server (see acceptance evidence) confirms a freshly-created conversation already contains 1 `system_message` (the agent's base instructions) populated into `in_context_message_ids` at create time. The Android client is fetching `GET /api/v1/conversations/:id/messages` after creating the conv and rendering whatever comes back, which includes that system blob.

Related (already fixed lettabot-side): `lettabot-y4j` — system-reminder envelope leak via the same proxy. That one was a true server/proxy bug. THIS bug is purely client-side and should NOT be patched in the lettabot proxy (filtering would mask history-reload semantics for other clients e.g. Debug mode).

Symptom (paraphrased from Emmanuel, 2026-04-28):
> 'I am in client mode, tap on agent name, select new conversation, send a message. There is no visual feedback. After a while my message pops in along with miscellaneous message history in this supposedly new conversation, plus the agent response.'

Doubled response is no longer happening (post-y4j fix). Remaining issue is the spurious history.
design:
## Investigation evidence (lettabot side, 2026-04-28)

Direct probe against `http://192.168.50.90:8289`:

```
POST /v1/conversations?agent_id=agent-54b6e7c9-...
→ creates conv-dba6c6c0-...
GET  /v1/conversations/conv-dba6c6c0-.../messages
→ returns 1 message:
  message_type=system_message
  content='<base_instructions>You are a helpful self-improving agent...'
```

`in_context_message_ids` on the fresh conv: `['message-880de551-...']` — confirms Letta itself seeds the conv with the agent's base prompt as a system message.

Distinct `message_type` values observed across this agent's conversations:
- `user_message`
- `assistant_message`
- `system_message`              ← never render in chat
- `tool_return_message`         ← Interactive mode only
- `approval_request_message`    ← never render in chat
- `approval_response_message`   ← never render in chat

## Recommended approach

**Option A (preferred): defer conv creation until first send.**

Pros: zero useless network calls, matches user mental model ('the conv doesn't exist until I commit to it'), avoids orphan empty convs in the agent's conversation list.
Cons: needs a session-state intermediate ('pending new conv') in `AdminChatViewModel`.

**Option B (cheaper): skip `GET /messages` on a freshly-created conv.**

After `POST /conversations` returns, set the chat view's message list to `[]` and don't call `GET /messages` at all. Only call it when the user reopens the conv via the conversation list. This requires tracking 'is this a brand-new conv from this session'.

**Belt-and-suspenders:** regardless of A or B, add a client-side render filter on `message_type` for the chat view (debug/interactive views can opt back in).

## Files likely involved (letta-mobile)

- `AdminChatViewModel.kt` — chat state, conv selection
- `ClientModeChatSender.kt` — send flow
- Wherever `GET /api/v1/conversations/:id/messages` is wired

## Out of scope (separate beads)

- No optimistic user echo on send → file separately
- No thinking/typing indicator → file separately
- User message arriving delayed alongside agent reply → file separately
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping 'New Conversation' and sending the first message MUST result in a chat view containing only the user's message and the agent's reply.
2. No `system_message` content is rendered.
3. No `approval_request_message` or `approval_response_message` content is rendered.
4. Two acceptable implementation strategies:
   (a) Defer `POST /conversations` until first user send, and never call `GET /messages` for unsent convs; OR
   (b) On entering a new (unsent) conv, skip the `GET /messages` round-trip entirely and start with an empty list.
5. Existing conversations (with prior user/assistant exchanges) MUST still hydrate correctly when reopened — only the 'brand new, never sent' path changes.
6. Unit/instrumentation test verifies that the chat list never contains a message whose `message_type` is `system_message`, `approval_request_message`, or `approval_response_message` regardless of code path.
notes:
Fixed in commit 3ec1122 on branch feat/flk1-keyed-prefix-blocks. Drop SYSTEM events at the chat-UI projection (TimelineEventToUiMessage) — both Confirmed and Local branches return null. Tests added to TimelineEventToUiMessageTest. Build + unit tests pass.

Other surfaces (Debug tab, tooling) observe the timeline directly and are unaffected.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: [migration] Map existing bound-agent chats → Letta conversations
description:
One-shot migration on app upgrade for users with existing chat history under the old bound-agent model.

For each existing chat:
- Read old (botId, agentId) and any local message history
- Call POST {lettabot}/api/v1/conversations?agent_id=X to mint a Letta conversation
- Store returned conv-xxx on the chat row
- Optional: replay local history into Letta via POST messages (or just abandon old history if continuity isn't critical)
- After migration: delete legacy BotConfig.agentId field, delete old per-bot conversation tracking

Edge cases:
- Old BotConfig.agentId null/invalid → mark chat 'needs agent selection', show picker on next open
- Multiple bots configured (legacy jo2p era) → pick most-recently-used for that chat

Acceptance: existing user opens app post-upgrade, all prior chats render with correct agent and continue to work.
notes:
Audited persistence surface for legacy bound-agent state.

Findings: there's nothing to migrate that w2hx.4 didn't already handle.

1. Persisted bound-agent state inventory:
   - BotConfig.agentId — already migrated v1→v2 in BotConfigStore.migrateBotConfigV1ToV2 (commit 26e0f1f). Promoted to heartbeat_agent_id when heartbeat enabled, dropped otherwise. Tests in BotConfigStoreMigrationTest.
   - SettingsRepository keys: FAVORITE_AGENT_ID, ADMIN_AGENT_ID, PINNED_AGENT_IDS, PINNED_CONVERSATION_IDS — these are user prefs (favorites, pins), not chat bindings. They stay as-is; no chat continuity affected.
   - PendingLocalEntity (Room) — already keyed on conversationId, not agentId. Nothing to migrate.
   - AgentEntity — agent metadata cache; agent_id is the natural key. Not bound-agent state.
   - There is NO Chat or ChatRow database entity (per w2hx.5 close note). Chats are nav routes keyed on (agentId, conversationId?). Server-side conversation list (GET /v1/conversations via w2hx.11 proxy) is the source of truth.

2. In-memory bound-agent state — already removed:
   - ConversationManager singleton (the activeConversationIds[agentId] map) — deleted in w2hx.6 (commit 9fa8a11).
   - forceNew plumbing — removed in w2hx.7 (commit a28adc2).
   - WS receive routing — now keyed on conversation_id, not agent (w2hx.8, commit 89b1440).
   - Per-VM activeConversationId is @Volatile and rebuilt from nav route args + most-recent-conversation lookup on each VM init.

3. The bead description's per-chat migration steps (mint conv per chat, replay history, etc.) presupposed a Chat database entity that this app doesn't have. The Letta server already owns conversation history; on first launch post-upgrade, AllConversationsRepository hydrates from GET /v1/conversations and lists all server-side conversations grouped by agent. No client-side replay needed.

Conclusion: closing as accepted-as-design. Migration of the only persisted bound-agent surface (BotConfig.agentId) shipped in w2hx.4. The heartbeat_agent_id continues to function as before — it's per-agent scheduled work, not interactive chat. Existing user opens app post-upgrade: prior bot configs migrate transparently, server-side conversations list as before via the proxy, and chats continue to work because routing is now conversation_id-keyed end-to-end.
close_reason: Closed accepted-as-design. Audit (see note) confirms the only persisted bound-agent state was BotConfig.agentId, already migrated in w2hx.4 via migrateBotConfigV1ToV2 (heartbeat_agent_id promotion + drop). Per w2hx.5 there is no Chat database entity — chats are nav routes; server-side conversations list hydrates from GET /v1/conversations via the w2hx.11 proxy. Per w2hx.6/.7/.8, all in-memory bound-agent state is gone. No further migration code required.

title: [bug] Markdown tables not rendering on device (regression after flk.1 keyed prefix blocks)
description:
Tables in assistant messages render as raw pipe-delimited text instead of a table grid. Confirmed on Pixel 2 XL with debug build installed at ~22:11 UTC 2026-04-26.

Most likely culprit: bf5572a 'feat(chat): keyed per-paragraph prefix blocks for streaming markdown [flk.1]' (today 17:35 EDT). That commit introduced splitMarkdownBlocks which is *supposed* to keep complete GFM tables atomic (header+separator+rows in a single block) but may be:
- shredding tables across blocks (each row in its own MarkdownText, mikepenz can't restitch)
- emitting only the header+separator without the rows (table looks like 2 lines of text)
- failing the table-close detector so the table never gets committed at all
- breaking on tables that arrive in the FINAL message (no streaming, full snapshot)

Reproduce: any assistant response containing a GFM table, e.g. ask the agent to 'show a 3-column table comparing Kotlin/Swift/Dart'.

Pre-flk.1 behavior (recent c8of/c8of.6): tables rendered correctly progressively. Regression window is ~last 4 hours.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tables render as a grid (column borders, aligned cells) on Pixel 2 XL debug build
- Both streaming (frame-by-frame) and final-snapshot tables render correctly
- splitMarkdownBlocks unit test added covering: (a) table in middle of prefix, (b) table at end of prefix mid-stream, (c) table as the entire prefix, (d) two consecutive tables
- Manual smoke: ask the agent for a markdown table, screenshot shows grid not raw pipes
notes: Tightened scroll recycle fix after codebase search: StreamingMarkdownText now keys its displayed state by (isStreaming, non-streaming text) so hydrated/recycled content initializes to the exact current text during composition. The 50ms polling LaunchedEffect is now gated to run only when isStreaming=true, eliminating any non-streaming reveal/replay path. Verified targeted streaming markdown tests, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, and installRootDebug on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: wscat smoke test of WS gateway against staging (default + progressive modes)
description:
Confirm the server-side gate at src/api/ws-gateway.ts:454-461 actually drops 'running' tool_call snapshots for non-opted-in connections, and still emits them when ?progressive_tool_calls=1 is present. This validates the gateway end-to-end before we trust the mobile build's behavior depends on the opt-in.

Run after issue letta-mobile-wgi8 (URL grep) is done.

## Context
Verification commands (replace LETTABOT_API_KEY):
# Default mode (Matrix-shape)
wscat -c 'ws://staging-host:8080/api/v1/agent-gateway' -H 'X-Api-Key: $LETTABOT_API_KEY'
# Then paste:
{"type":"session_start","agent_id":"<agent-id>"}
{"type":"message","content":"Read /etc/hostname","request_id":"smoke-1"}

# Progressive mode (mobile-shape)
wscat -c 'ws://staging-host:8080/api/v1/agent-gateway?progressive_tool_calls=1' -H 'X-Api-Key: $LETTABOT_API_KEY'
# Same paste

Test fixtures: /opt/stacks/lettabot/src/api/ws-gateway.e2e.test.ts 'flag matrix' describe block.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Connect via wscat WITHOUT ?progressive_tool_calls=1, send a session_start + a message that triggers a Read tool call, count tool_call frames for that tool_call_id. Expected: exactly 1 frame with status: 'completed'.
2. Connect via wscat WITH ?progressive_tool_calls=1, repeat. Expected: 2+ frames (running snapshots + 1 terminal completed).
3. If default mode emits >1 tool_call frame per id, the gate is broken — file back to lettabot side.
4. If progressive mode emits only 1 frame, the opt-in is broken — file back to lettabot side.
5. Capture frame counts in this issue's notes.
notes:
VERIFIED ✅

Smoke test against local lettabot gateway (ws://localhost:8407/api/v1/agent-gateway, X-Api-Key from /opt/stacks/lettabot/lettabot-api.json). Agent: agent-54b6e7c9-... (PM - letta-mobile, has Read tool). Test script: /tmp/wshk-smoke.js (uses Node + lettabot's bundled ws module, no wscat needed).

**Mode A — DEFAULT (no opt-in, Matrix-shape):**
```
tool_call_id=toolu_01VFsKbTAjrH3xtM1XCF7f1C  frames=1  statuses=completed  tool=Read
```
Exactly 1 tool_call frame per id, status='completed'. ✓

**Mode B — PROGRESSIVE (?progressive_tool_calls=1, mobile-shape):**
```
tool_call_id=toolu_01NY8hoF5D3kFhcE8iKmiuvT  frames=2  statuses=running,completed  tool=Read
```
2 frames per id (1 running snapshot + 1 terminal completed). ✓

The gate at src/api/ws-gateway.ts:454-461 is dropping running snapshots correctly for non-opted-in clients, and emitting them when the query param is present. Per-run nonces in the prompt force the agent to actually invoke the tool (otherwise it answers from cache and you get zero tool_call frames — added a guard for that to the verdict logic).

**Note on progressive frame count:** /etc/os-release produced only 1 'running' snapshot before the terminal frame, because the partial-JSON parser only emits when the buffer reaches a parseable boundary. With longer tool arg payloads you'd see more snapshots. The contract guarantee 'at least one running before terminal completed' is satisfied — that's the floor.

Test artifact left at /tmp/wshk-smoke.js for re-runs.

title: [client-mode][test] Compose UI test — progressive tool_call card recomposes from streamed WS frames
description:
Mirror counterpart to lettabot-uww.7 (server-side ws-gateway e2e harness).
