title: Mobile tool-result rendering: Bash/Edit/etc. collapse to opaque 'Approved' pills instead of rich expandable cards
description:
Desktop/ADE renders tool results as expandable cards with tool name ('Tool returned Bash'), approval label, command, stdout preview, and timing chips (requires_approval, end_turn, durations). Mobile collapses all of this to a stack of anonymous 'Approved' pills with zero context — no tool name, no command, no output preview, no timing.

Reference: user screenshots on 2026-04-19 showing desktop rendering vs phone rendering of the same turn (bd update call on letta-mobile-mge5.24).

Requirements:
- Surface tool name at card level (e.g. 'Tool returned Bash', 'Tool returned Edit').
- Show approval status + human label (e.g. 'Approved request to run tool' + reason) as a subheader.
- Show the command / tool input (collapsed preview, tappable to expand).
- Show stdout / tool output (collapsed preview, tappable to expand).
- Show timing + badges (requires_approval, end_turn, durations).
- Match ADE visual hierarchy reasonably; doesn't need pixel parity but info parity.

Out of scope: 
- Redesigning agent chat bubble colors.
- Streaming partial tool output (separate concern).

title: image attachments not rendering in mobile client — server persists them, mobile drops them on hydrate
description:
Emmanuel 2026-04-19 09:36 EDT: 'I sent an image earlier, the agent received it but it did not remain in the chatlog.'

Diagnosis (2026-04-19 13:40 UTC):
- Posted a probe user_message with image content to the agent's conversation via REST.
- The agent processed it (saw run start, assistant reply, tool calls).
- Subsequent listConversationMessages with limit=20 desc returned ZERO user_message records carrying image content. The probe otid never appears in the conversation log either.
- Confirmed by scanning 200 recent agent messages: ZERO contain image parts. 2 user_messages exist; both are text-only.

Conclusion: the Letta server does NOT persist user messages with non-text content. The agent receives + processes the image, replies, but the user-side bubble has no server record to reconcile against. The phone shows the Local SENDING/SENT bubble until the process dies, then it's gone forever.

Fix paths:
1. SERVER (preferred but out-of-scope here): Letta should persist user messages with image content. Track separately.
2. CLIENT (this bead): persist Local events with attachments to a small Room table keyed by (conversationId, otid). On hydrate, re-inject any persisted Local that the server hasn't echoed back. This keeps the user's image bubble across app restarts. Acceptance:
   - Send image, force-stop app, reopen → image bubble still in chat
   - Send text-only → no extra DB row written
   - Server eventually returns the user message → drop the persisted Local row

Workaround for now: image bubble survives until process death (already implemented via Local-preserving hydrate). Persistence is the real fix.
close_reason: Image attachments rendering — already handled. Confirmed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: 2026-04-19 Emmanuel: pending. Don't want to modify Letta server-side code, and not worth hacking a client-side Room persistence layer around a server-side gap. Disappearing images on process death is an accepted limitation for now. Revisit if/when Letta server persists non-text user message content, or if user demand makes the client workaround worthwhile. Priority dropped P1→P3.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: 2026-04-19 Emmanuel: DIAGNOSIS WAS WRONG. Desktop/ADE view DOES show the image user-message persisted in the conversation transcript. So the server is persisting image content after all. The bug is client-side: mobile is not rendering/hydrating user-message image attachments from server history. Re-scoping this bead to mobile rendering/hydration. Priority back to P1.

title: SSE /stream doesn't emit tool_return frames on this server — only approval_request/assistant
description:
Diagnosis (2026-04-19 02:01 EDT): a 30-second capture of the phone's SSE subscription during 5 external-run cycles showed 26 approval_request, 22 assistant_message, 0 tool_return frames. REST /messages showed tool_return_messages ARE stored. stop_reason='requires_approval' on every run — tool execution is pending approval. When approval is granted elsewhere (server-side auto-approval, another client), the server apparently advances the run but does NOT emit tool_return on the resume-stream endpoint — only stores it.

Implication: the mge5.19 attachment logic won't flip live; only hydrate will pick it up.

Workaround paths:
1. Periodic hydrate refresh (every N seconds when a decided-pending approval exists)
2. Subscribe to ApprovalResponseMessages at a different endpoint (e.g. polling /messages?after=X)
3. Trigger a reconcile when a stop_reason='requires_approval' is later followed by any new assistant_message for the same run_id (indicating it resumed server-side)

Also found: the stored approval_response_message records have approval_request_id=null and tool_call_id=null (only approvals[] is populated, with per-call toolCallId inside). Our decided-by-response path depends on top-level approval_request_id and therefore only matches a subset of responses. Causal fallback via tool_return still works when tool_return is present in the source (currently: REST-only).

Also surfaced: cleanup_error 'name "uuid" is not defined' on run finalization — server-side Python bug unrelated to mobile, but may be suppressing trailing frames.
notes: Shipped applyReturnsAndResponsesFromSnapshot() helper called after reconcileForExternalRun, plus stop_reason=requires_approval-triggered delayed reconcile (1.5s + 5s). This compensates for the server's SSE omitting tool_return/approval_response frames by polling REST storage shortly after the approval checkpoint.

title: merge tool_return into tool_call bubble; show actual command; collapsible output
description:
Emmanuel 2026-04-19 01:30 EDT: 'tool output and bash command should be a single field. Ideally where the tool output is collapsible. The bash command is expandable.'

Three-part change:

1. ATTACH ToolReturnMessage to its invoking TOOL_CALL event instead of rendering as a separate bubble.
   - Added toolReturnContent: String? and toolReturnIsError: Boolean to TimelineEvent.Confirmed.
   - ingestStreamEvent: when a ToolReturnMessage arrives, match on toolCallId → copy(approvalDecided=true, toolReturnContent=body, toolReturnIsError=...) → do NOT append a standalone event.
   - hydrate: index tool_returns by tool_call_id and pre-merge into TOOL_CALL events during conversion. Drops standalone TOOL_RETURN events from the snapshot.
   - VM: standalone TOOL_RETURN -> returns null (shouldn't happen anymore, but defensive).

2. SURFACE THE COMMAND as the primary header label.
   - ToolDisplayRegistry.resolve now prefers the extracted detail (Bash command, file path, search query, etc.) as the header label. Falls back to the canned verb ('Running command') only when extraction fails.
   - Detail line removed from below the label (no longer needed — the label IS the command).

3. COLLAPSIBLE OUTPUT inside the expanded card.
   - New inner collapse row: 'Output' label + line count + chevron. Tap toggles resultExpanded.
   - Collapsed default: maxLines=2 preview with ellipsis.
   - Expanded: maxLines=Int.MAX_VALUE, animateContentSize for smoothness.
   - Error path (isErr=true or status='error') styles text in error color and labels 'Error' instead of 'Output'.

Net UX: a single card per tool use showing '⚡ echo a' in the header. Tap once → Arguments + Output preview. Tap Output → full result.
notes: Shipped merge + command-label + collapsible output.

title: tool_return implies approval was granted — flip buttons off causally
description:
Emmanuel's observation 2026-04-19 01:15 EDT: 'If there's a tool output that means the tool was obviously running. If the tools run that means something gave it approvals and just no need to show the approval modals.'

Causal heuristic: the presence of a ToolReturnMessage for a given tool_call_id proves the tool executed, which means any approval gate must have been cleared. This works regardless of whether we successfully saw the approval_response frame, whether the server's discriminator matches our polymorphic deserializer, or whether the response was lost to a dropped SSE connection.

Added two paths:
1. ingestStreamEvent: when a ToolReturnMessage arrives, find the Confirmed event whose toolCalls contain this toolCallId and flip approvalDecided=true (in addition to continuing normal ingest of the return itself).
2. hydrate: build a set of returnedToolCallIds from the snapshot; any approval request whose tool_call_id is in that set is marked decided during state construction.

This complements letta-mobile-mge5.15 (explicit ApprovalResponseMessage handling) and letta-mobile-mge5.16 (discriminator fix). Belt and suspenders — whichever signal arrives first wins.
notes: Shipped both ingest and hydrate paths.

title: auto-approved tool calls still show approve/reject buttons
description:
Symptom (Emmanuel 2026-04-19 01:05 EDT): 'they all still have reject or approve visible though they were all autoapproved'. After mge5.14 surfaced tool-call bubbles for ApprovalRequestMessages, every one showed the approve/reject UI even when the server had already auto-approved them.

Root cause: ApprovalResponseMessage has always fallen into the 'else -> return null' branch of toTimelineEvent, so the phone never learned that a decision was recorded. The UI showed approve/reject because the ApprovalRequest event stood in isolation.

Fix:
- Added approvalDecided: Boolean to TimelineEvent.Confirmed.
- ingestStreamEvent now has a dedicated branch for ApprovalResponseMessage: locates the matching Confirmed event by approvalRequestId and flips approvalDecided=true via replaceByServerId. No bubble is created for the response itself.
- hydrate() scans the server response for ApprovalResponseMessages and seeds approvalDecided for any requests that already have responses, so past-decided approvals load correctly on app open.
- AdminChatViewModel sets UiMessage.approvalRequest=null and UiMessage.approvalResponse=Approved when decided, so the bubble renders the 'Approved' state instead of active buttons.
notes: Shipped. ApprovalResponseMessage is now ingested as an in-place decided-flag flip rather than a separate bubble.

title: external-run reconcile must block first frame so user_message sorts above reply
description: Follow-up to letta-mobile-mge5.11. The async (scope.launch) reconcile fired AFTER assistant frames had already been ingested, so the user_message bubble appeared BELOW the assistant reply in the timeline. Made the reconcile synchronous on the FIRST frame of an unseen run_id (blocking the SSE collector for ~200ms while we GET messages). Subsequent frames for the same run skip the reconcile via seenRunIds.add()=false. Tradeoff: streaming for that one frame is delayed by the reconcile latency, but the result is a correctly-ordered timeline (user above, assistant below).
notes: Done.

title: CI: make verify-sync target + sync-drift regression gate
description:
## Goal

Productionize the verification flow we used to prove the resume-stream approach:

```
make verify-sync
```

Runs:
1. `adb devices` sanity — ensure a device is connected.
2. `letta-cli sync-drift --agent $AGENT_ID --conversation $CONV_ID --watch --iterations 6 --interval 10`.
3. Assert every sample reports HEALTHY (drift < 30s, unprocessed = 0).

Exit code non-zero on any BROKEN verdict.

Wire into our release checklist (`docs/RELEASE.md`) so nobody ships a build that regresses this. Non-blocking on CI for now (we don't have a device-farm runner) — manual step on the release checklist.

## Bonus

Add a `make verify-stream` target that runs `letta-cli stream-watch` for 60s against a conversation, triggers a test message from a second shell, and asserts at least 1 event was received. Pure end-to-end smoke test of the resume-stream mechanism itself (independent of device/app).

LABELS: P1, ci, regression-gate, sync-drift
close_reason: Shipped in 997e042 — Makefile verify-sync/verify-stream/verify-all targets + docs/RELEASE.md checklist. verify-sync asserts every sync-drift sample HEALTHY (6 × 10s). verify-stream asserts >=1 SSE event received. Both wired into docs/RELEASE.md as blocking gates. Smoke-tested against live server; correctly FAILs on any mechanism regression.

title: settings: resumeStreamEnabled feature flag + kill-switch UI
description:
## Goal

Gate the new subscriber behind a boolean in `SettingsRepository` so:

- Default ON for debug builds (we dogfood).
- Default OFF for release builds until we soak for a week.
- User-visible toggle in the debug settings screen so we can flip it remotely via settings-sync without a rebuild.

## Implementation

- Add `resumeStreamEnabled: Boolean` to the settings data class (DataStore).
- Default per BuildConfig.DEBUG.
- Surface in the existing debug settings composable with a Switch + subtitle "Subscribe to live SSE streams for open conversations. Disables to save battery at cost of realtime updates."

## Rollout plan

1. Land with flag=on in debug. Soak 3 days on Emmanuel's device.
2. If no regressions, flip release default to on.
3. Keep the switch — users on constrained networks / battery-sensitive use cases can disable.

LABELS: P1, settings, feature-flag
close_reason: Flag removed — resume-stream is default behavior now. Confirmed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20

title: Observability: push-delivery telemetry + sync-drift CI gate
description:
## Goal

We're replacing a polling system (trivially measurable — bytes/second) with a push system (harder to observe). Need telemetry to prove push delivery is actually happening and to alert us the moment it stops.

## Telemetry points

1. **Server side (lettabot receiver):**
   - counter: `letta_webhook_received_total{agent_id, event_type, result}` where result = accepted|hmac_fail|unknown_conv|relay_fail.
   - histogram: `letta_webhook_to_matrix_ms` (time from webhook arrival to Matrix emit).
   - gauge: `letta_webhook_configured_agents` (how many agents have enabled webhooks pointing to us).

2. **Client side (Android):**
   - `Telemetry.event("TimelinePush", "received", conversationId, messageType, deliveryLatencyMs)`
   - `Telemetry.event("TimelinePush", "duplicate", conversationId)` (caught by dedupe — informational, not error)
   - `Telemetry.event("TimelinePush", "unknownConv", conversationId)` (event for a conv we don't have a loop for — normal when background)

## Latency budget

- Server → webhook receiver: < 500ms p95.
- Receiver → Matrix emit: < 200ms p95.
- Matrix → phone screen paint: < 1s p95 (dependent on Matrix client's sync cadence).
- End-to-end: < 2s p95.

## sync-drift as a CI gate

Promote the `letta-cli sync-drift` CLI added during f7yj triage to a first-class regression gate:

- Makefile target `make verify-sync` that runs `sync-drift --watch --iterations 6 --interval 10` against a connected device + dev server.
- Asserts every sample reports HEALTHY.
- Wire into release checklist so we can't ship a build that regresses this.

## Alerts

- Prometheus rule: `letta_webhook_received_total` rate < 0.1/min for 30 min → warning (no recent activity OR delivery dead).
- `letta_webhook_to_matrix_ms_p95 > 1s` for 5 min → warning.

## Acceptance

- Grafana dashboard shows live push delivery metrics.
- `make verify-sync` green on main.
- Alert fires in a simulated outage test.

LABELS: P1, observability, telemetry, ci
notes: Superseded by letta-mobile-resume-stream-epic (see replacement). The webhook+matrix bridge approach is abandoned in favor of the simpler, verified resume-stream pattern (POST /v1/conversations/{id}/stream). No external infra, no secrets, no bridge. Verified empirically 2026-04-18 21:47 via letta-cli stream-watch.

title: Auto-configure Letta webhook per agent on first launch (with rotation support)
description:
## Goal

Turn webhook delivery on automatically for every agent the mobile app (or desktop CLI, or any user action) interacts with. Without this, the push plumbing exists but no payloads flow because no agent has `enabled: true`.

## Trigger points

- On first successful `getAgent` call for an agent on a given lettabot install: check webhook config via `GET /v1/agents/{id}/webhook`; if `enabled: false`, configure it to point to our lettabot receiver.
- On lettabot startup, reconcile: list all agents, ensure each has webhook pointing to us with current secret.

## Secret handling

- Generate a random 32-byte secret per agent, persist in lettabot's sqlite.
- Send `PUT /v1/agents/{id}/webhook` with `{url, secret, events:["agent.message.sent"], enabled:true}`.
- Receiver looks up secret by agent_id extracted from webhook path.

## Rotation

- New command `lettabot webhook rotate --agent <id>` generates a new secret, updates server, keeps old secret valid for 5 min window for in-flight deliveries.

## Edge cases

- Server restart: webhooks persist server-side.
- lettabot restart: secrets persist in sqlite. No reconfig needed.
- Multi-tenant future: webhooks are per-agent, not per-user — fine for now (single-tenant).

## Acceptance

- First cold launch of mobile app, open any conversation: within 5s, `GET /v1/agents/{id}/webhook` returns `enabled: true` with our receiver URL.
- Post from CLI after that: message appears on phone as expected.
- `lettabot webhook list` prints every agent-id and its current webhook state.

LABELS: P1, lettabot, webhooks, bootstrap
notes: Superseded by letta-mobile-resume-stream-epic (see replacement). The webhook+matrix bridge approach is abandoned in favor of the simpler, verified resume-stream pattern (POST /v1/conversations/{id}/stream). No external infra, no secrets, no bridge. Verified empirically 2026-04-18 21:47 via letta-cli stream-watch.

title: Timeline observer latches onto first conversation — switching convs silently keeps stale subscription
description:
## Symptom

Open the app, navigate into any agent conversation (say conv-A). Navigate to a DIFFERENT conversation (conv-B). The message list for conv-B stays frozen at whatever was there on entry — no new messages land, no TimelineSync traffic is emitted, even though:

- The server has the newer messages (verified via `GET /v1/agents/{id}/messages?limit=5`).
- The conversations-list screen shows conv-B as "active 2 min ago" (that tile pulls from a separate endpoint).
- The app is foregrounded, the chat screen is open, the process is alive and healthy (no coroutine cancellation, no fatal errors).

Reproduced live 2026-04-18 ~20:25 EDT against agent-54b6e7c9-… on conv-1598043a-… . Zero HTTP traffic to the conversation while the screen was open for 30+ seconds.

## Root cause

`AdminChatViewModel.startTimelineObserver(conversationId)` (file: `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`, line 785–787) guards with:

```kotlin
private fun startTimelineObserver(conversationId: String) {
    if (timelineObserverJob?.isActive == true) return
    timelineObserverJob = viewModelScope.launch { … }
}
```

The guard checks "are we observing *anything*" but NOT "are we observing the conversation we were just asked to observe." So after the first `startTimelineObserver` runs, any subsequent switch is a silent no-op. The loop started for conv-A keeps emitting (into now-unread `_uiState.messages` for conv-B's screen? no — it updates the VM's shared `_uiState`, which is visible, but that state is for conv-A, not B). For conv-B specifically, `TimelineRepository.getOrCreate(convB)` is never called, so no per-conv sync loop starts, no GETs fire.

Also ungated: the sibling `hydrateSignal` job launched at :798 against `timelineRepository.getOrCreate(conversationId)` — even if we fix the main guard, that job also needs to be cancelled + restarted on conversation switch or we leak hydrate-signal subscriptions.

## Fix sketch

```kotlin
private var observerConversationId: String? = null
private fun startTimelineObserver(conversationId: String) {
    if (observerConversationId == conversationId && timelineObserverJob?.isActive == true) return
    timelineObserverJob?.cancel()
    hydrateSignalJob?.cancel()
    observerConversationId = conversationId
    timelineObserverJob = viewModelScope.launch { … }
}
```

Introduce a `hydrateSignalJob` field, cancel both on conv switch, set `observerConversationId` before launching.

## Reproduction

1. Install debug build, launch app, land on home/dashboard.
2. Open conversation A (e.g. any agent with recent messages).
3. Tap the conversation picker, switch to conversation B.
4. On another machine, POST a new message into conv-B (e.g. send via this agent from Letta Code CLI or curl `/v1/agents/{id}/messages/stream`).
5. Server has the new message; phone's screen does not refresh, no `TimelineSync` log events appear for conv-B.
6. Workaround: force-stop + relaunch. Cold-open starts the observer for the currently-selected conversation.

## Acceptance

- Switching conversations while the app is running causes `startTimelineObserver` to cancel the previous observer and start a fresh one for the new conversation id.
- The `hydrateSignal` collector is also cancelled + restarted on switch.
- New messages arriving server-side for the currently-selected conversation appear in the UI within the normal reconcile window without force-stopping the app.
- Regression test: `AdminChatViewModelTest` — verify calling `startTimelineObserver(convA)` then `startTimelineObserver(convB)` results in cancelling the first job and a `timelineRepository.observe(convB)` / `.getOrCreate(convB)` invocation.

## Related

- Bead `a44b` (P0 epic): "Message Sync Architecture Migration — Matrix-inspired rewrite" — this is a sub-bug in the current TimelineSync path, will be subsumed by that epic but worth fixing now since a44b is far from landing.
- Bead `jn4b" / `cm9a` (multimodal path) are unaffected — the image send flow goes through `sendMessage` directly, not through the observer.

LABELS: P1, admin-chat, timeline-sync, bug
notes:
## 2026-04-18 20:50 EDT — CLI-verified the fix is necessary but NOT sufficient

Built a programmatic diagnostic (`letta-cli sync-drift`, see below) to replace the need for eyeballing the phone. It reads the client's persisted cursor via `adb run-as` (`channel_sync_state.xml`) and diffs against the server's latest message.

After force-stop + fresh launch of the nw2e-patched build, drift is stuck at 671s and growing — **zero cursor advancement across 3 samples over 30s**. So the conversation-switch observer-rebind bug fixed by nw2e is real, but there's a second, deeper bug: **sync loops don't start AT ALL on cold launch** (both ChannelHeartbeatSync and TimelineSyncLoop dead).

Filed as letta-mobile-f7yj (P0). This nw2e fix stays landed — it's easy to repro the observer-latching symptom by switching conversations mid-session even when cold-start works — but closing the loop on the user's stale-chat report requires f7yj too.

## CLI diagnostic added

`letta-cli sync-drift --agent <id> --conversation <id> [--watch]`

Replaces manual phone inspection with an objective verdict (HEALTHY / STALE / BROKEN) based on server-latest vs client-cursor drift + unprocessed message count. Now we can regression-test sync bugs programmatically.

title: Multimodal image send wire-format mismatch: client sends OpenAI image_url, Letta server expects ImageContent.source.base64
description:
## Symptom
On dev device (192.168.50.202:5555) at 2026-04-18 19:34:47, sending an image attachment fails server-side with HTTP validation error:

```
detail: [{
  type: 'string_type',
  loc: ('body','messages',0,'function-after[generate_otid_if_missing(), MessageCreate]','content','str'),
  msg: 'Input should be a valid string',
  input: [{type:'text', text:'…'}, {type:'image_url', image_url:{url:'data:image/jpeg;base64,…'}}]
}]
```

The picker → addAttachment → composer → timeline send chain works perfectly (verified, jng2 closed). The failure is purely a wire-format mismatch between what the client emits and what the Letta server's MessageCreate schema accepts.

## Root cause (definitive)
File: `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/MessageContentPart.kt` lines 53–60.

The client emits OpenAI's `ChatCompletionContentPartImageParam` shape:
```json
{"type":"image_url", "image_url":{"url":"data:image/jpeg;base64,<…>"}}
```

But Letta server 0.16.7 (running at http://192.168.50.90:8289) defines `LettaMessageContentUnion` with a discriminator that ONLY allows: text, image, tool_call, tool_return, reasoning, redacted_reasoning, omitted_reasoning. There is NO `image_url` variant. The doc-comment in MessageContentPart.kt (lines 13–20) is incorrect — it claims the wire format mirrors OpenAI's parts, but the server requires Letta's own ImageContent shape.

The server's `ImageContent` schema (verified via OpenAPI):
```json
{
  "type": "image",
  "source": {
    "type": "base64",
    "media_type": "image/jpeg",
    "data": "<raw base64, no data: prefix>"
  }
}
```

The `source` object is a discriminated union of Base64Image (type=base64), UrlImage (type=url), or LettaImage (type=letta, server-managed file ref).

## Fix
1. Rewrite `List<MessageContentPart>.toJsonArray()` in MessageContentPart.kt:
   - For Image parts emit `{type:'image', source:{type:'base64', media_type:<mediaType>, data:<base64>}}` instead of the current image_url shape.
   - Drop `toDataUrl()` from the wire path (server does not want the data: prefix; just raw base64).
2. Update the doc comment (lines 11–23) to reflect Letta's shape, not OpenAI's.
3. Update `MessageContentPartTest.kt` to assert the new shape.
4. Re-verify on dev device that send succeeds and assistant replies referencing the image.

## Acceptance
- Unit test in MessageContentPartTest passes for new shape.
- On-device send of (text + 1 jpeg) succeeds — `send.failed` no longer fires; assistant streams a reply that demonstrates it saw the image.
- jn4b verification can complete.

## Evidence
Full request body, server error, and stack trace captured in /tmp/attach-verify.log on triage host (commit hash for app build: HEAD of matrix-clean per android-compose installDebug at 2026-04-18 ~19:30 EDT).

## Server reference
- URL: http://192.168.50.90:8289
- Version: Letta 0.16.7
- Schema confirmed via openapi.json (LettaMessageContentUnion, ImageContent, Base64Image)
notes: VERIFIED FIXED on dev device 2026-04-18 ~20:05. User-confirmed: image attach + send + assistant reply now works end-to-end on the new wire format ({type:'image', source:{type:'base64', media_type, data}}). Server (Letta 0.16.7) accepts the payload — no more string_type validation error.

title: Sentry Performance: finalize transaction taxonomy (startup, chat sync, bot turn, composer→render)
description: Define and implement Sentry transactions for: app.startup (cold/warm/hot split), chat.sync_cycle, chat.send_message (end-to-end until first streamed token), bot.turn (LocalBotSession.process), composer.keystroke_to_render. Use ISpan for sub-operations so transactions are navigable in Sentry UI. Document taxonomy in docs/performance/sentry-taxonomy.md.

title: Startup budget: move non-critical init off main thread in LettaApplication
description: Audit LettaApplication.onCreate for main-thread work: Sentry init, Hilt eagerly-constructed singletons, Coil ImageLoader build, Timber trees, MessageApi warmup, Letta identity bootstrap. Move anything non-critical to App Startup `Initializer` components or a background coroutine. Define a main-thread budget (target: <80ms on Pixel 2 XL) and enforce via macrobench assertion.
notes:
Final close rationale Apr 21: code changes for o7ob.2.5 are complete and validated, and the remaining slow-floor verification blocker belongs to letta-mobile-ce2c rather than this bead.

Completed app-side work:
- moved ChannelHeartbeatScheduler.schedule() off LettaApplication main thread
- added Telemetry.measure("App", "onCreate.main") around synchronous startup work
- stopped app.startup transaction from waiting on deferred startup tasks before finishing
- updated StartupBenchmark to capture TraceSectionMetric for App/onCreate.main on cold startup runs
- added perf gate startup.oncreate_main.sum_ms <= 80ms using App/onCreate.mainSumMs in perf/baselines.json

Validation completed:
- :app:compileDebugKotlin
- :macrobenchmark:compileBenchmarkKotlin
- :app:testDebugUnitTest
- make lint-telemetry
- make verify-telemetry

Primary evidence (reference API 36 test device, USB-targeted benchmark):
- App/onCreate.mainSumMs median = 1.884ms
- timeToInitialDisplayMs median = 1473.665ms
- far below the 80ms main-thread budget — refactor achieved its goal with substantial headroom

Slow-floor verification status:
- Pixel 2 XL / Android 11 / API 30 verification was pursued exhaustively on the dev device and reproduces an upstream AndroidX Macrobenchmark/profileinstaller blocker outside Gradle
- benchmark target APK and macrobenchmark test APK both install manually
- target package com.letta.mobile.benchmark is installed and launchable
- benchmark target manifest includes androidx.profileinstaller.ProfileInstallReceiver and ProfileInstallerInitializer
- benchmark target APK contains assets/dexopt/baseline.prof and baseline.profm
- direct instrumentation run still fails in MacrobenchmarkScope.dropShaderCache() with "The DROP_SHADER_CACHE broadcast was not received"
- direct shell broadcasts to com.letta.mobile.benchmark/androidx.profileinstaller.ProfileInstallReceiver return result=0 instead of AndroidX-expected success code 14

Conclusion:
- o7ob.2.5 is complete and should close as done
- remaining Pixel 2 XL / API 30 profileinstaller handshake investigation is deferred to letta-mobile-ce2c, which now owns that upstream/device-specific blocker

title: ChatScreen recomposition audit — stability, @Stable/@Immutable, derivedStateOf, remember-lambda hoisting
description: Enable Compose compiler report (reportsDestination + metricsDestination). For every composable in chat/, classify params by stability. Mark data classes used in UI state with @Immutable (UiMessage, Attachment, ChatBackground, etc.) where safe. Audit derivedStateOf usage vs. raw .collectAsStateWithLifecycle. Hoist lambdas with remember where they cause param-drift recompositions. Re-run report and document recomposition count deltas in notes.
notes: Done in commit ce07491. Audited the chat tree via Compose compiler reports (already enabled at app/build.gradle.kts:156). Found 14/16 of the chat surface's user-visible composables had ≥1 unstable param, which made the entire subtree non-skippable on every recomposition tick. Root cause was a chain of unstable parameter types: ChatUiState missing @Immutable + raw List/Map fields in three nested UI-state types (ProjectBriefUiState.sections, ProjectBugReportUiState.recentReports, ProjectAgentsUiState.agents, ProjectBugReportDraft.tags/attachmentReferences); ChatBackground hierarchy missing @Immutable; MessageContentPart.Image/Text missing @Immutable; and four composable signatures still typed as raw List<T> instead of ImmutableList<T> (MessageToolCalls.toolCalls, MessageAttachmentsGrid.attachments, ChatComposer.pendingAttachments, AttachmentStrip.attachments). After the fix only 2/16 stay non-skippable and both are unavoidably-unstable due to the ViewModel param at the very top of the tree (recommended pattern per Compose docs — ViewModels carry mutable state internally and should not be @Stable). Downstream tree starting at ChatContent is now fully skippable. Verification: :app:compileReleaseKotlin --rerun-tasks emits the post-fix report; :app:testReleaseUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.* green. Did not migrate UiMessage.attachments / UiMessage.toolCalls to ImmutableList because that ripples through MessageMapper and every sync code-path; instead wrap once at the call-site inside remember() so the wrap doesn't reallocate per recomposition. Lambda-hoisting / derivedStateOf passes were unnecessary once the type-stability fixes were in — the Jank events I'd seen earlier in the session correlated with raw-List re-allocations, not lambda churn.

title: Generate and ship a Baseline Profile
description: Add :baselineprofile module (androidx.baselineprofile plugin), write a BaselineProfileGenerator that walks cold-start → chat list → open a chat → scroll → send a message. Wire `./gradlew :app:generateReleaseBaselineProfile`. Ensure generated profile is bundled in app/src/main/baseline-prof.txt and included in release APK. Expected: ~10–25% cold-start improvement.
notes: Completed Apr 21: generated a real Baseline Profile on the dev device (API 36) with :app:generateReleaseBaselineProfile after fixing producer-plugin wiring, moving the com.android.test generator into baselineprofile/src/main, reading androidx.benchmark.targetPackageName, adding dev-installable benchmark/nonMinifiedRelease variants, skipping POST_NOTIFICATIONS prompts for .benchmark builds, switching launch flow to home + explicit shell am start, and upgrading androidx.baselineprofile / benchmark-macro-junit4 from 1.3.4 to 1.4.1 for Android 16 pm dump-profiles compatibility. Generated source profile saved to android-compose/app/src/release/generated/baselineProfiles/baseline-prof.txt and contains app-specific com.letta.mobile entries including MainActivity and LettaApplication. Verified release packaging: app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk contains assets/dexopt/baseline.prof + baseline.profm and app/build/outputs/bundle/release/app-release.aab contains BUNDLE-METADATA/com.android.tools.build.profiles/baseline.prof + baseline.profm. Release bundle task :app:bundleRelease succeeded.

No jank frames triggered during idle smoke — frames were under 16ms budget, which is correct behavior.

Release twin is a no-op so main sources can install() unconditionally. LettaApplication.onCreate defers install to applicationScope(Default) inside runCatching.

title: Add :macrobenchmark module with startup + chat-scroll + composer benchmarks

Wire `./gradlew :macrobenchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest` to run on any connected device. Emit CSV results under build/outputs/connected_android_test_additional_output/ for CI consumption.

All three benchmarks (Startup, ScrollJank, ComposerTyping) now live in src/main/java/ where com.android.test actually compiles them — fixed a scaffolding bug from ea0db13 where the benchmarks sat silently uncompiled in src/androidTest/. perf/baselines.json tracks composer.typing.jank.pct alongside the other two metrics. Verified via ./gradlew :macrobenchmark:compileBenchmarkKotlin producing the expected classes.

description:
Dev-time instrumentation that lets us see what's slow.
Children cover macrobench, microbench, JankStats(debug), StrictMode, androidx.tracing, Perfetto capture workflow, and LeakCanary.

title: [epic] Performance instrumentation & optimization — inspect / improve / monitor
description:
Comprehensive push to make letta-mobile's Android app performance impeccable, following Google's App Performance Guide (inspect → improve → monitor).

## Scope
Instrument the app end-to-end so we can measure cold start, frame timing, jank, recomposition, memory pressure, network latency, and battery impact both in development and production. Ship Baseline Profiles, audit R8 keep rules, and set up regression gates so perf wins stick.

## Swim-lanes
- **A. Inspect (dev-time)** — macrobenchmark, microbenchmark, JankStats(debug), StrictMode, androidx.tracing, Perfetto, LeakCanary.
- **B. Improve (ship wins)** — Baseline Profile, Startup Profile / DEX layout, R8 rule audit, profileinstaller, startup init off-main, Compose recomposition audit, LazyColumn tuning, Coil pipeline, network, battery.
- **C. Monitor (production)** — Sentry Performance taxonomy + device class / thermal tagging, startup metric, prod JankStats sample, ANR/crash dashboard + alerts.
- **D. Process / gates** — CI macrobench thresholds, perf playbook, quarterly App Performance Score review.

## Success criteria
- Cold-start p95 tracked with a CI threshold and a Baseline Profile in place.
- Median chat-scroll jank% under a committed threshold on Pixel 2 XL (dev device).
- Sentry Performance dashboard shows transactions for startup, chat sync, bot turns, composer keystroke → render.
- Zero new perf regressions merged without a benchmark result attached.

## Working rule
Code smells discovered in-flight get filed as new P3/P4 beads in this epic (or child epics) and circled back to once the instrumentation backlog is clean.
notes: Final follow-up review complete. Memoized inline allocations in ProjectHomeScreen and BlockLibraryScreen. Verified LazyList stable keys and proper derivedStateOf returns. The Epic letta-mobile-o7ob.2 is closed and the work for o7ob is effectively wrapped up.

title: Image vanishes from user bubble after server reconcile (Confirmed.attachments always empty)
description:
## Symptom (predicted — not yet observed because send is broken)
Once `letta-mobile-jng2` is fixed and a user sends a text + image message:
1. Optimistic `Local` bubble shows the image thumbnail (good).
2. Server echoes the user message via `reconcileAfterSend` → `replaceLocal` swaps Local → Confirmed.
3. **The image disappears from the user's own bubble** because `Confirmed.attachments = emptyList()` always.

## Root cause
Two cooperating defects:

1. **`extractContent` (Message.kt:85) drops image parts**:
   ```kotlin
   raw is JsonArray -> {
       raw.mapNotNull { element ->
           if (element is JsonObject) {
               element["text"]?.jsonPrimitive?.content   // <-- only "text" parts kept
           } else if (element is JsonPrimitive) element.content else null
       }.joinToString("\n")
   }
   ```
   `image_url` parts have no `text` field → silently filtered out.

2. **`toTimelineEvent` (TimelineSyncLoop.kt:352-374) never populates `Confirmed.attachments`** — it constructs `TimelineEvent.Confirmed(...)` with no `attachments` argument, defaulting to `emptyList()`.

Same defect also means agent-supplied images (when the agent ever produces them) won't render.

## Fix
1. Add `extractAttachments(raw: JsonElement?): List<MessageContentPart.Image>` that:
   - Walks a JsonArray, picks `image_url.url` strings starting with `data:`.
   - Parses the data URL into `MessageContentPart.Image(base64, mediaType)`.
   - Returns empty for string content / null / non-image arrays.
2. Add `attachments` getter to `UserMessage` / `AssistantMessage` / `SystemMessage` mirroring the `content` getter pattern.
3. Update `LettaMessage.toTimelineEvent` to pass `attachments = (this as? UserMessage)?.attachments ?: ... ?: emptyList()`.
4. Unit test: round-trip a `MessageCreate` with one text + one image part → `extractContent` returns text, `extractAttachments` returns the image.

## Acceptance
- Send text + image → after reconcile, the user bubble still shows the image thumbnail.
- Agent reply containing `{"type": "image_url", ...}` → assistant bubble shows the image.
- Existing text-only messages still render unchanged (extractContent returns same string).

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt:85` (extractContent + new extractAttachments)
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:352` (toTimelineEvent attachments)
- `core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/` (new round-trip tests)

## Dependencies
- Should land **before** `letta-mobile-jn4b` verification, otherwise the verifier will see the image disappear after ~1s and call jng2 unfixed.

title: Verify on-device: pick → addAttachment → sendMessage attachments=1 → server multimodal echo
description:
## Goal
End-to-end on-device verification once the diagnostic + fix beads are merged. This is the closeout for `letta-mobile-jng2`.

## Steps (run on dev device 192.168.50.202:5555)
1. `./gradlew :app:installDebug` from `android-compose/`.
2. `adb logcat -c && adb logcat | tee /tmp/attach-verify.log` in another terminal.
3. In the app: open admin chat → tap attach → pick a small JPEG → confirm a thumbnail appears.
4. Type "what is in this image?" and Send.
5. Confirm in `/tmp/attach-verify.log` (in order):
   - `Telemetry/ChatComposer: attach.pickResult result=decodedOk mediaType=image/jpeg base64Len=…`
   - `Telemetry/AdminChatVM: attachment.added size=… mediaType=image/jpeg totalCount=1`
   - `Telemetry/AdminChatVM: sendMessage.route via=timeline length=… attachments=1`
   - `D TimelineSync: send.requestBody otid=client-… preview=…image_url…` (if `4x9a` is merged)
   - `Telemetry/TimelineSync: send.post (…ms)` then a streamed assistant reply that references the image content.
6. Repeat with a cancel: open picker → back out → log shows `attach.pickResult result=cancelled`, no error UI.
7. Repeat with a forced failure if feasible (e.g. select a non-image via Files picker on API 30 doc fallback) → log shows `decodeFailed`, snackbar shows error message.

## Acceptance
- All five log lines from step 5 present in order with `attachments=1`.
- Server echo includes a multimodal user message in the next conversation fetch (Letta admin UI or API).
- `letta-mobile-jng2` can be closed.

## Dependency
Blocked by: `p35t`, `b26n`, `2c70`. Optionally `4x9a` for the request-body preview line.
notes: VERIFIED on dev device 2026-04-18 ~20:05. User confirmed image upload works. Full path: pick → addAttachment (telemetry) → sendMessage attachments=1 → server accepts new Letta ImageContent shape → assistant replies. Closes the jng2/cm9a/jn4b chain.

title: addAttachment telemetry: emit attachment.added and attachment.rejected with reason
description:
## Goal
Confirm via telemetry whether the staging step in the ViewModel ever runs and why a rejection happens (cap vs size).

## Scope
In `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`, instrument `addAttachment` (line 644) and `removeAttachment` (line 666):

```kotlin
fun addAttachment(image: ...): Boolean {
    val current = _uiState.value.pendingAttachments
    if (current.size >= MAX_COMPOSER_ATTACHMENTS) {
        Telemetry.event("AdminChatVM", "attachment.rejected",
            "reason" to "count_cap", "current" to current.size, "max" to MAX_COMPOSER_ATTACHMENTS)
        _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(error = ...)
        return false
    }
    val newTotal = current.sumOf { it.base64.length } + image.base64.length
    if (newTotal > MAX_COMPOSER_TOTAL_BYTES) {
        Telemetry.event("AdminChatVM", "attachment.rejected",
            "reason" to "size_cap", "newTotal" to newTotal, "max" to MAX_COMPOSER_TOTAL_BYTES)
        _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(error = ...)
        return false
    }
    _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
        pendingAttachments = (current + image).toPersistentList(),
    )
    Telemetry.event("AdminChatVM", "attachment.added",
        "size" to image.base64.length, "mediaType" to image.mediaType,
        "totalCount" to (current.size + 1))
    return true
}

fun removeAttachment(index: Int) {
    // existing body, then:
    Telemetry.event("AdminChatVM", "attachment.removed", "index" to index)
}
```

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`

## Acceptance
- Successful pick → logcat: `Telemetry/AdminChatVM: attachment.added size=… mediaType=image/jpeg totalCount=1`.
- 5th pick (cap is 4) → logcat: `Telemetry/AdminChatVM: attachment.rejected reason=count_cap current=4 max=4`.
- Existing `sendMessage.route` line should now show `attachments=1` instead of `0`.

## Dependency
Independent of `p35t` and `b26n` — can ship in parallel.

title: Surface picker errors to user: replace silent onError with snackbar via _uiState.error
description:
## Goal
Stop swallowing picker failures. Any decode/IO error must be visible to the user.

## Scope
1. In `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt` (around line 133), pass an explicit `onError` to `rememberImageAttachmentPicker`:
   ```kotlin
   val launchPicker = rememberImageAttachmentPicker(
       onPicked = { viewModel.addAttachment(it) },
       onError = { viewModel.reportComposerError(it) },
   )
   ```
2. In `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`, add:
   ```kotlin
   fun reportComposerError(message: String) {
       _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(error = message)
   }
   ```
   (Match the existing pattern used by `addAttachment` cap-rejection errors at `:647` and `:654`.)
3. Verify the existing snackbar/error surface in `ChatScreen` actually renders `state.error`. If not, wire a `LaunchedEffect(state.error)` → `SnackbarHostState.showSnackbar(...)` and `viewModel.clearError()`.

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt`
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatComposerAttach.kt` (only if the `onError` parameter signature needs adjustment — it's already there)

## Acceptance
- Dev device: trigger a picker failure → user sees a snackbar/inline error containing the failure message.
- Cancel picker → no error UI shown.
- Successful pick → no error UI, attachment thumbnail appears (existing behavior).

## Dependency
Stack on top of `letta-mobile-p35t` once telemetry lands so we can correlate UI errors with `attach.pickResult=decodeFailed`.

title: Picker telemetry: emit attach.pickResult cancelled|decodedOk|decodeFailed in rememberImageAttachmentPicker
description:
## Goal
Make the image-attach pipeline diagnosable from logcat alone by emitting a single structured telemetry line per picker invocation.

## Scope
In `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatComposerAttach.kt`:

1. Inside `rememberLauncherForActivityResult { uri -> ... }` callback:
   - If `uri == null` → `Telemetry.event("ChatComposer", "attach.pickResult", "result" to "cancelled")`.
   - On `loadAndNormalize` success → `Telemetry.event("ChatComposer", "attach.pickResult", "result" to "decodedOk", "mediaType" to img.mediaType, "base64Len" to img.base64.length)`.
   - On `loadAndNormalize` failure → `Telemetry.event("ChatComposer", "attach.pickResult", "result" to "decodeFailed", "error" to (t.message ?: t::class.simpleName))` AND `android.util.Log.w("ChatComposerAttach", "loadAndNormalize failed", t)` so the stack trace is preserved.

2. Use the existing `Telemetry` helper used by `AdminChatVM` (`Telemetry.startTimer`/`event` — match whichever API is already there; check `core/.../telemetry/Telemetry.kt` first).

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatComposerAttach.kt`
- (Reference) `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/telemetry/Telemetry.kt`

## Acceptance
- Cancel picker on dev device → exactly one logcat line: `Telemetry/ChatComposer: attach.pickResult result=cancelled`.
- Successful pick → `attach.pickResult result=decodedOk mediaType=image/jpeg base64Len=…`.
- Force a failure (e.g. revoke read permission, or pick a known-bad URI) → `attach.pickResult result=decodeFailed error=…` plus a `W/ChatComposerAttach` stack trace.
- No behavior change beyond logging.

## Out of scope
- UI changes (handled by `letta-mobile-b26n`).
- Telemetry plumbed beyond logcat (existing Telemetry sink is sufficient).

title: Image attach silently drops: picker fires but addAttachment never reached
description:
## Symptom
On dev device (Pixel 2 XL, API 30), tapping the attach button opens the system picker (`OPEN_DOCUMENT typ=image/*` — PhotoPicker fallback for pre-API 33), user picks an image, but the agent never sees it. Every `Telemetry/AdminChatVM: sendMessage.route` log line after the pick shows `attachments=0`, confirming `pendingAttachments` is empty when send fires.

## Evidence (logcat 2026-04-17 19:58–20:01, dev device)
```
04-17 19:58:28.133 ActivityTaskManager: START ... act=android.intent.action.OPEN_DOCUMENT typ=image/*
                                        cmp=com.google.android.documentsui/.picker.PickActivity
                                        from uid 10335
... (no addAttachment trace, no error, no toast)
04-17 20:00:56 sendMessage.route ... attachments=0
04-17 20:01:16 sendMessage.route ... attachments=0
04-17 20:01:44 sendMessage.route ... attachments=0
```

## Root-cause candidates (need instrumentation to disambiguate)
1. `rememberImageAttachmentPicker.onError = {}` default — any failure in `loadAndNormalize` (BitmapFactory decode, openInputStream, EXIF read) is swallowed silently. ChatScreen does NOT pass an `onError` handler.
2. `PickVisualMedia` callback receives `uri == null` on cancel/dismiss; that path is silent (correct), but currently indistinguishable from a real failure in logs.
3. `addAttachment` and the picker callback have no telemetry — we can't tell whether they were called at all.

## Fix
1. **Instrument the path end-to-end** so the next failure is diagnosable from logcat alone:
   - Picker callback: log `attach.pickResult=cancelled|decodedOk|decodeFailed`.
   - `loadAndNormalize` (or its caller): log success with bytes/mediaType, log failure with stack.
   - `addAttachment`: telemetry `attachment.added size=… mediaType=…` and `attachment.rejected reason=…`.
2. **Surface failures to the user**: ChatScreen must pass a real `onError` that pushes to `_uiState.error` (snackbar/inline). A silent picker is hostile.
3. **Verify the wire format reaches the server** with a known-good attachment by adding a debug-build flag that logs the request body in `streamAndReconcile` (size-bounded; truncate base64 to first 32 chars per part).

## Acceptance
- Fresh build on dev device: pick image → logcat shows `attachment.added size=… mediaType=image/jpeg` AND a follow-up `sendMessage.route ... attachments=1` AND server echoes a multimodal user message.
- Picker error path: corrupt/unreadable URI → user sees an inline error, logcat shows `attach.pickResult=decodeFailed`.
- Cancel: dismissing picker logs `attach.pickResult=cancelled`, no error UI.

## Context
- Picker: `app/.../chat/ChatComposerAttach.kt`
- Composer: `app/.../chat/ChatComposer.kt`
- VM: `app/.../chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt:644 addAttachment`, `:612 sendMessage`, `:682 sendMessageViaTimeline`
- Wire serializer: `core/.../data/model/MessageContentPart.kt`
- Send path: `core/.../data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:205 streamAndReconcile`
- Original feature ticket (closed): `letta-mobile-78qg`
notes:
VERIFIED FIXED on dev device (192.168.50.202:5555) at 2026-04-18 19:34. Full picker→addAttachment→send chain confirmed in /tmp/attach-verify.log:

- attach.launch picker=PickVisualMedia.ImageOnly
- attach.pickResult result=decodedOk mediaType=image/jpeg base64Len=100528
- attachment.added size=100528 mediaType=image/jpeg totalCount=1
- sendMessage.route via=timeline length=15 attachments=1
- send.requestBody preview shows {type:image_url,...} part included

The original silent-drop bug is gone — instrumentation from p35t/2c70/b26n + diag commit 31bbf4f closed the visibility gap and confirmed the path now works. Send subsequently fails at server with a separate content-shape mismatch (filed as new bead — see blocker on jn4b).

title: cli: add classify-approvals <conv_id> diagnostic to letta-cli
description:
## Context

During the 2026-04-17 investigation of the "every bypassPermissions Bash call shows as Rejected" UI bug, the root cause (ChatMessageComponents.kt:328 treating approve=null as false) was pinned in ~30 seconds once we fell back to a CLI-driven repro:

  tool_return_message.status:        109x success  (1x error)
  approval_response_message.approve: 109x null     (0x true, 0x false)

Two prior debugging iterations (lettabot attemptRecovery, Android isError heuristic) were wasted because we speculated from source instead of classifying the server's actual output first.

## What to build

Add a classify-approvals <conv_id> subcommand to cli/letta-cli.

### Behavior

- GET /v1/conversations/{conv_id}/messages (paginated if needed)
- Count distribution of:
  - tool_return_message.status (success / error / other)
  - approval_response_message.approve (true / false / null / missing)
  - approval_response_message.approvals[].approve (same bucketing)
- Print a one-screen summary table + a WARN line if ANY approval_response_message has all-null decisions (that's the bypassPermissions fingerprint that previously mislabeled as Rejected).
- --json flag for machine-readable output.

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] letta-cli classify-approvals <conv_id> runs and prints the distribution table.
- [ ] Warns explicitly when all-null approve decisions are present.
- [ ] Works against a live conversation on http://192.168.50.90:8289.
- [ ] Unit test covers the three buckets (all-null, explicit-true, explicit-false).
- [ ] README/help text mentions this is the first-line diagnostic for "UI is mislabeling tool results" bugs.

## Why P1

This is the repro tool that would have caught the approval-rejected regression at the CLI instead of burning two iterations in the Android source. Keeping the principle ("CLI-first repro before source speculation") honest requires the tool to exist.

## Related

- Fix commit for the actual UI bug: ChatMessageComponents.kt:326-355 (explicitDecisions guard)
- Memory: system/engineering/cli-first-repros.md
close_reason: Implemented in cli/letta-cli (commit 3e308f9). Expanded the existing cmd_classify_approvals (originally scoped to letta-mobile-o9ce) with: tool_return_message.status distribution, --json flag, --self-test covering 3 buckets, repositioned help text + comment block as 'first-line UI-mislabel diagnostic', and non-zero exit on bypass fingerprint. Verified live against conv-b7845352 — 48 auto-approval echoes, fingerprint=true, exit=2. Self-test passes.

title: Letta Code client: Bash tool results mis-labeled as rejected/denied in admin UI
description:
## Problem

In the Letta admin chat UI, Bash (and likely other) tool invocations executed by the Letta Code agent session are being rendered as **rejected / denied** even when they succeeded and returned output. This session is definitively running under `permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions'` (`--yolo`), so no interactive approval is expected or possible — every Bash call should show as ✅ (approved/succeeded).

Reproduction just now: while cutting release v0.1.3, the agent ran ~20 Bash commands (git, gradle, gh release create, bd create, grep, etc.). All succeeded and their outputs were visibly used in subsequent reasoning. In the admin chat view they are being shown as "rejected" to the user (Emmanuel), which is obviously wrong — the commands ran, their stdout fed the next step, and the release was published successfully.

## Where the classification lives

Two distinct paths contribute to the "status" of a tool call in the UI. The bug is in how the UI (or the code that writes into the turn log / gateway stream) chooses between them.

### 1. `tool_result.isError`
- File: `src/core/turn-logger.ts` lines 79–89 and 119–131
- Every `tool_result` event is persisted with `isError: !!msg.isError`.
- Rendered in `src/core/turn-viewer.html`:
  - line 89: `.ev-tool_result.is-error{background:var(--treb);border-color:var(--trebd)}`
  - line 356: `var errCls=e.isError?' is-error':''; icon=e.isError?'❌':'✅';`
- This path uses ❌ (error) vs ✅ (success). Not the source of "rejected" wording.

### 2. Approval recovery path (strong suspect)
- File: `src/core/bot.ts` `attemptRecovery()` (≈ line 1181–1249)
- Flow: when a run fails or the gateway sees a CONFLICT, the bot calls `getPendingApprovals(agentId, conversationId)` and then unconditionally:
  ```ts
  for (const approval of pendingApprovals) {
    byRun.get(key)!.push({ toolCallId: approval.toolCallId, reason: 'Session was interrupted - retrying request' });
  }
  for (const [runId, batch] of byRun) {
    await rejectApproval(this.store.agentId, batch, this.store.conversationId || undefined);
  }
  ```
- `rejectApproval` posts a `tool_return_message` marked as rejected/denied to the Letta server. Any client (admin UI, Matrix, WS gateway) rendering the conversation's message stream will then see those tool calls as rejected/denied — retroactively.
- In `bypassPermissions` mode there should be **no** legitimate pending approvals for Bash. The recovery path is swallowing healthy tool calls as collateral when it fires.
- Related: `bot.ts:540-546` (`/deny` command) labels manual user denials as "Denied". Those paths share terminology with the recovery path.

## Suspected root causes (one or more)

1. `getPendingApprovals` is returning entries for tool calls that are in-flight but not actually blocked on HITL approval (race between run completion and the `agent.pending_approval` field being cleared).
2. The recovery path doesn't check `permissionMode === 'bypassPermissions'` before rejecting — it should be a no-op in yolo sessions (nothing should ever legitimately require approval there).
3. Client/UI is coloring every entry where `tool_return_message.status === 'rejected'` or `approval_response_message.approve === false` as "rejected" without distinguishing "user denied" from "recovery auto-denied" from "real approval outcome".

## Reproduction

1. Start a Letta Code session with `--yolo` against an agent on the `letta-mobile` project (any admin chat session works).
2. Send a request that drives 5+ Bash calls in a row (e.g. "cut a release" — reproduced literally tonight).
3. Observe the admin UI: Bash calls are shown as rejected/denied even though stdout is present and subsequent tools clearly consumed it.

## Acceptance

- In a `bypassPermissions` session, successful Bash/Grep/Read/Edit/Write/etc tool calls are rendered as **approved/succeeded** (✅) in the admin UI.
- A real user denial via `/deny` or a genuine HITL rejection continues to show as rejected.
- An auto-recovery that rejects an orphaned pending approval either (a) does not happen in `bypassPermissions` sessions, or (b) is flagged in the stream with a distinct "auto-recovery" reason and does NOT repaint the originating tool call's result; the fresh retry's tool call is what the UI surfaces.
- Add a regression test in `src/core/bot.ts` (or `session-manager.ts`) asserting that `attemptRecovery` is a no-op when the session's `permissionMode === 'bypassPermissions'` AND no runs are actually stuck.
- Add a classifier/test around whatever layer reads the Letta conversation stream into the UI to assert: `tool_return_message` produced by auto-recovery does not override the status of a sibling `tool_return_message` that already carried real stdout/result.

## Context / evidence

- Release v0.1.3 tonight (2026-04-17 02:28–02:30 EDT): `bd create`, `gh release create`, `git push`, `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease`, etc. all succeeded; every stdout is in the turn log; Emmanuel reports the admin UI renders these as rejected.
- Session cmdline: `letta.js ... --yolo --disallowedTools EnterPlanMode,ExitPlanMode --no-memfs --conversation conv-4d764880-...`
- Tools we *do* block deliberately: `TodoWrite` (session-manager.ts:183 — for a very different reason: the built-in TodoWrite would require interactive approval during heartbeats, so it's disabled in favor of the custom `manage_todo` tool).

## Non-goals

- Redesigning the approval UX.
- Unrelated stuck-approval recovery changes — keep the recovery path, just stop it from repainting healthy calls.
acceptance_criteria: In bypassPermissions sessions, successful tool calls render as ✅ in the admin UI. Auto-recovery-rejected approvals do not repaint healthy tool_return_messages. A regression test covers both paths.
notes:
## 2026-04-18 20:10 EDT — Implementation complete

### What was already done (prior commits on this branch)
- `ApprovalResponseCard` (ChatMessageComponents.kt:406–436) has the defensive guard: `explicitDecisions.isEmpty() return` + `any { it }` predicate. Auto-approval echoes no longer paint 'Rejected' cards.
- `toUiMessages` (MessageMapper.kt:308–330) suppresses APPROVAL_RESPONSE entries with no explicit decision at the UI-mapping layer so they never enter the timeline.
- `MessageMapperTest` covers both: 'drop auto-approval response messages with no explicit decision' + 'preserve approval response when at least one decision is explicit' + 'preserve approval response when top-level approve is true'.

### What this commit adds
1. New `core/.../data/model/ToolReturnStatus.kt` — canonical `SUCCESS`/`ERROR` constants + `isError(status)` classifier.
2. `ChatMessageComponents.kt:544` flipped from `status != null && status != "success"` (strict, over-flags) to `ToolReturnStatus.isError(status)` (explicit-error-whitelist). null / "completed" / unknown values now render as ✅, matching ADE behaviour and the empirical server contract (109× 'success', 1× 'error' in conv-4d764880).
3. `ToolReturnStatusTest` — 6 cases pinning the exact-string-match contract.

### Build status
- :core:testDebugUnitTest with targeted filters: BUILD SUCCESSFUL.
- :app:installDebug: APK pushed to Pixel 2 XL - 11 @ 192.168.50.202:5555.

### Remaining on-device verification
Next admin-chat session that drives Bash/Grep/Read tools should show ✅ icons + non-red Result labels across the board. Will close this bead after user-confirmed on-device.

title: Chat crash when scrolling up to older messages
description:
Emmanuel reported: 'we just had a crash in the agent chat when i scrolled to see older messages'. No crash report was surfaced in the app.

## Suspected root causes
1. **LazyColumn duplicate keys** in ChatScreen.kt:
   - Line 368: item(key = "${message.id}-$index") — if groupedMessages ever contains the same id twice (reasoning dedup edge case), keys collide and Compose crashes.
   - Line 407: item(key = "date-$currentDate") — if the same date appears as a boundary twice after older messages merge in (day-A to day-B to day-A zigzag), duplicate keys crash.
2. **Index-based key is fragile**: prepending older messages shifts indices, so the "-$index" suffix changes for every message on every page load, defeating Compose recomposition stability.

## Acceptance
- Scroll up through >=3 pages of older messages without crashing.
- Replace keys with stable ids only: message key should be just message.id (guaranteed unique by mergeOlderMessages dedup). Date separator key should include enough context to be unique (e.g., tie it to the boundary message id).
- Add a regression test in AdminChatViewModelTest that merges a page with overlapping dates and asserts no duplicate LazyColumn keys would be emitted.
notes:
Fixed in 005f0ac. Switched LazyColumn keys to stable id-based keys (msg-$id and date-$boundaryMsgId), added defensive distinct-by-id on the reversed list, and added a regression test in AdminChatViewModelTest. Full suite: 31 pre-existing pass + 1 new test pass; the 3 pre-existing failures remain (unrelated).
Root cause was NOT just LazyColumn keys — it was a nested horizontalScroll in CodeFenceWithHeader wrapping MarkdownHighlightedCodeFence (which already applies its own horizontalScroll internally via the mikepenz lib). When scrolling back to a message containing a fenced code block, Compose's lookahead measure pass produced Constraints.Infinity width, triggering 'Horizontally scrollable component was measured with an infinity maximum width constraints'. Fixed in c2a47ce by removing the outer horizontalScroll. Also: Emmanuel's device was still on build 1.1.1 installed at 01:09 EDT, which is BEFORE 005f0ac (01:18) and 4b0c8ac (01:33) — so neither the LazyColumn key fix nor the CrashReporter was on the device. Just pushed a new APK via adb install to 192.168.50.202:5555. Keys fix + CrashReporter + markdown fix are all now on the device as of c2a47ce build.

title: Migration Phase 3: Update AdminChatViewModel to consume Timeline directly
description:
# ViewModel Refactor

Remove ViewModel's local pending state. Consume Timeline as single source of truth.

## Changes
- AdminChatViewModel._uiState.messages becomes a derived projection of Timeline events
- Remove local UiMessage pending creation
- Remove (existingMessages + newMessages) concatenation patterns
- Remove explicit reload-from-repo on Complete (not needed with Timeline)

## Tests
- UI test: pending indicator clears when server confirms
- UI test: message ordering preserved on screen transition
- UI test: response appears immediately on Complete
- UI test: second send while first is streaming works correctly

## Exit criteria
With flag ON, all known bugs resolved. With flag OFF, behavior unchanged.
notes: Phase 3 complete. AdminChatViewModel branches on settingsRepository.getUseTimelineSync(). When flag is on: sendMessageViaTimeline() delegates to TimelineRepository, and startTimelineObserver() mirrors the unified Timeline into _uiState.messages. Toggle exposed in ConfigScreen as 'Use Timeline Sync (experimental)'. Existing test fixtures updated to inject TimelineRepository mock + flag stub. APK builds and installs successfully on device 192.168.50.202.

title: Migration Phase 2: Add TimelineRepository alongside MessageRepository behind feature flag
description:
# Coexistence Phase

Add TimelineRepository as new implementation WITHOUT removing MessageRepository.

## Feature flag
USE_TIMELINE_SYNC (runtime toggle via debug menu or BuildConfig)
- true → new TimelineRepository
- false → existing MessageRepository (current behavior)

## Benefits
- Instant rollback if issues found
- A/B testing possible
- No big-bang migration risk

## Scope
- TimelineRepository wraps SyncLoop from Phase 1
- Factory that picks implementation based on flag
- AdminChatViewModel consumes either via common interface
notes: Phase 2 complete. TimelineRepository added as a @Singleton alongside legacy MessageRepository. Feature flag 'use_timeline_sync' added to SettingsRepository (getUseTimelineSync/setUseTimelineSync). Defaults to false so legacy path remains active.

title: Migration Phase 1: Extract validated sync into core module with unit tests
description:
# Extract Core Sync Module

After POC validates architecture, port it into the mobile core module as a reusable, tested library.

## Location
core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/sync/

## Content
- Timeline.kt — data model
- TimelineStore.kt — state holder (no Android deps)
- SyncLoop.kt — single event processor
- FakeApiSource.kt — test double
- *Test.kt — unit tests mirroring POC scenarios

## Constraints
- No Android dependencies
- 100% coverage on sync logic
- All 8 POC scenarios reproduced as unit tests with FakeApi

## Exit criteria
Tests pass, module has no Android deps (verified by gradle check).
notes: Phase 1 complete. Timeline + TimelineSyncLoop ported to core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/. 23 unit tests pass (17 Timeline invariants + 6 SyncLoop integration via fake API with real SSE byte channels). Uses existing MessageApi.sendConversationMessage and SseParser — no new HTTP layer introduced.

title: POC: Run all 8 validation scenarios and document results
description:
# POC Validation Scenarios

Execute all 8 scenarios against a real Letta server. Document pass/fail and any surprising behaviors.

## Scenarios
1. Basic send/receive — send "hi", see response, timeline consistent
2. Rapid consecutive sends — 5 messages in 100ms, all appear ordered
3. Send during active stream — msg A streaming, send B, both work
4. Network drop during stream — kill connection mid-response, reconnect, recover
5. Send identical content — "hi" twice, both appear separately
6. Long-running response — 30s stream, timeline updates progressively
7. Pagination — scroll up, fetch older, no duplicates, no reordering
8. otid echo verification — every outgoing otid appears in server response

## Deliverable
/opt/stacks/letta-mobile/docs/architecture/poc-validation-results.md

## Decision gate
- All pass → proceed to Phase 1 (extract core module)
- Any fail → iterate POC, do NOT begin mobile migration
acceptance_criteria: All 8 scenarios documented with pass/fail status and captured traces.
notes: All scenarios documented with results. Decision: proceed to Phase 1.

title: POC: Implement single sync loop with otid-based reconciliation
description:
# Single Sync Loop

Implement the ONE sync handler that processes all incoming events.

## Responsibilities
- Subscribe to server event stream (SSE or polling — POC can use either)
- For each incoming event:
  - If event.otid matches a Local event: replace in place with Confirmed
  - Else if event.position > liveCursor: append
  - Else: insert at correct position (backfill scenario)
- Update liveCursor after each batch
- Handle reconnection — resume from liveCursor

## Anti-requirements (things it must NOT do)
- Must not call any sort function on the timeline
- Must not use content hashing for matching
- Must not have multiple parallel write paths
- Must not use timestamps as sort keys

## Tests
- Reconcile local → confirmed via otid match
- Append new events in order they arrive
- Handle out-of-order delivery gracefully
- Reconnection resumes from cursor
notes: SyncLoop implemented with single mutex-guarded state + per-conversation send queue. Serialization required due to 409 Conflict on concurrent sends.

title: POC: Implement unified Timeline data model and state machine
description:
# Timeline Data Model

Implement the core Timeline state in the POC CLI:

## Types
- TimelineEvent.Local(position, otid, content, sentAt, deliveryState)
- TimelineEvent.Confirmed(position, otid, serverId, messageType, content, date, runId, stepId)
- DeliveryState: SENDING | SENT | FAILED
- Timeline(conversationId, events, backfillCursor, liveCursor)

## Operations
- append(event) → adds to end
- replace(otid, confirmed) → swaps Local for Confirmed in place
- insertOrdered(event) → inserts at correct position for backfill
- findByOtid(otid) → lookup for replacement

## Invariants
- Events are always ordered by position
- Positions are monotonic
- otid uniqueness enforced on insert

## Tests
Unit tests for every invariant. No API calls yet.
notes: Timeline data model implemented in Timeline.kt. 16 unit tests passing. Invariants: events ordered by position, otids unique.

title: Fix chat reload replacing visible messages with partial server snapshot
description: ChatViewModel on main still drops visible messages after send completes. The current reloadMessagesFromServer() path in android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatViewModel.kt can replace the visible list with a smaller or older server snapshot after the 500ms delayed reload. The chat screen renders ChatViewModel.uiState.messages directly, so this is a user-visible disappearance bug in the current app path. Acceptance: reload preserves visible optimistic/streamed messages when server returns a partial snapshot; app tests cover the regression; app unit tests pass.

title: Fix: LettaChatClient.reloadFromServer has same race condition as ChatViewModel had
Fix: letta-mobile-lbsy not completed - dual cache still exists
description:

**Current state of MessageRepository.kt:**

```kotlin
// Lines 71-72 - BOTH caches still exist

```

title: Fix: LettaChatClient still has local message state that can drift
description:
## Updated Understanding

Per architectural clarification, `LettaChatClient` is the **LettaBot embedded client path** — it's intentionally separate from `ChatViewModel` (vanilla Letta admin).

## Original Issue (Still Valid)
LettaChatClient maintains its own message state:
```kotlin
private val _messages = MutableStateFlow<List<ChatMessage>>(emptyList())
private val seenMessageIds = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet<String>()
```

## Updated Question
Is this local state appropriate for the LettaBot path, or should LettaChatClient also observe MessageRepository's cache?

**If LettaBot uses the same message semantics as vanilla Letta:**
- LettaChatClient should observe `MessageRepository._messagesByConversation`
- Local state creates drift risk

**If LettaBot has different message semantics:**
- Local state may be appropriate
- But should be explicitly documented as intentional

## Recommendation
Even if separate, the reload race condition fix (delay + merge) should still be applied to LettaChatClient.reloadFromServer() — see letta-mobile-v5zw.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decide: should LettaChatClient observe MessageRepository or maintain own state?
- [ ] If observing: remove local `_messages` and derive from repository
- [ ] If maintaining: document why this is intentional
- [ ] Either way: apply reload race condition fix
close_reason: Superseded: LettaChatClient is intentionally separate for LettaBot path. Project chat skips sync since it uses different server.

title: Epic: Messaging Architecture Refactor - Single Source of Truth
description:
## Overview
The messaging system has accumulated architectural debt causing recurring bugs:
- Messages disappearing after send
- Messages flashing then vanishing
- Duplicate messages
- Stale data on app restart

Root cause: **Multiple sources of truth** for message and conversation state.

## Architecture Issues Identified

| Issue | Impact | Bead |
|-------|--------|------|
| Dual message cache (byAgent + byConversation) | Write to one, read from another | letta-mobile-lbsy |
| Optimistic messages use temp IDs that never merge | Duplicates until reload | letta-mobile-wp67 |
| Silent conversation resolution failure | Wrong cache used | letta-mobile-9idj |
| 3 places track activeConversationId | State drift | letta-mobile-k208 |
| Async reload races with send | Message flash/loss | letta-mobile-35hj |
| LettaChatClient duplicates ChatViewModel | Confusion, drift | letta-mobile-5kza |

## Recommended Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Foundation (do first)
1. **letta-mobile-k208** - ConversationManager (single source for conversation ID)
2. **letta-mobile-lbsy** - Remove dual cache (single source for messages)

### Phase 2: Fix Merge Logic
3. **letta-mobile-wp67** - Pending flag + proper merge

### Phase 3: Error Handling
4. **letta-mobile-9idj** - Required conversation resolution

### Phase 4: Race Conditions
5. **letta-mobile-35hj** - Synchronous reload

### Phase 5: Cleanup
6. **letta-mobile-5kza** - Remove LettaChatClient (or simplify)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Only ONE place stores message state (MessageRepository._messagesByConversation)
- [ ] Only ONE place tracks conversation ID (ConversationManager)
- [ ] Optimistic messages correctly merge with server messages
- [ ] Conversation resolution errors shown to user
- [ ] No race conditions between send and reload
- [ ] Clean separation between ViewModel and Repository

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for merge logic
- Unit tests for ConversationManager
- Integration test for send → reload → no duplicates
- Integration test for rapid sends → no message loss
- Manual test: send message, restart app, message persists

## Estimated Effort
- Phase 1: 2-3 hours
- Phase 2: 1-2 hours
- Phase 3: 1-2 hours
- Phase 4: 1 hour
- Phase 5: 1 hour
- Testing: 2-3 hours

**Total: ~10-14 hours**
close_reason: Completed: ConversationManager added, dual cache removed, real-time sync implemented, pagination added, ChatViewModel renamed. Remaining cleanup (dead chat module) tracked in letta-mobile-j5s9.

title: Arch: Make reloadMessagesFromServer synchronous, eliminate race condition
Arch: Consolidate activeConversationId to single source of truth
Arch: Make conversation resolution required, fail loudly on null
Arch: Fix optimistic message updates with pending flag + localId
Arch: Remove _messagesByAgent cache, use conversationId as single key
notes: MessageRepository remains conversation-keyed only. Admin ChatViewModel and LettaBot LettaChatClient reload paths now capture conversationId, delay for persistence, and merge server snapshots instead of replacing visible messages. Targeted test suites passed: :chat:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.chat.LettaChatClientTest and :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.ChatViewModelTest.

title: Fix message fetch order: use desc and reverse for recent messages
description:
**Problem**: MessageRepository.fetchMessages() uses order='asc' with limit=100, returning the oldest 100 messages. When a conversation has >100 messages, the newest messages are not loaded on app restart.

**Root cause**: User sends message → appears in streaming cache → app restart clears cache → fetchMessages returns oldest 100 → newest messages missing.

**Fix**: Change order='asc' to order='desc' in MessageRepository.fetchMessages(), then reverse the result list so UI displays in chronological order (oldest→newest).

**File**: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt
close_reason: Fixed in commit 15eb8c6 - Changed message fetch order from asc to desc with reversal for chronological display

title: Use FTS fallback for homepage message search
description: Homepage ubersearch currently posts message searches in hybrid mode, but the upstream Letta endpoint stalls for vector/hybrid while FTS succeeds. Switch the dashboard message search request to FTS so homepage message results work again through both direct Letta and the oculair proxy.

title: Archive and delete project actions
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:

The project home screen already exposes archive and delete actions in the project action sheet, but both currently only show pending snackbar messages. Users can create and edit projects now, so project lifecycle actions need to become functional or be upgraded to honest confirmation flows tied to the available backend contract.

Implement project archive/delete actions for the bz40.2 project workflow. If VibSync exposes real archive/delete registry endpoints, wire them end-to-end through ProjectApi, ProjectRepository, ProjectHomeViewModel, and ProjectHomeScreen with confirmations and refresh. If only delete exists, implement delete fully and keep archive as an honest deferred flow or hide it behind capability-aware UI. Add focused tests for state transitions and success/failure handling.
design: Follow the existing create/update project flow patterns. Prefer ConfirmDialog or ActionSheet+confirm for destructive actions. Match backend capabilities rather than faking archive behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Project action sheet no longer uses pending-only snackbar stubs for delete; destructive actions require confirmation; available backend-supported actions persist and refresh the project grid; unsupported actions are handled honestly in UI; focused ViewModel tests cover success and failure states.
close_reason: Verified ship-ready. ui/screens/projects/ProjectHomeViewModel has full archive and delete lifecycle: startArchiveProject/dismissArchiveProject/confirmArchiveProject calling projectRepository.archiveProject + snackbar + refresh, same pattern for delete (startDeleteProject/dismissDeleteProject/confirmDeleteProject -> projectRepository.deleteProject). UI state carries showArchiveProjectDialog and showDeleteProjectDialog flags for the ConfirmDialog affordance. No pending-only snackbar stubs remain in the projects path.

title: Add chat HITL approval affordances
description: Build human-in-the-loop approval UI in the Android chat flow. Replace approval requests rendering as generic tool activity with a dedicated approval card that shows requested tool actions, supports approve/reject decisions, captures optional rejection reasons, and submits approval results through the existing runtime/message path.
notes: Completed the missing stream-time HITL mapping slice.
