design: Keep mapping logic in the core mapping layer, not scattered across repositories and ViewModels. Prefer internal/shared helpers over private copies in each file.
acceptance_criteria:
- Duplicate LettaMessage/AppMessage/UIMessage conversion helpers are removed
- Date parsing logic exists in one shared helper or mapper module
- Repository, paging, domain, and ViewModel call sites all use the shared implementation
- Behavior remains unchanged for existing message types

title: Extract shared screen-state components and remove duplicated ErrorContent implementations
description:
## Why
The codebase already has a shared designsystem ErrorContent, but at least 10 screens still define private copies. Similar loading/error/empty-state patterns and several hardcoded UI strings are repeated across screen files, which raises maintenance cost and keeps screens oversized.

## Scope
- Replace local ErrorContent composables with the shared component
- Standardize repeated loading/error/empty-state wrappers where it materially reduces repetition
- Externalize hardcoded user-facing strings found during the quality pass when they belong in resources

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/ErrorContent.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/**
- Relevant string resource files under app/src/main/res/values

## Deliverables
- Duplicated private ErrorContent functions removed
- Shared component usage is consistent across screen files
- Newly touched user-facing hardcoded strings are moved to resources where appropriate
design: Prefer small composable primitives in designsystem over per-screen reinvention. Keep this cleanup low risk: reuse existing behavior rather than redesigning screen UX.
acceptance_criteria:
- No screen keeps a copy-pasted private ErrorContent when the shared component covers the same behavior
- Repeated loading/error/empty-state patterns are reduced in touched screens
- Hardcoded strings addressed by this cleanup live in string resources unless intentionally debug-only
- Screen behavior and visuals remain functionally equivalent
notes: Implemented a safe non-chat subset: replaced duplicated private ErrorContent implementations with the shared designsystem component in AgentListScreen, EditAgentScreen, McpScreen, AgentSettingsScreen, ConfigScreen, ConfigListScreen, ToolsScreen, and TemplatesScreen. Intentionally left ChatScreen and ConversationsScreen untouched to avoid interfering with the ongoing chatclient module extraction.

title: [epic] Kotlin quality pass follow-through for sprint prep
description:
## Why
The Kotlin quality pass in android-compose surfaced a focused set of performance, complexity, and cleanliness issues that should be tracked explicitly before the next sprint.

## Scope
- Track all quality-pass remediation work for the Kotlin migration
- Break findings into independently shippable beads
- Preserve enough context that an engineer can start without redoing discovery

## Outcome
A prioritized set of child beads exists for startup performance, messaging/data flow cleanup, Compose screen simplification, navigation hardening, repository cleanup, settings storage cleanup, and regression tests.
design: Keep the parent bead thin and use child beads for implementation detail. Favor issue boundaries that match architectural seams: repositories, navigation, screen clusters, settings, chat pipeline, and tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- All identified Kotlin quality-pass issues are represented by child beads
- Each child bead has clear scope, files/modules, and acceptance criteria
- The epic can be used as the canonical parent for sprint planning

title: HIGH: Add missing repository interfaces for testability
description:
6 repos lack interfaces: MessageRepo, AllConversationsRepo, SettingsRepo, PassageRepo, ModelRepo, McpServerRepo.

Fix: Create I*Repository for each, make repos implement them.

Success metric: all repos implement an interface

title: HIGH: Extract Context from SettingsRepository into DI module
description:
SettingsRepo takes @ApplicationContext. Repos should be Android-agnostic.

Fix: Inject SharedPreferences and DataStore directly from DI module.

Success metric: zero @ApplicationContext in repository constructors

title: Make Letta Mobile a LettaBot channel — push notifications + background heartbeat
description:
LettaBot supports Telegram, Slack, Discord, WhatsApp, Signal as channels. Letta Mobile should be another channel.

What LettaBot channels provide:
1. Push delivery — agent can proactively message the user (heartbeats, cron tasks)
2. Background heartbeat — periodic check-ins
3. Voice messages — transcription + TTS replies
4. Silent mode — agent decides when to interrupt
5. Group support — multi-user agent conversations

What Letta Mobile needs to become a channel:
1. FCM push notifications — agent sends via lettabot-message CLI → FCM → phone
2. Background service for heartbeat polling
3. OpenAI-compatible API endpoint — lettabot already exposes /v1/chat/completions
4. Notification handling — show agent messages as Android notifications
5. Deep link from notification → conversation

This is the bridge between LettaBot (server-side bot framework) and Letta Mobile (client-side management app).

Success metric: agent proactively sends notification to phone, user taps to open conversation
notes: Implemented Letta Mobile channel delivery with a WorkManager heartbeat that polls recent conversations, dedupes message notifications, creates an Android notification channel, posts notifications for new agent/approval/system messages, and routes taps directly into the target conversation. Added runtime notification permission request, singleTop intent handling, and test-safe scheduling guards so background work does not leak into Robolectric. Follow-up issue 3prn tracks true direct server-push/device-token delivery once backend support exists.

title: [epic] Device control — give Letta agents phone interaction via Hermes pattern
description:
Inspired by hermes-android: give Letta agents ability to control the Android device.

Two integration approaches:
1. EMBEDDED: Build AccessibilityService bridge into Letta Mobile itself. Agent tools execute locally via client-side tool pattern.
2. COMPANION: Register hermes-android bridge as an MCP server in Letta. Agent calls tools that relay to the bridge app.

Approach 1 is cleaner (single app), approach 2 is faster to ship (just connect existing MCP).

14 capabilities to expose:
- Screen reading (accessibility tree)
- Screenshots
- Tap (coordinates, node ID, text match)
- Type text
- Swipe/scroll
- Open apps
- Press keys (back, home, recents)
- Wait for element
- Get installed apps
- Current foreground app

title: Register Hermes bridge as MCP server in Letta for device control
description:
Quickest path: Register the hermes-android bridge as an MCP server so Letta agents can use the 14 android_* tools.

Implementation:
1. In McpScreen, add a 'Connect Phone' button
2. User installs hermes-android bridge app on their phone
3. User enters the relay server URL in Letta Mobile
4. Register as MCP server: POST /v1/mcp-servers with bridge URL
5. Agent gets android_tap, android_type, android_screenshot etc. as tools
6. Agent can now control the phone through the relay

Success metric: Letta agent can take a screenshot of the phone via tool call
notes: Implemented the in-app registration path on McpScreen: added a Connect Phone CTA that opens the MCP server form prefilled for a streamable HTTP bridge with an Authorization header. Live relay/phone verification is tracked separately in a follow-up bead so this issue can stay focused on registration UX.

title: MCP server tool list + health indicator
description:
API probe shows tools have: id, name, description, source_type, tags, source_lines.
McpServerApi.listMcpServerTools() returns tools per server.

UI fixes:
1. Show tool count badge on each MCP server card
2. Expandable tool list per server (Accordions)
3. Real health check: ping server URL on refresh, show green/red dot

Success metric: MCP cards show tool count and real health status

title: Conversation fork + archive in long-press menu
description:
API probe shows conversations have: id, agent_id, summary, created_at, updated_at.
ConversationApi has: forkConversation(), updateConversation(archived=true).

UI:
1. Add 'Fork' to conversation long-press menu → calls forkConversation, navigates to new
2. Add 'Archive' to long-press menu → calls updateConversation with archived=true
3. Add filter chips on ConversationsScreen: Active / Archived / All

Success metric: can fork and archive conversations

title: Message reset in chat drawer
description:
MessageApi.resetMessages() exists. Need UI.

UI: Add 'Reset Messages' item in chat drawer with warning icon.
Confirmation dialog: 'This will clear all messages in this conversation.'
After reset: reload empty chat.

Success metric: can clear conversation history

title: Tag management — create new tags, remove individual tags
description:
Tags shown as chips but no creation/removal UI.

Fix: Add tag editor in EditAgent:
1. Show existing tags as deletable chips (X button)
2. Text field + Add button to create new tag
3. Update agent via AgentUpdateParams.tags

Success metric: can add and remove tags on an agent

title: MCP server tools display + health status
description:
Tools loaded but not displayed. Health hardcoded to true.

Fix:
1. Show tool count and tool names for each MCP server card
2. Add health check ping on refresh
3. Show green/red indicator based on actual reachability

Success metric: MCP server cards show tool count and real health status

title: Message history reset button
description:
No way to clear conversation history. API has resetMessages().

Fix: Add 'Reset Messages' in chat drawer with confirmation dialog. Calls MessageApi.resetMessages(). Reloads empty chat.

Success metric: can reset chat history from drawer menu

title: Conversation forking + archiving
description:
Fork and archive APIs exist but no UI.

Fix:
1. Add 'Fork' to conversation long-press menu → creates copy, navigates to it
2. Add 'Archive' toggle to conversation long-press menu
3. Add filter chip on conversations list: Active / Archived / All

Success metric: can fork a conversation and archive old ones

title: Extract shared ErrorContent composable — deduplicate 6+ copies
description:
Every screen has its own ErrorContent. Extract to designsystem.
Success metric: single ErrorContent used by all screens

title: Move 6 remaining hardcoded strings to strings.xml
description:
ArchivalScreen, ModelBrowser, AgentScaffold conversation picker.
Success metric: zero hardcoded user-visible strings

title: Add HomeScreen error display for admin agent failure
description:
DashboardViewModel sets error but HomeScreen ignores it.
Success metric: admin agent failure shows banner, not silent fail

title: Improve agent grid cards — add image placeholder + haze overlay info
description:
Kitshn recipe cards have 16:9 image with haze overlay badges. Our agent cards are plain text.

Fix:
- Add colored gradient placeholder (based on agent name hash) instead of just SmartToy icon
- Haze overlay at bottom with agent name + model
- More visual, scannable grid

Success metric: agent cards have visual distinction via color + overlay

title: Add adaptive dialog — fullscreen on phone, dialog on tablet
description:
Kitshn has AdaptiveFullscreenDialog: dialog mode with 900dp max width on tablet, fullscreen with TopAppBar on phone.

Implementation:
1. Create AdaptiveDialog composable in designsystem
2. Detect screen width: <600dp = fullscreen, >=600dp = dialog with max 900dp
3. Replace AlertDialog usage in EditAgent, AgentSettings with AdaptiveDialog for better mobile UX
4. CenterAlignedTopAppBar with close button in fullscreen mode

Success metric: edit screens are fullscreen on phone with proper back nav

title: Add haze frosted glass effect for overlays and badges
description:
Kitshn uses dev.chrisbanes.haze library for frosted glass overlays on images and badges. Much more modern than solid color backgrounds.

Implementation:
1. Add haze dependency to designsystem
2. Apply to: favorite agent hero card overlay, tool status badges, conversation card timestamps
3. Use hazeChild() with white 50% alpha and 16dp blur

Success metric: at least 3 components use haze frosted glass effect

title: Improve scroll pagination — bidirectional loading + smart auto-scroll
description:
Stream's MessageList handles bidirectional pagination with smart auto-scroll that only triggers when user is near the bottom.

Current issues:
- We only load messages once (no history pagination on scroll up)
- Auto-scroll sometimes fights with user scrolling

Fix:
1. Detect scroll-to-top → trigger loadMore() for older messages
2. Only auto-scroll on new message if user is within 3 items of bottom
3. Maintain scroll position when prepending old messages (maintainVisibleContentPosition equivalent)
4. Show 'New messages' badge on scroll-to-bottom FAB when new messages arrive while scrolled up

Success metric: scrolling up loads older messages, new messages don't steal scroll position

title: Add link preview cards for URLs in messages
description:
Stream detects URLs and shows OG preview cards. We render URLs as plain clickable text.

Implementation:
1. Create LinkPreviewCard composable: title, description, image, domain
2. URL detection regex in message content
3. Fetch OG metadata via HEAD request (or lightweight scraper)
4. Cache previews in memory (LRU map)
5. Show preview card below message text when URL detected
6. Fallback: just show clickable URL if preview fetch fails

Success metric: URLs in messages show preview card with title + domain

title: Add ConcurrentHashMap for thread-safe pending tool tracking
description:
OpenClaw tracks pending tool calls in ConcurrentHashMap with a StateFlow for UI. We track tools inline in the message list which can race during streaming.

Fix: Add pendingTools: StateFlow<List<PendingToolCall>> to ChatViewModel. Update on ToolExecution stream state. Show in chat as distinct pending tool bubble.

Success metric: pending tools visible during execution, removed on completion

title: Replace animated TypingIndicator with lightweight DotPulse
description:
OpenClaw uses 3 static dots with varying alpha (0.38, 0.62, 0.90) — no infinite animation. Our TypingIndicator runs 3 infinite transitions which is heavier on battery.

Fix: Replace animated bouncing dots with static alpha-varying dots like OpenClaw.

Success metric: typing indicator uses zero infinite animations

title: Add custom Inter font family for polished typography
description:
OpenClaw uses Inter font family across all text styles. We use system default.

Implementation:
1. Add Inter font files to designsystem/src/main/res/font/
2. Create FontFamily in Theme or Type.kt
3. Apply to MaterialTheme typography

Success metric: all text renders in Inter font

title: Add tool display registry — emoji + label + detail per tool
description:
OpenClaw resolves tool names to display info:
  ToolDisplayRegistry.resolve(context, name, args) → emoji, label, detailLine

Instead of showing raw tool name, show:
  🔍 web_search → 'Searching the web...'
  💾 archival_memory_insert → 'Saving to memory...'
  🧠 memory → 'Updating memory...'

Implementation:
1. Create ToolDisplayInfo data class (emoji, label, detailLine)
2. Create ToolDisplayRegistry object with known tool mappings
3. Use in ToolCallCard to show friendly names

Success metric: common tools show emoji + friendly label

title: Improve tool call rendering — status indicators, expandable args/output
description:
React app has dedicated ToolCallMessage showing:
- Tool name with status badge (pending/success/error)
- Expandable arguments section
- Tool return output with stdout/stderr

Our ToolCallCard is minimal — just name + result text.

Fix: Show tool name + status icon, collapsible args in monospace, collapsible output. Use Accordions component.

Success metric: tool calls show status and expandable sections

title: Add loading skeleton shimmer for message list
description:
React app shows animated shimmer placeholders while messages load — alternating user/assistant shapes with pulsing opacity.

Implementation:
1. Create MessageSkeleton composable with shimmer animation
2. Show 5-7 alternating skeletons during UiState.Loading
3. Use infinite transition with alpha oscillation (0.3 → 0.5 → 0.3)
4. User skeletons aligned right, assistant aligned left
5. 3 lines per skeleton (full, 80%, 60% width)

Success metric: loading state shows animated placeholders instead of spinner

title: Clean up 25 unused string resources OR wire them into screens
description:
25 string resources defined but never used. Meanwhile 7 screens have hardcoded strings that match existing resources.

Wire these into screens:
- screen_agents_empty → AgentListScreen empty state
- screen_conversations_empty → ConversationsScreen empty state  
- screen_tools_empty → ToolsScreen empty state
- screen_models_no_models → ModelBrowserScreen empty state

Remove truly unused strings after wiring.

Success metric: zero hardcoded empty-state strings, zero unused string resources

title: Fix visual inconsistencies — standardize corner radius + padding
description:
Inconsistencies found:
- TemplatesScreen L111: RoundedCornerShape(16.dp) → should be 12.dp
- Remove unused SuggestionChip import from AgentListScreen
- Replace 7 hardcoded empty-state strings with existing stringResources

Success metric: all cards use 12.dp corners, zero unused imports in screens

title: Add agent type, sleeptime frequency, and read-only config displays
description:
From the ADE:
- Agent type (read-only): 'letta_v1_agent'
- Sleep-time agent frequency (number): currently 5
- Reset message buffer (switch)
- Max files open (number)
- Per-file view character limit (number)
- Embedding config display (model, dimensions, chunk size — read-only)
- Compaction settings (mode, summarizer model — read-only)

Show as read-only info cards or in an 'Advanced' Accordions section.

Success metric: all agent config visible in an Advanced section

title: Add embedding model dropdown to EditAgent
description:
Agent has an 'embedding' field. Need a second dropdown for embedding models.

1. Add embeddingModels to EditAgentViewModel from ModelRepository
2. Add EmbeddingModelDropdown or reuse ModelDropdown pattern
3. Wire into EditAgentScreen below LLM model field
4. Save via AgentUpdateParams.embedding field

Success metric: embedding model selectable from dropdown

title: Wire ConnectivityMonitor state into AgentScaffold banner
description:
connectivityMonitorAvailable is hardcoded false. ConnectionStatusBanner exists but never reads real state.

Fix: Inject ConnectivityMonitor into a ViewModel accessible from AgentScaffold (or create a small ConnectivityViewModel). Collect isOnline + isServerReachable StateFlows. Map to ConnectionState enum.

Success metric: going offline shows red banner, reconnecting shows amber

title: Extract remaining ~11 hardcoded strings to strings.xml
description:
About screen (6 strings), ConversationsScreen (1), MessageContextMenu (3), ModelDropdown (1).

Success metric: grep Text\('"[A-Z]' returns zero in ui/ files

title: Fix NotImplementedError in MessageRepository agent-level streaming
description:
MessageRepository.kt L163: throw NotImplementedError('Agent-level streaming not yet implemented'). This will crash at runtime if triggered.

Fix: Either implement the feature or return a graceful error instead of throwing.

Success metric: no NotImplementedError in production code

title: Add pull-to-refresh to AgentListScreen and ChatScreen
description:
Pull-to-refresh only on ConversationsScreen. AgentList and Chat missing it.

Fix: Wrap AgentListScreen content in PullToRefreshBox. Wrap ChatScreen LazyColumn area in PullToRefreshBox wired to loadMessages().

Success metric: swipe-down refreshes on all 3 main list screens

title: Validate nav arguments — guard against empty agentId/conversationId
description:
ChatViewModel, EditAgentViewModel, ArchivalViewModel read agentId from SavedStateHandle with empty string fallback. Empty agentId causes API calls to /v1/agents//messages which 404s.

Fix: If agentId is blank, set UiState.Error('Invalid agent') immediately instead of making API calls.

Success metric: blank agentId shows error instead of crashing

title: Add About/Version screen with app info and logout
description:
No way for users to see app version, clear data, or switch servers without uninstalling.

Fix:
1. Create ui/screens/about/AboutScreen.kt showing: app version, build number, server URL, agent count
2. Add 'Logout / Clear Data' button that clears SettingsRepository and navigates to config
3. Add to ConversationsScreen overflow menu

Success metric: about screen shows version, logout clears all data

title: Add retry with exponential backoff to API calls
description:
All API calls fail on first error with no retry. Transient network hiccups show error to user immediately.

Fix: Add Ktor HttpRequestRetry plugin to LettaApiClient:
  install(HttpRequestRetry) { retryOnServerErrors(maxRetries = 3); exponentialDelay() }

Success metric: transient 500/503 errors auto-retry 3 times before surfacing

title: Fix silent exception in ChatViewModel summary update
description:
ChatViewModel.kt L86: catch (_: Exception) { } silently swallows conversation summary update failure. Also present in ConversationsViewModel agent name cache.

Fix: Add Log.w to all empty/silent catch blocks in ViewModels. Use safeLaunch where appropriate.

Success metric: zero empty catch blocks in ViewModel files

title: Link ModelBrowser from EditAgent model field
description:
All model fields across the app must be dropdowns populated from ModelRepository, not free-text.

Screens affected:
- EditAgentScreen: model field → dropdown/picker backed by ModelRepository.llmModels
- AgentSettingsScreen: if model is shown, same treatment
- ConversationCreateParams: if model is selectable
- AgentCreateDialog: if model is settable on creation

Implementation:
1. Create a reusable ModelDropdown composable that:
   - Shows current model as ExposedDropdownMenuBox
   - Loads models from ModelRepository on first open
   - Groups by provider (openai, anthropic, etc.)
   - Supports search/filter within dropdown
   - Shows model name + context window info
2. Replace ALL free-text model fields with ModelDropdown
3. ModelBrowserScreen can still exist as a full-page browser but the inline dropdown is the primary UX

Success metric: zero free-text model input fields in the app — all are dropdowns

title: Add save success feedback to all settings screens
description:
EditAgent, AgentSettings, Config screens save silently. No confirmation shown.

Fix: After successful save, dispatch snackbar 'Changes saved' and optionally navigate back.

Success metric: every save operation shows confirmation snackbar

title: Extract remaining ~25 hardcoded strings to strings.xml
description:
Screens still have Text('Delete'), Text('Edit'), Text('Select an Agent'), etc.

Files: ConversationsScreen, AgentListScreen, ArchivalScreen, ModelBrowserScreen, McpScreen

Success metric: grep Text\('"[A-Z]' in screens returns zero

title: Dismiss keyboard on message send in ChatScreen
description:
Keyboard stays visible after sending. Standard chat apps dismiss on send.

Fix: Get LocalSoftwareKeyboardController, call hide() after onSend.

Success metric: keyboard hides after sending a message

title: Wire SnackbarDispatcher into screens for save/delete feedback
description:
SnackbarDispatcher was built but never used. Save operations are silent.

Fix:
1. Provide SnackbarDispatcher via CompositionLocalProvider in MainActivity
2. Add SnackbarHost to AgentScaffold, ConversationsScreen, AgentListScreen Scaffolds
3. Dispatch on: save success, delete success, create success, copy
4. Show error snackbars for non-critical failures

Success metric: all mutations show snackbar confirmation

title: Add pull-to-refresh on conversations, agents, and chat
description:
Zero PullToRefresh in the app. Fundamental mobile UX pattern.

Fix: Add PullToRefreshBox to ConversationsScreen, AgentListScreen, and ChatScreen. Wire to respective refresh() methods on ViewModels.

Success metric: swipe-down refreshes data on all 3 list screens

title: Add last message preview snippet on conversation cards
description:
Conversations only show summary. Show a 1-line preview of the last message for context.

Fix:
1. Add optional lastMessagePreview field to a ConversationDisplay model (or enrich Conversation)
2. When loading conversations, fetch last message per conversation (or use summary as fallback)
3. Show as secondary text below agent name, bodySmall, maxLines=1, ellipsis
4. Strip markdown formatting from preview

Success metric: conversation cards show a snippet of the last message content

title: Add relative time formatting to conversation timestamps
description:
ConversationCard shows raw ISO timestamps like '2024-03-15T10:00:00Z'. Should show '2h ago', 'Yesterday', etc.

Fix:
1. Create util/RelativeTimeFormatter.kt with formatRelativeTime(isoString: String): String
2. Logic: <1min='Just now', <1h='Xm ago', <24h='Xh ago', <48h='Yesterday', else date
3. Apply in ConversationCard for lastMessageAt/createdAt
4. Add unit tests for formatter

Success metric: all timestamps show human-readable relative time

title: Fix ConnectivityMonitor thread safety
description:
ConnectivityMonitor has NetworkCallback writing _isOnline.value from main thread while scope.launch writes _isServerReachable.value from IO thread.

Fix:
1. Use MutableStateFlow.update{} instead of .value = 
2. Move all state writes to a single dispatcher
3. Add try-catch to scope.launch for connectivity checks
4. Add CoroutineExceptionHandler to prevent scope death

Success metric: all StateFlow writes in ConnectivityMonitor use .update{}

title: Add Kotlin lint rules + detekt for static analysis
description:
No static analysis configured. Catches bugs before runtime.

Implementation:
1. Add detekt plugin to build.gradle.kts
2. Create detekt.yml with rules:
   - TooGenericExceptionCaught (force logging in catch)
   - SwallowedException (no empty catch)
   - MagicNumber (flag hardcoded numbers)
   - UnnecessaryLet
   - UselessCallOnNotNull
3. Add lint options to android { lintOptions { ... } }
4. Configure as pre-commit check

Success metric: ./gradlew detekt passes with zero violations

title: Add global CoroutineExceptionHandler to prevent silent ViewModel crashes
Fix ChatScreen LaunchedEffect keys for reliable scroll behavior
Add memory block management — create, delete, reorder blocks
description:
We can edit block values but can't create new blocks or delete existing ones. Letta supports custom memory blocks beyond persona/human.

API endpoints:
- GET /v1/agents/{id}/blocks — list blocks
- POST /v1/blocks — create new block
- DELETE /v1/blocks/{id} — delete block
- PATCH /v1/blocks/{id} — update block (already have)
- POST /v1/agents/{id}/blocks/{id}/attach — attach block to agent

Implementation:
1. In EditAgentScreen, add 'Add Memory Block' button

title: Add model browser/selector with available models list
description:
Letta API exposes available models. We should show these when creating/editing agents instead of free-text model field.

title: Add Conversations API — parallel chat sessions per agent
description:
Letta Conversations API allows multiple parallel chat sessions on a single agent with shared memory. Critical for multi-user mobile use.

API endpoints:
- POST /v1/conversations — create conversation
- GET /v1/conversations/{id} — get conversation
- POST /v1/conversations/{id}/messages — send message to conversation
- DELETE /v1/conversations/{id} — delete conversation

Currently we have basic conversation CRUD but no dedicated conversation selector per agent.

Implementation:
1. Add ConversationSelectorSheet.kt — ModalBottomSheet showing conversations for current agent
2. In ChatScreen, add conversation picker in TopAppBar
3. Create new conversation button in selector
4. Show conversation summary/title
5. Switch between conversations without losing scroll position
6. Test: ConversationSelectorTest (4+ tests)

Success metric: can create, switch between, and delete conversations within a single agent

title: Add archival memory UI — search, browse, insert passages
description:
Letta agents have archival memory (semantic search over large text corpus). We don't expose this in the mobile app.

API endpoints needed:
- GET /v1/agents/{id}/passages — list passages with pagination
- POST /v1/agents/{id}/passages — insert new passage
- DELETE /v1/agents/{id}/passages/{passage_id} — delete passage
- GET /v1/agents/{id}/archival?query=... — semantic search

Implementation:
1. Create data/api/ArchivalApi.kt with above endpoints
2. Create data/repository/ArchivalRepository.kt
3. Create ui/screens/archival/ArchivalScreen.kt:
   - Search bar at top with semantic search
   - LazyColumn of passage cards with content preview
   - FAB to add new passage
   - Swipe-to-delete on passages
   - Show passage metadata (created_at, source)
4. Create ui/screens/archival/ArchivalViewModel.kt
5. Add navigation route from AgentScaffold drawer
6. Test: ArchivalViewModelTest (6+ tests)

Success metric: can search, browse, and add archival passages for any agent

title: [epic] Letta API feature expansion — agent administration
description:
Expand Letta Mobile to be a comprehensive agent administration tool.

Currently implemented: basic CRUD for agents, messages, conversations, tools, MCP servers, blocks.

Missing features from Letta API:
- Archival memory management (search, insert, browse)
- Agent export/import (.af files)  
- Conversations API (parallel sessions)
- Sleeptime agent configuration
- Tool rules (init, child, terminal)
- Agent secrets management
- Agent folders (document attachment)
- Model selection/browsing
- Usage statistics display
- Memory block create/delete (not just edit)
- Shared blocks between agents

title: Add HabiticaTestCase-style base test class with shared mocks
description:
Habitica has HabiticaTestCase base class that pre-configures all common mocks (apiClient, repositories, userViewModel). Every test extends it and gets a fully wired test environment for free.

Implementation:
1. Create test/.../testutil/LettaTestCase.kt:
   - Pre-creates FakeAgentRepository, FakeMessageRepository, etc.
   - Pre-creates test data (sampleAgent, sampleConversation)
   - Sets up Dispatchers.setMain(testDispatcher) in @Before
   - Resets in @After
   - Provides helper: fun createViewModel<T>() that wires all fakes
2. All ViewModel tests extend LettaTestCase
3. Reduces boilerplate from ~20 lines of setup to 1 line (extend class)

Reference: Habitica HabiticaTestCase.kt — 147 lines of shared setup.

title: Add JSON test fixtures for API response testing (Habitica/Pokedex pattern)
description:
Both Habitica and Pokedex load real JSON response fixtures from test resources for API testing. This catches deserialization bugs.

Implementation:
1. Create test/resources/ directory
2. Add JSON fixtures from real Letta API responses:
   - agents_list.json — GET /v1/agents response
   - agent_detail.json — GET /v1/agents/{id} response
   - messages_list.json — GET /v1/agents/{id}/messages response
   - send_message_response.json — POST /v1/agents/{id}/messages response
   - conversation_list.json
   - tool_list.json
   - sse_stream.txt — raw SSE stream for SseParser tests
3. Create TestResourceLoader utility:
   fun loadJson(filename: String): String = javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(filename)!!.readText()
4. Use in API tests and serialization tests

Capture from real API calls during development. Anonymize any sensitive data.

title: Adopt @Dispatcher qualifier for testable coroutine dispatchers (Pokedex pattern)
Test: AgentApi + MessageApi with Ktor MockEngine (12+ tests)
description:
Test HTTP request formation and response parsing for the two most critical APIs.

AgentApi tests (6+):
- listAgents() sends GET /v1/agents with correct params
- getAgent() sends GET /v1/agents/{id}
- createAgent() sends POST with correct JSON body
- updateAgent() sends PATCH with correct JSON body
- deleteAgent() sends DELETE to correct endpoint
- Error response (404/500) throws ApiException with correct code

MessageApi tests (6+):
- getMessages() sends GET to correct endpoint
- getMessages() with conversationId uses conversation path
- sendMessage() sends POST with MessageCreateRequest body
- sendMessage() with streaming parameter
- getConversationMessages() paginates with before cursor
- Error response handling

Use Ktor MockEngine to intercept and verify requests.
Pattern: MockEngine { request -> respond(mockJson, HttpStatusCode.OK) }

title: Test: SettingsRepository (8+ tests) — config persistence
description:
Test cases:
- saveConfig() persists to DataStore
- getConfigs() returns all saved configs

title: Test: MessageProcessor (12+ tests) — stream processing, tool execution
description:
Most complex domain logic. Recursive tool execution with depth limits.

Test cases:
- Process single UserMessage from stream
- Process single AssistantMessage from stream
- Process ReasoningMessage from stream
- Accumulate multi-chunk assistant content
- Handle ToolCallMessage → execute tool → ToolReturnMessage
- Max recursion depth (10) prevents infinite loops
- Tool execution timeout handling
- Stream cancellation mid-processing
- Error propagation from tool execution
- Multiple messages in single stream
- Empty stream returns empty list
- Malformed message in stream skipped gracefully

Dependencies: FakeClientToolRegistry, FakeMessageApi
Complexity: HIGH — needs careful flow testing with Turbine

title: Add date separator between message groups
description:
Element X shows date headers between messages from different days: 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'March 15, 2024'. This is standard chat UX we're missing.

Implementation:
1. Create ui/components/DateSeparator.kt:
   - Centered text with horizontal dividers on each side
   - Format: 'Today', 'Yesterday', or 'Month Day, Year' for older
   - Style: labelSmall, onSurfaceVariant color, 0.5f alpha dividers
2. Insert as virtual items in the LazyColumn:
   - Compare each message's date with the previous message's date
   - If different day, insert DateSeparator item between them
3. Logic in ChatViewModel or ChatScreen:
   - Group messages by date
   - Interleave DateSeparator items
   - Use stable keys: 'date-{yyyy-MM-dd}'
4. Format relative dates:
   - Same day as now → 'Today'
   - Yesterday → 'Yesterday'
   - Same year → 'March 15'
   - Different year → 'March 15, 2024'

Reference: Element X TimelineItemVirtualView date separators
Standard chat UX — users expect date grouping

title: Add connection status banner (online/offline/reconnecting)
description:
Element X shows encryption and connection status in the UI. For a chat app talking to an API server, showing connection state is critical.

Implementation:
1. Create data/repository/ConnectionRepository.kt:
   - sealed interface ConnectionState: Online, Offline, Reconnecting
   - Monitor network connectivity via ConnectivityManager callback
   - Monitor Letta server reachability via periodic health check (GET /v1/health)
2. Create ui/components/ConnectionStatusBanner.kt:
   - Animated banner at top of chat (AnimatedVisibility, expandVertically)
   - Offline: red background, 'No internet connection'
   - Reconnecting: amber background, 'Reconnecting...' with RotationalLoader
   - Online: green briefly, then auto-dismiss
3. Wire into AgentScaffold below TopAppBar
4. In ChatViewModel: disable send button when Offline
5. When Reconnecting→Online, auto-refresh messages

Reference: Element X encryption/connection status UI patterns
Critical UX for API-dependent chat app

title: Add copy message action + clipboard snackbar
description:
Element X has action buttons on messages including Copy. We built MessageActionButton but never integrated copy functionality.

Implementation:
1. Add Copy button to assistant message bubbles in ChatScreen:
   - Show Row of MessageActionButtons below assistant messages
   - Copy button: icon=ContentCopy, label='Copy'
2. On copy tap:
   - Get ClipboardManager from LocalContext
   - clipboardManager.setText(AnnotatedString(message.content))
   - Dispatch snackbar 'Copied to clipboard' via SnackbarDispatcher
3. Add long-press context menu as alternative:
   - combinedClickable(onLongClick = { showActions = true })
   - DropdownMenu with Copy, and optionally Regenerate
4. Add 'Regenerate' action for last assistant message:
   - Calls viewModel.regenerateLastMessage()
   - ViewModel removes last assistant message and re-sends last user message

Reference: Element X MessageActionButton, clipboard handling
Depends on: SnackbarDispatcher (for copy confirmation)

title: Add timeline pagination (prefetch at 40 items from top)
description:
Element X triggers loading more message history when the user scrolls within 40 items of the top. This prevents the 'loading...' stutter when scrolling up.

Implementation:
1. Add pagination support to MessageRepository:
   - getMessages(agentId, conversationId, before: String? = null, limit: Int = 50) → Flow<List<AppMessage>>
   - Track hasMoreMessages: Boolean
2. Add pagination state to ChatUiState:
   - isLoadingMore: Boolean = false
   - hasMoreMessages: Boolean = true
3. Add loadMore() function to ChatViewModel:
   - Get oldest message ID from current list
   - Call messageRepository.getMessages(agentId, conversationId, before = oldestId)
   - Prepend to existing messages
4. Trigger prefetch in ChatScreen:
   LaunchedEffect(listState) {
     snapshotFlow { listState.layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo.lastOrNull()?.index }
       .collect { lastVisible ->
         val totalItems = listState.layoutInfo.totalItemsCount
         if (lastVisible != null && totalItems - lastVisible < 40) {
           viewModel.loadMore()
         }
       }
   }
5. Show CircularProgressIndicator at top of list when isLoadingMore

Reference: Element X prefetch at 40 items threshold
Enables smooth infinite scroll through conversation history

title: Migrate string naming to Element X conventions (action_, common_, screen_)
description:
Element X uses a scalable string naming convention that prevents collisions as the app grows:
- action_* for verbs/buttons: action_copy, action_delete, action_save
- common_* for shared nouns: common_error, common_success, common_loading
- screen_*_* for screen-specific: screen_chat_empty_title, screen_agents_create_title
- a11y_* for accessibility: a11y_send_message, a11y_back_button
- error_* for error messages: error_network, error_auth_expired

Implementation:
1. Audit current strings.xml (104 entries) and categorize each
2. Rename string keys to follow convention:
   - 'agents' → 'common_agents'
   - 'delete' → 'action_delete'
   - 'retry' → 'action_retry'
   - 'start_conversation' → 'screen_chat_empty_title'
   - 'type_message_hint' → 'screen_chat_input_hint'
3. Update all stringResource(R.string.xxx) references across all .kt files
4. Add naming convention comment block at top of strings.xml
5. Sort entries alphabetically within each prefix group

This is a bulk rename — 104 entries across ~15 files.
Reference: Element X AGENTS.md string naming conventions

title: Add SnackbarDispatcher for non-blocking transient errors
description:
Element X has a SnackbarDispatcher via CompositionLocal for transient errors (network blips, copy confirmations). Critical errors still get full-screen treatment. We currently only have blocking UiState.Error.

Implementation:
1. Create ui/common/SnackbarDispatcher.kt:
   - data class SnackbarMessage(val message: String, val actionLabel: String? = null, val onAction: (() -> Unit)? = null)
   - class SnackbarDispatcher { fun dispatch(message: SnackbarMessage); fun collectAsState(): State<SnackbarMessage?> }
   - val LocalSnackbarDispatcher = staticCompositionLocalOf { SnackbarDispatcher() }
2. Provide SnackbarDispatcher in LettaTheme or MainActivity:
   CompositionLocalProvider(LocalSnackbarDispatcher provides snackbarDispatcher)
3. Add SnackbarHost to main Scaffold (AgentScaffold or AppNavGraph root):
   SnackbarHost(hostState = snackbarHostState)
4. Collect dispatched messages and show via snackbarHostState.showSnackbar()
5. Use in ViewModels for non-critical errors:
   - Network timeout → snackbar 'Connection timed out. Retrying...'
   - Copy to clipboard → snackbar 'Copied to clipboard'
   - Agent deleted → snackbar 'Agent deleted' with Undo action
6. Keep UiState.Error for critical failures (no connection, auth expired)

Reference: Element X SnackbarDispatcher
Unblocks non-critical error handling without full-screen error states

title: Add StateProvider pattern for all screen states
description:
Element X has a FooStateProvider.kt for every screen that provides sample states via PreviewParameterProvider. This powers both @Preview composables AND screenshot tests from a single source of truth.

Implementation:
1. Create state providers for all screens:
   - ui/screens/chat/ChatUiStateProvider.kt → sequenceOf(empty, loading, streaming, withMessages, withThinking, error)
   - ui/screens/agentlist/AgentListUiStateProvider.kt → sequenceOf(empty, withAgents, searching, noResults)
   - ui/screens/config/ConfigUiStateProvider.kt → sequenceOf(cloud, selfHosted, empty)
   - ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsUiStateProvider.kt
   - ui/screens/tools/ToolsUiStateProvider.kt
   - ui/screens/mcp/McpUiStateProvider.kt
2. Each provider extends PreviewParameterProvider<UiState<FooUiState>>
3. Include Success, Error, and Loading variants
4. Create sample data objects (sampleAgent, sampleMessage, sampleTool) in a shared test-fixtures location
5. Update existing Previews.kt to use @PreviewParameter(ChatUiStateProvider::class)
6. Each provider should cover all meaningful visual states

Reference: Element X FooStateProvider.kt per screen
Enables: better previews, screenshot test foundation, consistent sample data

title: [epic] Element X patterns adoption
description:
Adopt production patterns from Element X Android (element-hq/element-x-android) into Letta Mobile.

Source: 2k-star, 14k-commit Matrix chat client built with Compose.
Key areas: message grouping, scroll behavior, typing indicators, design tokens, testing infrastructure, error handling, and architecture patterns.

Phases:
- Phase 1 (Now): Message grouping, scroll FAB, typing indicator
- Phase 2 (Next sprint): StateProviders, snackbar errors, string conventions, pagination
- Phase 3 (Future): Multi-module split, Paparazzi screenshot tests, Presenter pattern

title: Extract 80+ hardcoded strings to strings.xml
description:
80+ hardcoded strings in UI code. Blocks localization, hard to maintain.

Files with hardcoded strings:
- ChatScreen.kt: 'Start a conversation', 'Type a message...', 'Send', 'Reasoning', 'Retry'

Add ~80 new string resources to strings.xml.

Also add usesCleartextTraffic=true in debug builds only.

title: Fix accessibility: add contentDescription to all icons + semantics

- TemplatesScreen.kt

title: Add centralized ErrorDialog composable

title: Add LatencyText and MessageActionButton components

Gallery patterns for post-message UI:

title: Add MessageSender label above chat bubbles

title: Add chat auto-scroll behavior (3 LaunchedEffect triggers)
description:
Gallery has 3 scroll triggers in ChatPanel:
1. Scroll on IME toggle: LaunchedEffect(WindowInsets.ime.getBottom(density))
2. Scroll on new message: LaunchedEffect(messages.size)
3. Scroll on streaming only if at bottom: derivedStateOf { lastVisibleItemIndex >= messages.size - 2 }

Currently we use reverseLayout=true which is a workaround. Replace with proper scroll behavior in ChatScreen.kt ChatContent composable.

title: Research and adopt Gallery project patterns into codebase
description:

1. ViewModel ↔ Repository wiring conventions (how they connect data layer to UI)
2. Chat message rendering architecture (message list, streaming, grouping)
3. Theme/design system organization
4. Navigation patterns
5. Error handling in ViewModels

Create a reference doc or apply learnings directly to our implementations.
This is a research task that informs all other implementation issues.

description: Migrate the scaffolded android-compose/ project from TODO stubs to a working Android app. The scaffold has 74 files / 6132 lines of Kotlin but needs Gradle wrapper, compilation fixes, ViewModel-repository wiring, and UI polish.

title: Bug: Alert.prompt in ConversationsScreen not supported on Android
description: handleEditConversation uses Alert.prompt to edit conversation summaries. Alert.prompt is iOS-only — it will crash or show nothing on Android. Replace with a cross-platform TextInput modal component.
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced Alert.prompt (iOS-only) with cross-platform TextInputModal component. New reusable modal at components/custom/modals/text-input-modal.tsx.

title: Bug: useResetChatMessages ignores conversationId
description: In use-agent-messages.ts, the conversationId parameter is destructured with underscore prefix and unused. Reset chat clears ALL agent messages instead of just the selected conversation's messages. Need to use conversations.messages endpoint when conversationId is provided.
close_reason: Fixed: useResetChatMessages now routes to conversations.messages.reset when conversationId is provided, falls back to agents.messages.reset for default conversation.

title: Breaking: MCP server config structure changed to nested format
description: MCP server configuration and messages.modify now use a nested config structure instead of flat. The MCP server form and config store need to be updated to match the new schema.
close_reason: SDK 1.10.2 diff confirms MCP server config structure is unchanged (flat). No migration needed.

title: Breaking: agent.messages.stream deprecated — migrate to conversation messages
description: agent.messages.stream is deprecated in favor of the new Conversations API. The chat UI streaming needs to be migrated to use conversations.messages.stream or the non-streaming conversation messages endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented: Chat UI now uses conversations.messages.list and conversations.messages.create when conversationId is set. Agent-direct fallback preserved for default conversation.

title: Breaking: shared_block_ids deprecated in favor of groups and blocks
description: Agent creation now uses 'groups' and 'blocks' fields instead of 'shared_block_ids'. The app needs to update any agent creation/update flows that reference shared_block_ids.
close_reason: shared_block_ids not used in codebase. Field still exists in SDK. No migration needed.

title: Breaking: update() methods renamed to modify() across SDK
description: In v1.10.0, all update methods were renamed to modify (e.g., agents.update() → agents.modify(), blocks.update() → blocks.modify()). All call sites in the app need to be migrated.
close_reason: SDK 1.10.2 diff confirms update() was NOT renamed to modify(). No migration needed.

title: Upgrade @letta-ai/letta-client from 1.6.8 to 1.10.2
description: The client was upgraded to 1.10.2 but had to be downgraded back to 1.6.8 due to breaking changes. Need to properly migrate to the latest version with all API changes addressed.
close_reason: Upgraded @letta-ai/letta-client from 1.9.0 to 1.10.2. Metro bundles clean. TypeScript compiles clean. All 8 sub-issues resolved — most anticipated breaking changes did not materialize.

title: A2UI surface lifecycle: auto-dismiss by default with agent-controllable opt-out
description:
## Problem

Today every A2UI surface persists until the agent explicitly emits a deleteSurface. In practice that means:
- Models forget to tear down one-shot prompts; surfaces linger on screen after the user has interacted with them.
- The convention is implicit — agents must remember the social rule 'dismiss after action'.
- Defaulting to persistence biases the renderer toward visual clutter rather than the common case (one-shot prompt → done).

Emmanuel's call (2026-05-19): default should be auto-dismiss; only the agent's explicit opt-in keeps the surface alive.

## Proposed model: three-state lifecycle

Each surface carries a lifecycle hint set at creation time. The renderer drives a state machine off it.

| State | Default? | Renderer behavior |
|-------|----------|-------------------|
| auto | YES (when field omitted) | On first user_action, renderer tears down the surface immediately. No deleteSurface from agent required. Snappy, one-shot. |
| wait_for_response | opt-in | On user_action, the surface persists through the agent's response turn. Any updateComponents / updateDataModel / new createSurface for the same surfaceId during that turn land normally. On turn_done of the synthetic-input turn, if no deleteSurface was emitted, the renderer auto-dismisses. Lets the agent say 'here's the result' inline before the surface goes away. |
| manual | opt-in | Renderer never auto-dismisses. Agent owns teardown — MUST emit deleteSurface when done. Use for persistent dashboards, multi-step flows, monitors. |

## Wire shape

Extend createSurface with an optional 'lifecycle' field:



Omitted → 'auto'. Older agents that don't know about the field get auto-dismiss for free; this is the right migration story (the field is additive and the new default matches user expectations).

## Implementation split

- Renderer (android-compose, A2uiRenderer.kt + surface state holder):
  - Track 'lifecycle' per surface alongside existing state.
  - On user_action emit: consult the lifecycle and either dismiss now, schedule a dismiss on turn_done, or no-op.
  - For wait_for_response: subscribe to the synthetic-input turn's terminal frame; if no deleteSurface arrived, dismiss.
  - For manual: leave alone.

- Shim (admin-shim/lib/a2ui-adapter.ts, a2ui-stream-splitter.ts validator):
  - Pass 'lifecycle' through unchanged.
  - Validate enum; reject unknown values with validation_error: 'createSurface.lifecycle must be one of: auto, wait_for_response, manual'.
  - Default to 'auto' in any normalization step.

- Prompt template (admin-shim/lib/a2ui-v09-basic-prompt.ts):
  - Document the field with when to use each.
  - Default guidance: 'most surfaces are auto; only set wait_for_response when you intend to follow up with content; only set manual for persistent surfaces.'

- Agent-side memory (Meridian's a2ui_protocol):
  - Already noted as a follow-up; will update after this bead lands.

## Acceptance

- createSurface with no lifecycle field → renderer dismisses on first user_action.
- createSurface with lifecycle: 'wait_for_response' → surface persists through one agent reply turn, then dismisses if not explicitly torn down.
- createSurface with lifecycle: 'manual' → never auto-dismissed; requires deleteSurface.
- Invalid lifecycle value → shim rejects with validation_error; surface not rendered.
- Existing test suites for A2UI surfaces all pass after default changes (regression check on test corpus that relies on persistent surfaces).
- Documentation lands in admin-shim/docs/MOBILE_WS_PROTOCOL.md (likely §12 or new subsection on surface lifecycle).

## Related

- Epic letta-mobile-51xm — A2UI renderer initiative.
- Epic letta-mobile-wq8v — Conversation state SSoT (the auto-dismiss-on-turn_done path piggybacks on the WS-canonical turn lifecycle this epic is formalizing).
- letta-mobile-g2qg (P2) — surface history flash on reopen. Once auto is the default, the set of 'live' surfaces on reopen shrinks dramatically, reducing flash exposure surface.
- Emmanuel's framing (2026-05-19 morning): 'the UI really should disappear when invoked unless you specifically request that it not do that.'

title: A2UI surface history flashes/replays entire timeline on conversation load
description:
Repro (2026-05-19 01:58 EDT, after multi-round log-watch-form-* test session):
1. Run a session that emits several A2UI surfaces over time (createSurface + updateComponents + eventual deleteSurface for each).
2. Close and reopen the conversation in Android Compose client.
3. Observe: the entire A2UI message history replays on load — every prior surface flashes through before settling on current state.

Expected: surface rehydration should be a state-reconstruction pass, not a render-log replay.
- deleteSurface is terminal — surfaces marked deleted must not be rendered at all on reload.
- For surfaces that are still live, only the final folded state (latest createSurface + collapsed updateComponents + final updateDataModel) should render.
- No intermediate frames should ever be visible to the user on conversation load.

Why this matters: every test surface from earlier in the night flashes before being torn down. It's visually noisy and looks broken to a user. Also implies the renderer is doing O(history) work on load instead of O(live-surfaces).

Implementation hint:
- Fold A2UI message log into a Map<surfaceId, SurfaceState> on load.
- Drop surfaceIds where the most recent message was deleteSurface.
- Render the folded snapshot in a single pass; subscribe to new messages thereafter.

Acceptance:
- Reopen conversation with N prior surfaces, 0 of them live → renders nothing related to A2UI.
- Reopen with M live surfaces → renders each one in its final state, no flicker, no prior state visible.

Related: letta-mobile-mvon (action queueing on stale WS), letta-mobile-92wq (ChoicePicker doesn't render). Same A2UI renderer initiative (epic letta-mobile-51xm).

title: Subscribe to vibesync SSE stream and invalidate cached project work on relevant events
description:
Vibesync emits a server-wide SSE stream at `GET /api/events/stream` (`vibesync src/api/routes/events.ts:7`) carrying `connected`, `sync:triggered`, `sync:started`, `sync:completed`, `sync:error`, `config:updated`, `health:updated`, and `temporal:schedule-*`. Today letta-mobile only refreshes work data on user pull-to-refresh / screen resume.

Add a `VibesyncEventStreamRepository` (similar shape to existing WS transports under `core/.../data/transport/`) that maintains a single SSE connection to the active backend while a Project workspace screen is foregrounded. On `sync:completed` or `config:updated` for the active project, evict the relevant `ProjectWorkRepository` cache entries and refresh ready-work + issue list.

# Implementation notes
- Use Ktor's SSE support (already in the Ktor client deps).
- Lifecycle: bind to the project workspace nav scope, disconnect on dispose; reconnect with exponential backoff (cap 30s) on drop.
- Per existing `ServerHealthRepository` precedent, treat non-2xx as "stream unavailable" and degrade silently — feature is additive.
- Out of scope: per-molecule event stream (`/molecules/:id/events`), formula UI — P2 follow-on.
- Useful for E1 (manual sync trigger) — see that bead for the coupling.
acceptance_criteria:
1. When a Project workspace is open and a sync:completed event arrives, the issue list and ready-work refetch automatically within 1s.
2. Disconnect + reconnect over background/foreground transitions does not duplicate refetches.
3. On a backend that 404s the stream endpoint, the workspace still works (manual refresh path unchanged).
4. Unit test for event-to-cache-eviction routing; instrumented test for one full reconnect cycle.

title: Surface PM-agent metadata on Project workspace header
description:
Vibesync exposes `GET /api/agents/lookup?repo={repo}` (per-project) and `GET /api/agents` (catalog), both returning the Letta PM agent metadata stored against a project (`vibesync src/api/routes/agents.ts`). The Android Project workspace currently has no link from a project to its PM agent.

Add a small "PM agent" chip on the Project detail header. Tapping it deep-links into the Chat tab opened to that agent (existing nav target — same one used for chat shortcuts today).

# Implementation notes
- New `ProjectAgentApi.lookup(repo: String): PmAgentMetadata?` returning `null` on 404.
- Use the project's `name` or repo alias as the lookup key (vibesync resolves aliases server-side — no client normalization needed).
- ViewModel exposes `pmAgent: StateFlow<PmAgentMetadata?>`; chip hidden when null.
- Tap opens Chat scoped to the resolved `agent_id` (use the existing chat-route deep link).
acceptance_criteria:
1. When /api/agents/lookup?repo=<projectName> returns 200, chip shows the agent name + status dot.
2. 404 returns chip hidden, no error toast.
3. Tap opens Chat scoped to the resolved agent_id (use the existing chat-route deep link).
4. Test: ProjectAgentApiTest covers 200 / 404 / 503; ProjectDetailViewModelTest covers chip visibility.

title: Dispatcher/scope discipline — audit and inject TestDispatchers in long-running tests
description:
# Why

Three test files reference `Dispatchers.IO` / `Dispatchers.Main` / `GlobalScope` directly:
- `core/.../data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoopTest.kt`
- `feature-chat/.../A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest.kt`
- `app/.../di/DispatcherModuleTest.kt`

More worrying: the systems under test (`TimelineSyncLoop`, `ChannelTransport`, `CronRepository`, …) launch their own coroutines on `Dispatchers.IO` via internal `CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)`. Tests can't deterministically join those — they leak across tests in a reused JVM.

# Scope

- Survey every `@Singleton` (or otherwise long-lived) class that constructs its own `CoroutineScope` with a hardcoded dispatcher.
- Add a primary constructor that accepts a `CoroutineScope` (or `CoroutineDispatcher`), with a Hilt-friendly secondary `@Inject` constructor that wraps the existing default. This is the pattern used in `CronRepository` already.
- Update tests to pass `TestScope`'s `backgroundScope` + `UnconfinedTestDispatcher` (or `StandardTestDispatcher` with `advanceUntilIdle`) so `runTest` can deterministically drive the SUT.
- Fix the three known offending test files explicitly.

# Acceptance

- Every long-lived class on the test path takes a constructor-injected scope/dispatcher.
- The three files above either delete their `Dispatchers.*` direct references or document why they're load-bearing (e.g., DispatcherModuleTest is intentionally testing the module).
- `:core:testDebugUnitTest` runs `TimelineSyncLoopTest` in a reused daemon JVM without flakes.

title: ConversationRepository: FakeConversationRepository against existing IConversationRepository
description:
# Why

`IConversationRepository` interface already exists. `ConversationPickerSheetTest` is the lone offender mocking the concrete class.

# Scope

- Hand-write `FakeConversationRepository` under `core/testutil/`.
- Ensure call sites (currently injecting the concrete) prefer the interface where the test surface needs to swap it.
- Replace `mockk<ConversationRepository>` in `ConversationPickerSheetTest`.

# Acceptance

- `ConversationPickerSheetTest` uses `FakeConversationRepository`.
- No new mockk of concrete `ConversationRepository`.

title: SettingsRepository: ISettingsRepository + FakeSettingsRepository (fixes ShimBackendDetectorTest flake)
description:
# Why

`SettingsRepository` is the canonical flake source — the ShimBackendDetectorTest CI failure (#126's red run, resolved via rerun) was a `MockKException` at `every { activeConfig } returns MutableStateFlow(config)` inside a `mockk<SettingsRepository>(relaxed = true)` block. The class holds DataStore-backed `StateFlow`s for active config, theme, favorite agent, pinned agents, etc.; mocking it via mockk's class-redefinition is exactly the pattern that contaminates a reused JVM.

# Scope

- Define `ISettingsRepository` (under `core/.../data/repository/api/`, mirroring the existing IAgentRepository etc.).
- Implement on the concrete `SettingsRepository`.
- Hand-write `FakeSettingsRepository` under `core/testutil/` exposing each flow as a mutable backing for tests + simple setters for the suspend writes.
- Update injection sites: `ShimBackendDetector`, `ChannelHeartbeatSync`, `ClientModeController`, plus any others that reach for the concrete class.
- Replace `mockk<SettingsRepository>` in `ShimBackendDetectorTest`, `ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest`, `ClientModeControllerTest`.

# Acceptance

- Three target test files use `FakeSettingsRepository` instead of mockk.
- `:core:testDebugUnitTest` runs `ShimBackendDetectorTest` cleanly in a reused daemon JVM (10 consecutive runs).
- No new mockk usage of the concrete `SettingsRepository` (gated by the lint child).

title: AgentRepository: FakeAgentRepository against existing IAgentRepository
description:
# Why

`IAgentRepository` already exists at `core/.../data/repository/api/IAgentRepository.kt`. But tests still mockk the concrete class.

# Scope

- Hand-write `FakeAgentRepository` under `core/testutil/`.
- Convert injection sites that don't already take the interface: ensure they declare `IAgentRepository` rather than the concrete class.
- Replace `mockk<AgentRepository>` in: `NotificationReplyHandlerTest`, `ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest`, `ConversationRepositoryTest`.

# Acceptance

- Three target test files use `FakeAgentRepository`.
- No new mockk of concrete `AgentRepository`.

title: ChannelTransport: extract interface + FakeChannelTransport (retire mockk in cron tests)
description:
# Why

`CronRepository` (and any future repo speaking shim WS) currently must mock the concrete `ChannelTransport` in tests. Mockk's bytecode redefinition of a final class with internal state (`Mutex`, `AtomicReference<WebSocket?>`, `CoroutineScope`, `ConcurrentHashMap` of pending requests) is the canonical daemon-contamination source.

# Scope

- Extract `ChannelTransport` public surface needed by repositories into an interface (e.g. `IChannelTransport` or split into `CronTransport` + `ChatTransport` if narrower seams are useful). Put under `core/.../data/transport/api/` mirroring `data/repository/api/`.
- Implement the interface on the existing concrete `ChannelTransport`.
- Repos (`CronRepository` today; future cron-adjacent code) inject the interface.
- `core/testutil/FakeChannelTransport` hand-written test double: exposes `MutableSharedFlow<ServerFrame>` for events, `MutableStateFlow<State>` for state, and synchronous return-value seams for the suspend send helpers.
- Replace `mockk<ChannelTransport>` in `CronRepositoryTest` with `FakeChannelTransport`.

# Out of scope

- Reworking `AdminChatViewModel`'s direct transport usage (separate sweep — and it already injects via Hilt, mostly mockk-free in test surface today).
- Adding wire-mocking to FakeChannelTransport (use the existing MockWebServer-backed pattern for true round-trip tests in `feature-chat`).

# Acceptance

- `CronRepositoryTest` no longer uses `mockk<ChannelTransport>`.
- The Fake supports all the suspend send helpers + state/events flows the repo touches today.
- Running `:core:testDebugUnitTest` repeatedly within the same gradle daemon JVM is stable (10 consecutive runs, no flakes).

title: Epic: Test isolation — make the suite daemon-safe, retire --no-daemon in CI
description:
# Summary

CI currently runs gradle with `--no-daemon` because shared-JVM state contaminates tests when the daemon is reused. The recent ShimBackendDetectorTest flake (3 tests, MockKException at line 113) on PR #126 is the canonical symptom: the file passes in isolation but fails in the full suite. Audit + fix the patterns that depend on a fresh JVM per build so we can re-enable the daemon and recover the build-time hit.

# Root causes (from audit)

1. **mockk<ConcreteClass>(...)** for stateful classes (74 call sites total). Mockk's bytecode instrumentation doesn't always reset cleanly across tests; concrete-class mocks for repositories with `StateFlow`/`Mutex`/`val cachedX` fields are the biggest contamination vector.
2. **Code under test launches on `Dispatchers.IO` / `GlobalScope`**. Tests can't deterministically join those.
3. **Inject-it-yourself singletons** (no constructor-injected `CoroutineScope` or `Dispatcher`) — tests cannot substitute a `TestScope`.

# Audit numbers (2026-05-19)

- 239 test files across core / designsystem / feature-chat / feature-editagent / app / bot.
- 74 `mockk<ConcreteClass>` call sites. Top stateful targets:
  - `TimelineRepository`: 11 mock setups (2 files).
  - `ConversationRepository`: 5 (1 file).
  - `LettaApiClient`: 23 (mostly mocks `getClient()` — see below).
  - `SettingsRepository`: 3 (2 files).
  - `AgentRepository`: 3 (3 files).
  - Other (NotificationReplyHandler, ChannelSyncStateStore, ChannelNotificationPublisher, BotGateway, ClientModeController, ServerHealthRepository, MessageRepository, NotificationDelivery, InternalBotClient, ClientModeChatSender): 1–3 each.
  - `LettaApiClient` is mostly an HTTP wrapper around an Engine-mocked HttpClient — generally low-risk, separate bead if at all.
- 3 test files reference `GlobalScope`/`Dispatchers.IO`/`Dispatchers.Main` directly: `DispatcherModuleTest`, `TimelineSyncLoopTest`, `A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest`.
- 0 `@HiltAndroidTest` — Hilt is not currently a test-isolation concern.
- 17 existing `Fake*Api` doubles in `core/testutil/` — pattern works, just needs to be extended to repositories + transports + handlers.

# Strategy

For each stateful collaborator on the hot path:

1. **Promote to an interface** (`IFooRepository`-style). The repo already has the pattern: `IAgentRepository`, `IBlockRepository`, `IConversationRepository`, `IToolRepository`.
2. **Hand-write a `FakeFoo` against that interface**, parked under `core/testutil/` next to the Fake APIs.
3. **Inject the interface** at the call sites whose tests mock the concrete class today.
4. Tests substitute the `FakeFoo`; mockk usage retires.

For dispatcher/scope contamination:
- Inject `CoroutineScope` and/or `CoroutineDispatcher` rather than touching `Dispatchers.IO` directly inside the SUT. Tests pass `TestScope`'s `backgroundScope` + `UnconfinedTestDispatcher`.

Plus a CI grep-gate to prevent new `mockk<*Repository>` / `mockk<ChannelTransport>` etc. from regressing the audit.

# Phases (filed as children)

1. ChannelTransport: extract interface + FakeChannelTransport; retire mockk in CronRepositoryTest (and any future cron tests).
2. SettingsRepository: extend `ISettingsRepository` + FakeSettingsRepository; retire mockk in `ShimBackendDetectorTest`, `ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest`, `ClientModeControllerTest`.
3. AgentRepository: leverage existing `IAgentRepository`; FakeAgentRepository; retire mockk in `NotificationReplyHandlerTest`, `ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest`, `ConversationRepositoryTest`.
4. ConversationRepository: leverage existing `IConversationRepository`; FakeConversationRepository; retire mockk in `ConversationPickerSheetTest`.
5. TimelineRepository: define a narrower test seam (interface OR functional decomposition — TimelineRepository is large); retire mockk in `NotificationReplyHandlerTest`, `WsChatSendCoordinatorTest`.
6. Smaller hotspots (NotificationReplyHandler, ChannelSyncStateStore, ChannelNotificationPublisher, BotGateway, ClientModeController, NotificationDelivery): one combined sweep.
7. Dispatcher/scope discipline: audit + inject Dispatcher/Scope in `TimelineSyncLoopTest`, `A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest`, `DispatcherModuleTest`.
8. CI grep-gate / preflight check forbidding `mockk<KnownStatefulClass>` patterns.
9. Re-enable Gradle daemon in CI; track build-time delta.

# Out of scope

- Migrating all 74 mockk sites — only stateful collaborators on the contamination path.
- Replacing mockk with a different mocking library entirely.
- LettaApiClient's 23 sites (mostly mock `getClient()` which returns an Engine-mocked HttpClient — separate concern).

# Success criteria

- CI runs `:core:testDebugUnitTest` (and per-module test tasks) WITHOUT `--no-daemon` and is stable across 10 consecutive PR runs.
- No new mockk-on-stateful-collaborator patterns added (gated by .8).
- Measured CI test-job wall-clock improvement reported in the .9 follow-up.

# Reported by

Test audit 2026-05-19 following PR #126 ShimBackendDetectorTest flake (resolved via rerun; root cause still present in main).

title: Provision Beads remote for a project from the Android UI
description:
Wire the action half of B1. Add a "Provision Beads remote" button on the Project detail screen (visible when `beads_remote.status != provisioned`) that calls `POST /api/projects/{id}/beads-remote/provision` with `{ "push": true }`. Show a confirmation dialog before posting; show a snackbar with the result and refresh the B1 status chip.

Replaces the placeholder `screen_projects_new_project_manual_pending` string.

# Implementation notes
- Add `suspend fun provisionBeadsRemote(id: String, push: Boolean = true)` to the same API class as B1.
- Surface `dry_run` field in the response in the snackbar (vibesync returns it when configured).
- Disable the button while in-flight; re-fetch B1 status on completion.
- Depends on B1's `BeadsRemoteStatus` model and view-model wiring.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Button visible only when status is not_provisioned or failed.
2. Confirmation dialog uses project-name and warns the action is irreversible (per vibesync semantics).
3. On 2xx the chip refreshes via the B1 call and the snackbar shows the resolved remote URL.
4. On 503 (DoltHub provisioning not available) the snackbar shows a clear feature-disabled message.
5. The screen_projects_new_project_manual_pending string is deleted.
6. Test coverage: API test for the POST shape; ViewModel test for the disabled-while-inflight transition.

title: Show Beads-remote provisioning status on Project detail
description:
Vibesync exposes per-project Beads-remote provisioning state at `GET /api/projects/{id}/beads-remote` (and as `beads_remote` on `GET /api/projects` summaries; see `serializeBeadsRemoteStatus` in `vibesync src/api/routes/projects.ts:170`). Today the Android Project detail screen has no surface for this; the only existing string referencing provisioning is `screen_projects_new_project_manual_pending` ("provisioning isn't wired").

Add a status chip on the Project detail header that shows one of: `provisioned`, `not_provisioned`, `failed`. Tooltip/secondary line shows `provisioned_at` when present. No mutation in this bead — sibling B2 wires the action.

# Implementation notes
- Extend `ProjectApi` (or new `BeadsRemoteApi`) with `suspend fun getBeadsRemoteStatus(id: String): BeadsRemoteStatus`.
- Add `BeadsRemoteStatus` Kotlin model with fields `status`, `provisioned_at`, `remote_url`, `error`.
- Wire into `ProjectDetailViewModel` as a side-load; failures degrade silently (status chip hidden).
- Vibesync resolves project aliases server-side (commit 130e902) — no client normalization needed.

# Files
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/ProjectApi.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Project.kt`
- `android-compose/feature-projects/src/main/.../ProjectDetailViewModel.kt`
- corresponding tests under `core/src/test/.../data/api/`
acceptance_criteria:
1. Project detail screen renders a chip reflecting the live beads_remote.status for the active project.
2. provisioned state shows the relative time of provisioned_at.
3. failed state shows the error string from the response on tap.
4. Endpoint failure (404 / 503) renders no chip and logs at info; does not break the screen.
5. Covered by a ProjectApiTest case and a ProjectDetailViewModelTest case.

title: Epic: Adopt vibesync v2 capabilities (provisioning, agent lookup, live events, sync, debug)
description:
Vibesync has shipped (commits since 2026-04-15) the following client-relevant capabilities that letta-mobile has not yet integrated: Beads-remote provisioning per project and fleet-wide, Letta PM-agent lookup, server-wide SSE stream, manual sync trigger + history, /health + /api/stats debug surface, and the AGENTS.md refresh admin endpoint. The OpenAPI 3.1 spec at /openapi.json + Scalar UI at /docs document all of it; analytics, ready-work, mutations, and aliases are already consumed by ProjectWorkApi/ProjectApi.

This epic groups the P0 user-visible work (B1, B2, C1, D1) and the P1 operator/debug work (E1, I1, K1). P2 capabilities (formula orchestration, temporal schedule controls, remote-file mount, OpenAPI contract gate, work-items projection) are out of scope; spawn follow-on epics if/when desired.

# Phases (children, in order)
1. B1 — Beads-remote status chip on Project detail
2. B2 — Beads-remote provisioning action (depends on B1)
3. C1 — PM-agent lookup on Project header
4. D1 — Vibesync SSE event stream then cache invalidation
5. E1 — Manual sync trigger per project
6. I1 — Backend status panel (debug)
7. K1 — AGENTS.md refresh admin action (debug)

# Non-goals
- formula/molecule UI
- temporal schedule controls
- remote file mount
- work-items projection adoption
- openapi-driven CI gate

# Vibesync references (commits on main since 2026-04-15)
- 210894b Phase 1 OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation and /openapi.json endpoint
- dd4a9cc feat(api) add Scalar documentation endpoint
- 2d3bbca docs(api) document generated API docs endpoints
- e4579de feat(api) batch Beads remote provisioning
- e057410 feat(api) expose issue mutation service
- bf66f2d feat(api) issue analytics endpoint
- 130e902 fix(api) resolve project aliases for Android routes
- 2ede6a9 / 9b8c221 / e4477bb Beads hydration on stale cache
