title: Temporarily disable PR perf gate
description: Disable the Android Perf Gate from running on pull_request for now because macrobenchmark CI is too slow/noisy for regular PR throughput. Keep manual dispatch available so perf checks can still be run intentionally.

title: Catalog: com.letta.mobile:schedule/v1 reference implementation
description:
# Why

End-to-end reference for what a domain catalog looks like in this codebase. Schedule is a good first pick: ScheduleApi + cron docs already exist; the surface is small enough to fit one cycle; the result is a template for project-issue/agent-dispatch/etc.

# Scope

- New catalog id com.letta.mobile:schedule/v1 negotiated in welcome capability handshake.
- Two domain widgets: ScheduleCard (status row with next/last run, name, agent, swipe affordances) and ScheduleSelectorInput (3-mode picker: Cron / Every / At, with human-readable preview).
- Fixed-shape actions: schedule.save, schedule.delete, schedule.pause, schedule.resume, schedule.run_now. All carry { id } or { create_params } in context.
- Editor composed from existing basic widgets + ScheduleSelectorInput (no third widget needed).
- A surface that emits N ScheduleCards uses the qr6r.2 ListView template.

# Out of scope

- Replacing or removing the native ScheduleListScreen (separate product decision; this catalog complements it).
- Cron-expression preview localization beyond English.
- Recurrence-rule full RFC 5545 support — match the letta cron --cron/--every/--at three modes.

# Acceptance

- Renderer recognizes schedule/v1 catalog id; ScheduleCard + ScheduleSelectorInput render.
- ListView + ScheduleCard end-to-end: agent emits 5 schedules from one template; user taps one; editor surface appears; user changes selector; submits; fixed-shape user_action fires.
- Tests cover all five schedule.* actions and the three selector modes (cron / every / at).
- Round-trip integration test against a mocked shim, modeled after A2uiToolApprovalRoundTripTest.

# Dependencies

- Blocked on qr6r.1, qr6r.2 (list templating), qr6r.6 (Dropdown) — selector mode uses Dropdown.
notes: PR #129 opened: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/129. Commit 047d1195 adds schedule/v1 capability negotiation, ScheduleCard, ScheduleSelectorInput, renderer tests for schedule actions/selector modes, and mocked shim WebSocket round-trip coverage. Local validation passed after rebasing onto merged PR #127: focused protocol/renderer/integration tests, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555. CI is running on PR #129; next step is monitor checks, fix if needed, then squash-merge and close this bead. Bootstrap: bd show letta-mobile-qr6r.12 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 129

title: Renderer: Tabs / Accordion (local-state widgets)
description:
# Why

First two consumers of the local-only UI state convention from qr6r.3. Tab selection and accordion-open are per-user UX state; round-tripping them via updateDataModel would create chat-traffic churn and break the no-on-device-state principle.

# Scope

- "Tabs" widget: { items: [{ key, label }, ...], default, child-template-id } — switches which child component is visible based on the local-state tab key.
- "Accordion" widget: { items: [{ key, title, child }] } — each item independently expandable; default-open via localState.
- Both consume the LocalA2uiLocalState scope from qr6r.3 for selection / open state.
- Smooth crossfade on tab change.

# Out of scope

- Tabs with badges in the tab label (use FilterChip if needed).
- Drag-to-rearrange.

# Acceptance

- Tabs widget renders N tabs; tapping a tab updates local-only state; child swaps with crossfade.
- Accordion items expand/collapse independently; state survives recomposition; resets on surface deletion.
- Tests cover both with the localState scope.

# Dependencies

- Blocked on qr6r.3 (local-state convention).

title: Renderer: Dropdown / Select
description:
# Why

Single-choice selection over options. Without it, an agent has to compose a Radio list, which gets ugly past 3-4 options.

# Scope

- "Dropdown" widget in A2uiComponentNode, rendered as Material3 ExposedDropdownMenuBox.
- Options source: static raw["options"] array (each { key, label }), or items-path binding for dynamic option lists.
- Two-way binding on selected key.
- Optional placeholder, label, isError supportingText.
- Respect surfaceSubmitting.

# Out of scope

- Multi-select dropdowns.
- Searchable/typeahead — separate widget if needed.
- Grouped options.

# Acceptance

- Renders options; user picks one; bound data-model path receives selected key.
- Tests cover static options and items-bound options paths.

title: Renderer: Chip / FilterChip / Badge
description:
# Why

Status indicators (Badge) and tappable tag chips (Chip/FilterChip) are bread-and-butter UI primitives the catalog is missing.

# Scope

- Three small widgets: "Chip" (Material3 AssistChip; emits user_action on tap), "FilterChip" (toggleable; two-way bound to a boolean path), "Badge" (visual-only; reads count or text via binding).
- Chip/FilterChip support a leading icon by name (reuses qr6r.9 Icon resolution if landed, otherwise skips).

# Out of scope

- ChipGroup as a container — agents can compose with Row + Chips.

# Acceptance

- All three render in A2uiRendererTest.
- Chip emits a user_action; FilterChip patches its bound boolean; Badge reflects updates via updateDataModel.

title: Renderer: form widgets — Checkbox / Switch / Radio
description:
# Why

Basic catalog has TextField and DateTimeInput but no boolean / single-choice inputs. Agents reach for these constantly in any settings/edit flow.

# Scope

- Three new widgets in A2uiComponentNode: "Checkbox", "Switch", "Radio".
- Two-way binding: read value via existing bindingPath() pattern; on change, apply patch via surface.dataModel.applyPatch.
- Radio: takes either a static options array or an items-path binding; current-value path; emits selected option key.
- Each respects surfaceSubmitting for the disabled state.

# Out of scope

- Multi-select checkboxes-as-list — covered by Checkbox + ListView (qr6r.2).
- Material3 styling overrides via raw["color"] — keep defaults for now.

# Acceptance

- Each widget renders via A2uiRendererTest with a literal initial value.
- Toggling each patches the bound data-model path with a JsonPrimitive(true|false|key).
- Tests: bound + unbound (local) value paths; surfaceSubmitting disables the input.
notes: Implemented A2UI Checkbox/Switch/Radio on feat/a2ui-form-widgets; PR #120 opened: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/120. Local validation passed: :feature-chat:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.feature.chat.A2uiRendererTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555. Remaining: wait for PR #120 checks (perf-gate, test), fix failures if any, squash-merge when green, then close this bead and bd dolt push. Bootstrap: bd show letta-mobile-qr6r.4 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 120

title: Renderer: numeric inputs — Slider / Stepper
description:
# Why

Numeric input via TextField with textFieldType:number works but is clunky on mobile. Slider and Stepper are the natural primitives.

# Scope

- Add "Slider" and "Stepper" branches to A2uiComponentNode.
- Slider props: min, max, step, value-binding. Renders Material3 Slider.
- Stepper props: min, max, step, value-binding, label. Compound +/- buttons around a value display.
- Both two-way bind: emit JsonPrimitive(Long) when step is integral, JsonPrimitive(Double) otherwise.
- Respect surfaceSubmitting.

# Out of scope

- Range slider (two thumbs) — separate widget.
- Discrete-tick rendering customization.

# Acceptance

- Each widget renders against A2uiRendererTest with min/max/step props.
- Bound value path updates as the user drags or taps +/-.
- Tests cover integer vs float emission per step type.
notes: Implemented A2UI Slider/Stepper on feat/a2ui-numeric-inputs; PR #121 opened: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/121. Local validation passed: :feature-chat:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.feature.chat.A2uiRendererTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug, and adb install to 192.168.50.235:5555. Remaining: wait for PR #121 checks, fix failures if any, squash-merge when green, then close this bead and bd dolt push. Bootstrap: bd show letta-mobile-qr6r.5 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 121

title: A2UI user_action drops TextField values — only surfaceId reaches the shim
description:
When a user submits a form rendered via A2UI (TextField + Button), the mobile A2UI client sends a user_action frame whose context contains ONLY surfaceId. The user's actual typed value is lost on the mobile side before the frame leaves the device.

# Evidence
Three recent user_actions captured by the shim (state/runs/<id>/user-actions.jsonl):
- demo-hello-1 / demo.send → context: {"surfaceId":"demo-hello-1"}
- feedback-001 / submit_feedback → context: {"surfaceId":"feedback-001"}
- feedback-002 / dismiss_surface → context: {"surfaceId":"feedback-002"}

Mobile is the source of context. The shim ack and the synthetic-turn dispatcher both surface whatever context arrived; they don't strip anything. Verified via the host's handleUserAction path (admin-shim/lib/mobile-channel-host.ts:438) and the recorded jsonl.

# Why it matters
Even though the shim now properly routes user_actions as a synthetic agent turn (cron-fix work landed today + lcp-crp), the agent gets a turn with no actual user input — only "the user submitted on surface X". The agent has nothing to respond TO. Users report 'the agent can not see what I responded with' in conversations that involved A2UI TextField submissions.

# Fix direction
Mobile A2UI client must populate user_action.context with the relevant values when emitting an action triggered by a form-style component:
- TextField submit → context.value (or context.text, or full field-by-field snapshot)
- ChoicePicker → context.selectedId / context.selectedValue
- Multi-field forms → context per component

A2UI v0.9 has two viable paths:
(a) Server enables createSurface.sendDataModel:true and mobile includes the data model with each user_action. The shim's current A2UI block instructs the agent NOT to set sendDataModel because the shim wasn't ready to consume it — that restriction can be lifted now that the synthetic-turn path exists.
(b) Mobile includes the relevant input values directly under user_action.context without going through the data_model channel.

Either works for shim consumption; the format function (formatSyntheticA2uiAction in mobile-channel-host.ts:498) does a full context JSON dump so anything mobile sends will reach the agent.
acceptance_criteria:
- After fix, a TextField submission produces a user_action whose context contains the typed value (e.g. context.value or context.<componentId>).
- The shim's recorded user-actions.jsonl shows the value, and the resulting synthetic agent turn's user input includes it.
- A test sequence (agent renders TextField+Button → user types 'hello' → user taps Submit) results in the agent receiving 'hello' as the user input on its synthetic turn.
notes:
Fix in progress on branch fix/lwmo-a2ui-textfield-values. Both paths implemented in A2uiRenderer.action(): (a) honors surface.sendDataModel == true; (b) safety-net auto-attach when declared context is empty and data model is non-empty. Tool-approval round-trip unaffected (separate code path). Tests added: empty-context fallback, sendDataModel:true preserves declared bindings, sendDataModel:false + declared context omits data_model (regression guard). Awaiting feature-chat unit-test results.
Fix committed as 358d951c on branch fix/lwmo-a2ui-textfield-values and pushed to origin. APK built + installed on Pixel 9 Pro (192.168.50.235:5555). Three unit tests pass: empty-context fallback, sendDataModel:true preserves declared bindings, sendDataModel:false + declared context omits data_model (regression guard). Existing buttonActionResolvesBoundContextAgainstCurrentDataModel still passes. Ready for device verification + PR.
PR opened: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/124. Rebased onto main (which now has qr6r.4/.5/.6 form-widget/numeric-input/dropdown commits). Root cause turned out to be TWO layers: (1) shim emits $<id>.<field> template shorthand that the renderer didn't resolve, (2) unbound TextField wrote to Compose-local state only (letta-mobile-ykkl) so even with shorthand resolution the data model was empty. Fix: renderer routes unbound input to a synthetic /_inputs/<id> data-model slot and the resolver matches it. Verified on device — agent received 'hello world' for $note-field.value reference. 33 A2uiRendererTest cases pass.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.6: Tests — WS roundtrip + Compose UI
description: Coverage: CronFrame serialization roundtrip (unit), ChannelTransport routes cron_list_response correctly, CronRepository (crons_updated re-fetch + request_id correlation + no leak on subscribe/unsubscribe), SchedulesScreen Compose (list/empty/swipe-delete), CreateScheduleScreen Compose (validation per recurrence kind).
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests green via project's standard runner.
- No real-network dependencies; FakeChannelTransport for WS.
- Covers: frame roundtrip, repo lifecycle, list+form UI states.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.5: REST read-only fallback for schedules when WS is offline
description: When mobile WS is disconnected, schedules list still renders by reading GET /v1/crons over the existing HTTP client. CronRepository merges WS-live + REST snapshot, preferring WS. Mutations stay WS-only per shim contract.
acceptance_criteria:
- List renders within 1s when WS offline via GET /v1/crons.
- WS reconnect supersedes REST snapshot without flicker or duplicates.
- Mutation while WS offline surfaces 'reconnect required', not an HTTP fallback.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.4: Schedules tab UI — create/edit form (Compose)
description: CreateScheduleScreen with fields: name (required), description (required), prompt (required, multi-line), recurrence (segmented control: Every / At / Cron), agent picker (defaults to current), conversation picker (defaults to 'default'). Every kind: presets (5m, 1h, 1d) + custom; resulting cron preview. At kind: time picker; reject times >5min in past. Cron kind: raw expression with validation + next-fire preview. Submit → CronRepository.addSchedule; spinner; error toast; success returns to list. Edit via delete+recreate (matches Letta Cloud — protocol has no cron_update).
acceptance_criteria:
- All three recurrence kinds produce a valid CronTask the shim accepts.
- Invalid cron expression shows inline error and does not submit.
- Past time on At kind shows error and does not submit.
- Submit during WS reconnect queues the request, retries once, surfaces failure cleanly.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.3: Schedules tab UI — list screen (Compose)
description: Top-level schedules tab showing current schedules for the selected agent. Observes CronRepository.schedulesFlow(currentAgentId). Item rendering: name, description, recurrence summary, next-fire estimate, fire_count + last_fired_at for recurring. Empty state with CTA. Top app bar action → create form. Swipe-to-delete → confirm → deleteSchedule. Tab integration with existing nav.
acceptance_criteria:
- Schedules tab visible alongside existing tabs; selecting it shows the list within one WS roundtrip.
- Swipe-to-delete invokes deleteSchedule and the item disappears on response.
- Rotation does not duplicate items or break the active subscription.
- Empty state renders correctly when there are zero schedules for the current agent.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.2: CronRepository (WS-reactive state + request_id correlation)
description:
Single source of truth for the schedules list, observing WS push frames and exposing a Flow the UI binds to.

# What to build
- CronRepository (likely under data/repository/) with: schedulesFlow(agentId): Flow<List<CronTask>>, addSchedule(task): Result<CronTask>, deleteSchedule(id): Result<Unit>, refresh(agentId): suspend.
- On observe: emit cron_list and start observing cron_list_response + crons_updated push frames.
- crons_updated push triggers an automatic cron_list refresh scoped to the affected agent_id (if scope present in the frame), else full refresh.
- Coroutine-scoped, cancellable; survives WS reconnects (re-subscribes on reconnect).
- DI wiring (Hilt/Koin per existing project conventions).

# Why
- Without the repo, every UI screen would manually wire WS observers, leak them on rotation, and duplicate fetches.
acceptance_criteria:
- Subscribing schedulesFlow returns the current snapshot within one WS roundtrip.
- A crons_updated push for the agent in scope causes schedulesFlow to re-emit a fresh list within 100ms.
- addSchedule/deleteSchedule resolve on the response, not on transport-ack.
- Repeated subscribe/unsubscribe doesn't leak frames or duplicate cron_list calls (test).
notes: PR opened (stacked on #126): https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/128. 8 CronRepositoryTest cases pass — dedup, push refresh, response-bound mutations, reconnect, parallel-refresh dedup, schedulesFlow snapshot. Wider compileDebugKotlin clean across core/feature-chat/app. d52f.3 (List screen UI) and d52f.4 (Create/edit form) are now unblocked at the data-layer; both need the repo's schedulesFlow + addSchedule/deleteSchedule.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.1: Kotlin types + WS cron frame parsing in ChannelTransport
description:
Add data classes + frame routing so the mobile WS layer can speak the shim's cron protocol.

# What to build
- CronTask data class mirroring the wire shape (id, agent_id, conversation_id, name, description, cron, timezone, recurring, prompt, status, created_at, expires_at, last_fired_at, fire_count, cancel_reason, jitter_offset_ms, scheduled_for, fired_at, missed_at). Place under core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/ next to the existing Message types.
- CronFrame sealed hierarchy with subtypes for each inbound/outbound frame (CronList, CronAdd, CronGet, CronDelete, CronDeleteAll, *_Response, CronsUpdated). kotlinx.serialization annotations.
- Wire CronFrame parsing into the existing ChannelTransport frame dispatcher (the same dispatcher that handles assistant_message, tool_call_message, etc.).
- Send helpers on ChannelTransport: sendCronList(agentId, conversationId?), sendCronAdd(task), sendCronGet(id), sendCronDelete(id), sendCronDeleteAll(agentId). All carry a generated request_id for correlation.

# Why
- Sister to lcp-d5g.2 (shim WS frames). Frame names mirror Letta Cloud verbatim so a single client implementation could later target Cloud listeners.

# Notes
- Reuse the existing JSON setup; do not introduce a new serialization library.
- request_id correlation pattern already exists for other frames — reuse it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Encoding a CronAdd round-trips through kotlinx.serialization to the documented wire JSON byte-for-byte (unit test).
- ChannelTransport routes a cron_list_response frame to a Flow/callback the repo layer can observe.
- request_id correlation: a sent cron_get's response is delivered to the original caller's continuation, not broadcast.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/126 (commit 4e0ab2b8 on feat/cron-frames-7j6o). 15 new MobileWsFramesTest cases pass (28 total). Wider compileDebugKotlin clean across core/designsystem/feature-chat/app. Acceptance met: encoding round-trip ✓, response routing via events Flow ✓, request_id correlation via CompletableDeferred-per-request ✓. d52f.2 (CronRepository) is now unblocked.

title: A2UI form surfaces should auto-dismiss on submit or expose close affordance
description: When the agent emits an A2UI form surface (Card + TextField + submit Button), tapping submit fires the event but the surface stays rendered. A same-turn deleteSurface after createSurface/updateComponents did not clear it; a delete-only next turn did. Fix renderer lifecycle so deleteSurface removes the surface regardless of message-stream position, and add either a built-in close affordance or documented form close convention.
acceptance_criteria: Form-style A2UI surfaces include a close affordance or documented auto-dismiss convention. deleteSurface removes the Compose surface registry entry even when it appears in the same agent turn as create/update. Regression coverage proves same-turn delete and next-turn delete both clear the surface.

title: Epic: Schedules tab via WS cron protocol (sister to lcp-d5g)
description: Consume the shim's new WS cron protocol from the Android mobile app so users can view, create, edit, and delete scheduled prompts.

title: Epic: Schedules tab via WS cron protocol (sister to lcp-d5g)
description:
Consume the shim's new WS cron protocol from the Android mobile app so users can view, create, edit, and delete scheduled prompts. Mirror frame style to Letta Cloud's existing cron protocol so the same view model could later target Cloud-hosted listeners without rewriting.

# Why this exists
- Sister epic to admin-shim's lcp-d5g (Scheduled prompts via mobile WS + read-only REST). The shim is gaining the server-side capability; mobile needs the client-side capability.
- Today the schedules tab in the official Letta Code app talks to letta server's WS. Our shim is now exposing the same WS frame style, so the mobile-side implementation can target both backends with one code path.

# Scope
- Wire CronTask + CronFrame Kotlin types into the existing ChannelTransport (mobile WS layer).
- Add a CronRepository observing crons_updated push frames + cron_list_response.
- Build the schedules tab UI (list + create/edit form + detail).
- Add a REST read-only fallback that hits GET /v1/crons/* when WS is offline.
- Test coverage for WS roundtrip + UI states.

# Out of scope
- Pushing schedules to Letta Cloud's cron service (we are deliberately staying off Cloud for these agents per the shim's design).
- Background fire-time notification (the agent receives the prompt server-side; the mobile app just observes state).

# Children
Tracked via dependencies on this epic. Targets letta-mobile codebase under android-compose/.
design: Sister to lcp-d5g (admin-shim/lib). WS frame names mirror Letta Cloud verbatim (cron_list, cron_add, cron_get, cron_delete, cron_delete_all + *_response + crons_updated). Read-only REST fallback uses /v1/crons. Mutations exclusively over WS per the same WS-first rule the shim follows.
acceptance_criteria:
- User can open Schedules tab and see the list of cron tasks for the selected agent, populated via cron_list over WS.
- User can create a recurring task (every 1h / cron expr) and a one-shot task (--at) over WS; the entry appears immediately via crons_updated push.
- User can delete a task and the list updates without a manual refresh.
- When WS is offline, the list still renders (stale-but-shown) by hitting GET /v1/crons over REST.
- The schedules tab survives device rotation and WS reconnect without leaking observers or duplicating items.

title: ShimBackendDetectorTest fails when run after other core tests (mockk pollution)
description:
When running the full :core:testDebugUnitTest suite, 3 tests in ShimBackendDetectorTest fail with:

  io.mockk.MockKException: Missing mocked calls inside every { ... } block: make sure the object inside the block is a mock
  at ShimBackendDetectorTest.kt:98

Line 98 is: every { activeConfig } returns MutableStateFlow(config)

Root cause: SettingsRepository is a final @Singleton class. mockk needs to bytecode-rewrite it via mockk-agent-jvm. When another test in the same JVM loads SettingsRepository directly (e.g., SettingsRepositoryTest), the class is already loaded and the agent can't retransform.

Reproduces locally: passes in isolation (./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests ShimBackendDetectorTest = 100%), fails when run with the full suite. Same on CI for PRs #104 and #107 (a2ui phase 3-6 rebased onto main).

Why it only surfaced now: a2ui PRs added ~196 new test lines (A2uiDataModelTest). With forkEvery=100 in core/build.gradle.kts:69, this shifted test batches, putting ShimBackendDetectorTest in the same JVM batch as a test that loads SettingsRepository directly.

Fix options (least invasive first):
1. Lower forkEvery so ShimBackendDetectorTest gets a fresh JVM (slower CI)
2. Refactor ShimBackendDetectorTest to use a tiny fake instead of mocking SettingsRepository (cleanest)
3. Extract an ActiveConfigProvider interface that ShimBackendDetector takes instead of full SettingsRepository (refactors prod code)
4. Make SettingsRepository.activeConfig open (touches prod for test reasons)

title: Address CodeRabbit feedback on A2UI phase 4 PRs
description:
Follow-up from merging A2UI phase 3-7 PRs. CodeRabbit raised 8 actionable comments on PR #104 plus 1 nitpick on #106. Items to address in follow-up commits:

PR #104 (a2ui phase 3+4):
- WsBotClient.kt:115 — A2UI stream loss check (Major, Quick win)
- A2uiDataModel.kt:63 — JSON Pointer tokens: trim()+filter changes valid pointers (e.g. /foo/ targets root key). Preserve exact tokens.
- A2uiProtocol.kt:267 — Component id defaulting to '' masks malformed payloads. Fail fast instead.
- A2uiSurfaceManager.kt:176 — A2uiSurfaceState issue (Major)
- MobileWsFramesTest.kt:47 — Assert catalog IDs directly in supported_catalogs check
- A2uiRenderer.kt:224 — Guard input interactivity on writable bindings (controls stay interactive when binding.path absent but drop updates)
- AdminChatViewModel.kt:524 — Make A2uiDebugFrameUi.id collision-proof (current id can repeat across frames)
- docs/architecture/a2ui-ws-phase1-contract.md:32 — supported_widgets missing TextField, DateTimeInput, Image
- A2uiRendererTest.kt:239-248 (nitpick) — Strengthen DateTimeInput coverage

PR #106 (theme-aware splash):
- values-night/colors.xml — add same documentation comment as light mode file

title: Backend toggle does not invalidate per-session state (stale UI after switch)
description:
## Symptom
After toggling between configured backends in Letta Mobile, the client retains session-scoped state from the previous backend instead of treating the switch as a hard boundary. Next message goes to the new backend, but UI / A2UI surface state from the prior backend persists and 'sticks' — the new backend's responses do not update the surfaces.

Workaround: rotate to a new conversation, which forces a clean session.

## Expected
A backend switch should be treated as equivalent to a full reconnect:
- Active conversation context cleared (or re-scoped per backend)
- In-memory A2UI surfaces invalidated / deleted
- Negotiated capabilities re-handshaked against the new backend
- Any session-scoped caches re-keyed (or wiped) on switch

## Repro
1. Connect to backend A.
2. Exchange a few messages, ideally ones that produce A2UI surfaces.
3. Toggle to backend B via the backend picker.
4. Send a message.
5. Observe: prior A2UI surface state persists; new backend responses fail to update it.

## Root cause guess
Backend identity is not part of the cache key for session-scoped client state. The picker swaps the transport target without firing the cleanup / re-handshake path that a fresh reconnect would.

## Priority rationale
P2 — workaround exists (new conversation), but this is a real footgun for the A2UI rollout where caps and surfaces will change a lot during dev. Likely to bite during demos.

## Related
- A2UI initiative epic: letta-mobile-51xm (renderer)
- Capability negotiation: see admin-shim lcp-uo5.2 (logs negotiation per hello)

title: A2UI Button renders frame but no text label - child Text by ID not resolved into Button slot
description:
Button renders frame but resolves no label - Text child by ID not pulled into Button slot.

Symptom (Pixel 9 Pro, PID 12909, 2026-05-17 12:58 attempt 3, debug build with envelope fix from letta-mobile-1zrd):

After the envelope fix landed, A2UI surfaces now render. A createSurface + updateComponents pair was emitted from this Meridian chat, transport chip shows "WS A2UI basic 2" indicating WS active with A2UI negotiated and 2 frames received. The Column root, Row inner layout, and both Text components (h3 heading + body paragraph) all render correctly. However the two Button components render as empty pill shapes with no text label inside.

The buttons in the payload were structured per the A2UI v0.9 Basic Catalog Button spec:

  { id: "ok-btn", component: "Button", variant: "primary", child: "ok-label", action: {...} }
  { id: "ok-label", component: "Text", text: "It works" }

Button.child is documented as "the ID of the child component. Use a Text component for a labeled button." Both Text children (ok-label, broken-label) are present in the same components array of the same updateComponents message. Top-down ordering is respected: parent Button appears before child Text in the array per the spec.

Likely root cause:

The Android renderer's Button widget is finding its component slot (correct hit area and pill background prove it's rendering the Button frame) but not resolving the Text component referenced by id in the child field. Three plausible mechanisms:

1. The component registry does the id->component lookup at render time but Button is reading child as if it were inline definition data, not an id pointer. So it sees a string "ok-label" where it expected a Text struct and renders nothing.

2. The lookup is correct but the Text component is being shadowed or filtered out of the per-surface registry by the time Button renders. Possible if the renderer ingests components in array order and Button is being instantiated before its sibling Text is registered.

3. The label is being rendered but with zero size/transparent color due to a theme or style miscalculation specific to the Button's child Text context.

The renderer source path is likely com.letta.mobile.ui.surfaces.a2ui.* or a similar package. The Button composable should have a getComponent(id) lookup that returns a Text Composable wrapped in a Box that occupies the button's content slot.

Diagnostic checklist before changing code:

1. Confirm both Text components arrive in the updateComponents message body (server-side log of the parsed payload).
2. Confirm the renderer's per-surface component registry contains ok-label and broken-label after the updateComponents message is applied (in-process debug log or instrumented assert).
3. Confirm the Button composable's child lookup is being called with the right id string.
4. Confirm the result of the lookup is non-null when called from inside the Button slot.

Non-goals:

- Inline Text inside Button (the A2UI v0.9 spec explicitly forbids inline child definitions).
- Button styling, variant rendering, or theming changes.
- Fixing other unrelated widget rendering paths.

Rollback signal:

If the fix causes other component-id lookups to behave differently (Card.child, Modal.trigger/content, Tabs.tabs[].child), revert. The fix must be Button-specific or must be a shared lookup that has been audited against all component-id-by-reference call sites in the catalog.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A Button referencing a Text by id in its child field renders the Text content inside the button.
2. Repro: emit a createSurface + updateComponents pair containing a Button with child='label' and a sibling Text id='label' text='Click me'. The button visibly shows 'Click me'.
3. Tapping the button still emits a user_action over the WS as before; this fix does not regress the action round-trip.
4. The fix is verified against the same payload structure for Card.child, Modal.trigger, Modal.content, and Tabs.tabs[].child - all component-id references resolve correctly.
5. A renderer-side unit or instrumentation test covers the Button-with-child-id case so regressions surface in CI.
notes:
REGRESSION CONFIRMED 2026-05-17 16:51 EDT: Button child-id label resolution broken again, same symptom as the original njzb report.

Test: emitted render-test-6 (Card + Column + TextField + DateTimeInput + Row + 2 Buttons + 2 Dividers) from the Meridian chat after the WS handshake regression (letta-mobile-i8xl) was fixed.

What renders correctly (large win for the renderer):

- Transport chip: WS A2UI basic 2 (green) - i8xl is closed, handshake stable.
- Card wraps content with inset.
- Two Divider components render as horizontal lines, correctly positioned above and below the input block.
- TextField "Your name" renders with proper Material-style outlined input + label.
- DateTimeInput "Pick a date" renders with the same outlined-input shape and label.
- Column layout, Row layout, Card child-id resolution, TextField/DateTimeInput child-id resolution - all good.

What is broken:

- Both Buttons (Submit primary + Cancel default) render as empty pill backgrounds with no visible text labels. Exact same symptom as the original njzb description.

Why it's a regression, not the original bug:

The original njzb was filed when this was the only render issue. The fix landed during the renderer iteration cycle and was confirmed working (later screenshot showed "It works" / "Still broken" labels visible). Now, after the merge round that fixed letta-mobile-i8xl (the WS handshake), the Button-label issue is back. So:

- Card.child resolution: works (root Card -> form-column Column)
- Column.children resolution: works (all 6 form-column children visible)
- Row.children resolution: works (Row with submit-btn + cancel-btn produces correctly positioned pills)
- Button.child resolution: BROKEN (submit-btn and cancel-btn cannot resolve submit-label and cancel-label Text children)

The same merge that re-introduced i8xl probably touched the Button.child resolver path specifically. Other component-id resolvers (Card, Column, Row, Tabs etc.) are unaffected. Suggesting this is Button-specific code, not a shared lookup.

Diagnostic suggestion:

- git log --since="2026-05-17 14:00" the Button-related render files; the diff that re-introduced this is very likely small and recent.
- If Button-specific child rendering was reworked (e.g. to support Button icon variant or to handle a different prop name), check whether the Text branch was preserved.

Why narrowly Button and not all id-by-reference:

The catalog also requires Card.child, Modal.trigger/content, Tabs.tabs[].child to resolve component ids the same way. Card works in this test; Modal/Tabs not exercised but likely fine for the same reason. So whatever broke is in Button's specialization of the lookup, not in the shared registry.

This re-opens letta-mobile-njzb. Worth adding an explicit acceptance criterion to the bead: when the fix lands, ship with a screenshot-diff smoke test that asserts a Button-with-Text-child renders the Text content - to prevent a third reopening.
close_reason: Button.child resolves sibling Text by ID via resolveButtonLabel, preserving action dispatch; A2uiRendererTest covers and passes the regression case.

title: Feedback: A2UI render test produced blank block — markdown ate the <a2ui-json> tag because session is on REST not WS
description:
A2UI render test attempt: blank block, no card.

Context: Phase 5 server-side A2UI shipped (lcp-uo5.2 through lcp-uo5.6 on letta-code-parallel). Renderer-side bead is letta-mobile-ldgy. Emmanuel asked Meridian (this agent) to emit a ToolApprovalCard so the renderer could be tested end-to-end. Result: a blank block in the chat surface where the JSON used to be. No card, no raw JSON, no fallback text.

Root-cause read from ADB logcat on Pixel 9 Pro (192.168.50.235:5555, com.letta.mobile PID 9404):

1. This conversation (Meridian, agent-597b5756-2915-4560-ba6b-91005f085166) is routed over the REST+SSE transport. LettaApiClient / okhttp3 connection lines, streamSubscriber.merged growing assistant_message bytes chunk by chunk. Standard /v1/agents/{id}/messages stream.

2. Zero a2ui / hello / negotiat / capability log lines in the entire app log. The new /shim/v1/mobile WebSocket transport is not being used by this conversation.

3. The A2UI splitter / validator / a2ui_frame emission lives in admin-shim/lib/mobile-channel-host.ts on the WS path. The REST path forwards assistant content verbatim, so the <a2ui-json> block emitted in the model output reached the device as literal text inside an assistant_message, not as a parsed a2ui_frame.

4. The Compose markdown renderer then ate the <a2ui-json> open/close tags as unknown HTML elements and hid the content inside, producing the blank block Emmanuel observed.

Why this matters for the renderer dev agent:

- letta-mobile-ldgy (Android A2UI v0.9 Basic renderer) cannot be tested end-to-end against an agent that is on the REST transport. Any test plan that loops through the existing Meridian chat will fail with this exact symptom.
- Phase 6 vertical slice (lcp-uo5.7) implicitly requires a session that is bound to /shim/v1/mobile with A2UI negotiated in the hello frame. The renderer code path is never invoked otherwise.
- The markdown renderer also needs a defensive fallback: if an <a2ui-json> tag arrives inside an assistant_message body (i.e. the splitter on the server did not peel it out), render the block as raw JSON in a code fence rather than swallowing it silently. That fallback is what would have made todays test "noisy but informative" instead of "blank and confusing."

Observable signals worth capturing in renderer tests:

- Transport: which transport is the chat currently on (REST vs WS)? Surface this in a dev overlay or in logs.
- Negotiation: when WS is used, did the hello frame request A2UI and did the server accept? Log the negotiated capability summary.
- Frame counts: per-turn count of a2ui_frame received vs. assistant_message chunks received. If a2ui_frame == 0 and the agent obviously emitted JSON, surface that as a warning.
- Markdown fallback: when an unknown <a2ui-...> tag survives into the markdown layer, render its inner content as a code block, not as a hidden element.

Repro recipe for the dev agent:

1. adb -s 192.168.50.235:5555 logcat -c
2. Open a chat with Meridian (this REST-bound agent).
3. Ask Meridian to emit a ToolApprovalCard via <a2ui-json>...</a2ui-json>.
4. Observe: blank block in chat surface.
5. adb logcat shows no a2ui activity; just standard streamSubscriber.merged on assistant_message.

Desired changes (renderer side, in priority order):

1. Defensive markdown fallback: render <a2ui-json> content as a code block when it reaches the markdown layer instead of swallowing it as an unknown HTML element. This converts "blank block" into "raw JSON visible" which is far more diagnostic.
2. Transport overlay in dev builds: small chip in chat header showing REST vs WS, and when WS, whether A2UI is negotiated.
3. Renderer regression test: a fixture that feeds a captured <a2ui-json> ToolApprovalCard through the assistant_message text path (not the a2ui_frame path) and asserts the markdown fallback emits visible JSON.
4. Document in letta-mobile-ldgy that the renderer test plan must use a WS-bound test agent, not a REST-bound one. Cross-link this bead.

Non-goals:

- Forcing all chats onto WS.
- Auto-detection of A2UI content in REST streams (the splitter is server-side by design).
- Theming or render-time polish for the fallback.

Rollback signal:

If the markdown fallback ends up rendering hostile/oversized JSON blocks as huge code blocks that destroy chat scroll, cap the rendered length and add an expand affordance.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Markdown renderer surfaces <a2ui-json>...</a2ui-json> content as a visible code block when it appears in assistant_message text (instead of swallowing as an unknown HTML element).
2. Dev-build transport overlay shows current transport (REST vs WS) on the chat header.
3. When WS is used, the dev overlay shows whether A2UI was negotiated and which catalog.
4. Regression test: feeding a captured ToolApprovalCard <a2ui-json> block through the assistant_message text path asserts the fallback renders the JSON visibly.
5. The letta-mobile-ldgy renderer bead description is updated (or a comment added) noting that end-to-end testing requires a WS-bound test agent, with a link to this feedback bead.
6. Capped fallback rendering: the JSON code block has a maximum visible height with an expand affordance for oversized blobs.
notes:
Second render attempt: blank block persists across debug build with PRs merged.

Follow-up to letta-mobile-cbjh. Re-tested rendering after Emmanuel built and installed the debug APK with the latest merged PRs. Same symptom: blank block where the <a2ui-json> JSON used to be. No card, no raw JSON visible, no fallback. Logged from Pixel 9 Pro at 192.168.50.235:5555, app PID 18323 (fresh start, post-merge).

Key new findings from this second attempt (which rule out the first hypothesis that this was just about transport state):

1. The JSON block fully reached the device. streamSubscriber.merged lines grow assistant_message body to mergedLen=2288 - matches the size of the prose + <a2ui-json> payload byte for byte. Content delivery is fine.

2. Still zero a2ui, hello, negotiat, capability, wsAdapter, MobileChannel, WSocket, or /shim/v1/mobile log lines in the app. The new transport / A2UI capability path is not being invoked at all for this conversation.

3. The conversation here is conv-default-agent-597b5756-2915-4560-ba6b-91005f085166 (Meridian, this agent). Sends go through send.externalTransportLocalAppended and receive via streamSubscriber.ingested + .merged. That is the standard REST + SSE path through LettaApiClient / okhttp3.

4. The shim splitter / validator / a2ui_frame emitter lives in admin-shim/lib/mobile-channel-host.ts, on the /shim/v1/mobile WS path. The REST endpoint /v1/agents/{id}/messages does NOT route through that host - it forwards stream-json content raw. So agents that are not registered with the mobile-channel host receive zero A2UI treatment, end to end.

Revised root cause:

The A2UI server-side path is only invoked for agents wired into the mobile channel host (accounts.json under home/.letta/channels/mobile/). Meridian is not wired into that host. So even with a 100 percent working renderer, no card will ever appear in this chat because the splitter / validator / frame emitter is never given a chance to fire.

This is upstream of the renderer fixes proposed in letta-mobile-cbjh. The markdown fallback and dev-overlay items there are still correct and high-value, but they would NOT have produced a card in this test - they would have produced a visible raw JSON code block in the chat, which is better but not the goal.

Additional renderer feedback for the dev agent:

A. Add a check in the chat surface that if the active conversations agent is not mobile-channel-wired, surface a visible dev banner explaining "this agent is on the legacy REST transport, A2UI rendering disabled" rather than silently letting A2UI tags arrive as text. This stops people wasting time testing A2UI in chats that cannot possibly produce it.

B. The transport overlay proposed in letta-mobile-cbjh AC#2 needs a third state: not just REST vs WS, but ALSO whether the active agent is mobile-channel-host-bound. A WS-capable build talking to a non-mobile-channel agent will still go through REST, so REST vs WS alone is not sufficient.

C. Test fixture refinement: any A2UI renderer regression test must run against a fixture conversation that is explicitly mobile-channel-wired on the shim side. Document this in the letta-mobile-ldgy bead so the dev agent does not pick the path of least resistance and test against an arbitrary chat.

Diagnostic command that confirms wiring:

cat /opt/stacks/letta-code-parallel/home/.letta/channels/mobile/accounts.json

The list of accounts there is the canonical answer to "which agents go through the A2UI-capable path." Anything not in that file is on the REST path no matter what the client supports.

Non-goals stay the same as letta-mobile-cbjh.

Rollback: none - this is observation, not a code change.
REGRESSION 2026-05-17 15:55 EDT: WS + A2UI handshake lost during PR merge round.

After the post-merge debug build, the Meridian chat is back on REST + SSE - same symptom set as before the renderer fix landed. Confirmed via adb logcat on Pixel 9 Pro 192.168.50.235:5555 (PID 15342):

- Zero a2ui, hello, negotiat, capability, ChannelTransport, MobileWs, WSocket, /shim/v1/mobile log lines in the entire app process trace.
- Active stack shows io.ktor.client.engine.okhttp + okhttp3.internal.http1.Http1ExchangeCodec - HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer, NOT WebSocket.
- streamSubscriber.merged is the message ingestion path - same SSE-over-REST shape we saw in the initial broken state.
- The transport chip ("WS A2UI basic N") that we added during letta-mobile-cbjh / ldgy work is NOT visible on screen, confirming the chat surface knows it is not on WS.

Emmanuel's framing: "we regressed a bit on the protocol handshake headers it got lost in the prs."

This is a classic merge-time regression - all PRs likely passed gates individually but the combined diff dropped the conditional that routes A2UI-capable chats over WS. Common surfaces to check:

1. The transport selection logic - the per-conversation or per-agent decision of "use WS to /shim/v1/mobile" vs "use REST to /v1/agents/{id}/messages". Probably a feature flag or build config that needs to be set to opt this chat onto WS.
2. The hello frame builder - if the client now sends an empty or downgraded hello, the server falls back to REST behavior. Worth grepping for any recent rename to hello / capabilities / a2ui fields.
3. The mobile-channel accounts.json wiring - if this Meridian agent was removed from mobile channel account binding during a merge, the server never offers A2UI for this session.

Diagnostic checklist:

a. cat the accounts.json under home/.letta/channels/mobile/ on the shim host. Confirm this agent's id is still listed and enabled.
b. Force a debug log of the hello frame the client sends on connect. Compare to the spec from lcp-uo5.2.
c. Check the WS endpoint resolution in the Android client. If the build config defaulted back to REST, no WS attempt happens.

Why this matters at the project level:

This exact regression class is what the transport overlay (cbjh AC#2) was designed to surface. It DID work briefly between the deserializer fix and now; the chip rendered "WS A2UI basic 2" on attempt 3 (the post-rebuild successful render). Then it disappeared.

The merge regression is fast to fix once the right line is found. The deeper lesson: any PR touching transport / handshake should be required to include the chip-visibility screenshot or a smoke test that asserts the chip renders, so this class of drift cannot ship green. File a separate bead for that gate if you want one.

Until the regression is fixed, A2UI cannot be tested in this chat. Same workaround applies as before: use a chat that is explicitly mobile-channel-wired, or wait for the regression PR to land.

title: Streaming 'active writing' glow persists too long / chat appears stuck
description:
## Summary

Two related (possibly same root cause) UI bugs observed in the current chat surface during streaming responses:

### Symptom A — Glow lingers
The 'active writing' visual treatment (glow / pulse / shimmer / typing indicator — whatever the active-streaming visual cue is) stays on the message **for an inordinate amount of time** after the response has clearly finished streaming.

### Symptom B — Stuck in active writing mode
Sometimes a chat **stays in active writing mode** without progressing — appears stuck. Response is either fully done or never resumes streaming, but the UI continues to show streaming state.

## Repro (rough)

Both observed during the May 16, 2026 evening session over Letta Code CLI v0.25.9 on the new transport (commits 3ac9733, 0ef20bf, 3794da2, 9f5aaab). Not yet 100% repro steps — Emmanuel observed both symptoms intermittently during a long multi-hour session.

## Hypotheses

1. **Missing turn_done / stream_end frame** — UI tracks streaming state by start/end markers; if end marker is dropped or never emitted, the active state never clears
2. **Race between final text chunk and turn-done event** — UI applies last chunk but turn-done arrives later or never; or arrives first and is discarded
3. **WS disconnect mid-stream** — if connection drops without a clean close, UI may never receive the terminator and stay in streaming state indefinitely
4. **Animation/state mismatch** — glow animation has its own timer/state separate from streaming flag; if they desync, glow outlives stream
5. **Tool-call vs text-stream confusion** — if a turn ends with a tool call, the UI may keep streaming state alive waiting for a text response that never comes

Relevant commit to investigate: `0ef20bf fix: messages.jsonl schema migration + turn_done error fields + stderr-on-exit` — touches turn_done; may have introduced or exposed this.

## Suggested investigation

- Log every state transition for the streaming flag (enter, exit) with timestamps
- Compare against WS frame log: was turn_done emitted? When? Did UI receive it?
- Try forcing a long response, screenshot the UI well after streaming ended, attach logcat
- Check if the glow animation has its own dismissal logic distinct from the streaming state

## Severity

P2 — UX paper cut that erodes trust. "Looks broken" is a real cost even when the message arrived. Could promote to P1 if user-reported (vs agent-reported).

## Reported by

Meridian, observing Emmanuel's experience May 16, 2026 evening session. Emmanuel said: 'the glow stays for an inordinate amount of time sometime a chat stays in active writing mode seemingly stuck'.

title: Phase 4: Remaining v1 widgets (TextField, DateTimeInput, Image, Divider, Row)
description:
## Goal

Fill out the v1 widget set so the renderer can handle the four examples shipped in google/A2UI (booking_form, confirmation, single_column_list, two_column_list).

## v1 widget targets

Adding to the Phase 2 four (Text, Column, Card, Button):

- **Row** — horizontal layout sibling to Column
- **TextField** — text input with label, value path, optional variant="number"
- **DateTimeInput** — date/time picker; props enableDate, enableTime, value path
- **Image** — URL-bound image; placeholder/error states
- **Divider** — visual separator

That's 9 widgets total, sufficient to render every shipped A2UI example.

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement each widget per A2UI v0.9 spec
- [ ] Each widget contributes to data model when user interacts (TextField writes /partySize, DateTimeInput writes /reservationTime, etc.)
- [ ] Two-way binding: when user types in TextField bound to /partySize, the data model updates AND the agent sees the new value on the next interaction context
- [ ] Material 3 styling consistent with app theme
- [ ] Accessibility: content descriptions, focus order, keyboard navigation
- [ ] Snapshot tests for each widget light/dark + multiple states (empty, populated, error)

## Acceptance criteria

- All four A2UI shipped examples render correctly
- User can edit TextField, see the value reflected in data model
- DateTimeInput picker opens correctly on Android
- Image handles slow loads + errors gracefully

## Dependencies

- Phase 3 (data binding)

## Reported by

Meridian + Emmanuel
notes: ﻿Phase 3 is complete in PR #103: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/103. Branch `codex/a2ui-phase3`, phase-only commit `8caacd2a` adds `A2uiDataModel`, JSON Pointer patch/merge/delete/array append semantics, pointer-scoped Compose observation, renderer path-bound Text/Button updates, skeleton-to-content streaming tests, and core data-model tests. This PR targets `main` for CI and logically stacks on PR #102 / Phase 2 and PR #101 / Phase 1 until those merge. Local validation passed: targeted A2UI core + renderer tests, A2UI screenshot test, `:app:compileRootDebugKotlin`, `:app:testRootDebugUnitTest`, and `:app:assembleRootDebug`. Device install attempted on `711KPAE0914240` but Android rejected with `INSTALL_FAILED_UPDATE_INCOMPATIBLE`; existing app was not uninstalled. GitHub connector shows Android APK CI run 721 and Android Perf Gate run 404 in progress for PR #103. `gh` CLI is not available on this machine PATH, so PR checks were verified through the GitHub connector. Retrieval: bd show letta-mobile-51xm.5 && git status --short --branch --ignore-submodules=all && gh pr view 103
close_reason: Implemented Phase 4 A2UI Row, TextField, DateTimeInput, Image, and Divider widgets with two-way data-model writes, capability advertisement, renderer tests, and light/dark snapshots.

title: WS protocol: add start_new_conversation flag to send_message (defer REST pre-create round-trip)
description:
Follow-up to letta-mobile-vcky.

vcky lands as Option B: mobile POSTs /v1/conversations on fresh-route's first send to mint a conv id, then sends via WS. Zero shim changes, works today.

This bead tracks the Option C optimization: bake the create call into the WS protocol so first-send avoids the extra REST round-trip.

## Wire
SendMessageFrame gains `start_new_conversation: boolean = false`. When true, `conversation_id` may be empty/null on the inbound frame; the shim mints a fresh `conv-<uuid>` and echoes it back on TurnStarted.conversation_id (which is already a populated field; mobile's TurnStarted handler at WsChatSendCoordinator.kt:151 already adopts whatever conversation_id the shim returns).

## Shim work
- /opt/stacks/letta-code-parallel/home/.letta/channels/mobile/lib/ws-handler.mjs `case "send_message"`: before invoking host.sendMessage, if start_new_conversation, call client.conversations.create({ agent_id }) and swap conversation_id.
- /opt/stacks/letta-code-parallel/admin-shim/lib/mobile-channel-host.ts bridgeSendMessage: receives the post-mint conversation_id directly; no resolver change needed.
- agent-pool.ts: no change — worker keys by (agentId, conversationId) and the spawn args already handle real conv ids via `--conversation X` branch.

## Mobile work
- core/data/transport/MobileWsFrames.kt SendMessageFrame: add `@SerialName("start_new_conversation") val startNewConversation: Boolean = false`.
- core/data/transport/WsChatBridge.kt + ChannelTransport.kt: forward the flag.
- feature-chat/WsChatSendCoordinator.kt: when `isFreshRoute && activeConversationId == null`, set startNewConversation=true and use an empty conversation_id placeholder (or omit the field — TBD by wire shape).
- Remove the REST pre-create from vcky once C lands; keep it as fallback if WS is not connected (per user choice on 2026-05-16).

## Not blocking
vcky's Option B fix covers the user-visible bug. This bead is a UX polish — saves one REST RTT on first send.

title: Move complex Compose form and dialog state into stable owners
description:
Several feature screens keep large dialog/form workflows in local remember state, which makes process-death behavior, validation, and testing fragile.

Start with AgentListDialogs, EditAgentContent, and AgentScaffoldDrawer because they contain many local mutable fields and repeated modal state flags. Move durable form state into ViewModels or stable form-state holders, using rememberSaveable only for truly local UI affordances.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent creation/import dialog state is represented by a testable form state holder or ViewModel state rather than scattered local remember fields.
- EditAgent picker/dialog state is reduced to stable screen-owned state with clear event handlers.
- Project bug-report drawer state separates durable draft data from ephemeral expanded/sheet booleans.
- Rotation/process-death behavior is either preserved through state owners/savers or explicitly documented where non-durable state is intentional.
- Existing UI tests/unit tests are updated or added around validation and submit behavior.

title: Standardize repository observable-state contracts
description: Repositories use a mix of hot StateFlow caches, ad hoc MutableStateFlow maps, paging flows, and detached snapshot flows. Define and apply a consistent contract for repository observable state so callers know whether a method returns live updates, a cached snapshot, or a one-shot network result.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repository API naming/docs distinguish live observable state from one-shot/snapshot reads.
- No repository method returns a detached MutableStateFlow snapshot when callers expect live updates.
- Representative repositories have tests covering cache update emission and stale-refresh behavior.
- Repository-owned CoroutineScopes and cache lifetimes are documented or migrated to injected scopes where appropriate.
- Follow-up beads are filed for repositories that need deeper refactors beyond the first standardization pass.

title: Fix PassageRepository observable cache flow
description:
PassageRepository.getPassages currently returns a new MutableStateFlow seeded from the current map value. That flow is detached from later _passages updates, so collectors can miss refresh/create/delete changes unless the caller explicitly reloads.

Replace it with an observable flow derived from the repository cache, and update tests so this behavior is covered by the real contract rather than mirrored by fakes.
acceptance_criteria:
- getPassages(agentId) emits future refresh/create/delete updates for that agent.
- The implementation does not expose mutable repository internals to callers.
- ArchivalViewModel and repository tests verify subscribers receive updates after refresh and mutation calls.
- Existing search behavior remains unchanged unless deliberately covered by the same observable contract.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #74 (codex/passage-observable-flow-4ouj). PassageRepository now returns stable read-only per-agent cache flows; refresh/create/delete emit to existing collectors; repository and ArchivalViewModel coverage added. Local core focused test passed; app focused test was blocked by unrelated ProjectHome test compile errors in this dirty worktree, with CI running on the clean PR branch.

title: Extract Application startup orchestration into explicit coordinators
description:
LettaApplication currently injects many services and launches multiple fire-and-forget startup tasks directly. This concentrates startup policy, ordering, and failure handling in the Application class.

Move startup work into injectable coordinator(s) with explicit ordering, failure policy, and test coverage while keeping crash reporter installation early.
acceptance_criteria:
- LettaApplication performs minimal process setup, installs crash handling early, and delegates nontrivial startup work to coordinator(s).
- Startup tasks such as notification channel setup, automation auth bootstrap, client-mode init, performance monitor install, heartbeat scheduling, and bot restore have explicit ordering/failure policy.
- Failures are observable enough for debugging without crashing normal app launch unless the task is truly required.
- Robolectric/test behavior remains deterministic.
- Unit tests cover coordinator sequencing and failure containment.
close_reason: Completed in PR #94 (merged as 22c12b12): startup work now delegates through AppStartupCoordinator with explicit task order, failure telemetry, cancellation behavior, and unit coverage.

title: Replace legacy static-bridge registries with injected access paths
description:
Several classes use companion-object INSTANCE bridges while also being Hilt singletons. LettaApplication eagerly injects them to avoid null static access during the migration, which keeps process-global mutable state in the code path.

Replace or isolate these bridges for GeneratedUiRegistry, ToolDisplayRegistry, and EncryptedPrefsHelper so production code uses constructor injection or explicit parameters rather than companion INSTANCE lookups.
acceptance_criteria:
- GeneratedUiRegistry call sites no longer require a companion INSTANCE bridge in production paths.
- ToolDisplayRegistry call sites no longer require a companion INSTANCE bridge in production paths.
- EncryptedPrefsHelper access from SettingsRepository is constructor-injected or isolated behind an explicit provider that does not depend on eager Application injection.
- Tests that previously relied on lazy default singleton construction are updated to inject fakes or use explicit setup.
- LettaApplication no longer needs to eagerly inject registries solely to initialize static bridges.

title: Decouple designsystem from core data models
description:
The designsystem module currently depends on :core and imports core data models such as AppTheme, ThemePreset, and LlmModel. This couples reusable UI foundations to app/domain data shape.

Refactor designsystem APIs to accept model-agnostic parameters or small UI-specific models, and move app/domain mapping into the app layer or a dedicated adapter boundary.
acceptance_criteria:
- designsystem no longer imports com.letta.mobile.data.model types.
- designsystem/build.gradle.kts removes implementation(project(:core)) if no other valid dependency remains.
- Theme and model dropdown callers map domain models to UI-friendly values outside designsystem.
- Public designsystem APIs remain small, stable, and reusable by app screens.
- Existing UI behavior and previews/tests continue to work.

title: Centralize dependency versions and Android Gradle conventions
description:
Gradle module files repeat many dependency versions and Android build settings directly. Introduce repo-native centralization for dependency coordinates and common Android module configuration after the SDK constant centralization work lands.

This is distinct from the minSdk migration bead: that bead centralizes SDK values; this one should reduce broader Gradle drift across app, core, bot, designsystem, cli, macrobenchmark, and baselineprofile.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repeated dependency versions are moved to a version catalog, convention plugin, or another established Gradle mechanism chosen to match the repo.
- Common Android library/application settings are centralized without obscuring module-specific behavior.
- app, core, bot, and designsystem no longer repeat the same library versions for Ktor, Hilt, coroutines, Compose, test libraries, and related shared dependencies.
- The change avoids opportunistic dependency upgrades unless they are required to complete the centralization safely.
- Existing build/test tasks continue to resolve the same variants.

title: Fix WsChatSendCoordinatorTest event-flow subscriber timing
description: 6 new tests in WsChatSendCoordinatorTest (PR 72) fail because MutableSharedFlow emit appears to fire before the coordinators init-launched events.collect subscriber subscribes — handleEvent never runs. Production code in PR 72 is correct. Recommended fix: make handleEvent internal and call directly, OR use Channel-backed Flow, OR add a subscription-ready latch.
close_reason: Duplicate of letta-mobile-xncj; keeping the detailed CI-blocker bead as source of truth.

title: Fix WsChatSendCoordinatorTest event-flow subscriber timing
description: 6 new tests in WsChatSendCoordinatorTest (PR #72) fail because MutableSharedFlow.emit appears to fire before the coordinator's init-launched events.collect{} subscriber subscribes — handleEvent never runs. The 'clean turn done' test passes trivially (exactly=0 verify) but does not actually prove handleEvent ran. Recommended fix: make handleEvent internal and call it directly from tests, OR use a Channel-backed Flow for deterministic delivery, OR expose a subscription-ready latch from the coordinator. PR #72 production code is correct; only the test verification path is flaky.
close_reason: Duplicate of letta-mobile-xncj; keeping the detailed CI-blocker bead as source of truth.

title: Fix WsChatSendCoordinatorTest event-flow subscriber timing
description:
6 of the new tests in WsChatSendCoordinatorTest (PR #72) fail because MutableSharedFlow.emit() in the test scope appears to deliver before the coordinator's init-launched events.collect{} subscriber subscribes — handleEvent never runs.

The existing 'turn done reconciles' test pattern worked: coordinator.send().join() then emit() then advanceUntilIdle(). New tests using the same pattern (e.g. 'lossy turn done forces a reconcile') still fail with the reconcile never being called.

Diagnostics already tried:
- replay = 64 on the SharedFlow (didn't help)
- explicit advanceUntilIdle() before emit (didn't help)
- coordinator.send('hello').join() warmup before emit (works for the 'clean turn done skips reconcile' case which has exactly=0 verify so trivially passes; doesn't actually prove handleEvent ran).

Failing tests:
- usage statistics writes tokens to ui state once per turn
- turn started resets per turn first wins state
- turn done failed surfaces an error message
- turn done cancelled does not set error banner
- failed turn surfaces buffered error message from preceding error frame
- lossy turn done forces a reconcile against external default conversation id

Recommended fixes (pick one):
1. Make WsChatSendCoordinator.handleEvent internal and call it directly from tests, bypassing the SharedFlow entirely.
2. Refactor the bridge mock to use a Channel-backed Flow (deterministic delivery to the collector).
3. Expose a CompletableDeferred latch on the coordinator that signals when its event subscription is active, then await it in tests.

Code in PR #72 is unchanged from what is intended; only the verification path is flaky.
close_reason: Stabilized WsChatSendCoordinatorTest by bypassing MutableSharedFlow subscriber timing in package-local event-handler tests; included in PR #76.

title: Validate minSdk 31 migration on local and device gates
description:
Run the quality gates and runtime smoke checks needed to prove the minSdk 31 migration is ready for review.

This bead should be handled after centralization, the SDK bump, and baseline documentation updates are in place.
acceptance_criteria:
- ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin passes.
- ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest passes.
- Any additionally affected module tests pass, or failures are filed with clear reproduction steps.
- ./gradlew :app:assembleRootDebug builds and the APK installs on the Pixel dev device or an API 31+ emulator.
- PR notes explicitly call out that Android 8-11 support is being dropped by the minSdk 31 migration.

title: Raise all Android modules to minSdk 31
description:
After SDK constants are centralized, raise the Android project minimum SDK from the current API 26/28 split to API 31 for app, library, CLI, design-system, benchmark, and baseline-profile modules as appropriate.

Preserve compileSdk and targetSdk at 36, preserve flavor/build variant behavior, and do not touch derived versionName or versionCode logic.
acceptance_criteria:
- App, bot, core, cli, designsystem, macrobenchmark, and baselineprofile modules resolve with a minSdk 31 baseline where applicable.
- Variant resolution still works for app, macrobenchmark, and baseline-profile builds.
- Gradle configuration and Kotlin compilation succeed after the bump.
- versionName and versionCode derivation in app/build.gradle.kts is not manually edited.

title: Centralize Android SDK version values in Gradle
description:
SDK values are currently repeated across Android module build files. Introduce a single source of truth for the app project SDK constants before raising the baseline.

Known module files to cover include app, bot, core, cli, designsystem, macrobenchmark, and baselineprofile. Choose the least surprising repo-native mechanism, such as a convention plugin, buildSrc constants, root extra properties, or version-catalog-backed constants if that matches existing Gradle patterns.
acceptance_criteria:
- compileSdk, targetSdk, and minSdk are defined centrally instead of repeated as raw literals across Android modules.
- All current Android module build.gradle.kts files consume the centralized values or document a deliberate exception.
- Benchmark and baseline-profile module behavior remains explicit where Android Gradle Plugin requires it.
- The change does not introduce unrelated dependency, plugin, or version churn.

title: Migrate Android baseline to minSdk 31
description:
Move the Android app project to an Android 12/API 31 minimum baseline so optional Google AI Edge / LiteRT / FunctionGemma integration work has a compatible floor and the legacy API 26 compatibility surface can be reduced intentionally.

Scope includes centralizing SDK values, raising module minSdk values, updating stale API 26 support text, auditing obsolete compatibility branches, and validating the migration on local Gradle gates plus an API 31+ device/emulator.
acceptance_criteria:
- Android SDK values have a single source of truth for compileSdk, targetSdk, and minSdk where the Gradle structure supports it.
- App, library, benchmark, and baseline profile modules use minSdk 31 where applicable while preserving compileSdk/targetSdk 36.
- User-facing, developer-facing, and tool metadata no longer claim Android API 26+ support.
- Migration is validated with local compile/unit gates and an API 31+ install/run smoke test.
- Any compatibility cleanup that is too risky for the migration PR is filed as follow-up beads.

title: Prototype FunctionGemma-backed Android host-tool parser
description:
Prototype a local Android action parser inspired by Google AI Edge Gallery Mobile Actions. The goal is to let simple device commands be interpreted offline by FunctionGemma and then executed only through letta-mobile's existing AndroidExecutionBridge and HostToolApprovalEngine.

This should not bypass Letta, Client Mode, or host-tool policy. Treat FunctionGemma as a local intent/tool-call router, not as a replacement for Letta memory or remote agent reasoning.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a small typed action/tool intent model for local Android commands matching existing host tools.
- Map LiteRT-LM @Tool callbacks into existing BotToolExecutionResult/AndroidExecutionBridge paths.
- Reuse HostToolApprovalEngine for direct device-state changes and user-mediated intents.
- Add tests for parsing/executing flashlight, Wi-Fi settings, map, email draft, calendar draft, and unknown action cases.

title: Spike optional FunctionGemma on-device action routing
description:
Google AI Edge Gallery shows a concrete Android path for offline function calling: LiteRT-LM 0.11.0, FunctionGemma 270M MobileActions/TinyGarden models, Kotlin ToolSet/@Tool annotations, and app-owned Android action execution. Evaluate whether letta-mobile can add an optional on-device action router without disrupting existing Client Mode/Letta paths.

Reference repo: https://github.com/google-ai-edge/gallery
Key reference files: Function_Calling_Guide.md, Android/src/app/src/main/java/com/google/ai/edge/gallery/customtasks/mobileactions, Android/src/app/src/main/java/com/google/ai/edge/gallery/ui/llmchat/LlmChatModelHelper.kt.

Relevant letta-mobile areas: android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/tools, AndroidExecutionBridge.kt, HostToolApproval.kt, BotConfigStore, ClientModeController.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify LiteRT-LM dependency compatibility with letta-mobile minSdk 26, compileSdk 36, Kotlin/JVM settings, APK size, and ABI packaging.
- Document FunctionGemma MobileActions model constraints, including download size, minimum RAM, accelerator, and terms/distribution implications.
- Decide whether this belongs in :bot, a new optional :edge module, or a separate flavor-gated module.
- Produce an implementation plan that preserves existing Letta Client Mode behavior.

title: Add idempotency keys for Android host-tool execution
description:
Observed from letta-city-sim: citizen actions include client_event_id and a receipt cache so reconnects/retries do not reapply the same world mutation. Android host tools can mutate device state or launch user-mediated drafts, and retries across stream recovery or notification reply handling should be safe. Add idempotency keys around host-tool execution and terminal tool results.

Relevant app areas: BotToolRegistry, HostToolApprovalEngine, AndroidExecutionBridge, RemoteBotSession, DefaultLettaRuntimeClient.submitToolResult.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each host-tool execution has a stable client event id tied to the model tool call/run.
- Retried execution for the same id returns the prior structured result when possible.
- Auditing records the idempotency key alongside operation metadata.
- Tests cover duplicate mutable tool requests and retry after transient stream recovery.

title: Add explicit Client Mode run lifecycle envelopes
description:
Observed from letta-city-sim: wake delivery has explicit event_id, sequence, expiry, duplicate tracking, wake_done, and wake_abort semantics. letta-mobile Client Mode has request_id/conversation routing, but background reply and long-running stream lifecycles are less explicit across notification replies, heartbeat, and WS streams. Design and add a common run lifecycle envelope for Client Mode/background agent events.

Relevant app areas: WsBotClient, ClientModeChatSender, NotificationReplyHandler, BotHeartbeatWorker, TimelineSyncLoop.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document the lifecycle fields needed by Android: run/event id, target agent id, conversation id, timeout/expiry, terminal state, abort reason.
- Add lifecycle tracking where Client Mode sends or resumes long-running streams.
- Duplicate or stale terminal events are ignored safely.
- Tests cover normal completion, abort/error completion, and duplicate completion.

title: Scope Android host tools per run with a manifest
description:
Observed from letta-city-sim: the citizen harness uses a server-owned tool manifest so each wake/run only exposes relevant tools. In letta-mobile, BotToolRegistry defines Android host tools and BotToolSync currently upserts/attaches tools by name. Evaluate and implement a scoped host-tool manifest layer for Client Mode and local bot runs so the active route/channel/run can restrict available Android tools without relying on globally attached tools.

Relevant app areas: android-compose/bot/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/bot/tools/BotToolRegistry.kt, BotToolSync.kt, RemoteBotSession.kt, WsBotClient.kt.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define a manifest/allowed-tool policy for Android host tools that can be scoped per run or channel.
- Unsupported tools are denied with a structured result instead of silently falling through.
- Existing globally synced tools continue to work during migration.
- Add focused unit tests for allowed, denied, and unknown tool paths.

title: Harden perf-gate against API 33 emulator noise (2-attempt retry insufficient)
description:
The Android Perf Gate currently retries cold-startup once if it fails (perf/check_baselines.py --retryable-single-cold-start-exit-code 3). PR #64 demonstrated this is insufficient when the shared GitHub Actions emulator has a degraded session: both attempts measured 2152/2594 ms (+42%/+71% over baseline 1512.749) on code that other runners on the SAME content measured at 1441–1652 ms.

PR #61 (mostly empty cleanup) ALSO failed at 1845 ms (+22%), then admin-merged — same root cause.

Evidence the regression is not real:
- PR #63 rebased onto post-#64 main: 1441.741 ms (-4.69%)
- PR #70 (full stack incl. #64): 1652.728 ms (+9.25%)
- PRs #66/67/68/69 all SUCCESS

The +20% tolerance was chosen based on historical drift of +17.2%, but PR-level samples now drift up to +43% on bad runners. Options to consider:

1. Increase retries from 1 → 2 (3 total attempts), gate fails only if 2/3 fail.
2. Take median of attempts instead of single-sample comparison.
3. Move the perf gate to a self-hosted or larger-tier runner with lower noise variance.
4. Widen tolerance to +50% as a stopgap (keeps the gate present but reduces false-positive admin-merges).

Track this so the next noise spike doesn't trigger another admin-bypass.

title: 6. Convert AdminChatViewModel.uiState to a Molecule presenter (after extractions)
description:
Once the per-concern controllers from beads ht0s/bx4z/xigx/dtwc/bvrs are in place, AdminChatViewModel.uiState is a natural Molecule fit. Today the VM imperatively mutates _uiState.update {...} in ~15 places (init handlers, send routing, banner dismissals, client-mode toggle observer, error reset, attachment add/remove, conversation-state transitions, etc.). Molecule replaces that with one @Composable present() function that reads each controller's MutableStateFlow via collectAsState and composes ChatUiState top-to-bottom.

Depends on:
- letta-mobile-ht0s (ChatSendStrategy controller) — owns isStreaming + currentRunId
- letta-mobile-bx4z (ChatSessionInitializer) — keeps init out of presenter
- letta-mobile-xigx (ChatBannerController) — owns error + clientModeConversationSwap

Pattern (sketch):
@Composable private fun present(): ChatUiState {
    val timeline by timelineObserver.state.collectAsState()
    val composer by composerController.state.collectAsState()
    val banner by bannerController.state.collectAsState()
    val sendState by sendStrategy.state.collectAsState()
    val clientModeEnabled by settingsRepository.observeClientModeEnabled().collectAsState(false)
    return ChatUiState(
        agentName = ...,
        conversationState = timeline.conversationState,
        messages = timeline.messages,
        isStreaming = sendState.isStreaming,
        error = banner.error,
        clientModeConversationSwap = banner.swap,
        ...
    )
}

val uiState: StateFlow<ChatUiState> = viewModelScope.launchMolecule(ContextClock) { present() }

Imperative methods (sendMessage / cancel / addAttachment / dismiss banner) stay as method calls into the controllers. The presenter just OBSERVES.

Carries over the ConfigListViewModel pilot's (rl0d) lessons: derivedStateOf for cross-state computations to avoid over-recomposition.

## Files
- AdminChatViewModel.kt: replace _uiState + mutateState helpers with launchMolecule + present()
- Each controller extracted in prior beads exposes a state: StateFlow<T> read inside present()
notes: Completed Molecule presenter bridge in commit dd31f906 on branch refactor/chat-molecule-ui-state-63da. PR #68: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/68 (base refactor/chat-banner-controller-xigx). Validation passed locally: :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests '*AdminChatViewModelTest*', :app:assembleRootDebug with JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64. Retrieve with: bd show letta-mobile-63da && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 68

title: 5. Collapse 9 project-related delegations into a ChatProjectBindings facade
description:
AdminChatViewModel delegates 9 methods one-line-each to projectChatCoordinator: refreshClientModeLocation, sendClientModeLocationChange, openClientModeLocationPicker, closeClientModeLocationPicker, browseClientModeLocation, selectClientModeLocation, refreshContextWindow, loadProjectAgents, loadRecentBugReports, submitStructuredBugReport, loadProjectBrief, saveProjectBriefSection.

Either (a) make ChatScreen call projectChatCoordinator directly via a CompositionLocal, or (b) expose the coordinator as a single property on the VM that the screen drills into for these methods. Removes 14 boilerplate delegation lines.

Approach (b) is safer (no Compose-tree refactor); approach (a) is cleaner but invasive. Pick on review.

## Files
- AdminChatViewModel.kt: remove 14 delegation methods
- ChatScreen.kt: route project actions via viewModel.projectBindings.* (b) or LocalProjectChatCoordinator.current (a)
