title: 4. Extract ChatRouteArgs to pull SavedStateHandle reads off the VM
description:
AdminChatViewModel reads from SavedStateHandle in 8 places (agentId, agentName, initialMessage, conversationId, freshRouteKey, scrollToMessageId, projectIdentifier, CLIENT_MODE_CONVERSATION_ID_KEY) and writes once (setClientModeConversationId).

Extract a ChatRouteArgs class constructed from SavedStateHandle that exposes typed accessors + the persisted client-mode conv mutator. The VM accepts ChatRouteArgs not SavedStateHandle. Side-benefits: easier testing (no SavedStateHandle stub), single source of truth for route-arg names + key constants.

## Files

- AdminChatViewModel.kt: replace 8 savedStateHandle.get<>() calls + the setClientModeConversationId helper
notes: Bootstrap handoff: dtwc implementation is committed locally in U:\\letta-mobile-trees\\letta-mobile-dtwc on branch codex/chat-route-args-dtwc at 80ec4150 (refactor(chat): extract route args wrapper). Focused verification passed before commit: :app:compileRootDebugKotlin and :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.route.ChatRouteArgsTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.AdminChatViewModelTest. Hold PR until PR #76 (bdf4eae, auto-merge enabled) finishes CI and lands; then fetch/rebase on origin/main, run :app:compileRootDebugKotlin and :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, push branch, open PR for dtwc. Resume with: bd show letta-mobile-dtwc && git -C U:\\letta-mobile-trees\\letta-mobile-dtwc status --short --branch && gh pr view 76

title: 3. Extract ChatBannerController for scattered _uiState.update banner/error mutations
description:
AdminChatViewModel mutates _uiState.update { copy(error = ..., clientModeConversationSwap = ...) } in ~10 places spread across init, sendMessage, dismissClientModeConversationSwap, the client-mode toggle observer, etc. The pattern obscures who owns what.

Extract ChatBannerController owning:
  - conversation error
  - clientModeConversationSwap banner
  - retry-after-load error suggestion
  - composer error coordination (delegates to ChatComposerController)

The VM exposes the controller's state in uiState (via combine or explicit subholder). Mutation methods (showError / dismissBanner / clearError) move off the VM.

## Files
- [NEW] android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/state/ChatBannerController.kt
- AdminChatViewModel.kt: 10+ _uiState.update sites collapse to controller calls
close_reason: Implemented and merged in PR #67: centralized chat banner state in ChatBannerController.

title: Backend gating: only use ChannelTransport when active config is letta-code-admin-shim
description: Probe the active backend: GET /v1/health and look for 'backend':'letta-code-local' (or similar shim marker). Cache per active-config id. AdminChatViewModel branches: WS path when shim, REST path otherwise. A user-facing settings toggle to force REST is a follow-up; for the initial roll we auto-detect.

title: VoiceInputViewModel: amplitude scale mismatch — normalizing against 32767 but emitting 0-65535
description:
## Problem

There is an amplitude range mismatch between VoiceInputViewModel and AudioAnimation:

- VoiceInputViewModel.convertRmsDbToAmplitude() scales the output to 0-65535 range (matching the original HoldToDictateViewModel).
- AudioAnimation normalizes with '(amplitude / 32767.0).pow(0.5)', dividing by 32767.

This means the animation receives values up to ~65535 and divides by 32767, producing a normalizedAmplitude up to ~2.0 before the pow(0.5). After pow(0.5) that is ~1.41. The shader then uses 'amplitude / 2.0' for the perlin noise offset, which will overdrive the wave beyond its designed range.

The original Edge Gallery has two different code paths:
- AudioRecorderPanel uses raw PCM peak amplitude (0-32767 range) — matches the shader's divisor.
- HoldToDictateViewModel uses RMS dB scaled to 0-65535 — but the original 'AudioAnimation' also divides by 32767.

So the original has the same bug! But it is masked because RMS dB values from SpeechRecognizer.onRmsChanged() rarely exceed ~12dB in practice, so the effective range after scaling is roughly 0-8000 (well within 32767).

## Fix

Standardize on 0-32767 as the amplitude contract. Change convertRmsDbToAmplitude() to scale to 32767 instead of 65535, matching the AudioAnimation divisor and the PCM amplitude range.

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/VoiceInputViewModel.kt
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/AudioAnimation.kt

title: AudioAnimation: pre-API 33 fallback is a blank rectangle
description:
## Problem

The original Edge Gallery has no pre-API 33 fallback because their minSdk is high enough. Our port added an else-branch that just draws a solid bgColor rectangle:

`kotlin
} else {
    Canvas(modifier = modifier.fillMaxSize()) {
         drawRect(color = bgColor)
    }
}
`

This is a dead, lifeless fallback. On older devices the user would see a blank colored rectangle while holding down the dictate button — no animation, no visual feedback at all.

## Fix

Implement a lightweight Compose-only fallback animation for API < 33:
- Use 'rememberInfiniteTransition' to animate a sine-based wave or pulsing circle using standard Canvas draw calls (no AGSL).
- Accept the same 'amplitude' parameter and scale the animation based on it.
- This ensures pre-Tiramisu users still get meaningful visual feedback, even if it is simpler than the full shader.

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/AudioAnimation.kt

title: Evaluate UnifiedPush for De-Googled notification support
description: Investigate and implement UnifiedPush to support users who do not rely on Google Play Services. This allows the app to receive push notifications via open-source distributor apps like ntfy.

title: Integrate FCM Push Gateway for standard notifications
description: Set up the Firebase Cloud Messaging integration on the Android client to receive metadata wake-up ticks from the backend. Ensure background handlers trigger the sync layer.

title: Implement Privacy-First Wake-Up Push Notifications
description: Based on the Matrix/Element X architecture, implement a push notification system where the push payload contains only metadata (e.g. agent_id). The app should intercept the push in the background, sync the delta securely with the backend, and generate the rich notification locally.

title: Bundle JetBrains Mono for code blocks (fixes ASCII-art alignment)
description: FontFamily.Monospace falls back to system fonts that lack box-drawing glyphs, so ASCII art in code blocks loses column alignment. Bundle JetBrains Mono (OFL) for full coverage and swap the markdown code-font reference.

title: PR #62 follow-ups: sticky headers, title-tap Index, prune dead conv state

(2) Tap the EditAgentScreen title to open a section-Index ModalBottomSheet that animateScrollToItem's to that section. (3) Strip the conversational-creation state and methods from ProjectHomeViewModel since CreateProjectViewModel owns the flow now.

(2) ProjectTile gets semantic badges + status surface wash. (3) EditAgent loses horizontal tabs, becomes one vertical scroll with sticky section headers + bottom-sheet Index on title tap; destructive actions move to a bottom Danger Zone CardGroup with confirm dialogs.

title: Address CodeRabbit feedback on PR #59 (wake-test polish)
description: Follow-up to merged PR #59. Items: (1) re-throw CancellationException in ServerHealthRepository.probe runCatching, (2) mock ServerHealthRepository in ConfigListViewModelTest, (3) key BackendSwitcherRow refusalTrigger to config.id, (4) explicit import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.collect, (5) move HealthDot hardcoded colors out of the composable.

title: Backend wake-test (health probe + dot in pickers)
description: Probe each configured backend with short timeout. Show green/red health dot beside each row in BackendSwitcherSheet and ConfigListScreen. Tap on a dead (red) row: do not switch — shake + flash red to indicate refusal. Re-probe on sheet/screen open.

title: Hide Projects page + bottom-menu entry when backend lacks project endpoints
description:
User-facing ask: backends that don't expose project management endpoints (e.g. the admin shim if it doesn't proxy /v1/projects/*) currently show the Projects page and the bottom-menu entry, but every action fails with 404. Hide both.

Behavior:
  - On config attach / switch, probe a representative project endpoint (e.g. GET /v1/projects/?limit=1).
  - If 404 / 501 / unsupported: stash 'projects unavailable' in a capability flag for that server.
  - HomeScreen / nav graph reads the flag:
    * Bottom-menu Projects entry hidden
    * Direct nav (ProjectsRoute / ProjectHomeRoute) intercept to fallback (Conversations? Home?)
  - When flag flips back to supported (config switch to a supporting server), entries reappear.

Implementation outline:
  - core/data/repository/ProjectRepository: add suspend fun probeAvailability(): Boolean
  - new CapabilityRepository or expose flag on existing ProjectRepository
  - SettingsRepository.activeConfigChanges already exists (letta-mobile-ze5l) — wire the probe to fire on switch
  - Bottom menu component (find it: likely in HomeScreen / dashboard): observe + omit Projects entry when unsupported

Fallback semantics if probe fails (network error rather than 404): assume supported. Don't punish users for transient errors.

Test plan:
  - Manual against vanilla Letta server (which has projects): entry visible
  - Manual against admin shim (when it doesn't proxy projects): entry hidden
  - Switch between the two via BackendSwitcherSheet: entry appears/disappears

Parent: letta-mobile-? (umbrella capability-detection bead — filed in same session)

title: Capability-detect server endpoints; hide UI affordances for missing endpoints (umbrella)
description:
General principle: be cognizant of what each connected backend supports and don't display options that have no real endpoint to work against. As backends vary (Letta vanilla server, admin shim, future variants), the mobile UI should adapt.

Tracker bead. Specific child issues will cover individual capability gates as they get implemented.

First child: Projects (filed separately).

Capabilities likely worth gating, surveyed:
  - Projects (/v1/projects/* family)
  - Folders (/v1/folders)
  - Identities (/v1/identities)
  - Groups (/v1/groups)
  - Blocks
  - Templates
  - Archival memory
  - Jobs
  - Runs
  - Message batches
  - MCP tools
  - Schedules
  - Models browser
  - System access dashboard

Implementation pattern (proposed, subject to refinement per child issue):
  - Probe-and-cache on first connect (or active-config change): issue HEAD/GET requests against each capability endpoint. 200/2xx = supported; 404/501 = unsupported. Cache the capability map keyed by server fingerprint.
  - Expose Flow<Set<Capability>> from a CapabilityRepository.
  - Top-level UI consumers (nav graph, bottom menu, drawer) observe and conditionally render.
  - Fall back to 'assume supported' if the probe fails (network error, ambiguous), to avoid silently hiding features when the server is fine.

Related: letta-mobile-iow7 (stale agent reference recovery) overlaps on the 'server identity' axis — both want a server_id fingerprint. Capability detection should layer on top once iow7's fingerprint exists.

title: Auto-refresh current screen data on active-backend switch
description:
When the user switches active backend via the new BackendSwitcherSheet, the screens they were on still show data from the prior backend until they navigate away and back. Per user direction: stay on the same screen, refetch data in place.

Implementation:
  - SettingsRepository.activeConfigChanges: Flow<LettaConfig> emitting on config-id change, drop(1) the initial value
  - Top-level data-driven ViewModels (Dashboard, AgentList, Conversations, ProjectHome, AdminChat) observe the flow + invoke their existing refresh entry points.

Out of scope:
  - Repository-level cache invalidation (stale data may briefly flash; will revisit if visible).
  - Chat-screen handling of agent/conversation that 404s on new server — that's letta-mobile-iow7 (stale agent reference recovery).

title: Sending a message in REST-path chat reanimates the prior assistant response
description:
Reproduction (REST path, post-h2b8 build):
1. Open an existing chat with prior history.
2. Type a message and hit Send.
3. Observe: the most-recent assistant response (the one ABOVE your new send) replays its streaming animation — smoother reveals it char-by-char, cursor flashes — as if it were just arriving.

Root cause: TimelineSendCoordinator.send flips uiState.isStreaming=true at line 34-37 BEFORE the optimistic user Local is appended via timelineRepository.sendMessage at line 61. In the gap (network call + create-conversation latency), state.messages.lastOrNull() is still the prior assistant response. ChatMessageList computes per-message isStreaming = state.isStreaming && message.id == lastOrNull?.id → true → AssistantResponseText sees isStreaming=true → LaunchedEffect sets hasStreamed=true → smoother engages → 'reanimation' visible.

The hasStreamed flip is sticky for the composition lifetime, so even after the new user Local lands and isStreaming routes correctly to the new assistant message, the prior one keeps smoothing until composition is destroyed.

Fix: in AssistantResponseText (MessageContentFactory.kt:80), gate hasStreamed not on isStreaming alone but on isStreaming AND text-has-actually-grown-since-first-composition. A momentarily-true isStreaming with unchanged text shouldn't engage the smoother.

Files:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/MessageContentFactory.kt

title: Stale agent reference recovery (404 on cached agent_id after backend switch / agent deletion)
description:
**Error class:** Stale agent reference — mobile holds an agent ID in its persistent cache; that ID does not exist on the currently-connected server.

**Manifestation:**
- mobile cache says agent X exists with name N
- server: GET /v1/agents/X → 404; GET /v1/agents/X/core-memory/blocks → 404; GET /v1/agents/X/messages → 404; POST /v1/agents/X/messages → 404 (send button silently fails)
- UI renders a partial agent screen (name from cache, blocks/messages empty); user actions touching the agent ID fail; mobile keeps retrying without realising the cache is poisoned.

**Root causes (priority order):**
1. Base URL changed — user repoints mobile from server-A to server-B; cached agent IDs are from server-A's universe (the situation we hit this session — agent-597b5756… cached from Python Letta, switched to shim where it didn't exist).
2. Agent deleted on server.
3. Agent ID changed on server (migration, rename-with-new-id, archive-and-recreate).
4. Same name, different agent across servers (cross-server name collisions are real).
5. Transient 404 (server restart, lazy load, network blip) — cache should NOT flush on a single failure.

**Why name-matching alone is wrong:** silently substituting an agent with the same name on a different server loses provenance and trust. Two unrelated agents on different servers can share a name.

**Spec:**

DETECT
- 404 on /v1/agents/{cached_id} for an id in persistent state. Also 404 on any /v1/agents/{cached_id}/… subroute, or empty result on /v1/conversations?agent_id={cached_id} when mobile expected a known conv.
- Don't flush on a single 404. Wait for two consecutive failures separated by ~5s to filter transient blips. After confirmation, mark the cache entry 'missing'.

BIND CACHE TO SERVER IDENTITY
- Cache entries keyed by (serverFingerprint, agentId), not just agentId.
- When serverFingerprint changes, treat the whole cache as a different namespace.
- Fingerprint candidates (increasing robustness): baseUrl → baseUrl + Letta version from GET /v1/health/ → baseUrl + new server_id field in /v1/health/ (server-instance UUID generated at install, persists across restarts — durable answer).

SURFACE STATE EXPLICITLY (never silent substitute)
When cache entry is 'missing':
- Banner: 'This agent is no longer available on this server.'
- Send button disabled.
- Three offered actions:
  a) **Re-resolve by name** — list of agents on the current server matching the cache name; user picks one; cache rebinds with explicit 'user accepted re-resolution' flag.
  b) **Forget this agent** — drop cache entry, return to agent list.
  c) **Wait and retry** — keep retrying in background; banner stays.

PULL-TO-REFRESH ON AGENT LIST = invalidate-by-server
- Re-fetch /v1/agents, diff against cache for current server fingerprint:
  - New ids → add to cache
  - Cached ids missing from fresh list → mark 'missing'
  - Existing ids with different names → update name, log telemetry (could indicate rename or server confusion)

TELEMETRY
- cache_invalidation: stale_agent_404 (fields: server_fingerprint, agent_id, screen). Aggregating tells us whether users are bouncing between servers, whether agent ids rotate on the fleet, whether transient 404s need more aggressive backoff.

SERVER-SIDE COMPLEMENT (separate, not blocking)
- Add server_id UUID field to GET /v1/health/ (generated on first start, persisted to disk). 4-byte change, outsized value: lets mobile distinguish 'same URL, different server instance' (e.g., localhost:8291 → localhost:8291 across server reinstalls). Tracked separately in the shim/Letta repo — flag to file there once this issue is in flight.

**Current band-aid:** for the specific failure observed this session, the user manually re-migrated the original ids into the shim so the cached id matched again. That's a one-time convenience; the cache invalidation logic in this issue is the durable answer.

**Files likely involved:**
- core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AgentRepository.kt (cache layer)
- core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/SettingsRepository.kt (server fingerprint storage)
- app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AgentScaffold.kt / AdminChatViewModel (missing-state banner + recovery actions)
- core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/LettaApiClient.kt (404 detection hook)
- new: a small util that fingerprints the active config + compares against cache namespace markers
acceptance_criteria: (1) Cached agent id that 404s on the current server is detected after 2 consecutive failures separated by ~5s. (2) UI renders explicit missing-state banner with the three recovery actions (re-resolve / forget / wait+retry); send button is disabled. (3) Cache entries are keyed by (serverFingerprint, agentId); backend switch via the switcher sheet triggers a different namespace and doesn't poison the new namespace with the old's agent ids. (4) Pull-to-refresh on agent list reconciles cache against fresh server listing (add new, mark missing, update renames). (5) Telemetry event  fires on first confirmed 404.

title: Backend switcher bottom sheet (replace full-screen ConfigList nav from pill)
description:
The active-backend pill on HomeScreen / ConversationsScreen / ProjectHomeScreen (plus TwoPaneConversationsLayout) currently navigates to the full-screen ConfigListRoute. Multi-config data model is already in SettingsRepository (saveConfig, setActiveConfigId, deleteConfig). Replace the navigation with a ModalBottomSheet for a lighter-weight switch experience.

Sheet contract:
  - List rows: each shows a label (server URL trimmed for SELF_HOSTED, 'Letta Cloud' for CLOUD) + active checkmark + edit pencil icon.
  - Tap row -> setActiveConfigId(id) and dismiss.
  - Tap edit pencil -> dismiss + navigate to ConfigScreen for that id (prefilled).
  - Long-press row -> show delete-confirm dialog inside the sheet.
  - Footer button '+ Add server' -> dismiss + navigate to ConfigScreen create-mode.
  - Sheet dismisses on swipe-down / scrim tap.

State layout: lift sheet visibility to AppNavGraph so a single sheet instance renders across all screens that surface the pill. Click handlers (onNavigateToBackendSwitcher across all consumers) set the boolean true; the sheet renders at the NavHost host level so it appears over whichever screen is active.

ConfigListRoute stays reachable via Settings → Configs for power users (per user direction).

Files (likely):
  - new: android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/config/BackendSwitcherSheet.kt
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt (lift sheet state + render)
  - No changes to HomeScreen / ConversationsScreen / ProjectHomeScreen call sites (they keep their onNavigateToBackendSwitcher param semantics; only what it does changes upstream)
acceptance_criteria: Pill tap opens bottom sheet across all four host surfaces. Sheet shows saved configs, active one marked, edit/delete affordances work, '+ Add server' opens the existing ConfigScreen. Selecting a row swaps the active backend and dismisses without rebuilding the nav stack.

title: Animate ToolCallCard expand/collapse with ChatMotion (matches RunBlock + ToolOutputRenderer)
description:
The tool-call card body in ChatToolCallCards.kt:237-345 uses bare `if (showDetails) { ... }` — tapping the header expands/collapses the card with a hard cut. The original author noted this was intentional to avoid LazyColumn scroll-anchor shifts, but ToolOutputRenderer + RunBlock both prove SizeTransform(clip = true) + ChatMotion.contentSizeSpec animates cleanly without the scroll-anchor problem.

Fix: wrap the expanded body in AnimatedContent keyed on showDetails, using ChatMotion.expandEnter / expandExit + SizeTransform(clip = true) { _, _ -> ChatMotion.contentSizeSpec }. Skip the animation while pinching (LocalChatIsPinching.current) — same guard ToolOutputRenderer uses to avoid cascading height interpolations during multi-touch.

Files:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatToolCallCards.kt

title: Optimistic-twin dedup in render layer (catches adjacent client-/cm-prefixed bubbles next to confirmed twins)
description:
Defense-in-depth fix from PM agent (2026-05-13, uncommitted in main repo working tree at session start). Complements letta-mobile-cdm5 (which widens the timeline-level USER fuzzy window) by adding a render-time safety net that collapses ADJACENT same-(role,content) pairs in dedupeGroupedMessagesForLazyKeys when at least one side carries a 'client-' or 'cm-' optimistic id prefix.

Scenarios cdm5 does NOT cover that this catches:
  - Two user_locals appended back-to-back (e.g., bootstrap-fresh-route swap path in ChatConversationCoordinator: client-user-initial-duplicate-* + the real Confirmed once it lands)
  - Reasoning-stream → final-assistant pairs that the upstream dedupeReasoningAssistantEchoes would normally handle but where one side has an optimistic id mid-flight
  - Any future race that produces an adjacent twin without an intervening event

Constraints baked into the dedup:
  - Only collapses ADJACENT pairs (non-adjacent are intentional repeats — user retried after typing more in between)
  - Requires at least one side to be optimistic (two server-issued same-content msgs are legitimate quick-fire repeats)
  - Same role required (never collapses user↔assistant)
  - isReasoning matches required (reasoning↔assistant collapse stays the responsibility of dedupeReasoningAssistantEchoes)
  - Skips tool-call bubbles (ownership: compactRunToolCallSteps)
  - Trim-tolerant content compare (whitespace differences ignored)
  - When both sides optimistic, keeps the EARLIER one (stable ordering during recompose storms)
  - When one side is confirmed, keeps the confirmed side (downstream consumers latch onto confirmed identity)

Tests: 10 new test cases in ChatRenderModelBuilderTest covering each constraint above.

Files:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt
  - android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatRenderModelBuilderTest.kt

title: Throttled hybrid pinch-to-zoom: mid-gesture font-scale checkpoints (d9zy.1)
description:
Replaces the current 'GPU-only-during-gesture, commit fontScale once on lift' pinch-to-zoom with a throttled hybrid: GPU scaling every pointer frame (zero recomposition) PLUS a 100ms periodic checkpoint loop that commits the real fontScale mid-gesture when transientPinchScale has drifted >5% from the reset baseline of 1.0f. Simultaneously resets transientPinchScale back to 1.0f so the GPU layer un-scales as the new typography lays out — preventing a visible jump.

Trade-off: the lift-only approach (5e0f.r3) had a final 'snap' on release as the layer rasters at 1.0 but typography re-flows. The throttled hybrid amortizes that snap into many small ones (at most ~10/sec) so the typography keeps up with the gesture without flooding the recomposition pipeline (which 5e0f.r2 tried and produced the strobe Emmanuel reported).

Source: agent worktree a4f92d57df3bebe5c. The diff is well-commented but I will REVIEW it carefully before adopting — per user directive 'do not blindly accept harvested work'. Watch for: midGestureBase tracking via mutableFloatStateOf, currentOnActiveFontScaleChange via rememberUpdatedState (correct Compose idiom), the rebase logic where liveZoom = transientPinchScale * zoom on each frame.

Files:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageList.kt

title: Restore expand/collapse motion on RunBlock chat sections
description:
Regression: commit 74314380 stabilized the run timeline (fixed a mid-stream sibling swap) but silently dropped the AnimatedContent wrapper that 50efc445 had added around the RunBlock collapsible body. User reported 'expressive animations on opening and closing sections in the chat timeline regressed'. ToolOutputRenderer keeps its expand/collapse motion via the same pattern (see 3wjn) — RunBlock should match.

Implementation: wrap the visible-step Column in AnimatedContent keyed on collapsed (NOT on messages), so user-driven expand/collapse plays the canonical ChatMotion ramp while streaming updates flow through the inner lambda without re-triggering the transition. Mirrors the proven ToolOutputRenderer pattern.

Plus a structural regression-guard test in ChatMotionTest that pins RunBlock.kt's wiring so a future refactor cannot silently drop the motion again.

Source: stash@{0} from 2026-05-13 PM-agent harvest. Already implemented and tested locally; just needs to land on a branch + PR.

Files:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlock.kt
  - android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMotionTest.kt

title: Hide composer Send button while keyboard is open (avoid duplicate with IME Send)
description:
Behaviour ask from 2026-05-13 design verification: when the soft keyboard is visible, hide our composer's trailing Send button because the keyboard already has its own Send action (LettaInputBar wires KeyboardOptions(imeAction=Send) → KeyboardActions.onSend). The two duplicate buttons are visually noisy and can confuse the user about which one to tap.

Constraint: the trailing button must STAY visible when isStreaming==true even with the keyboard open, because that button morphs into Stop and the user needs a way to abort the run while typing the next message.

Logic:
  visible = !keyboardOpen || isStreaming

Implementation outline:
  - LettaInputBar: new actionVisible: Boolean = true param. Wrap FilledIconButton in AnimatedVisibility (horizontal expand/collapse, ChatMotion-style) so the text field flows into the freed space. Reduced-motion path skips the animation.
  - ChatComposer: detect keyboard via WindowInsets.isImeVisible, compute actionVisible = !keyboardOpen || isStreaming, pass through.

Files:
  - android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/LettaInputBar.kt
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatComposer.kt

Test plan (manual on device):
  - Open conversation, tap text field. Keyboard opens. Send button slides out, text field grows.
  - Tap somewhere outside or back-button. Keyboard closes. Send button slides back in.
  - Send a message that takes >5s. While the keyboard is closed: Send morphs to Stop. Tap text field to reopen keyboard during streaming. Stop must STAY visible (does not slide out).

title: d9zy.5 retry: cursor fade + button morph + heartbeat scale-pulse
description:
Retry letta-mobile-d9zy.5 (Fluid Streaming-to-Settled Handoff) following the 2026-05-12 rollback.

User-approved approach:
1. Cursor fade: 500ms alpha tween in MessageContentFactory.kt when isStreaming flips false. Was rolled back as collateral on 2026-05-12 (not specifically rejected). Lowest blast radius.
2. Button morph: wrap the Send<->Close icon swap in ChatComposer.kt with AnimatedContent for a crossfade/scale transition. Fresh territory; not in the previous attempt.
3. Heartbeat scale-pulse: a subtle, repeating scale pulse on the Stop button while isStreaming==true. Replaces the previously-rejected CircularProgressIndicator ring (rejected for misalignment, 2026-05-12). User quote: 'I did not hate the ideas, I just hated the implementation we had Sonnet do.'

Constraints from bead memory (do not violate):
- DO NOT use CircularProgressIndicator around the stop button -- that's the rejected version.
- Animations must bypass cleanly when system animations are disabled (per d9zy.5 acceptance criteria).

Bundle scope:
User said 'roll all of it up into a new branch so we can follow our new workflow.' Also gather any PM agent in-flight work from these locked worktrees before starting:
  /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/.claude/worktrees/agent-a4f92d57df3bebe5c  (modifies ChatMessageList.kt)
  /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/.claude/worktrees/agent-a7bed4f80d46c6ab9  (modifies ChatComposer.kt, MessageContentFactory.kt, StreamingMarkdownText.kt -- relevant to d9zy.5)
  /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/.claude/worktrees/agent-afd5102370bdb5b98  (modifies ChatMessageComponents.kt)
All three are on branch '2047f2e5 feat: modernize Kotlin idioms...' Decide what to keep / merge.

Prerequisite: PR #43 (chore/ci-aggregator-and-protection-fix) must merge first so the new branch can land cleanly through the workflow. Then run scripts/bootstrap-branch-protection.sh once to restore build-apk-pass as a required check.

Files involved:
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatComposer.kt
  - android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/MessageContentFactory.kt
  - android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/LettaInputBar.kt
  - android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/StreamingMarkdownText.kt

Workflow:
  git checkout main && git pull
  git checkout -b feat/d9zy5-retry
  ./scripts/install-hooks.sh
  # ...implement, commit, push, gh pr create...

Test plan:
  - Install dev APK on Pixel 192.168.50.235:5555
  - Send a message, verify cursor fades over 500ms (not abrupt)
  - Verify Send<->Stop icon transition is smooth, not a hard swap
  - Verify Stop button has a subtle, RHYTHMIC scale pulse (heartbeat, not a ring)
  - Toggle 'Remove animations' in dev settings -> verify all three degrade cleanly
notes: Implementation shipped on PR #44 https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/44 (branch feat/d9zy5-retry, commit e12f3462). Three features: (1) cursor fade — AnnotatedString span with alpha tween 1f→0f over 500ms; refactored streamingDisplayText to no longer inject the cursor glyph (cursor injection moved into StreamingMarkdownText); 11 cursor tests rewrote, 4 new tests added for shouldShowStreamingCursor. (2) Send/Stop icon morph via AnimatedContent in LettaInputBar (150ms in, 120ms out, scale+fade). (3) Heartbeat 1.0→1.04 scale-pulse via Modifier.scale on FilledIconButton, 800ms cycle, infinite while actionPulse=true. New rememberReducedMotionEnabled() helper in designsystem reads ANIMATOR_DURATION_SCALE; all three motions degrade to instant when reduced motion is on. Dev APK installed on Pixel 192.168.50.235:5555. CI pending on PR #44; awaiting user manual verification before close. Two follow-up PRs queued from the harvest: pinch-to-zoom checkpoint from worktree a4f92d57 + RunBlock motion-restoration stash; not yet started — pausing for user verification of this PR first.

title: Address remaining PR #37 review items (outside-diff + minor)
description: Continuation of letta-mobile-rnyg. Critical value-class LazyList keys (~9 sites: AgentListScreen, BotConfigDialogs, ArchiveAdminScreen, McpServerToolsScreen). Major perf: map-inside-filter for excludedBlockIds and identity.agentIds. Minor items: hardcoded strings (AgentScaffoldPickers, HomeScreenSearch, EditAgentContent, EditAgentHelperDialogs), position-based message key, negative-int guard on limit field, Locale.US in formatEditAgentNumber, accessibility cue on validation warning, duplicate Close buttons in ToolDetailDialog.
close_reason: All outside-diff and minor items addressed: value-class LazyList keys at 7 sites, 2 map-inside-filter perf hoists, Locale.US -> getDefault, negative-int guard on character limits, hardcoded Copy/Section/Sections/Identity/Unnamed/Message + many AgentScaffoldPickers literals -> string resources (plus chat-mode locale-aware labels), duplicate Close buttons in ToolDetailDialog, position-based message key -> identity-based via messageId, accessibility cue (Icon + contentDescription) for validation warning. BONUS: fixed crash-on-open NPE in EncryptedPrefsHelper static-bridge by eagerly @Injecting it from LettaApplication (same syf4 pattern as the two registries). APK built and installed on Pixel.

title: Phase 4: Tighten :feature-chat visibility — lock the module boundary
description:
Make the module boundary actually enforced. Goal: \`public\` symbols in \`:feature-chat\` are *only* what external callers need; everything else is \`internal\`.

## Targets

- Sweep every \`public\` (default) class, function, and property in \`:feature-chat\` — flip to \`internal\` unless it's reached from outside the module.
- Confirm the only \`public\` surface is:
  - \`AgentScaffold\` (composable)
  - \`ChatGraph\` (NavGraphBuilder extension, from Phase 3)
  - Any abstractions that survived Phase 0 as cross-module shared types.
- Symbols formerly marked \`internal\` for cross-file access *within the package* can stay \`internal\` — \`internal\` now means module-scoped which is what we want.

## Why this matters

The 2026-05-12 streaming regression (letta-mobile-e1na) crossed the file boundary between \`RunBlock.kt\` and \`ChatMessageComponents.kt\` via the \`ToolCallEntrance\` symbol. Inside a single package, \`internal\` was effectively free access — no signal during code review that the wrapper was being shared across files in a way that would create a streaming hazard. With a module boundary, \`internal\` actually means something, and any future symbol that wants to cross feature boundaries has to do it via the explicit public API.

## Verification

- \`./gradlew :feature-chat:compileDebugKotlin\` clean (some \`public\` → \`internal\` flips may surface unused exposed types — fine, delete or hide them).
- Full app build still green.
- Run a \"would this build if I deleted my public API doc\" test: try removing one of the documented public symbols, confirm the compiler surfaces the breakage clearly.

## Estimate

Half-day.

## Dependencies

- Blocked-by: letta-mobile-rcyj.4 (chat must be inside its module before we can tighten its boundary).
close_reason: Merged PR #88: locked :feature-chat visibility, kept only route/nav/scaffold/version-provider API public, and removed app imports of chat-local internals.

title: Phase 3: Migrate chat code into :feature-chat module
description:
Move chat sources from \`:app\` into \`:feature-chat\` and update external callers.

## Moves

- \`android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/**\` → \`android-compose/feature-chat/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/feature/chat/**\`
- Package rename: \`com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat\` → \`com.letta.mobile.feature.chat\`
- Chat tests move: \`app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/**\` → \`feature-chat/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/feature/chat/**\`
- Strings: migrate chat-prefixed strings from \`:app/res/values/strings.xml\` into \`:feature-chat/res/values/strings.xml\`.
- Drawables / icons used only by chat: same migration.

## External call sites to update

- \`channel/NotificationReplyHandler.kt\` — import path updated, or switched to the \`:core\`-resident abstraction from Phase 0 if that move happened.
- \`ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt\` — replace direct \`AgentScaffold\` import with a \`ChatGraph\` extension function added to \`:feature-chat\`. Nav routes stay co-located with the feature.
- \`ui/screens/conversations/TwoPaneConversationsLayout.kt\` — import path updated.

## Test fixtures

- Audit \`core/src/testFixtures\` fakes (\`FakeAgentApi\`, \`FakeBlockApi\`, \`FakeToolApi\`, \`TestData\`) — they should stay in \`:core\` since multiple features use them.
- Any chat-only test helpers move with chat.

## Verification

- \`:app\` and \`:feature-chat\` both compile.
- Full unit-test suite green: \`./gradlew test\`.
- Manual smoke: open chat, send a message, run a tool call, navigate to a project that uses two-pane layout.
- APK size diff: should be neutral or slightly smaller (some inlining no longer applicable across module boundary is fine; watch for accidental duplication).

## Estimate

1 day. Largest part is the navigation entrypoint refactor.

## Dependencies

- Blocked-by: letta-mobile-rcyj.3 (Phase 2 must have created the module).
close_reason: Merged PR #87: migrated chat and voice sources/tests into :feature-chat, moved chat routes/nav graph behind the feature boundary, and addressed review fixes.

title: Phase 1: Split chat monolith files (AgentScaffold, ChatMessageComponents, ToolOutputRenderer)
description:
Pure refactor — no behavior change, no module move yet. Goal: prove the file seams before locking them behind a module boundary.

## Files to split

### \`AgentScaffold.kt\` (2,125 LOC) → 5–6 files
- \`AgentScaffold.kt\` — top-level scaffold only
- \`AgentScaffoldDrawer.kt\` — DrawerContent + chat-mode + edit-agent header
- \`AgentScaffoldTopBar.kt\` — top app bar incl. transport/location chips
- \`AgentScaffoldProjectInfo.kt\` — ProjectAgentsCard, ProjectBugReportSummaryCard, ProjectBriefCard, ProjectInfoTray, ProjectContextCard
- \`AgentScaffoldConversationPicker.kt\` — ConversationPickerSheet

### \`ChatMessageComponents.kt\` (1,789 LOC) → 6–7 files
- \`MessageBubble.kt\` — ChatMessageItem, MessageBubbleSurface
- \`MessageApproval.kt\` — ApprovalRequestCard, ApprovalRequestControls, ApprovalActionRow, ApprovalResponseCard
- \`MessageReasoning.kt\` — MessageReasoning + reasoningPreview
- \`MessageAvatar.kt\`
- \`MessageToolCalls.kt\` — MessageToolCalls dispatcher
- \`ToolCallEntrance.kt\` — the shared entrance composable (currently \`internal\`, called from RunBlock and three sites here — this is where the recent streaming regression crossed seams)
- \`ToolCallCard.kt\` — ToolCallCard + ToolCallExpandedSummary
- \`CompactToolCallGroup.kt\` — CompactToolCallGroupCard + CompactToolCallRow

Splitting \`ToolCallEntrance.kt\` out is load-bearing: it's the symbol that produced the cross-file regression in letta-mobile-e1na. Making it its own file forces every caller to be explicit.

### \`ToolOutputRenderer.kt\` (1,148 LOC) → dispatch + per-type renderers
- \`ToolOutputRenderer.kt\` — keep top-level dispatch
- \`renderers/\` subfolder with one file per \`ToolOutputDocument\` subtype

## Process

- One PR per file split, smallest possible diffs. Each PR runs the full chat test suite.
- No symbol visibility changes in this phase. Visibility tightening happens in Phase 4.
- No package moves — files stay in \`com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat\` for now.

## Verification

- All existing chat tests pass: \`:app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.*'\`.
- Visual / screenshot tests (if any apply) show zero change.
- Manual smoke: streaming, tool calls, approvals, drawer.

## Estimate

1–2 days. Mechanical but careful. 3–4 small PRs.

## Dependencies

- Blocked-by: letta-mobile-rcyj.1 (Phase 0 audit informs how we split symbols).
close_reason:
Completed decomposition of ChatMessageComponents.kt and AgentScaffold.kt.

ChatMessageComponents.kt (1777 lines) → 5 files:
- ChatMessageComponents.kt (330 lines): ChatMessageItem, MessageAvatar, copy utilities
- ChatToolCallCards.kt (935 lines): MessageToolCalls, ToolCallCard, compact groups, entrance animation
- ChatReasoning.kt (194 lines): MessageReasoning, reasoning preview
- ChatApprovals.kt (176 lines): approval card components
- ChatMessageBubble.kt (292 lines): MessageBubbleSurface, bubble utilities

AgentScaffold.kt (2126 lines) → 3 files:
- AgentScaffold.kt (607 lines): main scaffold, search bar, search results
- AgentScaffoldDrawer.kt (805 lines): ProjectAgentsCard, drawer content
- AgentScaffoldPickers.kt (895 lines): AttachmentPicker, BlockPicker, ArchivePicker

All extracted functions changed from private to internal. Build passes.
Commits: 6922ccb7, 2a07ee72

title: Phase 0: Audit chat package public surface and identify pre-extraction moves
description:
Decision / audit task. No code changes land in this issue beyond annotations and docs.

## Goals

1. Produce a list of every symbol currently exported from \`com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat\` (and sub-packages) that is used outside the chat package or that *should* remain reachable from outside once chat is its own module.
2. Decide which symbols should move to \`:core\` *before* extraction (e.g. \`ClientModeChatSender\` likely belongs in \`:core\` or a small \`:client-mode\` module shared with \`:bot\`).
3. Output a short markdown doc (or kdoc on the relevant symbols) declaring the intended public surface of \`:feature-chat\`.

## Inputs

- 3 known external import sites:
  - \`channel/NotificationReplyHandler.kt\` → \`ClientModeChatSender\`
  - \`ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt\` → \`AgentScaffold\`
  - \`ui/screens/conversations/TwoPaneConversationsLayout.kt\` → \`AgentScaffold\`
- Any string / drawable / resource references from outside chat into chat resources.

## Output

- A list of pre-Phase-3 moves (e.g. \"move ClientModeChatSender to :core\").
- A target public API for \`:feature-chat\` (probably just \`AgentScaffold\` + a \`ChatGraph\` NavGraphBuilder extension).
- Confirmation that no Hilt module currently in \`:app\` exposes chat-internal types that other features depend on.

## Estimate

Half-day.
notes:
## Phase 0 Audit Results

### External Surface (complete)

Only **2 symbols** from `com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat` are imported outside the package:

| Symbol | Type | External Callers |
|---|---|---|
| `AgentScaffold` | @Composable | `AppNavGraph.kt:629`, `TwoPaneConversationsLayout.kt:102` |
| `ClientModeChatSender` | @Singleton | `NotificationReplyHandler.kt:30` (injected), `NotificationReplyHandlerTest.kt:27` (mock) |

`AdminChatViewModel` referenced only in a doc comment in `CurrentConversationTracker.kt` — no code import.

### Internal Coupling (excellent)

- **Intra-package imports: 0** — no file in chat/ imports from `com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.*`
- All cross-file coupling via function calls and DI injection — no hidden import dependencies
- **47 `internal` declarations** — all should remain `internal` in :feature-chat
- **336+ `private` declarations** — safe, no changes needed
- **0 `@Module`, `@Provides`, `@Binds`** inside chat package — DI is external

### Resources

- **36 chat-only strings** (`screen_chat_*`, `action_attach_image`, `action_edit`, `action_search`, `action_stop_run`) → migrate to :feature-chat
- **11 shared strings** (`action_back`, `action_cancel`, `action_copy`, etc.) → keep in :app
- **No drawables, colors, or dimens** used only by chat
- **0 cross-module resource references** (no designsystem.R or core.R)

### DI Architecture (key blocker)

**⛔ CRITICAL: AdminChatViewModel CANNOT move to :feature-chat without creating circular dependency.**

`AdminChatViewModel` has 15 @Inject dependencies:
- 8 from :core (repositories) ✅ fine
- 1 from :bot (InternalBotClient) ✅ fine  
- 2 from :app/chat (ClientModeChatSender, ClientModeAgentLocationRepository) → would become internal
- **3 from :app/channel** (CurrentConversationTracker, NotificationDeliveryCoordinator, NotificationReplyHandler) ❌ circular

`CurrentConversationTracker` is a trivial state holder. `NotificationReplyHandler` ↔ `NotificationDeliveryCoordinator` form a circular pair (broken via Provider<>). All three are in :app/channel.

### Recommended Resolution

**Move AdminChatViewModel to :feature-chat, with pre-move of channel classes:**

1. Move `CurrentConversationTracker` → :core (trivial state holder, no app dependencies)
2. Move `NotificationReplyHandler` + `NotificationDeliveryCoordinator` → :bot (they depend on ClientModeChatSender which also moves to :bot)
3. Move `ClientModeController` + `ClientModeChatSender` + `ClientModeAgentLocationRepository` → :bot (bot infrastructure)

This makes :feature-chat depend on :core + :bot + :designsystem — no :app dependency needed.

**Target shape becomes:**
```
:app ─┬─→ :feature-chat ──┬─→ :designsystem ─→ :core
      ├─→ :designsystem ──┘                 ↗
      ├─→ :bot ──────────────────────────
      └─→ :core
```

### Pre-Phase-3 Moves

| Move | From | To |
|---|---|---|
| CurrentConversationTracker | :app/channel | :core |
| ClientModeController | :app/clientmode | :bot |
| ClientModeChatSender | :app/chat | :bot |
| ClientModeAgentLocationRepository | :app/chat | :bot |
| NotificationReplyHandler | :app/channel | :bot |
| NotificationDeliveryCoordinator | :app/channel | :bot |
| NotificationReplyReceiver (EntryPoint) | :app/channel | :bot |

### Public API for :feature-chat

- `AgentScaffold` @Composable
- `ChatGraph` NavGraphBuilder extension (wraps composable<AgentChatRoute>)
- All route types (AgentChatRoute stays co-located)

Everything else → `internal` (locked in Phase 4).
close_reason: Phase 0 audit is already recorded in bead notes; no code changes required.

title: Modularize :app — extract :feature-chat and split chat monolith files (epic)
description:
Epic to reduce \`:app\` module size and lock the chat feature behind a module boundary, eliminating the cross-file accidental-coupling failure mode that produced the 2026-05-12 streaming regression (letta-mobile-e1na).

## Current shape

- \`:app\` is 49,144 LOC of Kotlin across 164 files.
- \`ui/screens/chat/\` alone is 12,645 LOC across 39 files — 26% of all UI screen code.
- Three files dominate chat:
  - \`AgentScaffold.kt\` — 2,125 LOC
  - \`ChatMessageComponents.kt\` — 1,789 LOC
  - \`ToolOutputRenderer.kt\` — 1,148 LOC
- Only 3 external import sites use the chat package:
  - \`channel/NotificationReplyHandler.kt\` → \`ClientModeChatSender\`
  - \`ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt\` → \`AgentScaffold\`
  - \`ui/screens/conversations/TwoPaneConversationsLayout.kt\` → \`AgentScaffold\`

Extraction is feasible — the public surface is tiny, the internal coupling is large but self-contained.

## Target shape

\`\`\`
:app ─┬─→ :feature-chat ──┬─→ :designsystem ─→ :core
      ├─→ :designsystem ──┘                 ↗
      └─→ :bot ──────────────────────────────
\`\`\`

\`:feature-chat\` exposes only:
- \`AgentScaffold\` composable
- A \`ChatGraph\` extension function on \`NavGraphBuilder\` for nav wiring
- Whatever abstraction backs \`ClientModeChatSender\` (likely moved to \`:core\`)

Everything else stays \`internal\`.

## Phases (see child issues)

- Phase 0: Audit & decide public surface
- Phase 1: Split the three giant files in place (no module move yet)
- Phase 2: Create empty \`:feature-chat\` module
- Phase 3: Migrate code into \`:feature-chat\`
- Phase 4: Tighten visibility
- Phase 5 (stretch): Same treatment for \`:feature-editagent\`

## Non-goals
- Splitting \`:core\` — appropriately sized for its responsibility.
- Reorganizing \`:designsystem\`.
- Multiplatform / shared-source moves — separate conversation.
- Behavior changes to chat — every phase is a refactor.

## Wins
- Compile-time guarantees that chat internals don't leak.
- Faster incremental builds when iterating on chat.
- Smaller PR diffs naturally (no more 2,125-line files).
- Sets the pattern for \`:feature-editagent\`, \`:feature-projects\`, etc.

## Risks tracked in child issues
- Hilt + Compose Hilt cross-module: validated in Phase 2.
- Strings / resources migration: handled in Phase 3.
- Navigation entrypoint design: Phase 3.
notes: Planning conversation 2026-05-12 with user. Triggered by the discovery that the streaming regression (letta-mobile-e1na) crossed file boundaries via internal symbols inside a single oversized package. The fix is structural, not just tactical.

title: Fluid Streaming-to-Settled Handoff
description:
## Objective
Replace harsh state snaps at the end of message generation with fluid, choreographed transitions in the input composer and markdown renderer.

## The Problem
Currently, when `isStreaming` flips from true to false:
1. The Send button in `ChatComposer.kt` instantly swaps icons (from Close back to Send).
2. The `▎` streaming cursor in `StreamingMarkdownText` instantly vanishes.
These hard state cuts feel mechanical and unpolished, breaking the illusion of a premium interaction.

## Implementation Details
1. **Composer Button Morphing**:
   - In `ChatComposer.kt`, wrap the action button in an `AnimatedContent` block so the transition between `LettaIcons.Send` and `LettaIcons.Close` uses a scale or morph animation.
   - While `isStreaming == true`, wrap the `Close` button with an indeterminate, circular `CircularProgressIndicator` (or a pulsing ring) to map the visual to the active network connection.
2. **Cursor Fade-Out**:
   - In `MessageContentFactory.kt` (`StreamingMarkdownText`), do not instantly drop the `STREAMING_CURSOR` string when `isStreaming` becomes false.
   - Instead, trigger a `500ms` alpha tween using `animateFloatAsState`.
   - Render the cursor character with this fading alpha value so that it softly "dissolves" into the background when the agent finishes typing.
3. **Graceful Degradation**: Ensure these animations bypass cleanly if animations are disabled at the system level.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The Send/Stop button morphs smoothly between states and features active pulse indicators while streaming.
- The text cursor fades out elegantly over 500ms instead of snapping out of existence.
- The end-of-turn experience feels soft and intentional.

title: Subordinate the Reasoning Block
description:
## Objective
Significantly reduce the visual weight and hierarchy of the "Thinking..." reasoning block, ensuring it does not compete with the actual assistant response.

## The Problem
The `MessageReasoning` block occupies a full row, features an animated `ExpandMore` chevron, bold text, and a loading spinner. It draws too much attention to the LLM's internal scratchpad process, distracting from the ultimate answer.

## Implementation Details
1. **Streaming State (Thinking)**:
   - Remove the `ExpandMore` chevron and the preview text during the active stream.
   - Replace the bold "Thinking..." text with a minimalist, shimmering or pulsing gradient text ("Synthesizing...").
   - Restrict the maximum vertical height of the block while streaming so it remains a quiet footer indicator.
2. **Settled State (Post-Stream)**:
   - Once the final assistant answer begins streaming (or the run completes), aggressively auto-collapse the reasoning block.
   - Replace the full row with a highly compressed, inline assist-chip style UI. For example: `[ ✦ 4.2s thought process ]`.
   - Make this compressed chip tap-to-expand. If the user expands it, display the reasoning text in a muted, slightly smaller typography scale (e.g., `bodySmall` with `onSurfaceVariant`).
3. **Animation**: Ensure the transition from the active "Synthesizing" state to the collapsed "thought process" chip is a fast, smooth alpha crossfade rather than a harsh layout snap.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reasoning blocks are visually quiet during generation.
- Reasoning blocks automatically collapse into a small, unobtrusive chip once generation is complete.
- Users can still access the reasoning trace via tap.

title: Typographical Spine for Assistant Stream
description:
## Objective
Create a visual tether along the left edge of assistant message streams to unify "bubble-less" prose and heavy "bubbled" tool cards.

## The Problem
The current design removes bubble surfaces from plain assistant text to keep it lightweight. However, because tool outputs, generated UI, and approval requests *do* use heavy `Surface` cards, the lack of a unifying container makes the conversation feel fragmented. The eye lacks a continuous vertical anchor.

## Implementation Details
1. **Spine Drawing**: Use `Modifier.drawBehind` on the parent container of the assistant's message group (or within `ChatMessageItem` spanning the full height).
2. **Visual Properties**: Draw a 1px to 2px vertical line. Use a highly subtle color, such as `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary.copy(alpha = 0.15f)` or the `outlineVariant` color.
3. **Alignment**: The spine must align perfectly with the vertical center axis of the 24dp Assistant Avatar. 
4. **Continuity**: The line should start just below the avatar and run continuously down the left side, spanning across bubble-less text and alongside the edges of any bubbled components within the same `GroupPosition`.
5. **Termination**: The spine should terminate at the bottom of the last message in the contiguous block (when `groupPosition == GroupPosition.Last`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- A subtle vertical line visually connects all messages in a continuous assistant turn.
- The line aligns exactly with the center of the avatar.
- The UI feels grounded and cohesive despite mixing cards and plain text.

title: Avatar Redundancy Elimination
description:
## Objective
Eliminate redundant avatar headers in contiguous message streams to improve vertical reading rhythm and reduce visual noise.

## The Problem
When an agent or user emits multiple consecutive messages (e.g., a tool call, followed by reasoning, followed by plain text), `ChatMessageItem` renders the 24dp avatar and Role Label for *every single message*. This creates a disjointed experience where messages from the same actor feel like disconnected sticky notes.

## Implementation Details
1. **Group Position Utilization**: `ChatMessageItem` already receives a `groupPosition: GroupPosition` parameter. Rely heavily on this state.
2. **First Position Logic**: If `groupPosition == GroupPosition.First` or `GroupPosition.None`, render the Avatar and the Role Label as normal.
3. **Middle/Last Logic**: If `groupPosition == GroupPosition.Middle` or `GroupPosition.Last`, completely hide the `MessageAvatar` composable and the role text. 
4. **Vertical Padding Adjustments**: When an avatar is hidden, reduce the vertical spacing between the messages. Currently, spacing is typically `6.dp`. For `Middle` and `Last` positions, collapse this padding to `2.dp` or `0.dp` to visually link the contiguous blocks.
5. **Edge Cases**: Ensure that pending messages, error messages, and tool activities do not inadvertently break grouping. If an error block is injected, it should break the group and force a new avatar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Consecutive messages from the same actor share a single avatar header at the top.
- Vertical space between grouped messages is tightly collapsed.
- The reading flow feels like paragraphs of a single block.

title: Throttled Hybrid Reflow for Pinch-to-Zoom
description:
## Objective
Replace the current end-of-gesture typography reflow with a hybrid approach that provides high-frequency visual scaling combined with debounced true typographical reflows.

## The Problem
Currently, `ChatMessageList.kt` uses `Modifier.graphicsLayer { scaleX = transientPinchScale; scaleY = transientPinchScale }` during the pinch-to-zoom gesture. While this ensures a solid 60fps, it simply raster-stretches the existing layout. Text becomes visibly blurry and distorted mid-gesture, only snapping to crispy typographical bounds when the user lifts their fingers.

## Implementation Details
1. **State Management**: Introduce a debounced coroutine flow (or a 100ms throttle) that tracks the delta between the current `transientPinchScale` and the last committed `fontScale`.
2. **Threshold Trigger**: When the delta exceeds a noticeable threshold (e.g., 5% scale change) OR the throttle interval (e.g., 100ms) elapses, commit the true `fontScale` change by calling `onActiveFontScaleChange`.
3. **Seamless Handoff**: At the exact moment `onActiveFontScaleChange` fires, you must simultaneously reset `transientPinchScale` to `1.0f` in the same recomposition frame. This ensures the GPU scaling layer resets exactly as the new native text layout takes over, preventing visual jumps.
4. **Performance Bounds**: Ensure this debounce mechanism is suspended if `LocalChatIsPinching` is false. Keep the existing `animateContentSize` suppression active so that downstream components (like reasoning blocks) do not attempt to tween their heights during these periodic reflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pinch-to-zoom updates the actual text layout periodically during the gesture.
- Text does not stay blurry for the entirety of a long pinch gesture.
- Frame rate remains acceptable (no high-frequency stutter).

title: Chat UX Refinement
description:
This epic tracks the implementation of the UX/UI audit insights to elevate the Chat Screen from a performant but disjointed interface into a flawless, premium experience.

## Objectives

1. **Throttled Hybrid Reflow for Pinch-to-Zoom**
   - Keep GPU scaling for 60fps smoothness during the gesture.
   - Implement a debounced flow to commit true `fontScale` recalculations (e.g. every 100ms or 5% scale delta) mid-gesture to maintain typographical crispness.

2. **Avatar Redundancy Elimination**
   - Utilize existing `GroupPosition` logic in `ChatMessageItem`.
   - Show the 24dp avatar and Role Label ONLY for `GroupPosition.First`.
   - Hide the avatar and compress vertical spacing (from 6dp to 2dp) for `GroupPosition.Middle` and `GroupPosition.Last` to create contiguous message flows.

3. **Typographical Spine for Assistant Stream**
   - Draw a subtle 1px to 2px vertical hairline (using `Modifier.drawBehind`) aligned with the center of the avatar.
   - Run this hairline down the left side of the assistant's message group to visually tether "bubble-less" prose and heavy tool cards into a cohesive stream.

4. **Subordinate the Reasoning Block**
   - Minimize visual hierarchy of the "Thinking..." state.
   - During streaming: Hide preview text, show subtle shimmering gradient text ("Synthesizing...").
   - Post-streaming: Auto-collapse the block into a highly compressed, tap-to-expand inline chip (e.g., `[ ✦ 4.2s thought process ]`).

5. **Fluid Streaming-to-Settled Handoff**
   - Enhance the Send/Stop button in `ChatComposer.kt`: Wrap the Stop button in a circular, pulsing `CircularProgressIndicator` while streaming.
   - When streaming ends, fade out the `▎` cursor over 500ms instead of abruptly snapping it away.
notes:
Audit complete and epic created. Ready to begin work on Chat UX Refinement (Pinch-to-zoom, Avatar Redundancy, Typographical Spine, Reasoning Subordination, and Streaming Handoff).
Generated 5 detailed subbeads shaping the implementation for Pinch-to-zoom reflow, Avatar Redundancy, Typographical Spine, Reasoning Subordination, and Streaming Handoff.

title: Enable -Xcontext-parameters and refactor implicit dependencies
description: Add -Xcontext-parameters flag to build.gradle.kts and update Telemetry/Logger usages to use Context Parameters.

title: Introduce Kotlin Contracts for validation smart-casting
description: Create core/utils/Contracts.kt with requireValidAgent and requireLoadedState to remove redundant null checks and leverage compiler smart-casts.

title: Introduce @JvmInline value classes for domain IDs
description: Refactor AgentId, ProjectId, ToolId, BlockId to value classes and update Room DAOs and Ktor serialization.

title: Modernize Kotlin Idioms (Value Classes, Contracts, Context Params)
Discover and connect to LettaCode runtime targets (architecture)
description:
Architectural epic for adding LettaCode runtime hosts as a first-class transport option in the Android client. Builds on `letta-mobile-dm4z` (Environment picker), which already plumbs an `AgentEnvironment.LettaCodeRuntime(hostId, workingDir)` variant — this issue makes those hosts real.

## Goals

1. The Environment picker lists discoverable LettaCode runtime nodes (local + remote on the network).
2. Selecting one routes agent traffic through that runtime (chat, tool calls, file ops) instead of the hosted server.
3. Sessions can be "transported" between environments without losing context where possible, or with explicit confirmation when not.

## Sub-areas

### Discovery
- Local device LettaCode runtime: detect via on-device probe (existing IPC or socket).
- LAN runtimes: mDNS / Bonjour advertisement scheme (e.g. `_lettacode._tcp.local`). Discovery service runs while drawer/picker open; cached briefly afterward.
- Manual entry: paste a host string or scan a QR for off-LAN endpoints.
- Identity: each discovered host advertises an ID + display name + capabilities (does it run agents? does it have a working directory? local vs remote?).

### Auth & trust
- First-connect handshake. Probably a short-lived token / pairing code shown in the LettaCode UI, entered in the mobile picker.
- Trusted hosts persisted (with revocation).
- Threat model and consent flow noted explicitly — mobile shouldn't silently start sending agent transcripts to anything it finds on the network.

### Transport interface
- Define `Transport` sealed/interface with implementations for `LettaServer`, `LettaBotClient`, `LettaCodeRuntime`. Each owns its message send / tool-call routing / file-op endpoints.
- VM layer reads `state.environment`, resolves to a `Transport`, dispatches accordingly.
- Backpressure / online-offline behavior per transport.

### In-flight handoff semantics
- What happens when the user switches environment mid-conversation? Options:
  - **Hard switch**: cancel in-flight, ack to user, future messages go to new transport.
  - **Drain then switch**: finish current turn on old transport, queue new messages on new transport. Probably the default.
  - **Migrate state**: only meaningful if both transports have access to the same agent state. Forward-looking.
- Show a confirmation when switching with unfinished tool calls.

### Discoverability cues
- Top-bar chip in the chat header (`letta-mobile-dm4z` may already cover this, or it's a follow-up): color + label showing active transport, tap to open the picker.
- Drawer Environment row shows hostname + ping/latency indicator when applicable.

## Status

Design epic. No implementation work scheduled until:
- `letta-mobile-dm4z` lands (UI surface exists).
- LettaCode runtime mobile-transport contract is defined on the runtime side.

Will be broken into concrete sub-issues once the runtime API is firmer.

## Open questions

- Is the runtime contract going to mirror the Letta Server REST/WS API, or expose a distinct one?
- Per-agent default environment vs per-session — confirm persistence model with the dm4z work.
- Does "transport this agent to another environment" eventually mean *migrating the agent's state* (memory blocks, etc.) or just routing traffic? Probably both, in different flows.
notes: User direction (2026-05-12): LettaCode runtime support is coming and the mobile client needs to be able to move agents between environments. Filed as an epic / design issue while the runtime API matures. Sub-issues to be created once the contract is firmer.

title: Generalize client-mode location picker into an Environment picker (drawer)
description:
Today the chat drawer exposes two related-but-narrow concepts:
- A `Client Mode` toggle (lives in `EditAgent → Runtime` tab, ugly placement)
- A client-mode `Location` chip / picker (lives in the drawer, only meaningful when Client Mode is on)

These are really two halves of one thing: **where this agent is currently operating from**. As LettaCode-runtime support lands, that target won't just be "this device's local working directory" — it'll be one of: Letta Server (hosted), LettaBot Client (this device), LettaCode Runtime (this device or a remote host), and eventually other discovered targets.

Goal: collapse those two surfaces into a single drawer-scoped **Environment** picker that scales to N transports without burying anything in `EditAgent`.

## Shape

- Rename the drawer section currently labeled (effectively) Client Mode / Location to **Environment**.
- Single row showing the active environment with icon + name + secondary line (working dir / host / project).
- Tap opens a `ModalBottomSheet` "Where does this agent run?" with grouped sections:
  - **Hosted** — Letta Server (default)
  - **Local devices** — LettaBot Client (this device), with nested "Working directory…" row that opens the existing path picker
  - **LettaCode Runtimes** (placeholder / hidden until letta-mobile-3 lands) — local + remote nodes
  - **Add host…** (future)
- Selection is per-agent, persists across drawer close.

## Code changes

- Introduce sealed `AgentEnvironment` type:
  - `LettaServer`
  - `LettaBotClient(workingDir: String?)`
  - `LettaCodeRuntime(hostId: String, workingDir: String?)` *(plumbed but unused until host discovery exists)*
- Migrate existing `EditAgentUiState.isClientModeEnabled` + `clientWorkingDirectory` into the new field with a backward-compatible adapter so saved agents keep working.
- New `EnvironmentPickerSheet` composable opened from `DrawerContent`. Reuses the existing path picker for the LettaBotClient "working directory" sub-flow.
- Drawer Environment row updates `selectedTab`-style local state and writes through the agent VM.

## Verification

- Existing client-mode users see their agent default to `LettaBotClient(workingDir = <old value>)` with no UI churn.
- Switching to `LettaServer` clears the working-dir chip from the chat header.
- Tests:
  - `AgentScaffoldTest` adds a case opening the Environment sheet and switching transports.
  - Unit test on the state migration adapter.

## Out of scope

- Removing the Client Mode toggle from `EditAgent → Runtime` — separate bead.
- Discovering / connecting to LettaCode runtimes — separate bead.
notes: User direction (2026-05-12): client-mode placement in EditAgent → Runtime is wrong. Should be a drawer-level concept since it's session-scoped, not agent-config. Generalize so that future LettaCode runtime targets fit the same picker without IA changes.

title: Wrap inline Compose filter/map allocations in remember
description: Multiple screens (SystemAccessDashboardScreen, ConversationsScreen, BlockPickerDialog) allocate new Lists inline via filter/map during recomposition, which breaks Compose stability. Wrap these allocations in remember(key).

title: Migrate collectAsState to collectAsStateWithLifecycle
description: MainActivity and AppNavGraph use collectAsState() for flows like appTheme and navViewModel state. This keeps flows active in the background. Migrate to collectAsStateWithLifecycle().

title: Offload LettaApplication init to background scope
description: LettaApplication.onCreate() currently blocks the main thread with channelHeartbeatScheduler.schedule() and other tasks. Wrap these in applicationScope.launch to improve cold start TTFF.

title: Replace EditAgent PrimaryTabRow with bottom-sheet section picker
description:
EditAgentScreen.kt renders 6 fixed-width tabs (Basics/Models/Memory/Tools/Runtime/Advanced) via PrimaryTabRow. On 360-411dp screens this wraps mid-word ('Basic\ns', 'Mode\nls', 'Runti\nme', etc.) — see attached screenshot in chat. Horizontal scrollable tabs were rejected as 'useless' by Emmanuel.

Replacement: a section-picker chip at the top of the form. Tapping it opens a ModalBottomSheet listing all six sections with their labels and the existing '•' validation-warning marker. Selecting a section updates selectedTab and dismisses the sheet.

This is the same mobile-native pattern already used elsewhere in the app (agent switcher, client-mode location picker).
acceptance_criteria:
- Tabs are no longer rendered as a PrimaryTabRow. They are replaced by a single full-width Row showing the current section name + a chevron, tappable to open a section picker bottom sheet.
- The bottom sheet shows the same 6 sections in order, with the '•' warning marker preserved when EditAgentConfigTab.hasValidationWarning(state) is true.
- selectedTab state behavior is unchanged: tapping a section in the sheet updates selectedTab and dismisses the sheet.
- Test tags EditAgentTestTags.tab(label) remain wired up to the new picker rows so existing UI tests continue to find each tab by label.
- Compile clean (./gradlew :app:compilePlayDebugKotlin).

title: Redesign chat drawer: pencil-icon edit on agent header, segmented chat mode
description:
The drawer at AgentScaffold.kt DrawerContent (1929) is dense and wastes vertical space:

- 'Edit Agent' is a giant full-width NavigationDrawerItem for a single action.
- Chat mode is rendered as 3 stacked NavigationDrawerItems (Simple, Interactive, Debug), each ~48dp, totaling ~144dp for what is logically a 3-state toggle.

Redesign:
1. Inline the Edit Agent action: move the pencil icon to a trailing IconButton in the agent header row (next to the name). Remove the standalone NavigationDrawerItem. Move the '$messageCount messages' subtitle directly beneath the header row.
2. Replace the three chat-mode NavigationDrawerItems with a SingleChoiceSegmentedButtonRow (Material3 native). Three segments: Simple | Interactive | Debug. Keeps the 'Chat mode' label above. Removes the check-icon-spacer pattern.
3. Preserve all existing onClick handlers, semantics, and test tags. The DrawerContent signature stays unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent name row contains the agent icon, name, and a trailing pencil IconButton wired to onEditAgent. Tapping the pencil closes the drawer and opens Edit Agent.
- Chat mode is rendered as a SingleChoiceSegmentedButtonRow with three segments. Selection updates chatMode via onChatModeSelected. The selected segment is visually distinct.
- No regression: existing test tags remain valid.
- Compile clean (./gradlew :app:compilePlayDebugKotlin).

title: Restore eased expand/collapse on ToolOutputRenderer
description:
Commit 74314380 ("Stabilize streaming tool-call timeline motion") removed AnimatedContent + SizeTransform from ToolOutputRenderer when stabilizing the run timeline. That removal fixed a LazyColumn measurement collision during streaming, but it also stripped the easing from user-initiated tool output expand/collapse. Taps now snap with no motion.

## Context
Originating commit: 74314380. Related earlier issues referenced in code comments: letta-mobile-d2z6, letta-mobile-5e0f.r2, letta-mobile-lbur.
design: Re-wrap only the inner ToolOutputBody inside ToolOutputRenderer with AnimatedContent gated on !isStreaming && !isPinching. Use ChatMotion.expandEnter()/expandExit() and SizeTransform with ChatMotion.contentSizeSpec, matching the prior implementation. Do NOT touch RunBlock — keep its container static.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tapping to expand or collapse a tool output uses the shared ChatMotion expand/collapse animation when the run is NOT streaming.
- During active streaming (isStreaming == true), the renderer remains static (no animateContentSize / AnimatedContent) to preserve the lazy-list measurement fix from 74314380.
- During pinch (LocalChatIsPinching), the renderer remains static.
- No reintroduction of animateContentSize on the RunBlock container.

Suppressed during pinch via LocalChatIsPinching to preserve 5e0f.r2 stability path. RunBlock container remains static — no animateContentSize reintroduced. Compile verified via :app:compilePlayDebugKotlin.

title: Define agent config tab taxonomy and field ownership
description:
Before implementing tabs on the agent config page, define the field taxonomy and ownership for each tab.

Problem:
- The agent config surface has grown organically and field ownership is ambiguous.
- Without a taxonomy, tabs can become arbitrary buckets and regress into hard-to-find settings.

Scope:
- Inventory all current agent config/edit fields.
- Assign each field to a proposed tab: Basics, Models, Memory, Tools, Runtime, Advanced, or another better set if discovered.
- Identify fields that are duplicated, obsolete, or better moved to a separate screen/dialog.
- Document any cross-tab validation dependencies.
- Note which tab should be opened by default for create vs edit flows.

Acceptance:
- A concise mapping of current fields to tab groups is added to the bead notes or nearby implementation docs/tests.
- Ambiguous fields have explicit placement rationale.
- Implementation bead can proceed without debating field ownership ad hoc.
notes:
Design constraint from device review:

The agent config page likely cannot support the originally suggested 6 top-level tabs on phone width. Maximum usable top-level tabs appears to be 3, maybe 4, before labels crowd/truncate and navigation becomes harder than the original long form.

Refined direction:
- Use 3–4 top-level tabs max.
- Put lower-level categories as sections/cards inside those tabs rather than separate tabs.
- Candidate top-level taxonomy:
  1. Profile — name, description, identity/persona basics.
  2. Model — LLM, embedding, context/window/model settings.
  3. Capabilities — tools, memory, MCP/base tools, approvals.
  4. Runtime / Advanced — client mode, server profile, transport, expert diagnostics.

Alternative 3-tab taxonomy if 4 still crowds:
  1. Profile
  2. Model
  3. Capabilities & Runtime

Implementation should prefer readable, stable tabs over exhaustive categorical purity. The goal is findability on mobile, not one tab per conceptual domain.

title: Add agent config tab validation and error navigation
description:
Ensure validation and save behavior remain clear after the agent config page is split into tabs.

Problem:
- Moving a long form into tabs can hide invalid fields on inactive tabs.
- Users need clear feedback about where errors are and how to fix them.

Scope:
- When validation fails, mark tabs containing invalid fields.
- Prefer auto-switching to the first invalid tab or providing a visible error summary with tab labels.
- Preserve existing save/apply semantics across all tabs.
- Ensure unsaved changes are not lost when switching tabs.

Acceptance:
- Invalid field on inactive tab is discoverable.
- Tab switch does not reset field state.
- Tests cover validation failure on at least one inactive tab.

title: Organize agent config page into logical tabs
description:
Introduce a tabbed organization model for the Letta agent config/edit page so settings are easier to find and scan.

Reference:
- Models screen now uses clear tabs (`LLM Models`, `Embedding Models`) plus filter chips and dense dark cards.
- User explicitly requested this organization pattern for the Letta agent config page.

Problem:
- Agent config currently mixes many unrelated settings into a long form.
- Users have to scroll and remember where advanced/model/memory/tool/runtime settings live.
- This increases cognitive load as the config surface grows.

Suggested tab groups:
- Basics: name, description, core identity/profile fields.
- Models: LLM model, embedding model, context/window/model-related controls.
- Memory: memory blocks, archival/recall-related settings.
- Tools: attached tools, MCP/base tool controls, approvals.
- Runtime: client mode, server profile, transport/runtime settings.
- Advanced: low-frequency expert settings and raw/diagnostic controls.

Scope:
- Add Material-style tabs to the agent config/edit page.
- Preserve existing fields and behavior; this is an organization/refactor pass, not a semantics change.
- Keep save/apply behavior clear across tabs.
- Ensure validation errors can navigate/highlight the tab containing the invalid field.
- Use current dense dark surface language and avoid old unmigrated card styling.

Acceptance:
- Agent config settings are grouped into logical tabs.
- Users can find model/tool/memory/runtime settings without scanning one long form.
- Existing tests for editing/saving agent config still pass.
- Add/adjust tests for tab selection and at least one field per tab.
notes:
Implemented tabbed Edit Agent organization (Basics, Models, Memory, Tools, Runtime, Advanced), validation warning badges for invalid tab contents, and Compose tests. Verify with: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.editagent.EditAgentScreenTest --no-daemon
Scope refinement:

Do not implement 6 top-level tabs. Device review suggests 3–4 tabs is the practical maximum on phone width. Use tabs for broad navigation and nested sections/cards for details.

Preferred implementation shape:
- Top-level tabs: Profile, Model, Capabilities, Runtime/Advanced (or collapse the last two if width requires 3 tabs).
- Inside each tab, use dense dark cards/sections for subgroups such as tools, memory, approvals, server profile, and expert settings.
- Avoid horizontally scrollable tab overload unless testing shows it is clearly better than fewer tabs.
close_reason: Implemented tabbed agent config organization with validation highlighting and tests.

title: Clean up conversation view header and menu metadata
description: User-reported UI cleanup: conversation view displays the agent name twice, shows conflicting last-use telemetry numbers, and has inconsistent menu styling/layout/height. Standardize the menu presentation, remove redundant agent-name display, clarify or consolidate last-use metadata, and verify the root debug build.
close_reason: Removed duplicate agent-name fallback from conversation cards, standardized conversation activity labels, and replaced mixed picker/drawer conversation rows with a shared fixed-height two-line ConversationMenuItem. Verified compileRootDebugKotlin and assembleRootDebug, installed and launched on dev device.

title: Extract independently testable chat render components
