Continue extracting tool output, run grouping, reasoning, and approval rendering into independently testable components with stable contracts and targeted regression tests.
close_reason: Verified on origin/main after PRs #87-#93: chat render model/grouping, RunBlock, reasoning, approvals, and tool output rendering are split into focused feature-chat components with targeted tests.

title: Decompose TimelineSyncLoop into reducer and IO collaborators
description: Architecture follow-up: TimelineSyncLoop.kt is very large and mixes fetch/reconcile, stream ingestion, tool-return attachment, state mutation, and notification dispatch. Split it into focused collaborators: fetch/reconcile orchestration, stream ingestion, tool-return attachment, and a state reducer. Preserve behavior with characterization tests before extraction and keep lock boundaries explicit.
notes: Implemented first TimelineSyncLoop decomposition slice. Extracted pure TimelineHydrationReducer for hydrate snapshot -> Timeline replacement, including tool_return/approval folding, pending-local preservation, concurrent confirmed preservation, disk local restore, live cursor, and visible event count. Extracted TimelineIngestNotificationDispatcher IO collaborator so listener lookup/notification callback remains outside writeMutex. Added TimelineHydrationReducerTest for tool_return folding and preserving concurrent locals. Existing TimelineSyncLoopTest and TimelineReducerCharacterizationTest were run to preserve behavior. Quality gates: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.timeline.TimelineHydrationReducerTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.timeline.TimelineSyncLoopTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.timeline.TimelineReducerCharacterizationTest'; plus a focused rerun of TimelineHydrationReducerTest after cleanup.
close_reason: Extracted TimelineHydrationReducer and TimelineIngestNotificationDispatcher, preserving behavior with targeted timeline tests; filed follow-ups for remaining stream/reconcile extraction.

title: Fix ConversationsViewModel loadConversations error boundary
description: Finding P2: ConversationsViewModel.loadConversations around line 99 launches two child coroutines inside a try, but the catch does not catch failures inside those children. A child failure can cancel the parent and leave loading state wrong. Use supervisorScope with async/await or sequential calls with one final state update. Add tests for one child failing while loading state and error UI settle deterministically.
notes: Implemented loadConversations with supervisorScope + async/await Result collection so agent and conversation refresh failures are handled deterministically. Added regression tests for conversation refresh failure stopping loading with an error and agent refresh failure preserving loaded conversations. Gates: :app:testSideloadDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest' PASS; :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest' PASS.
close_reason: Fixed ConversationsViewModel loadConversations error boundary and added child-failure regression coverage.

title: Add single-flight guards to repository refresh paths
description: Finding P2: AgentRepository, AllConversationsRepository, ConversationRepository, ToolRepository, and ProjectRepository use unsynchronized lastRefreshAtMillis freshness checks, e.g. AgentRepository.kt around line 86 and AllConversationsRepository.kt around line 59. Two screens can observe stale state and issue duplicate list calls. Add per-repository refresh mutexes or in-flight Deferred guards, preserve current cache semantics, and add tests showing concurrent refresh callers share one API request.
notes: Completed in commit 20aa0c8e. Added Mutex-guarded refreshIfStale/refresh entry points for AgentRepository, AllConversationsRepository, ConversationRepository, ToolRepository, and ProjectRepository; tests cover concurrent stale refresh callers sharing one list API request. Verification: ./android-compose/gradlew -p android-compose :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests '*RepositoryTest'. Pre-existing uncommitted UI/designsystem changes remain outside this commit.
close_reason: Added mutex-guarded stale refresh paths and concurrent refresh tests for agent, conversation, all-conversations, tool, and project repositories.

title: Cancel ConnectivityMonitor active-config collector on stop
description: Finding P2: ConnectivityMonitor.startMonitoring around line 97 launches activeConfig.collectLatest, but stopMonitoring around line 106 only unregisters the network callback. Each foreground/background cycle can add another permanent collector and duplicate reachability checks. Track the collector as Job? and cancel it in stopMonitoring; verify repeated start/stop cycles leave only one active collector and one reachability path.
notes: Completed in commit b458bb95. ConnectivityMonitor now stores activeConfigCollectorJob, cancels/clears it on stopMonitoring, and regression coverage verifies repeated stop/start cycles cancel the prior collector and install one active replacement. Verification: ./android-compose/gradlew -p android-compose :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.util.ConnectivityMonitorTest'. Pre-existing uncommitted UI/designsystem changes remain outside this commit.
close_reason: Tracked the activeConfig collector Job, canceled and cleared it in stopMonitoring, and added a regression test for repeated stop/start cycles.

title: Remove superfluous text from chat page
description: User-reported UI issue: the chat page contains superfluous text. Investigate the chat screen/composer/page copy, identify the redundant text, remove or simplify it, and verify the chat page still communicates required state and actions clearly.

title: Bound tool output raw document cache memory
description: Finding: ToolOutputRenderer.kt around line 570 caches ToolOutputDocument, which retains raw = raw. The cache is process-global and bounded only by entry count, so a few very large tool outputs can remain retained after the message leaves active view. Add a max-cacheable raw length or cache only parsed/renderable blocks for large outputs.
close_reason: Fixed in 459f96ee: tool output document cache skips oversized raw strings and render caches are bounded by LRU entry count. ToolOutputRendererTest passed; compileRootDebugKotlin passed.

title: Densify conversations list cards and remove duplicate metadata
description:
The conversations list cards currently repeat the same metadata and waste vertical space.

Observed from device screenshot:
- Card headline shows agent/name plus age, e.g. `Meridian · 6h ago`.
- Second row immediately repeats icon plus the same agent/name.
- A third timestamp line repeats time information and can conflict with the headline age.
- Cards are tall despite having little unique content.

Scope:
- Redesign conversation row/card metadata so agent/name appears once.
- Show one canonical recency timestamp.
- Use the second line for useful content such as last-message preview, project/context, status, or omit it when unavailable.
- Keep tap target accessible while reducing visual height.
- Preserve dark/light theme readability.

Acceptance:
- No duplicated agent/name in a single conversation card.
- No conflicting duplicate timestamps in a single card.
- More conversation rows fit on a phone screen than current layout.
- Screenshot/device verification confirms readability and tap comfort.
notes:
Follow-up screenshot comparison against Projects page:

Conversation cards still do not match the intended dark card styling. Projects uses darker, richer cards that blend with the dark surface; Conversations cards are noticeably lighter/gray and feel off-theme.

Additional title/content issue:
- Conversation card headline currently says generic `Conversation` for every row.
- This is low-information and visually repetitive.
- The title should be a meaningful conversation label: agent name, participant/context, last message subject/preview, project/channel name, or a derived fallback that is better than literal `Conversation`.

Refined acceptance:
- Conversation cards use dark surface/container styling consistent with Projects cards and current dark theme hierarchy.
- No card headline repeats generic `Conversation` unless there is truly no better available data; even then prefer a softer fallback.
- First visible line should help distinguish rows at a glance.

title: Convert high-volume repository lists to PagingSource
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
AgentRepository.refreshAgents fetches limit=1000 and other repositories use fetch-all caches for large collections.

Proposed implementation plan:
Convert endpoints with limit/offset/cursor to PagingSource. Avoid new limit=1000 patterns. Keep small/ephemeral memory caches only where justified.

Dependencies:
Depends on repository policy and conversation cache foundation.

Explicit tests:
Fake API verifies correct limit/cursor/offset requests and paging append/prepend behavior.

Success gate checklist:
- Conversation list supports pagination and cold-start cached content.
- No new fetch-all limit=1000/10000 patterns.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Migrate one repository/screen at a time.
notes: Implemented first paging conversion slice. Added ConversationPagingSource with cursor/after support, filters, and tests. AllConversationsRepository now exposes getConversationsPaged(), seeds manual list state from Room ConversationDao for cold-start cached conversation rows, and writes loaded/optimistic pages back to Room. AgentRepository.refreshAgents no longer makes a limit=1000 request; it hydrates the compatibility StateFlow cache through AgentPagingSource.PAGE_SIZE offset pages and guards empty Room delete. FakeAgentApi now honors limit/offset/tags. Quality gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.paging.ConversationPagingSourceTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.AgentRepositoryTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.AllConversationsRepositoryTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.dashboard.DashboardViewModelTest'. Remaining legacy limit=1000 admin/detail helpers are still documented in the policy and should be handled incrementally as screens are migrated.
close_reason: Added cursor PagingSource for conversations, Room-backed all-conversation cold-start cache, and removed AgentRepository limit=1000 refresh.

title: Add repository TTL/refresh tests and 1k conversation performance gate
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Repository caching needs explicit stale/refresh semantics and performance proof for 1k+ conversations.

Proposed implementation plan:

Dependencies:

Explicit tests:
Cached-before-network, stale refresh in background, empty response caching, 1k+ fake data performance.

Success gate checklist:

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If macrobenchmark infra is unavailable, add repeatable JVM/perf smoke and file benchmark follow-up.
notes: Implemented TTL/refresh coverage and 1k smoke gates. ConversationRepository tests now cover stale refresh, failed stale refresh preserving cached Room/process data, existing cached-before-network, empty response freshness, and single-flight. AllConversationsRepository refresh now fetches first page before clearing existing state so failed refresh preserves the loaded list; tests cover stale refresh, failed stale refresh preservation, empty freshness, no count fetch, and single-flight. Added ChatSessionResolver 1k cached conversation resolution test and ConversationsViewModel 1k cached conversation load smoke (<2s threshold). Quality gate run: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.ConversationRepositoryTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.AllConversationsRepositoryTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.ChatSessionResolverTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest'.

title: Define repository cache and paging policy
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Repositories mix process-memory MutableStateFlow caches with fetch-all API calls and no clear policy for persisted/cold-start data.

Proposed implementation plan:
Document policy: Room-backed entities for cross-screen/cold-start data, PagingSource for large collections, memory only for ephemeral data. Identify endpoints with limit/offset/cursor support.

Dependencies:
Depends on Room migration safety gates.

Explicit tests:
Policy bead does not require code tests, but should list affected repos and test plan for follow-ups.

Success gate checklist:
- Written policy in bead notes or docs.
- Follow-up beads confirm no new fetch-all patterns.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Policy should be incremental and not block urgent bug fixes.
notes: Policy documented in docs/architecture/repository-cache-paging-policy.md. Audit covers current Room-backed entities, process-memory repositories, endpoint paging/count support, TTL/stale semantics, single-flight expectations, fetch-all guardrails, and follow-up test plan for yoic.3.2/3.3/3.4/3.5.

title: Add ConversationEntity/ConversationDao for cached lists and titles
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
ConversationRepository stores conversations in process memory and chat title hydration depends on network/cache timing.

Proposed implementation plan:
Add Room entity/DAO for conversation list/title cache, migrations, and repository read-through/write-through behavior.

Dependencies:
Depends on repository policy and Room migration safety.

Explicit tests:
DAO migration/entity tests; cold-start emits cached conversations/title before network; empty server response cached correctly.

Success gate checklist:
- Chat can show cached conversation title before network.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep existing memory flow until Room-backed flow is verified.
notes: Implemented ConversationEntity/ConversationDao and Room v5 migration with conversation_refresh_state. ConversationRepository now emits Room-backed flows, seeds process cache from Room, writes through refresh/create/update/archive/delete/fork/get, and treats cached empty refreshes as fresh. Tests added for Room round trip, migration v4->v5, cached-before-network emission, cached empty response freshness, and repository mutation behavior. Quality gates run: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.ConversationRepositoryTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseCharacterizationTest' --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.local.LettaDatabaseMigrationTest'; cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest'.

title: Replace conversation count fetch-all strategy
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
AllConversationsRepository.countConversations fetches limit=10000 just to count.

Proposed implementation plan:
Use API count endpoint when available or approximate/paged metadata. If API lacks support, expose unknown/loading instead of fetching 10k.

Dependencies:
Depends on repository policy.

Explicit tests:
Fake API proves no fetch-all call is made for count; UI handles unknown/loading count.

Success gate checklist:
- No count path uses limit=10000.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Prefer temporarily hiding exact count over high-volume fetch.
notes: Implemented explicit conversation count estimate semantics. AllConversationsRepository now exposes loadedCountEstimate(): ConversationCountEstimate? and countConversations() is a deprecated no-network compatibility shim. Dashboard uses loadedCountEstimate after refresh and handles approximate/unknown state. AllConversations empty refresh is now considered fresh via hasLoadedAtLeastOnce, avoiding repeated empty-list refreshes. Tests prove no count network request/listConversations is made for count, no 1000/10000 limits, approximate dashboard state, and unknown state. Quality gate: cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.repository.AllConversationsRepositoryTest' :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.dashboard.DashboardViewModelTest'.
close_reason: Replaced count fetch path with loaded-page estimate semantics and dashboard approximate/unknown handling tests.

title: Separate API DTOs, domain models, local entities, and UI state
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
core/data/model/Agent.kt is a giant API-shaped model used across UI, repositories, Room mapping, and update payload construction. AgentEntity serializes tags with comma join. AgentUpdateParams mixes high-level UI decisions with wire payload details.

Proposed implementation plan:
Introduce explicit API DTO naming where needed such as AgentResponseDto and AgentUpdateRequestDto, or at least separate wire payloads from domain. Introduce domain AgentSummary/AgentDetail. Replace lossy local serialization with JSON/type converters. Centralize mapping in mapper package. Keep raw JsonElement only at boundaries; UI receives validated text fields or semantic config models.

Dependencies:
Coordinate with Room tags migration and designsystem boundary work. Can proceed incrementally after characterization tests.

Explicit tests:
Serialization contract tests for update/create payload snake_case keys, null omission, booleans, JsonObject fields, compaction/model/response_format, and secrets/env map behavior. Mapper tests for DTO -> domain -> entity -> domain round trip, tags with commas/special chars, unknown fields ignored, missing optional defaults. Existing EditAgentViewModel tests continue passing.

Success gate checklist:
- No additional UI code directly constructs fragile wire JsonObject unless justified.
- API contract tests cover every advanced config field exposed in UI.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Create mappers/adapters first and migrate callers feature by feature. Keep wire serialization tests as rollback safety.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Clean up chat navigation origins and route classification
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Routes.kt AgentChatRoute carries agent, conversation, project, initial message, scroll target, and project metadata. AdaptiveScaffold hardcodes top-level route class lists and has an Admin special-case back-stack hack. Chat can open from Admin/Home/Conversation/Project/Notification with ambiguous parent tab semantics. TwoPaneConversationsLayout uses an AgentChatRoute(agentId=\"\") sentinel.

Proposed implementation plan:
Introduce explicit chat origin/parent metadata or nested graph structure; split project chat route concerns if warranted; move top-level selection into a single tested classifier; avoid brittle popToExistingTopLevel special cases; make two-pane detail nav start destination non-invalid.

Dependencies:
Can follow chat/session extraction and should not block safety/security work.

Explicit tests:
Route serialization tests. Navigation classifier tests for Admin shortcut, Conversation chat, Project chat product decision, and notification deep link behavior. Compose smoke tests for compact and expanded layouts if feasible.

Success gate checklist:
- No blank-agent sentinel route.
- Top-level selection/back behavior deterministic and tested.
- Adding a route requires updating one classifier, not scattered route lists.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Introduce classifier in parallel and switch callers incrementally. Preserve route serialization compatibility for existing deep links where possible.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Improve chat render performance and tool output parsing
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
ChatScreen.kt rebuilds full render model on every state.messages change. Streaming mutates state.messages frequently. ToolOutputRenderer.kt parses/highlights from composables using remember/produceState. Opening multiple large outputs has observed chug.

Proposed implementation plan:
Move render model construction into ViewModel/use-case or memoized incremental builder keyed by message ids/content hashes; avoid rebuilding all grouped items on every token; add ToolOutputCache keyed by raw content hash and expanded/highlight mode; parse/highlight large outputs off main thread before composition; keep composables as dumb normalized-data renderers; preserve max char/line limits.

Dependencies:
Should follow chat/timeline refactor enough that message identity is stable.

Explicit tests:
Pure incremental render model tests for stable keys, tail-only recompute, and stable run blocks. ToolOutputParser tests for JSON, diff, shell transcript, ANSI logs, stack traces, tables, truncation, whitespace stripping. Compose performance/macrobenchmark for 500 messages, 200 stream chunks, and expanding/collapsing 5 large tool outputs. Assert no horizontal scroll/unbounded text layout.

Success gate checklist:
- Measurable reduction in recomposition/frame time for streaming/tool output expansion.
- No regression in compact multi-tool blocks or approval controls.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep old builder behind a test comparison or debug flag until render models match on golden fixtures.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Scale repositories with Room cache, paging, and explicit fetch policy
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
AgentRepository.refreshAgents fetches limit=1000. ConversationRepository stores conversations in process memory and calls listConversations(agentId) without explicit paging. AllConversationsRepository.countConversations fetches limit=10000. Many repositories use MutableStateFlow fetch-all caches.

Proposed implementation plan:
Define repository caching policy; add ConversationEntity/ConversationDao for conversation lists/title caching; replace countConversations limit=10000 with count endpoint or unknown/loading/paged metadata strategy; convert high-volume lists to PagingSource where endpoints support it; add cache TTL and refresh policy tests.

Dependencies:
Starts after critical Room migration infrastructure is safe. Conversation cache work should coordinate with chat session resolver title seeding.

Explicit tests:
DAO migration/entity tests. Repository fake API tests for cached-before-network, stale background refresh, empty response caching, paging limit/cursor/offset, and no fetch-all count. Performance test/benchmark for opening chat/conversation list with 1k+ fake conversations.

Success gate checklist:
- No new limit=1000/limit=10000 fetch-all patterns.
- Chat opening can show cached agent/conversation title before network.
- Conversation list supports pagination and cold-start cached content.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Introduce Room-backed read paths in parallel with current network caches and flip screen callers one repository at a time.
close_reason: Completed repository cache/paging policy, Room conversation cache, count estimate strategy, paging conversion slice, and TTL/1k smoke tests.

title: Refine compact tool-output preview selection
description:
Make collapsed tool-output previews choose the most useful signal for each output type.

Context:
- ToolOutputRenderer currently previews the first parsed block with two max lines.
- Different output types benefit from different preview choices: JSON key summary, diff file/change summary, stack trace headline, shell command/output tail, table header, etc.

Scope:
- Define type-specific preview text for Json, Diff, StackTrace, ShellTranscript, Table, CodeLike, AnsiLog, PlainText.
- Prefer concise, high-signal previews over raw first lines when raw output is noisy.
- Preserve expanded raw/structured output for full inspection.
- Respect existing truncation/performance limits.

Acceptance:
- Collapsed previews are more informative for JSON, diffs, stack traces, and shell output.
- Unit tests cover preview selection for each ToolOutputBlock type.
- No extra expensive parsing on the UI thread for large output.

title: Persist compact tool-row expansion state
description:
Keep expanded/collapsed state for compact tool rows stable across recomposition, scrolling, and streaming updates.

Context:
- CompactToolCallRow currently owns local expanded state keyed by toolCallMotionKey().
- Local composable state may reset when LazyColumn items remount, run grouping changes, or streaming updates replace row keys.

Scope:
- Hoist or save per-tool expansion state using a stable key such as toolCallId plus message/run identity.
- Ensure expanding one row remains stable after result arrival and after final assistant text appears.
- Avoid retaining unbounded state for very old rows if a central store is used.

Acceptance:
- Expanded row remains expanded after result update/recomposition.
- Expanded row remains stable after scrolling away/back in a long chat.
- Tests cover stable expansion key behavior where practical.

title: Polish compact multi-tool row status density
description:
Refine the compact multi-tool batch rows to reduce repeated status chrome and reclaim horizontal space.

Context:
- Current dense multi-tool card is working well: 12 tool calls fit in one compact block with row-level icons, approval pills, checkmarks, and expand chevrons.
- On narrow phone screens, repeated "approved" pills plus success checkmarks consume a lot of width and make result/command previews truncate earlier.

Scope:
- Audit when the approval pill is semantically useful versus redundant.
- Prefer showing "approved" only for approval-gated tool calls where approval state matters to the user.
- For normal completed rows, consider checkmark-only success state.
- Ensure pending approval and rejected/error states remain visually obvious.
- Preserve accessibility semantics even if visual chrome is reduced.

Acceptance:
- Successful non-approval tool rows use less horizontal width than current screenshot.
- Approval-gated rows still communicate approved/rejected/requesting-input clearly.
- Error rows remain visually distinct.
- Existing compact tool group tests updated or extended.

title: Prefer command-first summaries in Bash compact tool rows
description:
Improve compact Bash/tool row previews so the row communicates what ran before showing output.

Context:
- Current compact rows often read like "Bash - Result: ...", which is useful but can hide the command identity when width is tight.
- For multi-tool batches, scanning the command sequence is often more valuable than seeing the first characters of stdout.

Scope:
- For Bash/shell tools, derive a concise command summary from arguments, e.g. pwd, git diff ..., bd list ....
- Keep result/error preview available after the command summary or in expanded details.
- Preserve non-Bash tool-specific summaries such as Read path, Glob pattern, Grep pattern, TodoWrite status.
- Avoid exposing sensitive env/secret values in compact previews.

Acceptance:
- Bash compact rows show the command identity in the visible text on phone width.
- Result preview remains accessible in expanded row details.
- Tests cover Bash command summary, long command truncation, and non-Bash fallback.

title: Optimize chat render model if profiling requires it
description: Optimize buildChatRenderModel only if profiling proves current rebuild cost is material. Success gates: preserve existing grouping/run-block behavior and LazyColumn keys; add or update tests for duplicate key handling, batched tool calls, and latency assignment; avoid broad refactors unless profiling shows the current O(n) rebuild is a real bottleneck.

title: UX polish: Navigation + state restoration
description:
## Goal
Make navigation predictable and resilient to lifecycle events.

## Checks
- Back behavior consistent (close sheet > back stack > exit)
- Deep links / notification taps land correctly
- Process death restores current conversation + draft
- Rotation doesn't lose scroll position or input
- Multi-pane/two-pane layouts keep selection stable

## Done when
- Verified via 'Don't keep activities' dev option + rotation tests

title: UX polish: Accessibility + dynamic type (text scaling)
description:
## Goal
Ensure the UI remains usable with accessibility features enabled.

## Checks
- Supports large font sizes without clipping/truncation
- Tap targets meet minimum size
- TalkBack labels for key controls (send, attach, retry, navigation)
- Contrast checks for text + disabled states
- Focus order makes sense in chat + settings

## Done when
- Verified with font scale 1.3–2.0 and TalkBack enabled

title: UX polish: Error/empty states with actionable recovery
description:
## Goal
Replace dead ends with helpful, actionable UI.

## Targets
- Network failures (timeouts, offline)
- Auth failures
- Empty screens (no conversations, no agents, no tools)
- Failed message send

## Checks
- One-tap retry where possible
- Clear copy + secondary diagnostic detail
- Non-blocking banners vs modal dialogs (when appropriate)

## Done when
- No screen leaves user stuck without a next step

title: UX polish: Composer/IME interactions
description:
## Goal
Make typing + attachments feel frictionless.

## Checks
- Multiline input grows sensibly
- Send button enabled state obvious
- Attachments tray doesn't fight IME
- Back button closes trays before navigating away
- Draft persists when switching conversations

## Done when
- No layout jump when keyboard opens/closes
- Attachment + emoji flows feel consistent

title: UX polish: Chat scroll + jump-to-bottom behavior
description:
## Goal
Ensure chat scrolling feels stable during streaming and new messages.

## Checks
- Don't auto-scroll if user is reading history
- Show 'Jump to bottom' affordance when scrolled up
- Keep scroll position stable while tokens stream
- Preserve position across rotations/process death

## Done when
- Manual QA passes across long threads
- No visible scroll jitter during streaming

title: Add repository coverage for model passage and bug report flows
description: Why: ModelRepository, PassageRepository, and BugReportRepository have no dedicated regression tests, leaving admin and archival-memory refactors dependent on manual QA. What: add repository tests using existing fake API patterns for request construction, pagination/listing, success paths, and API failure propagation.
acceptance_criteria: ModelRepository, PassageRepository, and BugReportRepository have dedicated tests; tests cover success and failure paths; tests run under :core:testDebugUnitTest.

title: Add ClientToolRegistry and AdminAgentManager tests
description: Why: domain-layer refactors are not fearless while ClientToolRegistry and AdminAgentManager have no dedicated tests despite dispatch/administration branching. What: add unit tests around registration/lookup/dispatch behavior and admin agent operations using existing domain test conventions.
acceptance_criteria: ClientToolRegistry and AdminAgentManager have focused tests; edge cases and failure paths are covered; tests run under :core:testDebugUnitTest.

title: Make streaming markdown partitioning incremental
description:
Static performance audit found StreamingMarkdownText ticks every PAINT_INTERVAL_MS and repartitions displayed text. partitionStreamingMarkdown scans/substrings the full text, and splitMarkdownBlocks also processes the text. Long streamed replies can become O(n) per paint tick despite the existing thoughtful throttling.

## Context
Files: android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/StreamingMarkdownText.kt around lines 130, 191, 611, and 1030.
acceptance_criteria: Streaming markdown keeps committed block state and scans only newly appended tail where possible. Long streaming replies avoid full-text repartitioning every paint tick. Add tests for incremental block boundaries and unchanged rendering behavior.

title: Run device macrobenchmark and Perfetto validation for static perf findings
description:
The static Android performance audit was code-level only and did not include emulator/device profiling. The repo already has macrobenchmarks, baseline profile wiring, JankStats sampling, and prior chat-rendering work. Validate the highest-risk findings with real traces before/after fixes.

## Context
Run :macrobenchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest on a real device for dashboard startup and long chat scroll. Capture Perfetto/JankStats traces where possible and connect measurements to the dashboard overfetch, timeline loop retention, image decode, streaming markdown, targeted fetch, and agent refresh beads.
acceptance_criteria: Real-device benchmark results are recorded for dashboard startup and long chat scroll; trace evidence identifies whether each static finding is measurable; follow-up beads are updated with measurement notes or reprioritized.

title: Optimize targeted message fetch for deep history
description:
Static performance audit found MessageRepository targeted fetch can scan up to 20 pages of 100 messages to find a target. It also repeatedly copies accumulated messages via mergedMessages = mergedMessages + page, which grows increasingly expensive.

## Context
File: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt around lines 124 and 145.
acceptance_criteria: Use a mutable accumulator at minimum; investigate server-side target/around-message endpoint or bounded approximate-page jump flow. Add tests for deep-history target fetch behavior and ensure list-copy churn is removed.

title: Page agent refreshes and reduce cache churn
description:
Static performance audit found AgentRepository refreshAgents calls listAgents(limit = 1000), then inserts all returned agents and deletes everything not in the returned IDs. This creates a scale cap and can cause large Room cache churn via broad NOT IN deletes.

## Context
File: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AgentRepository.kt around lines 67 and 74.
acceptance_criteria: Agent refresh pages all agents or otherwise removes the 1000-agent cap; cache replacement happens in a Room transaction; avoid large broad NOT IN churn when possible; add tests for pagination and stale-agent removal semantics.

title: Make total Kover report avoid benchmark/release unit-test compilation failures
description:
The custom RootDebug Kover coverage task passes, but the default aggregate total task (./gradlew koverHtmlReport) still compiles all app variants and fails at :app:compilePlayBenchmarkUnitTestKotlin because AutomationAuthBootstrapTest references debug-only automation constants that are unavailable in playBenchmark. Decide whether to exclude benchmark/release unit-test variants from total Kover, move debug-only tests under a variant-specific source set, or standardize on the custom RootDebug coverage task.

## Context
Observed after enabling Kover. Passing command: ./gradlew koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage. Failing command: ./gradlew koverHtmlReport. Failing file: android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/debug/AutomationAuthBootstrapTest.kt
acceptance_criteria: Either ./gradlew koverHtmlReport passes, or docs/build scripts explicitly route coverage baseline generation to koverHtmlReportRootDebugCoverage and avoid the total all-variants task.

title: Fix ChannelHeartbeatSync test fixture so RootDebug and PlayDebug suites pass
description: ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest failed because mockk could not intercept SettingsRepository.activeConfig property mocking. ChannelHeartbeatSync now reads the existing getActiveConfig() flow instead of the raw StateFlow property, preserving behavior while making the dependency mockable. RootDebug and PlayDebug app unit test suites pass after this fix.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :app:testRootDebugUnitTest and ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest both pass

title: Improve designsystem test coverage (currently ~22% — 11 tests for 51 files)
description:
The designsystem module has the weakest test coverage ratio at ~22% (11 test files for 51 production files). Areas most in need: UI components (StreamingMarkdown*, ActionSheet, ConfirmDialog, TextInputDialog, FormItem, CardGroup, EmptyState, ErrorContent), theme tokens (TypeHierarchy, CustomColors, Typography extensions), icon system, and preview/util components. Priority: test coverage for ActionSheet, ConfirmDialog, and CardGroup — these are the primary migration targets for old-style screen modernization per the design-system checklist.

## Context
Discovered during test coverage inventory. designsystem is the refactoring risk ceiling — most UI modernization work touches these components. Referenced from design-system migration checklist in AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria: designsystem module test count increases by at least 50% (to 17+ test files). Key reusable components (ActionSheet, ConfirmDialog, CardGroup) have dedicated tests.
close_reason: Added five designsystem contract test files, increasing module tests from 12 to 17 and covering ActionSheet, ConfirmDialog, CardGroup, EmptyState, and TypeHierarchy contracts. Verified with ./gradlew :designsystem:testDebugUnitTest --no-daemon --stacktrace.

title: Add Kover code-coverage measurement to multi-module Android build
description:
Root build.gradle.kts now applies kotlinx-kover 0.9.1 as the merging module. Submodules (app, core, designsystem, bot) inherit the plugin. Aggregate HTML + XML reports can be generated via ./gradlew koverHtmlReport / koverXmlReport. Filters exclude Hilt/Dagger generated code, databinding, and Compose singletons. Also fixed pre-existing bot test compilation (RemoteBotSessionTest.kt fakes missing abort() method).

## Context
Related: test coverage analysis identified zero coverage tooling. Kover chosen over JaCoCo for native Kotlin/Gradle integration. Report output: build/reports/kover/html/index.html and build/reports/kover/report.xml
design: Multi-module Android pattern: root module as merging module with kover(project(...)) dependencies. Filters exclude generated DI/Compose code.
acceptance_criteria: koverHtmlReport runs without Gradle configuration errors and produces an HTML report at build/reports/kover/html/index.html

title: Characterize AgentScaffold project context card rendering
description:
## Problem
AgentScaffold renders project-specific cards and route-specific context above or around chat content. The planned shell refactor intends to extract project header/cards and route-specific composition, but current rendering behavior is not directly covered.

## Test target
Project context rendering branches in AgentScaffold.kt, including project header/cards and bug-report/project brief surfaces where feasible.

## Behavior to cover
- When no project context is present, project-specific cards/headers are not shown.
- When project context is present, the expected project title/context affordances render.
- Project agents, brief, or bug summary cards render their current empty/loading/content states if these states are passed through AgentScaffold directly.
- Basic expand/collapse or click behavior is covered for any project card that owns local UI state inside the scaffold.

## Implementation notes
- Keep this as characterization, not redesign: assert current visible contract before extraction.
- If card internals are too broad, prioritize shell-level presence/absence and callback wiring first.
- Use Material/design-system test patterns; avoid raw timing assumptions.

## Acceptance
- Tests fail if project-context UI disappears when a project-backed chat is rendered.
- Tests fail if project-specific UI appears in normal non-project chat.
- Focused test task passes.
close_reason: ProjectContextCard tested for name/identifier rendering, expand/collapse, detail lines, and missing-value fallback. ProjectAgentsCard tested for loading, empty, error, and agent-entry states. ProjectBriefCard tested for loading, empty, and sections display. ProjectBugReportSummaryCard tested for title visibility. All 12 tests pass.

title: Gate Telemetry Logcat output separately from metrics collection
description: Telemetry currently couples metrics emission to Logcat logging. Metrics should still collect consistently when Logcat mirroring is disabled, especially outside debug builds.
close_reason: Logcat mirroring now has a separate debug-default gate and metrics collection remains covered by a focused unit test.

title: Replace ScheduleListScreen mode Switch with mutually-exclusive selector
description: PR #35 review noted ScheduleListScreen uses a Switch for one-time vs recurring schedule mode. Material3 segmented buttons are unavailable in the current 1.5.0-alpha17 dependency, so follow up with an in-repo mutually-exclusive pattern (for example two FilterChips or a reusable selector) without a version bump unless needed.

title: Migrate mixed JUnit4/Jupiter tagged tests to reliable unit tag filtering
description: PR #35 review found multiple tests mix org.junit.Test with org.junit.jupiter.api.Tag, which Vintage does not expose to includeTags("unit"). Batch migrate affected tests to Jupiter or introduce an equivalent Gradle/category strategy so tag-filtered unit tasks include them reliably.

title: Route ChatPushService timeline ingestion through notification coordinator
description: Refactor ChatPushService's IngestedMessageListener to submit notification candidates to NotificationDeliveryCoordinator instead of directly invoking ChannelNotificationPublisher.
design: Timeline ingestion remains the preferred push source for persisted server messages. The coordinator handles agent lookup fallback, suppression, dedupe, and publisher invocation consistently with websocket and heartbeat sources.
acceptance_criteria: ChatPushService no longer directly publishes channel notifications from IngestedMessageListener; timeline-ingested messages use the same dedupe/suppression path as websocket and heartbeat.
close_reason: Centralized notification delivery through NotificationDeliveryCoordinator: websocket Client Mode, ChatPush timeline ingestion, heartbeat fallback, and notification replies now submit candidates to the coordinator; coordinator owns foreground/reply-stream suppression and shared dedupe state. Added regression coverage for websocket final-only publish, visible conversation suppression, reply-stream delivery, heartbeat dedupe, and first-sync baseline. Verified focused notification tests, full AdminChatViewModelTest, and :app:assembleRootDebug.

title: Add notification delivery telemetry and diagnostics
description: Add structured telemetry/logging around notification candidate ingestion, suppression decisions, dedupe decisions, publish events, and heartbeat fallback decisions.
design: Telemetry should identify source transport, conversationId, messageId/runId, decision, reason, and whether preview was partial/final without logging sensitive full content.
acceptance_criteria: Logs make it possible to diagnose why a websocket notification did or did not publish; sensitive message content is not emitted.
close_reason: Finished dirty telemetry follow-up in e9da09a8. Timeline stream ingestion now emits listenerDispatch diagnostics with conversation/server/message type, listener presence, and redacted preview length; existing NotificationDeliveryCoordinator telemetry covers published/deferred/suppressed decisions and publisher telemetry covers publish outcomes. Quality gates passed: :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.data.timeline.TimelineSyncLoopTest.stream subscriber resolves ingested listener dynamically'; :app:testRootDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.letta.mobile.channel.NotificationDeliveryCoordinatorTest'; :app:compileRootDebugKotlin.

title: Notifications broken after stream_event fix (letta-mobile-mmnn)
description:
## Symptom

Notifications no longer fire after the streaming fix in commit 3381c75.

## Root Cause

**lettabot commit `3381c75`** (fix: enable token-level stream_event deltas for smooth streaming) changed two things:
1. `agent-session-manager.ts`: Set `includePartialMessages: true` in SDK session defaults
2. `ws-gateway.ts`: Handle `stream_event` messages from the SDK, routing per-token text deltas through the no-reply guard

This causes the Letta **server** (via the SDK) to emit `stream_event` SSE frames alongside the final `assistant_message`. For Client Mode / Mobile, these arrive via the SSE subscriber in `TimelineSyncLoop.runStreamSubscriber()`.

**Letta Mobile's SSE parser (`SseParser.kt`)** does not recognize the `stream_event` message type. It falls through to the deserializer's fallback, which maps to `UnknownMessage`.

In `TimelineSyncLoop.ingestStreamEvent()`, `UnknownMessage` returns `null` from `toTimelineEvent()` → no notification fires.

The final `assistant_message` still arrives correctly, but the notification logic has already been bypassed.

## Files Affected

- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt` — needs a `stream_event` case in `LettaMessageSerializer`
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/stream/SseParser.kt` — needs to parse `stream_event` frames (or treat as heartbeat if content is incremental)
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt` — `ingestStreamEvent()` needs to handle `stream_event` (buffer and wait for the complete `assistant_message`, then fire notification once)

## Notes

The `stream_event` frames are incremental text deltas (the same data now being used for smooth streaming in the WS gateway). The final `assistant_message` still arrives with the complete text. Notification should fire on the complete message, not the incremental frames.

`lettabot-mmnn` is the upstream lettabot bead.

title: Write tests to catch notification-reply streaming mishandling (regression coverage)
description:
## Scope
Write unit tests in `AdminChatViewModelTest.kt` that catch and prevent the following regressions:

### Test 1: Notification NOT published when user is viewing conversation
- Set CurrentConversationTracker to conversationId
- Start Client Mode stream via sendMessage
- Send text chunks through to done
- Assert: `channelNotificationPublisher.publish()` is never called

### Test 2: Notification IS published when user leaves conversation
- Set CurrentConversationTracker to null
- Start Client Mode stream
- Send a text chunk
- Assert: `channelNotificationPublisher.publish()` IS called once

### Test 3: isReplyStreaming keeps isStreaming=true during handler stream (flicker regression)
- Create mock notificationReplyHandler with activeReplyStreams containing convId
- Don't start VM stream (streamInFlight=false, prev.isStreaming=false)
- Emit timeline events via observer
- Assert: `uiState.isStreaming == true`, `uiState.isAgentTyping == true`

### Test 4: isAgentTyping stays true during handler stream (flicker regression)
- Same setup as Test 3
- Assert: `uiState.isAgentTyping == true` during handler streaming

### Test 5: Server Confirmed event arriving BEFORE Local does NOT create a duplicate
- Seed timeline with a Confirmed ASSISTANT event (simulating SSE arrived first)
- Then insert a Local CLIENT_MODE_HARNESS ASSISTANT event with compatible content
- Assert: timeline contains only one ASSISTANT message (Local collapsed into Confirmed, or Confirmed replaced by Local)

### Test 6: Server Confirmed event arriving AFTER Local is collapsed (existing path)
- Insert Local CLIENT_MODE_HARNESS ASSISTANT event first
- Then insert Confirmed ASSISTANT event with compatible content
- Assert: fuzzy collapse runs, only one message in UI

## Prerequisite
- Fix `letta-mobile-exrw` (compile error: missing notificationReplyHandler in createViewModel())
- Fix `letta-mobile-vf7i` (flicker regression: isReplyStreaming logic)
- Fix `letta-mobile-iuh6` (SSE/handler race condition)

## Files
- `app/src/test/.../AdminChatViewModelTest.kt`

## See Also
- `letta-mobile-9bs1` — notification-during-viewing bug (Tests 1-2)
- `letta-mobile-vf7i` — flicker regression (Tests 3-4)
- `letta-mobile-iuh6` — SSE/handler race condition (Tests 5-6)

title: Double onScreenResumed lifecycle calls in AgentScaffold DisposableEffect
description:
## Log Evidence
```
11:13:01.255 onScreenPaused clearing tracker prev=conv-...
11:13:01.257 onScreenResumed restoring tracker to convId=null  ← call 1
11:13:01.330 onScreenResumed restoring tracker to convId=null  ← call 2 (73ms later)
```

## Root Cause
`DisposableEffect` in AgentScaffold adds a lifecycle observer. Either:
1. Multiple recompositions cause `DisposableEffect` to re-trigger
2. Lifecycle emits duplicate ON_RESUME events

## Fix
Add a guard — debounce or check if already at the desired state before acting.

## Files
- `app/src/main/.../AgentScaffold.kt` (DisposableEffect, ~line 138)

title: Add tests for untested ViewModels and bot channel/context
description:
## Problem
3 ViewModels have no corresponding tests. Bot channel/context management has 0% coverage.

## Items

### Untested ViewModels (add tests)

title: Update deprecated API usage and remove @Suppress(DEPRECATION) annotations

title: Adopt CardGroup and FormItem in admin/detail screens
description:
## Problem
Several admin/detail screens use flat `Column` layouts for detail sections instead of `CardGroup` for grouped content. Some multi-field forms use raw `Column` + `Text` + control instead of `FormItem`.

## CardGroup migration targets

|--------|---------|--------|
| FolderAdminScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for folder details |
| ProviderAdminScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for provider details |

| ArchiveAdminScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for archive details |
| McpServerToolsScreen | Column with tool card list | CardGroup sections for tool details |
| ToolDetailScreen | Partial CardGroup usage | Complete CardGroup adoption |
| RunMonitorScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for run details |
| JobMonitorScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for job details |
| MessageBatchMonitorScreen | Column with Text+control rows | CardGroup sections for batch details |

## FormItem migration targets
| Screen | Current | Target |

title: Replace inline edit/delete icon rows with ActionSheet in admin/detail screens
description:
## Problem
Admin/detail screens use inline edit/delete icon rows (e.g., Row with pencil and trash IconButtons) for contextual actions. Per AGENTS.md rule 10, these should use `ActionSheet` for consistency.

## Screens needing ActionSheet migration
| Screen | Current pattern | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| FolderAdminScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Each folder card has edit/delete row |
| ProviderAdminScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Each provider card has edit/delete row |
| IdentityListScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Identity cards with actions |
| GroupAdminScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Group cards with actions |
| ArchiveAdminScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Archive cards with actions |
| McpServerToolsScreen | Inline edit/delete icons | Tool cards with actions |
| ToolDetailScreen | Already has ActionSheet | Reference implementation |
| RunMonitorScreen | Mixed patterns | Run card actions |
| JobMonitorScreen | Mixed patterns | Job card actions |
| MessageBatchMonitorScreen | Mixed patterns | Batch card actions |

## Pattern
Replace inline icon row with overflow menu (three-dot icon) that opens ActionSheet:
```
IconButton(onClick = { showActionSheet = true }) {
    Icon(LettaIcons.MoreVert, ...)
}
// Then:
if (showActionSheet) {
    ActionSheet(onDismiss = { showActionSheet = false }) {
        ActionSheetItem(icon = LettaIcons.Edit, label = "Edit", onClick = ...)
        ActionSheetItem(icon = LettaIcons.Trash, label = "Delete", onClick = ..., isDestructive = true)
    }
}
```

## Reference implementations
- AgentListScreen (agent card overflow → ActionSheet)
- ProjectHomeScreen (project card overflow → ActionSheet)
- ConversationsScreen (conversation card overflow → ActionSheet)

## Acceptance
- No inline edit/delete icon rows on admin card items in screens listed above
- ActionSheet used for all contextual actions
- `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin` passes

title: Migrate DEBUG logging to Telemetry system
description:
## Problem
7 files contain 30+ `Log.d(TAG, ...)` or `android.util.Log.d(...)` statements using ad-hoc tags. The project already has a `Telemetry` utility in core that provides structured logging.

## Files needing cleanup
1. **AdminChatViewModel.kt** — 13+ `Log.d("AdminChatVM-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 1616,1644,1767,1773,1887,2038,2060,2683,2690,2765,2785,2799,2812)
2. **ChatRenderModelBuilder.kt** — 4 `Log.d("ChatRenderModel-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 75,85,98,180)
3. **StreamingMarkdownText.kt** — 4 `Log.d("StreamingMd-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 170,179,180,187)
4. **ChatMessageComponents.kt** — 2 `Log.d("ChatMsgItem-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 130,132)
5. **MessageContentFactory.kt** — 2 `Log.d("StreamingDisplay-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 156,163)
6. **ChatMessageList.kt** — 2 `Log.d("ItemKey-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 319,325)
7. **RunBlock.kt** — 2 `Log.d("RunBlock-DEBUG", ...)` (lines 85,87)

## Fix
- Replace Log.d with Telemetry.debug/info/warn as appropriate
- Remove debug statements that are stale/no longer useful
- Keep only statements that provide actionable information during development
- Gate remaining debug output behind build variant (debug-only)

## Acceptance
- No `Log.d` in production chat path
- `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin :bot:compileDebugKotlin :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin` passes
close_reason: All 7 listed files have zero Log.d remaining — cleaned by streaming refactoring work (letta-mobile-flk2). Remaining Log.d in core (SseParser, TimelineSyncLoop, LettaApiClient, AdminAgentManager) and bot (WsBotClient) are already proper Telemetry or intentional.

title: Add streamingDisplayText content-level duplication probe
description:
## Problem

The streamingDisplayText function (MessageContentFactory.kt:103-148) applies:
1. clampToStableMarkdown — holds back trailing partial markdown constructs
2. STREAMING_CURSOR (▎) — appends at held-back boundary
3. Code fence detection — skips clamping inside open fences

When tool calls interleave with assistant chunks during multi-tool-call streams, the raw text arriving at this function may be fragmented differently than single-tool-call streams. The clamping could produce different results on successive calls with the same underlying content, or hold back text that later gets rendered twice.

The insideOpenCodeFence check (line 141) could also change mid-stream as the assistant text accumulates past a closing fence, changing the clamping behavior abruptly.

## What

Add DEBUG-gated telemetry at the entry and exit of streamingDisplayText:

```kotlin
internal fun streamingDisplayText(raw: String): String {
    if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
        val inFence = insideOpenCodeFence(raw)
        val clamped = clampToStableMarkdown(raw)
        val clampedLen = clamped.length
        val heldBack = raw.length - clampedLen
        Telemetry.event(StreamingDisplay, clamp,
            rawLen to raw.length,
            clampedLen to clampedLen,
            heldBack to heldBack,
            insideFence to inFence,
            heldBackEndsMidToken to (heldBack > 0 && heldBack < 40),
        )
    }
    // ... existing logic unchanged
}
```

Also log when insideOpenCodeFence status changes during a single stream — from true→false or false→true indicates the text just crossed a fence boundary and the clamping behavior will change.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Logs capture rawLen, clampedLen, heldBack, insideFence on every call
- [ ] Fence boundary transitions are flagged (insideFence changed since last call)
- [ ] heldBack > 0 during active streaming, heldBack → 0 when stream ends (verify clamping releases)
- [ ] No heldBack oscillation (same raw content should produce same clamped content)
- [ ] All code gated behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG`

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/MessageContentFactory.kt` (lines ~103-148)

title: Add haptic feedback to prompt send action
description:
## Context

The app already uses Jetpack Compose haptic feedback consistently across 6 screens (LongPress only), but the chat prompt send action has no haptic feedback at all. Adding a confirmation haptic on send gives the user tactile acknowledgment that their message was dispatched — a standard mobile UX pattern.

## What exists today

- **Haptic infrastructure**:  +  used in BlockLibraryScreen, AgentListScreen, ConversationsScreen, AgentScaffold, HomeScreen, ProjectHomeScreen — all LongPress only.
- **No VIBRATE permission needed**: Compose's `performHapticFeedback()` works through the framework without manifest permissions.
- **Available but unused types**: `Confirm`, `Reject`, `TextHandleMove`, `Tick`. `Confirm` is the semantic match for a send action.

## Send action integration points

Both live in `designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/LettaInputBar.kt`:

1. **Button click** — `FilledIconButton.onClick` (line 128): triggers `onSend(text)` when `canSend` is true
2. **Keyboard IME send** — `KeyboardActions.onSend` (line 119): triggers `onSend(text)` when `canSend` is true

A single `val haptic = LocalHapticFeedback.current` at the top of the composable body covers both paths.

## Scope

### In scope
- Add `HapticFeedbackType.Confirm` haptic to both send triggers in `LettaInputBar` (button click + keyboard IME)
- Add the same haptic to the steering message send button in `ChatComposer.kt` (line 97, `FilledTonalButton` onClick)

### Out of scope
- No new utility wrappers (the inline pattern is consistent with the rest of the codebase)
- No `VIBRATE` permission (not needed for Compose haptics)
- No custom `Vibrator`/`VibrationEffect` API usage
- No changes to stop button haptics (separate concern)

## Implementation steps

1. In `LettaInputBar.kt`:
   - Add imports: `HapticFeedbackType`, `LocalHapticFeedback`
   - Add `val haptic = LocalHapticFeedback.current` inside the composable body
   - In `FilledIconButton onClick` (line 128): add `haptic.performHapticFeedback(HapticFeedbackType.Confirm)` before `onSend(text)`
   - In `KeyboardActions.onSend` (line 119): add `haptic.performHapticFeedback(HapticFeedbackType.Confirm)` before `onSend(text)`

2. In `ChatComposer.kt`:
   - Add imports: `HapticFeedbackType`, `LocalHapticFeedback`
   - Add `val haptic = LocalHapticFeedback.current` inside the composable body
   - In steering `FilledTonalButton onClick` (line 97): add haptic confirm before `onSend(inputText)`

3. Build verification:
   - `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin` passes
   - `./gradlew :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin` passes (LettaInputBar is in designsystem)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Tapping the send button produces a Confirm haptic
- [ ] Pressing IME Send (keyboard Return) produces the same Confirm haptic
- [ ] Tapping the steering message send button during streaming produces a Confirm haptic
- [ ] Haptic only fires when `canSend` is true (no haptic on disabled/empty)
- [ ] No new permissions added
- [ ] Build compiles cleanly across all distribution flavors
design: Use HapticFeedbackType.Confirm (not LongPress) because it semantically represents a successful user-initiated action. This matches Android haptics guidance: LongPress is for gesture feedback, Confirm is for action completion. Inline pattern with LocalHapticFeedback follows the established convention in all 6 existing haptic-using screens.
acceptance_criteria: Send button produces Confirm haptic; IME send produces Confirm haptic; steering send produces Confirm haptic; haptic only fires when canSend is true; no new permissions; build compiles cleanly

title: Reasoning trace not shown when returning to chat from history
description:
## Summary
When a user returns to a chat after reloading the app, the agent's reasoning/chain-of-thought trace is not displayed. Only the final assistant response is visible — the collapsible "Show thinking" section is missing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Send a message that triggers an agent response with reasoning
2. Wait for the reasoning to complete and appear as a collapsible section
3. Close the app or navigate away
4. Return to the chat (reload from Letta server history)
5. Observe: only the final assistant text appears; no reasoning trace

## Expected Behavior
The reasoning trace should persist and be shown the same way it appears during a live session — as a collapsible "Show thinking" section that can be expanded to reveal the agent's reasoning.

## Root Cause (suspected)
In `AdminChatViewModel`, when hydrating messages from server history, `expandedReasoningMessageIds` is initialized as an empty set. This means all reasoning messages default to `collapsed = true` when loaded.

During live streaming, the reasoning state is maintained via the `onToggleReasoning` callback and `expandedReasoningMessageIds` state. But on initial load from history, there's no logic to pre-expand any reasoning messages.

## Affected Code
- `AdminChatViewModel` — initialization of `expandedReasoningMessageIds` set
- `ChatUiState.expandedReasoningMessageIds` — the state that controls collapse/expand
- `ChatMessageItem` composable — receives `reasoningCollapsed` prop
- `RenderChatMessage` — passes `reasoningCollapsed = message.id !in state.expandedReasoningMessageIds`

## Notes
- The `isReasoning` flag is correctly propagated through the mapper pipeline (`TimelineEventToUiMessage`, `UiMessage`)
- The `MessageReasoning` component in `ChatMessageComponents.kt` correctly renders the collapsible UI when `isReasoning = true`
- The issue is specifically that reasoning messages start collapsed with no user interaction to expand them

## Suggested Fix Options
1. Pre-expand the last N reasoning messages (configurable)
2. Add a user preference to default reasoning to expanded
3. Detect reasoning messages on history load and pre-expand them
4. Store expansion state in local preferences to restore on reload

title: Mermaid fullscreen SVG renders poorly
description: Mermaid diagram looks good inline but pixelated in fullscreen dialog. Root causes: 1) No size hints to SvgDecoder - renders at native resolution without knowing fullscreen dimensions. 2) fillMaxSize in fullscreen (400-1000dp) vs fillMaxWidth in message bubble (300-400dp) - large upscaling reveals artifacts. 3) graphicsLayer zoom (1x-4x) applies GPU scaling on decoded VectorDrawable which can introduce artifacts. Potential fixes: Add size constraints to ImageRequest, pre-scale SVG to target dimensions, or isolate graphicsLayer as the artifact source.
notes:
INVESTIGATION FINDINGS:

1. ImageRequest difference: Normal view uses fillMaxWidth() + defaultMinSize(120dp), fullscreen uses fillMaxSize() which renders at 400-1000dp vs 300-400dp

2. No explicit size constraints on ImageRequest - SvgDecoder doesn't know target display size

3. graphicsLayer applies zoom (1x-4x) via GPU transforms on already-decoded VectorDrawable

4. ContentScale.Fit is identical in both - but at vastly different container sizes

COMPARISON:
- Normal: Modifier.fillMaxWidth().defaultMinSize(minHeight=120.dp)
- Fullscreen: Modifier.fillMaxSize().graphicsLayer(scaleX=zoom, ...)

POTENTIAL FIXES TO INVESTIGATE:
1. Add .size(Size.ORIGINAL) or explicit pixel constraints to ImageRequest
2. Pre-scale SVG to target fullscreen dimensions before passing to decoder
3. Temporarily disable graphicsLayer to isolate if zoom is the artifact source
4. Check memory pressure on complex SVGs with Android Profiler

title: Notification taps navigate to wrong conversation
description:
Summary: When tapping a notification, the app navigates to the most recent conversation instead of the relevant conversation/agent.

Root Cause: ChatPushService.kt line 211 passes an empty agentId to the notification. When the notification is tapped, NotificationNavigationTarget.fromIntent() rejects empty agentId and returns null, causing the app to fall back to default navigation (HomeRoute) which shows the most recent conversation.

title: Fix stale gateway session: 'Send session_start first' error in client mode
description:
## Problem

When responding to existing conversations in client mode, the user bubble appears but the thinking indicator never shows and no response is received.

## Root Cause (confirmed from device logs)

WsBotClient.ensureSession() thinks the session is active (socket open, matching conversation IDs), but the gateway has lost its session state (expired/GC'd). No session_start is sent, the message goes out on a stale session, gateway rejects with 'Send session_start first'.

The entire send-exception lifecycle is ~60ms (within a single Compose frame), so the thinking indicator never renders.

## Key Files
- WsBotClient.kt:281 — exception thrown
- WsBotClient.kt:419-422 — needsNewSession check (too trusting)
- WsBotClient.kt:548-575 — sendClientMessageWithRetry
- AdminChatViewModel.kt:1534+ — sendMessageViaClientMode

## Fix Approaches Under Investigation
1. Recover in retry logic — treat error as recoverable, reconnect + resend
2. Proactive validation — re-send session_start when socket idle beyond threshold

## Open Question
- Does the gateway have dedup logic? A naive retry could cause duplicate messages.
- The user bubble is already appended before the send, so retrying the WS message needs care.
design:
## Fix: NO_SESSION recovery in WsBotClient.streamMessage

### Error flow:
Gateway sends { type: 'error', code: 'NO_SESSION', message: 'Send session_start first', request_id: ... }
→ WsBotClient.handleIncoming routes to routeInbound → channel delivers to streamMessage receive loop
→ BotGatewayException(code=NO_SESSION) thrown at line 281

### Fix in WsBotClient.kt:
In the streamMessage() receive loop, catch NO_SESSION specifically:
1. Remove current route from activeRoutes/pendingRoutes
2. Under connectionMutex: close socket, reconnect, initializeSessionLocked with same agent+conversationId
3. Create new route with fresh requestId
4. Re-register route in activeRoutes
5. Send new WsClientMessage with new requestId
6. Continue the receive loop with the new channel

### NOT changing:
- AdminChatViewModel — retry is transparent below the ViewModel
- No new protocol messages needed — session_start resume is already supported
- No dedup concerns — gateway rejected the original, message never processed
notes:
## Dedup analysis complete

### Key findings:
1. sendClientMessageWithRetry (line 548) only retries on STREAM_ERROR (socket failure), NOT gateway logical errors
2. 'Send session_start first' arrives as WsErrorMessage via routeInbound — thrown at line 280 in the receive loop, NOT caught by sendClientMessageWithRetry
3. No dedup on retry: appendClientModeLocal generates unique cm-uuid each call, so full send retry would duplicate bubbles
4. Fuzzy collapse has 10s window — retry beyond that orphans the old Local
5. Gateway has NO dedup protection — even otid uniqueness is NOT enforced server-side (confirmed in letta-api-behaviors.md)
6. request_id is client-side routing only, not an idempotency key

### Proposed fix:
- Catch the gateway session error in the streamMessage receive loop (WsBotClient.kt ~line 280)
- On catch: close socket → reconnect → session_start → retry ONLY the WS chat_message
- Generate NEW request_id on retry (not reuse old one) to avoid route confusion
- The user bubble stays in place — no appendClientModeLocal re-call — no duplication risk
- Fuzzy collapse on server echo handles the original Local bubble correctly

### Duplication risk analysis:
- User bubble: NOT duplicated (append only happens once at AdminChatVM line 1686)
- Agent message: NOT duplicated (gateway rejected the first send, message never reached the agent)
- On retry with new requestId: clean route, no stale state

### Open question (minor):
- Need to determine what error CODE the gateway sends for 'Send session_start first'. Could be NO_SESSION or an unknown code that falls back to STREAM_ERROR.
- Match strategy: either (a) check for NO_SESSION code, or (b) check message string for session-related keywords. Option (b) is more robust against gateway code changes.
- Can verify by adding temporary debug logging in WsErrorMessage handler (line 594) to log message.code before toGatewayErrorCode() maps it.

### Gateway error code CONFIRMED
- Gateway sends code='NO_SESSION' with message='Send session_start first' (ws-gateway.ts:634)
- This maps cleanly to BotGatewayErrorCode.NO_SESSION in mobile (valueOf('NO_SESSION') succeeds)
- NO fallback to STREAM_ERROR — the enum match is exact

### Session eviction cause CONFIRMED
- Gateway runs idle sweep every 60s (agent-session-manager.ts:339)
- Sessions idle >5min are evicted (DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 300000)
- Sweep never evicts busy sessions (line 1157)
- Mobile ping (20s interval) does NOT update lastActivity — only message send does
- So after 5min without a message, the gateway evicts the session while the socket stays open

### Fix is straightforward
1. In streamMessage receive loop (~line 280), catch BotGatewayException with code==NO_SESSION
2. Close socket → reconnect → send session_start with same conversation_id → retry message
3. Generate new requestId on retry
4. No bubble duplication risk — appendClientModeLocal not re-called
5. No agent duplication risk — gateway rejected the original message, it never reached the agent

### Handoff created: .sisyphus/handoff.md
- Complete implementation plan with exact line numbers
- Architecture context and gateway contract details
- Verification steps

title: Smooth tool card appearance in chat

Prefer small Material-style AnimatedVisibility/animateContentSize micro-motion local to the tool card.

title: Collapse completed agent runs by default
description: Why this exists: Completed agent runs can leave long tool/reasoning timelines expanded after the response is finished.\n\nWhat to do: After an agent response fully completes, collapse that run by default while preserving manual expand/collapse toggles and not collapsing the currently streaming run prematurely.
