title: Add full-screen pager image viewer for chat attachments
description:
Why this exists: Google Gallery lets users tap chat images to open a full-screen viewer with paging and close affordance. Letta Mobile renders image attachments statically in MessageAttachmentsGrid, making multimodal messages harder to inspect.

What to do: Thread image-click callbacks from MessageAttachmentsGrid through ChatMessageComponents/ChatMessageList/ChatScreen. Add a full-screen overlay in ChatScreen with AnimatedVisibility, HorizontalPager for multiple attachments, and a close button. Reuse Material color roles and stable motion; avoid fragile shared-element transitions. A first pass can use fit-to-screen images; zoom support is optional if scoped separately.

Reference: Gallery ChatView image viewer overlay with HorizontalPager and close button.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping a chat attachment opens a full-screen viewer. Multiple attachments are pageable starting at the tapped image. Viewer has an accessible close button and supports back/dismiss behavior. Existing attachment grid layout and composer thumbnails still work. Large images do not crash; decoding remains remembered/cached appropriately.

title: Dismiss chat composer focus when message list scrolls
description:
Why this exists: Google Gallery clears focus/keyboard when the user scrolls the chat, reducing keyboard obstruction. Letta Mobile has some global focus clearing, but ChatMessageList does not own this behavior, so chat scrolling can leave the composer focused.

What to do: Add a chat-list-level focus dismissal path in android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageList.kt, likely via LocalFocusManager plus a NestedScrollConnection. Only clear focus for intentional user scrolls; avoid clearing focus from programmatic auto-scrolls. Keep pinch-to-zoom pointer handling and reverseLayout behavior intact.

Reference: Gallery ChatPanel nestedScrollConnection.onPreScroll clears focus on downward scroll.
acceptance_criteria: When the composer is focused and the user scrolls the message list, focus is cleared and the keyboard dismisses. Programmatic auto-scroll and search-result jumps do not unnecessarily clear focus. Pinch font scaling still works. Manual verification covers both normal scroll and pinch gesture.

title: Persist and manage chat input history
description:
Why this exists: Google Gallery persists input history and lets users delete one prompt or clear all. Letta Mobile currently has in-memory ChatComposerState.inputHistory, so history disappears when the chat ViewModel dies and the sheet lacks management actions.

What to do: Add repository/settings-backed persistence for recent prompt history, wire AdminChatViewModel/ChatComposerController to load/save/promote entries, and extend ChatComposer.kt InputHistorySheet with delete-one and clear-all actions. Prefer existing design-system components such as ConfirmDialog for destructive clear-all confirmation; avoid raw AlertDialog. Preserve current insert-into-composer behavior unless product decides otherwise.

Reference: Gallery ModelManagerViewModel textInputHistory methods and TextInputHistorySheet.kt.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt history survives app/process restart. Sending a nonblank prompt adds/promotes it with duplicate removal and a bounded max size. The history sheet can insert a prompt, delete an individual prompt, and clear all prompts with confirmation. Empty state is clear. Unit tests cover add/promote/delete/clear persistence logic.

title: Center composer add image button vertically
description: The add image button in the chat composer should stay vertically centered against the prompt field, matching the scaled send/stop action alignment.
close_reason: Wrapped LettaInputBar leading content in a vertically centered box so the add-image button aligns with the prompt field like the send/stop action; rebuilt and installed on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Center animated composer stop button vertically
description: The streaming stop button scales to 70% but currently bottom-aligns against the input row. Keep the action button vertically centered relative to the prompt field while preserving the scale animation.
close_reason: Aligned the animated input action button to the vertical center of the input row so the 70% stop button stays centered against the prompt field; rebuilt and installed on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Animate chat composer stop button scale
description: When the chat composer send button turns into the stop/pause button during streaming, animate the button/icon size down to 70% using a smooth tween.
close_reason: Added a tweened actionSizeFraction to LettaInputBar and set the chat composer streaming stop button to animate to 70% size; rebuilt and installed root debug APK on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Make chat title open conversation picker
description: Chat screen currently shows a top app bar title and a separate tappable conversation header/card to open the conversation picker. Remove the redundant in-content conversation header and make the top app bar title itself tappable with a small dropdown arrow that opens the existing conversation picker.
close_reason: Removed redundant in-content conversation header and made the top app bar title row tappable with a dropdown arrow to open the existing conversation picker.

title: Move client location into chat mode pill and add filesystem picker
description: UX follow-up for client-mode location controls. Replace the full-width Client location card with a compact pill next to the Simple / Interactive / Debug chat display mode pills. Tapping the location pill should open a full filesystem picker rooted in the target lettabot/client-mode server filesystem so the user can select a new working directory, then send/apply that location change for the active agent/conversation.
design: Client-mode location belongs in the chat display controls row, not as a full-width card. Add a fourth compact pill beside Simple / Interactive / Debug in ChatScreen.kt. The pill label should show the leaf/current path or 'Location' when unknown; use tertiary/status styling only when needed, otherwise align with existing FilterChip/AssistChip semantics. Tapping opens a filesystem picker rather than a free-text path dialog.
acceptance_criteria: In client mode, a location pill appears next to Simple / Interactive / Debug. Tapping it opens a filesystem picker for target server paths. Selecting a directory updates the visible pill state and sends/applies the location change for the active agent. Non-client-mode chats do not show the pill. Existing mode pills continue to work.
notes: Current implementation: AgentScaffold.kt renders ClientModeLocationCard above AgentConversationHeader when uiState.isClientModeEnabled. AdminChatViewModel exposes ClientModeLocationUiState, refreshClientModeLocation(), and sendClientModeLocationChange(path). ClientModeAgentLocationRepository reads optional current_working_directory/default_working_directory/project_path from lettabot /api/v1/status agent_details. Lettabot WS gateway was updated locally/restarted so /api/v1/status now includes default_working_directory for tracked client-mode agents; Letta Mobile Admin now resolves /tmp/lettabot instead of unknown. Next UX work should remove/replace ClientModeLocationCard and wire the picker into sendClientModeLocationChange(). Relevant UI row: ChatScreen.kt ChatContent listOf("simple", "interactive", "debug") FilterChip row.
close_reason: Moved client-mode cwd from full-width card into compact chat mode pill, added server-side filesystem picker sheet, wired selection to sendClientModeLocationChange(), added WsBotClient browse API models, rebuilt and installed root debug APK on Pixel 9 Pro.

title: Client-mode location shows unknown for Letta Mobile Admin
description: On Pixel 9 Pro, client-mode chat for the Letta Mobile Admin agent shows Current path unknown. Need improve fallback/retrieval so non-project agents show a usable default/start location or clearer unavailable state.
close_reason: Fixed at lettabot WS gateway/status source: status now includes gateway agent_details with default_working_directory for tracked client-mode agents, including Letta Mobile Admin.

title: Define lettabot client-mode working-directory protocol
description: Mobile can display client-mode location metadata when /status agent_details expose current_working_directory/default_working_directory and can prompt the agent to switch locations. A robust implementation should add an explicit lettabot gateway contract for reading current cwd and changing cwd/session base path without relying on chat instructions.

title: Expose and change client-mode agent working directory in chat
description: Client-mode chat needs to show the agent's current path and provide a UI action to send the agent to another location, including non-project agents that use a default start location.
close_reason: Implemented client-mode location card, refresh support for lettabot status cwd metadata, and send-to-location chat instruction UI.

title: Increase chat thinking indicator top padding
description: Adjust the chat thinking/reasoning indicator spacing so the animated Material indicator and reasoning body have clearer top separation.
close_reason: Increased top spacing for the streaming TypingIndicator list item and expanded reasoning body; verified with :app:compileRootDebugKotlin.

title: Add granular app-private storage search and edit tools
description: Follow-up from letta-mobile-ifd7.3 mapping. Existing app-private storage foundation supports bounded list/read/write, but Letta Code-style search/edit/patch mappings need granular tool ids and implementations such as storage.app_private.list, storage.app_private.search, storage.app_private.edit, and storage.app_private.patch. Keep operations scoped to app-private roots with canonical containment, caps, and approval/audit hooks for overwrite/edit/patch.

What: clean up or baseline existing bot module detekt findings, including WsBotClient/BotApiServer complexity, generic exception handling, unused imports, and max-line-length violations. title: Map Letta Code file and shell tools to Android access layers
description:
Map Letta Code's filesystem and shell tool families onto Letta Mobile's layered Android system access architecture.

Reference Letta Code tool families:
- Bash / shell_command / run_shell_command
- Read / ReadFile / read_file
- Write / WriteFile / write_file
- Edit / Replace / ApplyPatch
- Glob / Grep / list_directory / search_file_content

Android execution layers to map:
1. App-private storage tools.
2. SAF-granted document/tree tools.
3. Media/library read tools where appropriate.
4. No-root local shell sandbox.
5. Shizuku/Sui privileged API bridge.
6. Root shell and root filesystem tools for root flavor.

This is not an implementation bead for raw shell/root execution. It should define name aliases, capabilities, approval requirements, audit fields, and which existing/open beads own each execution layer.
acceptance_criteria: A mapping table exists in docs or bead notes; each Letta Code-style file/shell tool name maps to an Android capability/tool id or an explicit unsupported state; dangerous operations identify approval/audit requirements; dependencies to root/local-shell/storage implementation beads are recorded.
notes: Delivered docs/architecture/letta-code-android-file-shell-tool-map.md. The doc maps Letta Code Bash/shell_command/run_shell_command, Read/ReadFile/read_file, Write/WriteFile/write_file, Edit/Replace/ApplyPatch, Glob/Grep/list_directory/search_file_content, and batch reads onto explicit Android layers: app-private storage, SAF documents/trees, media, no-root local shell, Shizuku/Sui typed bridge, root shell, and root filesystem. It includes capability ids/tool ids, flavor availability, approval/audit requirements, result contracts, unsupported states, and owner beads. Follow-ups filed: letta-mobile-euk5 for granular app-private search/edit/patch tools and letta-mobile-0zyo for an optional Letta Code alias resolver.
close_reason: Delivered docs/architecture/letta-code-android-file-shell-tool-map.md mapping Letta Code file/search/edit/shell families to explicit Android access layers with capability/tool ids, flavor availability, approval/audit requirements, result contracts, unsupported states, and owner beads; filed follow-ups letta-mobile-euk5 and letta-mobile-0zyo.

title: Implement root filesystem tools behind approvals

Enforce canonical paths, deny dangerous defaults, redact obvious secrets in previews, and prefer storage-capability paths when root is unnecessary.

title: Implement overlay permission and floating assistant surface
description: Add SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW support for sideload/root builds. Use Settings.canDrawOverlays and ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION. Start with a user-controlled floating assistant/status bubble; no hidden automation. Include clear on/off controls and persistent notification if required by Android/OEM behavior.

title: Implement notification listener access
description: Add sideload/root build notification listener patterned after Kai. Gate by manifest service declaration and Settings notification access. Capture visible non-secret non-ongoing notifications into bounded encrypted/persistent queues. Expose check_notifications, read_notification, and search_notifications tools only when enabled and granted.

title: Implement Android storage access tools
description: Provide storage access in layers: app private storage, SAF document/tree grants, MediaStore/photo picker, and only then sideload/root-only all-files or root filesystem access. Define tools for listing/reading/writing user-approved roots with path canonicalization, scoped grants, output limits, and destructive-action approval.

Filed follow-ups letta-mobile-i215 for MediaStore/photo picker and letta-mobile-3hse for SAF tree traversal/destructive approvals.

description: Add READ_CONTACTS support with explicit user opt-in. Provide search_contacts and read_contact tools. Keep write/update contact work out of scope or banner-gated as separate follow-up. Include privacy filtering and bounded results.

title: Build System Access settings dashboard
description: Add a Material 3 settings/admin surface that lists device-access capabilities with status chips, grant/request actions, risk explanations, and links to audit logs. Use existing CardGroup/FormItem/ConfirmDialog patterns. Hide unsupported capabilities per build flavor.

This is separate from root shell and should be safe enough for non-root sideload builds.

This blocks targeted Gradle test execution for stream parser work.

title: Persistent streams: measure screen-off delivery latency across modes
description:
Why:
The findings doc is architecture-derived and does not measure actual current or improved latency. We need a repeatable validation matrix to decide whether balanced mode is enough or wake-lock mode is necessary.

Scope:
- Define a manual/automated test matrix for screen on, screen off, Doze-ish idle, battery optimized, battery exempt, and high-priority mode if implemented.
- Record first-message latency after idle periods such as 5, 15, and 30 minutes.
- Capture service/stream telemetry alongside observations.
- Test at least Wi-Fi and mobile data where possible.
- Document results and recommended defaults.
design: Prefer shipping instrumentation before measurements. Use the diagnostics from the observability issue to avoid relying only on subjective notification arrival times.
acceptance_criteria:
- A reproducible measurement protocol exists.
- Results include baseline and post-heartbeat/watchdog/transport behavior.
- Results distinguish service death, stale socket reconnect, and Doze delay.
- Recommendation is recorded for whether wake-lock mode should ship/default off/default on.
- Any remaining reliability gaps are filed as follow-up beads.
notes: Added docs/architecture/screen-off-streaming-measurement.md with current reliability-stack protocol, telemetry filters, matrix, result templates, interpretation guide, and wake-lock decision gate. Historical findings doc now links to the current protocol. Issue remains open pending real device measurement rows and recommendation.

title: Persistent streams: add AlarmManager service revival safety net
description:
Why:
If ChatPushService is killed by memory pressure or OEM behavior, START_STICKY is not always enough. An inexact alarm can periodically attempt to restart the service and prevent permanent background-delivery death.

Scope:
- Schedule a periodic inexact AlarmManager alarm while background chat delivery is enabled/authenticated.
- Alarm receiver calls ChatPushService.start(context).
- Make start idempotent/no-op if service is already alive.
- Cancel alarm on logout or when background delivery is disabled.
- Add telemetry for alarm fire and service revival attempts.
design: Use inexact Doze-friendly alarms by default. Do not request exact alarm permission for this recovery path unless future evidence requires it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Alarm is scheduled and cancelled at appropriate lifecycle points.
- Alarm does not crash on modern Android background-start restrictions; failures are caught/logged.
- Existing live service is not duplicated or disrupted.
- If service is killed, the next alarm attempts revival.
- Behavior is documented as recovery-only, not instant Doze delivery.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/channel/ChatPushService.kt`
- New receiver candidate: `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/channel/ChatPushAlarmReceiver.kt`
- New scheduler/helper candidate: `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/channel/ChatPushAlarmScheduler.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/MainActivity.kt` or authenticated startup path for scheduling

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add a `BroadcastReceiver` whose `onReceive(context, intent)` calls `ChatPushService.start(context)` in a try/catch and records telemetry.
2. Register receiver in manifest with `android:exported="false"` unless it must receive system broadcasts.
3. Add scheduler helper with:
   - `schedule(context)` using `AlarmManager`
   - `cancel(context)`
   - stable `PendingIntent` request code and flags: `FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or FLAG_IMMUTABLE`
4. Prefer recovery-only inexact scheduling:
   - Consider `setAndAllowWhileIdle(AlarmManager.ELAPSED_REALTIME_WAKEUP, now + 15min, pendingIntent)` and reschedule from receiver after firing.
   - Or use inexact repeating if project preference is simpler, but document Doze behavior.
5. Schedule when background delivery/service should be active: after notification permission/auth config is present and service start is attempted.
6. Cancel on logout or when background delivery is disabled, if such a setting exists.
7. Catch `ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException`/`IllegalStateException`/`SecurityException` around service start; log and do not crash.

Telemetry:
- `ChatPushAlarm.scheduled`
- `ChatPushAlarm.cancelled`
- `ChatPushAlarm.fired`
- `ChatPushAlarm.startFailed`

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin`
- Add app unit tests for scheduler if PendingIntent/alarm helper is testable without Android framework; otherwise document manual verification.

Manual verification:
- Start app, verify alarm scheduled via log/telemetry.
- Force stop/kill service process where possible; wait for alarm or trigger receiver with adb in debug and verify service start path is attempted.
- Verify repeated alarms do not create multiple service instances.

Do not:
- Do not request `SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM` for this issue.
- Do not use exact alarms unless a later measured issue proves inexact recovery is insufficient.
- Do not crash if Android refuses background foreground-service start.
- Do not start service if notification permission guard in `ChatPushService.start()` says to defer.
close_reason: Implemented inexact AlarmManager recovery for ChatPushService with receiver, scheduler, telemetry, lifecycle scheduling/cancel paths, and compile validation.

title: Persistent streams: add optional high-priority delivery wake-lock mode
description:
Why:
For truly prompt locked-screen delivery during Doze, a foreground service may not be enough because CPU/network work can be deferred. A PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK can improve immediacy but has real battery cost and must be opt-in/kill-switchable.

Scope:
- Add a user/feature-flag controlled high-priority delivery mode.
- Request/use android.permission.WAKE_LOCK as needed.
- Acquire PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK only while ChatPushService is running and active streams exist.
- Release on service destroy, logout, zero active streams, or setting disabled.
- Add telemetry for acquire/release/failure and held duration.
- Measure battery impact on real device before defaulting on.
design: This should be the last/battery-heavy remediation after heartbeat, watchdog, transport, diagnostics, and exemption flow exist. Use a named lock such as LettaMobile:ChatPushService.
acceptance_criteria:
- Wake lock is never acquired unless high-priority delivery is enabled.
- Wake lock is released reliably across service destroy, logout, and setting changes.
- Active-stream count gates acquisition.
- Telemetry shows held duration and failures.
- Manual or automated validation demonstrates no wake-lock leak.
- Battery tradeoff is documented before rollout.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/channel/ChatPushService.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/SettingsRepository.kt` for persisted high-priority setting
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/config/ConfigScreen.kt` / `ConfigViewModel.kt` for opt-in UI
- Possibly `TimelineRepository`/`TimelineSyncLoop` if exposing active stream count to service

Permissions/APIs:
- Manifest: `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WAKE_LOCK" />`
- `PowerManager.newWakeLock(PowerManager.PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK, "LettaMobile:ChatPushService")`
- Prefer `wakeLock.setReferenceCounted(false)`.

Prerequisites:
- Do this after diagnostics (`jmzq.5`) and battery exemption UI (`jmzq.6`).
- Keep default OFF unless product explicitly decides otherwise.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add a persisted setting: high-priority/reliable background delivery enabled.
2. Add a settings row with explicit battery warning. This is not the same as battery optimization exemption; exemption may be requested separately.
3. In `ChatPushService`, hold a nullable `PowerManager.WakeLock`.
4. Acquire only when all are true:
   - service is running,
   - user setting enabled,
   - at least one active persistent stream exists OR warmup/subscriber architecture cannot expose count yet and service is in active delivery mode (document if using this fallback).
5. Release on:
   - `onDestroy()`
   - logout / setting disabled
   - active stream count reaches zero if count is implemented
6. Wrap acquire/release in try/catch; never crash service due to wake-lock failure.
7. Emit telemetry:
   - `ChatPushService.wakeLock.acquired`
   - `ChatPushService.wakeLock.released` with held duration
   - `ChatPushService.wakeLock.failed`
8. Add defensive helpers `acquireWakeLockIfNeeded()` and `releaseWakeLock(reason)` to keep lifecycle obvious.

Validation:
- Manual: enable setting, start service, confirm wake lock acquire telemetry; disable setting/destroy service, confirm release telemetry.
- Use `adb shell dumpsys power` if available to verify no leaked `LettaMobile:ChatPushService` lock after disabling/stopping.
- Ensure wake lock is not acquired when setting is off.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin`
- If settings tests changed: `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :core:testDebugUnitTest :app:testDebugUnitTest`

Do not:
- Do not silently acquire a wake lock by default.
- Do not leave a wake lock held after service destroy or logout.
- Do not use a full wake lock; only `PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK` is in scope.
- Do not combine this with aggressive exact alarms.

title: Persistent streams: add battery optimization exemption flow
description:
Why:
A foreground service does not exempt the app from Doze/App-Standby. Users who expect reliable real-time background chat need a clear opt-in path to allow unrestricted background execution.

Scope:
- Check PowerManager.isIgnoringBatteryOptimizations(packageName).
- Add settings/onboarding UI explaining reliable background delivery tradeoffs.
- Launch Settings.ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS only after explicit user action.
- Show current exemption status.
- Add telemetry for exempt/not-exempt and prompt decisions.
design: Position this as optional but recommended for reliable locked-screen chat delivery. Do not make wake locks implicit in this issue.
acceptance_criteria:
- Users can see whether battery optimization exemption is enabled.
- App requests exemption only from an explicit user action with explanatory copy.
- Flow handles denial/cancel gracefully.
- No Play-policy-hostile or misleading copy is introduced.
- Telemetry records status without nagging repeatedly.
notes:
Implementation notes for lower-context agent:

Files to inspect/edit:
- `android-compose/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/config/ConfigScreen.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/config/ConfigViewModel.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/SettingsRepository.kt` if persisting prompt/setting state
- `android-compose/app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml` and localized string files if present

Android APIs:
- `PowerManager.isIgnoringBatteryOptimizations(packageName)`
- `Settings.ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS`
- Intent data: `Uri.parse("package:$packageName")`
- Manifest permission likely required: `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS" />`

Recommended UI placement:
- Add to `ConfigScreen` under `screen_config_features_section` or a new background delivery section using existing `CardGroup` pattern.
- Use a user-initiated button/row, not an automatic prompt on app launch.
- Show current status: optimized/restricted vs unrestricted/exempt.
- Copy should say this improves locked-screen/background chat delivery and may use more battery.

Implementation steps:
1. Add a small platform helper if needed, e.g. `BatteryOptimizationHelper` in app layer, to query status and build intent.
2. Add state to `ConfigUiState` for `batteryOptimizationExempt: Boolean?` if querying from ViewModel, or query in composable via `LocalContext` if simpler and lifecycle-safe.
3. Add row/button:
   - headline: Reliable background delivery / Battery optimization
   - supporting: current status + explanation
   - action: Open Android settings / Allow unrestricted background delivery
4. Launch `ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS` only after tap. Catch `ActivityNotFoundException` and fall back to `Settings.ACTION_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATION_SETTINGS` if needed.
5. Refresh status when returning to app.
6. Add telemetry events: `batteryOptimization.status`, `batteryOptimization.requestLaunched`, `batteryOptimization.requestFailed`.

Commands:
- `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin`
- If ViewModel tests changed: `cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests '*ConfigViewModelTest*'`

Do not:
- Do not nag on every launch.
- Do not request exemption without explicit user action.
- Do not imply exemption guarantees instant delivery in all OEM/Doze cases.
- Do not add raw AlertDialog if existing Material/CardGroup/settings row patterns suffice.
close_reason: Implemented battery optimization exemption status UI, explicit request flow with fallback, manifest permission, and telemetry.

title: Persistent streams: make stream budget and dispatcher isolation explicit
description:
Why:
The service warms only five conversations because long-lived streams can saturate OkHttp's default per-host dispatcher and starve foreground sends/fetches. Persistent streaming needs an explicit budget and possibly dispatcher/client isolation.

Scope:
- Define max persistent stream count and selection policy.
- Ensure the current conversation has highest priority.
- Keep most-recently-active warmup within budget.
- Evaluate whether normal REST calls can be starved by streams.
- If needed, increase dispatcher limits or split streaming and REST clients/dispatchers.
design: Default budget should remain conservative, likely 5 streams, until measurements justify more. Prefer separate streaming vs REST clients if starvation remains a risk.
acceptance_criteria:
- Max persistent stream count is centralized and documented.
- Current conversation is prioritized over background warmups.
- Foreground send/fetch requests are not starved when background streams are active.
- Any dispatcher/client tuning is covered by tests or manual verification notes.
- Telemetry/logging can report active stream count and budget decisions.
notes: Implemented persistent-stream budget follow-up. Changes: ChatPushService now uses documented MAX_BACKGROUND_PERSISTENT_STREAMS=5; warmup plan prioritizes CurrentConversationTracker.current when set, then fills remaining slots from most-recent conversations without exceeding budget; warmup telemetry reports budget, requested/recent counts, current inclusion/prioritization, success/failure counts, and TimelineRepository cached loop counts before/after. TimelineSyncLoop stream open/close/watchdog telemetry now includes activeStreamCount via a process-wide AtomicInteger. TimelineRepository exposes cachedLoopCount() for telemetry only; no eviction behavior added. LettaApiClient dispatcher comment updated to reference the explicit budget and retain maxRequestsPerHost=16. Validation: original handoff command cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin is ambiguous for app flavors; ran cd android-compose && ./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin successfully. Manual device checks not run in this session.
close_reason: Implemented explicit persistent stream warmup budget, current-conversation prioritization, warmup success/failure/budget telemetry, active stream count telemetry, and dispatcher-budget documentation. Validated with :app:compileRootDebugKotlin and :core:compileDebugKotlin.

title: FullScreen tool/block pickers: loading skeleton mismatch and weak search highlighting
description:
# FullScreen tool/block pickers: loading skeleton mismatch and weak search highlighting

## Problem

User reports: "attach block skeletons don't make sense and search needs stronger matching and highlighting"

Exhaustive inspection of FullScreenToolPickerDialog and FullScreenBlockPickerDialog in EditAgentScreen.kt reveals concrete UX issues when compared against repo search/list patterns (AllToolsScreen, AgentListScreen, McpScreen).

## Concrete Issues

### 1. FullScreenBlockPickerDialog: Loading state shows generic spinner instead of skeleton (lines 1148-1166)
- **Current**: CircularProgressIndicator + "Loading" text in centered Box
- **Repo pattern**: AllToolsScreen uses ShimmerGrid (line 139) for loading state
- **Impact**: User sees blank screen with spinner instead of content-shaped skeleton
- **User complaint**: "attach block skeletons don't make sense"

### 2. FullScreenToolPickerDialog: No loading state at all (lines 1012-1096)
- **Current**: tools list passed as parameter, no UiState wrapper, no loading/error handling
- **Repo pattern**: AllToolsScreen wraps in UiState.Loading/Error/Success with ShimmerGrid
- **Impact**: If tools load slowly, user sees empty dialog or stale data with no feedback

### 3. Search highlighting exists but uses simple substring match (lines 1327-1361)
- **Current**: highlightMatches() does case-insensitive substring indexOf
- **Repo pattern**: AllToolsScreen uses same simple filter (lines 146-148) but no highlighting in result cards
- **User complaint**: "search needs stronger matching"
- **Impact**: No fuzzy matching, no word-boundary preference, no relevance scoring

### 4. FullScreenBlockPickerDialog search filter is weaker than FullScreenToolPickerDialog
- **Block filter** (lines 1174-1183): checks label, description, value
- **Tool filter** (lines 1022-1029): checks name, description
- **Inconsistency**: Both should have equivalent field coverage

### 5. EmptyState messaging is inconsistent
- **FullScreenToolPickerDialog** (line 1067): "screen_tools_empty_attached" (wrong context — this is picker, not attached list)
- **FullScreenBlockPickerDialog** (line 1188): "screen_blocks_empty_available" (correct)
- **AllToolsScreen** (lines 157-160): Differentiates between empty-all vs empty-search
- **Impact**: Confusing empty state message in tool picker

## Recommended Fixes

### Priority 1: Fix FullScreenBlockPickerDialog loading skeleton
Replace CircularProgressIndicator (lines 1148-1166) with ShimmerListItem pattern from ShimmerPlaceholder.kt

### Priority 2: Add UiState wrapper to FullScreenToolPickerDialog
Refactor to accept UiState instead of List, add loading/error states matching FullScreenBlockPickerDialog pattern.

### Priority 3: Strengthen search matching
Options:
- Add fuzzy matching library
- Implement word-boundary scoring (prefer matches at word start)
- Add relevance scoring and sort by match quality
- Highlight multiple match regions (current implementation only highlights first occurrence per iteration)

### Priority 4: Unify search filter coverage
Ensure both pickers search equivalent fields (name/label, description, value/tags).

### Priority 5: Fix EmptyState string resource
Change line 1067 from R.string.screen_tools_empty_attached to R.string.screen_tools_empty_search or similar.

## Files
- /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/editagent/EditAgentScreen.kt (lines 1012-1325)
- /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/ShimmerPlaceholder.kt (ShimmerListItem pattern)
- /opt/stacks/letta-mobile/android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/tools/AllToolsScreen.kt (reference implementation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- FullScreenBlockPickerDialog loading state shows ShimmerListItem skeleton instead of spinner
- FullScreenToolPickerDialog wraps tools in UiState and shows loading/error states
- Search highlighting uses stronger matching (fuzzy or word-boundary scoring)
- Both pickers have equivalent search field coverage
- EmptyState messages are contextually correct

title: Slim AgentScaffold into chat shell only
description: Reduce AgentScaffold to navigation chrome and shell responsibilities. Extract project header/cards, conversation switcher section, and route-specific chat content composition into smaller units so scaffold changes stop affecting chat behavior indirectly.

title: Extract IME and viewport behavior hooks for chat
description: Isolate chat-specific IME handling, bottom inset flooring, keyboard-driven list adjustment, and viewport/pinch behavior into dedicated hooks so keyboard/layout fixes stop touching unrelated list or composer code.

title: Remove Mermaid WebView fallback after client-mode validation
description: Streaming was confirmed to still work in client mode, so resume the native-only Mermaid renderer path and remove the WebView fallback again. Rebuild and validate on Pixel 2 XL.
close_reason: Merged completed Client Mode timeline streaming and removed Mermaid WebView fallback/stale HTML test into main integration.

title: Improve Mermaid text contrast against themed illustration surfaces
description: Some native Mermaid diagrams use app-theme-derived fills where labels end up too close to the background value, making text hard to read. Add contrast-aware color selection for Mermaid text and related themed surfaces in the native Rust renderer without changing layout geometry.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #28 — WCAG luminance-based contrast adjustment for text and line/border colors in native Rust Mermaid renderer

title: Expose gateway approvals and recovery UX in chat
description: Strengthen the Android chat surface around gateway-owned actions by making approvals, recovery banners, session swaps, resumability failures, and command-risk states first-class UI concepts. This follows the incumbent pattern of explicit control-plane feedback while remaining inside the single Android client.

title: Add project-scoped skills and tool visibility to Android
description: Build on the existing Huly/VibeSync-backed project shell by surfacing what tools, MCP capabilities, and skills are relevant to a given project and its agent. The goal is not a generic marketplace first, but stronger project-scoped capability visibility and enablement inside the single Android client.

title: Improve Android offline resilience and gateway observability
description: Strengthen the single Android client's resilience around gateway-driven chat by improving offline behavior, reconnect state, pending-work visibility, error diagnostics, and operator observability for client-mode/gateway failures. The intent is to make the app feel dependable as a daily driver before expanding to additional surfaces.

title: Android client: add manual run interruption button
description: Currently, runs can only be interrupted when the screen goes to sleep. It would be useful to have an intentional way to cancel/stop an ongoing run from the UI - for example, a stop button that the user can tap when they want to interrupt the agent mid-execution.

title: Android client: collapsible sections cause scroll position loss on toggle
description: When collapsible sections are toggled in the Android app, the UI reflows causing the user to lose their scroll position. This is annoying during normal browsing. The fix should track and restore scroll position after the resize completes.

title: Stabilize streaming markdown block rendering
description: Open fenced code blocks and other block-level markdown constructs reparse and restyle on every stream tick, causing visible flash in chat rendering. Implement boundary-aware streaming so completed markdown blocks stay stable while only the active tail updates.

title: Prefill LettaBot WS gateway settings via debug bootstrap
description: Extend the Android debug automation bootstrap so repeated debug deploys can prefill the separate LettaBot websocket gateway URL and API key in addition to the main Letta config.

title: Prefer wireless adb in debug deploy helper
description: Update the install-debug-with-creds helper so it automatically prefers a connected wireless adb transport when both USB and TCP/IP transports are present for the same Android device.

title: Enable wireless adb on connected Pixel 2 XL
description: Turn on adb over TCP/IP for the currently connected Pixel 2 XL and capture the device IP/connection command for future wireless debug sessions.

title: Locate project credentials for device bootstrap
description: Search approved local sources and configured secret-management paths for letta.oculair.ca credentials and websocket endpoint/auth details used by letta-mobile debug bootstrap and device workflows.

title: Prefill mobile credentials after debug deploys
description: Investigate and implement a way for the Android debug app to prefill or preserve server credentials so repeated adb installs do not force manual re-entry on the device.

title: Install debug APK on connected Pixel 2 XL
description: Build the Android debug APK from android-compose and install it onto the currently connected Pixel 2 XL over adb for local smoke testing.

title: Client Mode: user message renders late, alongside agent reply (delayed echo)
description:
Even ignoring the missing-optimistic-echo bug (`letta-mobile-9pfm`), the user's message currently arrives in the chat view at the same time as (or after) the agent's response, breaking the temporal ordering of the conversation visually. The user message should always appear before the assistant reply.

Symptom (Emmanuel, 2026-04-28):
> 'after a while my message pops in along with miscellaneous message history in this supposedly new conversation in addition to the agent response'

Likely root cause: the chat view is rebuilt from the `GET /messages` server-stored history once the agent's reply lands. That batch render dumps user + assistant + (any leaked system messages) all at once instead of streaming user → indicator → assistant chunks.
design:
## Cause hypothesis

`AdminChatViewModel` likely has logic like:
```
onResultFrame: refetchMessages() // pulls server state, rebuilds list
```

This wipes out anything in the local list and re-populates from server, which is also why the new-conv leaked `system_message` shows up at the same time (see `letta-mobile-e75s`).

## Fix direction

Stop rebuilding from `GET /messages` on every terminal frame. Treat the streaming WS frames as the source of truth for the active turn, and reconcile with server only on:
- conversation reopen (cold load)
- explicit user pull-to-refresh

This bug, `letta-mobile-9pfm`, and `letta-mobile-e75s` may all share a single root cause (the post-send refetch). Worth landing the three together as a single 'streaming-first chat state' refactor.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The user's message bubble appears in the chat view BEFORE the assistant's first chunk under all conditions.
2. After server-side history reconciliation completes, ordering by `created_at` MUST be preserved — newest at bottom, user-then-assistant pairs intact.
3. A reload/refresh of an existing conversation reorders by server timestamp without flicker.
notes:
Architecture review (2026-04-28, while fixing e75s):

Likely fixed (or made invisible) by e75s. The reported symptom — 'user message renders late alongside agent reply' — fits perfectly with the SYSTEM-bubble bug:

1. User taps send.
2. Optimistic user bubble IS appended (clientModeMessages path or appendClientModeLocal).
3. Then the timeline observer fires for the first hydrate emission carrying [system_message_seed], which projected as a SYSTEM bubble. _uiState.messages = [system_seed] — replacing the optimistic user bubble.
4. User's bubble is gone for the duration of the stream.
5. Stream completes; reconcile pulls the user_message Confirmed event from the server. _uiState.messages = [system_seed, user_message, assistant_reply] — user bubble reappears 'alongside' the agent reply.

With e75s landed, the SYSTEM event is dropped at the projection layer, so step 3 leaves the optimistic user bubble in place. Step 5 then merges in the Confirmed user_message via the otid/fuzzy collapse path (already exists for c87t).

Please re-test pv4y after e75s ships. If the late rendering still reproduces, the next suspects are:
- The Local→Confirmed collapse window: a slow Confirmed echo could still leave a visible 'gap'.
- handleClientModeStreamChunk overwriting messages with replaceAssistant=true.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Client Mode: no thinking/typing indicator while agent is generating
description:
After sending a message, while the agent is processing/streaming, the chat view shows no indicator that anything is happening. The user has no way to tell whether the request is in flight, the agent is reasoning, the model is generating, or the app is hung.

Expected behavior: an animated indicator (e.g. '...' bubble, 'thinking…' affordance, or progress spinner) should appear from the moment send is dispatched until the first `assistant` chunk arrives, then persist (perhaps in a different style) until the terminal `result` frame.

Symptom (Emmanuel, 2026-04-28):
> 'i don't see a thinking indicator. after a while my message pops in along with the agent response.'
design:
## States to wire

`AgentRunState`:
- `Idle`
- `Sending` (POST in flight)
- `Reasoning` (saw `reasoning` frame, no `assistant` yet)
- `Generating` (saw first `assistant` chunk)
- `ToolCalling` (last frame was `tool_call`)
- `Done`
- `Error`

The WS gateway already emits all of these frame types (`reasoning`, `assistant`, `tool_call`, `tool_return`, terminal `result`) — see `docs/channel-adapter-contract.md` §3.

## Composable

`@Composable fun ThinkingIndicator(state: AgentRunState)` — render below last user message; animate dot pulse for Reasoning/Generating, gear icon for ToolCalling.

## Related

- `letta-mobile-9pfm` — optimistic user echo (probably ship together)
- The message-arrives-late bug
acceptance_criteria:
1. Within one frame of send-tap, a thinking indicator is visible in the chat view (below the optimistic user bubble).
2. Indicator transitions naturally as state evolves:
   - PENDING_REQUEST (waiting for first server frame)
   - REASONING (if a `reasoning` frame arrives — optional sub-state)
   - GENERATING (once first `assistant` chunk arrives)
   - TOOL_CALL (optional, when `tool_call` frames stream)
3. Indicator disappears on terminal `result` frame OR on error.
4. If the WS connection drops mid-stream, indicator transitions to a 'reconnecting…' or error state, never silently disappears.
notes:
Architecture review (2026-04-28, while fixing e75s):

The thinking indicator is wired correctly in principle:
- ChatScreen.kt line 539: 'if (state.isStreaming) { TypingIndicator(...) }' — gated on isStreaming.
- AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode line 1063 (known-conv branch) and line 1113 (fresh-route branch): both set isStreaming=true BEFORE the launch.
- letta-mobile-5s1n's startTimelineObserver explicitly preserves isStreaming when streamInFlight is true (line 2154), so observer emissions don't clobber it.

So the indicator should appear on every send. If it still doesn't, suspect:
1. clientModeStreamInFlight race: it's set true at line 1015, BEFORE viewModelScope.launch, so the observer should see it. But if the observer fires sync inside the launch BEFORE line 1063 lands isStreaming=true, the observer could compute nextIsStreaming=anyLettaServerLocalPending=false and clobber. Need to verify ordering on real device.
2. The TypingIndicator composable itself might be invisible (transparent, sized 0, etc).

e75s likely masked b2u1 too — with a SYSTEM bubble at the top, the typing indicator at the bottom of a reverseLayout list might have been visible all along but visually drowned. Re-test post-e75s.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: WsBotClient: surface close code from OkHttp listener to reconnect controller
description:
Plumbing-only change: capture the int close code from OkHttp's WebSocketListener.onClosed(code, reason) and onClosing(code, reason), and pass it through to whatever orchestrates reconnect (likely RemoteBotSession or the client's state machine).

Currently the close code is dropped on the floor. This bead just makes it visible to the reconnect path; the actual branching logic lands in the next child bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- onClosed/onClosing capture (code, reason) and forward to reconnect controller
- A typed CloseReason or CloseInfo data class is introduced (code: Int, reason: String, wasClean: Boolean)
- Existing reconnect behavior unchanged (no branching yet — that's the follow-up)
- Lifecycle test asserts the controller receives the right code for synthetic 1000/4000/4001/4003 closes

title: WsBotClient: implement code-aware reconnect policy (4000 immediate, 4001 backoff, 4003 long backoff)
description:
Once close code is plumbed through (parent z1oz child #1), implement the actual policy:

| Code | Meaning           | Reconnect policy                                  |
|------|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| 4000 | SERVER_SHUTDOWN   | Immediate retry (small jitter, 100-500ms)         |
| 4001 | DEAD_CONNECTION   | Existing exponential backoff (network blip)       |
| 4003 | IDLE_TIMEOUT      | Long backoff (≥10s) + WARN log, this is our bug   |
| 1000 | normal close      | No reconnect (intentional disconnect)             |
| 1006 | abnormal close    | Existing exponential backoff                      |
| else | unknown           | Existing exponential backoff                      |

Constants should be a CloseCode enum/object that mirrors the gateway's enum in src/api/ws-gateway.ts.
acceptance_criteria:
- CloseCode constants object exists in bot/protocol with same values as gateway
- ReconnectPolicy.next(code) returns Duration | NoReconnect
- Unit tests cover all six rows of the table above
- 4003 path emits a WARN-level logcat entry naming the close reason
- No regression on existing reconnect tests

title: WsBotClient: branch reconnect strategy on gateway close codes (4000/4001/4003)
description:
Gateway (lettabot src/api/ws-gateway.ts) now emits structured close codes:
- 4000 SERVER_SHUTDOWN — planned gateway shutdown
- 4001 DEAD_CONNECTION — peer reaped after failed ping/pong cycle
- 4003 IDLE_TIMEOUT — client never sent session_start within idle window (default 30s)

WsBotClient.kt currently treats all closes uniformly. We should branch reconnect behavior on close code:
- 4000 → immediate reconnect (server is coming back, no need to back off)
- 4001 → transient network blip, normal exponential backoff
- 4003 → longer backoff + log warning (we failed to send session_start in time — likely a client bug, don't hammer the gateway)
- other / no code → existing behavior

This is wire-additive: ignoring close codes (current behavior) still works. Server-side hardening lives in lettabot-wsh.1 (closed).
acceptance_criteria:
- WsBotClient.kt close handler reads close code from OkHttp WebSocketListener.onClosed/onClosing
- Reconnect path branches on 4000 / 4001 / 4003 / other with documented timing
- Unit test (WsBotClientLifecycleTest) covers each code path: asserts reconnect delay or no-reconnect as appropriate
- Logcat tag for IDLE_TIMEOUT (4003) is WARN-level so it shows up in triage
- AGENTS.md or bot/README updated with the close-code contract (link to lettabot docs/channel-adapter-contract.md if applicable)

title: Verify tool_call handler still keys off tool_call_id (not uuid) in AdminChatViewModel
description:
Per channel-adapter-contract §7, mobile already does this correctly: AdminChatViewModel.kt:1283 uses localId = 'cm-tool-$toolCallId'. This issue is verification that the existing code didn't drift, not a refactor. If somebody silently changed it to key off uuid, the mobile bridge would still post one message per tool_call (because there's only one frame in default mode) but it'd be silently correct for the wrong reason — and would break the moment progressive mode is re-enabled or the snapshot semantics change.

Read-only verification, parallelizable with letta-mobile-h73e (status-running defense-in-depth audit).

## Context
Reference: /opt/stacks/lettabot/docs/channel-adapter-contract.md §7. Risk 3 in the lettabot writeup: keying off uuid would make every snapshot a new message — correct in default mode, broken in progressive mode. Read-only grep, no refactor expected unless drift is found.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Grep mobile repo for 'cm-tool-' and confirm it still resolves to tool_call_id (not uuid, not request_id, not anything else).
2. Confirm AdminChatViewModel.kt:1283 (or wherever it lives now) is the single source of truth for the localId derivation.
3. Note any other tool_call message-id derivations and confirm they all key off tool_call_id.

title: [client-mode][test] Snapshot fallback test — tool cards render correctly with PARTIAL_JSON disabled
description:
Mirror to lettabot-uww.8 (cross-feature flag matrix on the server). Mobile-side counterpart: regardless of whether the lettabot gateway has LETTABOT_PARTIAL_JSON_ENABLED on or off, the mobile tool card MUST render the final tool_call args correctly. Mobile cannot assume progressive snapshots.

Why: when uww.6 lands and flips the default to on, older lettabot servers in the wild will still emit single-frame tool_calls. The mobile app must work against both wire shapes:
- Single-frame (legacy): one tool_call event with full args.
- Multi-frame (new): N 'running' snapshots + 1 'completed' terminal frame.

Test scenarios (via fake WS frame sequences):
1. Legacy single-frame — tool card renders once, terminal state immediately.
2. New multi-frame — tool card recomposes through snapshots, terminal state on final frame.
3. Mixed within a single run — first tool call uses legacy shape, second uses new shape (e.g. agent backed by mixed lettabot versions in a multi-route setup).

Depends on the harness from the parent compose-test bead.
acceptance_criteria: Both legacy and new wire shapes render identically in terminal state; mixed sequence renders correctly; assertions cover dedupe by tool_call_id.
notes: Cross-repo mirror of lettabot-uww.8 (server-side flag matrix).

title: [client-mode][test] Tool card resilience — backgrounded stream + reconnect produces single terminal card
description:
Mirror to lettabot-uww.10 (cancel/disconnect mid-stream coverage on the server). Mobile-side: when the user backgrounds the app mid-tool-call (Client Mode WS), the OS may close the socket. On foreground, the snapshot-recovery path (per the wucn-snapshot-recovery scoping in lettabot a7d7974) should rejoin and produce exactly one terminal tool_call card — no duplicate cards, no leaked 'running' cards.

Test scenarios:
1. App backgrounded after first 'running' snapshot — verify no duplicate card on foreground; final state is the terminal frame from the recovery path.
2. App backgrounded after terminal frame already arrived — verify no duplicate card; foreground replays archived state.
3. Explicit user cancel mid-stream — verify card transitions to cancelled chip, no further snapshots applied even if a stale frame arrives.
4. Network drop + auto-reconnect — same conversation_id, recovery yields exactly-once terminal frame.

Use Compose UI test + a fake WS that can be paused/resumed mid-sequence. Lifecycle owner driven by androidx.lifecycle.testing.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 scenarios pass; exactly-once terminal frame asserted; no orphaned 'running' cards; flake rate <2%.
notes: Cross-repo mirror of lettabot-uww.10 (server-side cancel/disconnect coverage).

title: task(chat/streaming): add BuildConfig feature flag for c8of incremental streaming (deferred — implement if regression surfaces)
task(chat/streaming): measure + mitigate cumulative prefix re-parse cost on long replies (c8of follow-up)
description:
## Concern

ALT-2 architecture: each time the boundary advances, the prefix grows. MarkdownText (mikepenz) re-parses the ENTIRE prefix on each advance, even though the new prefix is a strict superset of the old one. For short replies (< 5 paragraphs) this is fine — total cost ~5 parses per reply. For long replies (50+ paragraphs of a tutorial), we re-parse the cumulative prefix 50 times, each parse covering a longer string than the previous.

Theoretical worst case: O(N²) total work where N = final length. In practice mikepenz's commonmark-java parse is fast (~1ms per KB), so a 50KB reply with 50 boundary commits = ~625ms cumulative parse cost spread over the stream duration. Probably invisible on Pixel 9.

But on slower devices (Pixel 2 XL, our reference low-end target) this could surface as Choreographer skipped-frames during the back half of long streams.

## Investigation

1. Measure: instrument findLastSafeBoundary call site to log boundary-commit timestamps and prefix length to logcat under a debug tag. Run a 5000-token stream on Pixel 9 Pro and Pixel 2 XL. Compute total parse time and per-commit parse time.

2. If Pixel 2 XL shows visible jank during long streams, evaluate fixes:

### Fix A — debounce boundary commits
Don't commit a new boundary until 50ms after the LAST boundary commit. Multiple `\n\n` arriving within a single chunk batch get coalesced into one prefix update.
Cost: low. Downside: smallest visible commit latency increases by up to 50ms.

### Fix B — incremental prefix renderer
Wrap MarkdownText in a composable that caches the previous parsed AST + the previous prefix string. On each invocation, if the new text starts with the previous prefix, ONLY parse the delta and append to the cached AST. Requires diving into mikepenz's parser API to compose AST trees.
Cost: high. Real win on long replies.

### Fix C — defer prefix advance during high stream rate
If chunks are arriving faster than 5/sec (sustained), pause boundary commits. Resume once rate drops. Tradeoff: visible "stickiness" during burst, smoother during steady-state.

## Decision deferred until measurement

Likely Fix A is enough; B is overkill unless A doesn't move the needle.

## Acceptance (when implemented)

1) Logged measurements on both devices for a 5000-token stream
2) If jank present: chosen fix implemented and measurements re-run
3) Choreographer skipped-frames count during long stream is < 5 (was: TBD)

title: task(chat/streaming): soft sentence-break boundary for single-giant-paragraph replies (c8of follow-up)
description:
## Gap

ALT-2 boundary detector only commits at `\n\n`. A reply that comes back as one giant paragraph (no paragraph breaks) sits ENTIRELY in the tail as plain text for the whole stream, then snaps to formatted markdown at stream-end — reproducing the original d2z6 snap behavior for this specific case.

Typical assistant replies paragraph-break on their own (`\n\n` arrives every ~1s), so this is rare. But terse replies ("Yes, the answer is **42**.") or long unbroken explanations can hit it.

## Approach

Add a SECONDARY soft boundary inside findLastSafeBoundary: a sentence-end punctuation followed by a single space and a capital letter (heuristic for "next sentence starts here"), e.g. `. A` or `! B` or `? C`. This soft boundary fires only when:

1. The hard `\n\n` boundary hasn't fired in the last N chars (N ~= 200 — past where the tail starts feeling "stale")
2. We're not inside an open code fence or display-math block (parity check, same as hard boundary)
3. We're not inside a markdown construct that spans sentences (link `[text](`, emphasis `*…*`, etc.) — but per c8of-architecture-doc the prefix never sees unclosed inline markers, so we just need to ensure the soft boundary lands after a `.` `!` `?` outside backticks.

Sentence-boundary regex: `(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])` — matches a sentence break followed by capitalized next sentence.

## Risk

Sentence-boundary commits inside a paragraph mean the prefix gets a bare `.` or `?` paragraph end where the source had no paragraph break. Markdown rendering of "Hello world." (one sentence) vs "Hello world.\n\nMore" (two paragraphs) is functionally identical for plain prose, but DIFFERENT for:

- A sentence followed by a fenced block (fence won't render until the next chunk arrives — it sits in the tail anyway, so OK)
- A sentence inside a list item (would split the list item — bad). Mitigation: don't soft-commit if the current "paragraph" begins with `- ` `* ` `+ ` `1. ` etc.
- A sentence inside a blockquote (would break quote continuity). Mitigation: don't soft-commit if the line begins with `> `.

## Acceptance

1) Single-giant-paragraph reply commits at sentence boundaries during streaming, not just at stream-end
2) List items don't split mid-item (verify with a 5-item list reply, no `\n\n` between items, all items in one stream)
3) Blockquotes don't split (verify with a multi-sentence quoted reply)
4) Code fences and math still take precedence (no soft-commit inside an open fence)
5) New unit tests cover: long-paragraph soft commit, list-item-no-split, blockquote-no-split, fence-takes-precedence
6) Hard boundary still preferred when available (soft only fires when hard hasn't fired in 200+ chars)

## Decision needed BEFORE implementing

Threshold N (chars without hard boundary before soft boundary fires). Suggesting 200 as starting point — long enough that normal paragraphs use the hard boundary, short enough that the giant-paragraph case sees rough sentence-cadence commits during streaming.

title: task(chat/streaming): on-device validation matrix for c8of streaming markdown
description:
## Goal

Validate the c8of ALT-2 boundary-aware streaming markdown landed cleanly with a structured on-device test pass on Pixel 9 Pro (100.79.179.71:5555). The implementation looks right and unit tests pass, but the visual UX is what matters and only a real device tells us if it feels good.

## Test prompts to send to the assistant

Each prompt is designed to exercise a specific c8of code path. Watch for:
- Did the snap at stream-end happen? (it should NOT)
- Did anything visibly flicker mid-stream?
- Did formatting "pop" in cleanly at boundaries, or did it look jittery?

### 1. Multi-paragraph plain prose
Prompt: "Write me a 3-paragraph explanation of how WebSockets differ from HTTP."
Expected: Each paragraph formats as `\n\n` commits. No snap on settle. Tail (in-progress paragraph) shows ▎ cursor, plain text, no flicker.

### 2. Markdown-rich reply (lists, bold, headings, links)
Prompt: "Give me a markdown-formatted summary of the top 5 differences between Kotlin and Java, with **bold** key terms and bullet points."
Expected: Headings, lists, **bold** all format LIVE as paragraphs commit. Tail still plain text with cursor. Stream-end produces no visible reflow.

### 3. Code fence in the middle
Prompt: "Show me a Kotlin function that calculates fibonacci, with explanation paragraphs before and after."
Expected: Intro paragraph commits. Open fence sits in tail as plain monospace-ish text. Closing ``` + \n\n commits the entire syntax-highlighted code block into the prefix. Outro paragraph commits. NO mid-fence highlighter thrash.

### 4. Math
Prompt: "Explain Pythagoras' theorem with a $$display equation$$ and an inline $a^2$ math example."
Expected: Display math renders only after $$ closes (lives in tail as `$$x^2 + y^2 = z^2$$` text until the closing $$). Inline math only renders when the paragraph commits.

### 5. Mixed turn (text -> code -> text -> list)
Prompt: "Tell me about Compose Modifier ordering. Include a code example, then a paragraph, then a 4-item bulleted list of key takeaways."
Expected: Each block commits independently. No layout shift on settle.

### 6. Pinch-to-zoom mid-stream
While reply 5 is streaming, pinch-zoom the chat. Expected: smooth as r3, no jank during the gesture, tail follows the pinch fontScale on lift.

### 7. Cancel mid-stream
Send any long prompt, hit cancel halfway through. Expected: tail freezes wherever cancel hit; prefix stays formatted; no crash or weird state.

### 8. Rapid back-to-back turns
Send prompt, immediately send another prompt while #1 is still streaming. Expected: turn #1 finalizes cleanly; turn #2 begins streaming in its own bubble; no animation glitches at the boundary between bubbles.

### 9. Stress: 5000-token reply with mixed content
Prompt: "Write a complete tutorial on building an Android Compose chat app with state management, navigation, theming, and testing. Include code samples for each section."
Expected: No ANR, no Choreographer skipped-frames warnings in logcat (filter by "Choreographer"), no jank visible during streaming. Final settled bubble is fully formatted.

## Logcat watch during validation

```bash
adb -s 100.79.179.71:5555 logcat -c
adb -s 100.79.179.71:5555 logcat | grep -iE "Choreographer|MarkdownText|StreamingMarkdownText|AndroidRuntime|FATAL"
```

Anything from MarkdownText complaining about parse errors → note the offending content for follow-up.
Choreographer "skipped X frames" warnings during streaming where X > 30 → note the prompt + which test.

## Pass criteria

All 9 tests pass visually + no new logcat warnings/errors.

## If anything fails

Note the test number, the prompt, what you saw, and any logcat output. File a follow-up bead under c8of for each distinct failure mode.

title: task(chat/streaming): build boundary-aware StreamingMarkdownText (commit-prefix MarkdownText + plain-text tail)
description:
Implement the ALT-2 boundary-aware streaming markdown composable in :designsystem.

## Public API

@Composable
fun StreamingMarkdownText(
    text: String,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    textColor: Color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface,
)

(No bubbleId needed — pure function of text. Boundary detection is O(N) char scan with parity tracking.)

## Implementation outline

1. Boundary detection function `findLastSafeBoundary(text: String): Int`
   - Single pass tracking ``` and $$ parity
   - Returns the index AFTER the last \n\n where both parities are EVEN
   - Returns 0 if no safe boundary exists yet

2. Composable splits content into prefix (full markdown) + tail (plain text):
   ```
   val boundary = remember(text) { findLastSafeBoundary(text) }
   val prefix = text.take(boundary)
   val tail = text.drop(boundary)
   Column(modifier) {
     if (prefix.isNotEmpty()) MarkdownText(prefix, textColor = textColor)
     if (tail.isNotBlank()) Text(tail, color = textColor, style = bodyMedium)
   }
   ```

3. The MarkdownText prefix RE-RENDERS only when boundary advances (prefix changed). Tail Text re-renders on every chunk (cheap — plain text layout). Critical perf property: mikepenz parses on each prefix change but those happen at PARAGRAPH cadence (~1Hz), not chunk cadence (~10Hz).

4. Edge case: chunk lands that ONLY adds to the tail (mid-paragraph) → boundary unchanged → MarkdownText composable's input identical → mikepenz no-ops via standard recomposition skipping. This is the win.

## Tests (unit, in :designsystem)

findLastSafeBoundary cases:
- empty → 0
- "Hello world" → 0 (no boundary yet)
- "Hello\n\nWorld" → 7 (after first \n\n)
- "Hello\n\nWorld\n\nFoo" → 14
- triple-backtick unclosed fence → boundary BEFORE the fence
- triple-backtick closed fence → boundary AFTER the fence
- $$math$$ closed → boundary after math
- $$math (no close) → 0 (parity odd, no safe split)
- Multiple closed fences in sequence

## Acceptance

1) Composable compiles + previews
2) All boundary unit tests pass
3) Visual check: feeding incremental chunks of a complex markdown doc reveals each paragraph rendering at boundary commits, with the in-progress tail visible as plain text
4) No regression on settled markdown (caller swaps to MarkdownText on isStreaming=false; the swap is now invisible because boundary == content.length on settle)
close_reason: Implemented + tested. StreamingMarkdownText composable + findLastSafeBoundary lives in :designsystem/.../StreamingMarkdownText.kt. 17 unit tests in StreamingMarkdownBoundaryTest pass (empty/single-char/no-boundary/single-newline/double-newline/multi-paragraph/trailing-newlines/extra-blanks/unclosed-fence/closed-fence/double-newline-inside-fence/multiple-closed-fences/closed-display-math/unclosed-display-math/boundary-before-fence-open/inline-emphasis-mid-tail/progressive-stream-simulation). Wired into TextMessageRenderer (MessageContentFactory.kt) replacing the d2z6 plain-Text streaming branch. tailTransform = ::streamingDisplayText preserves the 6p4o.1 cursor + word-boundary holdback for the in-progress paragraph. Build clean (assembleRelease, 3m53s). Install + launch clean on Pixel 9 Pro (PID 28617, no AndroidRuntime FATAL).

title: task(chat/jewel): wire StreamingMarkdownText into the assistant prose renderer with feature flag
description:
Wire the new StreamingMarkdownText into the chat rendering pipeline behind a feature flag.

## Changes

1. Find where the assistant prose renderer branches on isStreaming today (MessageContentFactory + ChatMessageComponents reasoning bubble at line ~618, and the main bubble's renderer.Render call at line ~313)
2. Replace the streaming branch's plain Text(message.content) with:
   if (BuildConfig.JEWEL_STREAMING_MARKDOWN_ENABLED) StreamingMarkdownText(bubbleId = message.id, text = message.content, ...) else Text(message.content, ...)
3. Add BuildConfig field via gradle (default true in dev, configurable for release)
4. The non-streaming path remains MarkdownText (mikepenz) — math, mermaid, syntax highlight all preserved
5. Stream-end transition: now Jewel formatted → mikepenz formatted, both visually equivalent → snap should disappear

## Acceptance

4. Pinch-to-zoom still smooth on streaming bubbles (5e0f.r3 not regressed)
5. Chunk-easing (d2z6.s1, 60ms tween) still applies and feels right

title: task(chat/jewel): on-device validation + stress test + build size delta

Final verification step before closing the c8of arc.

close_reason: Pre-empted by c8of-level pivot: Jewel publishes only JVM/Desktop artifacts (verified via POM + gradle module metadata — see c8of notes). Cannot be consumed by an Android module. Approach pivoting to ALT-2 (commonmark-java direct + boundary-aware streaming) tracked under c8of.
