title: [infra] Kotlin incremental cache daemon bug — app unit tests flake with 'Could not close incremental caches' on daemonized runs
description:
Discovered during gb57.3 implementation (2026-04-24). When running `./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest` on the default Gradle daemon with Kotlin incremental compilation enabled, builds intermittently fail with:

    Could not close incremental caches...

The code and tests themselves are fine. Workaround confirmed working:

    ./gradlew --no-daemon -Dkotlin.incremental=false :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests '<Fqcn>'

Scope:
- Identify root cause (likely Kotlin 2.x incremental compiler cache contention with parallel tasks, or a stale cache state bug)
- Options: pin/upgrade Kotlin version, disable incremental for :app test task only, clear cache on demand, or add a documented reset script
- Evaluate if the same flake has been hitting CI (review recent build logs for the signature)
- Add detection: if CI hits this error, emit a specific label/annotation so we don't chase it as a test failure

Does NOT affect correctness — only reliability of local test runs and potentially CI flakiness.
acceptance_criteria:
- Root cause identified and documented
- Either fix merged OR reliable workaround scripted (e.g., `make test-app` wrapper that applies the workaround flags)
- CI build logs audited for same signature; if present, mitigation added to workflow
- Developer docs updated with guidance

title: [security][bot-module] Migrate BotServerProfileStore authToken from plain DataStore to encrypted prefs
description:
Discovered during gb57.3 settings UI work (2026-04-24). BotServerProfileStore currently persists authToken in plain DataStore JSON. All new token storage paths (including Client Mode API key in gb57.3) use EncryptedPrefsHelper / EncryptedSharedPreferences — but the existing store path remains a latent issue for any user who has already configured a remote bot profile.

Scope:
- Migrate existing authToken fields in BotServerProfileStore to encrypted storage
- Handle one-time migration from plain DataStore → encrypted prefs on first launch after upgrade
- Add a migration test covering: existing plain token → moved to encrypted + cleared from plain store
- Verify no regression in ExternalBotClient HTTP/SSE auth path

Out of scope: touching Client Mode (gb57.x) storage — that already uses encrypted prefs from day one.
acceptance_criteria:
- authToken no longer persisted in plain DataStore for new or migrated users
- Upgrade path preserves existing configured tokens (moved, not lost)
- Regression test for ExternalBotClient auth
- Security audit note: only encrypted storage used for bearer tokens across the bot module

title: [client-mode][infra] Decide + implement external exposure for lettabot (letta-proxy extension vs. pangolin route)
description:
Current state: lettabot runs on 192.168.50.90:8407 with no reverse proxy. letta-proxy (8289→8283) is letta-core-only.

Decision required between:
- Option A: Extend letta-proxy to route /api/v1/agent-gateway (WS upgrade) + /api/v1/chat* + /api/v1/status to lettabot:8407. Pros: single ingress. Cons: requires ws upgrade handling.
- Option B: Add a pangolin route (pangolin stack already on this host) exposing lettabot on its own hostname with TLS. Pros: cleaner separation, TLS included. Cons: second ingress surface.

Recommend Option B unless there's a strong reason to consolidate.

Not blocking — Client Mode implementation can start against the direct LAN address. This bead lands before we ship Client Mode to anyone outside LAN.
acceptance_criteria:
- Decision recorded in docs/ with rationale
- Chosen path implemented + smoke tested (curl + wscat from outside LAN through the new route)
- TLS-terminated
- Auth preserved (bearer header passthrough)
- Android client default URL updated to the new external host in settings (once stable)
notes: Deferred 2026-04-24 by PM — no concrete outside-LAN requirement for Client Mode. LAN at 192.168.50.90:8407 sufficient for current dogfooding. Re-open when 'ship Client Mode externally' requirement materializes. Recommended path when re-opened: Option B (pangolin route) per bead spec.

title: [client-mode] Integration test — Android app ↔ local lettabot WS gateway end-to-end
description:
Stand up a lettabot test fixture (or point at the host's :8407) and run an end-to-end test:
- App in Client Mode with configured URL + key
- Send 'hello' → receive streamed assistant response
- Verify tool_call + tool_result events render
- Verify abort cancels mid-stream
- Verify reconnect after network drop preserves conversation_id

Prefer using the device-ready bootstrap gate (s0xx, just merged in PR #12) + the verify-release orchestrator (7973, PR #13) to make this reproducible on CI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Integration test can run locally against lettabot on 192.168.50.90:8407
- Test passes on CI emulator with a spun-up lettabot fixture (or is marked manual if fixture is infra-heavy)
- Flake rate <2% across 20 consecutive runs
notes:
Scope amended 2026-04-24: integration test must cover (a) defects fixed in frbh (tool calls + OFF-path 3-cycle), (b) multi-connection switching from bfj0 (configure 2-3 connections, switch via chips, verify each routes correctly), (c) settings migration from jo2p (legacy single-config → connection list, idempotent). Depends on: frbh, jo2p, tmhj, bfj0, gb57.5, gb57.6 — i.e. is the final integration gate before the epic closes.
AMENDED 2026-04-25: this becomes the acceptance test for the new Matrix-style routing model (gb57.9 + gb57.10 + gb57.11 + gb57.12). Test must cover: global pane config, master toggle gating, per-agent routing toggle, multiplexed sends to multiple agents, demux of inbound events.

title: [client-mode] Connection status indicator + error surfacing for Client Mode
description:
When Client Mode is on, surface the WS connection state to the user. Minimum: a small indicator in the chat top bar (CONNECTING / READY / RECONNECTING / ERROR). Error toasts for AUTH_FAILED, SESSION_BUSY, SESSION_INIT_FAILED with actionable copy ('Check API key in Settings').

Should consume WsBotClient's exposed StateFlow<ConnectionState>.
acceptance_criteria:
- Indicator reflects real WS state within 500ms of transition
- AUTH_FAILED shows 'Invalid API key — open Settings' with a deeplink
- SESSION_BUSY shows 'Another client is using this agent — try abort'
- Indicator hidden entirely when Client Mode is OFF
notes:
Scope amended 2026-04-24: multi-connection refactor (jo2p + tmhj) reshapes this bead.

1. Indicator queries the active client from tmhj's ClientModePool, not WsBotClient directly
2. Indicator hidden when selectedLettabotConnectionId is null (direct mode)
3. Indicator visible per-selection — switching connections via bfj0 chips updates the indicator to reflect the newly-active client's state
4. AUTH_FAILED deeplink still navigates to BotSettingsRoute, but Settings UI is now the connection-list view (jo2p) — deeplink should ideally focus the failing connection's row for editing. Stretch goal; basic deeplink-to-settings is acceptable
5. Now depends on: frbh, jo2p, tmhj. Wait for those before scheduling.

Original bead body remains the design north star for the indicator UI itself (states, copy, color tokens). The wiring underneath shifts.
Status reset to open 2026-04-24: dispatch was rerouted to letta-mobile-frbh (P1 defects) before any work on gb57.5 began. Resumes after frbh + jo2p + tmhj land, with the multi-connection scope amendment applied.
FOLD INTO gb57.9 (Global LettaBot connection settings pane). The connection status indicator + error surfacing is now part of the global settings pane work. Keeping this issue open as a checklist item under gb57.9 — when gb57.9 lands with status indicator implemented and error toasts/banners wired, close this.

title: [client-mode] Multimodal passthrough — send MessageContentItem[] as JSON content string
description:
The lettabot WS gateway auto-parses JSON arrays of {type:'text'|'image', ...} in the message.content string (see parseMultimodalContent in ws-gateway.ts). When the user sends an image attachment in Client Mode, serialize the outgoing content as this array format instead of plain text.

Touch points:
- WsBotClient.sendMessage / streamMessage — accept either String or MessageContentItem[] and serialize appropriately
- Attachment pipeline in the existing chat send path — ensure images are converted to the expected shape (base64 image_url or similar — match the SDK MessageContentItem format)
acceptance_criteria:
- Image attachment sent in Client Mode is received as inline image by lettabot (verify agent actually receives it — tool call that sees the image)
- Text-only messages continue to send as plain strings (no behavior change)
- Unit test for the serialization branch
notes: Scope amended 2026-04-24: multimodal sends now route through the currently-selected LettaBot connection's WsBotClient (from tmhj's pool), not 'the' WS client. WsBotClient API surface for multimodal stays the same, just resolved per-connection. Now depends on: frbh, jo2p, tmhj.

title: [epic] Agent list hydrate doesn't scale past ~200 agents — add pagination or progress streaming
description:
Root cause filed while diagnosing letta-mobile-2qt2. Server state drifted from 135 agents (2026-04-23T12:40Z) to 210 agents (2026-04-23T21:14Z) in 8 hours. Server-side endpoint /v1/agents returns ~56KB per agent (nested blocks, memory, tools, identities, sources, model_settings all inlined), so full-list response is now >10MB.

App symptoms (AgentListViewModel):
- hydrate latency scales linearly with agent count
- 'AgentList hydrated count=N' telemetry marker only fires after full-list state commit
- For users with many agents (especially admin-scoped users on servers with many tenant agents), the blocking hydrate produces noticeable lag on first open
- verify-device-ready bootstrap gate timed out at 30s with 210 agents — mitigated tactically in 2qt2 by raising to 120s, but that's symptom-treatment

Proposed scope:
1. Paginate GET /v1/agents at the client — fetch in pages of 50, stream into view
2. Or: have app request a lightweight agent summary (id + name + tags only) for list view; hydrate full agent objects lazily when user taps
3. Or: emit interim progress markers from AgentListViewModel ('AgentList hydrating... partial count=N') every 5s so bootstrap gates and users can observe progress
4. Re-baseline verify-device-ready agent-list timeout policy once pagination lands — can drop back to 30s if marker fires on first-page-complete

Acceptance:
- Agent list first-page render within 3s for any tenant size up to 5000 agents
- Bootstrap gate no longer needs arbitrarily long agent-list-hydrate timeout
- Memory footprint doesn't balloon on tenants with thousands of agents

Related:
- letta-mobile-2qt2 (tactical bootstrap timeout bump, symptom-treatment)
- letta-mobile-o7ob.2 (perf epic — candidate parent if this grows)
- Server-side: consider emitting lightweight /v1/agents?summary=true endpoint to reduce response payload

Priority P2: user-visible but not release-blocker; affects anyone with large agent tenant counts.
notes: Reopened after user reported searching 'meridian' still returned no agents. Root cause likely server ignoring offset pagination, causing duplicate first page and an incomplete searchable cache. Added no-progress fallback to count-sized full fetch in AgentRepository and AdminAgentManager.
close_reason: Fixed follow-up Meridian search miss: AgentRepository/AdminAgentManager now detect ignored offset pagination and fall back to a count-sized full fetch, so late agents enter the searchable cache even when /v1/agents repeats the first page. AgentList also renders a matching favorite during search instead of excluding it from both favorite and normal sections. Verified AgentRepository tests, AgentListViewModel tests, bootstrap shell syntax, root debug assemble, install/launch on 100.79.179.71:5555, and empty AndroidRuntime logs.

title: [bug] verify-sync flakes on first sample during post-bootstrap warm-up window
description:
Observed in Phase 4 final run 2026-04-23T16-03-03Z: verify-release reported verify-sync FAIL with 5/6 HEALTHY even though verify-device-ready had already reported Timeline ready.

Evidence:
- report: reports/verify-release-2026-04-23T16-03-03Z.md
- first failing sample occurs during the initial post-bootstrap warm-up window
- counterfactual pre-s8o4 APK run showed CLIENT_CURSOR=MISSING/BROKEN for the first 5 samples, then recovered to HEALTHY on the final sample

Root-cause hypothesis:
bootstrap readiness currently fires at first successful timeline hydrate, but ChannelHeartbeatSync has not necessarily processed its first heartbeat yet. The persisted processed::<conversation> cursor in SharedPreferences may not exist or may still lag behind server activity when verify-sync sampling starts.

Fix candidates:
- Option A: add a warm-up/discard window to verify-sync before samples count toward pass/fail
- Option B: extend bootstrap-device.sh readiness to wait for the first ChannelHeartbeatSync processed cursor before returning
- Option C: add an explicit sync-gate-ready app marker that verify-sync polls before sampling

Recommendation:
Option B. Keep verify-sync semantics pure and make device bootstrap guarantee the app has reached gate-ready state before sampling starts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Reproduce the warm-up flake on the target Pixel after verify-device-ready.
2. Prove whether the missing/lagging processed::<conversation> cursor is the immediate cause of the first-sample failure.
3. Land a fix (prefer bootstrap readiness over gate grace-window) that yields 6/6 HEALTHY on the post-bootstrap verify-sync gate for the target conversation.
4. Reference this issue in release gate policy/advisory metadata until fixed.

title: Gate promotion/demotion policy + docs/RELEASE-GATE-LEDGER.md
description:
Codify the promotion/demotion rules for verify-* gates so blocking vs advisory status follows evidence, not vibes.

Rules (from parent epic o5pp):
  - Advisory → Blocking: after 3 consecutive green releases with the gate PASSing on blocking data.
  - Blocking → Advisory: when flake rate > 10% in a 30-day window, paired with a root-cause bead.
  - New gates default to advisory for 3 releases before being eligible for promotion.

Deliverable:
  docs/RELEASE-GATE-LEDGER.md — a single source of truth listing:
    - Each gate's current status (blocking/advisory/skipped).
    - History of promotion/demotion events with dates + reasons.
    - Current flake rate if available.
    - Owner bead for any ongoing work (e.g. r6j3 owns the internal-gate repairs).
  docs/RELEASE.md — link to the ledger, remove stale per-target status docs.
  Makefile verify-release target — updates ledger on each run (via 7973).

Done when:
  - Ledger file exists, is current, and the release process references it.
  - Promotion/demotion is documented as a bead-file-required change, not a code change.

Parent: letta-mobile-o5pp

title: Build adb/uiautomator chat smoke test for send + receive
description: Build a standalone device-level smoke test that drives the installed APK via adb/uiautomator selectors and validates black-box chat health. This should prove that release verification can confirm send/receive without depending on internal cursor/state gates.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Runs as a standalone script (not Gradle-connected-test) against an installed APK.
2. Uses stable selectors from the test-tag bead to: enter text, trigger send, wait for assistant reply render, and detect visible UI errors (toast/snackbar or equivalent failure surface).
3. Produces non-zero exit code on failure and useful terminal output on each assertion.
4. Covers at minimum the Pixel 9 Pro and Pixel 2 XL ADB-connected test beds.
notes: Dependency: requires the stable chat test-tag vocabulary first. Keep scope focused on black-box send/receive health; do not mix in visual regression testing.

title: Add stable test tags to chat composer and message surfaces
description: Add stable UI identifiers for the chat surface so black-box automation can target the installed APK without screenshot heuristics. Define the tag vocabulary up front and apply it consistently across the main chat screen.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Introduces a documented tag/semantics vocabulary for at least: chat composer input, send button, attach button, message row container, message body, message role, and run-block gutter.
2. Applies those tags/semantics in the chat UI implementation, including the areas owned by ChatScreen.kt, ChatComposer, LettaInputBar, and related message row wrappers.
3. Leaves the tags stable enough for downstream adb/uiautomator and instrumentation selectors to rely on.
notes: Scope guard: no visual regression or screenshot testing work in this bead. This is the selector contract for later black-box smoke automation.

title: Release-verification automation — black-box UI smoke over brittle internal gates
description: Build a black-box release verification path that validates user-visible chat send/receive on installed APKs, reducing dependence on brittle internal-observability gates while coexisting with the r6j3 family of internal gate fixes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Defines a clear black-box release-verification strategy alongside, not instead of, r6j3 internal-gate work.
2. Has child beads for test tags, standalone adb/uiautomator smoke, release-flow integration, and post-smoke gate simplification evaluation.
3. Documents that this stream targets installed-APK verification, not dev-only connected test APKs.
notes: PM-approved on 2026-04-22 as a sibling stream to r6j3. Priority P2 because 1.2.6 shipped with manual smoke; this is the path to hands-off release verification.

title: Make scroll/composer macrobench scenarios deterministic on CI emulator
description:
## Context

During o7ob.4.1 (CI perf regression gate bring-up), scroll and composer macrobenchmarks failed on the canonical API 33 CI emulator with:

\`\`\`
At least one result is necessary, 0 found for frameDurationCpuMs
\`\`\`

Root cause: on a fresh emulator install, the app launches into `HomeRoute → ProjectHomeScreen` which can be an empty state with no guaranteed scrollable list or EditText. The benchmark scenario attempts to measure scroll/typing behavior against content that doesn't exist, so no frames matching `frameDurationCpuMs` are captured.

Startup benchmarks (cold/warm) work fine because they don't depend on populated screen state — they measure up-to-first-frame timing.

## Why this matters

The v1 CI perf gate (from o7ob.4.1) will only enforce startup cold/warm p95 because scroll/composer are not yet deterministic in this environment. That's a reasonable v1 but leaves two categories of user-experience regression unwatched:

- Scroll performance degradation in chat list
- Composer typing latency / keystroke-to-render

Both are documented in the Sentry transaction taxonomy (o7ob.3.1) and matter to actual users. The gate should eventually cover them.

## Resolution options (in rough preference order)

### Option A — Benchmark-owned seeded fixtures (recommended)

Add a `BenchmarkActivity` in the `macrobenchmark` target module (or an `@Before` setup in the scenario) that:

1. Pre-seeds the app DB with a known fixture (N chat messages for scroll, a composer-focused screen for typing)
2. Launches directly to the populated screen (bypass HomeRoute for benchmarks only)
3. Measures against that deterministic state

This keeps product launch UX decisions independent of benchmark determinism.

### Option B — Navigate-before-measure

Update scroll/composer scenarios to:
1. Launch normally through HomeRoute
2. Navigate to a known-populated screen (maybe via demo-content injection flag)
3. Only start macrobench measurement after the target surface is confirmed rendered

Drawback: extra navigation time counts against the measurement window and may be fragile if navigation behavior changes.

### Option C — First-run demo content

Ship demo content on fresh install so `ProjectHomeScreen` is never empty. This is a product decision and has user-facing implications; raise with Emmanuel before adopting.

## Done criteria

1. `ScrollJankBenchmark.scrollChatListCompilationPartial` runs successfully on the canonical CI emulator and emits valid `frameDurationCpuMs` measurements
2. `ComposerTypingBenchmark.typeComposerCompilationPartial` same
3. Both baselines seeded in `perf/baselines.json` (previously marked `null` + non-gating)
4. Both benchmarks added back to `PERF_BENCHMARK_FILTER` in `.github/workflows/android-perf.yml`
5. Both benchmarks enforce tolerances on PRs (verified via regression-simulation test)

## Non-goals

- Not changing product UX of the empty state
- Not requiring real user data in benchmark scenarios
- Not blocking on physical-device measurement (that's ce2c / wy3c)

## Related

- o7ob.4.1 — parent gate, currently ships startup-only v1 without this
- ce2c — Pixel 2 XL / physical-device slow-floor (independent)
- wy3c — BP sustainable CI generation path (independent)
- o7ob.3.1 — Sentry transaction taxonomy (defines what scroll/composer SHOULD measure)

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Note: letta-mobile-vtw9 was filed in parallel with this bead by OpenCode at essentially the same moment. Closed vtw9 as a duplicate; all tracking consolidated here. vtw9's framing was more prescriptive ("add a deterministic benchmark launch route"), which is a reasonable title variant for this problem — consider that phrasing when commits reference this bead.

title: Add deterministic benchmark launch route
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the API 33 emulator perf gate can now measure startup reliably, but ScrollJankBenchmark and ComposerTypingBenchmark fail on fresh CI emulators because the app launches to HomeRoute -> ProjectHomeScreen, which may be an empty state with no guaranteed scrollable list or editable composer. Add a deterministic benchmark-only launch route or fixture path so frame-timing benchmarks always land on an interactive surface in CI, then re-enable scroll/composer metrics in the perf gate and seed canonical baselines from the GitHub Actions API 33 emulator.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
Duplicate of letta-mobile-4ccv (filed at essentially the same moment by the PM agent).

Consolidating to 4ccv, which has more detailed resolution options and done criteria. The underlying problem statement is identical: make ScrollJankBenchmark and ComposerTypingBenchmark produce deterministic results on the canonical API 33 CI emulator so they can be re-added to the perf gate filter.

Closing this one; track work under 4ccv.

title: Flaky: ClientToolSyncTest intermittent UncaughtExceptionsBeforeTest failure
description:
## Symptom

`ClientToolSyncTest > syncTools attaches returned tool id` fails intermittently on Android APK CI with:

\`\`\`
kotlinx.coroutines.test.UncaughtExceptionsBeforeTest at ClientToolSyncTest.kt:15
\`\`\`

401 tests pass, 1 fails. Run-to-run flake; same commit can go green or red.

## Example failing run

https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/actions/runs/24749158249 (Android APK CI on feat/o7ob-4-1-perf-gate / commit 5ac4ef4, failed 2026-04-21 ~22:18 UTC). Earlier runs on the same PR (24746569994, 24746940750) were green on the same code.

## Likely root cause

`UncaughtExceptionsBeforeTest` is kotlinx.coroutines.test's signal that an exception was thrown in a coroutine launched BEFORE the test body ran — typically from:
- Unconfined coroutines spawned in a lazy Singleton that gets constructed by Hilt injection during setUp
- A class-level `StandardTestDispatcher` that starts collecting before `runTest` supervises it
- A dispatcher switch in an `init {}` or companion object that escapes the test scheduler

ClientToolSyncTest.kt:15 is the starting line — investigate whether a collaborator's init path launches an unsupervised coroutine.

## Why file separately

Discovered incidentally during `o7ob.4.1` perf-gate debugging. Not caused by perf-gate work — pre-existing flake. But intermittent green/red on core unit tests can silently mask real regressions, so worth fixing before it bites something more important.

## Done criteria

1. Root cause identified (which coroutine, launched where, throwing what)
2. Fix lands: either supervising the launch under the test scope, or removing the pre-test coroutine entirely
3. Test passes on 20 consecutive reruns of the same commit

title: Pixel 2 XL macrobench reliability: wireless ADB on API 30 times out before benchmark runs
description:
## Context
During letta-mobile-o7ob.2.5 (startup budget enforcement), macrobench runs targeting the Pixel 2 XL (192.168.50.202:5555, API 30) consistently fail with `ShellCommandUnresponsiveException` during install/instrumentation setup, before the benchmark itself starts.

## Root cause (per diagnostic 2026-04-21)
Three compounding factors:

1. **Network latency**: Pixel 2 XL on LAN shows 107–232ms ping with 52ms stddev. Not same-subnet shaped — likely different VLAN / AP / bridge than the Pixel 9 Pro. Macrobench's multi-chunk `am install-multiple` + long-lived `am instrument` session accumulates timeouts at this RTT.
2. **Discharging state**: `AC powered: false`, `USB powered: false`, battery level 94%. androidx.benchmark is hostile to discharging devices (thermal throttling inconsistency).
3. **API 30 wireless ADB bug**: AOSP has known reports of `ShellCommandUnresponsiveException` on API 30 wireless ADB with larger APKs. API 33+ rewrote the TCP ADB path.

## Consequence
Pixel 2 XL cannot currently serve as a slow-floor benchmark target, even though it's the right *class* of device for the role. Pixel 9 Pro is the only working macrobench target at time of filing.

## Options
1. **Physical USB attach**: bring Pixel 2 XL onto USB for benchmark runs. Kills latency + API-30 wireless bug. Requires physical access and keeping the device charged/tethered during CI.
2. **Swap reference device**: replace Pixel 2 XL with a newer mid-range device on API 33+ (e.g., Pixel 4a 5G, Pixel 6a). Preserves "slow floor" semantics, eliminates the API 30 bug.
3. **Firebase Test Lab for benchmarks**: run the macrobench slice in FTL against a physical Pixel device. Eliminates local device reliability entirely. Costs per run but is the CI-friendly answer.

## Related
- letta-mobile-o7ob.2.5 — startup budget (partially validated, Pixel 2 XL slow-floor measurement unverified)
- letta-mobile-o7ob.4.1 — CI perf regression gate (consumer — picks the canonical benchmark device)
- letta-mobile-wy3c — BP sustainable generation path (same infrastructure question)

## Done criteria
A reproducible, reliable benchmark run against a slow-floor reference device is possible, such that:
- `:macrobenchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest` completes without `ShellCommandUnresponsiveException` against the slow-floor target
- The slow-floor measurement is what CI enforces (via o7ob.4.1)
- The chosen approach is documented in `docs/performance/benchmark-device-strategy.md`

## Non-goals
- Does not block o7ob.2.5 from closing (engineering work is complete; this is an infra issue)
- Does not commit to any specific option — part of scope is evaluating the three

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
## Sharper diagnosis (2026-04-21 via OpenCode)

Original blocker framing (wireless ADB + discharging + API 30 wireless bug) was incomplete. After attaching Pixel 2 XL via USB and removing transport + power variables, the real failure isolated cleanly.

Actual failure signature:
- Device reachable over USB (711KPAE0914240)
- Both target APK and macrobenchmark test APK install manually
- Target package com.letta.mobile.benchmark launches
- APK contains assets/dexopt/baseline.prof + baseline.profm
- Manifest includes androidx.profileinstaller.ProfileInstallReceiver + ProfileInstallerInitializer
- MacrobenchmarkScope.dropShaderCache() fails with "The DROP_SHADER_CACHE broadcast was not received"
- Direct shell broadcast to com.letta.mobile.benchmark/androidx.profileinstaller.ProfileInstallReceiver returns result=0 instead of expected 14
- Reproduces outside Gradle (via direct am instrument), so not an AGP/UTP issue

Interpretation:
Specific AndroidX Macrobenchmark + profileinstaller + API 30 incompatibility. The receiver is present and responsive, but doesn't emit the benchmark-specific result codes that the 1.4.1 benchmark library expects. Two likely root causes (not yet distinguished):

1. Version mismatch: benchmark-macro-junit4 was bumped 1.3.4 -> 1.4.1 during o7ob.2.1 for Android 16 compatibility, but androidx.profileinstaller transitive version may lag and not emit the expected codes.
2. Pixel 2 XL / API 30 genuinely doesn't support the DROP_SHADER_CACHE broadcast pathway benchmark 1.4.1 relies on (API 31+ introduced the reliable mechanism).

Revised resolution options:
1. Upgrade androidx.profileinstaller explicitly to match or exceed benchmark 1.4.1's expected surface. Cheapest diagnostic.
2. Swap slow-floor target to a device on API 31+ (Pixel 4a 5G, Pixel 6a, etc.). Sidesteps the API 30 gap entirely.
3. Firebase Test Lab for the benchmark slice. Picks canonical benchmark hardware, eliminates local device quirks.

Recommendation: try option 1 first (cheap diagnostic - bump profileinstaller, re-run, see if result=14 appears). If that doesn't resolve it, option 2 is the right long-term answer since API 30 is fading anyway.

title: Baseline Profile: establish a sustainable API 33+ generation path (FTL / GMD with KVM / remote AS host)
description:
## Context
letta-mobile-o7ob.2.1 (Baseline Profile) ran into an environmental blocker: the only connected device on the build host (192.168.50.90) is a Pixel 2 XL API 30 non-rooted production build. BaselineProfileRule requires API 33+ or a rooted API 28+ device, neither of which is available here. The build host itself has no SDK tooling, no `/dev/kvm`, and no emulator images.

## Scope
Establish a sustainable, automatable path for running `:app:generateReleaseBaselineProfile` on an API 33+ surface. Options to evaluate:

1. **Remote Android Studio host (192.168.50.247)**: if KVM-capable, configure a Pixel 6 API 33 AVD there, expose via `adb connect` so build host can target it.
2. **Firebase Test Lab via AGP `firebaseTestLab` block**: offloads the API requirement entirely. Needs GCP project + service account.
3. **Gradle Managed Devices with a KVM-enabled build host**: migrate the build host or add a sidecar worker with `/dev/kvm`.
4. **Dev-machine manual generation**: run once on a dev box with emulation, check in `baseline-prof.txt`, schedule periodic regeneration. Simplest but least automated.

## Decision criteria
- Regeneration cadence (how often do critical UI paths change?)
- CI integration appetite (does o7ob.4.1 need BP in the macrobench gate?)
- Cost / infra complexity (FTL = $$$ over time, local = ops overhead)

## Related beads
- letta-mobile-o7ob.2.1 — Baseline Profile (parent work; may ship with short-term path)
- letta-mobile-o7ob.4.1 — CI perf regression gate (consumer of whatever path is chosen)

## Done criteria
- A documented, reproducible command that generates a valid baseline profile
- Profile artifact generation runs successfully end-to-end
- Path is compatible with CI (o7ob.4.1 can invoke it)
- `docs/performance/baseline-profile-generation.md` explaining the chosen path, trade-offs, and how to regenerate

## Non-goals
- This bead doesn't implement the BP generation logic itself (o7ob.2.1 owns that)
- Doesn't pick the winning path upfront — part of scope is evaluating the three viable options

title: Make URLs clickable in chat messages (autolink in MarkdownText)
description:
## Problem
URLs rendered inside chat messages are currently inert — they appear as plain text with no tap target. Users have to long-press → select → copy → paste into a browser to open a link from the app, which is friction for a messaging-shaped surface.

## Scope
Make URLs (and markdown-style `[label](url)` links) clickable in rendered chat message bodies. Tapping opens the URL via the platform's default handler (Chrome Custom Tabs preferred; fall back to `Intent.ACTION_VIEW`).

## Surface
Central rendering happens in:
- `android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/MarkdownText.kt`

Fixing it there propagates to every consumer:
- `ChatMessageComponents.kt`, `AgentScaffold.kt`, `MessageContentFactory.kt` (chat)
- `HomeScreen.kt` (dashboard)
- `MermaidDiagram.kt`, `MathBlock.kt` (if they render link-bearing text — verify)

## Approach
1. In `MarkdownText.kt`, build an `AnnotatedString` with `LinkAnnotation.Url` spans for:
   - Markdown-style `[text](url)` links (already parsed — wire to LinkAnnotation)
   - Bare URLs detected via `android.util.Patterns.WEB_URL` or `LinkifyCompat`
2. Use `LocalUriHandler.current` for handling clicks so Chrome Custom Tabs / default browser are respected.
3. Apply a subtle link style (underline + primary color) consistent with the existing theme.
4. Respect `@Composable` stability — don't regress recomposition (perf-adjacent, since `o7ob.2.6` already audited ChatScreen recomposition).
5. Long-press should still offer copy — preserve existing selection behavior.

## Edge cases
- Don't double-linkify URLs that are already inside a markdown `[label](url)` — the markdown parse should win
- Punctuation at URL tail (`...check https://example.com.` — don't include trailing `.`)
- Unicode / percent-encoded URLs
- Email addresses (`mailto:`) — optional scope bump if trivial
- Don't linkify inside code blocks / inline code — those are literal

## Done criteria
- Tapping a URL in any chat message opens it externally
- Markdown `[text](url)` links work as clickable regions
- Unit test(s) asserting AnnotatedString contains LinkAnnotation.Url at expected ranges
- Screenshot or test verifying code blocks don't get linkified
- `:designsystem:compileDebugKotlin` + `:app:compileDebugKotlin` clean
- `make lint-telemetry` + `make verify-telemetry` still clean

## Non-goals
- No new telemetry events (simple user-action; if desired later, can slot into existing UI event family)
- No deep-link routing (external browser only for now)
- No custom in-app browser

## Notes
Filed while `letta-mobile-o7ob.3.1` (Sentry Performance taxonomy) is in flight. Safe to run in parallel — disjoint surfaces.

title: observability: CI smoke test that asserts core Telemetry events fire on app launch
description:
Telemetry can silently regress: a refactor removes a Telemetry.event
call, unit tests still pass, and suddenly Grafana shows a flat line.
Add a small smoke test to catch this.

## Proposed

Instrumented test (Robolectric or connected) that:

1. Launches MainActivity with a mocked backend returning a small
   conversation.
2. Navigates into the chat screen.
3. Taps send once.
4. Asserts that within 5s, the Telemetry ring contains all of:
   - `ChatPushService/created`
   - `TimelineSync/hydrate.ok` (or legacy `hydrate`)
   - `TimelineSync/send.post`
   - `TimelineSync/send.firstEvent`
   - `TimelineSync/streamSubscriber.opened` (with a real run)

The test should NOT assert no-error events — that would be too
brittle. The point is positive-event coverage.

## Why not unit tests?

Unit tests assert specific code paths. They cannot catch
'someone accidentally wired up the wrong viewmodel and none of the
events fire'. A single integration smoke gives us that guardrail.

## Acceptance

- New test file under `android-compose/app/src/androidTest/` or
  equivalent Robolectric location.
- Runs under `make verify-sync` (or a sibling `make verify-telemetry`).
- CI runs it as part of the existing Android-test matrix.

title: observability: onTrimMemory + memory-pressure telemetry
description:
`Activity.onTrimMemory` and `ComponentCallbacks2.onTrimMemory` fire
repeatedly before OOM and during background transitions. We currently
do nothing with them. Capturing the signal lets Grafana correlate
'frequent TRIM_RUNNING_LOW on device X' with 'app was crashed on
device X' — the leading indicator for leak regressions.

## Proposed

In `LettaApplication` register a `ComponentCallbacks2` and emit:

```
Telemetry.event(
    "App", "trimMemory",
    "level" to levelName,  // COMPLETE | MODERATE | BACKGROUND | RUNNING_CRITICAL | ...
    "runtimeUsedBytes" to Debug.getNativeHeapAllocatedSize(),
)
```

Also emit `App.lowMemory` for the simpler legacy callback.

## Acceptance

- `LettaApplication` registers a ComponentCallbacks2.
- Events emitted with the canonical `App` tag.
- Dashboard panel: trimMemory events per device per hour; p95 heap
  usage at TRIM_RUNNING_LOW time.
- Alert: sustained > 5 `TRIM_RUNNING_LOW` / 10min on one device.

title: observability: ViewModel load.start/ok/failed coverage + AdminChatVM tag normalization
description:
Most non-chat ViewModels emit zero telemetry. If a screen hangs on load
or shows an empty state, the only signal today is user reports. Add
`<Screen>.load.start / ok / failed` spans to each hot ViewModel so
Grafana can surface blank-screen-on-load regressions within hours of
release.

## Targets (in rough usage order)

- `DashboardViewModel` — landing screen; blank dashboard is user-
  visible and has been reported before.
- `AgentListViewModel` — agent picker; blank picker blocks all chat.
- `ConversationsViewModel` — chat list; same.
- `TemplatesViewModel` — template picker.
- `JobMonitorViewModel` — job/run list.
- `ConfigViewModel` / `ConfigListViewModel`.
- `ConversationPickerViewModel` (inside AgentScaffold).

## Naming convention

Per `docs/observability/conventions.md`:

- One tag per ViewModel, matching its class name minus the trailing
  `ViewModel` (e.g. `Dashboard`, `AgentList`, `Conversations`).
- Events: `load.start`, `load.ok` (with rowCount/durationMs),
  `load.failed` (via `Telemetry.error`).
- For pagination: `page.fetched` (page, rowCount).

## Normalization

`AdminChatViewModel.kt` emits under both `AdminChatVM` and
`AdminChatViewModel`. Must be consolidated to `AdminChatVM` as part of
this bead — see `docs/observability/conventions.md §2`.

## Acceptance

- Every VM above has `load.start/.ok/.failed` wired.
- `AdminChatViewModel` normalized to the single `AdminChatVM` tag.
- Unit tests for one representative VM (e.g. DashboardViewModel) that
  assert the events fire.
- Grafana panel: "Screen load failure rate" pivoting on tag.
notes: Scope note: the 'AdminChatVM tag normalization' half was completed opportunistically as part of letta-mobile-3hnh (commit ead6572). Remaining scope is purely the ViewModel load.start/ok/failed coverage for DashboardViewModel / AgentListViewModel / ConversationsViewModel / TemplatesViewModel / JobMonitorViewModel / ConfigViewModel / ConfigListViewModel / ConversationPickerViewModel.

title: observability: TelemetryInterceptor richer attributes (requestId, retry, cache)
description:
TelemetryInterceptor today records {method, path, status, durationMs, bytes}. Make it answer richer questions:
  - correlate server traces via X-Request-Id (add attr 'requestId' from response header if present)
  - flag SSE vs JSON responses (attr 'contentType')
  - count OkHttp cache hits vs misses (CacheControl header)
  - on retry via OkHttp RetryAndFollowUpInterceptor, attach 'retryCount'
  - split 4xx/5xx into separate dashboard series

Acceptance:
  - TelemetryInterceptor.kt edits.
  - Update docs/observability/ with new attribute contract.
  - Panel: p50/p95 latency split by path prefix (/v1/conversations vs /v1/runs etc.).
  - Alert: 5xx rate > 1% over 5m.

title: observability: ConnectivityManager network-state telemetry
description:
We currently infer network state from the idle404 backoff pattern on the subscriber. That's indirect. Subscribe to ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback and emit canonical events so Grafana can correlate 'subscriber wedge' with 'phone just lost Wi-Fi'.

Proposed events:
  Net.available  (transport=wifi|cell|eth|vpn, metered=bool)
  Net.lost
  Net.capabilitiesChanged
  Net.validated (server reachable)

Acceptance:
  - New class NetworkTelemetry registered in ApplicationModule.
  - Panel: network-up minutes vs subscriber-idle404 count per conversation — they should correlate.
  - Alert: > 5 Net.lost / hour on the same device is a harness issue, not real.

title: [EPIC] Observability expansion — broaden Telemetry beyond TimelineSync
description:
## Context

mge5.5 (release gate) + mge5.6 (StreamSubscriber telemetry) gave us
structured observability for the sync path. Within 60s of first live
dogfooding, the pair surfaced + diagnosed a real P0 wedge (gqz3). Clear
ROI.

But the coverage is lopsided — TimelineSync has ~30 distinct events,
everything else has close to zero. There are large swaths of the app
where a bug would die silently in Log.w or not surface at all.

## Current state

Unique `(tag, name)` pairs emitted today:

- TimelineSync: 30+ (delivery path — well covered)
- Timeline:    4  (timeline data structure invariants)
- TimelineRepo: 3
- Http: 2  (generic request/request:failed via TelemetryInterceptor)
- ChatPushService: 4
- AdminChatVM:  3 (send routing)
- AdminChatViewModel: 3 (older-shape emission — same file, inconsistent tag)
- ChatComposerAttach: 4

Everything else emits nothing, including:
- LettaApplication.onCreate (cold-start timing, init failures)
- CrashReporter.install / the crash handler itself
- Sentry transport success/failure
- ChannelHeartbeatScheduler / BotHeartbeatScheduler
- BotServiceAutoStarter
- NavViewModel / DashboardViewModel / AgentListViewModel /
  ConversationsViewModel / TemplatesViewModel / JobMonitorViewModel /
  ConfigViewModel / ConversationPickerViewModel
- AuthRepository (login, token refresh, session expiry)
- Connectivity (network up/down, wifi/cell transitions)
- FCM / push token lifecycle
- Battery / doze / background-execution restrictions
- Memory pressure (onTrimMemory)
- Coil image cache hits/misses
- Sentry event uploads
- StrictMode violations (currently we just log them — should count them)

## Proposed children (prioritized)

### High value (near-term wins)

1. **gqz3.1 — Cold-start + lifecycle telemetry.** Instrument
   LettaApplication.onCreate to emit `App.onCreate.start/end` with
   durationMs. Emit `App.init.failed` for each heartbeat/autostart
   failure that currently goes to Log.w. This catches regressions where
   process start slows down or a critical scheduler fails silently.

2. **gqz3.2 — Crash + Sentry transport telemetry.** Emit events when
   CrashReporter.install runs, when a persisted crash is found on next
   launch, when Sentry begins/finishes uploading, and when uploads fail.
   Right now if Sentry itself is broken we have zero signal.

3. **gqz3.3 — Auth / session telemetry.** Emit `Auth.login.success`,
   `Auth.login.failed`, `Auth.token.refreshStart/Ok/Failed`,
   `Auth.session.expired`. Currently if someone gets silently logged out
   we only see the downstream 401s from TelemetryInterceptor.

4. **gqz3.4 — Connectivity telemetry.** Subscribe to
   ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback and emit `Net.available`,
   `Net.lost`, `Net.changed` with transport (WIFI/CELL/VPN). Pair with
   TimelineSync idle404 data to answer "was the subscriber backing off
   because the network was down?"

5. **gqz3.5 — TelemetryInterceptor upgrade.** Current emission is
   path-only; add `requestId` (X-Request-Id), cache-hit flag,
   SSE-vs-JSON discrimination, and retry count. Roll up 4xx/5xx
   separately in Grafana.

### Medium value

6. **gqz3.6 — ViewModel coverage for hot screens.** AdminChatViewModel
   has good coverage but uses two different tags (`AdminChatVM` and
   `AdminChatViewModel`) — normalize. DashboardViewModel,
   AgentListViewModel, ConversationsViewModel should emit
   `*.load.start/ok/failed` spans. This catches blank-screen-on-load
   bugs.

7. **gqz3.7 — StrictMode counter.** Right now StrictMode violations
   emit one fat log per violation. Add a counter so Grafana can alert
   on "dev build regression: spike in leaked closables."

8. **gqz3.8 — Memory pressure / onTrimMemory.** Emit
   `App.trimMemory` with the level. Correlate with crash rate — leaks
   usually correlate with frequent TRIM_RUNNING_LOW before the OOM.

### Low value / exploratory

9. **gqz3.9 — Battery + doze telemetry.** PowerManager idle-mode
   transitions, background-job scheduling outcomes. Useful only once
   we do real push work on background.

10. **gqz3.10 — Coil + resource telemetry.** Image cache hits/misses,
    disk usage, thumbnail decode time. Useful if/when image-loading
    bugs resurface.

## Non-goals

- Pushing to a remote backend. The existing logcat+inspector pipeline is
  enough for now; Grafana consumption via Loki is already documented in
  docs/observability/stream-subscriber.md and works for any tag.
- Adding a new Telemetry API surface. Current `Telemetry.event` + `span`
  + `error` cover every need here.

## Definition of done

- At least gqz3.1 through gqz3.5 shipped and wired into Grafana
  dashboards/alerts alongside the existing StreamSubscriber dashboard.
- A new doc `docs/observability/coverage-matrix.md` listing every
  (tag, name) pair and its owner.
- CI smoke test that launches the app, navigates to chat, and asserts
  a minimum set of telemetry events fire (as a guard against silent
  regression of telemetry itself).

title: fix(nav): bottom-bar tap targets only ~190dp instead of full 1/3 strip
description:
## Problem

The `LettaBottomBar` in `AdaptiveScaffold.kt` uses `Arrangement.SpaceEvenly`
on its inner `Row`, which sizes each `LettaBottomBarItem` to its content
width (icon + label, ~190dp). This leaves the rest of each 1/3 strip as
dead space — taps in the wide gaps between items (and past the Admin
label on the right edge of phones) do nothing.

## Fix

- Drop `Arrangement.SpaceEvenly` on the inner Row.
- Apply `Modifier.weight(1f)` to each `LettaBottomBarItem` so each item
  claims an exact 1/3 of the bar width.
- Existing `Modifier.fillMaxHeight()` + `clickable {}` on the item's
  Column already make the full strip a hit target once the column is
  weight-sized.

## Surface

- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AdaptiveScaffold.kt`
  - `LettaBottomBar`: drop `horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceEvenly`,
    pass `Modifier.weight(1f)` to each item.
  - `LettaBottomBarItem`: add comment explaining the click-area contract.

## Discovered during

`letta-mobile-m772` recovery — was bundled in the WIP stash alongside the
chat run-block scaffolding. Splitting it out into its own bead so the
m772 commits stay focused.
notes: Implemented in commit 1faae8a on branch recovery/m772-from-stash (PR https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/1)

title: test(chat): AdminChatViewModelTest.loadOlderMessages skips fetch — pre-existing red on dirty tree
description:
## Symptom

`:app:testDebugUnitTest --max-workers=1` is RED on the current working tree due to a single failure:

- Test: `com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.AdminChatViewModelTest.loadOlderMessages skips fetch when initial page proves there is no older history`
- File: `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModelTest.kt` (around line 414)
- Result: 309 of 310 tests pass; only this one fails.

## Triage

- The test file is **not** dirty in the working tree. Only `AdminChatViewModel.kt` is modified (m772-era changes).
- Diffing the modified `AdminChatViewModel.kt` against HEAD shows no changes touching `hasMoreOlderMessages`, `loadOlderMessages`, or `fetchOlderMessages`. The failure is therefore not caused by in-flight m772 work — it is pre-existing on this branch.
- Last commits touching the test file: `e80dc0a` (23h5), `ce07491` (o7ob.2.6), `c4576b1` (Phase 5 cleanup, letta-mobile-844e), `d6edd43` (timeline-sync Phase 1-3 integration). The Phase 5 cleanup explicitly removed sibling `loadOlderMessages` tests because the `hasMoreOlderMessages` signal moved from legacy `fetchMessages` pre-hydrate into `TimelineRepository` pagination — see the comment block at lines 406-411 of the test file.
- The remaining test asserts: when the initial page contains a single message, the VM should NOT call `messageRepository.fetchOlderMessages(...)` and `uiState.hasMoreOlderMessages` should be `false`. After the timeline-sync rewrite, the `hasMoreOlderMessages` signal flow has changed and this expectation no longer matches behavior.

## Surfaced by

`letta-mobile-08hs` (Gradle build cache spike) ran the full test suite and observed this failure. It is not introduced by 08hs — 08hs reverted all of its changes.

## Acceptance

- Decide whether the test should be:
  1. Updated to match new `TimelineRepository` pagination semantics (signal now flows from Timeline rather than `fetchMessages` pre-hydrate), or
  2. Removed and folded into `MessageRepositoryE2eTest` / TimelineRepository tests like the sibling `loadOlderMessages prepends older history` cases were in Phase 5 (see comment block at lines 406-411).
- Suite returns to green: `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 :app:testDebugUnitTest` passes 310/310.

## Notes

- Reproducible without daemon, without cache, with `--max-workers=1`. Not flaky — fails deterministically.
- Related Phase 5 commit: `c4576b1` and the in-file comment block at lines 401-411 explaining the prior removals.
close_reason: Resolved by deleting stale @Test 'loadOlderMessages skips fetch when initial page proves there is no older history' from AdminChatViewModelTest.kt. The test asserted the pre-23h5 contract (skip fetch + hasMoreOlderMessages=false on short page), which the 23h5 regression fix in AdminChatViewModel.kt deliberately inverted (timeline observer now optimistically flips hasMoreOlderMessages=true; loadOlderMessages settles via OLDER_MESSAGES_PAGE_SIZE downstream). Page-size pagination semantics remain covered by MessageRepositoryE2eTest. Replaced with Phase-5-style comment block citing rationale + follow-up bead letta-mobile-page-hasMore. Full :app:testDebugUnitTest green: 309/309, 0 failures. Commit 0039476 pushed to main.

title: Tag and segregate test tiers (unit / integration / screenshot)
description:
# Tag and segregate test tiers

## Context

Discovered during test-suite performance review. The `:app` test suite currently runs **all tests** (pure Kotest, MockK ViewModel tests, Robolectric + Compose integration tests, and soon-to-be-added snapshot tests) under a single `:app:testDebugUnitTest` task.

This creates two problems:

1. **Dev feedback loop**: a dev editing a pure-function classifier (like m772.5's RunStepClassification) has to wait for Robolectric tests (~2-5s each of Android-runtime setup) to run alongside their ~50ms unit test.
2. **CI cost**: PR checks run the full suite even for docs-only or config-only changes.

## Scope

Introduce a three-tier tagging system using Kotest tags + JUnit5 tags (for Robolectric/JUnit4 tests via the platform), and add Gradle tasks for each tier.

### Tier definition

| Tier | Marker | Typical Latency | Runs On |
|---|---|---|---|
| **unit** | `@Tags(\"unit\")` (Kotest) or `@Tag(\"unit\")` (JUnit5) | <50ms per test | every commit, pre-push hook |
| **integration** | `@Tags(\"integration\")` | 100ms-5s per test (Robolectric + Compose) | PR push |
| **screenshot** | `@Tags(\"screenshot\")` | 500ms-3s per test (Paparazzi/Roborazzi) | PR push + release gate |

### Gradle tasks

```kotlin
tasks.register(\"testUnit\", Test::class) {
    useJUnitPlatform {
        includeTags(\"unit\")
    }
    // Kotest tag filter via system property
    systemProperty(\"kotest.tags.include\", \"unit\")
}

tasks.register(\"testIntegration\", Test::class) {
    useJUnitPlatform {
        includeTags(\"integration\")
    }
    systemProperty(\"kotest.tags.include\", \"integration\")
}
```

### Tagging pass

All existing tests need explicit tags. Default assumption:
- `RunBlockStreamingTest`, `AdminChatViewModelTest`, `DashboardViewModelTest`, `ConversationsViewModelTest`, `ConfigViewModelTest`, `CrashReporterTest` → `integration`
- `MessageGroupingTest`, `RunStepDotIconTest`, `TimelineEventToUiMessageTest`, `MessageMapperTest`, all pure-logic `:core` tests → `unit`
- Future snapshot tests (m772.6 output) → `screenshot`

### CI wiring

Update `.github/workflows/android.yml` (or equivalent):

- `test-unit` job: fast, runs on every PR push, required check.
- `test-integration` job: PR push only, required check.
- `test-screenshot` job: PR push only, required check, can run in parallel.
- Release pipeline runs `check` (all tiers) + baseline profile regen.

## Acceptance

- Kotest + JUnit5 tag discovery working — `./gradlew :app:testUnit` runs only tagged unit tests; `./gradlew :app:testIntegration` runs only integration tests.
- All existing `:app`, `:core`, `:bot`, `:designsystem` tests tagged.
- `./gradlew check` still runs everything (back-compat).
- CI workflow updated to run tiers as separate jobs (optional; can be a follow-up if the Gradle task split lands first).
- README or TESTING.md section documenting the tier contract for future test authors.

## Non-goals

- No test deletion or migration between tiers beyond tagging.
- No new test infrastructure (Paparazzi/Roborazzi decision is separate via m772.6).
- No changes to `androidTest` source set.

## Risk notes

**Low risk.** Tagging is purely additive. Existing `testDebugUnitTest` task keeps working unchanged. CI changes can be landed incrementally.

## File hints

- android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts (add tasks)
- android-compose/core/build.gradle.kts (add tasks)
- android-compose/bot/build.gradle.kts (add tasks)
- android-compose/designsystem/build.gradle.kts (add tasks)
- android-compose/app/src/test/**/* (tag application)
- .github/workflows/android.yml (CI wiring, optional follow-up)
- android-compose/TESTING.md (new or extend existing)
notes: Implemented tiered test tasks across app/core/bot/designsystem, tagged all 101 existing test files, added android-compose/TESTING.md, and verified with full dry-run plus :designsystem:testUnit smoke run using --max-workers=1. CI split left untouched per bead optional follow-up and memory constraints.

title: Time-boxed test: re-enable Gradle daemon + validate Kotlin IC stability
description:
# Time-boxed test: re-enable Gradle daemon

## Context

Discovered during test-suite performance review. `android-compose/gradle.properties`:

```properties
org.gradle.daemon=false
```

The comment attributes this to historical Kotlin daemon / IC 'stale registered-storage' failures between invocations. That was a real Kotlin IC bug — **largely fixed in Kotlin 2.0+**. This repo is on Kotlin 2.x (per `org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.compose` being a first-class plugin in the build).

Disabling the daemon globally costs **10-15s of startup time** per invocation (JVM spin-up, Gradle init, Kotlin compiler classloader warmup). For dev iteration this adds up fast.

## Scope — time-boxed spike, not a commitment

This is a **spike**, not a refactor. Budget: 2 hours. At the end, either:

1. **Success path**: flip daemon back on, document validation results, close.
2. **Failure path**: revert, document what broke, close with notes pointing at the specific Kotlin/Gradle/KSP version combo that still has the issue.

### Spike steps

1. Flip `org.gradle.daemon=true` (temporarily).
2. Run **20 consecutive** invocations of each of these tasks with small source edits between each:
   - `:app:compileDebugKotlin`
   - `:app:kspDebugKotlin`
   - `:app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.MessageGroupingTest`
3. Monitor for any of the historical failure signals:
   - `java.io.IOException: The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process`
   - `The Daemon will expire after the build` unusual exits
   - Kotlin IC ".tab" cache corruption errors
   - KSP incremental failures / regen loops
4. Measure wall-clock on the 2nd through 20th invocation — expect 10-15s faster per build.

### If stable

- Keep `org.gradle.daemon=true` in gradle.properties.
- Update the comment to reflect that the Kotlin 2.0+ era resolved the earlier flake.
- Verification scripts should still pass `--no-daemon` explicitly for deterministic release verification (matches current convention).

### If unstable

- Revert to `org.gradle.daemon=false`.
- File follow-up with specific error signatures + Kotlin/Gradle/KSP versions so someone can revisit when the upstream fix lands.
- Consider intermediate stance: daemon on, but `org.gradle.workers.max=1` or similar narrowing.

## Acceptance

- 20-run validation executed on a clean working tree with representative source edits between runs
- Outcome (stable / unstable) + measured timing delta committed to the bead notes
- gradle.properties updated accordingly (either flipped to true or kept at false with a strengthened comment citing this spike)
- No change to verification-script behavior (`--no-daemon` still passed there)

## Risk notes

**Medium risk, low commitment.** Time-boxed, fully reversible. If the flake is still real, we learn that in 2 hours and go back to known-good config.

## File hints

- android-compose/gradle.properties
notes:
=== Phase 2: kspDebugKotlin (20 runs) — CLEAN ===
Run 1 (daemon already warm from compile phase, KSP internal cold): 2.26s
Runs 2-20 wall-clock samples: 2.38, 2.50, 2.29, 2.27, 2.83, 2.47, 2.33, 2.41, 2.33, 2.13, 2.81, 2.32, 2.13, 2.11, 2.09, 2.55, 2.33, 2.06, 2.02
Runs 2-20 median:               ~2.33s
Runs 2-20 p95 (worst warm):     2.83s
Failures:                        0
IC corruption signals:           0

=== Phase 3: testDebugUnitTest (MessageGroupingTest, 20 runs, --max-workers=1) — CLEAN ===
Run 1 (daemon warm, test JVM cold):  18.69s  (test runner JVM fork dominates)
Runs 2-20 wall-clock samples: 2.85, 3.06, 2.68, 2.91, 2.92, 2.50, 2.46, 2.69, 2.56, 2.75, 2.54, 2.71, 2.44, 2.84, 2.47, 2.49, 2.55, 3.08, 2.50
Runs 2-20 median:               ~2.68s
Runs 2-20 p95 (worst warm):     3.08s
Failures:                        0
IC corruption signals:           0

=== Outcome classification: CLASS 3 (clean across 20) ===
- 60/60 runs clean across all three workloads
- 0 IC corruption signals (none of: source-to-classes.tab, class-attributes.tab,
  lookups.tab, kspCaches/.../backups, "already registered", "Daemon disappeared",
  NoSuchFileException on kspCaches)
- Warm-run speedup vs 08hs no-cache cold baseline (31.63s avg compile):
  * compile warm median ~2.36s = ~13.4x faster
  * ksp     warm median ~2.33s
  * test    warm median ~2.68s
- Cold-start tax (one-time daemon JVM spin-up): ~+2s vs prior --no-daemon

=== Action taken ===
- gradle.properties org.gradle.daemon flipped permanently to true
- Comment updated to cite spike outcome and revert criteria
- Time-box: spike completed at ~17:13, well within 18:35 hard stop (started 16:35)

=== Follow-up ===
- letta-mobile-0voy filed for downstream doc/CI/hook surfaces still hardcoding --no-daemon
close_reason: Stable: 60/60 clean across compile/ksp/test, 0 IC corruption signals, ~13x warm-run speedup. Daemon flip kept permanent. Follow-up letta-mobile-0voy filed for downstream surfaces.

title: Fix RunBlock pendingCenters remember-key to survive streaming list identity changes
description:
# Fix RunBlock pendingCenters remember-key

## Context

Discovered during post-m772.7 deep review. The measurement-stability fix in `RunBlock` uses:

```kotlin
val pendingCenters = remember(messages) { mutableMapOf<String, Float>() }
```

The `remember` key is the `messages` List reference. During streaming, the upstream timeline observer emits **new List instances** on every append (Kotlin List equality doesn't equal reference identity after `+= newMessage`), which **rebuilds `pendingCenters` from scratch** on every streaming tick. This partially re-introduces the y=0 flash / recomposition churn that m772.7 was specifically built to fix — the overlay guard `if (stepMarkers.isNotEmpty())` masks it, but we churn the map every frame.

## Reproduction

Stream a run with 5+ steps. Watch `pendingCenters.hashCode()` via a debug log in the `onGloballyPositioned` callback — it will change on every new step append, indicating the map was reallocated.

## Fix

Key `remember` on the **set of message IDs**, not the list reference:

```kotlin
val messageIds = remember(messages) { messages.map { it.id }.toSet() }
val pendingCenters = remember(messageIds) { mutableMapOf<String, Float>() }
```

Or equivalently use `remember(messages.size, messages.lastOrNull()?.id)` for a cheaper key.

## Acceptance

- Streaming a message into a RunBlock does not reset `pendingCenters` for existing steps
- Integration test added (or extended to `RunBlockStreamingTest`) that captures `pendingCenters` identity before/after streaming append and asserts it's the same instance (via an exposed test-only accessor or by inference from dot y-position stability with fewer `waitForIdle` passes)
- `:app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.RunBlockStreamingTest` still passes
- No visible change to production behavior — pure perf / correctness fix

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt (or RunBlock.kt if the split bead landed first)
notes: Implemented in commit 50efc44 on branch recovery/m772-from-stash (PR https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/1)

title: Integration test: RunBlock measurement stability under streaming
description:
# Integration test: RunBlock measurement stability under streaming

## Context

Epic: `letta-mobile-m772`. Discovered during review of `letta-mobile-m772.3` — the current `stepCenters: mutableStateMapOf<String, Float>` + `onGloballyPositioned` pattern can cause recomposition churn and a first-frame flash where dots render at y=0 before repositioning. We need a behavioral test that pins this down.

Blocks `letta-mobile-m772.6` (snapshot tests) because snapshots taken during the flash will produce non-deterministic baselines.

## Scope

Compose UI / Robolectric integration test that streams messages into a `RunBlock` and asserts:

1. **No y=0 flash in the visible frame**: after the first measured layout, the overlay `Box` containing dots is either (a) not composed, or (b) composed with dots at y > 0. No frame where dots are rendered at origin.
2. **Measurement recomposition count is bounded**: appending a new `UiMessage` with `isPending = true` to an N-step run does not cause the gutter `drawWithCache` lambda to re-execute more than 2 times (once for the new step, once after the final measurement settles). Use `Modifier.drawWithCache` with a side-effect counter in test-only wrapping.
3. **Streaming step transition**: flipping `isPending` from true → false on the trailing step does not cause other dots to shift y-position. Captures regression scenarios where position keys collide.

### Test harness

- Use `createComposeRule()` from `androidx.compose.ui.test.junit4`.
- Drive state via a `MutableStateFlow<List<UiMessage>>` and advance the list in-test.
- Assert dot positions via `SemanticsNodeInteraction.fetchSemanticsNode().layoutInfo` — add `testTag(\"run-dot-${messageId}\")` to each dot's `RunDot` composable (minor source edit to expose hooks).

## Acceptance

- Test file `RunBlockStreamingTest.kt` added under `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/`.
- All 3 assertions pass under `./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.RunBlockStreamingTest`.
- If measurement churn exceeds the bound, the test fails with a clear diagnostic — this bead's PR should also include the fix (migrate `mutableStateMapOf` → derived `mutableStateOf(ImmutableList)` computed once per layout pass, or move measurement into a `SubcomposeLayout`).
- Document the chosen measurement strategy in a KDoc on `RunBlock`.

## Implementation notes

Suggested fix for concern #1 / #2 from the m772.3 review:

```kotlin
// Instead of:
val stepCenters = remember(messages) { mutableStateMapOf<String, Float>() }
// …which invalidates derivations per-frame during streaming.

// Prefer a single state holder updated in an aggregated positional effect:
var stepMarkers by remember(messages) { mutableStateOf<ImmutableList<RunStepMarker>>(persistentListOf()) }
// Collect y-positions into a local Array<Float?>, only commit stepMarkers
// once all messages have reported positionInParent().
```

Also add a guard `if (stepCenters.isEmpty()) return@Box` on the dot overlay to eliminate the y=0 flash entirely.

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt (RunBlock + runGutter)
- android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlockStreamingTest.kt (new)
close_reason: Merged PR #91: added RunBlock streaming layout stability coverage for positive/monotonic gutter dot positions, stable existing-step positions, bounded row recomposition, and documented row-owned gutter measurement strategy.

title: Snapshot-test RunBlock layouts (Paparazzi/Roborazzi)
description:
# Snapshot-test RunBlock layouts

## Context

Epic: `letta-mobile-m772`. Discovered during review of `letta-mobile-m772.3` — verification skipped screenshot tests due to memory constraints, relying on Compose previews alone. Before closing the epic we need automated visual regression coverage for the gutter rail + dots.

## Scope

Add screenshot tests for the three existing preview fixtures in `RunBlockPreviews.kt` plus three streaming/error variants.

### Required snapshot cases

1. **Single-step completed run** — one assistant message, runId set, no tools. Verifies: container tint, no rail visible (single dot, no line).
2. **Multi-step tool run** — reasoning → tool success → assistant text. Verifies: rail connects all three dots, icon ordering (◇ → ⚙ → ○), solid line.
3. **Streaming run** — last step is `isPending = true`. Verifies: dashed line segment to the trailing dot, pulsing ring rendered (at scale=1.0, captured mid-animation paused).
4. **Tool error run** — tool step has `status = \"error\"`. Verifies: error-colored dot with X overlay, rest of rail unaffected.
5. **Pending approval run** — approval request dot renders as tertiary-colored with the `!` mark.
6. **Run with null runId assistant at the boundary** — confirms grouping correctly falls back to `Single` rendering and no container is drawn.

### Tooling choice

Codebase already has Paparazzi or Roborazzi somewhere (check `settings.gradle.kts` and `:app` dependencies). Prefer whichever is already wired:
- If neither is set up: use **Roborazzi** (works with Robolectric, doesn't need a device, already aligns with the `:app:testDebugUnitTest` task).
- If both: use the one with existing baselines elsewhere in the app.

### Deterministic captures

- Pause `rememberInfiniteTransition` for snapshots — capture pulse at animation time `t=0` (use `TestClock` or wrap the preview in a test harness that disables animations). The pulsing ring should still render (alpha=0.2 at scale=1.3) but without frame-to-frame drift.
- Use a fixed `LocalDensity` and `LocalConfiguration` so dp→px conversions are stable across CI hosts.
- `@Preview` fontScale = 1f (already set in `PreviewRunBlockContainer`).

## Acceptance

- 6 snapshot tests added under `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/` (e.g., `RunBlockScreenshotTest.kt`).
- `./gradlew :app:verifyRoborazziDebug` (or `:app:verifyPaparazziDebug`) passes locally and in CI.
- Baselines committed to `android-compose/app/src/test/snapshots/` (or wherever the tool is configured).
- README / `TESTING.md` note added explaining how to regenerate baselines (`recordRoborazziDebug` / `recordPaparazziDebug`).
- Memory note: if full-suite run OOMs on the dev host, split into `--tests RunBlockScreenshotTest` with `--max-workers=1` and document that in the bead's completion note.

## Implementation notes

- Reuse `RunBlockPreviews.kt` fixtures — don't duplicate data. Wrap them in `@RunWith(ParameterizedRobolectricTestRunner::class)` or a `@Test` per case that invokes `captureRoboImage { PreviewSingleStepRunBlock() }`.
- Gutter rail uses `drawWithCache` + `onGloballyPositioned`; Roborazzi runs layout under Robolectric so measurement should converge before the capture — verify the first-frame `stepCenters.isNotEmpty()` race from the m772.3 review doesn't leak into baselines. If dots appear at y=0 in the snapshot, that's the race — fix upstream in m772.4 (concern #2) before baselining.

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlockPreviews.kt (fixtures to reuse)
- android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts (add Roborazzi/Paparazzi plugin + deps)
- android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlockScreenshotTest.kt (new)
close_reason: Merged PR #92: added feature-chat Roborazzi RunBlock screenshot coverage and baselines.

title: Unit-test runStepDotIcon() branch coverage
description:
# Unit-test runStepDotIcon() branch coverage

## Context

Epic: `letta-mobile-m772`. Discovered during review of `letta-mobile-m772.3` — the `runStepDotIcon()` extension on `UiMessage` is a pure function with 6 distinct branches (Reasoning / Tool / ToolError / Approval / Text / Error) and branch precedence matters, but it currently has zero direct test coverage.

## Scope

Add unit test file `RunStepDotIconTest.kt` under `android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/` covering every branch of the when-expression.

### Required test cases

1. **Approval precedence**: `approvalRequest != null && approvalResponse == null` → `StepDotIcon.Approval` — even when `isReasoning = true` or `toolCalls` are also present. Verifies Approval wins over all other signals.
2. **Reasoning**: `isReasoning = true` with no pending approval → `StepDotIcon.Reasoning`.
3. **Tool error**: `toolCalls` contains at least one entry with `status == \"error\"` → `StepDotIcon.ToolError` — even when other tool calls succeed.
4. **Tool (success)**: `toolCalls` non-empty, all non-error statuses → `StepDotIcon.Tool`.
5. **Denied approval**: `approvalResponse?.approved == false` with no pending request and no tools → `StepDotIcon.Error`.
6. **Plain text**: no reasoning, no tools, no approval signals → `StepDotIcon.Text`.

### Also cover color mapping

Add parallel tests for `runStepDotColor(colorScheme)` ensuring each icon maps to the documented color token (primary / primary@0.8 / error / tertiary / onSurfaceVariant@0.6). Use a fake `ColorScheme` with distinct sentinel colors per slot.

## Acceptance

- All 6 branches of `runStepDotIcon()` execute under the test suite.
- Precedence test explicitly demonstrates Approval > Reasoning > ToolError > Tool.
- Color mapping test covers all 6 icon values.
- `./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.RunStepDotIconTest` passes.
- No `@Composable` or Android framework dependencies in the test file (pure Kotlin / JVM test).

## Implementation notes

`runStepDotIcon()` and `runStepDotColor()` are currently `private`. Either:
- Change to `internal` + annotate with `@VisibleForTesting`, OR
- Extract the pure logic to a top-level `internal` function in `MessageGrouping.kt` (better — keeps it out of the composable file and testable without importing Compose runtime).

Prefer option 2. It also lets m772.4 reuse the icon classification for the run summary header tally (`⚙×2 ◇×1 ○×1`).

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt (current home of runStepDotIcon)
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/MessageGrouping.kt (suggested new home)
- android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunStepDotIconTest.kt (new)
notes: Implemented in commit b9bb0ca on branch recovery/m772-from-stash (PR https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/1)

title: observability: StreamSubscriber telemetry events + Grafana panels
description:
## Goal

Emit structured telemetry for every state transition in the subscriber so we can see push delivery working in prod:

- `StreamSubscriber.streamOpened` — on successful connect
- `StreamSubscriber.streamClosed` — on clean close (run finished); include run duration
- `StreamSubscriber.eventReceived` — per event; include message_type
- `StreamSubscriber.eventDeduped` — dropped duplicate (by server id or otid)
- `StreamSubscriber.idle404` — NoActiveRunException path; include current backoff
- `StreamSubscriber.networkError` — transient failure
- `StreamSubscriber.dormant` — subscriptionCount=0, skipping probe

Grafana dashboard:
- Rate of eventReceived per conversation.
- Average backoff value (high = idle).
- Ratio of eventReceived / idle404 ("activity density").
- Alerts: if a chat screen is reported open (subscriptionCount > 0) for > 10 min with zero events and zero opened streams, fire warning — likely subscription broken.

LABELS: P2, observability, telemetry
close_reason: Shipped in 997e042 — added 5 missing StreamSubscriber telemetry events (opened, eventReceived, eventDeduped, closed w/ durationMs+eventsReceived, idle404, networkError) in TimelineSyncLoop. Grafana dashboard JSON (6 panels) + alert rules YAML (4 rules: SubscriberSilence, RunFinishedWithZeroEvents, NetworkErrorsSustained, DedupeSpike) under docs/observability/. Telemetry contract documented in stream-subscriber.md. Note: no 'dormant' event — subscriber runs for full TimelineSyncLoop lifetime by design (ChatPushService keeps process alive), documented so future readers don't expect the old subscriptionCount-gated shape.

title: FCM fallback for app backgrounded or Matrix socket dropped
description:
## Goal

v1 assumes the Matrix sync socket is always available while the app is running. That's usually true (Matrix client maintains long-poll /sync), but not when:

- App is fully backgrounded and Android kills the background work.
- Matrix server is briefly unreachable.
- User is on a network that blocks Matrix but allows FCM.

For these cases, we want a secondary path: lettabot also emits an FCM data message for every `agent.message.sent`, triggering a brief wake of the app's push handler which calls `TimelineRepository.getOrCreate(conv_id).hydrate()`.

## Design

- lettabot webhook receiver: after Matrix relay, also POST to FCM Data API with device token.
- Phone: register FCM token on launch, store it server-side.
- Android push handler: parse FCM payload → `conversation_id` → if loop exists, trigger a one-shot `reconcile()`; if not, ignore (next hydrate handles it).

## Why v2 not v1

FCM requires Google services and a project. Matrix-only works today with no extra infra. Ship v1 first, add this when the gap matters.

## Acceptance

- App backgrounded for 10 min. Desktop posts message. App gets FCM data wake. Opening chat shows message already present (not just "new since last visible").
- FCM quota usage stays within free tier for expected traffic.

LABELS: P2, fcm, push, fallback
notes: Superseded by letta-mobile-resume-stream-epic (see replacement). The webhook+matrix bridge approach is abandoned in favor of the simpler, verified resume-stream pattern (POST /v1/conversations/{id}/stream). No external infra, no secrets, no bridge. Verified empirically 2026-04-18 21:47 via letta-cli stream-watch.

title: CI perf regression gate — macrobench thresholds for startup p95 + scroll jank%
description: Add a CI job that runs :macrobenchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest against an emulator (or lab device), parses CSV results, and fails the build if startup p95 or scroll jank% regress beyond a committed threshold relative to the baseline in perf/baselines.json. Seed baselines from the first green run of Lane A1.
notes: Apr 21 follow-up split: created letta-mobile-vtw9 (Add deterministic benchmark launch route) for the non-blocking work required before ScrollJankBenchmark and ComposerTypingBenchmark can be re-enabled in CI. Current .4.1 landing plan is startup-only gate v1 on the canonical API 33 emulator; vtw9 will add a deterministic benchmark surface and then restore frame-timing metrics + baselines.

comments.text: CI gate scaffolding landed on main: perf/baselines.json (schema + null-seeded metrics for startup.cold/warm p95 and scroll.jank.pct) and perf/check_baselines.py (parses androidx.benchmark JSON, compares against baseline±tolerance%, supports --rebaseline). Still TODO: (1) Android Emulator runner job in .github/workflows that runs :macrobenchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest, (2) seed first baselines from a green run, (3) wire check_baselines.py as a required check.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
## Close-readiness criteria (2026-04-21)

All code + docs + scripts + tests are implemented and validated locally (see progress update 2026-04-21 16:03 EDT). Bead is not closed yet because the bead description explicitly includes seeding the authoritative baselines from a real CI emulator run, which cannot be done from the local environment.

**Exactly what closes this bead:**

1. `.github/workflows/android-perf.yml` is on a branch where CI can execute it (draft PR branch or main after merge; both work)
2. First CI run succeeds end-to-end — workflow completes without error, emitting candidate `baselines.json` as an artifact
3. Seeded baseline values from that run are committed to `perf/baselines.json` (replacing the null-seeded values)
4. A second CI run on the seeded baselines passes the `check_baselines.py` verification (proves the gate is live, not inert)

Once those four things have happened, the close comment should link: (a) the CI run that seeded the baselines, (b) the commit that landed the seeded values, (c) the CI run that verified against the seeded baselines.

**Recommended execution path (avoids merge-before-verification footgun):**

1. Open draft PR with workflow file
2. Push triggers first CI run — seeds baseline artifact
3. Download artifact, commit seeded `baselines.json` to the same PR
4. Push triggers second CI run — verifies against seeded baselines
5. Take PR out of draft, merge
6. Close bead with links to runs (1), (3), and the merged PR

This keeps the "first green run" inside the bead's scope and avoids closing-then-finding-out-it's-broken.

**If the workflow fails on first CI run for environmental reasons** (emulator image availability, accel, timing) — that's a legitimate new debugging task and remains in `.4.1`'s scope until resolved.

Both adjustments prioritize gate trustworthiness over tolerance tightness. Trust is the scarcer resource for a new gate.

title: App startup metric via androidx Metrics API + Sentry custom measurement
