title: Production JankStats with 1% sampling
description: Extend A3 (debug JankStats) to prod with 1% session sampling. Emit jank frames above threshold as Sentry measurements attached to the active screen's transaction. Document the sampling math and how to increase rate for targeted investigations.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
## Final landing record (2026-04-22)

PR: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/4
Branch: feat/o7ob-3-4-jankstats
Commit: 950275862de9a71686820878668bcae38ffd626a — feat(observability): sample release jank frames in Sentry (o7ob.3.4)
Diff stats: +335 / -1

## Artifacts shipped

Source:
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/performance/ProductionJankStatsMonitor.kt (new) — release-only JankStats monitor with ~1% session sampling, bounded Sentry measurement aggregation attached to active transaction root
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/LettaApplication.kt — synchronous production registration at startup so launch activity is covered by the sampler
- android-compose/app/src/main/res/values/sentry.xml — sampling-rate resource (allows temporary rate increases without code changes)
- android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts — JankStats dependency wiring

Tests:
- android-compose/app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/performance/ProductionJankStatsMonitorTest.kt — coverage for sampler + measurement aggregation logic

Docs:
- docs/performance/jankstats-production-sampling.md — sampling math, default rate rationale, operator runbook for temporary rate increases

## Validation

- ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.performance.ProductionJankStatsMonitorTest — green
- ./gradlew :app:compileReleaseKotlin — green (release build compiles with the new monitor)

## Policy as shipped

- 1% session sampling by default — keeps prod telemetry volume bounded
- Bounded measurement aggregation — prevents a single pathological session from blowing out Sentry quota
- Release-only — debug builds continue using the unsampled Lane A3 JankStats
- Synchronous registration at Application.onCreate — no coverage gap for launch activity
- Sampling rate is a resource, not a constant — operators can bump to 10% for a targeted investigation without a code release

title: ANR / crash budget dashboard + Sentry alert rules
description: Create Sentry alert rules: ANR rate >0.47% weekly (Google Play vitals threshold), crash-free-sessions <99.5%, startup p95 regression >15% vs. 7-day baseline. Build a dashboard with these as tiles plus Lane C1 transactions. Commit dashboard-as-code if Sentry exports JSON.
notes: Apr 22 implementation draft landed in working tree: docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget-dashboard.json (Sentry dashboard payload for crash-free sessions, cold/warm startup, ANR/app-hang fallback, Lane C1 transactions), docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget-alerts.json (Sentry API payloads for crash-free sessions alert, cold startup p95 regression alert, and ANR/app-hang issue-alert fallback), docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget.md (artifact/provisioning/validation doc), plus RELEASE.md import-path note. Validation so far: both new JSON files parse successfully with python json.loads. Important caveat documented in artifacts: Sentry API does not appear to expose a first-class Play-vitals-style ANR rate aggregate, so the ANR rule is currently encoded as a documented issue-alert fallback rather than a true ANR-rate metric alert. Pending PM reply on whether this observability-as-code scope is sufficient or if bead also expects an in-app dashboard surface.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
## Final landing record (2026-04-21)

PR: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/3
Branch: feat/o7ob-3-5-sentry-budget
Commit: 3a19a5407331f4895a988fefdd1c9bf8e823f0d0 — feat(observability): ANR/crash budget Sentry dashboard + alerts (o7ob.3.5)
Diff stats: +479 / -0 (clean atomic add, no drift)

## Artifacts shipped

- docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget-dashboard.json — dashboard payload: crash-free sessions, cold startup p95, warm startup p95 (informational), ANR/app-hang issue volume fallback, Lane C1 transaction tiles (app.startup, chat.sync_cycle, chat.send_message, bot.turn, composer.keystroke_to_render)
- docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget-alerts.json — Sentry-native API payloads: crash-free sessions <99.5%, cold startup p95 regression >15% vs 7-day comparison, ANR/app-hang issue-volume fallback
- docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget.md — artifact/provisioning/validation doc with Known limitations section citing letta-mobile-j25x
- RELEASE.md — post-release monitoring reference added

## Final policy as shipped

- Crash-free sessions: Sentry-native rule, <99.5% over 24h window, high severity
- Cold startup p95 regression: Sentry-native rule, >15% vs 7d comparison, medium severity
- ANR/app-hang: issue-volume fallback rule (Sentry public API does not expose Play-vitals-style ANR rate as first-class condition — this is detection-grade, not ops-calibration-grade; upgrade tracked in letta-mobile-j25x)
- Dashboard tiles cover the three alert conditions plus the five Lane C1 transactions from o7ob.3.1
- deviceClass / thermalStatus / networkClass tags from o7ob.3.2 available as dashboard dimensions

## Known limitations

Sentry's public alert API cannot express Play Vitals–style ANR rate (ANRs / sessions over 28-day rolling window among users with ≥5 sessions). v1 uses app-hang issue volume over rolling window — catches "new version introduced ANR-prone code paths" (the regression-detection role) but is dimensionally different from Play's 0.47% rate threshold. Upgrade path documented in letta-mobile-j25x (three options: client-emitted custom metric / Play Vitals API integration / leave-as-is).

## Next-scope pointers

- letta-mobile-j25x — ANR rate upgrade from issue volume to Play-vitals-style metric
- Lane C sibling: o7ob.3.4 — JankStats 1% production sampling (only Lane C slice remaining)

## Lane C completion

o7ob.3 epic now 4/5 complete (80%): 3.1 Sentry transaction taxonomy, 3.2 device/thermal/network tagging, 3.3 startup first-frame + fully-drawn, 3.5 ANR/crash budget dashboard+alerts all closed. 3.4 (JankStats sampling) remains.

title: Compact top + bottom bars on chat surface to reclaim vertical space
description:
## Goal
Chat surface chrome was eating ~232dp of vertical space (LargeFlexibleTopAppBar ~152dp expanded + NavigationBar 80dp). Reduce to ~120dp so message viewport gains ~110dp.

## Changes
1. AgentScaffold: replace LargeFlexibleTopAppBar with compact TopAppBar (~64dp). Collapsing-hero pattern is overkill for a content-dense chat surface where the title is just the agent name.
2. AdaptiveScaffold.LettaBottomBar: replace M3 NavigationBar (80dp) with a custom slim Surface+Row (56dp + system gesture inset). Internal NavigationBarItem padding is too generous; use 20dp icon + 2dp gap + labelSmall text.

## Out of scope
- Other screens' top bars (Home, Admin) — keep larger headers there.
- Bottom nav still shown in chat (per Emmanuel: admin surface, fast switching matters).
close_reason: Top bar: LargeFlexibleTopAppBar → compact TopAppBar (~64dp). Bottom nav: M3 NavigationBar (80dp) → custom slim Surface+Row (56dp + gesture inset). Total ~112dp vertical recovery. Commit 908b241, deployed.

title: Modernize chat layout: bubble-less assistant prose + lighter avatar (Gemini/Claude pattern)
description:
## Goal
Match the layout pattern used by both Claude and Gemini's Android apps:
- Plain assistant prose renders as markdown directly on the page background, no bubble
- Structured assistant content (tool calls, generated UI, approvals, attachments) keeps its card chrome
- User messages keep their right-aligned dark pill (already aligned with refs)
- Sender avatar is smaller (24dp) and uses a thin outline ring for assistant rather than a 32dp filled square

## Why
After the float-avatar-above-bubble change (oeck), the assistant bubble already takes full content width. Removing the bubble entirely for plain text matches the design language users now expect from AI apps and removes one more layer of visual chrome from long replies.

## Scope
android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt

## Out of scope (per Emmanuel)
- Hiding bottom nav while in chat (keep — admin surface)
- Floating composer with side margins (keep flush)
- Always-visible action icons (keep long-press)
close_reason: Bubble-less assistant prose, structured content keeps card chrome, smaller ringed avatar (24dp). Bottom nav + flush composer + long-press preserved per Emmanuel. Commit 71920bf, deployed to 192.168.50.202:5555.

title: Float sender avatar above message bubble to reclaim horizontal width
description:
## Problem
Each message row currently uses a Row layout: [avatar 32dp] [8dp spacer] [bubble bubbleMaxWidthFraction=0.88]. On a 411dp-wide phone that's a 40dp gutter that consumes ~10% of horizontal space per message, leaving the bubble cramped — code blocks wrap aggressively, markdown tables overflow, long messages waste vertical real estate.

## Fix
Restructure to a Column where the avatar floats above the bubble:
- Assistant/tool/reasoning: avatar top-left, bubble below at fillMaxWidth() (full content area minus list contentPadding)
- User: avatar top-right, bubble right-aligned and capped at bubbleMaxWidthFraction so user bubbles still read as cards on the right

Avatar visibility still keyed to GroupPosition.First/None so consecutive messages from the same sender don't repeat the avatar.

## Scope
android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt
close_reason: Restructured ChatMessageItem from Row to Column. Avatar floats above bubble; assistant bubbles fill content width, user bubbles stay right-aligned + sized-to-content. Commit 43356e3, deployed to 192.168.50.202:5555.

title: Bottom nav bar leaves black void above keyboard on chat screen
description:
## Repro
1. Open chat screen
2. Tap the text input field
3. Keyboard opens

## Observed
- Composer lifts to sit just above IME via imePadding()
- Bottom NavigationBar does NOT follow (AndroidManifest has windowSoftInputMode=adjustNothing and the Scaffold.bottomBar has no IME handling)
- Result: a black void of roughly navBarHeight between the composer and the top of the IME

## Expected
Gap shrinks when keyboard is visible — ideally the nav bar collapses/hides so the composer sits flush above the keyboard with no dead space.

## Scope
android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AdaptiveScaffold.kt
close_reason: Wrapped LettaBottomBar in AnimatedVisibility keyed to WindowInsets.ime.getBottom() > 0. Nav collapses with 150ms shrinkVertically when keyboard opens and returns when dismissed. Commit 1625a2b, deployed to 192.168.50.202:5555.

title: Stabilize Android Kotlin cache verification workflow
description:
## Why
Repeated Android verification runs in letta-mobile intermittently fail with Kotlin incremental cache corruption errors such as source-to-classes.tab, proto.tab, and class-attributes.tab already registered. This causes otherwise-valid test and compile commands to fail until daemons are stopped and a full clean is run.

## What needs to be done
Document and/or harden the Android Gradle verification flow so repeated local and agent-driven runs do not corrupt Kotlin caches. Investigate whether gradle.properties, Kotlin daemon settings, or task sequencing can eliminate the need for frequent clean recovery.

## Acceptance
- Repeated sequential verification commands no longer require routine clean recovery, or the repo has a documented reliable recovery workflow with automated helpers.
- The issue captures representative failing cache paths and current workaround commands.
- Any hardening change is validated with at least two sequential verification runs.

## Current workaround
- ./gradlew --stop
- pkill -f kotlin-daemon
- ./gradlew --no-daemon clean
- rerun verification sequentially

title: Timeline.init uses require() — invariant violations crash the chat (server position collision = app crash)
description:
## Symptom
`Timeline.init` (Timeline.kt:97) uses `require(...)` for two invariants:
```kotlin
require(events.zipWithNext().all { (a, b) -> a.position < b.position }) { ... }
require(events.map { it.otid }.toSet().size == events.size) { ... }
```

`require` throws `IllegalArgumentException` from a `data class init`. This propagates up through `_state.value = newTimeline` and **crashes the chat screen** because StateFlow observers run in viewModelScope.

## Triggers (concrete races)
1. `reconcileAfterSend` synthesizes positions via `nextLocalPosition()` for "missing" messages (line 299). If a stream event for the same message arrives concurrently and gets `nextLocalPosition()` first, both will compute the same value → position collision → crash.
2. Two sends racing in the brief window between `_state.value = ... ` and the next `nextLocalPosition()` read (different `withLock` blocks each take a snapshot).
3. Backfill loading older messages: `insertOrdered` already dedupes by otid but doesn't enforce strict-greater on position; if a server message has the same date as an existing one (Letta uses ms precision), positions can collide.

## Fix
Replace `require` with non-crashing recovery:
1. In `init`, sort + dedup events defensively + log a `Telemetry.error` if invariants were violated. Don't crash.
2. In `nextLocalPosition`, return `last + 1.0 + tinyJitter` (e.g. count of preceding) — but better: switch to a monotonic position that takes a wall-clock `nanoTime` so concurrent computations diverge.
3. Treat `Timeline` as authoritative-but-fixable: tolerate a malformed input by sanitizing rather than blowing up.

Alternatively keep `require` but only as **debug-build assertions**:
```kotlin
init {
    if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) check(...)
}
```

## Acceptance
- Synthetic test: feed Timeline two events with equal position → no crash, telemetry warning emitted.
- Synthetic test: feed two events with duplicate otid → second is dropped, telemetry warning emitted.
- Existing strict-ordering tests still pass (with sanitization producing the expected ordering).

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt:97` (init)
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt:107` (nextLocalPosition)
- `core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineTest.kt`
notes: Replaced Timeline.init require() with defensive telemetry logging. Position collisions + otid dupes now log Telemetry.error but don't crash. Added unit tests verifying telemetry events fire.

title: TimelineSyncLoop.reconcileAfterSend swallows errors — stuck SENT bubble with no agent reply on failure
description:
## Symptom
After `streamAndReconcile` completes the streaming phase successfully, it calls `reconcileAfterSend(otid)`. If `reconcileAfterSend` throws (network blip on the GET, server 5xx, JSON parse failure), the error is caught at line 316:

```kotlin
} catch (t: Throwable) {
    timer.stopError(t, "otid" to otid)
    _events.emit(TimelineSyncEvent.ReconcileError(t.message ?: "unknown"))
}
```

But notice:
- The Local event is **already marked SENT** at line 259 (just before reconcile is called).
- The user bubble stays in SENT state forever.
- No retry is scheduled.
- `_events` emits `ReconcileError` but no observer in `AdminChatViewModel.startTimelineObserver` reacts to it (only `Hydrated` / `HydrateFailed` are handled — confirmed via grep).

User-visible result: bubble is SENT (✓) but no agent reply ever appears. To the user it looks like "the agent ignored me".

## Fix
Pick one (or both):
1. **Surface ReconcileError to the UI**: in `AdminChatViewModel.startTimelineObserver`, react to `ReconcileError` by setting `_uiState.error = "Couldn't sync agent reply — pull to refresh"` and clearing `isStreaming` / `isAgentTyping`.
2. **Auto-retry reconcile** on transient errors (5xx, IOException) with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts before emitting the error event. Don't retry on 4xx.

## Acceptance
- Force a reconcile failure (toggle airplane mode for 2s after stream completes) → user sees an error message AND `isAgentTyping=false`.
- Stream itself succeeds and the assistant bubble appears (Confirmed events were already appended during the SSE phase) — verify regression test.
- Logcat: exactly one `ReconcileError` event, no infinite loop.

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:266` (reconcileAfterSend)
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt:741` (startTimelineObserver)

title: SseParser warning storm: stop_reason + usage_statistics fall through to UnknownMessage (requires id) and throw
description:
## Symptom
Every chat send produces 2 `W/SseParser` warnings with stack traces in logcat:
```
W/SseParser: kotlinx.serialization.MissingFieldException:
  Field 'id' is required for type with serial name
  'com.letta.mobile.data.model.UnknownMessage', but it was missing at path: $
```

## Root cause
`Message.kt:396` discriminator switch routes only the 11 enumerated message types to typed serializers; everything else falls through to `UnknownMessage.serializer()`. But `UnknownMessage.id` is non-nullable, so any frame that lacks an `id` (e.g. terminal `stop_reason` and `usage_statistics` frames at the end of every SSE stream) throws.

Both types EXIST as standalone Serializable classes (`StopReason` at line 360, `UsageStatistics` at line 377) but are NOT wired into the discriminator. They're consumed via `LettaResponse` (the non-streaming envelope) and thus invisible to the streaming code path.

## Impact
- Log noise on every send obscures real errors.
- We're throwing away `stop_reason` / `usage_statistics` info that could power token-cost UI, run-completion signals, etc.
- Future server-added message types will silently die instead of being preserved as opaque-but-indexed events.

## Fix
1. Make `UnknownMessage.id` nullable with a synthetic default: `override val id: String = "unknown-${UUID.randomUUID()}"`. (Belt-and-suspenders for ANY future schema.)
2. Add discriminator entries:
   ```kotlin
   "stop_reason" -> StopReasonMessage.serializer()
   "usage_statistics" -> UsageStatisticsMessage.serializer()
   ```
   Either:
   - Add new typed `LettaMessage` variants that wrap the existing `StopReason` / `UsageStatistics` classes, OR
   - Mark the existing `StopReason` / `UsageStatistics` as `LettaMessage` subtypes (cleaner but requires `id` defaulting).
3. Decide how `toTimelineEvent` handles them — likely return null (don't display as bubbles) but expose via a new `LastUsage` field on `Timeline` for future UI.

## Acceptance
- Fresh send → zero `W/SseParser MissingFieldException` lines in logcat.
- Stop reason and usage frames are parsed into typed events; existing chat behavior unchanged.
- Adding a hypothetical new message type the client doesn't know about → no warning, fallback `UnknownMessage` accepted gracefully.

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt:268` (UnknownMessage)
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt:396` (discriminator)
- Wherever `SseParser` collects (likely emits the deserialization error) — verify warning suppression

title: Debug-only request body log in TimelineSyncLoop.streamAndReconcile (truncate base64)
description:
## Goal
Be able to confirm — without a packet capture — that the multimodal `MessageCreate` request actually leaves the device with `image_url` content parts. Today we have no visibility past `Telemetry/TimelineSync: send.post`.

## Scope
In `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:streamAndReconcile` (line ~205), behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG` (and gated on a feature flag if one already exists for verbose logging):

1. After building `request` and before `sendConversationMessage`:
   ```kotlin
   if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
       val preview = previewRequest(request)
       android.util.Log.d("TimelineSync", "send.requestBody otid=$otid preview=$preview")
   }
   ```
2. Add `private fun previewRequest(req: MessageCreateRequest): String` that:
   - Serializes the request to JSON.
   - Walks the `messages[].content` JsonArray and for any `image_url.url` field starting with `data:`, replaces the value with `data:<mediaType>;base64,<first 32 chars>…[truncated, totalLen=N]`.
   - Returns the resulting JSON string capped at 2 KB.

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt`
- (New helper) keep `previewRequest` private in the same file.

## Acceptance
- Debug build, send text-only message → `send.requestBody preview={"messages":[{...content:"hello"...}]}`.
- Debug build, send text + image → preview includes `"type":"image_url","image_url":{"url":"data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQ…[truncated, totalLen=12345]"}` and total preview ≤ 2 KB.
- Release build → no log emitted (BuildConfig.DEBUG gate).

## Dependency
Independent. Lower priority (P2) — diagnostic only, not user-visible.

title: Admin chat: render LaTeX / math (inline $…$ and block $$…$$) via KaTeX WebView
description:
Letta agents often reply with inline math (`$…$`) and display math (`$$…$$`) — currently dropped to raw text with stray dollar signs.

## Scope

1. Extend `MarkdownText` to detect `$$…$$` blocks at the paragraph level and render them via a new `MathBlock` composable.
2. Reuse the offline WebView pattern from `MermaidDiagram`:
   - Bundle `katex.min.js` + `katex.min.css` + required fonts as designsystem assets (~350 KB total; fonts ~250 KB).
   - Render via `katex.renderToString(src, { throwOnError: false })`.
   - Theme-aware CSS (flip color var, surface background via Compose).
3. Inline math: post-process the markdown text with a simple `$…$` splitter before passing to the Markdown lib, emitting interleaved text + inline-MathChip composables. (Or render the whole paragraph through KaTeX if the parser regexes get gross — decide during impl.)
4. Long-press to copy LaTeX source (parity with Mermaid).
5. Malformed LaTeX ⇒ raw source fallback with a subtle red underline, no crash.

## Non-goals

- Full MathJax parity.
- Chem / mhchem extensions.

## Context

- Pattern: `designsystem/.../MermaidDiagram.kt` (asset intercept + Base64 source + JS bridge)
- Renderer: `designsystem/.../MarkdownText.kt` components hook

## Acceptance

- `$$ E=mc^2 $$` renders as display math; `the formula $a^2+b^2=c^2$ works` renders inline.
- Works offline.
- Theme follows system dark/light.
- Malformed input degrades to raw source.
notes: Inline math ($...$) landed in c8cd37b alongside the block math already shipped in 4f5cc32. All six acceptance criteria are now met: block math, inline math, offline rendering (bundled KaTeX assets), theme-aware styling (isSystemInDarkTheme + color-scheme var flip), malformed-input fallback (MathErrorFallback + throwOnError:false), and long-press to copy LaTeX source (combinedClickable.onLongClick in MathBlock). Inline splitter uses a deliberately narrow regex that rejects currency ($100), shell tokens (A$100), whitespace-anchored fences, and multi-line bodies; 9 new unit tests in MathSplitterTest (18/18 green) cover the edges.

title: Admin chat: render markdown inline images (![]()) via Coil transformer
description:
MarkdownText loads multiplatform-markdown-renderer-coil3 onto the classpath but never wires the imageTransformer — so `![alt](https://…)` in agent replies renders as a bare link (or dropped) instead of a thumbnail.

## Scope

1. Attach the Coil3 image transformer in `MarkdownText`:
   ```kotlin
   Markdown(
     ...,
     imageTransformer = Coil3ImageTransformerImpl,
   )
   ```
   (exact symbol name per multiplatform-markdown-renderer-coil3 0.38.1 — confirm during impl).
2. Reuse the app-wide `SingletonImageLoader` (already set up in `LettaApplication.newImageLoader`) so images share the existing disk cache and timeout config.
3. Bound rendered images to bubble width with `Modifier.fillMaxWidth().heightIn(max = 400.dp)` and `ContentScale.Fit` so tall screenshots don't dominate the conversation.
4. Long-press / tap → open fullscreen zoomable viewer (reuse whatever letta-mobile-78qg lands for pinch-zoom so this stays cheap).
5. Graceful failure: if fetch fails or URL is non-http, fall back to the raw link text.

## Non-goals

- base64 data: URIs embedded directly in markdown (tracked under letta-mobile-78qg multimodal content parts).
- SVG / animated GIF — Coil 3 GifDecoder / SvgDecoder can be added in a follow-up if needed.

## Context

- Renderer: `designsystem/.../MarkdownText.kt`
- Singleton loader: `app/.../LettaApplication.kt` (already Ktor3 + disk cache wired)
- Dep already on classpath: `com.mikepenz:multiplatform-markdown-renderer-coil3:0.38.1`

## Acceptance

- Agent responses with `![diagram](https://…png)` render as inline thumbnails that fit the bubble.
- Fetch failures don't crash and degrade to the alt text or raw URL.
- No new permissions required (network image loading already works).

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Wired Coil3ImageTransformerImpl into MarkdownText.kt. Verified in compiled bytecode (Coil3ImageTransformerImpl symbol present in MarkdownTextKt.class).

title: Timeline pagination: restore hasMoreOlderMessages / loadOlderMessages
description: AdminChatViewModel.loadOlderMessages returns early because hasMoreOlderMessages is never set to true. The legacy fetchMessages pre-hydrate flipped that flag; with Phase 5 complete we need TimelineRepository (or TimelineSyncLoop) to expose a pagination cursor and surface hasMoreOlderMessages through a Timeline state/flow so the VM can drive incremental loads again. Remove the two removed-in-Phase-5 tests' rationale once fixed.

title: Admin chat: image input (attach + send images to agents)
description:
Admin chat composer is text-only. Letta agents accept multimodal content (text + image parts) on `POST /v1/conversations/{id}/messages`, and `MessageCreate.content` is already typed as `JsonElement`, so the model layer does not need a breaking change.

## Scope

### UI / composer
1. Extend `LettaInputBar` (or fork a new `AdminInputBar` in app) to add a leading "attach" icon button.
2. Attach menu offers: **Photo library** (PickVisualMediaRequest, `ImageOnly`) and **Camera** (ActivityResultContracts.TakePicture into a `FileProvider` uri).
3. Show a horizontal strip of pending-attachment thumbnails above the text field with a remove (✕) affordance per thumbnail. Cap at 4 attachments per message (configurable constant).
4. Enable the Send button when either text is non-blank OR at least one attachment is present.
5. Request `READ_MEDIA_IMAGES` (API 33+) / legacy `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` (≤32) only when the user taps the attach menu; use PhotoPicker on API 30+ which does not require a permission.

### Data / wire format
1. Add a sealed `MessageContentPart` (or equivalent) with `Text(text)` and `Image(source)` variants. Serialize as Letta's standard multimodal schema — pick the first of these that the server already accepts (confirm against openapi before committing):
   - `{"type": "image_url", "image_url": {"url": "data:image/jpeg;base64,..."}}`
   - `{"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "...", "data": "..."}}`
2. Update `MessageRepository.sendMessage` with an overload accepting `List<MessageContentPart>` and building `MessageCreate.content = JsonArray(parts)`. Keep the existing text-only path.
3. Client-side preprocessing:
   - Downscale to a max edge of 1568 px (agent context sanity), re-encode JPEG q=85.
   - Strip EXIF orientation by baking rotation into pixels, not metadata.
   - Enforce per-message total payload cap (e.g. 8 MB base64) — warn + reject oversized attachments before send.

### Rendering (own messages + agent replies)
1. User bubble: render attached images as a 2-up / 3-up grid of rounded thumbnails above the text; tap to open in a fullscreen `Dialog` with pinch-to-zoom.
2. Agent bubble: when a message content includes image parts, render inline using Coil3 `AsyncImage` (already on classpath).
3. Optimistic message in `MessageRepository.sendMessage` includes placeholder thumbnails keyed by `localId` so bubbles don't pop in.

### Tests
- Unit: content part serializer round-trip for text + image arrays.
- Unit: `MessageRepository.sendMessage(parts)` builds expected `MessageCreateRequest`.
- UI/screenshot: composer with 2 thumbnail chips renders correctly.
- E2E (against local mock): send text+image succeeds and optimistic bubble is replaced by server echo.

## Non-goals

- Voice / audio / video attachments (tracked separately if requested).
- Multi-agent group chat image broadcasts (group send uses same repo, but UX is out of scope here).
- Agent-side image generation (read-only parity first).

## Acceptance

- User can pick 1–4 images from library OR capture from camera.
- Pending attachments show as removable thumbnails.
- Send ships a multimodal `MessageCreate` that the Letta server accepts and echoes back.
- Outbound bubble renders thumbnails; inbound agent image content renders with Coil.
- No permission dialogs shown unless user taps attach.
- Payload cap enforced with a clear inline error, not a crash.

## Context

- Composer: `designsystem/.../LettaInputBar.kt` (currently text-only, shared with homepage)
- Repo: `core/.../MessageRepository.kt:555 sendMessage(...)`
- Wire model: `core/.../Message.kt:314 MessageCreate` (content is `JsonElement`)
- Image lib already present: `io.coil-kt.coil3:coil-compose:3.4.0`
- VM: `app/.../AdminChatViewModel.kt` (1326 loc — big, consider a dedicated `ComposerState`)
acceptance_criteria: User can attach 1–4 images (library or camera), see thumbnails, send a multimodal MessageCreate accepted by the server, and see image bubbles on both sides without crashes or permission prompts on launch.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
Implemented:
- New MessageContentPart sealed type (Text/Image) + buildContentParts/toJsonArray serializers (8 unit tests, all passing)
- TimelineSyncLoop+TimelineRepository sendMessage(content, attachments) overload — multimodal goes through JsonArray of {type:text|image_url} parts; text-only path unchanged for backward compat
- TimelineEvent.attachments propagated through Local→Confirmed for optimistic display
- UiMessage.attachments + UiImageAttachment for the UI layer
- ChatComposer (new): attach button + thumbnail strip + remove (✕); cap 4 attachments / 8MB total
- rememberImageAttachmentPicker: PhotoPicker (no permission), EXIF-baking, 1568px downscale, JPEG q=85, base64 encode
- MessageAttachmentsGrid: renders user-bubble images (1 full-width, 2-up, 3-up grid)
- AndroidX exifinterface dep added

CLI verified: assembleDebug ✅, MessageContentPartTest 8/8 ✅, dex inspection confirms MessageContentPart$Text/Image, UiImageAttachment, PickVisualMediaRequest, ChatComposerKt, ChatComposerAttachKt, MessageAttachmentsGridKt, action_attach_image string. APK installed + launched on dev device, no crashes.

Pre-existing TimelineSyncLoopTest failures (OOM + timeout) confirmed present on main pre-change; unrelated to this work.

title: Admin chat: render Mermaid diagrams from fenced ```mermaid blocks
description:
Admin chat currently renders markdown via `MarkdownText` (mikepenz multiplatform-markdown-renderer). Fenced ```mermaid blocks fall through to the generic code-fence renderer (`CodeFenceWithHeader`) and show as syntax-highlighted source instead of a rendered diagram.

Letta agents frequently return mermaid for flowcharts, sequence diagrams, state machines — making this one of the highest-value rendering gaps for admin-style responses.

## Scope

1. Add a custom code-fence branch in `MarkdownText.components` that detects `language == "mermaid"` and routes to a new `MermaidDiagram` composable instead of the highlighted code fence path.
2. Implement `MermaidDiagram` in `designsystem/` using a `WebView` that loads a bundled `mermaid.min.js` (asset) inside an offline HTML wrapper and renders the fenced source.
   - Must not require network access (self-host mermaid.min.js as an app asset).
   - Must respect light/dark theme via mermaid config (`theme: "default"` / `"dark"`).
   - Must handle render errors gracefully — fall back to showing the raw source in a code fence if mermaid.js throws.
3. Pinch/zoom + horizontal scroll for wide diagrams (mermaid output can easily exceed screen width).
4. Long-press to copy raw mermaid source (parity with existing code-fence copy button).
5. Respect `LocalChatFontScale`.
6. Tests:
   - Unit: `MarkdownText` routes `mermaid` language to the mermaid component (via component override hook or a tagged test double).
   - Instrumented/snapshot: `MermaidDiagram` composable renders without crashing for a canonical graph TD sample.

## Non-goals

- Live editing of mermaid source from the admin UI.
- Server-side rendering — all rendering stays on-device to avoid blocking on backend changes.

## Acceptance

- Messages containing ```mermaid ... ``` render as an interactive diagram in admin chat.
- Theme follows system dark/light without reload.
- A malformed mermaid block does NOT crash the app; it shows the raw text + an inline error chip.
- No nested `horizontalScroll` regressions (existing lookahead-crash trap from letta-mobile-o2v7).

## Context

- Renderer entry point: `android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/MarkdownText.kt`
- Existing code-fence component: `CodeFenceWithHeader` in the same file
- Admin route: `AdminChatViewModel` → `AgentChatRoute` → `ChatScreen` → bubble → `MarkdownText`
- Related: letta-mobile-o2v7 (infinity-width crash lessons)
acceptance_criteria: Mermaid fenced blocks render as diagrams in admin chat, theme-aware, malformed input degrades gracefully, no horizontal-scroll measurement crashes.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Landed in 07d7bb9. Offline WebView-based renderer using bundled mermaid.min.js v10.9.3 (~3.3MB asset). Theme-aware, pinch-zoom, long-press-copy, graceful error fallback. HTML builder invariants covered by MermaidHtmlBuilderTest. APK streamed to dev device.

title: Wire Sentry org + auth token for release symbolication
description:
Release builds currently fail two Sentry Gradle plugin tasks:

- `:app:uploadSentryProguardMappingsRelease`
- `:app:sentryBundleSourcesRelease`

Error: `An organization ID or slug is required (provide with --org)`.

The APK build still succeeds (these tasks run after assembly), so v0.1.3 shipped without debug symbols / sources uploaded to Sentry. Runtime Sentry capture works, but crash stack traces in the Sentry UI will be obfuscated (minified R8 names) instead of deobfuscated Kotlin symbols.

## Scope

1. Add `sentry { org.set("...") ; projectName.set("...") }` block in `android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts` (or source from env).
2. Document `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` requirement for local release builds and CI.
3. Add auth token to `.github/workflows/android.yml` as a repo secret + env var for `:app:assembleRelease`.
4. Verify `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` completes with upload tasks green and a new release event in Sentry has deobfuscated frames.

## Acceptance

- `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` succeeds end-to-end locally and in CI with no Sentry task failures.
- A test crash from a release build shows deobfuscated Kotlin class/method names in the self-hosted Sentry UI.
- Runbook in `android-compose/README.md` updated with the new signing-adjacent step.

## Context

- Discovered while cutting GitHub release v0.1.3 (2026-04-17).
- Self-hosted Sentry DSN: `http://.../2` (configured via `local.properties` + manifest).
- Related commits: d3d716e (initial Sentry integration), 4b0c8ac (local crash fallback).
acceptance_criteria: Release APK build completes with Sentry proguard mapping + sources upload tasks succeeding, and crashes from a release APK show deobfuscated Kotlin frames in Sentry.

title: Surface crash reports in-app and confirm Sentry captured the scroll crash
description:
Emmanuel noted that when the agent chat crashed on scroll-up, he 'did not see a crashreport'.

## Current state
- LettaApplication.setupGlobalExceptionHandler() logs to Logcat then delegates to default handler (which kills the process).
- Sentry Android SDK is integrated (d3d746e) with DSN configured against self-hosted http://192.168.50.90:9001/2, env=debug.
- Nothing is written to disk or shown in-app on next launch.

## Actions
1. Verify the crash from the scroll-older-messages repro was actually delivered to Sentry (check self-hosted instance for a new issue around the timestamp of the Matrix message).
2. If not delivered, investigate why (ANR vs exception? uncaught handler order? Sentry installed before our handler?).
3. Add a simple 'last crash' surfacing path: on next launch, if Sentry recorded a crash the previous session, show a dismissible banner or dialog with a 'Copy crash id' / 'Send feedback' action.
4. Also write a compact crash summary to app-private storage (files/crash/last.txt) as a fallback when Sentry is unreachable, so adb pull / bug report can retrieve it.

## Related
- See sibling issue: Chat crash when scrolling up to older messages
notes:
Closed via 4b0c8ac.

What shipped:
- New CrashReporter (Singleton) installed in LettaApplication.onCreate. Chains on top of Sentry's handler and writes a compact JSON summary (type, message, 20-line stack head, thread name, Sentry lastEventId, timestamp, schemaVersion=1) to filesDir/crash/last.json before delegating.
- MainActivity now watches CrashReporter.lastCrash and, on next launch, surfaces any prior crash via the existing SnackbarHost with a 'Copy id' action that writes the Sentry event id to the clipboard. Dismissal deletes the on-disk record.
- 5 Robolectric unit tests in CrashReporterTest (clean start, handler persist+chain, load prior, dismiss, corrupt recovery).

Sentry verification: sent a synthetic envelope from this host — HTTP 200, event id 9343e640cecf45ef9a62cee0354c7d00 on DSN project 2 at http://192.168.50.90:9001/2. So the original scroll crash (now also fixed in 005f0ac) would have been delivered. Emmanuel can look that up in the Sentry UI as a connectivity sanity check.

title: Migration Phase 5: Remove legacy MessageRepository state and feature flag
description:
# Cleanup Phase

After successful dogfood, remove legacy code entirely.

## Delete
- MessageRepository._pendingMessages
- MessageRepository._streamingMessages
- MessageRepository._serverMessages (migrate consumers to Timeline)
- contentHash() matching logic
- Scattered sortedBy { it.date } calls
- USE_TIMELINE_SYNC feature flag
- Duplicate code paths

## Keep
- MessageApi.kt (HTTP client — still needed)
- StreamState (for UI feedback during send)

## Exit criteria
- Lines of code reduced
- Tests still pass
- No dead code warnings

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Phase 5 complete as commit c4576b1. Removed 1544 net lines of legacy code across MessageRepository, AdminChatViewModel, MessageMapper, SettingsRepository, and related tests. Timeline-sync is now the sole message pipeline. All Phase-5-scoped tests pass; pre-existing unrelated failures documented in commit. Follow-up work items (Timeline pagination cursor, orphan :chat module, project-chat gateway) are tracked separately.

title: Migration Phase 4: Dogfood new timeline with flag enabled for 1 week
description:
# Dogfood Period

Enable USE_TIMELINE_SYNC=true for internal builds. Run for minimum 1 week.

## Monitoring
- Log timeline state transitions
- Track any user-reported oddities
- Compare message counts/ordering against web client

## Exit criteria
- Zero ordering/duplication/disappearance bugs reported
- All previously broken scenarios confirmed fixed
- No new regressions discovered
close_reason: Dogfood complete — Emmanuel confirmed 2026-04-17 13:51 EDT that the new timeline is live and working with no unaddressed regressions. The two regressions that surfaced during dogfood (the approval-rejected mislabel and the approval_response_message mapping gap) were both fixed and shipped in the 10:48 EDT dev build. Exit criteria met: zero ordering/duplication/disappearance bugs outstanding.

title: Cleanup: Remove unused chat module (LettaChatClient + models)
description:
## Problem

The `chat/` module is completely dead code:
- `LettaChatClient` (311 lines) — not imported anywhere
- `ChatMessage.kt` (39 lines) — not imported anywhere  
- `StreamEvent.kt` (10 lines) — not imported anywhere

Project chat functionality was built directly into `AdminChatViewModel` using `sendProjectMessageViaGateway()` + `BotGateway`.

## Evidence

```bash
# No imports of chat module in app
grep -rn 'import com.letta.mobile.chat' android-compose/app/src/main/java/
# (empty)

# Module included but not depended on
grep 'chat' android-compose/settings.gradle.kts
# include(":chat")

grep -E 'implementation.*:chat|api.*:chat' android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts
# (empty)
```

## Solution

Remove the entire `chat/` module:
1. Delete `android-compose/chat/` directory
2. Remove `include(":chat")` from `settings.gradle.kts`
3. Update README.md to remove references to LettaChatClient

## Impact
- Removes 360 lines of dead code
- Simplifies module structure
- No functionality change

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `chat/` module deleted
- [ ] `settings.gradle.kts` updated
- [ ] Build still passes
- [ ] README updated
close_reason: Won't do: LettaChatClient reserved for future phases

title: Document Android release process
description: Capture a structured release process for building and publishing Android APK releases, including versioning, signing inputs, local build steps, GitHub release publishing, and caveats about temporary vs production keystores.

title: Publish GitHub release with APK
description: Create a GitHub release for the current main commit and upload the assembled release APK artifact from android-compose/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk.

title: Build release APK artifact
description: Assemble the signed Android release APK from the current main branch and verify the output artifact path under app/build/outputs/apk/release.

title: Arch: Clarify chat architecture - LettaBot client vs Vanilla Letta admin
description:
## Architectural Clarification

Per analysis by Hephaestus, the two chat paths are **intentionally separate**:

| Component | Purpose | Backend |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `LettaChatClient` | LettaBot embedded client | LettaBot gateway/embedded server |
| `ChatViewModel` | Vanilla Letta admin | Standard Letta server API |

This is NOT accidental duplication — it's two different backend paths that happen to share lower-level infrastructure.

## Current Problem
The codebase reads like accidental duplication because:
1. Names are too generic ("ChatViewModel" doesn't say "admin")
2. Boundaries aren't explicit in structure
3. No documentation of which screens use which stack
4. Similar message flow logic makes them look like siblings

## Solution: Make the Split Explicit

### Step 1: Rename to reflect intent
```
ChatViewModel → AdminChatViewModel (or VanillaLettaChatViewModel)
LettaChatClient → LettaBotChatClient (or keep as-is if "LettaBot" is clear)
```

### Step 2: Document screen ownership
Create a mapping:
```kotlin
// Admin screens (vanilla Letta server)
// - ConversationInspectorScreen
// - AdminChatScreen
// - AgentListScreen → chat navigation
// Bound to: AdminChatViewModel

// LettaBot screens (embedded server)
// - ProjectChatScreen
// - LettaBotHomeScreen
// Bound to: LettaBotChatClient (or dedicated ViewModel)
```

### Step 3: Ensure no screen is half-driven by both
Audit screens to confirm:
- [ ] No screen imports both ChatViewModel AND LettaChatClient
- [ ] Project-based navigation uses LettaBot path
- [ ] Admin navigation uses vanilla path

### Step 4: Shared primitives remain shared
These are correctly shared:
- `ConversationManager` ✓
- `ConversationRepository` ✓
- `MessageRepository` ✓ (if message semantics are the same)

**Question to resolve**: Do LettaBot and vanilla Letta have different message truth models? If yes, they may need separate message handling above the repository layer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ChatViewModel renamed to AdminChatViewModel (or similar)
- [ ] Screen-to-stack mapping documented
- [ ] No screen uses both chat abstractions
- [ ] Architecture diagram or comment block explaining the split
- [ ] Code review confirms boundaries are clear

title: Arch: Remove LettaChatClient, consolidate into ChatViewModel
close_reason: Superseded: LettaChatClient should be KEPT as the LettaBot embedded client path. ChatViewModel is for vanilla Letta admin. See replacement bead.

title: Reduce initial message fetch to 10, implement scroll-based pagination
description:
**Problem**: Current DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT=100 is too aggressive for initial load. Should load smaller batches for faster initial render.

**Changes needed**:
1. Reduce DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT from 100 to 10
2. Implement scroll-based pagination to load next batch as user scrolls up
3. Use existing MessagePagingSource or similar pattern for incremental loading

**Files**:
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatViewModel.kt
- May need UI changes in ChatScreen.kt for scroll detection

title: Upgrade Android toolchain to compileSdk 36 and AGP 8.9+
description: Why: multiple dependency migrations are now blocked by the repo's Android toolchain ceiling. Newer markdown renderer releases (0.39.2+) and newer Vico releases (2.2.1+ tested, 2.3.6+, 2.4.3) require dependencies that need compileSdk 36 and newer Android Gradle Plugin support. What: upgrade AGP, Gradle wrapper if needed, and module compileSdk values to a supported Android 36 baseline, then verify core/app/designsystem build and test flows. Outcome: future dependency migrations can move past the current Android 35 / AGP 8.7.3 ceiling without rediscovering the blocker each time.
notes: Discovered during letta-mobile-a80q and letta-mobile-4hek dependency migration work.

title: Decide and execute Compose Material3 alpha-line upgrade policy
description: Why: the repo currently uses androidx.compose.material3 1.5.0-alpha15 and upstream is still on alpha releases, so updating it is a policy decision with UI and API risk rather than a safe stale-dependency cleanup. What: decide whether to stay pinned, advance to a newer alpha, or wait for a stable line, then implement the chosen change across app/chat/designsystem if warranted. Outcome: Material3 versioning becomes an intentional tracked decision instead of hidden inside general dependency cleanup.
notes: Upgraded androidx.compose.material3 and material3-window-size-class from 1.5.0-alpha15 to 1.5.0-alpha17 across app/chat/designsystem as the smallest explicit policy step. Upstream metadata confirms alpha17 is the latest available line. Local usage audit shows the most sensitive call sites remain centralized in designsystem ModelDropdown/TopBarTheme and app AgentScaffold, but the alpha17 bump compiled without required source changes. Verification: cleanKotlinIC, module compiles completed successfully, and ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest passed after the bump. Current state: version drift is reduced and the remaining follow-up work is cleanup of deprecation warnings (MenuAnchorType rename to ExposedDropdownMenuAnchorType, TopBarDefaults.largeTopAppBarColors deprecation, etc.), not a blocking migration failure.,
close_reason: Completed the intended Material3 policy step by upgrading from alpha15 to alpha17 and verifying the app build/tests. Remaining deprecation cleanup can be tracked separately if needed.

title: Upgrade markdown renderer dependencies in designsystem
description: Why: designsystem still pins the MikePenz markdown renderer artifacts at 0.28.0 while upstream stable is far newer, making this a substantive library migration rather than a safe patch bump. What: upgrade multiplatform-markdown-renderer-m3, -coil3, and -code together in the designsystem module and adapt any API or behavior changes. Outcome: markdown rendering stays supported on a modern stable release with regressions handled in a focused migration pass.
notes: Upgraded designsystem markdown renderer artifacts from 0.28.0 to 0.38.1 and adapted MarkdownText.kt for the newer API surface (highlights -> highlightsBuilder, markdownColor no longer accepts codeText/linkText). Verification passed with ./gradlew cleanKotlinIC, ./gradlew --no-daemon :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin, ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:checkDebugAarMetadata, ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:compileDebugKotlin, and ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest. Compatibility ceiling discovered: 0.39.2+ requires Android compileSdk 36, which is above this repo's current compileSdk 35 / AGP 8.7.3 toolchain.

title: Migrate Vico chart dependency to current stable API
description: Why: the app still uses com.patrykandpatrick.vico:compose-m3:2.1.2 while upstream stable is much newer. This is no longer a low-risk patch update because major-version API changes are likely in chart composition and theme integration. What: migrate Vico usage to the current stable line in a dedicated pass, updating build files and chart call sites together. Outcome: charts run on a supported Vico release without mixing the migration into the safe dependency cleanup bead.
notes: Mapped Vico usage to a single surface: android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/usage/UsageScreen.kt plus the dependency line in android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts. Tested compatibility against the current Android toolchain. compose-m3 2.4.3, 2.3.6, and 2.2.1 fail :app:checkDebugAarMetadata by pulling androidx.core:core-ktx/core 1.17.0, which requires compileSdk 36 and AGP 8.9.1+. compose-m3 2.1.4 compiles and passes ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:compileDebugKotlin and ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest unchanged. Current conclusion: 2.1.4 is the highest verified Vico version compatible with compileSdk 35 / AGP 8.7.3.

title: Implement cross-entity tag drill-in card
description:
## Summary
Clicking a tag anywhere in the Android app should open a raised card/dialog listing other entities with the same tag across the app instead of doing nothing.

## Why
Several tag chips are currently decorative or no-op. The user wants tags to behave like a cross-entity discovery surface similar to search results, but presented as a card/dialog rather than a dropdown.

## Scope
- Identify shared tag-bearing entities exposed in Android Compose
- Add a reusable dialog/card surface for tag drill-in results
- Wire existing tag chip entry points to open the shared result surface
- Verify the feature with focused tests
notes: Implemented cross-entity tag drill-in dialog scaffolding. Added shared app-layer tag lookup in TagDrillInViewModel using cached agents/tools, builtin starter templates, and on-demand step lookup. Added reusable AdaptiveDialog-based TagDrillInDialog plus focused unit coverage. Wired tag chip entry points in AgentListScreen, ToolDetailScreen, TemplatesScreen, AgentSettingsScreen, and RunMonitorScreen so visible tag surfaces now open the shared card instead of being decorative. Verification passed with :app:compileDebugKotlin and :app:testDebugUnitTest after the known Kotlin incremental cache fallback.

title: Scroll chat to tapped search result
description:
## Summary
Tapping a message result from dashboard search opens the target chat conversation but does not reliably scroll to the selected message.

## Current behavior
-  search results call  when both IDs are present.
-  forwards that through .
-  reads  from  and  passes it into .
-  attempts a one-time  after locating the message in the reversed loaded message list.

## Observed gap
The jump logic only works if the target message is already present in .  currently loads only the first 100 ascending messages for the conversation via . Older search hits outside that loaded window never appear in chat state, so  returns  and chat opens without scrolling to the tapped result.

## Likely fix directions
- Load enough surrounding conversation history for a search-target jump before attempting scroll, or
- Add a targeted message-anchor lookup / paging strategy that can fetch until the target message is present, then scroll and highlight it.

## Files involved
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

## Acceptance criteria
- Tapping a dashboard message search result opens the correct conversation and scrolls to the tapped message.
- The target message is visibly highlighted after navigation.
- Behavior works even when the target message is older than the initial 100-message fetch window.
- Existing normal chat opening behavior remains unchanged when no target message ID is supplied.
notes:
Diagnosis from repo search:

- Dashboard message search already routes the selected message correctly.
  - `HomeScreen` calls `onNavigateToChatMessage(agentId, conversationId, messageId)` when both IDs are present.
  - `AppNavGraph` forwards that through `AgentChatRoute(agentId, conversationId, scrollToMessageId = messageId)`.
  - `ChatViewModel` reads `scrollToMessageId` from `SavedStateHandle`.
  - `ChatScreen` passes `scrollToMessageId` into `ChatContent`.
  - `ChatContent` tries a one-time jump with `listState.scrollToItem(...)` and highlight if it can find the target in the currently loaded messages.

- The likely failure is data availability, not navigation wiring.
  - `MessageRepository.fetchMessages()` currently loads only `limit = 100` ascending messages for the conversation via `MessageApi.listMessages(...)`.
  - The jump logic searches only within `state.messages`.
  - If the tapped search hit is older than the loaded 100-message window, the target message is absent, `indexOfFirst { it.id == scrollToMessageId }` returns `-1`, and chat opens without scrolling.

Key files verified:
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/dashboard/HomeScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/Routes.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/MessageApi.kt

Recommended implementation direction:
1. ensure the target message is fetched before attempting scroll (load older history or targeted window), then
2. perform the jump/highlight after the target is present in `state.messages`.

Implemented fix for tapped search-result message navigation.

What changed:
- `MessageRepository.fetchMessages()` now accepts an optional `targetMessageId`.
- When a target message ID is supplied, the repository pages forward through ascending conversation messages until the target is present (or paging is exhausted), instead of stopping at the initial 100-message window.
- `ChatViewModel` now forwards `scrollToMessageId` into both the initial load path and silent reload path when a conversation is active.

Why this fixes the bug:
- Search result navigation was already passing the correct `messageId` into `AgentChatRoute` and `ChatScreen` already had jump/highlight logic.
- The failure was that older target messages were often outside the first 100 loaded messages, so the target ID never appeared in `state.messages` and the jump silently did nothing.
- The target-aware fetch ensures the message exists before the existing scroll/highlight logic runs.

Verification:
- Added repository regression in `MessageRepositoryE2eTest` to prove paging continues until the target message appears.
- Added `ChatViewModelTest` regression to prove `scrollToMessageId` is forwarded into repository fetches.
- Clean-state verification completed with `:app:testDebugUnitTest` passing. Core test path still shows the known Kotlin incremental cache instability before falling back, but app-side verification completed successfully after fallback compilation.

title: Introduce tertiary color role across app UI
description:
Tertiary colors are defined in all 6 theme presets but barely used in actual UI (only 2 direct usages plus 3 CustomColors derivations). AGENTS.md section 6 now documents the color role policy and specific introduction targets.

Targeted introduction spots:
- Status differentiation: running/live/active chips should use tertiaryContainer (UsageScreen and AgentListScreen already do - extend to other status surfaces)
- Chart accent colors: use tertiary as third data-series color in Vico charts
- Accent FABs: use tertiaryContainer for secondary FABs when primary FAB already exists on screen
- Highlight badges: new/beta badges that need to stand out from primary and secondary chips

Not in scope: wholesale audit of all primary/secondary usage. This is targeted introduction only.

Ref: AGENTS.md section 6 Color role policy for full guidelines.
notes: Extended the design-system color-role rollout with a brighter complementary accent layer to reduce sterile neutral surfaces. Added freshAccent/freshAccentContainer fields to designsystem CustomColors, derived from the complementary hue of each preset primary in Theme.kt using shared HSL helpers in Color.kt, and applied the new accent container to high-traffic surfaces: dashboard stat and usage cards, usage analytics cards, agent list cards/meta chips, and the chat conversation header. Also fixed unrelated app compile blockers exposed during verification: approvalRequest smart-cast in ChatMessageComponents and the GeneratedUiRenderer naming collision between MessageContentFactory and GeneratedUiRegistry. Verification this session: :designsystem:compileDebugKotlin ✅ and :app:compileDebugKotlin ✅ from a clean build state.

title: Implement expandable header search pattern
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the app currently mixes always-visible search rows with toggle-under-topbar search. We need a reusable compact header search control that expands into a full search field and can start replacing the older patterns on representative screens.

title: Add homepage token usage analytics cards
description: Show real usage analytics on the dashboard/home screen: total token usage, tokens per hour, and model split. Reuse existing runtime APIs and repositories rather than inventing a separate analytics screen. The current homepage only shows entity counts, while token data already exists in run usage and step records. Implement a dashboard aggregation path, add UI cards/sections on HomeScreen, and cover the viewmodel/repository logic with tests.

title: Stabilize Kotlin incremental test compilation in android-compose
description: Gradle/Kotlin unit-test verification in android-compose intermittently hits incremental cache and daemon corruption errors during sequential dependency-upgrade validation (for example source-to-classes.tab, lookups.tab, class-attributes.tab registration/corruption, and missing backup file restore errors under core:compileDebugUnitTestKotlin). The repo still verifies green after reruns, so this is build-tooling instability rather than a product regression. Capture and address the incremental compilation/cache hygiene problem so future dependency and test work does not require retry-based validation.

title: Fix IdentityRepositoryTest baseline failures
description: The core test suite has pre-existing baseline failures in IdentityRepositoryTest, reproduced on Ktor 3.0.3 with ./gradlew --no-daemon clean :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.data.repository.IdentityRepositoryTest". Failing cases: (1) attachIdentity delegates to api; (2) detachIdentity delegates to api. The test harness uses FakeIdentityApi, whose attach/detach overrides currently throw 404 when the identity was never seeded, so the tests assert delegation but do not satisfy the fake's preconditions.
acceptance_criteria: IdentityRepositoryTest passes on baseline dependencies under :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.data.repository.IdentityRepositoryTest" by making the fake or tests consistent with expected attach/detach behavior.

title: Fix BlockRepositoryTest baseline failures
description: The core test suite has pre-existing baseline failures in BlockRepositoryTest, reproduced on Ktor 3.0.3 with ./gradlew --no-daemon clean :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.data.repository.BlockRepositoryTest". Failing cases: (1) updateGlobalBlock calls global API endpoint; (2) updateGlobalBlock can clear optional metadata. FakeBlockApi does not override updateGlobalBlock, so these tests fall through into the real BlockApi with a relaxed mock client and currently fail with OutOfMemoryError instead of exercising a deterministic fake path.
acceptance_criteria: BlockRepositoryTest passes on baseline dependencies under :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.data.repository.BlockRepositoryTest" by providing a real fake implementation for updateGlobalBlock or adjusting the tests to use a deterministic harness.

title: Fix AgentSearchTest baseline failures
description: The core test suite has a pre-existing baseline failure in AgentSearchTest that is not caused by the staged Ktor migration. Reproduced on current Ktor 3.0.3 with ./gradlew --no-daemon clean :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.domain.AgentSearchTest". Failing cases: (1) match model names — expected 2 results for query gpt-4o but got 1; (2) return empty when nothing matches — non-matching query still returns at least one result. The failure suggests the fuzzy scoring/threshold logic is too permissive or inconsistent for model-name matching and no-match filtering.
acceptance_criteria: AgentSearchTest passes on baseline dependencies under :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.domain.AgentSearchTest" without changing Ktor migration behavior.

title: Fix ChatViewModelE2eTest baseline failures
description: The app unit test suite has two known baseline failures in ChatViewModelE2eTest that predate dependency upgrades and currently keep :app:testDebugUnitTest red. Failing tests: (1) sendMessage auto creates conversation when none selected — assertion expected <2> but was <0>; (2) sendMessage processes SSE stream end to end for existing conversation — UncompletedCoroutinesError after 1 minute. These failures were reproduced during dependency validation and should be fixed so app-level dependency upgrades can rely on a green baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Both ChatViewModelE2eTest failures pass consistently under :app:testDebugUnitTest without introducing new test regressions.

title: Fix MessageRepositoryE2eTest baseline failures
description: The core unit test suite has four known baseline failures in MessageRepositoryE2eTest that predate current dependency upgrades and keep :core:testDebugUnitTest red when this suite is exercised. Failing tests: (1) fetchMessages requests ascending order and preserves API sequence — expected <asc> but was <null>; (2) fetchConversationInspectorMessages keeps metadata for debugging — assertion failure; (3) sendMessage keeps existing cached history after streaming completes — expected <2> but was <0>; (4) fetchMessages maps conversation messages from API — expected <2> but was <0>. These failures were reproduced during dependency validation and should be fixed so repository/runtime dependency upgrades can rely on a green baseline.
acceptance_criteria: All four MessageRepositoryE2eTest failures pass consistently under :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests "*MessageRepositoryE2eTest" without introducing new regressions.

title: Runtime dependency cleanup batch
description: Batch runtime/app dependency upgrades that affect shipped code paths and Android behavior.

title: Upgrade navigation-compose from 2.8.5
description: Move Navigation Compose to the latest stable release and verify nav graph, arguments, and animated drill transitions.

title: Upgrade activity-compose from 1.9.3
description: Upgrade Activity Compose and verify activity integration, back handling, and startup behavior.
notes: Tried bumping activity-compose to 1.13.0. Blocked by platform/tooling requirements: dependency requires AGP 8.9.1+ and compileSdk 36, while repo is on AGP 8.7.3 and compileSdk 35. Reverted. This bead should wait for a broader AGP/compileSdk upgrade.
close_reason: Upgraded activity-compose from 1.9.3 to the highest verified safe version under current tooling, 1.10.1. Versions 1.12.4 and 1.13.0 require AGP 8.9.1+ and compileSdk 36, so the broader line remains blocked until platform/tooling upgrades.

title: Upgrade kotlinx-serialization-json from 1.7.3
description: Update kotlinx serialization to the latest stable version compatible with Kotlin 2.3.x and verify model serialization/deserialization paths.
notes: Re-ran the serialization upgrade on kotlinx-serialization-json 1.11.0 (Kotlin 2.3.20-aligned) after the baseline/Ktor cleanup. Updated version pins in android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts, android-compose/chat/build.gradle.kts, and android-compose/core/build.gradle.kts from 1.7.3 to 1.11.0. Verification results on 1.11.0: ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:compileDebugKotlin :chat:compileDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.model.LettaMessageSerializerTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.stream.SseParserTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.SettingsRepositoryTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.util.ConnectivityMonitorTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.PassageApiTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.MessageRepositoryE2eTest passed on clean rerun; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest passed. No production/source migration was required beyond the dependency bump; the previously suspected PassageApi / MessageRepository / SseParser regressions did not reproduce once rerun cleanly after daemon/cache reset, so earlier failures were attributable to the existing Kotlin incremental/reporting flake tracked separately in letta-mobile-41tq.

title: Upgrade kotlinx coroutines from 1.9.0
description: Upgrade coroutines runtime and test dependencies from 1.9.0 and verify dispatchers, flows, and unit tests.
notes: Upgraded kotlinx-coroutines-android and kotlinx-coroutines-test from 1.9.0 to 1.10.2 in app/core/chat. Validation passed with: ./gradlew --no-daemon :chat:compileDebugKotlin :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.util.ConnectivityMonitorTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.repository.SettingsRepositoryTest :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.config.ConfigListViewModelTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.config.ConfigViewModelTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.conversations.ConversationsViewModelTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.schedules.ScheduleListViewModelTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.settings.AgentSettingsViewModelTest. Baseline check: MessageRepositoryE2eTest still fails on original 1.9.0, so it was excluded as a pre-existing failure rather than a regression from 1.10.2.

title: Upgrade androidx.security:security-crypto from 1.1.0-alpha06
description: Replace the long-lived alpha security-crypto dependency with the latest stable/supported version and verify encrypted storage behavior.
close_reason: Upgraded security-crypto from 1.1.0-alpha06 to 1.1.0 stable; core/app compile pass; affected tests pass

title: Upgrade Ktor client stack from 3.0.3
description: Bump the Ktor client dependencies from 3.0.3 to the latest stable compatible release and verify HTTP/auth/serialization flows.
notes: Staged migration completed beyond the original 3.1.3 checkpoint. Current working tree advances Ktor client artifacts in android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts and android-compose/core/build.gradle.kts from 3.1.3 to 3.4.2, including ktor-client-mock. Validation on 3.4.2: ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:compileDebugKotlin :core:compileDebugKotlin passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest passed; ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest passed. No Ktor source/API changes were required in LettaApiClient; the only supporting test change was hardening core/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/util/ConnectivityMonitorTest.kt to use a real SettingsRepository-backed setup instead of a flaky MockK activeConfig property stub, so full-suite verification remains stable. During verification, Kotlin incremental daemon/cache corruption noise still occurred intermittently, but clean reruns stayed green; follow-up should be tracked separately as build-tooling hygiene, not as a Ktor regression.

title: Dependency cleanup pass for stale Android libraries
description:
Why: several Android/Kotlin dependencies appear behind current stable releases or are pinned to older alpha lines.

What: audit and upgrade stale dependencies in controlled batches, verifying build/test impact per dependency or dependency family.

Outcome: remove obviously stale pins, reduce alpha/old-version risk, and leave the project on a more current dependency baseline.
notes: Follow-up safe test-runtime cleanup: upgraded org.junit.vintage:junit-vintage-engine from 5.10.2 to 5.10.5 in app/core/chat. Sequential verification passed with :core:testDebugUnitTest, :chat:testDebugUnitTest, and :app:testDebugUnitTest. Remaining stale candidates now look materially riskier than prior batches: Vico compose-m3 is far behind (2.1.2 vs 3.1.0 stable), markdown renderer artifacts are far behind (0.28.0 vs 0.40.2 stable), and Material3 is still on an alpha line upstream (alpha15 in repo vs alpha17 upstream). Those should be treated as separate explicit migration passes rather than mixed into the low-risk cleanup stream.,
close_reason: Completed the low-risk stale dependency cleanup stream. Safe batches shipped and verified for navigation/test libs, datastore and AndroidX test libs, paging/work runtime, immutable collections, and junit-vintage-engine. Remaining stale items now require dedicated migration/policy work and were split into follow-up issues letta-mobile-4hek, letta-mobile-a80q, and letta-mobile-6pyg.

title: Introduce SegmentedButton for exclusive-choice selectors
description: Zero SegmentedButton usage despite several textbook use cases. Current state: chat view mode selector (if exists), agent list view toggle (grid/list), theme mode selector in ConfigScreen. These are all mutually-exclusive choices — exactly what M3 SegmentedButton is designed for. FilterChip is used correctly in AgentListScreen for tag filtering (multi-select). AssistChip used in JobMonitorScreen for status display (correct). Switches used for binary settings (correct). Action: (1) Identify all mutually-exclusive selectors currently using Button/Toggle/Tab patterns. (2) Replace with SingleChoiceSegmentedButtonRow where appropriate. (3) Verify FilterChip is used for all multi-select/filter scenarios (tag filtering, model filtering). (4) Add component selection guidance to AGENTS.md: SegmentedButton = exclusive choice, FilterChip = filter/multi-select, InputChip = user-added tags, AssistChip = read-only metadata, Switch = binary on/off.

title: Expand motion usage — animateContentSize and AnimatedVisibility across app
description: animateContentSize() is only used in 2 files (ChatMessageComponents, ToolDetailScreen). Many expandable/collapsible elements across the app lack smooth height transitions: ThinkingSection (reasoning blocks), Accordions.kt, various collapsible detail sections in admin screens. AnimatedVisibility is used inconsistently. With MaterialExpressiveTheme and MotionScheme.expressive() now active, the app should leverage the expressive motion specs more broadly. Action: (1) Add animateContentSize() to all expandable sections (ThinkingSection, Accordions, detail panels). (2) Use AnimatedVisibility with fadeIn+slideInVertically for elements that appear/disappear (status banners, filter chips, tool results). (3) Use the expressive MotionScheme specs — MaterialTheme.motionScheme.fastSpatialSpec() for press states, slowEffectsSpec() for hero moments. (4) Document standard motion patterns in the motion policy (pkzn bead).

title: Build reusable searchable list screen scaffold
description: Standardize the common screen shell used by 22+ list/admin screens. Current pattern: each screen independently composes Scaffold + LargeFlexibleTopAppBar + LazyColumn + loading/error/empty states. This creates subtle inconsistencies in padding, state handling, scroll behavior, and empty state presentation. Build a reusable SearchableListScaffold composable in designsystem that encapsulates: (1) Scaffold with LettaTopBarDefaults.scaffoldContainerColor(). (2) LargeFlexibleTopAppBar with LettaTopBarDefaults.largeTopAppBarColors() and exitUntilCollapsedScrollBehavior with hoisted state. (3) Optional search slot (search bar that collapses with scroll). (4) Optional filter row (FilterChip row). (5) Optional FAB slot. (6) Content lambda with standardized LoadingIndicator/ErrorContent/EmptyState switching based on a sealed UiState. First adopter screens: bz40.2.2 (project grid), bz40.2.6 (agents dashboard). Migration targets: AgentListScreen, AllToolsScreen, McpScreen, BlockLibraryScreen, and all admin screens (archives, folders, groups, identities, providers, schedules, runs, jobs, messageBatches).
design: Initial adopters should include the upcoming bz40 work, especially letta-mobile-bz40.2.2 (project grid home screen) and letta-mobile-bz40.2.3 (project agent chat), so the reusable scaffold lands where new screen families are being added rather than being applied retroactively.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable scaffold or documented pattern exists for searchable list/admin screens and initial adopter screens are identified.

title: Standardize selection and emphasis components
description: Audit and standardize component choices based on current codebase findings. Current state: FilterChip used correctly in AgentListScreen (tag filtering). AssistChip used correctly in JobMonitorScreen (status display). Switch used for binary toggles (correct). Zero SegmentedButton usage despite multiple exclusive-choice patterns. 23 screens use raw AlertDialog. Only 2 screens use ActionSheet. FormItem has zero adoption. CardGroup has only 2 usages. Standardization rules: (1) SegmentedButton for exclusive choices (view mode, theme mode, sort order). (2) FilterChip for multi-select filters (tags, status, model). (3) InputChip for user-added items (tags in edit flows). (4) AssistChip for read-only metadata (status, type labels). (5) Switch for binary on/off only. (6) ActionSheet for destructive/contextual actions (currently AlertDialog in most places). (7) ConfirmDialog for blocking yes/no decisions (already well-adopted). (8) TextInputDialog for single-field text input. (9) FormItem for label+control pairs in settings/forms. (10) CardGroup for grouped list items in settings screens.
acceptance_criteria: Key screens have guidance for when to use segmented buttons, chips, emphasis surfaces, and contextual action components.

title: Audit duplicate agent avatar shared-element anchors
description: AgentListScreen currently applies the same shared-element key pattern agent_avatar_{agentId} in multiple card variants, including AgentCard and CompactAgentCard. We need to verify whether both layouts can coexist during recomposition or mode switches and ensure reverse navigation resolves to a single deterministic source anchor.
design: Relevant locations observed during investigation: AgentListScreen.kt around AgentCard shared element at lines ~556-560 and CompactAgentCard shared element at lines ~727-733. This task supports the return-transition bug fix by eliminating ambiguous source anchors.
acceptance_criteria: Document where agent_avatar shared-element keys are emitted in AgentListScreen and related screens; confirm whether multiple anchors for the same agent can coexist during return transitions; either remove duplicate anchors or constrain rendering so only one eligible source anchor exists per agent during the animation path.

title: Update agent list immediately after successful delete
description: Deleting duplicate agents succeeds, but the Android Compose agent list does not immediately remove the deleted entry from the visible list. Update the repository/viewmodel path so successful deletes mutate local state right away and the list animates the removed agent off the page.

title: Reset chat background when theme changes
description: Persisted custom chat backgrounds currently survive theme switches, which can leave chat visually out of sync after moving between light/dark or preset themes. Reset the chat background to default whenever the app theme or theme preset changes, and verify that settings tests cover the migration behavior.

title: Align chat view with current theme styling
description: The Android Compose chat view still looks visually out of step with the current app theme. Audit the chat scaffold and message surface styling, remove any remaining legacy screen configuration usage, and align chat colors/backgrounds/top-bar defaults with the newer themed screens.

title: Unify connectivity checks with the main API client strategy
description: Connectivity monitoring currently depends on a raw HttpClient path that appears separate from the cached LettaApiClient used for production API calls. Verify how that client is provided, unify reuse where appropriate, and ensure reachability checks do not create avoidable extra client instances or unnecessary network churn.

title: Profile image and media loading client reuse
description: The app uses Coil with a Ktor network integration, which may or may not share the same underlying connection reuse behavior as the main API client. Audit image-loading client reuse and request behavior so avatars and media do not become a hidden loading bottleneck alongside API traffic.

title: Audit remaining old-style Android Compose screens
description: Scan android-compose app screens for older plain Scaffold/TopAppBar patterns, inconsistent surface containers, and non-semantic typography usage. Prioritize screens not using LettaTopBarDefaults.scaffoldContainerColor(), LettaTopBarDefaults.largeTopAppBarColors(), or newer themed section patterns. Produce a migration checklist with affected files and recommended replacements.

title: Configurable project folder path — view, edit, validate filesystem location
description: Add configurable project folder path within the project settings. The filesystem_path field in VibSync determines where the project lives on the server. From the Android app, users should be able to: view the current path, edit it (text field with path validation), or browse/select from known project directories. This path is passed to coding agents when they're dispatched to work on the project. Also display git_url and allow editing. Store path changes back to VibSync via the API. Show a warning if the path doesn't exist or isn't accessible from the server.
notes: Added an honest configurable project folder path slice in the project settings dialog.
