Path editing still saves through the existing registry PATCH flow, but the dialog now distinguishes missing, non-absolute, known-project, and unknown-server-access states. Added tappable known directory suggestions derived from existing project filesystem paths, plus focused tests for suggestion derivation, guidance classification, and clearing git_url on save. Verification passed with :app:compileDebugKotlin and :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.projects.ProjectHomeViewModelTest.

title: Roll out animateContentSize to expandable detail surfaces
description: Audit follow-up from d2ar.9. animateContentSize currently appears in only one obvious place. Identify the shipped expandable/collapsible UI surfaces that should resize smoothly and add bounded animateContentSize behavior where it improves perceived polish without destabilizing layout.
acceptance_criteria: 1) animateContentSize is applied to more than the current single ToolDetailScreen usage. 2) Only genuinely expandable/collapsible surfaces are updated; no random blanket application. 3) At least the key expandable surfaces touched by the audit show smooth size transitions in code. 4) Compile/tests pass. 5) A grep for animateContentSize shows the rollout extending beyond the pre-audit single location.

title: Run an app-wide typography hierarchy normalization pass
description: Audit follow-up from d2ar.11. Typography usage improved in spots, but there is no clear app-wide hierarchy pass. Normalize headings, section labels, metadata text, and supporting copy across the main screens so section structure is deliberate and consistent instead of screen-by-screen drift.
acceptance_criteria: 1) The pass touches the high-traffic screens called out in the audit (chat, conversations, agents, config/settings, major admin detail screens) rather than a token one-file change. 2) Section headings, metadata labels, and supporting text use a deliberate hierarchy with consistent token choices. 3) New typography choices are applied consistently, not ad hoc. 4) Compile/tests pass. 5) The resulting diff clearly shows a hierarchy pass, not isolated copy edits.

title: Add conversation date groups and pinned ordering to conversation list
description: Audit follow-up from d2ar.10. ConversationsScreen has admin/context actions, but the review target also expects date grouping and pinned ordering. Add deterministic grouping in the list model/rendering, pinned presentation, and any minimal state needed to support the UI behavior without regressing navigation or existing admin actions.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Conversation list renders date groups rather than a single flat list. 2) Pinned conversations, if present, are surfaced ahead of normal groups. 3) Existing click, long-press, rename, fork, and delete actions still work. 4) The list implementation shows concrete grouping/pinning logic rather than only context menus. 5) :app:compileDebugKotlin and :app:testDebugUnitTest pass.

title: Add preset theme system — multiple color schemes beyond light/dark
description: RikkaHub supports preset themes (Autumn, Black, Sakura, Spring, Ocean) beyond just light/dark + dynamic color. Each preset defines a full ColorScheme for both light and dark modes. Current Letta app has a static light/dark theme. Add: preset theme selection in settings, at minimum 3-4 presets (Default, Ocean, AMOLED Black, Sakura) each providing lightColorScheme and darkColorScheme. Store selection in SettingsRepository. Allow dynamic color (Material You) as an option that overrides presets on Android 12+. Reference: RikkaHub's PresetTheme.kt and presets/ directory.

title: Tighten typography hierarchy across all screens
description: Typography needs tightening to match RikkaHub's hierarchy. RikkaHub uses: titleSmallEmphasized (primary color) for CardGroup section titles, bodyLarge for headlines, bodyMedium for supporting text in onSurfaceVariant, labelLarge+Bold for date section headers. Current Letta app likely uses default MaterialTheme.typography without consistent semantic application. Audit all screens: section titles should use titleSmall in primary, card headlines should use bodyLarge, supporting text should use bodyMedium in onSurfaceVariant. Remove any hardcoded fontSize values. Exact font family (Inter?) TBD from Penpot mocks.

title: Wire up conversation date grouping, pinning, and long-press menus
description: RikkaHub conversation list has: date-based section headers (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 days) styled with primary color + bold label, pin support with AnimatedVisibility pin icons, and long-press dropdown menus (pin/unpin, regenerate title, move to assistant, delete). TimelineGrouper exists in Letta codebase (tested!) but verify it's wired up to the conversation list UI. Ensure: SectionList with date headers, pinned section at top, long-press context menu with DropdownMenu, animated pin icon. Reference: RikkaHub's ConversationList.kt.

title: Add smooth layout animations — animateContentSize on expanding elements
description: RikkaHub applies animateContentSize() on expanding/collapsing elements — reasoning blocks, tool call results, collapsible sections all smoothly animate height changes. Also uses AnimatedVisibility with fadeIn/fadeOut + slideInVertically/slideOutVertically for message elements appearing. Current Letta app has limited animation — ToolDetailScreen uses animateContentSize but chat messages, reasoning blocks, and tool results lack smooth transitions. Add: animateContentSize() on ThinkingSection/reasoning blocks, AnimatedVisibility on tool results expanding, fadeIn/slideIn on new chat messages appearing. Use the expressive motion scheme specs for spring configs.

title: Adopt consistent icon set — HugeIcons or Lucide for uniform stroke weight
description: RikkaHub uses HugeIcons (stroke style) consistently — a premium icon set with consistent 1.5px stroke weight across all icons. Current Letta app uses default Material icons which have inconsistent visual weight and feel generic. Either: (a) add hugeicons dependency and replace key icons (settings, chat, nav, actions), or (b) switch to Lucide icons which have a similar consistent stroke aesthetic and are well-supported in Compose. Focus on navigation icons, list leading icons, action icons, and toolbar icons. Standardize icon sizing: 24dp for nav/toolbar, 20dp for list leading, 16dp for inline/status.

title: Add smooth content size animations on expanding/collapsing elements
description: RikkaHub applies animateContentSize() on expanding elements — reasoning blocks, tool call results, collapsible sections all smoothly animate their height changes. Current Letta tool interactions and reasoning messages just appear/disappear with no transition. Add LayoutAnimation or react-native-reanimated layout transitions to: tool-interaction.tsx (expand/collapse tool results), reasoning-message.tsx (expand/collapse reasoning), memory-blocks/ (expand/collapse block content), any collapsible/expandable section. Use Reanimated's Layout.springify() or FadeIn/FadeOut entering/exiting animations for list items appearing in chat.
close_reason: Killed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20 — RikkaHub design-parity arc abandoned (React Native direction dropped)

title: Sharpen typography hierarchy — section titles, headlines, body, captions
description: RikkaHub section titles use MaterialTheme.typography.titleSmallEmphasized in primary color with consistent hierarchy: section titles are small+bold+primary, headlines are bodyLarge, supporting text is bodyMedium in onSurfaceVariant. Letta Mobile uses generic preset='heading' and preset='bold' with no clear typographic scale. Define a proper type scale in typography.ts: sectionTitle (12-13px, bold, primary color, uppercase tracking), headline (16px, semibold), body (14px, regular), caption (12px, regular, textDim). Apply consistently: AgentSettingsScreen section headers, CardGroup titles, list item text hierarchy. Remove ad-hoc fontSize overrides scattered through screen files.
close_reason: Killed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20 — RikkaHub design-parity arc abandoned (React Native direction dropped)

title: Add bottom sheet action menus for destructive/contextual operations
description: RikkaHub uses ModalBottomSheet for destructive and contextual actions — feels intentional and modern. Letta Mobile uses SimpleContextMenu (native iOS/Android context menus) which work but feel platform-default rather than polished. Add a reusable ActionSheet/BottomSheet component using @gorhom/bottom-sheet or react-native-modal. Use for: agent delete confirmation, agent actions (edit/delete/duplicate), chat message actions (copy/retry/delete). Include icon + title + optional destructive red styling for delete actions. The sheet should have a drag handle, rounded top corners (20px), and backdrop blur if possible.
close_reason: Killed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20 — RikkaHub design-parity arc abandoned (React Native direction dropped)

title: Standardize icon library — consistent stroke weight and sizing
description: RikkaHub uses HugeIcons (stroke style) consistently across the entire app — every icon has matching stroke weight and visual language. Letta Mobile currently uses Lucide icons which are decent but the stroke weights and design language differ from RikkaHub's look. Either: (a) switch to react-native-hugeicons or a HugeIcons SVG set for full visual match, or (b) audit all icon usage to ensure consistent stroke width (1.5-2px), consistent sizing (20-24dp for list leading, 16dp for trailing/status), and consistent color (using theme semantic colors, not hardcoded). Standardize icon sizing across the app.
close_reason: Killed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20 — RikkaHub design-parity arc abandoned (React Native direction dropped)

title: Add conversation list date grouping, pinning, and context menus
description: RikkaHub groups conversations by date (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 days, etc.) with primary-colored section headers, supports pinning with AnimatedVisibility pin icons, and long-press context menus for pin/delete/regenerate-title/move. Current Letta conversation-selector.tsx is a flat unsorted list. Add: date-based grouping with section headers styled in primary color + bold label. Pin support (pinned section at top). Long-press dropdown menu matching RikkaHub's pattern (pin, regenerate title, delete). Show pin icon with animated visibility on pinned items.
close_reason: Killed by Emmanuel 2026-04-20 — RikkaHub design-parity arc abandoned (React Native direction dropped)

title: Add press-state animations — animated corner radius and spring scale
description: RikkaHub animates corner radius on press using animateDpAsState with MaterialTheme.motionScheme.fastSpatialSpec(). Items visually 'pop' their corners when pressed — the pressed item gets full 20dp corners while siblings keep 4dp. Current Letta uses TouchableOpacity with flat activeOpacity: 0.8 which just dims the element. Replace TouchableOpacity press states with Pressable + react-native-reanimated: on press-in, animate borderRadius expansion and subtle scale (0.98), with spring config (damping: 15, stiffness: 150). Apply to agent list items, CardGroup items, and any tappable list rows.

title: Add direct mobile push transport for Letta channel delivery
description: Bead wjkc now delivers proactive updates through background heartbeat polling plus Android notifications and tap-through into conversations. The remaining gap is true server-initiated mobile delivery: device-token registration, server-side push fanout, and transport-specific delivery semantics once backend support exists. Track that follow-up explicitly instead of leaving it folded into poll-based delivery.

title: Set up voice message infrastructure for mobile transcription
description:
## Why
Voice message support in Letta Mobile is blocked on missing infrastructure: there is no confirmed server-side transcription endpoint or provider-backed speech pipeline available to the app yet. Before implementing the mobile UI/recording flow, we need a supported backend/integration path for transcription (and optionally TTS).

## What needs to be done
- Decide whether voice transcription should go through Letta server, a new backend endpoint, or direct provider integration
- Document auth/provider requirements for Whisper or equivalent STT
- Expose a stable API contract the Android app can call for audio transcription
- Optionally define reply-audio/TTS support if that remains in scope

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete infra/API path exists for mobile voice transcription
- Required credentials, endpoints, and request/response shapes are documented
- Mobile implementation can proceed without guessing backend behavior
notes: Created as prerequisite while pausing letta-mobile-9gho at user request. This infra/API bead now owns the missing backend path for transcription/TTS.

title: Triage remaining app unit test failures exposed after config cleanup
description:
## Why
After fixing the pre-existing BlockLibraryViewModelTest and ConfigViewModelTest blockers, :app:testDebugUnitTest now runs far enough to reveal a broader set of unrelated failing suites that were previously masked. These need their own tracked follow-up so verification debt stays visible and scoped.

## What needs to be done
- Investigate and fix the remaining app unit test failures now exposed by the cleanup
- Current failing suites include ArchivalViewModelTest, JobMonitorViewModelTest, ModelBrowserViewModelTest, RunMonitorViewModelTest, ScheduleListViewModelTest, AgentSettingsViewModelTest, AllToolsViewModelTest, and ToolDetailViewModelTest
- Separate true regressions from stale test expectations or broken test scaffolding
- Restore a clean :app:testDebugUnitTest run or split the work into smaller beads if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remaining exposed app unit test failures are tracked and no longer unowned
- Follow-up work documents the currently failing suites and the command used to reproduce them
notes: Repro command: env JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64 ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest (run from android-compose). After fixing the masked blockers, the currently exposed failing suites are: ArchivalViewModelTest (9 failures, NPE at test setup), JobMonitorViewModelTest (cancelJob active-only assertion), ModelBrowserViewModelTest (5 failures, NPE at setup), RunMonitorViewModelTest (loadRuns assertion), ScheduleListViewModelTest (createSchedule comparison failure), AgentSettingsViewModelTest (saveSettings assertion), AllToolsViewModelTest (2 filter assertion failures), ToolDetailViewModelTest (3 Turbine timeout failures).

title: Fix pre-existing app unit test compile blockers
description:
## Why
App unit test verification is currently masked by two unrelated compile blockers: BlockLibraryViewModelTest fails because its fake repo is missing the retrieveBlock implementation, and ConfigViewModelTest still references outdated theme state. This prevents clean :app:testDebugUnitTest runs and hides whether new app-layer tests actually pass.

## What needs to be done
- Repair the fake/repository test scaffolding so BlockLibraryViewModelTest compiles again
- Update ConfigViewModelTest to the current theme model/API so it compiles and validates the intended behavior
- Re-run targeted app unit tests and, if possible, the broader app test task to confirm these blockers are gone
- Keep the fix scoped to test infrastructure and test expectations unless a true production bug is uncovered

## Acceptance Criteria
- BlockLibraryViewModelTest compiles and passes
- ConfigViewModelTest compiles and passes
- App unit test verification can run without these two compile failures masking results

title: Audit tool write-model parity against current Letta tool schemas
description: Why: the 9v37 audit surfaced that Android tool create/update payloads may lag current Letta tool request models, but the exact parity boundary is complicated by server-side schema generation and existing UI assumptions around editable tool names. What needs to be done: compare android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Tool.kt and tool-related UI flows against /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/tool.py and routers/v1/tools.py, then implement any safe request-model parity changes with UI updates and tests. Evidence: ToolApi/ToolRepository, ToolDetailViewModel, AllToolsViewModel, local Letta tool schemas and router wrappers.

title: Add count endpoints for remaining Android admin APIs
description: Why: the 9v37 parity audit found multiple backend count endpoints already exposed by Letta but still missing in Android admin API/repository layers beyond tools. What needs to be done: add and verify count coverage for providers and any other remaining admin surfaces once upstream router support is confirmed in local Letta source, matching existing patterns used by agents, folders, groups, identities, blocks, and now tools. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/
close_reason: No remaining Android implementation gap. Verified upstream count routes exist only for agents, blocks, folders, groups, identities, tools, and deprecated sources. Android already implements count support for agents/blocks/folders/groups/identities/tools; providers/jobs/archives/mcp/models/messages have no upstream count route, and deprecated sources are not exposed as an Android source admin surface yet.

title: Add group and provider admin screens
description: Why: fnpr adds core support for groups and providers, but the Android app still has no UI for managing multi-agent groups or provider configurations. What needs to be done: add group and provider list/detail/create/update flows wired to GroupRepository and ProviderRepository, covering group messages/reset and provider validation actions where sensible. Evidence: app/src/main/java has no group or provider screens and no usages of the new repositories.

title: Add message batch monitoring UI
description: Why: fnpr extends MessageApi and MessageRepository with message batch create/list/retrieve/cancel/message endpoints, but there is no app-layer workflow to inspect or operate Letta message batches. What needs to be done: add a batch jobs screen or extend existing job monitoring to show Letta message batches, their per-batch messages, and cancellation controls. Evidence: core now exposes createBatch/listBatches/listBatchMessages but app/src/main/java has no consumers for these methods.

title: Add archive and folder admin screens
description: Why: fnpr adds core model/api/repository support for archives and folders, but the Android app still has no UI to browse, create, edit, inspect files/passages, or attach these resources operationally. What needs to be done: add archive and folder list/detail screens plus viewmodels wired to ArchiveRepository and FolderRepository, including folder files/passages metadata and archive agent relationships. Evidence: app/src/main/java currently has no archive or folder screens, while core now exposes ArchiveRepository and FolderRepository.

title: Add ConfirmDialog and Input utility components to designsystem
description: RikkaHub has reusable ConfirmDialog and Input components in ui/components/ui/. Letta Mobile builds AlertDialogs ad-hoc in each screen. Create standardized: ConfirmDialog (title, message, confirm/cancel with destructive variant), TextInputDialog (label, placeholder, validation), and BottomSheetPicker (for model/tool/block selection). Place in designsystem module for cross-screen reuse.

title: Add ChatMessage component decomposition — separate avatar, actions, reasoning, tools
description: RikkaHub decomposes chat messages into focused composables: ChatMessageAvatar, ChatMessageActions (copy, regenerate, branch), ChatMessageBranch (message branching UI), ChatMessageCot/ChatMessageReasoning (chain-of-thought display), ChatMessageTools (tool call rendering), ChatMessageTranslation. Letta Mobile's ChatScreen handles message rendering more monolithically. Decompose into: MessageAvatar, MessageActions (with context menu), MessageToolCalls (expandable tool display), MessageReasoning (collapsible thinking section). This enables per-component iteration and testing.

title: Add ModalBottomSheet action menus for destructive/contextual operations
description: RikkaHub uses ModalBottomSheet for contextual actions (clone, delete) with avatar header, divider, and ListItem rows with colored icons. Letta Mobile currently uses DropdownMenu and AlertDialog for most actions. Adopt ModalBottomSheet pattern for agent actions, tool actions, block actions, and conversation actions — it's more mobile-native and gives more room for information. Reference: rikkahub AssistantActionSheet (lines 482-578).

title: Redesign agent list cards with avatar, tags, metadata chips, and drag-to-reorder
description: RikkaHub's AssistantItem uses: UIAvatar (48dp with hero animation), titleMedium name, Tag chips for memory count and categories, MoreVertical menu, and drag-to-reorder via rememberReorderableLazyListState with haptic feedback. Letta Mobile's AgentListScreen uses a basic 3-column grid with Card+Column. Redesign to: single-column list with richer cards showing avatar placeholder, agent name+description, tag chips (agent tags), tool count badge, and optional long-press reorder. Keep grid option as toggle but default to list view. Reference: rikkahub AssistantPage.kt lines 391-480.

title: Add collapsible LargeFlexibleTopAppBar with scroll behavior to main screens
description: RikkaHub uses LargeFlexibleTopAppBar with exitUntilCollapsedScrollBehavior and custom topBarColors across all screens, giving a polished collapse-on-scroll effect. Letta Mobile uses basic TopAppBar everywhere. Upgrade AgentListScreen, ChatScreen, BlockLibraryScreen, AllToolsScreen, McpScreen, and other list-heavy screens to use LargeFlexibleTopAppBar with coordinated scroll behavior. Reference: rikkahub AssistantPage.kt lines 114-131.

title: Add preset theme system with Material Expressive + AMOLED dark mode
description: RikkaHub uses MaterialExpressiveTheme with MotionScheme.expressive(), preset themes (Sakura, Ocean, Autumn, Spring, Black), AMOLED dark mode (pure black background), and extended color tokens. Letta Mobile currently has basic MaterialTheme with hardcoded light/dark color schemes and no preset themes. Add: PresetTheme data class + 4-5 preset color schemes, AMOLED dark mode toggle, MaterialExpressiveTheme with motion scheme, user-selectable theme in settings.

title: Bring Android agent config models into parity with current Letta schemas
description: Why: the Android Compose app's Agent, LlmConfig, and related config models expose only a thin subset of the local Letta schema. Verified omissions include major llm_config fields, agent configuration fields, and richer provider/config metadata already present in /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/agent.py and llm_config.py. What needs to be done: expand Android core models and dependent API/repository parsing for agent and configuration payloads so the admin app can faithfully inspect and edit current Letta config surfaces. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Agent.kt, LlmConfig.kt, ModelSettings.kt, Model.kt and /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/agent.py, llm_config.py, embedding_config.py

title: Bring Android message and usage models into parity with current Letta response unions
description: Why: the Android Compose app's shared message/usage models are narrower than the local Letta source contracts. Verified gaps include missing terminal/meta response shapes such as stop_reason and usage_statistics, incomplete approval response fields, and narrower handling of tool return and message union variants. Oracle review also flagged that run and step admin work will depend on this parity. What needs to be done: compare android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt and Approval.kt against /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/letta_message.py, message.py, job.py LettaRequestConfig, and OpenAI-style usage schemas; add missing models/serializers and update dependent APIs/tests. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Message.kt, Approval.kt, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/letta_message.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/message.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/openai/chat_completion_response.py

title: Expose agent-adjacent admin resources missing from Android service models
description: Why: the Android Compose service layer only exposes part of the upstream agent admin surface. Verified missing API-backed domains include agent counts/import, tool approval toggles, attached folders/files/groups, archive attach/detach, message modify/cancel, and other adjacent agent resources already supported by local Letta source. What needs to be done: audit and expose the missing agent-adjacent resources in core data/api, data/repository, and data/model, then wire high-value admin actions into UI beads. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/AgentApi.kt, MessageApi.kt, related repositories, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/agents.py

title: Expose block relationship and retrieval parity in Android admin
description: Why: local Letta source exposes global block retrieval, counts, connected agents, and identity attach/detach on blocks, while the Android Compose block layer currently covers only a subset focused on list/create/update/delete and agent attachment. What needs to be done: add missing block API/repository/model support for retrieve-by-id, counts, list agents for block, and identity attach/detach workflows; expose these in admin surfaces as appropriate. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/BlockApi.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/BlockRepository.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Block.kt, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/blocks.py

title: Add missing admin domains for archives, folders, groups, providers, and batch jobs
description: Why: local Letta source exposes several admin domains with dedicated schemas and routers — archives, folders, groups, providers, and message batch jobs — that are not represented in the Android Compose core model/api/repository layer today. What needs to be done: add model/service support for these domains and break UI work into follow-up beads where needed, so the mobile admin app can grow toward parity with the upstream operational surface. Evidence: /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/archives.py, folders.py, groups.py, providers.py, messages.py plus corresponding schemas under /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/, compared against android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model and data/api.

title: Add Android model/service exposure for steps, traces, and step metrics
description: Why: local Letta source exposes a full steps surface with list/retrieve, step metrics, provider traces, feedback mutation, and step message listing, but the Android Compose admin app has no corresponding core models or APIs. What needs to be done: add step-related models, APIs, repositories, and follow-up UI/admin surfaces so operational run/step inspection can reach parity with the backend. Evidence: /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/steps.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/step.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/step_metrics.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/provider_trace.py, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/

title: Expose identity relationship queries and counts in Android admin
description: Why: local Letta source exposes identity admin capabilities beyond simple CRUD, including count, list agents for identity, list blocks for identity, and property upsert. The Android Compose app currently only models list/retrieve/create/upsert/update/delete plus agent attach/detach, so identity relationship/admin visibility is incomplete. What needs to be done: add missing identity API/repository/model support for count and relationship queries, and expose supported property operations where useful in the admin UI. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/IdentityApi.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Identity.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/IdentityRepository.kt, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/identities.py

title: Add run subresource model parity and admin exposure
description: Why: the Android Compose app models the base run object but does not expose verified upstream run subresources already supported by local Letta source under /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/runs.py. The server exposes run messages, usage, metrics, steps, and delete, but android-compose/core currently only models list/retrieve runs. What needs to be done: add missing run-related models and API/repository methods for the supported run subresources; update run admin surfaces and tests to expose them without inventing unsupported mutations. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/RunApi.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Run.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/RunRepository.kt, android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/runs/RunMonitor*.kt, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/runs.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/run_metrics.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/step.py

title: Bring MCP server models into parity with Letta server schema
description: Why: the Android Compose MCP server model appears thinner than the upstream Letta MCP server schemas and may hide supported server types or configuration fields. What needs to be done: compare android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/McpServer.kt and related API/repository code with /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/mcp.py and mcp_server.py, then expose missing API-backed fields and any supported admin operations required by the mobile admin surface. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/McpServer.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/McpServerApi.kt, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/McpServerRepository.kt, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/mcp.py, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/mcp_server.py

title: Audit and expose missing API-backed model fields across Android admin surfaces
description: Why: the Android Compose app has several API-backed models that only partially expose upstream Letta schema fields, which hides supported admin capabilities and risks schema drift. What needs to be done: perform a service-wide parity audit across core data/api, data/repository, and data/model against the local Letta source in /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source; identify missing API-backed fields and missing response models; file or implement follow-up work for each concrete gap. Initial verified examples include Agent model omissions such as metadata, identities, timezone/file limits/hidden/parallel tool settings parity concerns; MCP server parity concerns; and missing run subresource models. Evidence: android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/, android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/schemas/, /opt/stacks/letta/letta-source/letta/server/rest_api/routers/v1/

title: Add Letta job monitoring and admin actions
description:
## Why
The current Kotlin admin surface includes run monitoring but not the broader Letta job surface. Jobs are a distinct operational domain and should be visible in the admin app where the backend exposes them.

## Scope
- Add a jobs monitor/admin screen if the current Letta API surface supports jobs in this deployment target
- Expose useful job details and any safe admin actions such as cancellation where supported
- Keep runs and jobs separate in the UX unless the API makes them effectively the same concept

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/runs/RunMonitorScreen.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/RunApi.kt
- external Letta API review identified jobs as a separate admin surface missing from the app

## Deliverables
- Jobs appear as a first-class operational/admin surface when supported
- Admin users can inspect job state without leaving the app
- Any exposed action is safe, explicit, and backed by real API support
design: Do not overload the existing run monitor unless the data models are nearly identical. Prefer a separate operational surface if jobs are distinct in Letta.
close_reason: All deliverables already implemented: JobMonitorScreen with list/search/active-only toggle, detail dialog with cancel+delete, JobMonitorViewModel with full CRUD, JobApi+JobRepository, navigation wired at 'jobs' route, 8 ViewModel tests.

title: Deepen MCP admin with execute and debug workflows
description:
## Why
The Kotlin app now supports MCP server CRUD, discovered-tool listing, and parent-server navigation, but deeper MCP lifecycle operations are still missing. Admin users often need to validate, execute, register, or debug MCP tools and server behavior directly.

## Scope
- Add the highest-value MCP lifecycle/debug operations supported by the current Letta/MCP surface
- Consider tool execution, registration, validation, and richer server diagnostics where supported
- Keep current discovered-tool and parent-server flows intact
- Avoid turning this into a generic browser for deprecated ingestion domains

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/mcp/McpScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/mcp/McpServerToolsScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/mcp/McpViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/McpServerApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/McpServerRepository.kt

## Deliverables
- MCP admin supports deeper server/tool lifecycle operations beyond CRUD and discovery
- Admin users can debug MCP behavior more directly from the mobile app
- New UI clearly distinguishes destructive actions from diagnostics/testing
design: Prioritize workflows that reduce debugging friction: server validation, tool execution, and registration-style operations if the backend supports them.

title: Expose identity update and attachment workflows
description:
## Why
Identity management exists in the Kotlin app only as list/detail/create/delete, while the repository and Letta API also support update, upsert, attach, and detach operations. That leaves identity administration incomplete for agents and memory relationships.

## Scope
- Add edit/update flows for identities
- Expose attach/detach identity workflows where Letta supports them
- Decide whether upsert belongs in the mobile admin UX or should stay internal
- Surface identity relationships clearly enough to manage agent/block associations

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/identities/IdentityListScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/identities/IdentityListViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/IdentityApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/IdentityRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Identity.kt

## Deliverables
- Identity edit/update flow exists in the UI
- Attach/detach workflows are available where supported by the API
- UI makes identity relationships visible enough for admin usage
design: Build on the existing identity detail surface rather than creating a separate subsystem. Keep CRUD, update, and relationship management in one coherent admin flow.

title: Add conversation message inspector and admin surface
description:
## Why
Conversation administration currently covers rename, archive, fork, cancel, and recompile, but there is no standalone message inspection/admin surface beyond entering chat. For admin/debugging work, message history often needs direct inspection outside the chat UI.

## Scope
- Add a conversation message inspector/admin view separate from the main chat composer
- Surface useful message metadata and ordering clearly
- Support conversation debugging tasks without depending on interactive chat usage
- Reuse the existing message and conversation repositories where possible

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/MessageApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt

## Deliverables
- Admin users can inspect conversation messages without entering normal chat flow
- Message metadata/order are visible enough for debugging and auditing
- Conversation admin is no longer limited to top-level conversation operations
design: Keep the inspector read-focused unless the API clearly supports safe message-level admin actions. Favor reuse of normalized message ordering and repository logic already present in chat.

title: Expand run and schedule admin beyond basic monitor flows
description:
## Why
The app now has basic run monitoring plus schedule list/create/delete support, but both domains remain thin compared with the operational/admin needs of a Letta deployment. Runs are mostly read-only, and schedules lack richer detail/edit workflows.

## Scope
- Add higher-value run detail/admin actions where supported by the API
- Add schedule detail and edit capabilities where supported by the API
- Keep the operational UX explicit about destructive actions and current execution state

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/runs/RunMonitorScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/runs/RunMonitorViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/schedules/ScheduleListScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/schedules/ScheduleListViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/RunApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/ScheduleApi.kt

## Deliverables
- Run monitoring is more than read-only inspection where API support exists
- Schedule administration includes richer detail/edit workflows where API support exists
- Operational admin coverage is more complete without collapsing runs and schedules into one screen
design: Treat runs and schedules as adjacent operational domains with separate lifecycles. Share components if useful, but do not flatten their distinct admin semantics.
notes: Implemented and pushed run admin expansion in four commits: 59b7260 adds run API subresources and models, 77ce45e adds repository support and fake APIs, 7b5a5d4 expands run monitor viewmodel state and tests, 146b1cc adds run detail UI actions and strings. Linux verification still blocked by missing kotlin-lsp plus Android SDK license acceptance; Windows build/test verification remains required.
close_reason: Implemented and pushed run admin expansion; user confirmed build passed during Windows testing.

title: Add archive management beyond per-agent passages
description:
## Why
The Kotlin app already exposes per-agent archival passages, search, and passage CRUD, but it does not expose a broader archive-management surface. The Letta admin model includes archive-level concepts beyond individual passage browsing.

## Scope
- Research and implement archive-level management flows supported by the current Letta API
- Distinguish archive management from passage CRUD/search
- Exclude deprecated sources/files/folders ingestion work from this issue
- Keep archival UI grounded in current API support rather than speculative abstractions

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/archival/ArchivalScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/archival/ArchivalViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/PassageApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/PassageRepository.kt
- external Letta API review identified archive-level admin gaps beyond passages

## Deliverables
- Archive-level admin capabilities are identified and exposed where supported
- Per-agent passage CRUD remains intact but is no longer the only archival admin surface
- Deprecated ingestion domains remain out of scope
design: Do not fold sources/files/folders back into archival just because they are adjacent in the API. Keep this bead focused on archives and archival-memory management.
notes: Implemented archive-agent relationship management in the archive admin surface. Added AgentApi attach/detach archive endpoints, threaded them through AgentRepository, exposed attach/detach controls in ArchiveAdminScreen/ArchiveAdminViewModel, and added focused coverage in AgentApiTest and ArchiveAdminViewModelTest. Verification: env JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64 ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.api.AgentApiTest ✅; env JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64 ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:compileDebugKotlin ✅. Narrow app unit-test task still hits pre-existing unrelated blockers in BlockLibraryViewModelTest and ConfigViewModelTest.

title: Add agent import and clone admin flows
description:
## Why
The Kotlin admin app supports create, edit, delete, and export-oriented agent administration, but it still lacks visible import and clone workflows. That makes it harder to duplicate or migrate agent setups from the mobile admin surface.

## Scope
- Expose clone flow for existing agents if supported by the current Letta API/client surface
- Add import workflow for agent payloads or exports where supported
- Reuse existing export behavior rather than duplicating it
- Keep the UX explicit about destructive or overwriting operations

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/AgentApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AgentRepository.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/agentlist/AgentListScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/editagent/EditAgentScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/settings/AgentSettingsScreen.kt

## Deliverables
- Agent clone is reachable from the admin surface if API support exists
- Agent import is supported from exported payloads or Letta-compatible config where API support exists
- Agent lifecycle actions are more complete without relying on external tools
design: Treat clone/import as agent lifecycle actions, not chat actions. Keep export work aligned with the existing settings/admin flows rather than inventing another export entrypoint.

title: Add standalone block create and edit admin workflows
description:
## Why
The Kotlin app has a global block library plus agent-scoped attach/create flows, but block creation and editing still mostly happen indirectly through agent edit. The Letta admin surface for blocks is broader than inspect/delete plus attach-only workflows.

## Scope
- Add standalone create flow in the block library
- Add edit flow for mutable block metadata and value
- Preserve current protections for read-only/template blocks
- Keep agent-scoped attach/detach behavior separate from library-wide editing

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/blocks/BlockLibraryScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/blocks/BlockLibraryViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/BlockApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/BlockRepository.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/editagent/EditAgentScreen.kt

## Deliverables
- Users can create blocks directly from the block library
- Users can edit mutable block fields without going through an agent screen
- Regression coverage protects create/edit behavior
design: Keep block-library workflows focused on library-wide block management. Reuse existing BlockCreateParams and BlockUpdateParams paths rather than inventing parallel models.
close_reason: All deliverables already implemented: FAB create flow with BlockEditorDialog, standalone edit flow for mutable blocks (label locked), read-only protection, and 12 regression tests covering create/edit/delete/filter/attach/detach.

title: Add Kotlin run models, API, and repository support
description:
## Why
Run monitoring is absent from the Kotlin stack, so another agent should first add the underlying operational models and APIs before attempting the run-monitor UI.

## Scope
- Add run models to `core`
- Add `RunApi` and repository support
- Cover the new operational surface with repository/API tests

## Deliverables
- The Kotlin stack can query Letta run data
- Repository APIs are ready for a read-heavy run-monitor screen
- Tests protect the new client/repository behavior
design: Favor a read-heavy operational API surface first. Safe controls can be layered on later.
acceptance_criteria:
- Run models/API/repository exist in Kotlin
- The implementation maps to official Letta run semantics
- Tests cover the new client/repository layer
- The resulting stack is ready for UI work

title: Build a read-heavy run monitor screen
description:
## Why
Once run data is available in Kotlin, another agent should expose it as a mobile-friendly operational console so admins can inspect active and recent work without leaving the app.

## Scope
- Add a run monitor screen using the new repository
- Focus on status, timestamps, identity/agent linkage, and refresh
- Keep the first milestone read-heavy with only safe actions

## Deliverables
- A run monitor screen exists in the app
- Active/recent runs are inspectable from mobile admin UI
- The first milestone provides immediate operational visibility
design: Start with visibility, not control. Keep the first UI milestone operationally useful and low risk.
acceptance_criteria:
- Run monitor UI exists and uses the new repository layer
- The screen exposes useful run status/summary information
- Refresh/state handling is implemented cleanly
- The first slice avoids risky operational actions unless clearly supported

title: Add schedule list and create/delete admin UI
description:
## Why
After the schedule client layer exists, another agent should turn it into a practical admin flow for inspecting and managing automation from mobile.

## Scope
- Add list/create/delete schedule UI using the new repository
- Keep the first milestone focused on the core schedule workflows
- Follow the current admin list/detail pattern language

## Deliverables
- A schedule management screen exists
- Core schedule workflows are available in the UI
- State handling is consistent with the rest of the app
design: Do not over-design automation builders in the first slice. A clean list/create/delete flow is enough to unblock adoption.
acceptance_criteria:
- Schedule list/create/delete flows are available in the app
- The UI uses the Kotlin repository layer rather than direct API shortcuts
- Error/empty/loading states are handled consistently
- The resulting surface is practical for admin use

title: Add identity list and detail management UI
description:
## Why
After the identity client layer exists, another agent should expose it through a simple but real admin flow instead of leaving it as an internal-only capability.

## Scope
- Add identity list/detail screens or flows
- Show the key identity metadata and supported actions
- Integrate with navigation and existing admin patterns

## Deliverables
- Identities are browseable and manageable from the app
- Detail views expose supported identity metadata/actions
- Navigation and state handling match the rest of the admin UI
design: Keep this first UI slice simple and operationally useful. Integration into agent forms can follow later if needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Identity list/detail UI exists and is wired to the new repository
- The UI exposes the core identity workflows selected for the app
- Loading/empty/error states are handled consistently
- The implementation remains grounded in official Letta identity behavior

title: Add Kotlin schedule models, API, and repository support
description:
## Why
Schedule management is another completely missing admin domain. Another agent should first add the data/client layer cleanly before attempting UI workflows.

## Scope
- Add schedule models in `core`
- Add `ScheduleApi` and repository support
- Cover the new client/repository behavior with tests

## Deliverables
- Schedule support exists in the Kotlin client stack
- Repository APIs are ready for list/create/delete UI
- Tests protect the new schedule surface
design: Treat schedules as a new admin domain. Keep the first step data-layer only.
acceptance_criteria:
- Schedule models/API/repository exist and compile within the Kotlin stack
- The behavior maps to official Letta schedule semantics
- Repository/API tests exist for the new surface
- The stack is ready for UI implementation

title: Add a conversation detail/admin action surface
description:
## Why
Once richer conversation operations exist in Kotlin, another agent should expose them in a dedicated admin flow so conversation administration is not limited to list cards and contextual chat affordances.

## Scope
- Add a conversation detail/admin surface reachable from the conversations list
- Expose the approved conversation admin actions from the client layer
- Keep the UI clearly distinct from day-to-day chat use

## Deliverables
- Conversation admin has a dedicated screen/sheet/detail flow
- List-level CRUD grows into a richer admin surface
- The UI remains decoupled from the chat module split
design: Build on the client-layer bead first. Keep the first UI slice read-heavy with a few explicit admin actions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Conversation details/admin actions are reachable from the conversations screen
- The new UI uses only supported client-layer operations
- The flow distinguishes administration from normal chatting
- Tests or verification notes cover the new state transitions

title: Add Kotlin identity models, API, and repository support
description:
## Why
Identity management is completely absent from the Kotlin stack. Another agent should first build the vertical client foundation before attempting screens or agent integration.

## Scope
- Add official Letta identity models to `core`
- Add `IdentityApi` and repository support
- Add tests for the new client/repository behavior

## Deliverables
- Identity operations exist in the Kotlin client stack
- The repository layer is ready for UI consumption
- Tests protect the new identity surface
design: Treat this as a clean vertical foundation bead. Avoid prematurely mixing identity work into agent screens.
acceptance_criteria:
- Identity models/API/repository exist in Kotlin
- The implementation maps to official Letta identity semantics
- Repository/API tests cover the new surface
- The stack is usable by future UI work without redesign

title: Add archival search/filter polish and safer destructive actions
description:
## Why
Once passage detail exists, the next archival improvement is better search/admin ergonomics and safer delete flows so the screen can support real operations without confusion.

## Scope
- Improve archival search/filter behavior around the existing repository/API surface
- Add clearer destructive confirmations and better empty/error feedback
- Keep the bead strictly within passage-level archival admin
design: Keep this as follow-through on top of the passage-detail bead. Do not expand into broader ingestion management.
acceptance_criteria:
- Search/admin filtering is more useful than raw text search alone where the API supports it
- Destructive actions are confirmed and update state correctly
- The archival admin flow is measurably safer and easier to operate
- The work remains separate from deprecated source/file concepts

title: Audit and extend Kotlin conversation APIs for missing admin operations
description:
## Why
Conversation administration is still limited to create/rename/delete/fork. Another agent should first identify and add any missing official Letta conversation operations to the Kotlin client before building richer UI on top.

## Scope
- Compare current `ConversationApi` / repository coverage to the official Letta conversation/admin capabilities chosen for this app
- Add the missing high-value operations to models/API/repository
- Keep the work separate from chat rendering concerns

## Deliverables
- Kotlin conversation client support covers the next set of admin-relevant operations
- Repository APIs are ready for richer conversation admin UI
- Tests cover newly added client/repository behavior
design: Treat this as the client-layer foundation bead. UI can come next once the operations are exposed cleanly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Missing conversation admin operations are added to the Kotlin client/repository stack
- The additions are grounded in official Letta behavior
- Tests cover the new repository/API calls
- No chat-module rendering work is pulled into this bead

title: Add MCP health, refresh, and discovered-tool admin actions
description:
## Why
Even after richer state and forms exist, MCP administration is incomplete without explicit operational actions. Another agent should add refresh/test affordances and make discovered tools first-class in the screen.

## Scope
- Add health/test/refresh actions backed by repository behavior
- Surface discovered tools clearly in list/detail views
- Make error states and partial failures actionable for admins

## Deliverables
- MCP screens expose real operational actions
- Discovered tools are visible and refreshable
- Error/health feedback is grounded in actual repository/API outcomes
design: Treat this as the operational follow-through bead after state/forms land. Keep actions honest and tied to real API calls.
acceptance_criteria:
- Refresh/test actions exist and behave predictably
- Tool discovery results are clearly rendered in the UI
- The screen no longer implies health with placeholders when the server has not been checked
- The behavior is covered by targeted tests or manual verification notes

title: Add archival passage detail and metadata inspection
description:
## Why
Archival memory is still mostly a text list. Another agent should first add a read-heavy detail surface so passage metadata becomes visible and useful before expanding filters or editing.

## Scope
- Add a passage detail surface from the archival list
- Show timestamps, sourceId, and metadata where present
- Keep this explicitly separate from deprecated source/file ingestion work

## Deliverables
- Passage detail inspection exists in the UI
- Metadata/provenance is visible, not hidden in the model only
- The archival screen becomes useful for admin inspection rather than bare CRUD
design: Start with inspection before filters. Keep the flow read-heavy and grounded in the existing `Passage` model.
acceptance_criteria:
- A user can open a passage detail/inspection view from the archival screen
- `sourceId`, timestamps, and metadata are shown meaningfully when present
- Loading/error states are handled consistently
- No deprecated source/file workflow is reintroduced

title: Add tool-to-agent attachment workflows from tool management
description:
## Why
Tool management remains incomplete until admins can attach/detach tools from a tool-centric surface rather than bouncing back into agent forms.

## Scope
- Add attach/detach agent workflows from tool detail or a related tool admin surface
- Reuse existing repository attach/detach support
- Keep the UX manageable on mobile admin screens

## Deliverables
- Tool-centric attachment flows exist
- Attach/detach results update relevant UI state predictably
- The tool admin surface becomes useful beyond inspection/editing
design: Prefer a focused attach sheet/dialog over embedding the full agent form inside the tool screen.
acceptance_criteria:
- An admin can attach or detach a tool to/from an agent from the tool management flow
- The implementation uses existing repository semantics cleanly
- The resulting UI avoids duplicating large agent-edit screens inside tool management
- Tests cover the attachment behavior where practical

title: Add block metadata safeguards and management polish
description:
## Why
Once blocks become reusable admin objects, the UI needs clearer safeguards around read-only/template semantics, delete/detach actions, and metadata edits so another agent does not have to infer the intended behavior.

## Scope
- Clarify destructive actions for reusable blocks vs attached blocks
- Tighten read-only/template handling in UI and ViewModels
- Add remaining tests around metadata edit/attach/delete edge cases

## Evidence / Files
- `EditAgentScreen.kt`
- future block library screen/ViewModel
- `BlockRepositoryTest.kt`, `EditAgentViewModelTest.kt`

## Deliverables
- Delete/detach behavior is explicit and less error-prone
- Read-only/template blocks are handled consistently across library/editor surfaces
- Tests cover the key edge cases for block metadata workflows
design: Keep this as a follow-through bead after the core library and attach flows land. It should tighten behavior, not invent new block capabilities.
acceptance_criteria:
- UI clearly distinguishes detach from delete where both exist
- Read-only blocks cannot be edited through normal flows
- Template/read-only semantics are visually and behaviorally consistent
- Edge-case tests exist for the new behaviors

title: Add repo-level Android build guardrails and deprecation cleanup
description:
## Why
Recent cycles have shipped compile-breaking Kotlin changes that would have been caught by a local compile step, and the repository still lacks committed contributor guidance for the current Android Compose build flow. There are also lingering Compose deprecation warnings from  usage in active screens.

## Scope
- Add repo-level contributor guidance for Android Compose builds
- Add a committed pre-push hook template that runs the Kotlin compile gate
- Clean safe  deprecations in active screens
- Do not rely on local-only .git/hooks state or modify git config automatically
design: Use committed docs and a hook template inside the repository so contributors can opt in without hidden machine state. Keep code cleanup low-risk by limiting it to the known Divider deprecations surfaced in current screens.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repo contains committed build instructions for Android Compose contributors
- Repo contains a reusable pre-push hook template that runs compileDebugKotlin with JAVA_HOME guidance
- Remaining touched Divider() usages are replaced with HorizontalDivider()
- Work avoids local-only config mutations
notes:
Implemented repo-level build guardrails and deprecation cleanup in progress: added CONTRIBUTING.md with Android Compose build commands and environment setup, added a reusable .githooks/pre-push compile gate template, and replaced safe Divider() usages with HorizontalDivider() in active admin screens. Remaining verification in this environment is still blocked by Android SDK license acceptance before Gradle compilation starts.
Added the next build-guardrail slice: android-compose/README.md with Kotlin build commands, android-compose/local.properties.example, and scripts/install-hooks.sh for installing the committed pre-push hook template. This complements CONTRIBUTING.md and keeps machine-specific config out of version control.

title: Add a Letta run monitor for long-running admin and agent operations
description:
## Why
Long-running Letta work currently has no dedicated Kotlin admin surface. The review identified run monitoring as a missing capability, and there is no existing run API/model/repository/screen stack in the app. For an admin tool, visibility into active and recent runs is important operationally.

## Scope
- Add Kotlin support for run inspection and core run operations
- Build a run-monitor surface suited to mobile admin workflows
- Keep the first milestone focused on visibility and safe operational controls

## Evidence / Files
- No run-related API/models/screens exist in the current Kotlin stack
- Existing admin list/detail screen patterns can be reused for the run monitor

## Deliverables
- Run models/API/repository support in Kotlin
- An admin screen for inspecting active/recent runs and core run actions
- Better operational visibility for long-running Letta work
design: Favor a read-heavy operational console first: visibility, status, and safe controls. Build the run layer cleanly so later work can add richer filtering/analytics without rewriting the foundation.
acceptance_criteria:
- The Kotlin app includes a real run-monitoring client/repository layer
- The UI exposes the core run inspection workflows expected in an admin tool
- The implementation matches official Letta run behavior
- The first milestone provides immediate operational value even before any advanced analytics

title: Add schedule management for Letta automated/admin workflows
description:
## Why
Scheduling/automation surfaced as another missing admin capability in the Letta review, and there is currently no Kotlin API/client/UI layer for schedules. An admin app should be able to inspect and manage automated Letta tasks without dropping to another client.

## Scope
- Add Kotlin schedule models/API/repository support
- Add a basic admin screen or flow for listing and managing schedules
- Keep the first milestone centered on official Letta schedule functionality with obvious admin value

## Evidence / Files
- No schedule-related API/models/screens exist in the current Kotlin stack
- Existing admin screen and repository patterns provide templates for the new surface

## Deliverables
- Schedule support added to the Kotlin client stack
- Admin UI for list/create/delete or equivalent core schedule workflows
- Clean extension points for future automation controls
design: Introduce schedules as their own admin domain. Start with the smallest official workflow that makes the app materially better for Letta administration, then grow outward. Avoid coupling schedule management to chat flows.
acceptance_criteria:
- Schedule management is represented in the Kotlin API and repository layers
- The app exposes the core schedule workflows required for admin use
- The implementation follows official Letta schedule behavior rather than local approximation
- The new surface is testable and documented through acceptance criteria/tests where practical

title: Expose advanced conversation admin options supported by Letta
description:
## Why
The current conversation surface supports create/rename/delete/fork, but the second admin review found broader Letta conversation controls still missing from the Kotlin stack. This includes stronger non-chat admin operations and, where officially supported, advanced per-conversation options such as isolation/override settings that are valuable in an admin tool.

## Scope
- Expand conversation administration beyond the current list-level CRUD affordances
- Prefer official Letta conversation/admin options with clear admin value
- Keep the work distinct from chat UX polish and avoid coupling it to the active chat module split

## Evidence / Files
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/ConversationApi.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/ConversationRepository.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsViewModel.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsScreen.kt`

## Deliverables
- The conversation admin surface exposes more than basic rename/fork/create/delete
- Missing Letta conversation options with real admin value are wired into the Kotlin client/UI stack
- Conversation admin is easier to access from dedicated admin surfaces, not only from in-chat flows
design: Start from `ConversationApi` / `ConversationRepository` and extend outward. Add only officially supported Letta options with clear admin utility; do not invent conversation metadata fields locally. Keep the bead scoped away from chat rendering concerns.
acceptance_criteria:
- Conversation admin capabilities expand beyond the current baseline CRUD set
- Any newly supported advanced conversation settings are grounded in official Letta behavior and tested through the Kotlin client layer
- The work does not depend on reworking the chatclient module extraction
- The resulting UI clearly distinguishes conversation administration from day-to-day chat use

title: Add identity management to the Kotlin Letta admin app
description:
## Why
The second admin review identified identity management as a missing Letta admin capability, and the current Kotlin codebase has no identity API/client/UI layer. For an admin-focused app, that absence prevents managing agent identity context and multi-user deployment flows that Letta supports.

## Scope
- Add the Kotlin client/repository layer needed for identity management
- Build the minimum UI/admin flow to browse and manage identities
- Keep the work anchored to official Letta identity capabilities rather than speculative mobile-only concepts

## Evidence / Files
- No `IdentityApi`, identity models, or identity screens currently exist in `android-compose/core` or `android-compose/app`
- Existing admin navigation and screen patterns in `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/**` can be reused for the new surface

## Deliverables
- Identity models/API/repository support in Kotlin
- Admin screens or flows for listing and managing identities
- Clear integration points from agent/admin flows where identity attachment matters
design: Treat identities as a new admin domain in the Kotlin client stack. Build the vertical slice cleanly: models -> API -> repository -> ViewModel -> screen. Reuse existing admin screen patterns instead of burying identity work inside agent edit forms initially.
acceptance_criteria:
- The Kotlin app contains a real identity client/repository layer rather than just placeholder UI
- At least the core identity admin workflows are available from the app
- The implementation is grounded in official Letta identity semantics
- Tests cover the new client/repository behavior where practical

title: Deepen archival memory admin beyond plain passage CRUD
description:
## Why
Archival memory exists in Kotlin as passage CRUD and search, but the UI currently treats passages as plain text entries. `Passage.kt` already includes `sourceId`, timestamps, and `metadata`, and `ArchivalViewModel` only exposes a shallow passage list plus search/add/delete.

## Scope
- Expand archival memory from simple passage text management into a richer admin surface
- Expose passage metadata already present in the Kotlin model
- Improve the archival screen so it supports real admin inspection rather than bare CRUD

## Evidence / Files
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/model/Passage.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/PassageApi.kt`
- `android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/PassageRepository.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/archival/ArchivalViewModel.kt`
- `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/archival/ArchivalScreen.kt`

## Deliverables
- Passage detail/inspection that includes metadata already available from the model
- Better archival admin UX for search, provenance, and destructive actions
- A clearer foundation for any future non-deprecated archival/admin expansion
design: Keep this bead scoped to archival memory that already exists in Kotlin. Do not pull deprecated source/file/folder workflows back into scope. Focus on passage metadata, provenance, and admin inspection quality.
acceptance_criteria:
- Archival UI exposes more than just passage text
- `sourceId`, timestamps, and metadata are represented meaningfully where present
- Search/add/delete remain intact while the admin inspection surface improves
- The archival flow stays explicitly separate from deprecated source/file ingestion work

title: Fix MessageProcessor dead branches and preserve approval-tool execution results
description:
## Why
MessageProcessor has several near-empty or empty branches, including approval-tool execution where the result is computed and then discarded. This is both a cleanliness issue and a correctness risk if client tool results are expected downstream.

## Scope
- Review MessageProcessor handling for ApprovalRequestMessage, ApprovalResponseMessage, EventMessage, HiddenReasoningMessage, and UnknownMessage
- Preserve or surface tool execution results where the current implementation drops them
- Remove obviously dead/no-op branches or replace them with intentional logging/documentation

## Files
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/domain/MessageProcessor.kt
- Any adjacent model/mapper utilities needed to represent processed results

## Deliverables
- No silently discarded client-tool result in approval handling
- Empty/no-op message branches are made intentional and explicit
- Stream processing behavior is easier to understand and debug
design: Favor explicit intent over silent branches. If a message type is intentionally ignored, encode that decision clearly in code rather than leaving an empty branch behind.
acceptance_criteria:
- ApprovalRequestMessage handling no longer computes a result and drops it unused
- Non-user-visible no-op branches are either removed or documented/logged intentionally
- Existing stream processing for known message types remains correct
- The touched paths have targeted test coverage or regression protection

title: Add regression coverage for Kotlin quality-pass refactors across startup, chat, repositories, and screen state
description:
## Why
The quality-pass changes touch startup behavior, repository mutation paths, message processing, and Compose screen state. The current test footprint is lighter than the risk surface of these refactors, especially in chat and repository flows.

## Scope
- Add or expand tests around the most failure-prone quality-pass changes
- Prioritize startup cache hydration, shared message mapping, chat display derivation, repository mutation updates, and MessageProcessor behavior
- Use the project's existing Kotlin/Compose test patterns rather than inventing a new stack

## Files
- app/src/test/java/... and/or core/src/test/java/... as appropriate
- Potential Compose UI or snapshot coverage for refactored screen-state components if justified

## Deliverables
- Focused regression coverage for the quality-pass work
- Clear tests for changed message/repository/state behavior
- Enough coverage to safely iterate on the refactors next sprint
design: Test the seams that the cleanup changes: derived state, mutation paths, startup hydration, and stream/message conversion. Keep UI tests targeted; use unit tests where behavior is easier to isolate.
acceptance_criteria:
- Refactors in startup/repository/chat/message-processing areas are covered by targeted tests
- New tests prove the intended behavior, not just happy-path object construction
- Coverage additions follow existing project testing conventions
- The bead documents any intentionally deferred test gaps

title: Unify SettingsRepository storage strategy and remove unnecessary backend mixing
description:
## Why
SettingsRepository currently mixes DataStore, encrypted SharedPreferences, and in-memory StateFlow-backed state. The split is harder to reason about than necessary, especially because DataStore appears to be used for only a narrow slice of settings while most configuration uses another backend.

## Scope
- Re-evaluate which settings need encryption and which do not
- Reduce the number of persistence backends used by SettingsRepository
- Keep current persisted behavior and migrations safe

## Files
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/SettingsRepository.kt
- Any small supporting persistence/DI files touched by the simplification

## Deliverables
- Fewer persistence mechanisms in active use
- Clearer ownership of sensitive vs non-sensitive settings
- Easier-to-test settings persistence flow
design: Prefer one persistence backend per category of data. Only keep multiple backends if there is a concrete security or platform constraint that justifies the added complexity.
acceptance_criteria:
- SettingsRepository uses a simpler, documented storage strategy than the current mixed approach
- Sensitive settings remain protected appropriately
- Existing persisted settings continue to load correctly or are migrated safely
- Repository API to callers is no more complex than today

title: Introduce type-safe navigation route definitions for AppNavGraph
description:
## Why
AppNavGraph currently relies on raw string routes and inline route interpolation. This makes route changes fragile and pushes correctness to runtime instead of compile time.

## Scope
- Introduce route definitions/helpers for screen names and arguments
- Replace raw string navigation calls with typed or centrally defined route builders
- Preserve existing navigation graph behavior and arguments

## Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt
- New route-definition file(s) under the navigation package
- Any affected call sites in screen navigation lambdas

## Deliverables
- Navigation routes are defined in one place
- Call sites stop hand-building route strings inline
- Argument names and route shapes are harder to break accidentally
design: Prefer the strongest type-safe navigation approach supported by the current Compose Navigation version. If full typed destinations are not practical, use a sealed route model with centralized builders.
acceptance_criteria:
- AppNavGraph no longer depends on scattered inline raw route strings for its main navigation paths
- Route builders/constants cover agent, conversation, config, templates, MCP, and about flows touched during the refactor
- Existing navigation behavior remains intact
- Future route additions have a single obvious extension point

title: Break up ConversationsScreen and McpScreen list/dialog patterns into reusable feature components
description:
## Why
ConversationsScreen and McpScreen combine list rendering, tabs, dialogs, cards, and destructive actions inside oversized screen files. They also repeat local dialog and menu patterns that make feature files harder to navigate.

## Scope
- Extract list cards, picker/dialogs, and tab-content sections into focused components
- Keep behavior unchanged while reducing per-file complexity
- Reuse any shared screen-state components created by the quality pass where appropriate

## Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/conversations/ConversationsScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/mcp/McpScreen.kt
- Supporting feature-local component files

## Deliverables
- Smaller feature entry screens
- Dialog/menu/card logic moved into focused components
- Easier-to-maintain feature packages for conversations and MCP
design: Prefer feature-local reusable components before promoting anything to designsystem. Extract only patterns that are real within the feature.
acceptance_criteria:
- ConversationsScreen and McpScreen are materially reduced in size
- Core flows (open, create, rename, delete, fork, add server) still work
- Extracted components have clear names and ownership
- The refactor does not introduce generic abstraction for one-off UI

title: Simplify repository cache updates and avoid unnecessary full refreshes after single-item mutations
description:
## Why
Several repositories are doing more work than necessary: MessageRepository repeatedly copies maps during updates, AgentRepository refetches full agent lists after single-agent mutations, and ConversationRepository's optimistic update path is more complex than needed for the behavior it provides.

## Scope
- Review repository mutation/update paths for avoidable full refreshes and wasteful cache rewriting
- Replace obviously redundant full-list refetches with targeted local cache updates where safe
- Simplify optimistic update/rollback logic if it is not providing proportional UX value

## Files
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/AgentRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/MessageRepository.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/ConversationRepository.kt

## Deliverables
- Less redundant network/cache churn on create/update/delete flows
- Simpler cache update logic in repositories
- Repository behavior remains correct under success and failure paths
design: Favor targeted cache mutation over blanket refreshes when the API response is authoritative enough. Keep correctness ahead of cleverness; if an optimistic path is too fragile, simplify it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent create/update/delete no longer force a full agent refresh when the mutation response already contains enough data to update cache safely
- MessageRepository update paths reduce avoidable whole-map copying where practical
- ConversationRepository mutation logic is simpler and still safe under failure
- Repository tests or targeted regressions cover touched behavior

title: Split ChatViewModel UI state and remove ephemeral input churn from shared screen state
description:
## Why
ChatViewModel currently carries a broad UiState and performs many copy-based updates, including ephemeral input text updates on every keystroke. That makes state changes noisier, larger, and more expensive than necessary in the app's busiest feature.

## Scope
- Revisit ChatViewModel state boundaries
- Hoist purely local input state out of long-lived shared state where appropriate
- Reduce broad copy churn and separate concerns such as streaming state, input state, and message display state

## Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt
- Related state/model files if needed

## Deliverables
- Chat state ownership is clearer and more narrowly scoped
