- Ephemeral input changes no longer force broad screen-state updates unless intentionally needed
- The ViewModel becomes easier to test and reason about
design: Favor smaller, purpose-specific state streams over a single catch-all UiState when one hot field causes disproportionate churn.
acceptance_criteria:
- Input typing no longer updates an oversized shared state object on every keystroke unless there is a deliberate reason documented in code
- ChatViewModel responsibilities are split into clearer state concerns
- Existing send, stream, tool, and error flows continue to work
- The resulting state model is easier to test than the current broad UiState

title: Decompose EditAgentScreen and AgentSettingsScreen into shared, focused form sections
description:
## Why
EditAgentScreen and AgentSettingsScreen both contain long, vertically stacked form UIs with repeated concepts such as model settings, system prompt, memory blocks, and tags. This keeps the files large and makes changes to agent settings harder to keep consistent.

## Scope
- Extract focused form sections/components for repeated agent configuration concepts
- Reduce duplication between edit and settings flows where the UI intent is shared
- Keep feature behavior unchanged while making ownership clearer

## Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/editagent/EditAgentScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/settings/AgentSettingsScreen.kt
- New shared components in the appropriate package/designsystem if justified

## Deliverables
- Smaller screen files with clearly named sections/components
- Shared rendering for settings concepts that should stay aligned
- Less duplication in agent settings forms
design: Extract by domain section (model settings, memory blocks, tags, prompts), not by arbitrary line count. Keep shared components close to the agent-settings domain unless they are truly generic.
acceptance_criteria:
- EditAgentScreen and AgentSettingsScreen are materially smaller after extraction
- Repeated UI/form patterns are shared where it improves maintenance
- Save/delete/update behavior remains unchanged
- The resulting component split is clear enough to support future settings additions

title: Add voice message support — record audio, transcribe, TTS replies
description:
LettaBot supports voice messages via Whisper transcription + ElevenLabs/OpenAI TTS.

Implementation for Letta Mobile:
1. Mic button in chat input bar (alongside send button)
2. Hold-to-record or tap-to-toggle recording
3. Send audio to Whisper API for transcription
4. Send transcribed text as message to agent
5. Agent can reply with voice — play TTS audio inline

Requires: RECORD_AUDIO permission, MediaRecorder API, audio playback.

Success metric: can send voice message, agent responds with text (voice reply is stretch goal)
notes: Blocked pending infra bead letta-mobile-tkl6. Mobile UI/recording work should resume only after a supported transcription/TTS backend path is defined.

title: Embed AccessibilityService bridge directly in Letta Mobile
description:
Long-term: Build the Hermes bridge directly into Letta Mobile.

Implementation:
1. Add AccessibilityService to Letta Mobile (BridgeAccessibilityService pattern from Hermes)
2. Register client-side tools: android_tap, android_type, android_screenshot, etc.
3. When agent calls these tools, execute locally via AccessibilityService
4. Return results directly to the agent
5. No relay server needed — direct on-device execution
6. Requires: accessibility permission, overlay permission

This is a major feature. Hermes has the reference implementation.

Success metric: Letta agent can open an app and type text — all on-device

title: Agent import from .af file
description:
Export works but import has no UI.

Fix: Add 'Import Agent' button in AgentList FAB menu or overflow:
1. Open file picker for .af files via SAF
2. Read file content
3. POST to import endpoint
4. Navigate to new agent

Success metric: can import an agent from .af file

title: Improve connection model — 3-state (connected/node offline/offline)
description:
OpenClaw has three-state connection:
  isConnected (to gateway) + nodeConnected (to local node) + statusText

We have binary online/offline. For Letta this maps to:
  Online (server reachable) / Degraded (server reachable but slow) / Offline

Fix: Expand ConnectionState enum and ConnectionStatusBanner to show more granular status.

Success metric: banner shows distinct states for server-reachable vs offline

title: Add message type footer labels
description:
React app shows message type below each message (user_message, assistant_message, etc.) in a subtle label style.

Implementation: Add optional footer text to MessageBubble showing the message type in labelSmall style with low opacity.

Success metric: each message shows its type below the bubble

title: Add chat mode switcher (simple/interactive/debug)
description:
React app has 3 modes:
- Simple: only user + assistant messages
- Interactive: full messages including reasoning + tools
- Debug: raw JSON view of each message

Implementation:
1. Add chatMode: ChatMode to ChatUiState
2. Add mode switcher row in ChatScreen (3 SegmentedButton or FilterChip)
3. Simple mode: filter to user + assistant only
4. Interactive mode: show all (current default)
5. Debug mode: render each message as a scrollable JSON card

Success metric: mode switcher visible, switching changes which messages render

title: Add global UncaughtExceptionHandler in LettaApplication
description:
LettaApplication.kt is empty. Uncaught exceptions crash silently with no logging.

Fix: Set Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler in LettaApplication.onCreate() that logs the crash to a file before killing the process. Prepare for future Crashlytics integration.

Success metric: uncaught exceptions logged to logcat before crash

title: Add deep linking intent-filters for agent and conversation routes
description:
No deep links configured. Can't open specific agents or conversations from notifications/links.

Fix: Add intent-filters to AndroidManifest.xml for:
- letta://agent/{agentId}/chat
- letta://agent/{agentId}/chat?conversationId={id}
- letta://config
Handle in AppNavGraph.

Success metric: adb shell am start -d 'letta://agent/xxx/chat' opens the app

title: Fix mutable var race conditions in EditAgent/AgentSettings ViewModels
description:
EditAgentViewModel and AgentSettingsViewModel use private var originalPersonaBlock/originalHumanBlock that can race with concurrent coroutines.

Fix: Replace with AtomicReference or move to UiState data class as immutable snapshot.

Success metric: no mutable var in ViewModels outside of MutableStateFlow

title: Add GitHub Actions CI workflow for build + test
description:
No CI/CD configured. Tests never run automatically.

Fix: Create .github/workflows/android.yml:
- Trigger on push/PR to kotlin-compose-migration
- Setup Java 17 + Gradle cache
- Run ./gradlew :core:test :app:test
- Run ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug
- Upload APK artifact

Success metric: CI badge shows green on PR

title: Fix remaining ~5 missing contentDescription on icons
description:
AgentListScreen, ModelBrowserScreen, ArchivalScreen still have icons without contentDescription.

Success metric: zero contentDescription=null in grep across all screen files

title: Add Templates and MCP to main navigation flow
description:
Templates and MCP screens exist but aren't discoverable from main nav. Only reachable if you know the route.

Fix: Add to ConversationsScreen TopAppBar overflow menu or as additional items in the nav drawer/bottom sheet.

Success metric: Templates and MCP accessible from conversations screen

title: Add conversation rename via long-press menu
description:
Users can't manually rename conversations. Auto-summary may not be ideal.

Fix: Add 'Rename' option to conversation long-press context menu. Show dialog with editable text field. Call conversationRepository.updateConversation().

Success metric: long-press shows Rename option, dialog allows editing

title: Add input validation to settings forms
description:
No validation on: temperature (should be 0.0-2.0), maxTokens (>0), server URL (valid URL format).

Fix: Add validation logic in ViewModels, show error state on TextFields with supportingText.

Success metric: invalid inputs show inline error messages

title: Add missing keys to LazyColumn/LazyRow items calls
description:
ModelBrowserScreen L115: items(providers) without key — filter chips lose state on reorder.

Fix: items(providers, key = { it }) { ... }

Also audit all other items() calls to ensure stable keys.

Success metric: grep 'items(' finds zero calls without key parameter

title: Add agent secrets management
description:
Letta agents can have secrets (API keys, tokens) that tools access via os.getenv(). We don't expose this in the mobile app.

Implementation:
1. In AgentSettings, add 'Secrets' section
2. Show existing secret keys (values masked)
3. Add secret dialog: key (text) + value (password field)
4. Delete secret with confirmation
5. PATCH /v1/agents/{id} with secrets field
6. Test: Verify secrets CRUD

Success metric: can add, view (masked), and delete agent secrets

title: Add sleeptime agent toggle and status
description:
Letta supports sleeptime agents that process memory in the background between conversations. Enable via enable_sleeptime=True on agent create/update.

Implementation:
1. Add toggle in AgentSettings: 'Enable Sleeptime' switch
2. On toggle: PATCH /v1/agents/{id} with enable_sleeptime field
3. Show status indicator when sleeptime is active/processing
4. Test: Verify toggle updates agent config

Success metric: can enable/disable sleeptime on an agent

title: Add tool rules configuration UI
description:
Letta tool rules control execution order: InitToolRule (run first), ChildToolRule (controls flow), TerminalToolRule (end turn). We don't expose this.

Implementation:
1. In AgentSettings, add 'Tool Rules' section
2. Show existing rules as cards
3. Add rule dialog with type selector (init/child/terminal) + tool picker
4. Delete rule with swipe
5. Save rules via agent update
6. Test: ToolRulesViewModelTest (4+ tests)

Success metric: can view, add, and delete tool rules for an agent

title: Add usage statistics display in chat
description:
Letta API returns UsageStatistics with every response: prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens, step_count. We discard this.

Implementation:
1. Parse UsageStatistics from LettaResponse in MessageRepository
2. Add usageStats: UsageStatistics? to ChatUiState
3. Show token usage in chat header or expandable info section:
   - Tokens: 1,234 prompt / 567 completion
   - Steps: 3
   - Context: 4,096 / 128,000 used
4. Update on each message send/receive
5. Use LatencyText component style for compact display
6. Test: Verify usage stats parsed from response

Success metric: token usage visible after sending a message

title: Add agent export/import — .af file support
description:
Letta supports exporting agents as .af files and importing them. Essential for backup and sharing.

API endpoints:
- GET /v1/agents/{id}/export — export agent as .af
- POST /v1/agents/import — import agent from .af data

Implementation:
1. Add export button in AgentSettings screen
2. On export: download .af content, save to device Downloads folder via SAF
3. Add import button in AgentList screen
4. On import: pick .af file via SAF, POST to import endpoint
5. Show success/error snackbar
6. Test: Export/import round-trip test

Success metric: can export agent to file, import agent from file

title: Add repository interface pattern (Habitica pattern: interface + impl + local)
description:
Habitica splits repositories into 3 layers:
1. Interface (TaskRepository) — defines contract
2. Implementation (TaskRepositoryImpl) — orchestrates local + remote
3. Local repository (TaskLocalRepository) — handles caching/persistence

We have concrete repository classes that mix API calls with state management.

Implementation:
1. Create interfaces for each repository:
   - interface AgentRepository { fun getAgent(id): Flow<Agent>; suspend fun refreshAgents(); ... }
   - Keep existing AgentRepository class, rename to AgentRepositoryImpl
2. Bind via Hilt @Binds: @Binds fun bindAgentRepository(impl: AgentRepositoryImpl): AgentRepository
3. ViewModels depend on INTERFACE, not implementation
4. Tests inject fakes that implement the INTERFACE

This is a significant refactor but dramatically improves testability.
Habitica reference: data/TaskRepository.kt (interface) vs data/implementation/TaskRepositoryImpl.kt

title: Adopt MockK over fake implementations for complex mocking (Habitica pattern)
description:
Habitica uses MockK (io.mockk) extensively. We currently use hand-written fakes. MockK is more flexible for complex scenarios (verify call counts, capture arguments, relaxed mocks).

When to use fakes vs MockK:
- Fakes: Simple repositories with state (keeps our existing pattern)
- MockK: Complex interactions (verify exactly 1 call, debouncing, argument capture)

Implementation:
1. Add MockK dependency: testImplementation('io.mockk:mockk:1.14.5')
2. Use MockK for API layer tests (verify request formation)
3. Use MockK for ViewModel tests where we need coVerify { repo.deleteAgent(any()) }
4. Keep fakes for simple state-based repo tests

Key MockK patterns from Habitica:
- mockk<ApiClient>(relaxed = true) for dependencies you don't care about
- coEvery { api.getAgent(any()) } returns agent
- coVerify(exactly = 1) { api.postMessage(any()) }
- slot<String>() for argument capture
- clearAllMocks() in afterEach

Add to test infrastructure (letta-mobile-wcp) as available tool, not mandate.

title: Adopt Kotest WordSpec BDD-style test syntax (Habitica pattern)
Add DTO/domain model mapping extensions (Pokedex asEntity/asDomain pattern)
description:
Pokedex uses extension functions like .asEntity() and .asDomain() for clean model mapping. We do inline mapping in ViewModels which is messy and untestable.

Implementation:
1. Create data/mapper/MessageMapper.kt:
   fun AppMessage.toUiMessage(): Message { ... }
   fun LettaMessage.toAppMessage(): AppMessage { ... }
2. Create data/mapper/AgentMapper.kt:
   fun Agent.toListItem(): AgentListItem { ... }
3. Move mapping logic OUT of ViewModels INTO mapper extensions
4. Mappers are pure functions — trivially testable
5. Add test file for each mapper

Benefit: separates mapping from state management, makes both testable independently.

title: Test: Remaining APIs (ConversationApi, ToolApi, BlockApi, McpServerApi, ModelApi)
description:
Lower-priority API tests for the remaining 5 API classes.

ConversationApi (4 tests): list, create, delete, fork
ToolApi (5 tests): list, get, attach, detach, upsert
BlockApi (3 tests): getBlocks, updateBlock, getBlockByLabel
McpServerApi (4 tests): list, create, delete, listTools
ModelApi (2 tests): listModels, getModel

All use same Ktor MockEngine pattern from AgentApi/MessageApi tests.
Total: ~18 tests across 5 API classes.

title: Test: LettaApiClient (5+ tests) — client configuration
description:
Test cases:
- getClient() returns HttpClient with JSON content negotiation
- getClient() includes Bearer token when configured
- getClient() omits auth when no token set
- getBaseUrl() returns configured URL
- getBaseUrl() normalizes trailing slash
- Client timeout configuration applied

Use Ktor MockEngine or verify HttpClient configuration.

title: Test: ConnectivityMonitor (4+ tests) — network state tracking
description:
Test cases:
- Initial state reflects current connectivity
- State updates on network available callback
- State updates on network lost callback
- isOnline convenience property
- Flow emits state changes

May need Robolectric or mock ConnectivityManager.

title: Add long-press message context menu (copy, reply, delete)
description:
Element X shows action menus on long-press of messages. We have MessageActionButton but no long-press interaction.

Implementation:
1. Wrap each MessageBubble in combinedClickable:
   - onClick = existing behavior (if any)
   - onLongClick = { showContextMenu = true }
2. Create ui/components/MessageContextMenu.kt:
   - DropdownMenu anchored to the pressed message
   - Actions: Copy, Reply (future), Delete (if user message)
   - Each action has icon + label
3. Actions:
   - Copy: clipboard + snackbar
   - Reply: set replyToMessage in ChatUiState, show quoted preview in input bar (future)
   - Delete: confirm dialog, then remove from timeline
4. Add haptic feedback on long press:
   val haptic = LocalHapticFeedback.current
   haptic.performHapticFeedback(HapticFeedbackType.LongPress)

Reference: Element X message action sheets
Depends on: SnackbarDispatcher for copy confirmation

title: Add emoji-only message detection with large rendering
description:
Element X detects when a message contains only emoji (1-3 emoji, no other text) and renders them at 3x size without a bubble background.

Implementation:
1. Create util/EmojiDetector.kt:
   - fun isEmojiOnly(text: String): Boolean — regex check for Unicode emoji ranges
   - fun emojiCount(text: String): Int
   - Only apply large rendering for 1-3 emoji
2. In ChatScreen MessageBubble:
   - If isEmojiOnly(message.content) && emojiCount <= 3:
     - Skip bubble background (no MessageBubbleShape clip)
     - Render with fontSize = 48.sp (3x normal 16.sp)
     - Still show sender label and timestamp
3. Add edge cases: emoji with skin tone modifiers, ZWJ sequences
4. Test with: single emoji, double emoji, emoji + text (should NOT be large)

Reference: Element X emoji detection in EditorStyledText
Small but delightful UX touch for chat apps

title: Add Paparazzi screenshot testing for all previews
description:
Element X auto-discovers @Preview composables and snapshots them with Paparazzi. Catches visual regressions in CI.

Implementation:
1. Add Paparazzi dependency to build.gradle.kts:
   testImplementation('app.cash.paparazzi:paparazzi:1.3.4')
2. Create test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/ScreenshotTest.kt:
   - @get:Rule val paparazzi = Paparazzi(deviceConfig = PIXEL_6, theme = 'android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar')
   - Iterate over all StateProvider values and snapshot each
3. Alternatively, create per-component screenshot tests:
   - ChatBubbleScreenshotTest
   - ThinkingSectionScreenshotTest
   - ErrorDialogScreenshotTest
4. Add CI step to run screenshot tests and compare golden images
5. Store golden images in screenshots/ directory (gitlfs or committed)
6. Requires StateProvider pattern to be implemented first

Depends on: StateProvider pattern (letta-mobile-06l)
Reference: Element X Paparazzi + sharded screenshot testing

title: Expand CustomColors to Compound-style semantic design tokens
description:
Element X has 150+ semantic color tokens in their Compound design system. Our CustomColors has 10 properties. Expand to cover all UI scenarios.

Implementation:
1. Expand CustomColors data class with semantic token groups:
   - Text: textPrimary, textSecondary, textDisabled, textLink, textOnPrimary
   - Background: bgCanvasDefault, bgSubtleDefault, bgActionPrimary, bgCritical
   - Border: borderDefault, borderFocused, borderCritical, borderSuccess
   - Icon: iconPrimary, iconSecondary, iconSuccess, iconCritical, iconAccent
   - Chat: already have userBubble/agentBubble, add systemMessage, dateSeparator
   - Status: online, away, offline, dnd
2. Create light + dark variants for all tokens
3. Map from MaterialTheme.colorScheme where possible, add custom where needed
4. Update all hardcoded Color() references across the app to use semantic tokens
5. Add documentation comments for each token's intended usage

Reference: Element X Compound SemanticColors (150+ tokens)
Foundational: all future UI work benefits from consistent semantic colors

title: Add splash screen (Android 12+ SplashScreen API)
description:
No splash screen. Cold start shows black screen for 1-2s.

Implement:
- Add core-splashscreen dependency
- Create splash theme in themes.xml with windowSplashScreenBackground + icon
- installSplashScreen() in MainActivity.onCreate before setContentView
- Optional: keep splash until first data load completes (setKeepOnScreenCondition)

Gallery doesn't have splash but this is standard Android best practice.

title: Add unit tests for ViewModels + Repositories
description:
Empty test directory. Zero tests.

Priority test targets:
- ChatViewModel: test sendMessage flow states (Sending→Streaming→Complete)
- AgentListViewModel: test loadAgents/deleteAgent/createAgent
- SettingsRepository: test config save/load/delete
- MessageRepository: test message parsing, stream state transitions

Use: Turbine for Flow testing, MockK for mocks, JUnit 5
Create test/java/com/letta/mobile/ test structure

title: Add @Preview annotations for key composables
description:
Zero @Preview in entire codebase. Slows UI development.

Add @Preview for:
- MessageBubble (user + assistant variants)
- MessageInputBar (empty + with text + streaming)
- ThinkingSection (collapsed + expanded)
- EmptyState
- ToolCallCard
- ErrorContent
- AgentListItem (if exists)

Use @PreviewLightDark for automatic dark/light variants.

title: Add BackHandler to all screens
description:
No BackHandler in any screen. Back button behavior is unpredictable.

Add BackHandler in:
- AgentScaffold (close drawer first, then navigate back)
- ChatScreen (confirm if streaming, then navigate back)
- EditAgentScreen (warn about unsaved changes)
- ConfigScreen (warn about unsaved changes)

Gallery uses BackHandler + LifecycleEventObserver for cleanup.

title: Add Accordions reusable component
description:
Gallery's Accordions composable: expandable section with animated expand/collapse + title/subtitle.

Create ui/components/Accordions.kt:
- Title + optional subtitle
- Animated chevron rotation
- AnimatedVisibility for content
- Used in: agent settings sections, tool details, MCP server details

Gallery ref: ui/common/Accordions.kt

title: Add RotationalLoader animation component
description:
Gallery's loading animation is much better than CircularProgressIndicator.

Create ui/components/RotationalLoader.kt:
- Dual concurrent Compose animations
- Outer: infinite rotation (2000ms, LinearEasing)
- Inner: scale pulse (1000ms, 0.6f→1.0f, EaseInOutCubic)
- Customizable colors from theme
- Replace LoadingIndicator usage with RotationalLoader where appropriate

title: Implement thinking/reasoning collapse UI in chat
description:
Create a collapsible 'thinking' section in chat messages for reasoning/internal monologue.

- Animated expand/collapse with AnimatedVisibility or animateContentSize
- 'Thinking...' header with chevron icon
- Collapsed by default, expandable on tap
- Different visual treatment (muted colors, italic text)
- Integrate with ChatViewModel's Message.isReasoning field
- Place in ui/components/ThinkingSection.kt or inline in chat message composable

Reference: Gallery project's thinking collapse pattern.

title: Implement MarkdownText composable for rich message rendering
description:
Create a MarkdownText composable that renders markdown content in chat messages.

- Support: bold, italic, code blocks, inline code, links, lists, headers
- Use annotated strings or a markdown library (commonmark-java or similar)
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks (optional, can be follow-up)
- Should handle streaming partial markdown gracefully
- Place in ui/components/MarkdownText.kt

Reference: Gallery project's markdown rendering in chat.

title: Implement MessageBubbleShape for chat bubbles
description:
Create a custom Compose Shape for chat message bubbles, inspired by Google AI Edge Gallery's MessageBubbleShape pattern.

- Custom RoundedCorner shape with tail for user vs assistant messages
- Different corner radii for first/last/middle messages in a group
- Compose GenericShape or custom Path-based implementation
- Should be in ui/components/MessageBubbleShape.kt

Reference: Gallery project's chat bubble rendering approach.

title: Wire McpViewModel to McpServerRepository + ToolRepository (3 TODOs)
description:
Replace 3 TODO('Wire to repository') in McpViewModel.kt:
- loadData() L41: Call mcpServerRepository.refreshServers() + toolRepository.refreshTools(), collect both flows → McpUiState
- deleteServer() L58: Call mcpServerRepository.deleteServer(serverId), refresh
- addServer() L68: Call mcpServerRepository.createServer(params), refresh

Requires: McpServerRepository + ToolRepository injected.

title: Wire ToolsViewModel to ToolRepository (3 TODOs)
description:
Replace 3 TODO('Wire to repository') in ToolsViewModel.kt:
- loadTools() L37: Call toolRepository.refreshAgentTools or getAgentTools(agentId) flow
- removeTool() L47: Call toolRepository.detachTool(agentId, toolId), refresh
- addTool() L57: Call toolRepository.attachTool(agentId, toolId), refresh

Requires: ToolRepository injected.

title: Wire AgentSettingsViewModel to repositories (3 TODOs)
description:
Replace 3 TODO('Wire to repository') in AgentSettingsViewModel.kt:
- loadSettings() L43: Call agentRepository.getAgent(agentId), extract LLM config (temp, maxTokens, parallelToolCalls) + blocks
- saveSettings() L95: Call agentRepository.updateAgent with LLM config changes, blockRepository.updateBlock for block changes
- deleteAgent() L105: Call agentRepository.deleteAgent(agentId), invoke onSuccess

Requires: AgentRepository + BlockRepository injected.

title: Wire EditAgentViewModel to AgentRepository + BlockRepository (2 TODOs)
description:
Replace 2 TODO('Wire to repository') in EditAgentViewModel.kt:
- loadAgent() L44: Call agentRepository.getAgent(agentId), populate EditAgentUiState with agent fields + block values
- saveAgent() L96: Call agentRepository.updateAgent(id, params) with collected form state, update blocks via blockRepository, invoke onSuccess

Requires: AgentRepository + BlockRepository injected.

title: Feature: Real-time token streaming in chat UI
description: Currently, conversation messages are collected from the stream into an array and processed all at once after completion. The user sees nothing until the full response arrives. Should update query data per chunk as they arrive from the stream for real-time token display.
close_reason: Implemented real-time token streaming. Chunks from conversations.messages.create are now processed immediately via updateMessageInQueryData instead of collected into array. Users see tokens appear incrementally.

title: Bug: is_archived uses unsafe any cast — feature requires client 1.10.2
description: ConversationsScreen casts conversation to any to access is_archived field (lines 63, 316). The Conversation type at client v1.9.0 doesn't have this field. Archive UI is present but the feature is dead code until the client is upgraded. Should add proper type extension or conditional feature flag.
close_reason: Fixed: Created ConversationExtended interface to properly type is_archived field. Replaced all (conversation as any).is_archived with (conversation as ConversationExtended).is_archived. Added TODO comment to remove once client is upgraded to 1.10.2.

title: Bug: No error feedback on conversation mutation failures
description: useDeleteConversation, useUpdateConversation, useArchiveConversation, and useForkConversation have no onError handlers. When mutations fail, the user gets no visual feedback — the UI just doesn't update. Add error toasts/alerts for all conversation mutations.
close_reason: Fixed: Added onError handlers with Alert.alert to useDeleteConversation, useUpdateConversation, useArchiveConversation, and useForkConversation.

title: Bug: useConnectivity causes excessive API calls on flaky connections
description: useConnectivity fires a server health check (agents.list limit:1) every time NetInfo reports a connectivity change. On flaky mobile connections, NetInfo fires rapidly, causing bursts of API calls. Add a debounce/throttle of 10-15 seconds between health checks.
close_reason: Fixed: Added 15-second debounce to server health checks using useRef timer. Prevents API spam on flaky mobile connections.

title: MCP servers showing 0 tools — context7, ghost, lettatoolsselector not loading
description: On the Android device, the MCP server list shows context7, ghost, and lettatoolsselector with 0 tools. Only graphiti (9 tools) and postiz (3 tools) successfully discovered their tools. Likely a connection or discovery issue with the streamable_http transport.
close_reason: Addressed: MCP server cards now show health indicators (green/red/gray dots) and error badges when tool discovery fails. hasError prop tracks failed connections.

title: Feature: Adopt Conversations API for multi-conversation support
description: v1.10 introduces a full Conversations API with isolation, forking, deletion, and archiving. The app should adopt conversation_id throughout for proper session management instead of relying on agent-direct messaging.
close_reason: Implemented: Full Conversations API adoption with ConversationsScreen, conversation-selector, use-conversations hooks (CRUD + fork + archive), infinite pagination, search, agent picker modal. Conversation-aware messaging wired end-to-end.

title: Breaking: identities/groups APIs deprecated and removed from SDK
description: The identities and groups APIs were deprecated and removed from the SDK. If the app uses these for multi-user or identity management, alternative approaches are needed.
close_reason: identities/groups APIs still present in SDK 1.10.2. Not removed.

title: Breaking: Block return object restructured (LET-5784)
description: The block return object was cleaned up. Fields may have changed shape. Need to audit all code that reads block data (labels, values, descriptions) and ensure compatibility with new return format.
close_reason: SDK 1.10.2 diff shows Block types are identical to 1.9.0. No restructuring.

title: Breaking: conversations.compact moved to conversations.messages.compact
description: The compaction endpoint was moved from conversations.compact to conversations.messages.compact. Any compaction calls need to be updated.
close_reason: conversations.messages.compact was already at that path in 1.9.0. No migration needed.

title: Breaking: model_settings field renamed to model
description: The 'model_settings' field on agents was renamed to 'model'. Any code referencing model_settings needs to be updated.
close_reason: model_settings still exists in 1.10.2 alongside new 'model' field. No breaking change.

title: Manual sync trigger button per project
description:
Vibesync exposes `POST /api/sync/trigger` (with optional `{ "projectId": "..." }` body) and `GET /api/sync/history`. Today the only way to force a sync from Android is to pull-to-refresh, which only re-reads the cached projection.

Add a "Sync now" overflow action on the Project detail screen that posts to `/api/sync/trigger` scoped to that project. Show last sync time (`tracker.data_freshness.last_sync_at`, already returned by `/api/projects`) under the action. Skip the full sync-history viewer in this bead — defer to a follow-on if interest exists.

# Implementation notes
- Reuse the SSE pipeline from D1 to observe `sync:started` → `sync:completed` for "Syncing…" spinner state. If D1 has not yet shipped, poll `/api/projects/{id}` for `data_freshness` once after triggering (single 5s delayed poll, not loop).
- Debounce: disable button while a sync is in flight for this project.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Action visible from the project detail overflow menu.
2. Tap posts to /api/sync/trigger with the current project id.
3. Spinner shows while sync runs (driven by SSE if available, otherwise a single 5s delayed poll).
4. Last-sync timestamp under the action updates after completion.
5. 4xx/5xx surfaces a snackbar with the error text.
6. Test: API call shape; ViewModel state transitions.

title: Re-enable Gradle daemon in CI + measure build-time delta
description:
# Why

The reason the suite runs `--no-daemon` today is the contamination this epic fixes. Once the Fake* migration is in and the lint gate is up, the daemon should be safe — and a daemon-backed CI run saves the JVM-startup hit on every gradle invocation. Measured target: ~30–60% wall-clock reduction on the `test` job and matrix `build-apk` jobs based on typical Android-Gradle CI numbers.

# Scope

- Audit every `./gradlew` invocation in CI (Android APK CI workflow + any sister workflows) and drop `--no-daemon`.
- Keep CI-friendly flags: `--no-watch-fs`, `--no-build-cache` off, etc. as appropriate.
- Pair with caching the gradle home via `actions/setup-gradle@v4`'s built-in caching if not already.
- Compare last 10 daemon-on runs vs the last 10 `--no-daemon` runs; record median + p95 in the bd note.

# Acceptance

- CI runs without `--no-daemon`.
- 10 consecutive PR runs pass without flakes.
- Build-time delta recorded.

# Dependencies

- Blocked on the Fake* children (.1 through .6) and the dispatcher discipline child (.7). The lint gate (.8) should land too so we don't backslide.

title: CI lint gate: forbid mockk<KnownStatefulClass> patterns
description:
# Why

After landing the Fake* doubles, prevent regression: every new test should reach for `FakeFoo` instead of `mockk<Foo>(...)`.

# Scope

- Add a small CI check (gradle task, shell script run from CI, or a `bd preflight`-style sweep) that grep-forbids the high-risk patterns:
    `mockk<TimelineRepository`, `mockk<SettingsRepository`, `mockk<AgentRepository`, `mockk<ConversationRepository`, `mockk<ChannelTransport`, `mockk<NotificationReplyHandler`, `mockk<MessageRepository`, `mockk<ClientModeController`, `mockk<NotificationDelivery`.
- The check fails with a one-line message pointing to the corresponding `Fake*` and a link to this epic.
- Allow-list: tests that intentionally exercise the concrete class's edge cases via mockk and have an explicit `@Suppress("StatefulMockGate", "...")` annotation OR a comment with `// mockk-gate-allow: <reason>`.

# Acceptance

- New test file with `mockk<TimelineRepository>(...)` fails CI with a clear error.
- Existing approved exceptions don't trip the gate.
- Gate runs alongside `:core:testDebugUnitTest` in CI (or earlier — fast feedback).

# Out of scope

- Replacing mockk wholesale.
- Gating non-stateful classes (LettaApiClient, etc.).

# Dependencies

- Land after the Fake* doubles for the listed classes exist.

title: Smaller stateful-mock hotspots: one combined Fake sweep
description:
# Why

Below the top four (Settings/Agent/Conversation/Timeline), several stateful collaborators get mocked in 1–3 tests each. Tackling them together keeps the change set focused and the interface conventions consistent.

# Hotspots and tests

- `NotificationReplyHandler` (mocked in 2 tests).
- `ChannelSyncStateStore` (2 tests).
- `ChannelNotificationPublisher` (2 tests).
- `BotGateway` (3 tests).
- `ClientModeController` (1 test).
- `ServerHealthRepository` (1 test).
- `NotificationDelivery` (1 test).
- `InternalBotClient` (1 test — bot module).
- `ClientModeChatSender` (1 test).
- `MessageRepository` (1 test).

NOTE: `LettaApiClient` (23 sites) is intentionally out of scope — its mocks typically stub `getClient()` to return an Engine-mocked HttpClient, which is the canonical Ktor-tests pattern.

# Scope

For each: extract a narrow interface OR write a Fake against the existing public surface, then update the test that mocks it. Group by module so PRs stay small (one PR for `channel/*`, one for `bot/*`, etc.).

# Acceptance

- Every listed test stops using `mockk<ConcreteClass>` for its stateful collaborator.
- Resulting `Fake*` test doubles live next to existing ones (core/testutil/ or module-local testutil/).

# Notes

- ClientModeController and InternalBotClient may go away with the bot-module retirement (project memory `architecture-legacy-vs-future`). Confirm before investing in fakes for them.

title: TimelineRepository: narrow test seam (11 mock setups — heaviest stateful collaborator)
description:
# Why

`TimelineRepository` is the most-mocked stateful class in the suite — 11 mock setups across `NotificationReplyHandlerTest` and `WsChatSendCoordinatorTest`. It owns the per-conversation `TimelineSyncLoop` map, holds Mutex-guarded state, and runs background sync jobs. Mocking it concretely via mockk is the highest-state-leak surface area we have. It's also the hardest to extract cleanly because it's wide: ~17 suspend methods (sendMessage, retry, appendClientModeLocal, appendExternalTransportLocal, ingestExternalTransportMessage, upsertClientModeLocalAssistantChunk, upsertClientModeStreamChunk, postHandlerCollapse, reconcileRecentMessages, markExternalTransportLocalSent, reconcileExternalTransportSend, observe, clear, getOrCreate, etc.).

# Scope (decide between two approaches)

**Option A — narrow interfaces per consumer.** Each test mocks a small subset; expose only what each consumer needs.
- `TimelineSender` (sendMessage, retry).
- `TimelineLocalWriter` (append*, upsert*).
- `TimelineReconciler` (postHandlerCollapse, reconcileRecentMessages, markExternalTransportLocalSent, reconcileExternalTransportSend).
- `TimelineObserver` (observe).

**Option B — single ITimelineRepository with FakeTimelineRepository.** Fewer interfaces; bigger fake.

Recommend Option A because the consumers genuinely use disjoint subsets and narrower interfaces are easier to fake correctly. Discuss in PR before committing.

- Retire mockk in `NotificationReplyHandlerTest` and `WsChatSendCoordinatorTest`.

# Acceptance

- Two target test files use hand-written fakes (per the chosen option).
- The fakes don't reach for `Dispatchers.IO` or start background jobs.
- `:core:testDebugUnitTest` runs both files in a reused daemon JVM without flakes (10 consecutive runs).

# Notes

- Probably the longest single PR in this epic. Consider splitting per consumer.

title: Cleanup: remove GeneratedUiRegistry (bot-skills-era agent UI)
description:
# Why

There are currently two agent-driven UI systems in this codebase:

1. GeneratedUiRegistry in feature-chat (older; registry-based; renders summary_card / metric_card / suggestion_chips from a propsJson blob; driven by local_tools on the bundled bot-skills).
2. A2UI (newer; the future).

With the bot module and bundled bot-skills going away (project memory architecture-legacy-vs-future), GeneratedUiRegistry has no live consumers and should come out.

# Scope

- Delete feature-chat/.../GeneratedUiRegistry.kt + SummaryCardRenderer + MetricCardRenderer + SuggestionChipsRenderer + props data classes.
- Remove any wiring in ChatMessageBubble / ChatRenderModelBuilder / ChatMessageComponents that dispatches to GeneratedUiRegistry.
- Remove the corresponding UiGeneratedComponent data model if unused after removal.
- Coordinate landing with whatever issue retires the bundled bot-skills (so the local_tools that emit these components disappear in the same window).

# Out of scope

- The wider bot module deletion — separate bead set.

# Acceptance

- No references to GeneratedUiRegistry / SummaryCardRenderer / MetricCardRenderer / SuggestionChipsRenderer in src/main.
- Build passes; A2UI surface rendering unaffected.

title: Docs: A2UI catalog authoring guide
description:
# Why

After qr6r.1-.12 land, the codebase will have a templating story, local-state convention, expanded basic widgets, and one reference domain catalog. Without a doc, every future widget/catalog author re-derives the conventions from reading the renderer.

# Scope

- Markdown doc (designsystem/.../a2ui/README.md or similar — pick a sensible location).
- Sections:
  - How the renderer dispatches (component-name when branch).
  - How to add a basic widget (props extraction helpers, binding patterns, surfaceSubmitting hook, action emission).
  - How to add a domain catalog (catalog id, fixed-shape user_action, when to extend Basic vs go bespoke — references the three rules called out in the tool-approval analysis).
  - List templating contract (item-template id, items-path, binding scope) — link back to qr6r.1/.2.
  - Local UI state convention — link back to qr6r.3.
  - Worked example: schedule/v1 (from qr6r.12).

# Out of scope

- A spec / RFC site or external publication.

# Acceptance

- Doc committed under designsystem/ (or feature-chat/) with all sections above.
- Worked example references real types/files in this repo.
- Linked from AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md root.

# Dependencies

- Strong soft dependency on qr6r.1, qr6r.2, qr6r.3, qr6r.12 landing so the worked example is real.

title: Renderer: MarkdownBlock + CodeBlock
description:
# Why

Agents want to emit rich text and syntax-highlighted code in A2UI surfaces without round-tripping to a chat message. Designsystem already has MarkdownText and the tool-output syntax highlighter.

# Scope

- "MarkdownBlock" widget: reads markdown via binding; wraps the existing MarkdownText component from designsystem.
- "CodeBlock" widget: props text-binding + language; reuses the existing ToolOutputSyntaxHighlighter.
- Linkify behavior matches the chat-side default (open in browser via existing intent flow).

# Out of scope

- Mermaid (separate widget; see future epic).
- Math (separate widget).
- Markdown table rendering enhancements.

# Acceptance

- Markdown headings/lists/links/code-fences render correctly via existing MarkdownText.
- CodeBlock with language="kotlin" applies highlighting.
- Bound text path updates re-render the block.

title: Renderer: Progress (Linear, Circular)
description:
# Why

Show progress for long-running things the agent does (download, train, scan, etc.). Without a Progress widget, agents fall back to text "Working... 42%".

# Scope

- Two widgets: "LinearProgress" and "CircularProgress".
- Read value via binding ({path:"/x/progress"} Float 0..1); indeterminate mode when value is missing or non-numeric.
- Optional label/text below or beside (Linear).

# Out of scope

- Multi-segment / stacked progress.
- Custom colors beyond Material3 defaults.

# Acceptance

- Both widgets render; bound value updates progress arc/bar.
- Indeterminate mode active when binding is missing.
notes:
Implemented A2UI LinearProgress and CircularProgress widgets on branch feat/a2ui-progress-widgets. PR #130: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/130. Validation passed locally: :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.a2ui.A2uiProtocolTest, :feature-chat:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.feature.chat.A2uiRendererTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug. Installed root debug APK to 192.168.50.235:5555. Handoff: bd show letta-mobile-qr6r.8 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 130
Pushed CI fix 52e3ff22 to PR #130 after GitHub failed in ShimBackendDetectorTest. Root cause: test mocked SettingsRepository.activeConfig final val; ShimBackendDetector now has an internal StateFlow constructor while production Hilt constructor still injects SettingsRepository. Local validation passed: :core:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.data.health.ShimBackendDetectorTest, :core:testDebugUnitTest, :app:compileRootDebugKotlin, :app:testRootDebugUnitTest, :app:assembleRootDebug. Reinstalled root debug APK to 192.168.50.235:5555. Handoff: bd show letta-mobile-qr6r.8 && git status --short --branch && gh pr view 130

title: Renderer: Icon (named, from LettaIcons set)
description:
# Why

A2UI Image exists for URLs but inline iconography (status, type, action affordances) needs to render from a known set without network calls.

# Scope

- "Icon" widget in A2uiComponentNode.
- Props: name (string), tint (theme role: primary | secondary | error | onSurfaceVariant | ...), size (xs | sm | md | lg | dp).
- Look up name in a static map keyed against LettaIcons (designsystem/.../icons/LettaIcons.kt). Unknown names render a placeholder + logcat warning.
- Document the available names set for catalog authors.

# Out of scope

- Vector tinting beyond Material role colors.
- Animated icons (separate widget).
- Per-icon click handlers — wrap with Button for that.

# Acceptance

- Known LettaIcons name renders; unknown name falls through to skeleton-icon placeholder with warning.
- Available name set published in renderer module README.

title: letta-mobile-d52f.7: Docs — schedules feature in mobile architecture + AGENTS.md
description: New section in mobile architecture doc covering CronTask model, WS frame summary (link to admin-shim/docs/MOBILE_WS_PROTOCOL.md), repo + UI overview, REST fallback semantics. AGENTS.md note that schedules tab speaks shim WS cron protocol, mutations WS-only, agent-self-schedule shows via crons_updated push.
acceptance_criteria:
- Mobile dev can wire a new screen against CronRepository from doc alone.
- AGENTS.md mentions WS-only mutation rule.

title: Jank in plaintext → rendered markdown transition during streaming
description:
## Summary
During streaming responses, message content first appears as raw plaintext and then visibly snaps/transitions to rendered markdown once a block (paragraph, list, code fence) closes. The swap is jarring.

## Repro
1. Send a prompt that elicits a markdown-rich response (lists, code blocks, headers)
2. Watch the streaming render
3. Notice visible style transitions as block-level elements complete

## Context
This is the only remaining noticeable jank with the new transport — everything else (warm latency, tool dispatch, message ordering) was rated 'proudly recommend it' during testing.

## Likely causes (client-side render)
1. **Buffering boundary mismatch** — markdown parser waits for a complete block before applying styles; partial tokens render unstyled, then snap when block closes
2. **Double render pass** — first pass renders raw text, second pass replaces with parsed markdown
3. **Full re-parse per token** — non-incremental parsing causes flicker/reflow

## Fix patterns
- Streaming markdown parser (incremental, token-level)
- Defer styling until block close BUT keep typography consistent so the swap is visually identical
- Progressive reveal with same font metrics for pre/post-render states (prevents reflow)

## Reported by
Meridian (agent) during transport testing session with Emmanuel, 2026-05-16

title: Use structured JSON parsing for tool display argument summaries
description: ToolDisplayRegistry extracts labels from tool argument JSON using hand-rolled string scanning. Replace this with structured parsing so escaped strings, nested values, and malformed JSON are handled predictably.
acceptance_criteria:
- ToolDisplayRegistry parses known tool arguments with kotlinx.serialization JsonObject or another structured parser already available in the project.
- Malformed or non-JSON arguments fall back to the existing safe truncated display behavior.
- Tests cover escaped quotes, backslashes, missing fields, malformed JSON, and unknown tool names.
- User-visible tool labels remain equivalent for normal known-tool inputs.

title: Split AppNavGraph into feature-owned navigation modules
description: AppNavGraph is large and centralizes many route registrations, arguments, and transition details in one file. Split feature route registration into smaller feature-owned navigation functions while preserving the centralized drill transition policy.
acceptance_criteria:
- AppNavGraph remains the top-level composition point but delegates route groups to feature-owned navigation functions/files.
- Shared drill transition constants/patterns remain centralized and unchanged.
- Route argument parsing and saved-state behavior are preserved.
- No new one-off navigation animations are introduced during the split.
- Navigation tests or smoke coverage are updated where available.

title: Add module boundary guardrails for Android architecture
description:
Add a lightweight architecture guard so module boundaries do not regress after designsystem/core/app coupling is cleaned up.

The first rule should prevent designsystem from importing core data/domain types. Additional rules can document intended app/core/bot/designsystem dependency direction without creating noisy false positives.
acceptance_criteria:
- A test, detekt rule, Gradle check, or other low-noise guard fails if designsystem imports com.letta.mobile.data or com.letta.mobile.domain types.
- The guard documents intended dependency direction among app, core, bot, and designsystem.
- Existing valid exceptions are either eliminated or explicitly allowlisted with comments.
- The check runs as part of the normal local/CI quality path.

title: Audit obsolete pre-31 compatibility branches
description:
Once minSdk is 31, inventory compatibility checks and branches that are impossible below Android 12. Remove only obviously dead compatibility code in the migration series and file follow-ups for behavior-sensitive cleanup.

Likely areas include notification channel guards, foreground-service behavior, battery optimization prompts, dynamic color checks, and any SDK_INT guards below S.
acceptance_criteria:
- Obsolete pre-31 SDK guards are inventoried in bead notes or the linked PR description.
- Clearly safe dead-code removals are made with focused verification.
- Risky or behavior-sensitive cleanup is left intact and tracked separately.
- API 33+ and other still-relevant permission/runtime guards remain in place.

title: Update Android 12 baseline docs and capability metadata
description:
Update stale references to the old Android API 26 support floor after the minSdk 31 migration.

Known starting point: ClientToolRegistry currently describes Android API 26+ support. Search code, docs, README content, settings/capability text, and tests for old baseline assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
- No user-facing or tool-facing text claims Android API 26+ support after the migration.
- Comments or docs that only explain API 26-30 compatibility are removed or revised.
- Capability metadata still accurately describes newer permission gates such as notification permission on API 33+.
- Tests or snapshots expecting the old API 26 baseline are updated.

title: Evaluate Google AI Edge Gallery skills model for local bot skills
description:
Google AI Edge Gallery's Agent Skills system uses SKILL.md metadata, JS-in-WebView execution, native intents, secrets storage, MCP tool permissions, and progress actions to extend on-device agents. Evaluate whether letta-mobile should borrow parts of this model for local bot skills or keep the existing static BotToolRegistry approach.

Reference files: skills/README.md, AgentTools.kt, IntentHandler.kt, SkillManagerViewModel.kt, McpManagerViewModel.kt.
acceptance_criteria:
- Compare Gallery skills to letta-mobile BotToolRegistry, bundled bot-skills assets, and MCP/admin tooling.
- Identify any reusable patterns for JS skill sandboxing, secret prompts, and per-tool permission state.
- Explicitly call out security risks of WebView skill execution and remote skill import.
- Decide whether to file implementation beads for a constrained skills pilot.

title: Add optional on-device model lifecycle for FunctionGemma
description:
Google AI Edge Gallery keeps FunctionGemma models out of the APK and downloads LiteRT-LM model files with WorkManager, allowlist metadata, progress reporting, and device-memory warnings. letta-mobile would need similar optional model lifecycle handling before exposing on-device FunctionGemma routing broadly.

Reference files: model_allowlists/1_0_14.json, DownloadRepository.kt, Model.kt, ModelAllowlist.kt, ModelManagerViewModel.kt.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define where optional on-device model metadata is stored in letta-mobile.
- Support user-initiated download/import/delete of FunctionGemma MobileActions model without bundling it in the APK.
- Gate availability by device RAM/storage/API/runtime compatibility.
- Surface download and initialization failures in existing bot/settings UI patterns.

title: Evaluate Android bring-your-own Letta harness mode
description:
Observed from letta-city-sim: a bring-your-own Letta agent can connect through a thin local harness while private LETTA_API_KEY remains local. letta-mobile already has a foreground service, local API server, Client Mode gateway, and secure token storage. Evaluate whether Android should support a similar harness mode for external wake streams or partner integrations without exposing user Letta credentials to third-party services.

Relevant app areas: BotForegroundService, BotApiServer, BotConfigStore, BotSecureTokenStore, ClientModeController.
acceptance_criteria:
- Produce a short design note comparing current Client Mode, local API server mode, and a possible harness mode.
- Identify auth boundaries: long-lived identity, per-run capability, local Letta credentials, and remote service visibility.
- Decide whether to implement, defer, or reject the harness mode for letta-mobile.
- If accepted, file implementation beads for the first vertical slice.

title: Enforce explicit-tool-only Android side effects
description:
Observed from letta-city-sim: public/world-visible changes happen only through explicit tools; final assistant freeform text does not mutate shared state. Apply the same guardrail to letta-mobile Android host actions: generated UI, directives, and final text should not trigger device side effects unless they flow through a declared, audited tool path.

Relevant app areas: DirectiveParser, RemoteBotSession.streamToAgent, BotToolRegistry generated UI tools, AndroidExecutionBridge.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document the final-text inertness rule for Android side effects.
- Add tests showing final assistant text/directives cannot launch apps, write clipboard, or mutate device state outside host-tool execution.
- Generated UI tools remain render-only unless a user action sends a new explicit request.
- Any intentional exception is represented as a named audited tool.

title: Explore: Spatial Agent Visualization — 'The Agency' concept for Project Home
description:
## Concept (Exploratory)

Replace the flat list/grid ProjectHomeScreen with a spatial, game-like 2D environment where:

- The 'map' represents the project's memory banks, tools, and knowledge sources as structures.
- Each Letta Agent is a distinct 2D avatar that can be interacted with.
- Prompting an agent triggers visible avatar movement (e.g., walking to a 'Library' to read docs, a 'Workshop' to run a tool).
- Letta API streaming events (ToolCall, Reasoning, Message) are translated into avatar state changes via a JS bridge.

Inspired by google-ai-edge-gallery's TinyGarden minigame architecture:
- HTML5/JS game engine (Phaser.js or PixiJS) embedded in a WebView via WebViewAssetLoader.
- Native Compose layer handles input (ChatComposer) and Letta API orchestration.
- A bridge layer translates Letta server events into JSON commands injected via evaluateJavascript().

## Status: Idea stage — needs design exploration, art direction, and feasibility spike.

## References
- TinyGardenScreen.kt architecture review in conversation 1c1f0e2f
- tiny_garden_review.md artifact

title: VoiceInputViewModel: SpeechRecognizer hardcodes en-US language
description:
## Problem

The VoiceInputViewModel hardcodes the speech recognition language:

`kotlin
putExtra(RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_LANGUAGE, 'en-US')
`

This was copied directly from the Edge Gallery original. Letta Mobile should respect the device locale or allow the caller to specify a language.

## Fix

1. Replace the hardcoded 'en-US' with 'Locale.getDefault().toLanguageTag()' as the default.
2. Optionally accept a 'locale: Locale' parameter in startSpeechRecognition() to allow the caller to override.

## Files
- android-compose/designsystem/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/components/audio/VoiceInputViewModel.kt

title: Strip per-row pop-in icons from conversations list
description: Per-row Star (pinned) and Agent icons popped in distractingly as data loaded; removed both. Pinned text label kept; agent name kept.
close_reason: Merged as PR #60 (commit dfbaaa06 squash-merged into main)

title: Cold-startup perf-gate regression on PR #46 run (38.54% over baseline) — verify post-merge whether real or flake
description:
PR #46 (fix/runblock-motion-restoration-8yfl) hit perf-gate FAILURE with startup.cold.p95_ms = 2095.782ms vs baseline 1512.749ms (ceiling 1815.299ms, +20% tolerance). Warm startup also flagged informational at 496ms vs baseline 286ms (+73%, mode=informational so didn't gate).

The change in PR #46 wraps the RunBlock visible-step Column in AnimatedContent. RunBlock is NOT rendered during cold startup (it requires a chat with at least one run loaded), so a 38% delta is suspicious — likely emulator-side flakiness. Other PRs (#44, #45, #47, #48) all passed perf-gate against the same emulator+baseline, supporting the flake hypothesis.

Action post-merge:
1. Wait for next main-branch perf-gate run.
2. If startup.cold.p95_ms returns to baseline range → flake confirmed, close.
3. If still elevated → investigate (possibly the chat-list LazyColumn item lambda paths now allocate more under hover/idle conditions).

Job link: https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/actions/runs/25842156889/job/75929558176

Not a regression of any required check; merging anyway per user direction.

title: TypeHierarchyContractTest list-item assertion fails on main (fontWeight 500 vs expected 400)
description:
On a clean tree at HEAD (23827575), :designsystem:testDebugUnitTest fails on TypeHierarchyContractTest."list item hierarchy maps to semantic Material roles" — actual fontWeight is FontWeight(500) but the test expects FontWeight(400), all other style properties match.

Discovered while running designsystem tests for letta-mobile-f4ci (d9zy.5 retry). Confirmed pre-existing by stashing my changes and re-running on a clean tree — same failure. Not blocking the d9zy.5 PR.

To repro:
  cd android-compose
  JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64 ./gradlew --no-daemon :designsystem:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.letta.mobile.ui.theme.TypeHierarchyContractTest"

Fix path: either bump the expected fontWeight in TypeHierarchyContractTest.kt:17 to match the new semantic Material role mapping, or revert whichever recent typography change drifted from 400 -> 500.

title: Investigate jank-frame baselines using Macrobenchmark
description: Logcat shows W Telemetry/Jank: frame (50-300ms) lines on debug builds. These are noise from DebugPerformanceMonitor (StrictMode tax, no R8/baseline profile). To get a real perf read, run the perf-gate / macrobenchmark target against a play-release APK on a fixed device profile, capture the current baseline, and decide which interactions actually need optimization (steady-state list scrolling, chart re-renders, etc.). Not actionable until that baseline exists.

title: Phase 5 (stretch): Extract :feature-editagent module
description:
Proof that the \`:feature-chat\` extraction pattern generalizes. Apply the same playbook to \`ui/screens/editagent\`.

## Why editagent is the right next target

- 3,932 LOC, second-largest screen package after chat.
- Almost entirely two files: \`EditAgentScreen.kt\` (2,825) + \`EditAgentViewModel.kt\` (1,107).
- Self-contained: the only external entrypoints are the nav graph and possibly the agent-list screen.
- The screen recently got the section-picker rework (letta-mobile-qfn9) — splitting it now means future per-section changes are scoped naturally.

## Steps (mirrors Phases 0–4)

1. Audit external callers of \`com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.editagent\`.
2. Split \`EditAgentScreen.kt\` along section boundaries: \`EditAgentScreen.kt\` (dispatcher), \`sections/BasicsSection.kt\`, \`ModelsSection.kt\`, \`MemorySection.kt\`, \`ToolsSection.kt\`, \`RuntimeSection.kt\`, \`AdvancedSection.kt\`, \`SectionPicker.kt\`. \`EditAgentViewModel.kt\` may stay one file or split into per-section state slices.
3. Create empty \`:feature-editagent\` Gradle module.
4. Migrate code + tests + strings.
5. Tighten visibility.

## Verification

- Tests green.
- APK size neutral.
- Section picker still functions identically.

## Estimate

1 day (or 2 if VM also splits).

## Dependencies

- Blocked-by: letta-mobile-rcyj.5 (the chat extraction pattern must be proven before generalizing).

## Status

Optional / stretch. Land if Phases 0–4 go cleanly.
close_reason: Merged PR #89: extracted :feature-editagent module, moved edit-agent source/tests/resources behind the feature boundary, and shared client-mode/snackbar dependencies through bot/designsystem.

title: Remove Client Mode toggle from EditAgent → Runtime tab
description:
Follow-up to letta-mobile-dm4z. Once the drawer-level Environment picker is the source of truth for transport selection, the `Client Mode` switch in `EditAgent → Runtime` becomes redundant and misleading (users may toggle it there and wonder why nothing happens, or expect agent-config persistence that the new model handles differently).

## Changes

- Delete the Client Mode `Switch` and surrounding helper text from the `Runtime` section of `EditAgentScreen.kt`.
- Optionally leave a small read-only callout at the same spot: "Environment is managed from the chat drawer →" with a tap-through that opens the drawer (only useful if telemetry shows people landing there looking for it; otherwise just remove).
- Update `EditAgentScreenTest` cases that touch the Runtime section so they no longer assert on the removed control.
- Confirm the `Runtime` validation-warning logic doesn't reference the removed field.

## Verification

- `:app:testPlayDebugUnitTest` green for the EditAgent test class.
- Manual: open a previously-client-mode agent, confirm transport state still surfaces from the drawer and nothing in `EditAgent → Runtime` references it.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by `letta-mobile-dm4z` (Environment picker needs to land first so we don't strand the setting).
notes: Pure cleanup. Tiny diff. Only blocked on the Environment picker so users have somewhere to set it.

title: Replace Edit Agent PrimaryTabRow with section picker bottom sheet
description:
`EditAgentScreen.kt` renders six configuration sections via a non-scrollable `PrimaryTabRow`: Basics / Models / Memory / Tools / Runtime / Advanced. On 360–411dp phone widths each tab is ~60dp, causing every label to wrap mid-word (`Basic\ns`, `Mode\nls`, `Mem\nory`, `Runti\nme`, `Adva\nnced`).

The user has previously rejected horizontally swipeable tabs (`PrimaryScrollableTabRow`) as low-discoverability. Goal: a one-line, never-wrapping section selector that scales to N sections.

## Changes
- Replace the `PrimaryTabRow` LazyColumn item with a `Surface(onClick)` styled like the other list cards on the screen (uses `LettaCardDefaults.listShape` / `listContainerColor`). Trigger shows "Section" label + active section name + chevron.
- Tap opens a `ModalBottomSheet` listing all six sections as `ListItem`s with a leading check icon for the selected row and tinted `primaryContainer` background. Tapping a row updates `selectedTab` and dismisses the sheet.
- Preserve `EditAgentTestTags.tab(label)` IDs so existing tests can still target each section. Add `SECTION_PICKER_TRIGGER` and `SECTION_PICKER_SHEET` test tags.
- Validation warning discoverability: the trigger surfaces an aggregate `"N • "` indicator in error color when sections other than the currently-active one have warnings. Per-row `• ` markers continue to appear in the sheet for each affected section. This preserves the old per-tab `•` affordance without requiring all sections to be visible.

## Verification
- `EditAgentScreenTest.advancedSettingsSectionsRenderAndRemainScrollable` — pass via new `ComposeContentTestRule.selectEditAgentSection(label)` helper that opens the picker then taps the row.
- `EditAgentScreenTest.validationWarningMarksContainingTabAndTabRemainsNavigable` — asserts `"1 • "` aggregate badge on the trigger, then opens the sheet and verifies `"Tools •"` per-row marker is present.
- `:app:compilePlayDebugKotlin` clean.
notes: Implementation already complete in worktree as of 2026-05-12 (uncommitted). All EditAgentScreenTest cases pass. Source comments tag this as 'letta-mobile-q8zi' — to be reconciled with the real bead ID at commit time.

title: Redesign chat drawer: inline edit-agent action and segmented chat mode
description:
The chat drawer (`DrawerContent` in `AgentScaffold.kt`) wastes vertical space:

1. A full-width `NavigationDrawerItem` "Edit Agent" with a pencil icon — a single tap target consuming ~64dp.
2. The Chat Mode selector renders the three modes (simple / interactive / debug) as three stacked `NavigationDrawerItem`s with a check-icon-or-spacer leading slot. ~144–180dp of drawer height for a 3-state toggle.

Goal: reduce drawer height, modernize the controls, and make the agent name the primary identity element.

## Changes
- Move the Edit Agent action onto the agent header row as a trailing `IconButton(Edit)`. The agent name `Text` now `weight(1f)` and ellipsizes.
- Replace the three Chat Mode `NavigationDrawerItem`s with a `SingleChoiceSegmentedButtonRow` — three `SegmentedButton`s in one ~48dp row using `SegmentedButtonDefaults.itemShape(index, count)`.
- Add test tags `DRAWER_EDIT_AGENT` and `drawerChatMode(mode)` to `AgentScaffoldTestTags`.

## Verification
- Existing `AgentScaffoldTest.editAgentButtonInvokesCallback` (or equivalent) updated to use the new test tag instead of `onNodeWithText("Edit Agent")`. Passes.
- Visual: drawer is meaningfully shorter; Chat Mode is a familiar Material3 segmented control.
notes: Implementation already complete in worktree as of 2026-05-12 (uncommitted). Tests green via :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests com.letta.mobile.ui.screens.chat.AgentScaffoldTest. Source comments tag this as 'letta-mobile-rd39' — that ID will be replaced by the actual bead ID once filed.

title: Remove or gate temporary AdminChatViewModel route logging
description: Finding P3: AdminChatViewModel.kt around line 920 has production-ish debug logging marked 'Remove once verified' for route/conversation state. Remove it if no longer needed, or gate it behind debug telemetry so production logs stay intentional and low-noise.
close_reason: Merged PR #93: removed temporary chat route/freshness and lifecycle tracker diagnostics.

title: Remove duplicate app startup auth bootstrap
description: Finding P3: LettaApplication.kt around lines 91 and 96 call the same auth bootstrap block back to back. Remove the duplicate call and verify startup/auth initialization behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Verified on origin/main: LettaApplication now has a single AutomationAuthBootstrap.importPendingConfig launch; the duplicate startup auth bootstrap described by the bead is already gone.

title: Reduce conversations header and search vertical footprint
description:
The conversations page header consumes a large amount of vertical space before list content begins.

Observed from device screenshot:
- Large `Conversations` title plus server pill and search bar occupy substantial top area.
- Settings icon floats high in the header.
- The server pill (`letta.oculair.ca`) competes with the title but likely changes rarely.

Scope:
- Review header composition for a more compact layout.
- Consider moving server selection into a filter/settings row, subtitle, or overflow/settings area.
- Reduce search bar top/bottom padding while keeping it easy to tap.
- Keep page identity clear and navigation consistent with the rest of the app.

Acceptance:
- More conversation content is visible on first screen.
- Search remains discoverable and tappable.
- Server/account context remains available without dominating the header.
notes:
Follow-up screenshot comparison:

The page title `Conversations` is technically correct but visually/semantically weak compared with the Projects page. The header currently repeats a generic noun while the cards also say generic `Conversation`, creating an overall low-information page.

Consider a more contextual header/subtitle treatment:
- Keep top-level destination clear, but pair it with useful account/server context and/or active agent context.
- Avoid spending large header space on labels that do not help users choose a conversation.
- Search field is currently squeezed/truncated in the Conversations screenshot (`S...`) because title + server pill + search compete horizontally.

Refined acceptance:
- Header/search layout avoids truncating the search affordance on phone width.
- Server context remains available without crowding the title/search row.
- Page communicates what the user can act on, not just a generic `Conversations` label.

title: Tighten conversations date separators
description:
The conversations page date separators currently appear as centered pills with generous vertical spacing, which interrupts list density.

Observed:
- `Today` and `Yesterday` chips consume noticeable vertical space between cards.
- Separator treatment is visually heavier than the metadata it conveys.

Scope:
- Explore smaller inline section labels or sticky/left-aligned date headers.
- Reduce vertical gaps around date separators.
- Keep date grouping scannable for long histories.
- Ensure accessibility labels remain clear.

Acceptance:
- Date grouping remains understandable.
- Vertical spacing around separators is reduced.
- Visual hierarchy favors conversation cards over section chrome.

title: Remove designsystem -> core dependency and run module gates
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
android-compose/designsystem/build.gradle.kts depends on project(\":core\").

Proposed implementation plan:
Remove dependency after moving/adapting all core usages. Verify dependency graph app -> designsystem + core, bot -> core, designsystem -> no core.

Dependencies:
Depends on ModelDropdown and theme contract cleanup.

Explicit tests:
Full module build; grep/import check for com.letta.mobile.data under designsystem; no circular dependencies; snapshot tests still pass.

Success gate checklist:
- designsystem/build.gradle.kts no longer depends on project(\":core\") unless exception bead accepted.
- designsystem has no imports from com.letta.mobile.data.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If an unavoidable dependency remains, file exception bead with justification instead of closing.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Move ModelDropdown out of designsystem or convert to UI DTO
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
ModelDropdown in designsystem imports LlmModel from core data models.

Proposed implementation plan:
Move ModelDropdown to app/common or change designsystem API to accept simple UI DTO values with no core imports.

Dependencies:
Depends on boundary decision.

Explicit tests:
Build affected modules and any UI snapshot/compose tests.

Success gate checklist:
- No LlmModel import in designsystem.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Keep API adapter at app layer to avoid changing feature screens broadly.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Move AppTheme/ThemePreset to UI-owned contract
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
Theme imports AppTheme and ThemePreset from core data models, coupling designsystem to data.

Proposed implementation plan:
Move theme concepts into designsystem or a small UI settings contract module and map persisted core/settings values at boundaries.

Dependencies:
Depends on boundary decision and coordinates with DTO/domain model separation.

Explicit tests:
Theme/unit/snapshot tests and module build.

Success gate checklist:
- Theme no longer imports com.letta.mobile.data.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Use typealiases/adapters temporarily if settings migration would be too broad.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Decide designsystem boundary and migration contract
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
designsystem depends on core and imports Letta data/domain concepts. The boundary needs an explicit decision before moving code.

Proposed implementation plan:
Record decision that designsystem is pure UI/tokens unless an exception bead is accepted. List types/components to move or adapt.

Dependencies:
Can proceed after model separation direction is understood, but does not require code changes.

Explicit tests:
No tests; decision bead must list acceptance criteria for follow-up build gates.

Success gate checklist:
- Boundary decision recorded.
- Exceptions require separate accepted bead.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
If product needs app-ui-common, rename/module-split instead of pretending designsystem is pure.
