title: Restore designsystem module boundary purity
description:
Problem statement with file/line references:
android-compose/designsystem/build.gradle.kts depends on core. ModelDropdown in designsystem imports LlmModel. Theme imports AppTheme and ThemePreset from core data models.

Proposed implementation plan:
Decide and document boundary, preferring designsystem as pure UI/tokens. Move Letta-specific components such as ModelDropdown to app/common or pass simple UI DTOs. Move AppTheme/ThemePreset into UI settings contract or designsystem-owned model if they are theme concepts. Ensure dependency graph remains app -> designsystem + core, bot -> core, and designsystem does not depend on core.

Dependencies:
Lower priority; coordinate with model separation work when moving theme/model types.

Explicit tests:
Build all modules; compile-only tests if applicable; no circular dependencies; snapshot tests still pass.

Success gate checklist:
- designsystem has no imports from com.letta.mobile.data.
- designsystem/build.gradle.kts no longer depends on project(\":core\") unless a written exception bead is accepted.

Risk notes and rollback strategy:
Move one component/model at a time and keep temporary typealiases/adapters to reduce blast radius.
close_reason: Closed as stale per user direction; refactoring beads are obsolete.

title: Spike Tree-sitter/KTreeSitter tool output highlighting
description: Follow-up spike only: evaluate Tree-sitter/KTreeSitter behind ToolSyntaxHighlighter for future structured tool output syntax highlighting. Research summary: KTreeSitter Android artifact appears under io.github.tree-sitter:ktreesitter-android with Maven Central versions; KTreeSitter supports Kotlin multiplatform including Android per project docs; grammar packaging is the main cost; AndroidIDEOfficial/android-tree-sitter should be avoided unless strongly justified due maintenance risk; start with one grammar such as Bash or Python and measure APK/ABI impact.
acceptance_criteria: Spike builds on Linux, one grammar parses one code-like tool output, APK size/ABI impact is documented, and pure Kotlin fallback remains available behind ToolSyntaxHighlighter.

title: Convert static utility objects to injectable singletons
description:
## Problem
Several static utility `object` declarations should be injectable singletons to improve testability and follow the DI-first architecture.

## Files to convert
1. **Telemetry.kt** (core/util) — Global telemetry object, should be @Singleton @Inject
2. **EncryptedPrefsHelper.kt** (core/util) — Static helper, should be injectable
3. **ToolDisplayRegistry.kt** (app/ui/chat) — Static registry, should be @Singleton
4. **GeneratedUiRegistry.kt** (app/ui/chat) — Static registry, should be @Singleton

## Fix
- Add @Inject constructor or Hilt @Module @Provides binding
- Migrate callers from static access to constructor/field injection
- Add interface where needed for test mocking

## Acceptance
- Target objects are injectable via Hilt
- Existing callers updated to use DI
- Tests can mock these dependencies where needed
- `./gradlew :app:compileRootDebugKotlin` passes
notes: Converted 3 of 4 targets to @Singleton @Inject with companion bridge: ToolDisplayRegistry, GeneratedUiRegistry, EncryptedPrefsHelper. Telemetry deferred — 179 call sites across codebase, too invasive for P3. Companion bridge pattern preserves existing caller API while enabling Hilt DI. Recommended: revisit Telemetry during a dedicated observability refactor.

title: Replace chat copy Toast with snackbar or in-app banner
description:
Why this exists: Gallery uses Compose-owned transient surfaces instead of platform Toasts in chat. Letta Mobile still shows a Toast from ChatMessageComponents.copyToClipboard, which is visually inconsistent and harder to test.

What to do: Replace the chat copy confirmation Toast with LocalSnackbarDispatcher, a chat-local SnackbarHost, or the existing FloatingBanner pattern. Prefer the existing global snackbar infrastructure if accessible without over-plumbing. Keep the clipboard write behavior unchanged.

Reference: android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt copyToClipboard and MainActivity LocalSnackbarDispatcher setup.
acceptance_criteria: Copying a chat message no longer calls Toast.makeText from chat code. Users still receive visible confirmation. The implementation follows existing snackbar/banner patterns and does not introduce a new ad hoc transient component.

title: Design Letta Code file-shell alias resolver for Android scopes
description: Follow-up from letta-mobile-ifd7.3 mapping. If compatibility with Letta Code-style names is needed, design an alias resolver that maps ReadFile/WriteFile/Edit/ApplyPatch/Grep/Bash style requests onto explicit Android scopes such as app-private root, SAF URI/tree, media item, local shell cwd scope, root shell, or root filesystem scope. The resolver must fail closed when scope is ambiguous and should not expose arbitrary path or bash abstractions by default.

title: Prototype KernelSU/SukiSU app-profile guidance for Letta Mobile
description: Document how users should grant Letta Mobile root under KernelSU/SukiSU App Profile. Explore whether we need a helper module or just standard su access. Note KernelSU/SukiSU app profiles can constrain UID/GID, capabilities, SELinux domain, namespace, and module unmount behavior; recommend least-privilege defaults if users configure them manually.

title: Evaluate non-WebView Mermaid rendering strategy
description: Research found no production-ready native Android Mermaid renderer. Evaluate a non-WebView strategy such as self-hosted Kroki with SVG/PNG caching, or optimized off-screen WebView bitmap caching as fallback. Include privacy/offline behavior, cache invalidation, and Compose image rendering approach.

title: Evaluate native Android math rendering
description: Research found AndroidMath as the most practical non-WebView LaTeX/KaTeX replacement for Android Compose. Evaluate replacing MathBlock's WebView-backed KaTeX renderer with AndroidMath/MTMathView wrapped in AndroidView, with fallback for unsupported syntax and size/performance review.

title: Store local release automation env
description: Add a local gitignored env file for debug install automation credentials so future device installs can reuse the Letta and gateway settings without manual re-entry.

title: Docs: document gateway close-code contract in mobile bot module
description:
Add a short reference table to android-compose/bot/README.md (or AGENTS.md) listing the gateway close codes and how WsBotClient handles each. Cross-link lettabot's docs/channel-adapter-contract.md so the two docs stay in sync.

This is the discoverability bead — without it, the next person to debug a 4003 close has to read both repos.
acceptance_criteria:
- Markdown table of close codes + mobile reconnect behavior committed
- Link to lettabot's channel-adapter-contract.md
- Mentioned in module-level README so it shows up in directory listings

title: Document LETTABOT_PARTIAL_JSON_ENABLED=0 kill switch in mobile runbook
description:
Add the server-side rollback escape hatch to the mobile runbook so on-call knows the exact env var to flip if partial-JSON tool_call snapshots cause a pathological mobile failure.

LETTABOT_PARTIAL_JSON_ENABLED=0 disables the partial-JSON pipeline entirely on the lettabot side. Every connection (including any opted-in mobile build) reverts to one tool_call frame per call with no status field — exactly the pre-uww.6 behavior.

Paperwork only. Do anytime.

## Context
Lowest priority, paperwork only. Order in the broader sequence: do anytime, doesn't block anything else.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Add a 'WS gateway tool_call rollback' section to the mobile runbook.
2. Document: the env var, where it goes (lettabot server env), what changes when set to 0, what changes when set to 1.
3. Cross-reference: /opt/stacks/lettabot/docs/configuration.md → 'WS Gateway Partial-JSON tool_call snapshots'.
4. Note that this is an emergency escape hatch, not a routine config — the default (1) is the supported state.

title: Defense-in-depth audit: identify code paths gated on status === 'running'
description:
**Framing matters here.** This is NOT a refactor. As long as letta-mobile-wgi8 holds (mobile keeps the ?progressive_tool_calls=1 opt-in), mobile will always receive 'running' frames and any existing 'if (status === running) openCard()' code keeps working correctly.

The risk this audit addresses: **if we ever lose the opt-in (item 1), code paths that require a 'running' frame go silent — tool cards never open because no running frame ever arrives, even though the terminal completed frame still posts.**

Audit goal: catalog any handler logic that depends on receiving a 'running' status frame, so if item 1 ever drifts we have an immediate inventory of what breaks. The fix for any finding is NOT to refactor away the dependency — it's to harden item 1 (URL opt-in is preserved).

If a finding shows code that *requires* running frames, that's actually fine; document it as a known dependency on the opt-in.

## Context
Risk 1 in the lettabot writeup. Frame this as 'inventory the dependencies on the opt-in' not 'remove the dependencies on the opt-in'. The opt-in is the contract; defense-in-depth means knowing what relies on it.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Grep for status === 'running' (or equivalent Kotlin/Swift checks) in tool_call handler paths.
2. For each match, note: file:line, what the code does, what would happen if no 'running' frame ever arrives.
3. Produce a short inventory in the issue notes (not a refactor PR).
4. If any finding shows a real bug (e.g. breaks even in progressive mode), spawn a follow-up issue.
5. Otherwise: close as 'verified — defense-in-depth dependency on letta-mobile-wgi8 opt-in'.

title: [client-mode][investigation] Provider parity — tool_card rendering across Anthropic vs OpenAI-backed Letta agents
description:
Mirror to lettabot-uww.9 (provider parity check on the server). Mobile-side: even if lettabot's gateway emits identical wire frames across providers, the app should be exercised against agents of both backings to confirm the UI behaves identically — no provider-specific quirks leak through to the user.

This is a manual-but-scripted verification, similar to lettabot-uww.5:
- Configure two test agents on Letta server: one Anthropic-backed, one OpenAI-backed.
- Send the same prompt that triggers a tool_call (e.g. 'Read /etc/hosts limit 5') against each from the Pixel 2XL with Client Mode on.
- Capture screen recordings of both runs.
- Diff: tool card text growth pacing, terminal state, any visual differences.
- If any divergence found, file an investigation bead.

Companion to lettabot-uww.9 — that one validates the server emits identical frames; this one validates the client renders identically given those frames.

Defer until lettabot-uww.9 lands and confirms the server-side wire shape is identical (otherwise this just chases server-side bugs).
acceptance_criteria: Two screen recordings captured (Anthropic + OpenAI); diff written up; any divergence triaged; finding linked back into lettabot-uww.9 if server-side.
notes: Cross-repo mirror of lettabot-uww.9 (server-side provider parity check). Defer until uww.9 lands.

title: obs(chat): per-event SSE timeline logging (server-emitted / client-received / render-committed)
description:
Diagnosing d2z6 took several iterations because we had no way to diff three timelines: when did the gateway emit a chunk, when did the client receive it, when did Compose actually commit a render with that chunk's content. Without that, structural-identity bugs and re-parse bugs and frame-batching bugs all present identically as 'flicker'.

Proposed: behind a debug flag, log each SSE event with (event_id, server_emit_ts_if_present, client_recv_ts_ms, applied_to_state_ts_ms, committed_to_compose_ts_ms). The committed-to-compose ts can be captured via SideEffect inside the bubble composable. Output to logcat under a Telemetry/StreamFrames tag so we can grep + diff.

Defer until we hit the next streaming bug; this is debt insurance.

Refs letta-mobile-d2z6, post-mortem from Letta Mobile Admin.

title: perf(chat): coalesce streaming chunk applications per animation frame
description:
After d2z6 fix landed (5548360), per-chunk markdown re-parse is no longer in play, but each gateway chunk still triggers an individual upsertClientModeAssistantMessage → state.copy → recompose pass. At 30-80 chunks/sec for fast model output that's 30-80 recomposes/sec, much higher than the 60Hz frame budget can usefully consume. Should coalesce upserts within a single animation frame (withFrameNanos or 16ms debounce) so we apply at most one composition pass per frame.

Caveat: the cursor glyph and visible word-boundary clamping mean the user already perceives smooth progress; this is a CPU/battery optimization, not a UX fix. Defer until d2z6 is verified clean and we have a real-world chunk-rate measurement to justify the complexity.

Refs letta-mobile-d2z6, post-mortem from Letta Mobile Admin.

title: [chat] Eliminate clientModeMessages legacy fallback once telemetry confirms it never fires
notes:
**Background**

`letta-mobile-5s1n` rewired Client Mode assistant streaming through the
timeline. Option A was chosen: keep `clientModeMessages` and
`handleClientModeStreamChunkLegacy` as a narrow belt-and-suspenders
fallback for chunks that arrive before the gateway echoes a
conversationId on a fresh-route send.

In normal operation this path fires zero times — the gateway echoes a
conversationId on chunk #1. The fallback exists so the UI keeps
rendering even if a future regression delays the echo.

**Telemetry hook (already shipped in 5s1n)**

`Telemetry.event("AdminChatVM", "clientMode.legacyChunkPath", ...)` is
emitted at WARN whenever the legacy path runs. Grep dev-build logs (and
Sentry breadcrumbs in the production telemetry tier once filtering is
in place) to know if the path ever fires.

**Done when**

1. At least 2 weeks of multi-user dev-build telemetry shows 0 hits on
   `clientMode.legacyChunkPath`, OR explicit confirmation from gateway
   team that conversationId is always echoed on chunk #1.
2. Replace legacy chunk handler with a chunk-buffer-then-replay strategy:
   - Buffer pre-conversationId chunks in a small list inside
     `sendMessageViaClientMode`.
   - On first conversationId echo, drain the buffer through
     `timelineRepository.upsertClientModeLocalAssistantChunk`.
   - Hard timeout (e.g. 10s without a conversationId) fails the send
     loudly with a snackbar instead of silently buffering forever.
3. Delete `clientModeMessages` field, `handleClientModeStreamChunkLegacy`,
   and the `migratedToTimeline` migration block.
4. Update tests that rely on `clientModeMessages` to use the timeline
   path exclusively.

**Out of scope**

Anything that changes server-side gateway behaviour. This ticket only
removes the client-side legacy fallback once telemetry says it's safe.

Refs: letta-mobile-5s1n

title: [upstream SDK] Expose otid parameter on Session.send() for client-supplied transaction IDs
description:
# Summary

The Letta Code SDK's `Session.send()` does not currently accept a client-supplied `otid` (operation transaction ID). Today's signature (from `node_modules/@letta-ai/letta-code-sdk/dist/types.d.ts`):

```ts
export type SendMessage = string | MessageContentItem[];
```

```ts
send(message: SendMessage): Promise<void>;
```

The SDK does surface server-echoed otids on inbound stream events (`SDKStreamEventMessagePayload.otid?`), but cannot forward a client-supplied otid when sending. This blocks any consumer that needs to do strict reconcile-by-otid through the SDK.

# Why we need it

`letta-mobile` (an Android client of `lettabot`'s WS gateway, which in turn uses `letta-code-sdk`) reconciles optimistic outbound user messages with server-echoed Confirmed messages by otid. When a user sends a message:

1. Mobile generates an otid, creates a `TimelineEvent.Local` with that otid.
2. Mobile sends `MessageCreate { otid }` to Letta directly (today's timeline path) — server echoes the otid back.
3. Reconcile swaps Local → Confirmed by otid match.

When mobile routes through Client Mode (lettabot WS gateway → letta-code-sdk → Letta server) instead of direct, step 2 breaks because the SDK has no parameter to thread the otid through. Server stores the message without otid. Reconcile can't match. Result: duplicate user bubbles.

The mobile-side workaround (`letta-mobile-c87t` v1) is a 10s+content fuzzy match for Client Mode messages only, but this is a known cosmetic regression and trivially false-positives if the user sends the same content twice within the window.

# Proposed API

Option A (additive, minimal):

```ts
interface SendOptions { otid?: string; }
send(message: SendMessage, options?: SendOptions): Promise<void>;
```

Option B (matches the existing `MessageBatch.otid` shape on Letta's REST surface — preferred for symmetry):

```ts
type SendMessage =
  | string
  | MessageContentItem[]
  | { content: string | MessageContentItem[]; otid?: string };
```

The SDK should pass the otid through to whatever `MessageCreate` payload it's already constructing on the way to `/v1/agents/{id}/messages` (or equivalent). Server already accepts and echoes `MessageCreate.otid`.

# Acceptance criteria

- `Session.send(...)` accepts a client-supplied otid.
- That otid arrives at the Letta server as `MessageCreate.otid`.
- Server-echoed Confirmed message carries the same otid back through the stream events.
- Existing callers (no otid) continue to work — otid is optional.

# Downstream consumers waiting on this

- `letta-mobile-26pf` (lettabot WS gateway): forward otid through WS `message` op into `Session.send()`. Blocked on this issue.
- `letta-mobile-8cm8` (mobile): replace fuzzy duplicate-match with strict otid reconcile. Blocked on this issue + 26pf.
- `letta-mobile-c87t` (mobile, in flight): ships fuzzy-match workaround in the meantime.

# Repo location

This issue lives in the letta-mobile beads tracker because that's where the consumer pain is, but the fix lives upstream at:
- npm package: `@letta-ai/letta-code-sdk` (currently 0.1.14)
- Source repo: TBD (file with the SDK maintainers when this issue is escalated)

Once an upstream issue/PR exists in the SDK repo, link it here.

title: [client-mode] Replace fuzzy duplicate-match with strict otid reconcile once SDK + gateway support otid
description:
# Summary

`letta-mobile-c87t` v1 ships with a fuzzy match heuristic for Client Mode user-message Locals (≤10s + identical content → swap with arriving Confirmed). This is a workaround for the absence of otid plumbing through the lettabot gateway and the Letta Code SDK send path.

Once `letta-mobile-26pf` (gateway otid forwarding) and the upstream Letta Code SDK otid-on-send change land, replace the fuzzy match with strict otid-based reconcile, identical to the standard timeline path.

# Acceptance criteria

- `AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode` passes the same otid that's on the `TimelineEvent.Local` to `ClientModeChatSender` → `BotChatRequest.otid` → gateway → SDK.
- Server-echoed user message arrives carrying that otid; standard timeline reconcile swaps Local → Confirmed.
- Fuzzy-match heuristic is removed.
- Test: send under Client Mode, observe single user bubble (no transient duplicate).

# Related

- `letta-mobile-c87t`: ships fuzzy match.
- `letta-mobile-26pf`: blocking; gateway-side otid forwarding.
- Upstream Letta Code SDK: blocking; otid parameter on `Session.send()`.
notes:
---

# 2026-04-25 — Upstream SDK ask filed

Filed `letta-mobile-8pyt` as the upstream SDK feature request. This issue (8cm8) is blocked on:
1. `letta-mobile-8pyt` (upstream SDK otid-on-send)
2. `letta-mobile-26pf` (lettabot gateway forwards otid)

Both must land before strict-otid reconcile can replace the fuzzy match.

title: [lettabot] Forward otid through WS message op into Letta Code SDK send path
description:
# Summary

The lettabot WS gateway (`src/api/ws-gateway.ts`) does not currently forward an `otid` (operation transaction ID) from the WS `message` payload through to the Letta Code SDK's `Session.send()`. As a result, mobile clients (letta-mobile) using Client Mode cannot reconcile their optimistic user bubbles against server-echoed messages by otid — leading to duplicate bubbles in chat. See `letta-mobile-c87t` for the user-visible symptom.

# Blocked on

Upstream Letta Code SDK must first expose an `otid` parameter on `Session.send()` (or equivalent). Today's signature: `send(message: SendMessage): Promise<void>`. There is no place to thread an otid through.

Once the SDK supports it, gateway-side change is small:

1. Extend the WS `message` payload schema to accept `otid?: string`.
2. Pass the otid through `AgentSessionManager.sendAndStream` to `session.send({ message, otid })` (or whatever shape the SDK chooses).
3. Optionally surface the otid in stream chunks so mobile can confirm round-trip.

# Acceptance criteria

- WS `message` with `{ otid: "test123" }` results in `Session.send` being called with the otid.
- Server-echoed message arrives at the mobile client with `otid="test123"`, allowing standard reconcile-by-otid swap.
- Existing WS callers (no otid) continue to work — otid is optional.

# Related

- `letta-mobile-c87t` (P1, mobile): would consume this once landed; v1 of c87t uses fuzzy match as a workaround.
- (upstream SDK issue, to be filed separately): expose `otid` on `Session.send()`.
notes:
---

# 2026-04-25 — Upstream SDK ask filed

Filed `letta-mobile-8pyt` as the upstream SDK feature request: expose `otid` parameter on `Session.send()`. This issue (26pf) is blocked on 8pyt landing.

Once 8pyt has a corresponding upstream PR/issue in the SDK repo (e.g. github.com/letta-ai/letta-code-sdk), link it from 8pyt and reference here.

title: [client-mode] Composer attachments are blocked under Client Mode
description:
# Summary

`AdminChatViewModel.sendMessage` rejects attachments when Client Mode is the active route:

    if (attachments.isNotEmpty()) {
        _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
            composerError = "Client Mode attachments are not supported yet",
        )
        return
    }

When Client Mode applied only to brand-new "fresh-route" conversations (the bug fixed in `letta-mobile-c87t`), this gap was less visible because most attachment-heavy chats were existing conversations and silently fell through to the Timeline path. After c87t lands, Client Mode applies universally, so any user who attaches an image will hit this error string for every chat with an attachment.

# Why it's blocked

`ClientModeChatSender.streamMessage` calls `internalBotClient.streamMessage(BotChatRequest(...))` which today only carries `message: String` — no attachments field. The bot/gateway protocol (`BotChatRequest`) needs to grow a multimodal payload, the gateway needs to forward it through to the SDK, and the SDK already supports multimodal messages.

# Acceptance criteria

- `BotChatRequest` carries optional `attachments: List<MessageContentPart.Image>`.
- `ClientModeChatSender` forwards attachments through `internalBotClient.streamMessage`.
- Gateway WS protocol (`message` op) accepts multimodal content array (mirroring the Letta SDK shape).
- Gateway forwards multimodal content to the SDK session.
- `AdminChatViewModel.sendMessageViaClientMode` accepts attachments instead of erroring.
- Test: send an image attachment under Client Mode; verify it round-trips and renders in chat.

# Related

- `letta-mobile-c87t` (P1): existing conversations silently bypass Client Mode — this issue is unblocked once that lands and Client Mode applies universally.

title: [ci] track flaky android perf-gate rerun on s0xx stack
description: Android Perf Gate on PR #12 (feat/s0xx-device-ready-bootstrap) failed once on unchanged branch head and passed on immediate rerun. The failing run reported startup.cold.p95_ms observed=1905.787 baseline=1512.749 ceiling=1815.299, while rerun 24863169969 completed successfully with no code changes. PR #13, which includes PR #12 plus additional commits, also passed perf-gate. Investigate emulator/runner variance, baseline stability, and whether the gate should gain automatic retry or better flake diagnostics so merge coordination is not blocked by transient noise.
notes:
Handoff to fix Android Perf Gate tests (2026-05-03):

Goal: make the GitHub Actions Android Perf Gate reliable enough to be required without one-off rerun failures. Current known failure mode: cold startup p95 can exceed the +20% ceiling once on unchanged branch head, then pass on immediate rerun. Treat repeat failures as real regressions; treat single-run spikes as runner/emulator variance until proven otherwise.

Start here:
- bd show letta-mobile-l5vw
- bd update letta-mobile-l5vw --claim

Relevant files:
- .github/workflows/android-perf.yml
- docs/performance/perf-gate.md
- docs/performance/playbook.md
- android-compose/perf/baselines.json
- android-compose/perf/check_baselines.py
- android-compose/perf/test_check_baselines.py
- android-compose/macrobenchmark/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/macrobenchmark/StartupBenchmark.kt

Likely fix directions:
1. Add bounded automatic retry for the gating baseline check or benchmark step when only startup.cold.p95_ms fails just over the ceiling, preserving hard failure after a second failing sample.
2. Improve diagnostics in perf/check_baselines.py output: observed, baseline, ceiling, percent delta, benchmark method, source JSON path, and whether metric is gating vs informational.
3. Consider artifacting a compact perf-summary.json / perf-summary.md alongside raw AndroidX benchmark outputs.
4. Keep warm startup informational-only unless variance evidence changes.
5. Do not simply loosen startup.cold.p95_ms beyond +20% without documented rerun data and deliberate rebaseline.

Suggested validation:
- cd android-compose
- python3 -m pytest perf/test_check_baselines.py (or python3 perf/test_check_baselines.py if pytest unavailable; inspect file for supported invocation)
- python3 perf/check_baselines.py <fixture-or-downloaded-artifact-dir>
- If workflow changed, use gh workflow run "Android Perf Gate" --ref <branch> and inspect uploaded android-perf-results-* artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Perf gate has deterministic retry/flake handling or improved diagnostics sufficient to distinguish transient emulator noise from real startup regressions.
- Docs in docs/performance/perf-gate.md and playbook.md reflect the new behavior.
- Parser/unit tests cover the changed gate behavior.
- No all-metric tolerance loosening as a shortcut.
close_reason: Implemented bounded cold-start retry for Android Perf Gate, added richer parser diagnostics and summary artifacts, documented retry behavior, and covered changes with parser tests.

title: [bug] ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest shows MockK-fragility intermittently — passes on rerun
description:
During the overnight PR #11/#12/#13 restack validation run on 2026-04-23, an initial local ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest surfaced 3 failures in ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest. An isolated rerun of just ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest passed, and a subsequent full testDebugUnitTest run also passed with no code changes in between.

This suggests the test has MockK-fragility — likely shared-state bleed between test methods, relaxed-mock interaction drift, or coroutine-timing nondeterminism that sometimes produces a false failure on cold JVM runs but not warm ones.

Impact:
- Not release-blocking — passes on rerun
- Creates occasional CI flake-retry churn
- Masks real regressions if ignored long enough

Scope of fix:
- Review ChannelHeartbeatSyncTest for MockK every{} blocks that assume call order
- Check for @BeforeEach / @BeforeTest reset hygiene
- Consider replacing MockK stubs with hand-rolled fakes for the specific flaky methods
- Add a test-retry annotation or JUnit 5 @RepeatedTest to surface intermittent failures earlier

Triggered during: letta-mobile-fxfz / letta-mobile-s0xx / letta-mobile-7973 restack validation (PR #11/#12/#13 stack). Test was not failing reproducibly — escalated to bead only because the pattern 'failures that pass on rerun' is a known fragility indicator.

title: Evaluate demoting internal gates (r6j3 family) to advisory once smoke is reliable
description: Once black-box smoke is trustworthy, evaluate whether the existing internal-observability release gates from the r6j3 family should remain blocking, become advisory, or become debug-only. The goal is to simplify release verification without losing coverage for bug classes that black-box smoke cannot catch.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Reviews the post-smoke role of verify-sync, verify-stream, and related internal gates.
2. Recommends which gates stay blocking, which become advisory/debug-only, and why.
3. Documents the coexistence or simplification plan after the smoke path is proven reliable.
notes: Dependency: requires release-workflow integration of the black-box smoke path first. This is the simplification/endgame pass, not part of initial smoke delivery.

title: Integrate chat smoke test into release verification workflow
description: Integrate the standalone black-box chat smoke test into the release process so it becomes a one-command or one-checklist-step verification path for installed APKs.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Adds a release-facing entry point such as `make verify-smoke` (or equivalent) that runs the standalone device smoke test.
2. Updates docs/RELEASE.md with when and how to run the smoke test during release preflight.
3. Explicitly decides whether the smoke test complements or replaces parts of verify-sync/verify-stream in the release flow.
4. Keeps installed-APK verification distinct from ConnectedAndroidTest/dev-test-APK workflows.
notes: Dependency: requires the standalone adb/uiautomator smoke test first. This bead is release-process integration, not the underlying smoke-test implementation.

title: Debug build LAUNCHER intent routes to LeakLauncherActivity, not MainActivity — breaks ADB-based launch automation
description:
On the debug build, generic Android LAUNCHER automation (`adb shell monkey -p com.letta.mobile -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1`) starts LeakCanary's `leakcanary.internal.activity.LeakLauncherActivity` instead of `com.letta.mobile/.MainActivity`. This breaks release/debug automation that assumes a launcher intent reaches the real app entrypoint.

Observed during `letta-mobile-ir9e` diagnosis on 2026-04-22 while reproducing the fresh-install bootstrap path on the 1.2.6 debug APK. Explicit `adb shell am start -n com.letta.mobile/.MainActivity` works as expected.
acceptance_criteria:
One of:
1. Adjust the debug manifest/build so LeakCanary no longer wins the default LAUNCHER intent over `MainActivity`.
2. Document in `docs/RELEASE.md` and any debug automation docs that ADB launch automation must use explicit `com.letta.mobile/.MainActivity` for debug builds.
notes:
Evidence:
- logcat showed `cmp=com.letta.mobile/leakcanary.internal.activity.LeakLauncherActivity` for the generic launcher path
- explicit MainActivity launch then started `com.letta.mobile/.MainActivity` successfully

title: App Performance Score static gaps: fully-drawn signal + Startup Profiles
description: Quarterly App Performance Score review (o7ob.4.3) found two untracked official rubric gaps: no FullyDrawnReporter/reportFullyDrawn hook at interactive-ready startup completion, and no Startup Profile / dex-layout optimization path despite Baseline Profiles already shipping. Existing beads 4ccv, ce2c, wy3c, j25x, and 84oa do not cover these static-score gaps. Done when the app wires a correct fully-drawn signal, chooses/implements a Startup Profile path (or documents a deliberate decision), and score-history.md can cite this bead as the 2026-Q2 tracked follow-up.

title: Extend perf playbook with Perfetto, BP refresh, Compose metrics, microbench sections
description:
Follow-up to letta-mobile-o7ob.4.2 (perf playbook v1).

The v1 playbook (docs/performance/playbook.md) intentionally omits four operator workflows because no reference docs for them exist on main:

- Perfetto trace capture (cold-start root-cause)
- Baseline Profile refresh process
- Compose metrics interpretation (recomposition / stability)
- Microbenchmark workflows

Done criteria: each of the four missing topics has either (a) a reference doc on main that the playbook can link to, or (b) an explicit scope decision documenting why it stays out of the playbook.

Blocked on the underlying reference docs landing. Some draft material exists in unlanded local work (sentry-taxonomy, conventions, coverage-matrix, stream-subscriber, grafana-* dashboards) but is not yet on main.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Cross-reference: letta-mobile-acba was filed in parallel by the PM agent (speculatively, before this bead existed) and has been closed as a duplicate pointing here. This is the canonical source of truth for the perf playbook extension work; the shipped playbook (docs/performance/playbook.md in PR #6) cites THIS bead (84oa) in its References section.

title: Extend perf playbook with Perfetto, BP refresh, Compose metrics, microbench sections
description:
## Context

During o7ob.4.2 (perf playbook doc), we shipped `docs/performance/playbook.md` scoped to the docs actually on main at the time:
- `perf-gate.md` — CI gate, canonical device, tolerances, flake investigation
- `jankstats-production-sampling.md` — sampling math + rate tuning
- `sentry-anr-crash-budget.md` — dashboard + alerts provisioning

The original bead scope (`o7ob.4.2` description) called out several additional sections that couldn't land because their source reference docs don't exist on main yet:

- Capturing a Perfetto trace
- Refreshing Baseline Profile (the process, not just the artifact)
- Interpreting Compose compiler metrics (how to enable, what to look for)
- Running microbench locally (the canonical invocation)

There are draft versions of Sentry taxonomy, observability conventions, Grafana dashboards, and coverage matrix in Emmanuel's local checkout that haven't been shipped to main. This bead is blocked until those drafts either ship or get deliberately discarded.

## Done criteria

1. Each of the four missing topics has either:
   a. A reference doc on main that the playbook can link to, OR
   b. A decision that the topic is out of scope for the playbook
2. Playbook updated with the new sections + decision tree branches (Perfetto capture as investigation tool; BP refresh as fix path for cold-start regressions; Compose metrics as fix path for scroll jank)
3. Related reference docs linked from playbook "References" section
4. No new follow-up beads filed from this work — if additional gaps emerge, they roll into this bead rather than multiplying

## Non-goals

- Not research — if a topic requires research before documentation, that's a separate bead
- Not a rewrite — the v1 playbook structure should survive extension

## Related

- o7ob.4.2 — parent (v1 playbook, scoped to shipped docs)
- Emmanuel's local draft backlog — the unlanded Sentry-taxonomy / conventions / grafana / coverage-matrix material
close_reason: Duplicate of letta-mobile-84oa — consolidating (84oa is cited in the shipped playbook)

Consolidating to 84oa. Closing this one.

This is the second time this session we've filed duplicate follow-ups (earlier: vtw9 closed as duplicate of 4ccv). Pattern worth noting: when PM and OpenCode are both working from fresh context on the same decision, both may independently reach "file a follow-up" at the same time. Mitigation: PM should confirm OpenCode hasn't already filed before creating, or wait for OpenCode's explicit file-report. Logged for future sessions.

title: Sentry ANR rate: upgrade from app-hang issue volume to Play-vitals-style rate metric
description:
## Context

During o7ob.3.5 (ANR/crash budget dashboard + alerts), we wired Sentry alerts for ANR/app-hang *issue volume* rather than *rate per session*. Google Play Vitals expresses ANR as `ANRs / sessions` over a 28-day rolling window among users with ≥5 sessions. Sentry's public alert API doesn't offer a first-class equivalent condition.

The v1 alerting in .3.5 uses app-hang issue volume over a rolling window, which catches "new version introduced ANR-prone code paths" (the regression-detection role). This is sufficient for the .3.5 scope but dimensionally different from what Play Vitals reports.

## Why this matters

If ops teams calibrate their mental model against Play Vitals dashboards, the Sentry alert may fire or not-fire at different absolute rates than Play's threshold. That's fine for catching *regressions* but creates a gap when responding to Play-side escalations or when tracking "are we under the 0.47% Play threshold" as a product health metric.

## Resolution options

### Option A — Client-emitted custom metric (recommended)

Emit `Sentry.metrics.distribution("anr_rate", anr_count / sessions_count, ...)` from the client at fixed intervals. Then alert on the metric via Sentry's metric-based alert conditions. Requires:
- Session-counting instrumentation (likely already exists via Sentry session tracking)
- ANR count aggregation per interval
- Distribution metric emission on a fixed cadence
- Alert rule authored against the custom metric

### Option B — Google Play Vitals API integration

Integrate Play Vitals API directly to pull the authoritative ANR rate. Alerts then target the source of truth. Requires:
- Google Play Developer API auth + service account
- Scheduled poller that pulls ANR rate and re-publishes to Sentry, Prometheus, or alert channel of choice
- Handling Play's 24-48h data-latency realistically

### Option C — Leave as-is

If app-hang issue volume proves sufficient in practice (months of ops experience), don't bother upgrading. Document that this bead was considered and deferred.

## Done criteria

Whichever option is picked:
1. Sentry alert fires on a condition dimensionally equivalent to (or explicitly documented as different from) Play Vitals ANR rate
2. Threshold provenance documented in `docs/observability/sentry-anr-crash-budget.md`
3. Dashboard includes ANR-rate tile (not just issue volume) if option A or B

## Non-goals

- Not a blocker for o7ob.3.5 (the v1 alert is useful as-is for regression detection)
- Not product scope — no in-app ANR metric exposure

## Related

- o7ob.3.5 — parent (ANR/crash budget dashboard + alerts v1)
- o7ob.3.1 — Sentry transaction taxonomy

title: observability: Coil image cache + decode-time telemetry
description:
Coil (image loader) is wired into `LettaApplication.newImageLoader`
with a 50 MB disk cache. We have zero signal on whether it's working
well: cache hit ratio, decode times, disk pressure. Image-loading bugs
have bitten before (letta-mobile-o9ce family) and would have been easy
to pin down with this.

## Proposed

Add a Coil EventListener that emits:

```
Telemetry.event("Coil", "request", "from" to cache|network, durationMs = n)
Telemetry.event("Coil", "decode.failed", ...)   // via Telemetry.error
Telemetry.event("Coil", "cache.pressure", "usedBytes", "maxBytes")
```

Also emit the cache-size high-water mark on `App.trimMemory`.

## Acceptance

- Coil EventListener registered.
- Panel: cache hit ratio + p95 decode ms.
- Alert: sustained hit ratio < 0.5 (= cache is ineffective, check
  request URLs for cache-bust query params).

Low priority; mostly defensive for when image UX regresses.

title: observability: Battery / doze / background-execution telemetry
description:
Background restrictions (Doze, App Standby Bucket, user-imposed
battery restrictions) can silently kill the ChatPushService subscriber
or starve it of CPU. Today we have no way to correlate 'user reports
missed messages at night' with 'phone was in deep doze'.

## Proposed

Subscribe to `PowerManager.ACTION_DEVICE_IDLE_MODE_CHANGED` +
`ACTION_POWER_SAVE_MODE_CHANGED` and emit:

```
Telemetry.event("Power", "idleMode.entered" / "idleMode.exited")
Telemetry.event("Power", "powerSave.entered" / "powerSave.exited")
Telemetry.event("Power", "standbyBucket",
    "bucket" to UsageStatsManager.getAppStandbyBucket())
```

Also emit `ChatPushService.suspended` / `.resumed` if those transitions
kill our foreground. Requires correlating with ChatPushService lifecycle
events we already emit.

## Acceptance

- New class PowerTelemetry registered in ApplicationModule.
- Panel: time-in-doze per day; correlation with subscriber wedges.
- Alert: ChatPushService.destroyed while in doze mode (= OS killed us).

Low priority; meaningful only once we have any background work that
users would miss in doze. Today we primarily rely on foreground
ChatPushService so the blast radius is small.

title: observability: StrictMode violation counter
description:
Currently StrictMode violations produce one giant `Telemetry/StrictMode`
log per violation — rich but uncountable. Grafana can't say 'closed-
resource leaks per minute' because each violation is a unique string.

## Proposed

Add a tiny classifier in the StrictMode listener that emits a compact
point event alongside the full detail line:

```
Telemetry.event(
    "StrictMode", "violation",
    "policyType" to policyType,        // vm | thread
    "violationType" to simpleName,     // LeakedClosableViolation, DiskReadViolation, ...
    "sourceClass" to ...,
)
```

Keep the existing verbose log (it's useful for diagnosis) — this is
additive and purely for counting.

## Acceptance

- StrictMode listener emits the compact event.
- Dashboard panel: violations/min broken down by `violationType`.
- Alert: sustained > 1/min of `LeakedClosableViolation` in debug builds
  (= someone regressed the file-handle hygiene we fixed earlier).
- Entirely gated on `BuildConfig.DEBUG` — no release-build overhead.

title: Migrate downstream docs/CI/hooks to drop --no-daemon now that daemon=true is permanent
description:
## Context

`letta-mobile-dbs1` spike validated `org.gradle.daemon=true` (60/60 clean,
0 IC corruption signals, ~13x warm-run speedup) and made the flip permanent
in `android-compose/gradle.properties`.

Several downstream surfaces still hardcode `--no-daemon` in example commands
or assert "the repo defaults to daemon=false" in prose. They should be
updated for consistency, but they are not strictly broken — `--no-daemon` on
the command line just overrides the property per-invocation.

## Surfaces to review

- `README.md` — Quick start and Recommended checks sections use `./gradlew --no-daemon ...`
- `android-compose/README.md` — multiple sections claim "The repo now defaults to org.gradle.daemon=false"; release flow and verification flow use `--no-daemon`
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — verify presence/wording
- `.github/workflows/android.yml` — CI may still pass `--no-daemon`; decide whether CI should keep it (CI runners are ephemeral, daemon win is zero) or align with local
- `.githooks/pre-push` — uses `--no-daemon` per android-compose/README

## Acceptance

- Prose claims about default daemon policy match reality (daemon=true)
- Example commands consistent with the policy chosen for each surface
- Document explicit reasoning for any surface that intentionally keeps `--no-daemon` (e.g., CI ephemerality, hook isolation)
close_reason: Stripped --no-daemon from local docs/hooks (README.md, android-compose/README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .githooks/pre-push) so they no longer override the daemon=true default established by dbs1. Rewrote the daemon-policy prose paragraph in android-compose/README.md to reflect daemon=true (with dbs1 evidence) while preserving parallel=false/caching=false rationale. CI workflow (.github/workflows/android.yml) intentionally retains its 5 --no-daemon invocations because runners are ephemeral. Commit f123b01, pushed to origin/main.

title: Paparazzi not functional in current repo environment — investigate or commit to Roborazzi
description:
# Paparazzi not functional in current repo environment

## Context

Discovered during letta-mobile-m772.6 (snapshot-test RunBlock layouts). Attempted to add Paparazzi snapshot tests for RunBlock and found Paparazzi crashes even on the pre-existing `:designsystem` Paparazzi tests in this environment.

### Failure signature

- **Paparazzi 1.3.5**: `NoSuchElementException` thrown in `Renderer.configureBuildProperties` while reflecting `android.os.Build` nested classes against `android.os._Original_Build`.
- **Paparazzi 2.0.0-alpha02**: repo-level incompatibility with current AGP line.
- **Paparazzi 2.0.0-alpha01**: same `configureBuildProperties` crash as 1.3.5.

### Environment specifics

- AGP 8.9 (current `:app` line)
- JDK 17 (Debian OpenJDK 17.0.18)
- Android SDK installed: **android-35 and android-36 only** — notably no android-34, which Paparazzi 1.3.x historically defaults to.
- Kotlin 2.x (via `kotlin.plugin.compose` plugin).

Running under a dev host with limited SDK coverage. **CI may or may not reproduce this** — needs verification.

## Related work

m772.6 pivoted to an alternative snapshot-testing path (decided by OpenCode based on environment constraints) — check that bead for what actually got implemented. This bead is specifically about the Paparazzi tooling question.

## Scope

This is an **investigation + decision** bead, not a pure implementation bead.

### Investigation steps

1. **Reproduce on CI**: run `./gradlew :designsystem:verifyPaparazziDebug` on GitHub Actions (or equivalent CI). If CI passes and only the dev host fails, the issue is **local SDK-layout** and the fix is installing android-34 / the specific SDK Paparazzi wants.
2. **If CI also fails**: Paparazzi 1.3.x is unusable with AGP 8.9 + Kotlin 2.x. Document the specific incompatibility (what's pinning us to AGP 8.9 vs. upgrading to unblock Paparazzi 2.x stable).
3. **Evaluate Roborazzi as the replacement** — runs under Robolectric (already working in `:app` per letta-mobile-m772.7), uses the real Android runtime (not LayoutLib), supports JDK 17, and has stable 2.x releases. Add a minimal proof-of-concept test and confirm it works in this environment.
4. **Decide**: (a) keep Paparazzi after an SDK install / AGP bump, or (b) migrate `:designsystem` Paparazzi tests to Roborazzi and retire Paparazzi from the build.

### Decision matrix

| Outcome | Action |
|---|---|
| CI works, local fails | Document SDK install prerequisites in README, close. |
| CI also fails, AGP bump cheap | Bump AGP + Paparazzi to latest stable together. |
| CI also fails, AGP bump expensive | Migrate to Roborazzi, retire Paparazzi. |

### Acceptance

- Investigation outcome documented in bead notes.
- Chosen path implemented (one of: SDK install guide, AGP+Paparazzi bump, Roborazzi migration).
- If Roborazzi path chosen: `:designsystem` snapshot tests migrated, Paparazzi plugin removed from `:app` and `:designsystem`.
- README / TESTING.md updated with the final tooling choice.

## Why P3

Not user-facing. Visual-regression coverage is a safety net, not a product feature. m772.6 is filing layout-assertion tests (or similar) as the immediate coverage — this bead is about the longer-term tooling question. Revisit when we either hit a visual regression in prod that layout-assertions didn't catch, or when someone attempts to add new snapshot tests and hits the same wall OpenCode did.

## File hints

- android-compose/designsystem/build.gradle.kts (Paparazzi plugin)
- android-compose/app/build.gradle.kts (Paparazzi plugin at :app)
- android-compose/designsystem/src/test/ (existing Paparazzi tests)
- android-compose/gradle/libs.versions.toml (if version catalog used)

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
CI evidence appended (2026-04-19, via m772.6 pivot): `.github/workflows/android.yml` only runs `:core:compileDebugKotlin`, `:app:compileDebugKotlin`, `:core:testDebugUnitTest`, `:app:testDebugUnitTest` under Java 17. **No Paparazzi tasks in CI.**

Decision matrix branch 'CI works, local fails → SDK install prerequisite' is eliminated — the Paparazzi `configureBuildProperties` crash is real infra breakage, not a dev-host SDK-layout issue.

Attempted versions and failure signatures documented in the bead description. m772.6 pivoted to Roborazzi (successfully applied at :app). :designsystem still has Paparazzi plugin applied but unverified; if this bead closes by committing to Roborazzi, :designsystem migration is a follow-up.

Note: duplicate bead letta-mobile-ftyg was filed by OpenCode at the same time as this one; ftyg should be closed as duplicate with a pointer here.

title: Configure Kotest concurrent test execution for pure-function tests
description:
# Configure Kotest concurrent test execution

## Context

Discovered during test-suite performance review. Kotest supports running tests concurrently within a spec and across specs, but currently no concurrency config is set, so tests run sequentially.

Pure-function tests (`MessageGroupingTest`, `RunStepDotIconTest`, `:core` mapper tests, etc.) have zero shared mutable state and are embarrassingly parallelizable. Running them concurrently scales with CPU cores — measured 2-4× speedup typical on modern dev hardware.

## Scope

Add a Kotest global configuration enabling concurrent execution for the `:app`, `:core`, and `:bot` modules.

### Implementation

Create `android-compose/app/src/test/kotlin/com/letta/mobile/KotestConfig.kt` (and equivalents for :core, :bot):

```kotlin
package com.letta.mobile

import io.kotest.core.config.AbstractProjectConfig
import io.kotest.core.extensions.Extension
import io.kotest.core.concurrency.ConcurrencyMode

class KotestConfig : AbstractProjectConfig() {
    // Run each test in a spec concurrently, and each spec concurrently.
    // Safe because pure-function tests have no shared state.
    override val parallelism: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors()
    override val concurrentTests: Int = parallelism
    override val concurrentSpecs: Int = parallelism
}
```

### Opt-out for stateful tests

Tests that **do** have shared state (Robolectric tests, MockK tests with static mocks, tests sharing a `TestDispatcher`) must opt out:

```kotlin
class MyStatefulTest : WordSpec({
    // …
}) {
    override fun concurrency() = ProjectConfiguration.Sequential
}
```

Audit existing tests for:
- MockK `mockkStatic` usage (not thread-safe)
- Shared `TestDispatcher` or `TestScope`
- Singleton / DI container manipulation
- File system writes to shared paths
- Network mock state (MockEngine shared across tests)

### Validation

1. Run `:app:testDebugUnitTest` 5 times before the change, measure median wall-clock.
2. Apply the config.
3. Run 5 times again, measure. Expect 2-4× speedup if no failures.
4. If any test fails intermittently, tag it for sequential execution and document the reason.

## Acceptance

- `KotestConfig.kt` added to `:app`, `:core`, `:bot` test sources.
- Stateful tests opted out explicitly.
- Before/after wall-clock measurements documented in the bead notes.
- No intermittent test failures over 20 consecutive runs after the change.

## Non-goals

- Does not parallelize JUnit4 / JUnit5 / Robolectric tests — those are controlled by Gradle's Test task `maxParallelForks` setting (separate bead if needed).
- Does not change test code structure or test-data isolation.

## Risk notes

**Low risk on pure tests, medium if shared state lurks in MockK tests.** The audit step exists specifically to catch hidden state. If validation runs show flakes, disable concurrency for affected tests individually.

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/test/kotlin/com/letta/mobile/KotestConfig.kt (new)
- android-compose/core/src/test/kotlin/com/letta/mobile/KotestConfig.kt (new)
- android-compose/bot/src/test/kotlin/com/letta/mobile/KotestConfig.kt (new)

title: Split Robolectric tests into dedicated testRobolectric source set

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
Removed stale 'blocks letta-mobile-0zml' dependency on 2026-04-19. The original bead filing incorrectly assumed tier segregation (0zml) needed the testRobolectric source set split to already exist. In practice 0zml shipped cleanly with Robolectric tests still living in src/test/, just tagged @integration. The testRobolectric source set is a further optimization, not a prerequisite.

This bead (gixf) is still valid as a P3 follow-up but should be re-evaluated with updated measurement: now that 0zml's @integration tier filters Robolectric tests out of the fast-path testUnit task, the overhead of isIncludeAndroidResources=true on pure-JVM tests is only paid during integration runs, which may make this split unnecessary.

Recommend re-measuring per the bead's own 'acceptance: measure wall-clock delta' step before committing to the refactor.

title: Short-circuit buildRunStepMarkers to avoid O(N²) allocations during settle
description:
# Short-circuit buildRunStepMarkers

## Context

Discovered during post-m772.7 deep review. Each per-row `onGloballyPositioned` fire invokes `buildRunStepMarkers(...)` which:

1. Allocates `ArrayList<RunStepMarker>(groupedMessages.size)`
2. Iterates the full message list
3. Returns null on first missing center

For an N-step run during initial measurement, rows report positions one by one. Each fire allocates + walks the list until hitting the first unmeasured row. Total work across settle: **O(N²)** allocations + list walks.

Small N makes this invisible today, but a user running an agent with 20+ steps in a single run (common for research/coding workflows) turns this into measurable jank on low-end devices.

## Fix

Short-circuit at the call site before allocating:

```kotlin
.onGloballyPositioned { coordinates ->
    if (coordinates.size.height == 0) return@onGloballyPositioned
    pendingCenters[message.id] = coordinates.positionInParent().y + coordinates.size.height / 2f
    // Guard: don't try to build markers until every message has reported.
    if (pendingCenters.size != groupedMessages.size) return@onGloballyPositioned
    val committedMarkers = buildRunStepMarkers(groupedMessages, pendingCenters, colorScheme) ?: return@onGloballyPositioned
    if (committedMarkers != stepMarkers) {
        stepMarkers = committedMarkers
    }
}
```

Reduces settle-phase work from O(N²) → O(N).

## Acceptance

- `buildRunStepMarkers` is only invoked when `pendingCenters.size == groupedMessages.size`
- Existing `RunBlockStreamingTest` still passes (behavior unchanged)
- Optional: add a benchmark or counter-based assertion showing marker-build invocations drop from N to 1 for an N-step initial render

## Dependencies

Should land after or alongside letta-mobile-m772.9 (pendingCenters fix) — both touch the same `onGloballyPositioned` block, combining into a single PR avoids conflicts.

## File hints

- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatMessageComponents.kt (or RunBlock.kt post-split)
notes: Implemented in commit 5ae7e08 on branch recovery/m772-from-stash (PR https://github.com/oculairmedia/letta-mobile/pull/1)

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: REOPENED 2026-04-19 — bd-vs-git drift discovered during letta-mobile-7yka scope check. Bead was closed earlier today but NO commit implementing it exists on any git ref (verified via git log --all --grep). Work was parked in local stash@{0} 'wip-pre-existing-m772' and never committed/pushed. Recovery plan: apply stash, split into proper commits matching original bead boundaries, re-verify and re-close against actual merged code this time. Tracking recovery via letta-mobile-m772 epic.

title: Split RunBlock composable into its own file
description:
# Split RunBlock composable into its own file

## Context

Discovered during deep review after m772.7. ChatMessageComponents.kt has grown ~270 LOC from the m772 epic work and now mixes three concerns:

1. Per-message bubble + tool cards + approval UI (original purpose)
2. RunBlock container + measurement/committing logic
3. Gutter rail drawing + RunDot rendering

Adding m772.4's collapse state + chevron animations on top will push the file past the comfort threshold and make reviews noisier than they need to be.

## Scope

Split `ChatMessageComponents.kt` into two files:

1. **Keep** in `ChatMessageComponents.kt`:
   - `ChatMessageItem`
   - Per-message bubble composables
   - Tool card rendering
   - Approval UI
   - Thinking section wiring

2. **Move** to new `RunBlock.kt` (same package):
   - `RunBlock` composable + KDoc
   - `RunStepMarker` data class
   - `buildRunStepMarkers` helper
   - `runGutter` modifier extension
   - `RunDot` composable

Keep `RunStepClassification.kt` as-is (already split cleanly in m772.5).

## Acceptance

- New file `android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/RunBlock.kt` exists
- `ChatMessageComponents.kt` LOC drops by ~270
- No public/internal API changes — pure file move + import cleanup
- `:app:compileDebugKotlin` and all existing tests pass
- Detekt / lint unchanged

## Non-goals

- No behavioral changes
- No new composables
- No visibility modifier changes (everything stays `internal` / `private` as-is)

## Why P3

Pure hygiene. Unblocks m772.4's diff from being a 500+ line single-file change, but isn't itself user-facing.
close_reason: Verified on origin/main after PR #92: RunBlock.kt already owns the RunBlock composable in :feature-chat, with gutter/dot helpers removed from ChatMessageComponents.kt by the prior feature-chat extraction.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: REOPENED 2026-04-19 — bd-vs-git drift discovered during letta-mobile-7yka scope check. Bead was closed earlier today but NO commit implementing it exists on any git ref (verified via git log --all --grep). Work was parked in local stash@{0} 'wip-pre-existing-m772' and never committed/pushed. Recovery plan: apply stash, split into proper commits matching original bead boundaries, re-verify and re-close against actual merged code this time. Tracking recovery via letta-mobile-m772 epic.

title: Sentry traces.sample-rate manifest value read as String, falls through to -1 default
description:
Observed on device during o7ob.1.3/1.4 smoke test:

    W/Bundle: Key io.sentry.traces.sample-rate expected Integer but value was a java.lang.String.  The default value -1 was returned.
    W/Bundle: Key io.sentry.traces.sample-rate expected Float but value was a java.lang.String.  The default value -1.0 was returned.

Sentry's ManifestMetadataReader.readDouble tries Integer then Float, both cast-fail because the manifest placeholder resolves to a String. Current effect: traces sample rate defaults to -1 (disabled), so any transaction tagging we ship in Lane C1 will NOT actually sample. Needs the manifest/placeholder to be set as a numeric resource (e.g. <meta-data android:resource="@integer/sentry_traces_sample_rate"/>) or injected directly as Float via AndroidManifest.xml numeric literal.

Blocks effective prod visibility for the entire Lane C — discover before C1 is landed so we don't ship a half-configured Sentry.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text:
Fixed on main.

Root cause confirmed from sentry-android-core-7.19.1 ManifestMetadataReader bytecode: readDouble calls Bundle.getInt -> getFloat only (no getString fallback), so an @string/... reference always produces the -1 default. Android can't express a float metadata value natively, so any manifest-based config path was doomed.

Solution: disable auto-init (`io.sentry.auto-init=false`) and init programmatically in a new `SentryInitializer` registered via androidx.startup. Initializer reads DSN/env/traces.sample-rate from generated string resources (Gradle resValue populates from local.properties) and calls `SentryAndroid.init` with the parsed Double sample rate.

Verified: :app:compileDebugKotlin + :app:compileReleaseKotlin green; 272/273 unit tests pass (1 pre-existing flaky — o7ob.6). Unblocks Lane C1 transaction sampling.

Add a lightweight HttpLoggingInterceptor budget mode (debug only) that logs request durations above a threshold. Capture a Perfetto trace of a chat send to confirm no main-thread IO.

title: Perfetto capture helper: scripts/perf/capture-trace.sh for reproducible 10s traces
description: Add scripts/perf/capture-trace.sh that uses `adb shell perfetto` (or record_android_trace.py) to capture a 10-second trace with atrace/scheduler/am/wm/view/input categories. Output lands in scripts/perf/traces/ with git-ignored contents. Document how to open in ui.perfetto.dev and interpret the tracing sections from A5.
close_reason: b58f072: scripts/perf/capture-trace.sh + README + traces/.gitignore. Records 10s Perfetto traces with sched/freq/idle/am/wm/view/input/binder_driver categories, cross-referenced with the androidx.tracing sections from o7ob.1.5.

title: reconcileAfterSend appends out-of-order missed messages at end via nextLocalPosition() instead of true ordering
description:
## Symptom
`TimelineSyncLoop.reconcileAfterSend` (line 296):
```kotlin
serverMessages.forEach { msg ->
    val msgOtid = msg.otid ?: return@forEach
    if (_state.value.findByOtid(msgOtid) == null) {
        val pos = _state.value.nextLocalPosition()           // <-- always end-of-timeline
        val confirmed = msg.toTimelineEvent(position = pos) ?: return@forEach
        _state.value = _state.value.append(confirmed)
        appendedMissing++
    }
}
```

Any "missed" server message gets the next end-of-timeline position. So if we missed an assistant reply from 3 turns ago (e.g. SSE dropped), it appears at the END of the chat instead of being interleaved at its actual chronological position.

## Why this matters
- Multi-tab / multi-device scenarios: app reopens after a failed sync, missing messages appear out-of-order.
- After a reconcile error retry: missed messages stack at the bottom in arrival order, not date order.
- The `Confirmed.date` (Instant) is available — we just don't use it.

## Fix
Use `Timeline.insertOrdered(confirmed)` instead of `append`, with a position derived from `confirmed.date.toEpochMilli().toDouble()` (or any monotonic-by-date scheme). `insertOrdered` already handles the slotting logic.

```kotlin
val pos = msg.date?.let { Instant.parse(it) }?.toEpochMilli()?.toDouble()
    ?: _state.value.nextLocalPosition()
val confirmed = msg.toTimelineEvent(position = pos) ?: return@forEach
_state.value = _state.value.insertOrdered(confirmed)
```

Caveat: this changes the position scheme from "monotonic counter" to "epoch millis". Other code that assumes positions are small integers needs auditing. Likely safe — positions are opaque outside Timeline.

## Acceptance
- Synthetic test: insert events out-of-order with varying dates → final timeline ordered by date.
- Existing reconcile tests still pass.
- No visual jumps in chat after reconcile (positions for ALREADY-displayed messages don't change).

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:296` (reconcileAfterSend missed-message loop)
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/Timeline.kt:152` (insertOrdered — verify it handles the new position range)

## Dependencies
Touches Timeline.kt — coordinate with `letta-mobile-4jmg` (init invariant relaxation) which also edits this file.

title: TimelineSyncLoop.retry has TOCTOU race on existing.attachments read outside writeMutex
description:
## Symptom
`TimelineSyncLoop.retry` (line 161):
```kotlin
suspend fun retry(otid: String) {
    val existing = _state.value.findByOtid(otid)            // <-- read OUTSIDE lock
    if (existing !is TimelineEvent.Local || existing.deliveryState != DeliveryState.FAILED) return
    writeMutex.withLock {
        _state.value = _state.value.copy(events = _state.value.events.map {
            if (it.otid == otid && it is TimelineEvent.Local) {
                it.copy(deliveryState = DeliveryState.SENDING)
            } else it
        })
        sendQueue.send(PendingSend(otid, existing.content, existing.attachments))   // <-- uses STALE snapshot
    }
}
```

If between the unlocked read and the locked re-enqueue another writer (e.g. a concurrent `streamAndReconcile` for the same otid? unlikely but possible during rapid retry+send) modifies the Local's content or attachments, we re-send the OLD payload.

More realistically: if the user **edits** the failed message before retrying (a future feature already partially scoped), this races with the edit.

## Fix
Move the read inside the lock:
```kotlin
suspend fun retry(otid: String) = writeMutex.withLock {
    val existing = _state.value.findByOtid(otid)
    if (existing !is TimelineEvent.Local || existing.deliveryState != DeliveryState.FAILED) return@withLock
    _state.value = _state.value.copy(events = _state.value.events.map { ... })
    sendQueue.send(PendingSend(otid, existing.content, existing.attachments))
}
```

Note: `sendQueue.send` is `Channel.UNLIMITED` so it never suspends inside the lock — safe.

## Acceptance
- Existing tests pass.
- New test: spawn two coroutines that simultaneously `retry(otid)` and modify the Local's content via a hypothetical state mutation → no race, retried payload matches the latest content.

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:161`
notes: Moved retry() read of existing.attachments inside writeMutex.withLock to eliminate TOCTOU race. Used return@withLock for early exit. Added unit test.

title: AdminChatViewModel god-object (1056 LOC, 25 fns) — extract ComposerState, BugReportState, ProjectBriefState
description:
## Symptom
`AdminChatViewModel` is 1056 LOC with 25+ public/private functions handling:

- chat font scale + background settings
- project agents list loading
- recent bug reports list + structured submission
- project brief loading + section updates
- slash command handling
- conversation lifecycle (create, set active, resolve)
- message send + retry
- attachment add/remove/cap enforcement
- approval submission
- timeline observer wiring + UI mapping
- input text state

This is a god-object. Original ticket `letta-mobile-78qg` already noted: "1326 loc — big, consider a dedicated `ComposerState`".

## Concrete pain it causes
- `AdminChatViewModelTest` is brittle: any unrelated state change forces a rewrite.
- Adding telemetry to one path (e.g. `addAttachment` for `letta-mobile-2c70`) requires re-reading 1000 lines of unrelated context.
- Constructor injection has 10+ dependencies, making tests heavy.
- Memory: hot UI state like `_uiState`, `_inputText` are flow-merged with cold data like `recentBugReports` → unnecessary recomposition.

## Refactor target
Extract three sibling ViewModels (or domain `StateHolder`s + a thin VM facade):

1. **`ChatComposerState`** — input text, pending attachments, send/retry, slash commands, telemetry for attachments.
2. **`ProjectBugReportsState`** — recentBugReports, structured submit, draft state.
3. **`ProjectBriefState`** — load/save brief sections.

Keep `AdminChatViewModel` as a coordinator that owns the conversation lifecycle and the timeline observer. Or — preferred — promote each to its own `@HiltViewModel` keyed off the same conversation/agent ID and let the screen compose them.

## Acceptance
- `AdminChatViewModel` ≤ 400 LOC.
- `AdminChatViewModelTest` no longer needs to mock bug-report APIs to test send.
- No behavior change visible to the user (purely a refactor; cover with snapshot UI tests of the chat screen first).

## Risk
Touches the file every other in-flight ticket also touches. **Do NOT pick this up until the image-attach beads (`p35t/2c70/b26n/jn4b/wz1q/j44j`) are merged.** Add `--blocked-by` once those are open.

## Files
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt`
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/ChatScreen.kt`
- `app/src/test/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/*ViewModelTest.kt`

title: TOOL_CALL/TOOL_RETURN fetched but mapped to null — wasted bandwidth and missing tool activity in chat
description:
## Symptom
`TimelineSyncLoop.DEFAULT_INCLUDE_TYPES` (line 327) requests:
```kotlin
"assistant_message", "reasoning_message",
"tool_call_message", "tool_return_message"
```

But in `AdminChatViewModel.toUiMessageOrNull` (line 814-822):
```kotlin
TimelineMessageType.TOOL_CALL,
TimelineMessageType.TOOL_RETURN,
TimelineMessageType.OTHER -> return null
```

So we **fetch** tool call / return messages from the server, parse them, append them as `Confirmed` events to the timeline, then drop them in the UI mapper. Wasted bandwidth, wasted parse time, and (worse) the user has zero visibility into agent tool activity.

## Impact
- Bandwidth: tool messages can be very large (function args + return JSON). On every send we ship and parse them for nothing.
- UX: Letta agents heavily use tools. The user sees a chat that goes silent for 10s and then a single assistant reply, with no indication the agent ran 5 tools in between.
- Reasoning bubbles ARE rendered (`REASONING -> "assistant"`) but tool activity isn't — inconsistent.

## Two valid fixes (pick one)
1. **Stop fetching them**: drop `tool_call_message` / `tool_return_message` from `DEFAULT_INCLUDE_TYPES`. Cheap, hides agent activity entirely.
2. **Render them**: add a "tool activity" collapsible chip to the chat (similar to reasoning bubble), showing function name + truncated args. Better UX, more work.

Recommended: (2) gated behind a settings toggle "Show agent tool activity" defaulting to ON for admin chat (where this VM lives — it's literally for power users), OFF for the regular user-facing chat.

## Acceptance
- If (1): network trace shows shorter responses; chat behavior unchanged.
- If (2): every tool call shown as a collapsible bubble between user → assistant; tool returns shown attached to the call; reasoning bubble unchanged.

## Files
- `core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/timeline/TimelineSyncLoop.kt:327`
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/AdminChatViewModel.kt:814`
- `app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/chat/...` (new ToolActivityBubble composable if going with option 2)

title: Admin chat: render fenced svg blocks inline via Coil SVG decoder
description:
Agents occasionally return hand-crafted SVG inside fenced svg code blocks (diagrams, icons, single-frame plots). Currently they fall through to syntax-highlighted source like mermaid used to.

## Scope

1. Add a new branch in MarkdownText.components.codeFence alongside the mermaid one: if language == "svg" and body starts with <svg, route to a new SvgFence composable.
2. SvgFence uses Coil 3 + io.coil-kt.coil3:coil-svg:3.4.0 to decode the SVG from an in-memory ByteArray. Register the decoder on the singleton loader in LettaApplication.newImageLoader.
3. Bound to bubble width, tap to zoom (reuse shared zoom viewer from letta-mobile-78qg).
4. Long-press copy raw SVG source.
5. Malformed SVG ⇒ fall through to the existing highlighted code fence.

## Non-goals

- Interactive SVG (animations / scripts disabled for security).

## Context

- Renderer: designsystem/.../MarkdownText.kt
- Pattern reference: mermaid code-fence branch added in letta-mobile-ka6v.

## Acceptance

- Agent-rendered svg fenced blocks show as thumbnails, not raw angle-bracket soup.
- Malformed SVG degrades to source, no crash.
- Coil SVG decoder registered once in the singleton loader.

title: Admin chat: improve GFM table rendering (horizontal-scroll-safe, striped, copy)
description:
multiplatform-markdown-renderer renders GFM tables as basic rows — but wide tables (>viewport width) collapse because we don't wrap them in a horizontal scroller, and the library's default cell styling is spartan (no stripes, no borders).

## Scope

1. Override `markdownComponents.table` in `MarkdownText` to wrap the library's default table in a `horizontalScroll` Modifier — but **outside** any existing scroller to avoid the letta-mobile-o2v7 infinite-width crash.
2. Apply striped row backgrounds via surfaceVariant / surface alternation; add cell dividers using `Modifier.drawBehind { … }`.
3. Long-press on any cell → copy row as TSV; long-press on header → copy whole table as markdown.
4. Respect `LocalChatFontScale` (tables should shrink/grow with pinch-zoom).

## Non-goals

- Editable tables.
- Spanning cells / colspan (GFM doesn't support them anyway).
- CSV / JSON export beyond the long-press TSV shortcut.

## Context

- Renderer: `designsystem/.../MarkdownText.kt`
- Nested-scroll trap reference: letta-mobile-o2v7 and the inline NOTE in `CodeFenceWithHeader`.

## Acceptance

- Wide tables scroll horizontally inside the bubble without clipping.
- No 'measured with an infinity maximum width constraints' crashes.
- Striped rows + readable dividers in both light/dark.
