title: Add shared element transitions between screens
description: RikkaHub uses hero/shared element animations for screen transitions — tapping a conversation smoothly morphs into the chat screen. Compose supports SharedTransitionLayout for this. Also: Scaffold transitions between screens should use Material motion patterns (container transform for drill-in, shared axis for peer navigation). This is lower priority cosmetic polish but makes a big difference in perceived quality. Requires navigation-compose 2.8+ with AnimatedNavHost.

title: Add chat background customization and conversation drawer
description: RikkaHub has Background.kt for customizable chat backgrounds (color, image, gradient) and ChatDrawer.kt for a slide-out conversation history panel. Letta Mobile has a plain surface background and separate ConversationsScreen. Add: optional chat background picker (solid colors + gradients), and a ChatDrawer for quick conversation switching without leaving the chat screen. Reference: rikkahub ui/pages/chat/Background.kt and ChatDrawer.kt.

title: Add hero/shared element animations for screen transitions
description: RikkaHub uses heroAnimation() modifier on UIAvatar components to create shared element transitions when navigating from list to detail screens (e.g., assistant avatar morphing from list card to detail header). Letta Mobile has no shared element transitions. Add hero animations for: agent avatar (list→chat/detail), tool icon (list→detail), block icon (list→detail). Uses Compose shared element transition API available in Material3.

title: Add custom icon library (HugeIcons or Lucide) to replace default Material icons
description: RikkaHub uses HugeIcons (stroke variants) throughout — Add01, Search01, Delete01, Cancel01, MoreVertical, Copy01. These have a more refined, consistent stroke style vs the filled Material Design defaults. Letta Mobile uses standard Icons.Default.* everywhere. Consider adopting compose-icons/lucide (referenced in rikkahub README) or HugeIcons for a more distinctive visual identity. This is a lower-effort high-impact change — just swap icon references.

title: Add tag-based filtering with FilterChip row and reorderable tag chips
description: RikkaHub's AssistantPage has a reorderable LazyRow of FilterChip tags for filtering assistants by category, with haptic feedback on drag. Letta Mobile's agent list has search but no tag-based filtering. Add: horizontal FilterChip row for agent tags, reorderable with drag handles, multi-select filtering (AND logic), integrate with existing agent tag metadata from Letta API. Apply the same pattern to tool filtering and block library filtering.

title: Add rich text rendering — code highlighting, LaTeX, Mermaid diagrams
description: RikkaHub has a full richtext component library: HighlightCodeBlock (syntax highlighting), LatexText/MathBlock (LaTeX formulas), Mermaid (diagram rendering), SimpleHtmlBlock, and ZoomableAsyncImage. Letta Mobile has a basic MarkdownText component. Extend message rendering to support: syntax-highlighted code blocks (using compose-code-editor or similar), LaTeX formulas (for math-heavy agents), and optionally Mermaid for diagram display. Reference: rikkahub ui/components/richtext/ directory.

title: Review and expose higher-level Letta cloud admin governance
description:
## Why
The mobile app focuses on agent-centric admin today, but the broader Letta admin surface also includes higher-level governance domains such as organizations, users, groups, tokens, or cloud-only admin controls. None of that is currently represented in the Kotlin admin surface.

## Scope
- Determine which higher-level governance domains are relevant and supported for this app target
- Add backlog-ready scope for org/user/group/token style administration only where the deployment mode makes sense
- Keep self-hosted and cloud semantics distinct

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/config/ConfigScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/about/AboutScreen.kt
- external Letta API review identified higher-level admin governance as uncovered by the current app

## Deliverables
- The team has a concrete plan for cloud/admin governance coverage if it belongs in the mobile app
- Self-hosted-only builds are not burdened with irrelevant cloud admin complexity
design: This bead may end in a narrowed scope decision rather than a large implementation. The key is to turn a vague missing domain into an explicit product decision.

comments.author: Claude Code
comments.text: Assessment complete. The Letta OSS/self-hosted API does not expose org/user/group/token governance endpoints — those are cloud-only (Letta Cloud). The existing /v1/groups/ endpoints are multi-agent group chat, not organizational governance. Decision: cloud admin governance is out of scope for this self-hosted mobile admin client. No implementation needed. Self-hosted-only builds remain unburdened with irrelevant cloud admin complexity, which was the acceptance criterion.

title: Expose provider and model administration beyond model pickers
description:
## Why
The app can browse models and populate LLM/embedding dropdowns, but provider- and model-administration coverage is still shallow. A fuller Letta admin surface should expose more of the provider/model domain than simple selection.

## Scope
- Review the current Letta provider/model admin surface available to this client
- Expose the most useful provider/model metadata and configuration workflows in the app
- Keep this distinct from basic create/edit agent model pickers

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/models/ModelBrowserScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/models/ModelBrowserViewModel.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/api/ModelApi.kt
- android-compose/core/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/data/repository/ModelRepository.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/agentlist/AgentListScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/editagent/EditAgentScreen.kt

## Deliverables
- Provider/model admin coverage goes beyond browsing and dropdown population
- Model/provider metadata is visible enough for real admin decisions
- Scope remains grounded in actual Letta API support
design: Keep model/provider admin separate from per-agent editing so it can grow independently if Letta expands provider-level controls.

title: Replace local stub templates with real template admin behavior
description:
## Why
The current Templates screen is backed by a small hardcoded local list, not true Letta template administration. That creates an admin surface mismatch because the UI implies server-backed template management but currently behaves like a local shortcut.

## Scope
- Replace the hardcoded template list with real Letta-backed template behavior where supported
- If real template support is not appropriate for this app target, remove or rename the current screen so it does not imply server-backed admin coverage
- Preserve a clear path for creating useful starter agents without misleading admin semantics

## Evidence / Files
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/templates/TemplatesScreen.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/screens/templates/TemplatesViewModel.kt
- android-compose/app/src/main/java/com/letta/mobile/ui/navigation/AppNavGraph.kt
- external Letta API review identified templates as a broader domain than the current hardcoded implementation

## Deliverables
- Templates surface either reflects real Letta template capabilities or is deliberately reframed
- The app no longer presents a local stub as if it were full admin functionality
design: Prefer truthful product semantics over preserving an inaccurate screen name. If true template management is unavailable, rename or relocate the shortcut behavior instead of pretending parity. Include a path to promote an existing agent configuration into a reusable template so templates can be authored in-app, not only consumed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Templates surface reflects real Letta-backed template capabilities or is deliberately reframed
- Admin users can create a reusable template from an existing agent configuration where API/product support exists
- Template creation preserves the right subset of agent configuration for later reuse without misleading semantics
- The app no longer presents a local stub as if it were full admin functionality
notes: Reframed the misleading local Templates stub into an honest Starter Agents surface. The screen now presents starter presets as quick local agent creation with default system prompts/tags/base tools, adds explicit copy that real Letta template administration depends on server/cloud/admin support, and points reusable workflows toward clone/export/import via the Agents flow. Navigation now routes the helper CTA back to the agent list instead of implying a full server-backed template admin surface.

title: Evaluate Presenter pattern for new features (over ViewModel)
description:
Element X uses Composable Presenters instead of Android ViewModels. Benefits: no Dispatchers.setMain hacks in tests, natural Compose lifecycle, lighter weight.

Evaluation scope:
1. Build ONE new feature using Presenter pattern as proof-of-concept:
   - Pick a simple screen (e.g., Templates or a new About screen)
   - Create FooPresenter.kt with @Composable fun present(): FooState
   - Create FooEvent sealed interface
   - Wire via FooNode or directly in NavGraph
2. Compare testability:
   - Presenter: test with Molecule's moleculeFlow + Turbine
   - ViewModel: test with Dispatchers.setMain + Turbine
3. Compare code size and boilerplate
4. Document findings and recommend: adopt for new features, migrate existing, or skip
5. If positive, create migration plan for existing ViewModels

This is research/evaluation — NOT a mandate to migrate.
Reference: Element X Presenter.kt, Molecule library
Dependencies: none (evaluation only)

title: Split monolithic app into multi-module (features/libraries/services)
description:
Element X has 40+ modules: features/foo/api, features/foo/impl, features/foo/test. Each feature module is isolated. We have one monolithic app module.

Implementation plan:
1. Create module structure:
   - :libraries:core (utilities, extensions)
   - :libraries:designsystem (theme, CustomColors, reusable components)
   - :libraries:network (Ktor client, ApiResult, LettaApiClient)
   - :features:chat:api (ChatScreen interface, navigation)
   - :features:chat:impl (ChatViewModel, ChatScreen, components)
   - :features:agentlist:api
   - :features:agentlist:impl
   - :features:config:api
   - :features:config:impl
2. Create convention plugins:
   - letta.android.library (base Android library config)
   - letta.android.compose (adds Compose deps)
   - letta.android.application (app module config)
3. Move shared models to :libraries:core
4. Move API layer to :libraries:network
5. Move theme/components to :libraries:designsystem
6. Wire navigation through :app module (features don't know each other)
7. Update settings.gradle.kts for multi-module

This is a major refactor — 1 week+ effort. Do as a dedicated initiative.
Reference: Element X module structure, convention plugins

title: Add derivedStateOf for computed state in ViewModels/Composables
description:
Gallery uses derivedStateOf for scroll position, download status, etc. We don't use it anywhere — potential unnecessary recompositions.

Audit and add derivedStateOf where computed state is used:
- ChatScreen: isAtBottom for scroll behavior
- AgentListScreen: filtered agents based on searchQuery
- ConversationsScreen: filtered conversations
- McpScreen: tool count per server

This is a performance optimization pass.

title: Wire TemplatesViewModel to AgentRepository (2 TODOs)
description:
Replace 2 TODO('Wire to repository') in TemplatesViewModel.kt:
- loadTemplates() L38: Load hardcoded template list (or from a future API). For now, emit static list of AgentTemplate objects
- createFromTemplate() L48: Call agentRepository.createAgent with template-based params, invoke onSuccess with new agent ID

Requires: AgentRepository injected.

title: Feature: Optimistic updates for conversation mutations
description: When deleting/archiving conversations, the UI waits for server response + refetch before updating. Use React Query's onMutate for optimistic removal from the list with rollback on error for snappier UX.
close_reason: Added optimistic updates to useDeleteConversation and useUpdateConversation. Both per-agent and all-conversations query caches update instantly with automatic rollback on error.

title: Feature: Add embedding_config now optional on agent creation
description: embedding_config is now optional when creating agents. The agent creation flow can be simplified by removing mandatory embedding config fields.
close_reason: embedding_config already optional. Not used in codebase.

title: AGENTS.md refresh admin action under debug menu
description:
Vibesync exposes `POST /api/admin/agents-md/refresh` (`vibesync src/api/routes/agentsMd.ts`) which re-renders every project's `AGENTS.md` from the global templates. Useful after editing a template — saves opening a terminal on the host.

Add a button on the developer-options screen that triggers this with `dryRun: true` first (so the operator sees the summary), then a confirm to run for real.

# Implementation notes
- New `VibesyncAdminApi.refreshAgentsMd(projectId: String? = null, dryRun: Boolean)` returning the `{ total, updated, dryRun, skipped, errors, results }` summary.
- UI: button → dry-run dialog showing summary → "Apply" confirms with `dryRun=false`.
- Gate behind developer-options toggle (same as I1).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Button reachable only when developer options enabled.
2. Dry-run renders the updated/skipped/errors counts before applying.
3. Apply path posts with dryRun=false and re-renders the summary.
4. 503 (Database not available) shows a clear disabled-state message.
5. Test: API DTO parsing for the response shape.

title: Backend status panel (vibesync /health, /api/stats) under debug menu
description:
Add a read-only debug screen, reachable from Settings → Developer options, that renders the active vibesync backend's `/health` and `/api/stats` payloads. Useful for triaging "is the server up?" / "why is sync stuck?" against the deployed instance without SSH.

This is operator tooling, not a user feature — gate behind the existing developer-options toggle.

# Implementation notes
- New `VibesyncDebugApi` with `getHealth()` + `getStats()`.
- Render as a key/value list; pretty-print nested objects (memory, connectionPool, syncHistory, database).
- Single-shot fetch on screen entry plus a manual refresh affordance; no auto-poll.
- Note: vibesync `/health` is distinct from Letta core `/v1/health/` used by `ServerHealthRepository`. Do not conflate.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Screen reachable only when developer options enabled.
2. Fields: status, uptime, sync, memory, connectionPool, sseClients, syncHistory, database rendered when present.
3. Endpoint failure renders the error verbatim, does not crash.
4. Test: API DTO parsing for /health and /api/stats fixtures.

title: Swap local deviceClass heuristic for androidx.core.device.DeviceClass when it ships
description:
## Context
During letta-mobile-o7ob.3.2 (device/thermal/network tags on Sentry transactions), we investigated `androidx.core.device.DeviceClass` and confirmed no released AndroidX API is available in the current SDK/docs. The bead shipped using a local heuristic combining RAM + launch-era signal as a fallback.

## Scope
When the canonical AndroidX DeviceClass API lands, swap the local heuristic in `DevicePerformanceContext.kt` for the official API.

## Why P4
The local heuristic is good enough for slicing — deviceClass needs to be coarse (low/mid/high) for transaction tagging, not precise. Swapping to the AndroidX API later is a drop-in change if the helper abstraction is respected. Zero user impact from deferring.

## Trigger
Check on AndroidX releases periodically, or when the first report using the current heuristic shows misclassification skew worth correcting.

## Parent
Related to letta-mobile-o7ob.3 (Lane C — Monitor).

title: androidx.emoji2 FileChannel leak tripping LeakedClosableViolation on startup
description:
Discovered via o7ob.1.4 StrictMode penaltyListener on Pixel 2 XL (API 30):

    android.os.strictmode.LeakedClosableViolation at
      androidx.emoji2.text.FontRequestEmojiCompatConfig$FontRequestMetadataLoader.createMetadata
      → androidx.core.graphics.TypefaceCompatUtil.mmap (FileChannelImpl not closed)

Not our code — upstream AndroidX emoji2 defect. Options: (a) pin emoji2 to a newer version if fix exists, (b) suppress LeakedClosableViolation in StrictMode VmPolicy, (c) upstream bug report. Recommend (a)+(b) combo until upstream fix lands.
